The college student scanned the code, paid, and bought a cup of mung bean drink.

Everyone else watched her, their eyes full of anticipation.

The noodles were so delicious, surely the mung bean drink would be great too.

Ssssip!

The college student’s face immediately lit up with joy, exclaiming.

“This mung bean drink is amazing!”

Her face full of delight, the drink was smooth and creamy, giving her a refreshing feeling, like a gentle spring breeze.

“I’m convinced now. Lu’s mung bean drink is definitely good. I want one too!”

“I’ll take one! Just scanned the code!”

“I’m thirsty, I’ll have two!”

With so many orders coming in all at once, Lu Xiyao busily handed out the mung bean drinks, almost tumbling into the cooler box.

But her little hands couldn’t keep up with the demand.

“Hey kiddo, let the big brothers and sisters get their own drinks. You go sit on the little stool.”

“Alright~! But big bros and sis, don’t forget to scan the code, okay?”

“We got it, we won’t forget, you little clever one!”

A long line formed, each person holding a cup of chilled mung bean drink.

Some couldn’t wait and started sipping through their straws right away.

After tasting the drink, all the customers gave it rave reviews.

“The texture of this mung bean drink is incredible, super delicious!”

“Sweet but not overwhelming, with an ice-cold, refreshing taste—I’m in love with it!”

“My gosh, Lu, your mung bean drink is incredible! Way better than milk tea!”

“Two sips and the whole cup’s gone! I need two more to be satisfied!”

…

It was now 8 PM, the time when the night market had the most foot traffic. Word spread quickly. Lu Qin’s stir-fry stall had completely blown up. Over 50 people were lined up, forming a long, crooked queue that filled up the alley.

Some of the customers, bored from waiting, took out their phones to record videos for their social media and Douyin (TikTok).

[This stir-fry spot in the alley of Changlin Road’s University Town is amazing, super tasty. Come try it out, you’ll regret it if you don’t!] (with location tag)

[Stir-fry that’s an absolute YYDS (best of all time), so good it’ll make you shake. If you come before 9, you might still have a chance to get some. Hurry, hurry!] [Stir-fry plus mung bean drink, so good you won’t want to go home tonight!] [Stir-fry.jpg, Mung bean drink.jpg] (No address, just to make the line longer.)

[All the stir-fry you’ve had before is trash, challenge this if you don’t believe me!] (tagged @Changlin Road University Town)

…

At the entrance of the alley, Master Liu and Boss Guo stood side by side, staring at the packed crowd deeper in the alley. They had never seen anything like this before, and never imagined a stir-fry stall could be this popular.

“Old Liu, have you tried their stir-fry?”

“No.”

“Aren’t you curious? Don’t you want to know what it tastes like?”

“Not really.”

“Oh, come on, admit that their stir-fry is better than yours. Is it that hard?”

“It can’t taste that different. They’re just after something new.”

Seeing that Master Liu was still being stubborn, Boss Guo lost interest in arguing.

“Alright, you keep watching. I’m going to buy a portion and try it out.”

“Get me one too,” Master Liu finally gave in after a long internal struggle.

He knew he had to taste it to understand his competition.

“Pay up,” Boss Guo replied and extended his hand.

“A portion of stir-fry only costs a few bucks, I’ll pay you later.”

“Don’t back out on this.”

“You’re so naggy. Just go get in line.”

Boss Guo had only taken a few steps when he noticed waves of customers walking out from the other side. Some were carrying stir-fry, some had empty hands, and some had oil stains around their mouths.

Both Boss Guo and Master Liu were taken aback. What was going on? Were they already sold out?

Just as Boss Guo was about to ask someone, he overheard the empty-handed customers grumbling.

“Unbelievable! It’s not even 9 PM, and the stir-fry’s already sold out, even the mung bean drink gone!”

“There were just too many people! I waited forever and still didn’t get any. I’m so mad!”

“Missed out yesterday, missed out again today. Tomorrow I’m skipping class and coming early. No way I’m missing it three days in a row!”

“Smelled that delicious aroma for half an hour, and didn’t get a single bite. So frustrating!”

…

“Damn! Sold out already?” Master Liu couldn’t believe it. He’d only sold a dozen portions of stir-fry from his own stall. Ever since Lu Qin set up shop, his business had been dead.

Boss Guo spotted a chubby guy carrying six portions of stir-fry.

“Hey man, you bought so much stir-fry, can you spare me a portion?”

“Sure,” the chubby guy agreed so easily that it made Boss Guo’s face light up.

“How much?”

“100 yuan.”

“What? 100 yuan?” Boss Guo thought he had misheard.

The chubby guy nodded casually.

“Yep, 100 yuan. Not a penny less.”

“Are you robbing me?”

“Take it or leave it. I’ll eat them all myself.”

In the end, Boss Guo didn’t buy it. Paying 100 yuan for a portion of stir-fry? That’s crazy! He couldn’t believe there were scalpers for stir-fry now.

Master Liu stood silently, watching the crowd disperse, feeling utterly frustrated.

It was clear he couldn’t continue selling alongside Lu Qin’s stir-fry stall anymore. There would be no business for him. He would have to find a new spot.

chapters.title: Chapter 21 - Boss, Same Place Tomorrow, Rain or Shine, We’ll Be Waiting for You!
chapters.content:
Chapter 21 Boss, Same Place Tomorrow, Rain or Shine, We’ll Be Waiting for You!

Lu Qin was still walking out of the alley with his daughter when some customers ran after him.

“Boss Lu, don’t forget to come tomorrow. I can’t sleep at night without your fried noodles.”

“Yeah, handsome boss, come earlier tomorrow. We can’t wait for your fried noodles and mung bean soup!”

“Boss Lu, rest well tonight, recharge, and continue making fried noodles for us tomorrow!”

“Boss, same place tomorrow, rain or shine, we’ll be waiting for you!”

“Got it~! We’ll be there~! My dad and I will come~!”

“You guys go home and rest early too~!”

“Goodbye~!”

Lu Xiyao sat next to Lu Qin, one hand holding onto the side rail, the other raised high, waving. The three-wheeled food cart rolled out of the narrow alley.

The little girl’s fair and soft face was filled with happiness.

“Daddy, it’s so fun setting up the stall. Let’s do it again tomorrow.”

“Are you tired, Yao Yao?”

“Not tired at all, way easier than going to kindergarten.”

Lu Qin’s lips curled slightly. Kindergarten is harder than making money.

They arrived home at 9:20 PM.

After helping the little one take a bath and dry her hair, Lu Qin tucked her into bed.

“Daddy, tell me a story.”

The little girl yawned, starting to feel sleepy.

Lu Qin gently patted her on the shoulder and began telling the children’s story “Mother Rabbit Recognizes Her Babies.” But before he even got a third of the way through, the little one had already drifted off to sleep.

Lu Qin quietly turned off the lights, closed the door, and left the room. He went out to the yard and gave the three-wheeled food cart a thorough cleaning, using the system’s powerful detergent and cleaning cloth, which made the process four to five times faster and more effective.

No matter how dirty the spot was, one wipe made it gleam like new. He also cleaned the plastic baskets and insulated foam boxes used to store ingredients, leaving them out to dry in a well-ventilated area.

Lu Qin liked things clean, especially when it came to anything related to food. It had to be hygienic so that customers could eat with peace of mind.

Most street food stalls nowadays had poor hygiene. The visible areas were covered in greasy grime, not to mention the hidden corners.

There were also many news reports of people getting food poisoning from street food. Moreover, many studies suggest that eating street food can lead to cancer, and Lu Qin believed this was mainly due to poor hygiene and unhealthy ingredients.

Most starch sausages, squid, and meat skewers were frozen and came in cheap packaging. Stall owners would thaw the food and sometimes add chemicals to make it look fresh.

Lu Qin would never do such a dishonest business. Nor would he set up a stall on a busy roadside where cars pass by, filling the food with dust.

After finishing the cleaning, Lu Qin took a shower and comfortably lay down on the bed.

It was almost 10 PM.

Listening to the crickets chirping outside, Lu Qin started feeling a bit sleepy. He checked his Zhifubao and WeChat Wallet. The combined earnings from the stall had reached nearly 10,000 yuan.

And that was just from last night and today’s full day of work.

The income was quite substantial, and Lu Qin was very satisfied. He no longer had to worry about daily necessities, and his life felt fulfilling every day. The system’s points had reached 628.

Just as Lu Qin was about to draw a lottery, his phone’s WeChat notification chimed. Lu Qin checked his phone and raised an eyebrow slightly—it was his ex-wife, Shen Muyan.

Lu Qin had not deleted the chat history with Shen Muyan. Her past messages were all tied to the word ‘busy’.

…

Lu Qin didn’t blame Shen Muyan, perhaps she really was busy. As the CEO managing several five-star hotels, the pressure must be enormous. That left him to accompany their daughter, acting as both father and mother. Though they were divorced, Shen Muyan was still the mother of his child.

Lu Qin typed back.

A few seconds later, his phone buzzed again.

Inside a villa, the large, luxuriously decorated room felt cold and empty. Shen Muyan lay on the bed in a dark gray silk robe, her slender, fair fingers holding the phone as she pondered her next message.

At work, she was decisive and composed, issuing clear instructions to hundreds of employees. But when it came to her ex-husband, Lu Qin, she didn’t know what to say.

Father and daughter were renting a place in the old district of the south of the city.

“A nice house? Good environment?”

Shen Muyan didn’t think so. The houses in that area were 20 to 30 years old. How could living in an old house be comfortable?

Lu Qin was just trying to act tough. He had fallen so far as to set up a food stall. How could life be good? She was certain the little one wasn’t used to living there either.

Furrowing her brows, Shen Muyan held back and continued messaging.

A food stall counts as a job? Decent income?

Shen Muyan thought Lu Qin was just pretending. She couldn’t probe further, or she’d break the unspoken understanding and hurt his pride.

Frustrated, she didn’t know how to handle the situation. She couldn’t let Lu Qin and the little one live off a food stall forever. At this rate, they wouldn’t even be able to afford her kindergarten tuition by the second half of the year.

Sighing, she decided to change the topic.

Lu Qin thought for a moment, then sent a location.

Before their divorce, they had agreed that as long as the child was willing, she could visit her mother every weekend.

After that, neither of them said anything more.

Shen Muyan didn’t know how to continue the conversation. After thinking for a while, she sent another message.

A pang of discomfort hit Shen Muyan. In the villa, the little one had an entire room dedicated to storing her toys and stuffed animals.

Since father and daughter moved out, she realized that most of the gifts she had bought for her daughter were left behind. She only took the things Lu Qin had given her.

Reflecting on this, Shen Muyan admitted to herself that she hadn’t given her daughter enough attention.

How could a little girl possibly like toy cars?

…

chapters.title: Chapter 22 - Master-Level Tricycle Driving Skills!
chapters.content:
Chapter 22 Fang Ya Discovers the Stir-Fried Noodle Stand in University Town! Master-Level Tricycle Driving Skills!

On the other side of town, Fang Ya was reviewing a food video she’d edited and posted it to her Douyin account. She had over 15 million followers on both Weibo and Douyin.

Recently, her food videos had been underwhelming because most of the popular dishes had already been covered. Finding truly delicious, new food was becoming harder and harder, and repeating the same foods wasn’t an option.

Sighing, she decided to take a break from posting for a while. Bored, she scrolled through Douyin. After a beauty video, the next one that popped up was a street food video.

“This fried noodle stall is incredible! So good it’ll make you cry!”

The video showed a close-up of stir-fried noodles, with a young woman eating and giving a thumbs up. The video’s creator had only a few hundred followers, but it had already garnered four to five thousand likes.

Curious, Fang Ya clicked into the comment section, which had over a hundred comments.

…

Fang Ya browsed the comments. They didn’t seem like ads—they felt genuine.

She left a comment.

Within two minutes, the video creator excitedly replied.

The owner is super handsome and young!! If you go around 7 PM, the stall with the longest line is definitely the one!].

This was the first time such a big influencer had commented on their post, and the creator couldn’t help but send multiple enthusiastic replies.

Fang Ya didn’t respond further, just liked the comments and noted down the address. She decided to visit University Town when she had some free time to see if the stir-fried noodles were as good as everyone said.

…

The next morning, Lu Qin woke up at 5 AM, while the little one was still fast asleep in her room, hugging her teddy bear.

After washing up, Lu Qin went to the yard, unplugged the charger from his three-wheeled food cart, and stashed it under the seat. Then he hopped on the cart and set off to buy ingredients. The night before, before going to sleep, Lu Qin used the system points to enter a lottery draw.

With 628 points, he drew six times. The rewards were decent—dozens of mint candies, a tube of children’s toothpaste, and a skill—Master-Level Tricycle Driving.

The children’s toothpaste from the system wasn’t just any ordinary toothpaste. For children under 10, it effectively prevented cavities and thoroughly cleaned their mouths—perfect for the little one.

Now that Lu Qin had Master-Level Tricycle Driving skills, the moment he sat on the tricycle food cart, his muscles and nerves instinctively felt incredibly proficient. He could now pass a driving test with his eyes closed, even performing drift parking maneuvers.

Not only that, but Lu Qin also noticed that while riding the tricycle, his senses become sharper, allowing him to accurately gauge the surrounding traffic and avoid accidents.

He twisted the handle, and the tricycle cart roared to life. With a swift and elegant U-turn, it shot out of the small courtyard.

At the farmers’ market, Lu Qin bought enough ingredients for the stir-fried noodles—-cabbage, bean sprouts, ham, and more. The vegetables had to be fresh every day, and with how popular his stall had become, ingredients rarely lasted until the next day. He could buy enough ham for three days at a time, but the lean pork also had to be fresh daily.

Lu Qin also bought 10 pounds of old rock sugar to make mung bean soup. Today, Lu Qin planned to introduce a new item to his stall—Bingjing Cake, a delicate pastry that takes considerable time and effort to make. He didn’t plan to make too many, opting for a limited quantity to attract more attention while also diversifying the food offerings at his stand.

At the farmer’s market, Lu Qin spent about half an hour browsing before stopping at a rice and flour store.

“Boss, is this rice from Linggu?” Lu Qin asked, picking up a handful of rice from a woven sack and rubbing it between his fingers.

“You’ve got a good eye, young man. You can tell this rice is from Linggu?”

“Linggu’s rice is more rounded and translucent, and it’s also slightly flatter than rice from other places.”

“You’re a connoisseur.”

“How much is it per pound?”

“5.3 yuan.”

“I’ll take 10 pounds.”

“Alright.”

“Do you have glutinous rice?”

“Yes, on the left, it’s 5.6 yuan. On the right, it’s 6.4 yuan. Which one do you want?”

“Is the one on the left from Xingyang?”

“Authentic Xingyang glutinous rice. I guarantee it.”

“Give me 60 pounds.”

“Okay.”

After scanning the QR code to pay, Lu Qin hoisted the bags of rice and took a quick glance around to memorize the store’s location.

“Boss, I’m off. Give me a discount next time!”

“Sure, take care!”

To make authentic and delicious Bingjing Cake, the quality of the ingredients is crucial. The specific regions where the rice is grown make all the difference. For this cake, the best rice and glutinous rice come from two small, neighboring counties in the north—Linggu and Xingyang.

When it comes to traditional Chinese pastries, the ingredients and processes must be perfect. The Western methods of milling rice and flour with modern machines may speed things up, but they significantly diminish the texture and taste. A discerning palate could easily tell the difference.

Lu Qin also managed to track down an old woman in her eighties who sold him a small millstone for 30 yuan. It was a 12-inch millstone, capable of grinding five to six pounds of rice at a time. The stone had been gathering dust in her storage room for years.

In addition, Lu Qin purchased a steamer for making buns, peanut oil, honey, charcoal, and molds—everything essential for making Bingjing Cake. The honey had to be osmanthus honey, and the peanut oil needed to come from a local workshop—commercially available peanut oil just wouldn’t do.

After nearly an hour and a half of shopping, Lu Qin had spent over 600 yuan on the various ingredients and tools. The three-wheeled cart was fully loaded as he headed back towards the southern part of the city.

When he reached his neighborhood, he noticed Grandpa Ge and a few other neighbors seemingly waiting for him.

Seeing his cart, Grandpa Ge greeted him with a broad smile.

“Xiao Lu, back from shopping? Are you still setting up your stall near Sunshine Primary School today, even though it’s the weekend?”

Lu Qin slowed the cart down and responded, “Yes, even though the students aren’t in school, there are still plenty of residents from the nearby apartment complexes, so there’s business to be done.”

“Great! We’ll come by later to eat your fried noodles and mung bean soup.”

“Sounds good.”

“Xiao Lu, how do you make your fried noodles so delicious? They’re not greasy at all, and after the first bite, I’m already craving the second!”

“My daughter-in-law hasn’t had much of an appetite lately, hasn’t eaten breakfast in days. But yesterday, she had your noodles and almost broke the chopsticks! She wants more today!”

“My little Tiger is the same—he insisted on having your noodles today!”

“When will you be setting up? We want to make sure we’re there on time.”

“In about two hours. I need to prepare everything first.”

“Alright, we’ll be waiting!”

Once back home, Lu Qin saw that the little one was still sleeping. Since it was the weekend, he decided to let her sleep in.

Meanwhile, he began prepping the ingredients for the stall.

The rice noodles for the stir-fried dish only needed to be soaked and then drained. Chopping the vegetables and meat into small pieces and storing them in baskets was also a quick task.

While the mung bean soup was simmering, Lu Qin started preparing to make the Bingjing Cake.

chapters.title: Chapter 23 - Making Ice Crystal Cake! Lu Xiyao Spends the Weekend With Her Grandparents!
chapters.content:
Chapter 23 Making Ice Crystal Cake! Lu Xiyao Spends the Weekend With Her Grandparents!

Lu Qin began making ice crystal cakes. He first washed the glutinous rice and japonica rice, then ground them separately into flour using a small millstone. The ratio of japonica rice to glutinous rice was 3:7. He then added boiled water, white sugar, and several complex auxiliary ingredients, mixing them into a thick dough.

The dough was placed on a board, and he repeatedly slapped it with both hands. The slapping wasn’t random, it required skill and effort. This technique is professionally known as ‘dough slapping’.

Dough slapping is a crucial step in making ice crystal cakes. If done well, the ice crystal cakes will turn out transparent and bubble-free. This technique is somewhat similar to the pounding of sticky rice cakes.

However, sticky rice cakes are pounded after steaming to make them stickier. Dough slapping, on the other hand, allows the dough to absorb oil better.

Next, he added a certain amount of roasted peanut oil. Once the oil was added, no manual stirring was needed. If the dough slapping was done correctly, the dough and the peanut oil would blend seamlessly within a few minutes.

After waiting for a while, sure enough, the dough and the peanut oil had perfectly combined, with no trace left of either. It was quite magical. Then, Lu Qin arranged cleaned four-centimeter square molds and filled them with the dough before placing them in the steamer.

The charcoal used for steaming had to be fruitwood charcoal, these small details were essential, as overlooking them could affect the final taste of the ice crystal cakes.

The next step was controlling the heat. Without at least five years of experience in making ice crystal cakes, it’s hard to master heat control. But for Lu Qin, who had mastered the art of making ice crystal cakes, controlling the heat was not a problem.

After thirty minutes, Lu Qin lifted the lid of the steamer. The ice crystal cakes, crystal clear and flawless, appeared before him.

They were perfect. Absolutely flawless. This was an authentic ice crystal cake. He had steamed sixty pounds of ice crystal cakes in three layers of bamboo steamers.

The cakes were neatly arranged on bamboo boards, looking quite pleasing to the eye. Finally, Lu Qin drizzled a bit of osmanthus honey and marigold on the surface of the ice crystal cakes. After cooling, the ice crystal cakes were complete.

Lu Qin picked up a piece and took a bite. The taste was very unique—at first, cool and sweet, followed by a rich, distinct flavor after chewing a bit.

The ice crystal cakes had a unique flavor and clear layers, leaving a lingering taste that was unforgettable. It could easily surpass any Western cake or dessert.

Lu Qin packed the ice crystal cakes into transparent plastic boxes, each containing half a pound. The price was 25 yuan per box.

Lu Qin wrote the price on the whiteboard, noting.

He also packed mung bean paste into cups, sealed them, and placed them in a foam insulated box. He would sell the mung bean paste hot in the morning since chilled mung bean paste wasn’t good for digestion.

After loading the ingredients onto the food cart, Lu Qin checked the time and went to wake up Lu Xiyao.

“Xiyao, it’s time to get up. It’s almost 9 a.m. Today, your mom will send someone to pick you up. You’ll be spending the weekend with your grandparents.”

“Mmm… Daddy~! In my dream, I was eating ice cream, but when you called me, it disappeared.”

“So?”

“So, Daddy, you need to make up for it and give your little cutie a chocolate-flavored cone.”

“Let’s see how you behave. You need to be good and not cause trouble at your grandparents’ place today.”

Lu Qin pinched the little one’s nose.

“Daddy, can I come back early? I’ll miss you.”

“Sure, I’ll pick you up tomorrow at 3 p.m.”

“Yay!”

Lu Xiyao jumped up, hugged Lu Qin, gave him a little cheek rub, and then started brushing her teeth and washing her face.

Breakfast was fried eggs, ham, and oatmeal. The little one ate her fill.

A few minutes later, a Bentley pulled up outside.

Lu Qin handed the little one three boxes of ice crystal cakes to take with her.

“Daddy~! What’s this?”

“It’s ice crystal cake. One box is for you, keep it for yourself, and the other two are for your grandparents.”

“Ice crystal cake?”

The little one looked at the transparent and delicate cakes in the box, excited.

“Daddy, can I try it now? It must be delicious!”

Lu Qin gently held her hand down, “You just had breakfast, no eating now, maybe later.”

“Okay.”

The little one nodded obediently. If she opened the plastic box, she wouldn’t be able to resist eating it.

Lu Qin thought for a moment and grabbed another box.

“This one is for your mom.”

“Oh.”

The driver took the boxes of ice crystal cake and gently guided Lu Xiyao into the car, protecting her head as she got in.

He respectfully said to Lu Qin, “Mr. Lu, I’ve picked up Miss Xiyao. The chairman and his wife haven’t landed yet, so I’ll take her to Miss Shen first.”

“Alright,” Lu Qin nodded at the driver.

“Daddy, bye! Don’t forget to think of me!”

“I will, goodbye, Xiyao.”

Watching the Bentley drive away, Lu Qin turned back, got on his tricycle cart, and headed towards Sunshine Elementary School.

Fifteen minutes later, the tricycle cart arrived at the school gate. The school doors were shut, and the campus was empty.

With no students attending school, most of the breakfast stalls were missing. It was the weekend, and the nearby residents were just waking up. Some were walking their dogs, others were buying breakfast. Without work, people’s routines slowed down.

As soon as Lu Qin set up his stall, a customer approached.

“Good morning, boss. I’ve been waiting for your stir-fried noodles. Give me three portions with eggs, ham, and lean pork.”

“Alright, coming right up.”

“Boss, do you have mung bean paste today?”

“Yes, it’s in the foam box on the right. Feel free to help yourself.”

“Okay! Three cups of mung bean paste as well!”

While stir-frying, Lu Qin hung up a small whiteboard displaying the menu.

One customer glanced at it and noticed ice crystal cakes priced at 25 yuan a box, with a purchase limit.

“Boss, you’ve got something new today, huh? Ice crystal cake? Where is it?”

“In the large box on the left. Freshly made this morning.”

“25 yuan a box, that’s a bit pricey.”

Lu Qin smiled and explained.

“Ice crystal cakes take a lot of effort and time to make, so they’re a little more expensive, but they’re worth it.”

The customer didn’t doubt the quality. The stir-fried noodles and mung bean paste were top-notch, so the ice crystal cake must be equally good.

“I’ll take a box to try.”

While Lu Qin stir-fried the noodles, the customer bent down to grab a box. The delicate and beautiful ice crystal cakes lay in the transparent plastic box.

The customer couldn’t help but praise.

“Ice crystal cake, right?”

“I’ve never had it before, but it looks great. I’ll give it a try.”

Carefully picking up a piece, it was so exquisite that the customer hesitated to take a bite. He brought it close to his nose, the fragrance of flowers mixed with the scent of rice wafting into his senses, hitting him deeply.

“So fragrant!”

Finally, the customer took a bite, and the soft, sweet texture immediately lit up his face.

“Delicious!”

“So sweet!”

Excited, the customer was thrilled by the unique texture and flavor of the ice crystal cake, savoring the taste. He had never eaten such a delicious dessert before.

“These cakes weren’t even refrigerated, but they still have a cool, refreshing sensation. How amazing!”

“I’ll take two more boxes, boss.”

“Sorry, the ice crystal cakes are limited, two boxes per person maximum.”

“Ah, they’re limited! Okay, I’ll take one more then.”

Slightly disappointed, the customer scanned to pay. The WeChat Wallet notified Lu Qin of a 99.5 yuan deposit. The customer hurried off with the food, eager to share the tasty treats with his family.

After the first customer left, more quickly gathered. These were all people who had enjoyed the stir-fried noodles the day before. They had been thinking about that delicious taste ever since.

“Good morning, boss. Two portions of stir-fried noodles, fully loaded.”

“My favorite stir-fried noodles, one portion, please! And a cup of mung bean paste.”

“Boss, you’re finally here. I came down at 8 a.m., but you weren’t here. I thought you weren’t coming. Quick, give me three portions of stir-fried noodles, fully loaded with extra chili.”

“Hey, boss, what’s this ice crystal cake on your board? Is it good?”

Lu Qin packed up some fresh stir-fried noodles and handed them to a customer. Then he brought out a dozen boxes of ice crystal cakes and stacked them on the counter.

“These are ice crystal cakes. They taste amazing, but they’re limited to two boxes per person.”

chapters.title: Chapter 24 - The Customers’ Eyes Lit up After Tasting the Ice Crystal Cake!
chapters.content:
Chapter 24 The Customers’ Eyes Lit up After Tasting the Ice Crystal Cake!

All the customers in line looked eagerly at the ice crystal cakes on the counter. The crystal-clear cakes looked like art pieces made of crystal, with an incredibly appealing appearance.

“Is this ice crystal cake? So delicate and beautiful!”

“It does look good. 25 yuan a box, and only two boxes per person?”

“I’ve had osmanthus cake and mung bean cake before, and they were pretty good. I wonder if Boss Lu’s ice crystal cake is just as good!”

“There’s no label or date on the box. Is this some kind of unregulated product?”

“Are you dumb? Boss Lu makes these himself. He’s not reselling something from elsewhere.”

At that moment, Lu Qin looked up and explained.

“These ice crystal cakes were made fresh this morning. The main ingredient is glutinous rice, and it’s entirely handmade with no food additives. You can rest assured.”

“I trust Boss Lu on this. Just look at how clean and sanitary his cart is. All the ingredients are covered with cloth, and there isn’t a speck of grease on the counter!”

“Exactly! I never used to eat from street vendors, but I tried his stir-fried noodles because of how clean it looked, and now I’m hooked.”

“Enough talk, Boss Lu, I’ll take two boxes of ice crystal cake.”

“First come, first served. I’ll take two as well.”

“Better act fast, or they’ll be gone. I’ll take two boxes too.”

About ten customers bought the ice crystal cakes, scanning and paying one after another. The sound of payments being received through Alipay and WeChat Wallet kept chiming.

While stir-frying noodles, Lu Qin listened to the notifications, knowing that no one was eating without paying. Some customers couldn’t wait to open the box as soon as they got it, eagerly taking a bite of the cake.

The texture of the ice crystal cake was soft and chewy, giving a pleasant experience with each bite.

One customer was amazed and exclaimed, “So chewy, soft, sweet, and delicious! It’s so good it makes me want to dance to ‘Beat It’!”

He even got goosebumps. Other customers couldn’t hold back and opened their boxes to taste the ice crystal cake. After the first bite, their reactions were all the same.

Astonishment!

Delight!

And then goosebumps!

“Oh my! This ice crystal cake is out-of-this-world delicious!”

“Forget osmanthus cake, mung bean cake, or crystal cake—those are nothing compared to this ice crystal cake!”

“I used to only eat Western desserts like tiramisu and cheesecake, but this ice crystal cake… I won’t say anything else, but I can’t believe I used to be so obsessed with foreign desserts. Our country’s ice crystal cake is the real deal!”

“This ice crystal cake is amazing! The girl I’ve been chasing would definitely love it. Boss, don’t limit the quantity! Two boxes aren’t enough—can I buy one more? Please!”

“Yeah, Boss, the limit is too painful!”

“Exactly, I want to buy two boxes for my parents, and also for my wife, my second uncle, my aunt, my cousin…”

“Boss Lu, money isn’t an issue. I’ll pay extra if you lift the limit.”

“Yeah, I’ll pay double!”

Lu Qin thought for a moment, then shook his head and apologized.

“Sorry, everyone. I just don’t want anyone to buy too much and leave other customers without a chance to try it.”

“If you want more, you can come back every day.”

Hearing this, the customers had no choice but to accept it.

Lu Qin had a point—if someone bought ten boxes at once, there wouldn’t be any left for others.

Who knows, you might be the late customer tomorrow!

“Boss Lu, there’s no limit on stir-fried noodles, right? I’ll take te—”

A customer started to make a big order, but then hesitated and said quietly,

“Ahem… just one portion of stir-fried noodles, please. If I buy too many, they’ll get cold and won’t taste as good.”

“Hahaha,” The other customers laughed.

The lively scene in front of the stir-fried noodle stall broke the peaceful calm of the weekend morning. The residents of the nearby neighborhood all had a group chat.

Some couldn’t help but share their experience.

“The new stir-fried noodle stall downstairs is amazing! Super tasty and very clean. If you haven’t tried it, come quick. You’ll regret missing out!”

“That young man’s cooking skills are excellent! He’s also got ice crystal cakes today, but you can only buy two boxes per person—limited sale!”

“Really? Stir-fried noodles are a bit greasy though. Isn’t it bad to eat something so oily in the morning?”

“You don’t know what you’re talking about. Boss Lu’s stir-fried noodles are flavorful but not greasy at all. They’re refreshing and delicious!”

“I usually wouldn’t bother sharing about regular noodles, but this is seriously good!”

“It’s finally my turn to order. He’s packing up soon. If you get to eat this, consider yourself lucky! The noodles and ice crystal cakes are out of this world!”

“Don’t post it in the group! If more people come, we’ll be waiting in line forever!”

…

Residents from different complexes shared in their respective chat groups. Before long, people started coming downstairs to buy stir-fried noodles.

“Let me see what’s so special about these noodles.”

“It must be that stall with the line. The others don’t have any customers.”

“Wow! It smells amazing. I’ll buy a portion and try it.”

“Sister Chen, why did you buy so much stuff? Did you go to the supermarket?”

“No, this is all from Boss Lu’s noodle stall! Four portions of stir-fried noodles, two boxes of ice crystal cakes, and eight cups of mung bean paste.”

“That’s a lot!”

“You don’t understand. The ice crystal cakes are limited. If I could, I’d buy three more boxes to keep in the fridge.”

“Enough chatting. My husband and kids are waiting for these noodles!”

Not far away, the stall owners selling flatbread and stuffed pancakes looked on enviously. Compared to their stalls, with only one or two customers, Lu Qin’s stir-fried noodle stall had a line of over ten people. Comparing themselves to him would only make them furious!

In just half an hour, Lu Qin sold out more than 200 cups of mung bean paste. There were only fifty portions of stir-fried noodles left, and only six boxes of ice crystal cake remained.

Grandpa Ge and a few neighbors arrived, each ordering stir-fried noodles. They also wanted mung bean paste but were disappointed to find it had sold out.

“Mung bean paste sold out so quickly. What a shame.”

Grandpa Ge’s eyes lit up when he saw the ice crystal cakes.

“Little Lu, what’s this?”

“Ice crystal cakes, a new product.”

“Taste samples? There are only a few left?”

Lu Qin smiled and said, “I’ve already sold over 100 boxes today.”

“Wow! You should’ve told me sooner. I’ll take all the boxes left!”

Grandpa Ge, being sharp, knew that if over 100 boxes had sold, these cakes must be delicious. Plus, they looked amazing.

Hearing Grandpa Ge say that, the other elderly neighbors weren’t too happy.

“Old Ge, be fair!”

“Exactly. We’ve been neighbors for years, and you’re buying all the cakes? Don’t you feel guilty?”

Grandpa Ge didn’t care.

“I love sweets. You guys either have high blood sugar or diabetes—can you even eat sweets? I’m doing this for your own good!”

Wang Guifen spat.

“Nonsense, Old Ge! You just want to hog all the ice crystal cakes for yourself!”

Lu Qin chuckled and shook his head.

“No need to argue, everyone. There are six boxes left, and there are nine of you. I’ll divide the cakes into nine portions, so each of you can have one.”

“Look at that, Boss Lu is fair and just.”

“How much is a box?”

“It was originally 25 yuan per box. Let’s say 20 yuan per box now.”

“That’s a bit expensive.”

“Expensive? Just looking at the presentation, it’s worth it.”

“Not expensive at all. Yesterday I took my granddaughter to a cake shop, and a tiny cake cost 36 yuan. It wasn’t even a golden cake!”

Grandpa Ge chimed in.

“Anything Little Lu makes is worth the price. It’s not expensive. Let me taste it first.”

Grandpa Ge gulped, being a big fan of sweets, and could hardly wait. He took a bite of the ice crystal cake, and his eyes immediately lit up. He didn’t say a word, just savored it slowly and chewed carefully. The others watched him.

“Well, Old Ge, how is it? Is it good?”

“Of course it’s good! Look at his expression—he’s so happy, his wrinkles just got deeper!”

…

chapters.title: Chapter 25 - Getting Used to Shen Muyan's Busy Schedule! Sister, You Don't Understand!
chapters.content:
Chapter 25 Getting Used to Shen Muyan’s Busy Schedule! Sister, You Don’t Understand!

At the top floor of the O’Rei Hotel, in the CEO’s office, Lu Xiyao sat obediently on a high stool, watching cartoons on her tablet at the tea bar.

Shen Muyan’s assistant, Song Xuanxuan, sat next to her, keeping her company. Shen Muyan wasn’t there.

Recently, the hotel’s room service had been slacking, and President Shen had gone to give a morning briefing. Lu Xiyao had gotten used to her mother’s busy schedule. Spending weekends with her mom was bound to be boring. If it weren’t for waiting to see her grandparents, she wouldn’t have wanted to come.

Once her grandparents arrived, Lu Xiyao was going to the amusement park. Although the little girl wanted her dad to take her, she knew he had to work hard to earn money. She didn’t want to tire Lu Qin out, plus, going to the amusement park cost money. Her grandparents, on the other hand, were quite wealthy.

After watching Frozen for about ten minutes, Shen Muyan pushed open the door and entered the office, followed by four middle-aged men, likely department managers.

In a rush, Shen Muyan walked over to Lu Xiyao, patted her on the head, and apologized,

“Xiyao, Mommy has a bit more work to do. I’ll come with you later. Be a good girl and keep watching your cartoons. If you need anything, just ask Sister Song.”

“Got it,” Lu Xiyao nodded mechanically.

“Song Xuanxuan, stay with Xiyao.”

“Understood,” Song Xuanxuan also nodded mechanically.

Shen Muyan quickly headed to her desk, and the department managers followed closely behind.

Lu Xiyao rested her chin in her hand and said to Song Xuanxuan, “Sigh, I’m already used to this.”

Song Xuanxuan pinched her soft little cheek and said helplessly, “I’m used to it too.”

The two shared a small smile.

“Xiyao, don’t just watch cartoons. There’s plenty to eat here, let’s eat while we watch.”

On the bar, there were many snacks, fruits, and pastries—macarons, cupcake desserts, chocolates, dried plums, and all kinds of fruits.

Song Xuanxuan picked up a green macaron and handed it to Xiyao.

“Our hotel’s pastry chef made this. It’s really delicious. Try it.”

“Sister Song, I don’t want it,” the little girl shook her head.

She had eaten these kinds of treats before and used to enjoy them, but perhaps her taste had become more refined after eating her dad’s cooking. She wasn’t particularly interested.

Song Xuanxuan, a true foodie, couldn’t resist taking a bite herself.

“It’s really good, Xiyao. Are you sure you don’t want to try it?”

“Not interested,” Xiyao shook her head again.

“Then how about this strawberry cupcake? Here!”

Song Xuanxuan tried offering something else.

“Still not interested.”

“Huh? A strawberry cupcake, and you’re not interested?”

Song Xuanxuan was puzzled. Usually, these fancy Western pastries were irresistible to kids.

Even as an adult, she couldn’t resist.

“Are kids these days really this picky?”

Song Xuanxuan was a bit speechless. She took a spoonful of the strawberry cake and popped it into her mouth.

“If you don’t want it, Sister Song will eat it. Look, it’s really good.”

She exaggerated her enjoyment, chewing with delight in front of Lu Xiyao.

It truly was delicious, as the hotel’s pastry chef was hired from Italy and had exceptional skills.

The little girl glanced at her and sighed,

“Sister, you don’t understand.”

“What?”

Song Xuanxuan was stunned.

What didn’t she understand?

Well, she hadn’t tasted Lu Qin’s cooking, so it wasn’t really her fault.

Then, Lu Xiyao remembered the boxes of ice crystal cakes she had brought along.

She immediately perked up.

“Sister, where are the boxes I brought?”

“Oh, you mean those boxes of sugar cakes? They’re in my drawer,” Song Xuanxuan said, digging out the strawberries from the cupcake and eating them.

“Could you bring them to me, please?”

“Xiyao, those probably don’t taste good. There are so many delicious treats here, just eat these.”

The few boxes of sugar cakes were in a plain white plastic bag, like something you’d buy from a street vendor. They didn’t look promising, to say the least. Apparently, the little girl’s dad had made them for her grandparents, the chairman and his wife.

But street-bought sugar cakes…

It felt a bit shabby.

After thinking for a moment, Song Xuanxuan finally understood.

It seemed like Shen’s ex-husband had left with nothing, and now he was probably struggling financially, unable to afford anything fancy. So, he had sent the little girl over with just some casual snacks.

Sigh…

Song Xuanxuan thought that when the chairman and his wife arrived, it might be better not to bring out those boxes, to save the ex-husband some dignity.

“Sister, you don’t understand. Please bring them over for me.”

Song Xuanxuan was speechless. This little girl, talking like an adult—what was there not to understand?

Even though she thought that, Song Xuanxuan still fetched the boxes.

The little girl, full of joy and excitement, took them with both hands and placed them on the table.

“One of these is mine. I’ll eat it first.”

The clever little girl knew her grandpa might not be satisfied with just one box and would come to snatch hers. Grandpa often stole her snacks!

She opened the box, and upon seeing the delicate, translucent square cakes inside, she couldn’t help but exclaim,

“Wow~! So beautiful~!”

Song Xuanxuan, who was just scraping the last bit of strawberry cake from her cup and licking the cream off her lips, heard the little one’s voice.

Turning her head, she saw exquisite, crystal-clear cakes lying in the simple plastic box.

This… wasn’t just some sugar cake. It actually looked pretty good.

“Xiyao, what is this?”

Song Xuanxuan leaned over curiously.

As she got closer, a rich, fragrant aroma wafted into her nose.

It smelled wonderful.

“Daddy says it’s called ice crystal cake.”

“Ice crystal cake? Never heard of it. Is it tasty?”

“Of course, it’s delicious~! Everything my daddy makes is super tasty.”

Her ex-husband made it?

Not something bought from a street vendor?

She had misunderstood.

Song Xuanxuan, being a foodie, swallowed her saliva. This ice crystal cake did look quite good, and she wanted to try it. But she had just said it probably wouldn’t taste good, so now it was a bit awkward to ask.

Besides, it was the child’s snack. Would an adult really feel right asking for it?

Lu Xiyao picked up a piece and took a bite. It was soft and chewy, with the smoothness of jelly and the fine texture of sugar cake. This unbeatable texture and flavor were even more appealing to a child.

As she chewed the ice crystal cake, Xiyao said nothing, simply enjoying the deliciousness. Her big, grape-like eyes narrowed into happy crescents.

“So tasty~!”

Xiyao quickly finished one piece, then licked her fingers.

Song Xuanxuan pursed her lips.

Was it really that good?

Kids don’t lie.

But what could she do? She really wanted to try it.

How could she ask?

Xiyao picked up another piece and eagerly stuffed it into her mouth, chewing noisily and enjoying every bite.

Finally, Song Xuanxuan couldn’t resist any longer.

“Um… Xiyao, could you let Sister try a little bit?”

“Sister, didn’t you just say this isn’t as good as those cakes~?”

The little girl had a good memory. Sister had just said that Daddy’s food wasn’t tasty, so why give her any now?

Song Xuanxuan quickly smiled apologetically,

“Sorry, Xiyao. Sister didn’t know what she was talking about. Your daddy’s ice crystal cake must be delicious.”

“Just looking at it is making my mouth water~!”

Hearing Song Xuanxuan plead and praise, Xiyao finally nodded.

“Alright, but you can only have half a piece. Just a taste is enough.”

“Half a piece is fine. Hehe, thank you, Xiyao.”

Xiyao used a fork to cut off half a piece and handed it to Song Xuanxuan.

Song Xuanxuan popped it into her mouth. Soft, sweet but not too rich, with a strong fragrance of rice and osmanthus.

Some flavors are impossible to describe with words.

Now Song Xuanxuan understood why the little girl had said she didn’t understand. Because she really hadn’t understood.

How could something be this delicious?

chapters.title: Chapter 26 - How Could Something Taste So Good in This World?
chapters.content:
Chapter 26 How Could Something Taste So Good in This World?

How could something taste so good in this world?

As a self-proclaimed foodie, Song Xuanxuan thought she had tasted plenty of delicious things.

But!

She had never tasted anything like this ice crystal cake.

The key was, it was unbelievably delicious!

So good that… she almost wanted to cry!

The macarons and strawberry cakes she ate earlier didn’t even compare to this half-piece of ice crystal cake.

Seeing Song Xuanxuan’s blissful expression, Lu Xiyao proudly asked, “ Hmph , Sister Song, how is it? My daddy’s ice crystal cake, delicious, right?”

“It’s amazing!”

“Xiyao, half a piece is too little. Let Sister have another half, okay?”

“No way, I don’t even have enough for myself!”

“Just one piece, I know Xiyao is very generous.”

Song Xuanxuan, acting like a child, started pleading for more.

“No, no! You can eat those cakes, but the ice crystal cake is mine, and I don’t have enough for myself!”

“Xiyao, sweetie, you’re the best! You’re so generous! Give Sister a bite, and I’ll take you to play the claw machine~!”

“Then I want to catch Miffy Rabbit and Pikachu.”

“No problem.”

“Only a small bite, okay?”

“Don’t worry, just a small bite.”

Lu Xiyao stretched out her delicate little hand and handed over a small piece of ice crystal cake.

Song Xuanxuan, satisfied, ate the small piece. But it wasn’t enough. She wasn’t satisfied. However, there was no more left.

The little girl had finished the entire box, and Song Xuanxuan had only managed to get a small taste. Song Xuanxuan eyed the other boxes.

Lu Xiyao grinned mischievously.

“Sister Song, do you want some from those boxes?”

“I… uh… no, no, I don’t want any.”

Song Xuanxuan quickly waved her hand. Those were meant for the chairman and President Shen. She wouldn’t dare touch them.

“Alright, let’s execute the plan as discussed…”

At the desk, Shen Muyan finished discussing work with the department managers, who then exited the office.

“Xiyao.”

Shen Muyan walked over to the tea bar and saw Song Xuanxuan and the little girl chatting and laughing, her furrowed brows relaxing.

“Mom, you’re done working?”

Lu Xiyao closed her tablet and looked up at Shen Muyan.

“Yes, I’ll spend some time with you now, okay?”

“Xuanxuan, get me a cup of coffee.”

“Yes, President Shen.”

Song Xuanxuan stood up, wiped some crumbs from her mouth, and went to make coffee.

Shen Muyan sat next to Lu Xiyao and squeezed her little hand.

“Xiyao, what were you eating?”

“Ice crystal cake, Mom, do you want some?”

Shen Muyan glanced at the white plastic bag and frowned. It looked like something bought from a street vendor, likely filled with additives and artificial colors.

How could Lu Qin give her this to eat?

“Mom doesn’t eat sweets, and you shouldn’t eat too much either, okay?”

“Oh.”

The little girl’s eyes darted mischievously, and she said, “Mom, this box is from Dad for you. If you don’t want it, I’ll keep it for myself~!”

“Alright, alright, it’s all yours.”

Hearing that it was from Lu Qin, Shen Muyan was momentarily surprised and felt a small touch of warmth in her heart. But she didn’t eat these kinds of cakes.

Song Xuanxuan glanced at Lu Xiyao with a puzzled expression. This clever little girl intentionally didn’t mention how delicious the ice crystal cake was, so now she could claim Shen’s portion for herself.

Song Xuanxuan thought that perhaps the boss should try her ex-husband’s ice crystal cake. It seemed like she didn’t even know he had such a talent.

After drinking her coffee and spending about twenty minutes with Lu Xiyao, Shen Muyan returned to work.

Left alone, Xiyao continued playing Plants vs. Zombies on her tablet.

“Xiyao, you should plant the Ice Peashooter. It slows down the zombies.”

Song Xuanxuan suggested.

“I don’t like it. I want to plant a lot of sunflowers.”

“I love sunflowers~!”

After playing for a while, the little girl got hungry again, so she took out Shen Muyan’s box of ice crystal cake and started eating.

“Xiyao, look! There’s a rainbow outside the window.”

“Huh?”

“There’s no rainbow. Where did you see one?”

Lu Xiyao turned her head in confusion, only to see Song Xuanxuan standing there expressionless, saying nothing. The little girl felt something was off. She glanced down at the box and noticed that a piece of ice crystal cake seemed to be missing.

Just as she was about to count the pieces, a cheerful voice came from the door.

“Xiyao, darling! Grandpa and Grandma are back!”

“Hello, Chairman! Hello, Madam!”

Song Xuanxuan quickly swallowed the ice crystal cake in her mouth and respectfully greeted them.

“Grandpa!”

“Grandma! You’re back already?”

Seeing Shen Hongda and Jiang Qin, Lu Xiyao beamed with joy.

Shen Hongda strode over and scooped Xiyao up in his arms.

Jiang Qin gently stroked her head, her eyes filled with kindness and affection.

“Grandpa, Grandma, were you tired from flying?”

“We’re not tired at all when we see our little darling.”

Shen Hongda and Jiang Qin took the little girl out, first taking her to the amusement park for some fun.

Shen Hongda, still in good health, even went on the pirate ship ride with her.

“Xiyao, are you hungry? Grandpa will take you to eat something delicious.”

Their Mercedes-Benz drove into a secluded mansion in the countryside, home to an exclusive private chef. Dishes were brought to the table, and both Shen Hongda and Jiang Qin had two bowls of rice each. They couldn’t get used to foreign food after all, and this meal was much more to their liking. Xiyao, however, only took a few bites and didn’t eat much.

“Why aren’t you eating, Xiyao? There’s so much delicious food here,” Shen Hongda asked curiously.

“Grandpa, I’m not very hungry,” she replied.

In truth, she wasn’t hungry—she had already eaten the ice crystal cake, plus ice cream and fries at the amusement park.

On top of that, while the dishes from the private chef looked exquisite and used the best ingredients, they just didn’t taste as good as the simple home-cooked meals her dad made.

“No worries. When you’re hungry later, Grandpa will take you to eat something else.”

…

By the time they returned to the villa, it was already 6:30 p.m. Lu Xiyao video-called Lu Qin.

Lu Qin had just finished dinner and was preparing ingredients to set up his night market stall near the university. Shen Hongda and Jiang Qin didn’t appear in the video, choosing to sit outside of the camera’s view, silently observing Lu Qin on the screen. They saw him organizing the ingredients on his food cart, looking busy and a bit worn out.

Shen Hongda and Jiang Qin exchanged glances, both feeling a mix of emotions. Since the divorce, Lu Qin had not only been making a living but also raising Xiyao on his own. It was clear he was working hard. He even had to set up a food stall at night. Raising a child as a single father was no easy task.

Jiang Qin’s eyes welled up, and she quickly stood up and went to the bathroom to hide her tears. Shen Hongda also stood up and walked to a corner, pulling out his phone to make a call to an old friend.

…

Later in the evening, Jiang Qin stayed with Lu Xiyao in an upstairs room, playing games.

In the living room, an older man with graying hair sat beside Shen Hongda, and the two sipped tea together.

“Oh, I think I understand the situation,” the man said.

“Your son-in-law—or rather, your ex-son-in-law—can’t find a job, and you can’t step in directly. So, you’ve asked me to help him find a decent job?”

“That’s right,” Shen Hongda nodded.

“That’s easy. Consider it done.”

Shen Hongda added, “But, Old Chen, it has to be discreet. He mustn’t realize that someone is helping him.”

Chen Dahai reassured him.

“Don’t worry, I’ve got it covered.”

“By the way, why did your daughter get divorced in the first place?” Chen Dahai asked.

Shen Hongda sighed.

“It’s the young people’s relationship. We old folks don’t meddle.”

“It’s a pity, though. I always liked Lu, I thought of him as my own son.”

“Now that I’m helping you out, how do you plan to thank me?” Chen Dahai teased.

“Hey, didn’t you say it was no big deal?” Shen Hongda shot back, annoyed.

“There’s no such thing as a free lunch,” Chen Dahai quipped.

Shen Hongda rolled his eyes at him, and then his gaze fell on the two boxes of simply packaged cakes on the table.

“Here, take these two boxes of… oh, rice cakes. I brought them from abroad just for you.”

“Rice cakes? From abroad?”

Chen Dahai picked up the boxes, looking skeptical.

chapters.title: Chapter 27 - I Don't Want Moutai, I Only Want Ice Crystal Cake!
chapters.content:
Chapter 27 I Don’t Want Moutai, I Only Want Ice Crystal Cake!

Shen Hongda and Chen Dahai were long-time friends. They have always been informal with each other.

When Chen Dahai asked for a favor, Shen Hongda tossed the few boxes of unknown pastries on the coffee table to him, brushing him off. These two boxes of pastries seemed to have been taken from the car by the driver. They were brought by a little guy.

Looking at the packaging, they appeared to be the kind bought from the street, possibly something kids would enjoy. Chen Dahai picked up a box of ice crystal cake with disdain, glared at Shen Hongda, and said, “Good one, Lao Shen, are you just going to send me off with these two lousy cakes?”

“From abroad, huh? Trying to fool kids!”

“This is definitely from the street.”

“Stop talking nonsense, hurry up and get that treasured Moutai of yours. I’m taking a bottle.”

As soon as Shen Hongda heard that Chen Dahai wanted his treasured Moutai, he immediately retorted.

“What nonsense! This isn’t from the street, it’s exquisite pastries made by a renowned pastry chef from abroad.”

“That pastry chef has been featured in foreign magazines, and I wouldn’t even give this to anyone else, you don’t believe me? Just try it!”

Chen Dahai was taken aback, looking at Shen Hongda with suspicion.

“Really? Then let me try.”

Shen Hongda felt a little guilty and laughed, saying, “Well… try it at home, it’s so late, you should head back. I’ll treat you to dinner another time.”

Seeing Shen Hongda’s expression, Chen Dahai understood he was being deceived. He sneered and said, “No, I’m trying it now.”

“I want to see how good this so-called pastry made by a renowned foreign pastry chef can be.”

“Then… go ahead and try it.”

Shen Hongda kept his expression unchanged but felt a bit uneasy inside. Chen Dahai opened the plastic box, revealing five crystal-clear ice crystal cakes.

“Hmm… they do look quite delicate.”

“But… these pastries usually look pretty, but they taste just average, overly sweet.”

Chen Dahai glanced at Shen Hongda and asked, “Lao Shen, are you sure this is Western pastry?”

“I swear it looks more like a Chinese pastry, it resembles osmanthus cake a bit.”

Shen Hongda looked at the ice crystal cakes in the box and impatiently said, “Who cares if it’s Western or Chinese, as long as it’s good!”

“Alright, I’ll give it a try. If it’s not good, I’m definitely taking a bottle of your Moutai.”

After saying that, Chen Dahai picked up a piece of ice crystal cake and took a bite. Shen Hongda didn’t want to part with his cherished Moutai.

If Chen Dahai said it wasn’t good, he’d just claim that everyone has different tastes and that Chen Dahai was too picky. Those foreigners really love these pastries.

Yeah, that’ll work. Chen Dahai chewed and swallowed. Then, surprise lit up his eyes. “Hmm, it’s really delicious!”

Chen Dahai praised sincerely,

“Lao Shen, you’ve got good taste, this pastry is indeed a rare delicacy.”

“I’ve never had such a delicious pastry in my life!”

“Lao Shen, what’s the name of this pastry?”

“Hmm? This pastry is called…”

Shen Hongda couldn’t answer right away and countered, “Is it really good?”

Chen Dahai took another bite of the ice crystal cake, his face full of joy and enjoyment.

“What? You haven’t tried it yourself?”

“Not yet, I gave it to you before I could.”

“Come on, let me try a piece.”

Chen Dahai hurriedly gathered the two boxes of ice crystal cakes.

“Didn’t you just give them to me? Why are you eating them?”

Shen Hongda was taken aback, raising an eyebrow.

“Why so stingy? I gave these to you!”

“Once you give them to me, they’re mine!”

“Not giving them to you!”

“Not giving? Then I won’t help you with that matter!”

…

The two old men, both over a hundred years old combined, started arguing over two boxes of ice crystal cakes.

Jiang Qin heard the commotion and came downstairs.

“You two, keep it down. Yao Yao just fell asleep watching cartoons.”

Shen Hongda took a bite of the ice crystal cake and waved to Jiang Qin.

“Wife, come and try this pastry, it’s really delicious.”

Chen Dahai hid one of the boxes of ice crystal cakes in his arms, warily saying, “This box I must take with me. Lao Shen, you promised me you’d bring two more boxes tomorrow.”

Shen Hongda ignored Chen Dahai and had Jiang Qin try a piece of the ice crystal cake.

After tasting it, Jiang Qin’s eyes lit up as she nodded.

“What is this? It tastes really good, better than any pastries I’ve ever had!”

Chen Dahai raised an eyebrow.

“What? You two don’t even know what it is? Where did these two boxes come from?”

“Lao Shen, tell me you’ll bring me two more boxes tomorrow. I’ll share one with you.”

“You think you know nothing, saying you’ll bring me two more boxes tomorrow is just to fool me?”

“Who’s fooling you? Just wait until tomorrow,” Shen Hongda replied.

“Alright, it’s settled then. We’re all people of status, so we can’t go back on our word.”

“Okay, stop talking nonsense.”

Shen Hongda dismissed Chen Dahai and then called over the driver.

“Old Zhong, where did you get those two boxes of pastries from the car?”

Shen Hongda assumed they were bought from a roadside vendor while picking up Yao Yao.

“Oh, Chairman, when I picked up your granddaughter this morning, your son-in-law asked me to bring them.”

“Lu Qin?” Shen Hongda paused for a moment.

“Yes, it seems he made them himself, called ice crystal cake. There were a total of four boxes—two for you, one for Ms. Shen, and one for your granddaughter to eat on the way.”

Jiang Qin was surprised.

“I didn’t expect Lu Qin to have this kind of skill!”

Shen Hongda was also quite astonished. Having lived most of his life, he had tasted countless delicacies, including pastries made by famous chefs. However, this ice crystal cake was better and more unique than any pastry he had ever tried.

To make such exquisite ice crystal cakes wasn’t just an ordinary skill.

Shen Hongda sighed.

“Little Lu is really thoughtful.”

At first, he thought the unassuming packaging was from some small roadside stall. It turned out Lu Qin had made them himself. This was his heartfelt intention.

Despite the simple packaging, given the flavor of the ice crystal cake, it could stand out anywhere. The fact that Chen Dahai would forgo his Moutai for the ice crystal cake said it all.

“Alright, Old Zhong, you can take a break now.”

After the driver left, Shen Hongda and Jiang Qin sat on the sofa, and the plastic box in front of them was already empty.

The couple had only eaten one piece of the ice crystal cake each. That was hardly satisfying. They were reminiscing about the delightful taste of the ice crystal cake. It was sweet but not cloying, soft and chewy.

After chewing, there was a particularly rich flavor. Pastries of such quality were hard to come by.

“Does Xiao Yan know that Lu Qin has this kind of skill?” Jiang Qin asked.

(ED Note: Xiao Yan is a nickname of Shen Muyan)

Shen Hongda shook his head.

“She definitely doesn’t know, that girl only knows work and doesn’t care about her husband or daughter at all.”

“I’ve mentioned it several times before she got divorced, but it was no use.”

“Look at her now, such a good son-in-law has left, where can she find another man like him?”

Jiang Qin agreed wholeheartedly.

“Indeed, Lu Qin voluntarily quit his job to stay at home and take care of Yao Yao. Yao Yao is so well-behaved and understanding, thanks to Lu Qin’s upbringing.”

Shen Hongda sighed.

“We’ve treated Little Lu unfairly.”

The couple fell silent for a moment.

“Wife, do you still want to eat the ice crystal cake?” Shen Hongda suddenly asked.

“Of course I want to, I only had one piece earlier, and I’m still craving it.”

“I’ll ask Xiao Yan to tell Lu Qin to make a few boxes of ice crystal cake for us.” Shen Hongda replied.

“I’ve already promised Chen Dahai two boxes.”

Jiang Qin expressed her concerns.

“Now that they’re both divorced, Xiao Yan might find it awkward to bring it up.”

“Let her figure it out, that’s an order!”

With that, Shen Hongda dialed Shen Muyan’s phone.

……

chapters.title: Chapter 28 - Night Market Street’s Popular Food Stands Have No Line! The Streamer is Devastated!
chapters.content:
Chapter 28 Night Market Street’s Popular Food Stands Have No Line! The Streamer is Devastated!

At Changlin Road, University Town, Night Market Street, a popular food vlogger on Douyin, known for showcasing the best spots to eat, drink, and hang out in Jiangcheng, was live-streaming with a selfie stick in hand at the night market.

“Family, this bustling street is the famous University Town Night Market Street in the southern part of the city.”

“For all you late-night snack lovers, this street is a must-visit.”

“As a genuine local foodie, I highly recommend Fat Sister’s Braised Meat Wraps. They’re tender and juicy, and just 12 yuan for a filling portion. Every time I come here, I always get one.”

“But the only downside is the line—let’s head over and take a look…”

The streamer, known as ‘Panda Loves Food & Drink’, approached the Fat Sister’s Braised Meat Wrap stall.

She froze.

Viewers in the chat started to get curious.

Feeling awkward, ‘Panda Loves Food & Drink’ quickly explained.

“Alright, family, as we eat this braised meat wrap, we’ll head over to grab some grilled squid. Let me tell you, that place is so good you usually have to wait at least ten minutes in line.”

As the camera followed her steps to a barbecue stand, more question marks filled the chat.

‘Panda Loves Food & Drink’ looked at the chat, then back at the stall in front of her, confused.

The chat started roasting her.

‘Panda Loves Food & Drink’ watched the comments piling up, feeling on the verge of breaking down.

Some local Jiangcheng residents in the chat finally said something in her defense.

‘Panda Loves Food & Drink’ responded, “Family, maybe today is just a weird day. Let me take you to the next stall—Liu’s Fried Noodles are also super delicious…”

She walked towards the entrance of an alley, only to find Liu’s Fried Noodle stand was gone.

“Uh… did Mr. Liu not set up today?”

The chat erupted with more sarcasm.

‘Panda Loves Food & Drink’ was on the verge of tears.

What is going on today?

The usual stalls with long lines suddenly have no customers.

As she puzzled over this, a group of university students hurried past the camera.

“Hurry up, the fried noodles are almost sold out!”

“Ahhh! I’ve been craving Boss Lu’s fried noodles for two days. I couldn’t get any yesterday, but I must get some today!”

“Our class rep said there’s a new dish today called Ice Crystal Cake. It’s supposed to be amazing and super limited. Let’s go check it out!”

Before ‘Panda Loves Food & Drink’ could react, the chat blew up.

Finally realizing what was happening, ‘Panda Loves Food & Drink’ turned her camera in the direction the students had run. Deep in the alley, there was a stall bustling with activity.

In stark contrast to the other empty stalls.

“Wait… which stall is this? I’ve never seen it before…” ‘Panda Loves Food & Drink’ said, perplexed.

The chat grew impatient.

‘Panda Loves Food & Drink’ was lost, with the chat now calling the shots, forcing her to head in that direction.

“Boss Lu, do you still have any green bean soup left?”

“Sorry, it’s sold out.”

“Damn! You can’t even be a minute late!”

“Why is the Ice Crystal Cake limited to two boxes? I devoured them in under a minute and still want more!”

“The guy who ordered 10 portions of fried noodles up front is too much! Are you reselling? There’s a huge line behind you—so selfish!”

“Hey, miss, why are you cutting in line?”

“Hey there, I was late, can I cut in? I’ll add you on WeChat.”

“Flirting won’t work here, not even if you call me ‘daddy.’ Get to the back. Women only slow down my fried noodles!”

“…”

“Boss Lu, can you make bigger batches? I can’t wait anymore—I’m drooling!”

In front of Lu Qin’s fried noodle stand, there were at least 40-50 people lined up.

A long line.

Even the stall owners selling oden and sushi abandoned their own stalls to join the line for fried noodles. The scene was impressive.

‘Panda Loves Food & Drink’ and her livestream audience were in total shock!

A few seconds later, the livestream chat went wild.

‘Panda Loves Food & Drink’ was completely baffled by what she was seeing.

When did this fried noodle stand pop up?

No wonder the other stalls were so quiet—all the business had been stolen by this one!

She ignored the chat and pushed her way forward.

“Hey, what are you doing? Lady, get to the back of the line!”

“Is she live-streaming?”

“Did the camera just catch me? Am I on TV?”

“Here, let me flash a peace sign for the noodles!”

‘Panda Loves Food & Drink’ managed to squeeze to the front of the fried noodle stall.

Her phone camera zoomed in on Lu Qin.

“Wow! This young owner is drop-dead gorgeous!”

She grabbed a customer who had just bought their noodles and asked, “Excuse me, handsome, are these fried noodles any good?”

The guy shot her a look like she was an idiot.

As if to say, With this many people in line, do you even need to ask? Obviously, it’s delicious!

chapters.title: Chapter 29 - We’re Buying Raw Rice Noodles to Have Boss Lu Cook Them!
chapters.content:
Chapter 29 We’re Buying Raw Rice Noodles to Have Boss Lu Cook Them!

Usually, when a streamer like ‘Panda Loves Food & Drink’ showed up at the night market, they got some attention. But today, Lu Qin’s fried noodle stall had customers who couldn’t be bothered to care. Everyone was fixated on the wok, eagerly waiting for their food.

In front of Lu Qin’s stall, those who had already gotten their noodles stayed put, eating right there, savoring the aroma that filled the air.

As a foodie herself, ‘Panda Loves Food & Drink’ couldn’t resist the mouthwatering smell and hurried to the back of the line. Her livestream viewers were buzzing with excitement.

But just a minute after the streamer joined the line, Lu Qin called out from the front.

“Sorry, the last eight customers will be served, but after that, we’re sold out for the night. Each person can only order one portion.”

A wave of disappointment rippled through the customers at the back.

“I came here full of hope, and now I didn’t make it. Damn it!”

“ Ugh , I came around this time yesterday too. Why can’t I get my turn?”

“Dude, today’s crowd is way bigger than yesterday’s. Tomorrow I’ll have to come earlier!”

“I’ve been smelling it this whole time, and now I can’t eat it. This is torture!”

“Lu Boss’s business is too good—this stuff is impossible to get at night!”

“I heard he also sets up a morning stall near Sunshine Primary School. I’m going there tomorrow morning!”

The streamer stood there in shock, mumbling to herself, “It’s only 8:30, and the fried noodles are already sold out?”

Her livestream chat was filled with schadenfreude.

Lu Qin handed over the last portion of fried noodles to a customer.

“Thanks for coming. Enjoy!”

“Thanks, Boss Lu. I’m so lucky I got the last one!”

Several customers who didn’t make the cut lingered, frustrated at missing out. Then one guy had a bright idea and blurted out excitedly.

“Boss, what if I go to another noodle stall, buy some raw noodles, and bring them here for you to fry? You can charge me the regular price.”

“Dude, you’re a genius! That’s brilliant!”

“Boss, I’ll do the same! I’ll buy noodles and you fry them for me!”

“This move is genius! I’m doing it too!”

Lu Qin chuckled, seeing the determination in these five or six customers who weren’t willing to leave without their noodles. After thinking it over, he agreed.

“I use authentic Anyang rice noodles. Other stalls may use something different. The flavor and texture might vary.”

“Are you okay with that?”

The customers hesitated for a moment but then, with a sigh of resignation, said, “Doesn’t matter, we’re starving! We trust your cooking skills, Boss. It’ll taste great either way!”

“Exactly! You’re a pro, Boss. The difference won’t be that big!”

Lu Qin added.

“Just for today, I’ll make an exception and fry one portion per person. Don’t buy too much, and come earlier tomorrow.”

“Thanks, Boss! One portion is enough.”

“Thank you, Boss Lu! I’ll definitely get here early tomorrow!”

“Boss, take your time, we’ll be back in two minutes.”

With that, the few customers hurriedly ran out of the alley.

Old Liu, who had previously set up his fried noodle stall at the alley entrance, had moved elsewhere. Ever since Lu Qin appeared, staying in the same spot meant Old Liu would be left with nothing but the cold wind. Today, he set up at the school’s side gate, far away from Lu Qin’s stall. Although business wasn’t as good as before, it was still decent.

“Master Liu, your fried noodles are pretty good! You sold quite a few tonight!” The neighboring seller of rice cakes said enviously.

Master Liu, puffed up with pride, replied, “I’ve been frying noodles for eleven years! My skills are top-notch!”

Just then, five or six customers came running up, panting.

“Boss, one serving of rice noodles!”

“Me too!”

“And for me!”

Master Liu, pretending to sigh, said, “Look, business is rolling in again. Can’t be helped.”

“Take your time, folks. No need to rush. You don’t need to be in such a hurry for my fried noodles. Do you want egg-fried or meat-fried?”

“No need to fry, we just want the raw noodles.”

“What?”

“We just want raw rice noodles. How much?”

Master Liu was dumbfounded. It was the first time someone asked to buy raw rice noodles from him.

This is a fried noodle stall—if they want raw noodles, they should go to the market.

One of the customers explained.

“Boss, just sell us the raw noodles at the same price as fried noodles. We’re taking them to Lu Boss to fry!”

“What?”

Master Liu’s nose almost flared with anger. This was downright insulting! He didn’t want to sell to them. But after hesitating for a few seconds, knowing he had to maintain good business relations, he reluctantly sold them the raw noodles. The customers, rice noodles in hand, rushed off again.

The rice cake vendor next door, seeing Master Liu’s displeased expression, wisely kept quiet.

In the alley, Lu Qin finished frying noodles for the last few customers. They all took a bite and agreed the taste was still excellent, though it lacked a little something—likely due to the different rice noodles.

“No wonder Lu Boss’s fried noodles are so delicious. It’s not just your skills but also the high-quality ingredients!”

“Well, that’s it for tonight. See you all tomorrow.”

“Lu Boss, let us help you pack up.”

The enthusiastic customers helped Lu Qin tidy up. He hopped onto his motorized food cart and disappeared into the night.

…

As Lu Qin left, Fang Ya arrived at the night market street. She had been wandering the area, looking for the highly praised fried rice noodles she’d seen on Douyin, but couldn’t find it.

While there were three or four stalls selling fried noodles, every vendor was an oily, middle-aged man. The hygiene of the stalls was appalling—each wok had at least three centimeters of burnt oil residue.

Closing her eyes, Fang Ya bought a portion from each stall. After tasting them, not one could pass a second bite, and they all ended up in the trash.

Fang Ya never liked fried noodles much anyway. After two or three bites, she found them greasy and hard to swallow.

Before leaving, she encountered a fan and, after taking a photo together, asked about the noodle stall.

“They’re sold out by 8:30 and already packed up.”

Fang Ya was speechless.

Is that noodle stall really that popular?

“I can’t believe it. I’ll be back tomorrow!”

…

Meanwhile, Shen Muyan had just returned to her villa from work when her phone rang. It was Shen Hongda calling.

“Dad, what’s this about an Ice Crystal Cake?”

“Don’t ask. Just call him now.”

“Dad, we’re divorced. How am I supposed to bring this up?”

“Figure it out. I’m still the chairman, so you follow orders. That’s it, end of discussion.”

Beep beep beep—

Shen Muyan furrowed her perfectly shaped brows, feeling utterly exasperated.

Asking Lu Qin… to make Ice Crystal Cakes? He even knew how to make that? Did he learn it in the past?

But that wasn’t the real issue—the issue was… how was she supposed to ask him?

chapters.title: Chapter 30 - If It's for Business, Then It'll Be Justified!
chapters.content:
Chapter 30 If It’s for Business, Then It’ll Be Justified!

Around 9 PM, Lu Qin rode his motorized food cart back to the neighborhood. At this time, the old district was quiet and peaceful. This area had many elderly residents who tended to go to bed early.

Two overworked young professionals, dragging their tired bodies home from overtime, passed by. Living a life where they ‘wake up earlier than the chickens and sleep later than the dogs’, they stopped at the convenience store to buy instant noodles for a late-night snack. Seeing Lu Qin return, they quickly greeted him.

“Lu Boss, you’re done for the night? Any fried noodles or mung bean smoothies left?”

Lu Qin braked and replied, “Sorry, everything’s sold out.”

“Ah, I knew it. Your fried noodles are so good, they are bound to sell out.”

“Guess it’s instant noodles for us tonight. How sad.”

“Lu Boss, you should set up your stall under our company building. I’d eat your fried noodles for all three meals, and I’d even pay for a monthly pass!”

Lu Qin chuckled.

“Thanks for the support. Stop by the stall when you get a chance, and get some rest.”

“You too, Lu Boss. Good night.”

Lu Qin unlocked the gate to his courtyard, pushed the food cart inside, and closed the door behind him. Without his daughter, Lu Xiyao, calling out Daddy in her sweet voice, he found himself missing the little one. It had only been half a day apart. He thought about video-calling her but figured it was too late and that she was probably already asleep.

By tomorrow afternoon, he’d be able to pick her up again. After a full day of work, Lu Qin didn’t feel tired. Thanks to the system’s fatigue-reducing aura, he had boundless energy.

Business at his stall was getting better by the day, and more and more customers were discovering his fried noodles. This was a good thing.

Today, he sold over 550 portions of fried noodles, 600 cups of mung bean smoothies, and around 240 boxes of Ice Crystal Cakes. After deducting costs, he still made a comfortable profit of over ten thousand yuan. The total revenue for the day was 13,288 yuan, and he accumulated 1,433 system points.

Lu Qin didn’t rush to use the points. Instead, he focused on cleaning up his food cart, washing the plastic baskets, condiment containers, and other items.

There was a washing sink in the courtyard, making cleanup easy. A bit of the system’s powerful detergent effortlessly dissolved the grease, and with a strong cloth, everything was spotless in no time.

Lu Qin was efficient, finishing the cleaning in just over ten minutes. Next, he began preparing ingredients for tomorrow’s morning stall. He had already established a long-term partnership with a local rice noodle vendor and Zhao, the owner of a disposable tableware store.

As his stall’s sales grew steadily, the demand for fresh rice noodles and takeaway boxes also increased. Lu Qin arranged with these suppliers to have a batch of noodles and containers delivered to him every afternoon.

Zhao had a small delivery truck, so Lu Qin also had him bring items like ham, lean meat, vegetables, mung beans, and glutinous rice, saving him the time of going to the market daily.

After preparing some ingredients and condiments, Lu Qin was about to take a shower when his phone rang. To his surprise, it was Shen Muyan calling.

…

On Shen Muyan’s side, she had just finished her work at the company and missed dinner. She called Fang Ya, hoping to meet up for food.

Fang Ya was always good at picking out gourmet spots, and eating with her would give her a better appetite. But Fang Ya was at the University City night market tonight and wasn’t available.

The weekend buffet at the Ourei Hotel on the 6th floor was open to the public to attract more foot traffic and provide a bit of publicity. Initially, promotional deals had drawn in many customers, but people mostly came out of curiosity and rarely returned for a second visit. As a result, the buffet wasn’t as popular anymore.

It was the usual fare—seafood, steak, barbecue, and free-flowing drinks. Having grown up with access to such foods, Shen Muyan had long lost interest in them.

She had someone from the hotel’s kitchen pack her a ham and cheese croissant, then drove back to her villa. Throughout the drive, she kept thinking about what Shen Hongda had asked of her.

How was she supposed to bring this up with Lu Qin?

Before the divorce, Shen Muyan had never asked Lu Qin for anything, let alone now that they were separated. She parked her car in the garage, sat in her sports car for a moment, and then, after some thought, dialed his number. There was no other excuse but to use their child as a pretext for this matter.

“Hello.”

On the other end of the line, Lu Qin’s calm and gentle voice sounded the same as before.

“Hey, Lu Qin, are you asleep?”

“Not yet. I was just about to take a shower. How’s Yao Yao? Is she asleep?”

“Yeah, she is. My parents took her to the amusement park today, so she was exhausted and fell asleep not long after coming back.”

“Your parents are very kind to do that.”

After their divorce, their daughter spent weekends with her mother, but since Shen Muyan was always busy with work, it was her parents who spent time with the child.

As a mother, Shen Muyan often felt guilty about this, and she acknowledged it. She always wanted to make up for it but never seemed to get the chance. Yet, Lu Qin never once voiced any complaint or dissatisfaction about the situation. He was such a good man.

After a few moments of hesitation, Shen Muyan finally spoke up.

“The Ice Crystal Cakes you sent with the little one today—did you make those yourself?”

“Yeah. Did you like them? How about your parents?”

“Uh…”
