In fact, she hadn’t even tasted them. She assumed they were store-bought and hadn’t given them a second glance.

“They were delicious. My parents loved them too. You’re quite skilled.”

“Good to hear. I’m glad they liked them.”

“Uh… the little one keeps asking for more. Do you think you could make five or six more tomorrow? I’ll send someone to pick them up.”

Lu Qin refused gently.

“You can’t spoil her too much. She loves sweets, and whether it’s five or six boxes, she’ll gobble them all up. I’ll let her indulge a little when I bring her back tomorrow.”

Shen Muyan’s parents doted on Lu Xiyao endlessly, and with just a bit of pleading from the little one, they’d let her eat anything she wanted.

“She probably had ice cream at the amusement park yesterday, right? Could you remind your parents tomorrow to limit the sweets for her? Too much modern ice cream is full of additives and too sweet for young kids.”

“Got it.”

Shen Muyan felt awkward. She knew nothing about parenting. But what about the Ice Crystal Cakes? How should she bring it up?

An idea came to her, and her delicate face flushed with embarrassment.

Lu Qin interrupted her thoughts.

“If that’s all, I’m going to hang up and get some sleep.”

“Wait… hang on.”

“Is there something else?”

“Actually… um… the Ice Crystal Cakes… I’m the one who wants them. Could you make some for me?”

By the time she finished the sentence, her voice was as quiet as a whisper. It was utterly embarrassing. Shen Muyan couldn’t believe she was saying this.

“Of course, I’ll bring them over at 9 a.m. tomorrow.”

Lu Qin agreed readily.

“Thanks.”

Shen Muyan’s composure gradually returned.

“How many boxes?”

“Hmm… six.”

“One box is 25 yuan, so six boxes will be 150. Just send it over via WeChat.”

Huh?

He wanted her to pay?

She was stunned, holding her phone with a complex expression.

Lu Qin clarified.

“Don’t take it the wrong way. It’s not about being stingy. We’re divorced, and if I keep giving you things for free, people might misunderstand. But if it’s business, then everything’s above board.”

Shen Muyan was speechless. There were countless men who would willingly give her anything for free. Yet here was Lu Qin, hesitating over a few boxes of food.

This man!

“Fine, I’ll send you the money. Will you need a delivery fee for bringing it to the hotel?”

With Lu Qin’s attitude, she figured there was no point sending a driver, considering the cost of gas.

“No need. I’ll drop it off at the hotel’s front desk after I finish my morning shift at the stall.”

“Alright.”

Despite herself, Shen Muyan felt a bit annoyed. She wanted to transfer the money right away but then reconsidered.

Why pay before she even received the cakes?

She’d wait until tomorrow.

chapters.title: Chapter 31 - Mr. Rossi's Taste Buds Are Very Discerning!
chapters.content:
Chapter 31 Mr. Rossi’s Taste Buds Are Very Discerning!

On the phone, Lu Qin mentioned street vending of his own accord.

Shen Muyan pretended to ask, “Morning stall?”

Lu Qin explained, “I’m currently working as a street vendor selling snacks, like ice crystal cake.”

“Why don’t you work at the company? You graduated from Qingbei, your major is strong, so you shouldn’t have trouble finding a job.”

“Just because it’s a major doesn’t mean it’s a passion. Street vending is pretty good, the hours are flexible, making it easier to take care of the little one.”

“As long as you’re happy.”

Shen Muyan felt a bit annoyed hearing Lu Qin talk so casually about vending as if it were a legitimate job. Even if he enjoyed it, he should consider reality.

The income from vending was unstable. After the divorce, with no house and no car, how could he support his daughter?

Shen Muyan found it hard to understand Lu Qin, believing he lacked ambition. But now that they were divorced, she couldn’t say much. It was probably for the best, if he couldn’t support the little one, she could reclaim custody later.

“Let’s leave it at that. Don’t forget to bring the things tomorrow. Goodbye.”

Shen Muyan hung up the phone, feeling gloomy.

…

After showering, Lu Qin lay on the bed, charging his phone on the bedside table.

He closed his eyes and opened the system.

With over 1400 points, he could draw 14 times.

“Draw.”

【Consuming 100 points, draw awarded 20 mint candies】

【Consuming 100 points, draw awarded insect repellent】

【Consuming 100 points, draw awarded “Fluent in Italian”】

【Consuming 100 points, draw awarded “Fluent in German”】

…

…

【Consuming 100 points, draw awarded “Max Level Twice-Cooked Pork Skills”】

【Consuming 100 points, draw awarded one freshness-locking temperature control box】

…

After 14 draws from the system, the rewards were plentiful. As knowledge and skills were awarded, a wealth of experience flowed into Lu Qin’s mind, nerves, and muscles, all seamlessly integrating.

The system’s insect repellent was impressive, designed specifically for street vending. Just place it inside the food cart, and no flying or crawling insects would come near, regardless of the environment. Moreover, the insect repellent had a faint scent, unless one had a keen sense of smell, most people wouldn’t notice it. This was a great find.

Lu Qin’s eyes lit up. But even better items were yet to come. That was the ‘freshness-locking temperature control box’.

With a thought, a sturdy 120L plastic box with a light green shell appeared on the floor of his room.

Lu Qin got up from the bed and studied the box closely. From its appearance, it resembled a typical outdoor thermal box. However, this system box didn’t just keep items warm. The most crucial function was to lock in freshness.

According to the system’s explanation, using overnight food as an example, if one placed the meal in this box and locked it, the next day when opened, the food would show no signs of spoilage at all. The taste and temperature would be exactly the same as when it was put in the night before. It was truly magical.

If a dish were placed inside, it could guarantee the original flavor within seven or eight hours. As for preserving fruits and vegetables, that was even less of a concern, it felt somewhat like overkill. After all, fruits and vegetables could be preserved in a refrigerator too.

This ‘freshness-locking temperature control box’ had a built-in battery that could last three days on a full charge. Of course, the items drawn from the system wouldn’t be discovered by others.

For instance, if someone else used this box, it would appear just like an ordinary thermal box. Through this draw, Lu Qin even mastered two foreign languages.

One never knew, if foreign customers visited his snack stall, those languages might come in handy. Fluent in Italian meant he had reached a C2 level, equivalent to a native speaker, and he could work as a translator without any issues. German was the same.

Among the fourteen draws, the best reward was the ‘Max Level Twice-Cooked Pork Skills’. Now Lu Qin could make the most delicious and authentic twice-cooked pork.

…

The next morning, Lu Qin woke up at six o’clock. He didn’t need to go to the market to purchase ingredients today, as he had arranged for Boss Zhao to deliver a batch of ingredients and packaging boxes yesterday afternoon.

Lu Qin soaked the rice noodles and placed them in a strainer to dry naturally. He then began to cook green bean paste and prepare the ice crystal cake. Twice-cooked pork wasn’t suitable for sale in the morning, so he didn’t prepare the corresponding ingredients.

After more than an hour, Lu Qin loaded the items for his stall into the cart. He rode out to set up the morning stall at the entrance of Sunshine Primary School.

It was Sunday, so there were no parents dropping off students. Residents in the nearby community, who could have slept in, had already started coming downstairs.

Before Lu Qin’s food cart arrived, several people were already lingering around the stall area. It was obvious they were waiting for him.

Beep beep —

The horn honked twice, and people quickly made their way. Seeing the familiar three-wheeled food cart they had been eagerly anticipating, a group of people’s eyes lit up.

“Boss Lu, you finally made it!”

“I came first, I want three portions of stir-fried noodles, three cups of green bean paste, and two boxes of ice crystal cake.”

“Boss Lu, your ice crystal cake is too delicious, my daughter ate a box yesterday and didn’t have enough. She cried all night asking for more!”

“Boss Lu, I gave the ice crystal cake to the girl I secretly like, and she asked me out!”

“Yesterday I drank eight cups of green bean paste, my belly almost burst. I’m scared to drink it today, I’ll just have the stir-fried noodles.”

…

In an hour and a half, Lu Qin had sold nearly 200 portions of stir-fried noodles. The 250 cups of green bean paste also cleared out without a hitch. Of the 100 boxes of ice crystal cake, Lu Qin saved six boxes, and the rest were sold out.

After the morning stall, Lu Qin rode his cart to the Ourei International Hotel. The Ourei Hotel in the Annan District is the largest among the six chain hotels in Jiangcheng, standing 42 stories tall. The hotel boasts 588 luxury suites, 18 dining venues, and over 1,000 dining seats, offering guests a variety of distinctive Chinese and Western dishes.

Lu Qin had hardly visited the hotel before, and the staff didn’t recognize him. At the hotel’s guard booth, there was a barrier that only recognized cars.

Lu Qin parked his cart at a roadside designated parking spot, then carried the ice crystal cake inside. Two doormen at the hotel saw Lu Qin parked on the roadside but still greeted him with smiles, displaying high quality of service.

“Sir, how may we assist you?”

“Oh, hello, I’d like to go to the front desk.”

“Sure, this way please.”

The doorman led Lu Qin inside, assuming he was a food delivery person because he was carrying items. However, guests staying at this hotel rarely order takeout, as the hotel provides various specialty dining options.

The doorman was puzzled as he brought Lu Qin to the front desk. At the front desk, three attractive ladies dressed in professional suits were present, the one in the middle stood up.

With a sweet smile, she politely asked, “Sir, how can we assist you?”

“I’m here to deliver takeout,” Lu Qin said directly.

“Okay, may I ask which room the guest is in?”

The front desk lady maintained her composure, but inside she shared the doorman’s confusion.

“I’m looking for…”

Before Lu Qin could finish his sentence, a foreign man in his thirties appeared next to him, followed by an assistant.

The hotel’s VIP guest manager hurried behind him, apologizing repeatedly.

“Mr. Rossi, you originally planned to stay for seven days, it’s only the third day, and you’re leaving. Is there something about our hotel service that you’re dissatisfied with?”

Guests have the freedom to come and go as they please, but the manager still wanted to understand the reason, especially since Mr. Rossi was a VIP guest. It was unusual for him to leave abruptly, and they wanted to clarify for reporting purposes.

“I’m very satisfied with your service, but the food at your hotel is really inedible!”

Rossi explained in his native language.

Lu Qin understood nearby and realized that this foreign guest was Italian. The manager only spoke English and didn’t understand, so he turned to the assistant beside him.

The assistant translated, and the manager expressed his deep apologies again.

Rossi said with frustration, “Jiangcheng may be bustling, but when it comes to food, I’m truly disappointed.”

The assistant helplessly translated again and then whispered in Chinese to the manager.

“Our Mr. Rossi’s taste buds are very discerning, this is the third five-star hotel he’s tried in Jiangcheng.”

chapters.title: Chapter 32 - How Could He Make Something So Delicious?
chapters.content:
Chapter 32 How Could He Make Something So Delicious?

The hotel’s room manager could only smile wryly at this. Ourei Hotel had a decent reputation for its dining options in Jiangcheng. They invested heavily, hiring top chefs for their restaurants like Tianbao Pavilion, Frasca Café, and Four Seasons House, even employing famous chefs. However, as people’s living standards improved, their culinary expectations also rose. It was a reality they had to face.

It was fortunate that Mr. Rossi hadn’t complained about Ourei’s dining, picky guests like him appeared several times a month. Among them, VIP guests were often the most critical. The wealthy tend to have more refined palates after experiencing a variety of gourmet cuisines.

Once the VIP room manager understood the situation, he ceased further comments. As Rossi pulled out his room card, he accidentally brushed against the ice crystal cake that Lu Qin was holding.

The thin plastic bag tore, and several boxes of ice crystal cake fell to the floor.

“I’m so sorry, sir! I accidentally damaged your items, I can compensate you.”

Rossi, demonstrating good manners, quickly apologized to Lu Qin and bent down to pick up the fallen items. His assistant, quicker on the draw, bent down first to gather them. He handed a few boxes of ice crystal cake to Lu Qin, noticing that one box had a small dent, but the cake inside remained clean.

“It’s fine, nothing’s broken, so no need for compensation.” Lu Qin replied in fluent Italian.

Rossi and his assistant were taken aback, surprised that this handsome young man spoke Italian so well. The manager and the three front desk staff, along with the doorman, all looked curiously at Lu Qin.

Rossi apologized again, “Sir, your Italian is excellent! I truly apologize, I see one box is damaged. How much do I owe you for it?”

“It’s okay, nothing inside is ruined.”

“That’s not acceptable…”

Lu Qin sighed, realizing that Rossi was quite stubborn about compensation. Reluctantly, Lu Qin sold the box of damaged ice crystal cake to him.

The assistant took out 50 yuan in cash, but Lu Qin couldn’t provide change.

“No need for change.”

Rossi’s gaze lingered on the ice crystal cake. He asked, “Sir, what is this?”

“Ice crystal cake, it’s a type of Chinese pastry made from rice flour,” Lu Qin explained in Italian.

“It smells amazing.”

Rossi picked up a piece and brought it to his nose, inhaling the fragrant aroma. The cake looked delicate and tasted delightful, prompting Rossi to take a bite. His assistant attempted to stop him, but it was too late.

How could something that looked so street-vendor-esque satisfy his boss’s discerning palate?

Yet, after taking a bite, Rossi’s expression changed to one of astonishment and disbelief.

“È delizioso!”

“Sir, how can your ice crystal cake taste this good?”

Rossi gave a thumbs up, finishing the remaining half of the cake in his mouth. He savored each bite, allowing the exquisite flavors to dance on his taste buds.

The manager’s eyes widened, the ice crystal cake, with such simple packaging, looked like something sold on the streets.

Could it really be that delicious?

To everyone’s surprise, Rossi ate several pieces, finishing an entire box of ice crystal cake. He nearly licked the box clean.

The assistant looked at his boss with a strange expression, wondering if the boss’s reaction was a bit exaggerated for a pastry one could find on the street.

Rossi pointed at the ice crystal cake in Lu Qin’s hands, asking,

“Sir, could I also buy the other ice crystal cakes? They’re truly amazing! I’ve never had such a delicious dessert before.”

Lu Qin shook his head, smiling, “I’m sorry, these boxes are for someone else.”

Rossi displayed a look of disappointment but quickly asked, “Sir, what’s your name?”

“My name is Lu Qin.”

“Mr. Lu, please tell me where I can buy this ice crystal cake. I really want some.”

“I made it myself.”

Rossi was both surprised and delighted.

“That’s fantastic! I want to buy some ice crystal cakes!”

“They’re sold out for the morning. If you want to buy some, I’ll be setting up a stall at People’s Square Walking Street in the afternoon, and I’ll also go to the university city night market in the evening. You’re welcome to visit.”

“Oh! Oh! Great!”

Rossi quickly urged his assistant to note down the address.

At this moment, Lu Qin turned to the front desk staff, saying,

“Hello, these boxes are for President Shen. I’ll leave them here, please let her know.”

Lu Qin then turned and left the hotel.

The front desk staff was stunned for a few seconds before reacting.

“Wait… President Shen? Could it be our president?”

The front desk was shocked, as were the doormen and the manager. Rossi turned to find that Lu Qin had already left and slapped his forehead.

“Darn it, I forgot to ask Mr. Lu for his contact information.”

…

On the top floor of Ourei Hotel, in the president’s office, the VIP room manager personally delivered the boxes of ice crystal cake to Shen Muyan’s office.

Shen Muyan asked why the ice crystal cake was there. The manager recounted the incident at the front desk from beginning to end.

Shen Muyan raised her clear eyes slightly.

He can even speak Italian?

Did he learn it at home after marrying her?

Shen Muyan glanced at the boxes of ice crystal cake.

With such unremarkable packaging, the contents had somehow impressed the picky Mr. Rossi. Shen Muyan recalled how Chairman Shen had ordered her to get an ice crystal cake from Lu Qin. This indicated that it was truly delicious.

Shen Muyan had someone take four boxes of ice crystal cake to Shen Hongda, keeping one box for herself.

In the president’s office, Song Xuanxuan looked like a child caught misbehaving, her head lowered.

She protested, “President Shen, you can’t blame me! You said yesterday you wouldn’t eat it…”

Although Song Xuanxuan did have the crime of not reporting the situation, she had cleverly claimed the box of ice crystal cake that belonged to Shen Muyan.

Later, when Chairman Shen arrived, the little one forgot to take her ice crystal cake, which worked out well for her. Song Xuanxuan had devoured it all. It was like a windfall from the heavens.

Shen Muyan shot a cold glare at her.

“Get back to work.”

Song Xuanxuan returned to her desk, pouting. Wasn’t it unfair that she couldn’t even see it? She wanted to eat it too.

Shen Muyan opened the box, seeing the crystal-clear ice crystal cake, its appearance quite appealing. She picked up a piece, her lips slightly parted, and took a bite. The soft and sweet taste instantly blossomed on her tongue, an indescribably delightful flavor. It was truly delicious!

Shen Muyan’s beautiful eyes sparkled. The cool, sweet sensation brightened her mood.

How could he make something so delicious?

Where did he learn this skill?

When did he learn it?

Shen Muyan’s curiosity piqued.

Finishing one piece, she couldn’t resist reaching for a second. Just then, the phone rang.

chapters.title: Chapter 33 - When Did You And Lu Qin Get Back together?
chapters.content:
Chapter 33 When Did You And Lu Qin Get Back together?

Shen Muyan glanced at the caller ID. It was Fang Ya. She answered the call.

“My dear President Shen, do you have time tonight? Come with me to try some really delicious fried rice noodles.”

Fried rice noodles?

Shen Muyan had no interest in such greasy food, so she flatly refused.

“Not going.”

“Come on, it’ll be boring by myself,” Fang Ya said.

Fang Ya often had free time and frequently asked Shen Muyan to accompany her shopping, for beauty treatments, or to work out.

Shen Muyan mostly declined, she didn’t have that much free time.

“Do I look like someone who’s that bored?” Shen Muyan asked.

“I just don’t want you to be too busy to eat! I’m bringing you to something good.” Fang Ya said.

“I’m already eating.”

“What are you eating?”

“Ice cake.”

“Ice cake? What is that?” Fang Ya asked.

“Lu Qin made it.”

Fang Ya got confused.

“Lu Qin made it?”

“Mm-hmm,” Shen Muyan said.

“Is it good?”

“Not telling you.”

Shen Muyan was in a great mood, chatting on the phone while eating her third piece of ice cake.

“Wait a minute, Shen Muyan! I feel like I missed an important detail…” Fang Ya said.

“When did you and Lu Qin get back together?”

Back together?

Get together?

Shen Muyan almost choked on her ice cake.

This Fang Ya—could she possibly use more outrageous words?

Shen Muyan wasn’t the type to explain things in detail, but to prevent Fang Ya from gossiping further, she briefly explained the situation.

On the other end, Fang Ya’s facial expressions were probably quite animated, but Shen Muyan couldn’t see them.

“Wow! Lu Qin speaks Italian too?”

“Is the ice cake really that good?”

“Even Uncle Shen and Aunt Jiang like it?”

“So, Lu Qin is like a hidden treasure boy!”

“Shen Muyan! Where did you get the nerve to divorce Lu Qin?”

…

Fang Ya kept rambling on, but Shen Muyan, having anticipated this, turned down the volume and set the phone aside.

She continued eating her ice cake. The cake really was delicious. Just this one dessert was enough to surpass all of the desserts made by the pastry chefs at her hotel.

Shen Muyan was confident that none of the desserts her pastry chefs made could beat this ice cake. She could still vaguely hear Fang Ya’s voice through the phone.

“Let me try the ice cake,” Fang Ya said.

For the first time, Shen Muyan felt stingy and said, “No.”

Then she hung up.

Fang Ya, after being hung up on, didn’t have time to complain. She got straight into her car and drove to the hotel at top speed.

Fang Ya stormed into Shen Muyan’s office. Shen Muyan had already taken a bite out of the last piece of ice cake.

“Show some mercy.”

Fang Ya rushed over and snatched the half-eaten piece of ice cake right from her mouth. The ice cake got squished out of shape.

Shen Muyan, naturally, didn’t try to snatch it back. She was amazed by Fang Ya’s speed. Did this woman fly here just for a bite of food?

Fang Ya shoved the half-eaten ice cake into her mouth, and a few seconds later, she burst out in amazement.

“This is so good!”

“It’s absolutely divine!”

Fang Ya shook Shen Muyan’s shoulders excitedly.

“Oh my God, Shen Muyan, your husband’s ice cake is freaking amazing! So good!”

Fang Ya couldn’t help but curse out of excitement.

Husband?

Shen Muyan was stunned for a moment. That title felt unfamiliar. In the six years they were married, she had never called Lu Qin her husband. Now that they were divorced, it was even less likely.

“Do you have any more ice cake?”

“Nope.”

“Call Lu Qin and ask him to make more!”

“Not calling.”

Fang Ya grew desperate, trying to persuade and beg Shen Muyan to call Lu Qin, but she remained unmoved and got back to work, ignoring Fang Ya.

Fang Ya was about to explode in frustration. She didn’t have Lu Qin’s number, and Shen Muyan wouldn’t give it to her either. Fang Ya felt like cutting ties with this heartless woman.

…

Meanwhile, at the villa, Jiang Qin had taken Lu Qin and Shen Muyan’s daughter, Lu Xiyao, to the park. Shen Hongda had already received the four boxes of ice cake Shen Muyan had sent over.

In the living room, Chen Dahai had been waiting eagerly.

“Wow, Old Shen, you weren’t lying.”

Shen Hongda smiled proudly.

“Of course, I always keep my word.”

If Lu Qin and Shen Muyan hadn’t gotten divorced, he would’ve shown off Lu Qin to Chen Dahai, bragging about how this ice cake was made by his son-in-law.

What a shame…

“Thanks for this. I’ll take the ice cake, and don’t worry, I’ll get that thing you asked for sorted out. I always keep my word too.”

Chen Dahai reached for the ice cake, stacking the four boxes neatly as he prepared to take them all.

Shen Hongda widened his eyes in disbelief.

“Old Chen, you can only take two boxes! Why are you taking them all? Where’s your shame?”

Shen Hongda quickly walked over and snatched two boxes back. He wasn’t willing to give all the ice cake to Chen Dahai, especially since it took some effort to get them from Lu Qin. Asking for them once was okay, but asking again would be much harder.

“Old Shen, why are you being so stingy? Where did you get this ice cake? Come on, give me a hint.”

Chen Dahai wasn’t inexperienced. He could tell this kind of ice cake wasn’t something just anyone could make. It was probably a special favor Shen Hongda had called in.

Definitely not something you could buy easily.

“Even if I told you, you wouldn’t be able to get it.”

Shen Hongda said, acting all mysterious.

After Chen Dahai left, Jiang Qin returned with Lu Xiyao.

The little girl spotted the two boxes of ice cake on the table, her face lighting up with excitement.

“Grandpa, is that ice cake?”

“Is Daddy here?”

Shen Hongda picked Lu Xiyao up and kissed her cheek, smiling.

“Your dad didn’t come, but he sent over the ice cake he made.”

“Yay! Grandpa, Grandma, let’s eat the ice cake together.”

Lu Xiyao opened the plastic box and divided the ice cake between Shen Hongda and Jiang Qin. The three of them happily ate the ice cake while chatting.

From Lu Xiyao’s stories, Shen Hongda and Jiang Qin learned that Lu Qin was actually selling the ice cake at a stall. Both of them were shocked.

“What? Is making this ice cake really that easy?”

Shen Hongda had thought that with how delicious the ice cake was, the process must be complex and difficult. If Lu Qin was selling it at a stall, he must be making it in large quantities.

Lu Xiyao shook her head.

“I don’t know. I didn’t see Daddy make the ice cake. I was still asleep.”

“Daddy works so hard. He gets up very early to make it.”

As she spoke, Lu Xiyao felt a few seconds of sympathy for Lu Qin before taking another bite of her ice cake.

“It’s so good! Soft and sweet!”

Shen Hongda sighed inwardly and asked again.

“Lu Xiyao, this ice cake is so delicious. Your dad’s stall must be doing really well, right?”

Lu Xiyao nodded enthusiastically.

“Very well! Lots and lots of people—big brothers, big sisters, grandpas, and grandmas—all line up to buy the things Daddy makes!”

Hearing this, Shen Hongda felt a bit more relieved.

It seemed like Lu Qin’s ice cake was selling quite well.

chapters.title: Chapter 34 - Delicious Twice-Cooked Pork! Food Stall at Huaguo Mountain Park!
chapters.content:
Chapter 34 Delicious Twice-Cooked Pork! Food Stall at Huaguo Mountain Park!

At the Ourei Hotel, in the president’s office, Fang Ya sat half-perched on the edge of Shen Muyan’s desk.

“Shen Muyan, where is Lu Qin setting up his food stall?”

“I don’t know.”

“Can’t you ask him? I want to eat that ice cake!”

“If you want to ask, ask yourself,” Shen Muyan replied while flipping through some documents.

Fang Ya clenched her fists, almost ready to hit someone.

Actually, Shen Muyan also wanted more of Lu Qin’s ice cake. But she was too embarrassed to ask him again.

…

After leaving the Ourei Hotel, Lu Qin rode his tricycle to the market. By this time, the rush hour for grocery shopping had passed, so the market wasn’t crowded.

Lu Qin found a pork stall and checked the color of the pork—it was freshly butchered today. Fresh pork is bright or dark red, while chilled pork has a pinkish hue.

“Boss, how much is your pork per pound?”

“19.5 yuan per pound, but if you buy more than three pounds, I’ll cut it down to 19.”

Lu Qin chuckled, guessing the pork seller was quoting high because he saw Lu Qin was a young guy. In reality, Lu Qin knew market prices better than the elderly who frequented the market.

“Too expensive, I’ll pass. I’ll check somewhere else.”

Lu Qin turned to leave.

“19.5 is already cheap!” The vendor exclaimed.

“I’ll buy from the stall next door.”

“Wait, wait, young man, how many pounds do you want?”

Seeing Lu Qin head towards the neighboring stall, the vendor quickly called him back.

“Twenty pounds.”

The vendor was stunned, then quickly put on a smile.

“Alright, alright, handsome, if you’re buying twenty pounds, I’ll sell it to you for 18.5 per pound, that’s the lowest price.”

“16.8 per pound. If you can’t do that, I’ll buy next door.”

The current market price for ordinary pork was around 17 yuan per pound, and since Lu Qin was buying twenty pounds, bargaining for two cents less per pound was reasonable.

If it had been one of those relentless bargaining aunties, she’d pull up a stool and haggle for half an hour, forcing you to sell it for 16.5.

Realizing Lu Qin wasn’t a novice and wasn’t budging on price, the vendor reluctantly agreed.

“Alright, alright, 16.8 it is. But come back next time, okay? Which part of the pork do you want?”

Lu Qin needed the pork to make twice-cooked pork, so selecting the right cuts was essential.

Four parts fatty hind leg, two cuts lean, six parts wide, and three fingers thick. Too fatty and it was greasy, too lean and it was dry, too wide or too narrow and it wouldn’t cook properly.

After buying the pork, Lu Qin left the market and returned home to prepare the ingredients for his afternoon food stall. He added a line to the small whiteboard he used at his stall.

Typically, twice-cooked pork was made to order in restaurants, so selling it at a food stall could be tricky. But Lu Qin had a special fresh-keeping thermal box he had won from a system lottery. He could blanch the pork, slice it, and store it in the thermal box until it was time to stir-fry.

Why not just stir-fry the pork in advance and store it in the thermal box, selling it as a ready-to-eat dish?

Well, that wouldn’t work. Anyone with some cooking knowledge would know that stir-fried dishes lose flavor and texture after sitting for a while.

Besides, Lu Qin’s twice-cooked pork was extraordinary. If it could maintain its perfection after being pre-cooked, it wouldn’t make sense. Modern technology hadn’t yet invented a magical box that could pull that off.

After spending two hours, Lu Qin finished preparing the mung bean smoothies and ice cakes he planned to sell in the afternoon.

300 cups of mung bean smoothie, 150 boxes of ice cake. The mung bean smoothies were put in the fridge to chill, while the ice cakes were placed in a cool area.

Lu Qin then started preparing the ingredients for his twice-cooked pork. He put the pork he had bought into cold water, adding ginger slices, green onions, peppercorns, and cooking wine. After cooking the pork, he quickly transferred it into ice water, which made it easier to slice.

With his mastery of cooking skills, Lu Qin’s knife work was impeccable. He sliced the pork into pieces as thin as coins. Next, he prepared a large stainless steel basin, laid a layer of lettuce at the bottom, and placed the pork slices on top—enough for about 80 servings of twice-cooked pork. He also chopped onions and garlic sprouts, which are essential side ingredients for the dish.

By the time he finished, it was already 12:30, and Lu Qin was feeling hungry. He cooked some rice—keeping it simple since it was just him—and stir-fried a portion of twice-cooked pork. As soon as the dish came off the stove, a rich aroma filled the air. The pork looked golden and incredibly appetizing.

Lu Qin took a bite, savoring the tender yet crispy texture. The flavor was rich but not greasy—perfect in every way. There wasn’t a single flaw in the dish, a testament to his top-tier cooking skills.

Having worked all morning and now eating such a delicious dish, Lu Qin ended up finishing two and a half bowls of rice, feeling completely full.

Although he wanted to take a nap, he had eaten too much and couldn’t rest comfortably. Since he wasn’t one to laze around, he decided to head out to set up his food stall. The sooner he sold out, the sooner he could pick up Lu Xiyao from school.

Lu Qin checked the stall map app on his phone. At this hour, the foot traffic at People’s Square wasn’t at its peak. According to the app, the busiest spot was Huaguo Mountain Park on the west side of town. It was Sunday, and there were plenty of people strolling through the park. There was a designated spot for stalls at the foot of the mountain, but business there was usually slow since most people were there for exercise, sightseeing, or hiking, not to buy food.

Selling water might do well in that area, but there weren’t many snack vendors. For Lu Qin, though, this wasn’t an issue. Having never been to Huaguo Mountain Park, Lu Qin opened up his GPS and set off.

After about 20 minutes on his tricycle, he arrived. Sure enough, at the foot of the mountain, there was a vendor selling grilled sausages, and the rest were mostly stalls selling water, drinks, and balloons or toys.

The park had a bit of altitude, and climbing up and down required quite a bit of energy. People were sweating, so drinks were in high demand. Lu Qin’s fried noodle stall, however, stood out awkwardly, looking out of place amidst the serene and green surroundings.

Most visitors were there for fitness, so the idea of eating stir-fried noodles didn’t really appeal to them. Some passersby, curious about what Lu Qin was selling, came closer but lost interest when they found out it was fried noodles and other snacks. It would definitely seem odd to carry around a box of fried noodles while hiking through the park.

The two women selling water at the neighboring stalls quietly laughed at Lu Qin’s situation. Seeing his brand-new food cart, they kindly gave him some advice.

“Young man, is this your first time setting up a stall?”

“There’s no way you’ll get much business selling food in a place like this.”

“You should try a busier part of town.”

Lu Qin smiled and responded, playing along with their comments.

“You’re right. I don’t have much experience. I’ll choose a better spot next time.”

As expected, there wasn’t much business at first. Some tourists and parkgoers bought water or drinks from the women next door, but no one came to Lu Qin’s stall.

Lu Qin did have chilled mung bean smoothies, but since customers were already heading to the neighboring stalls for drinks, he wasn’t going to start shouting out to steal their business. That just wouldn’t feel right.

At one point, a couple leisurely strolled by. They didn’t buy anything but stood near Lu Qin’s stall, admiring the view of the lake.

“Babe, I want some bubble tea,” the woman said.

“Why didn’t you say so earlier? There’s no bubble tea shop in a place like this,” the man replied.

“Do you want bottled water or a drink?”

“No, I only want bubble tea.”

“Such a beautiful view, and no bubble tea. What a shame.”

The man was at a loss. There was no bubble tea shop anywhere nearby.

At that moment, Lu Qin turned to them with a smile and said, “How about trying some chilled mung bean smoothies? They’re just as refreshing as bubble tea.”

chapters.title: Chapter 35 - Discovered Lu Qin’s Stall, Hurry Over!
chapters.content:
Chapter 35 Discovered Lu Qin’s Stall, Hurry Over!

Chilled mung bean smoothies?

The man’s eyes lit up.

“Honey, how about a chilled mung bean smoothie?”

“I don’t really like mung bean smoothies… Can’t you just get me some bubble tea?” The woman pouted.

The man really didn’t want to go. The bubble tea shop was just too far.

“The stall owner said his mung bean smoothie is better than bubble tea. Let’s just give it a try.”

“A mung bean smoothie better than bubble tea? You actually believe that? It’s just a sales pitch.”

Lu Qin, still smiling, chimed in, “I’m not lying. My mung bean smoothies really are better than bubble tea. That’s what everyone says.”

To be fair, other customers had said that before, so Lu Qin wasn’t making it up.

Hearing Lu Qin’s confident tone, the man grew curious and decided to buy a cup to try.

He joked, “Boss, you say it’s better than bubble tea. If it’s not, can we skip paying?”

“Sure, if you don’t like it, you don’t have to pay.”

Hearing that, the woman, now intrigued, came closer.

“Boss, you sure talk big!” She teased.

At that moment, she got a good look at Lu Qin’s face and thought he was quite handsome. But still, just because he was handsome didn’t mean he could make outrageous claims.

Could mung bean smoothies really be better than bubble tea? The couple focused their attention on the cup of mung bean smoothie in their hands. The man inserted the straw and took a sip first. He had mung bean smoothies plenty of times before, so how could this possibly compare to bubble tea?

He figured he might really end up not paying. But then the smooth, rich texture hit his taste buds, and he was instantly captivated.

“Wow, honey, you have to try this! It’s amazing.”

Curious, the woman took a sip, and the refreshing, soft mung bean flavor slid down her throat, melting in her stomach. Her eyes lit up.

“Boss, your mung bean smoothie is totally different from others! It’s so good, better than bubble tea!”

She quickly took two big sips, and before they knew it, the cup was two-thirds empty. The man immediately ordered two more cups.

Lu Qin took the opportunity to upsell.

“How about trying some ice cakes? The perfect combo with mung bean smoothies, especially in the summer.”

“Ice cakes? What are those?” The woman asked.

Lu Qin explained, “They’re a traditional Chinese dessert made from glutinous rice and japonica rice flour. The outside has an icy skin with a hint of jasmine and marigold, giving it a floral fragrance. It’s really delicious.”

Since they were already impressed with the mung bean smoothies, the couple didn’t hesitate and bought two boxes of ice cake.

“Boss, the ice cakes are limited too?” The man asked.

Lu Qin smiled.

“Some customers are pretty intense and might buy ten boxes at a time. I have to limit it so others can get a chance to try it too.”

The man thought Lu Qin was joking. “Buying ten boxes? That’s a bit of an exaggeration, isn’t it, Boss?”

Lu Qin didn’t explain further, simply handing over the two boxes of ice cakes. The man scanned the QR code and paid. WeChat notified Lu Qin that 57 yuan had been received.

The man was a little surprised. At 25 yuan per box, it was pricier than the usual street snacks like rice cakes or mung bean cakes, which typically go for 10 or 8 yuan a box. But he didn’t say anything—young people these days were willing to spend more. After all, they wouldn’t hesitate to buy a bubble tea for over 20 yuan.

The woman opened one of the ice cake boxes and was immediately drawn to the intricate, beautiful presentation.

“This ice cake is so pretty.”

“Honey, I want to take a picture! Hold it for me.”

She snapped a few photos, framing the cakes against the green grass and lake in the background, creating some aesthetically pleasing food shots.

After admiring her photos, she immediately posted them to her social media feed.

“Honey, can we eat now?” The man asked, swallowing hungrily. The ice cakes looked incredibly delicious, and he was getting impatient.

“Go ahead.”

The couple bit into the ice cakes, and as expected, they were blown away.

“This ice cake… it’s unbelievable!”

“Holy crap! This is freaking amazing!”

Their exaggerated reactions and expressions quickly caught the attention of the people around them.

A few curious onlookers gathered nearby.

“Hey, what are you two eating? Is it really that good?”

“Mmm… It’s so good! You’ve got to try this ice cake. The taste is out of this world!”

The couple continued eating, unable to stop. The surrounding crowd, seeing how much they were enjoying it, couldn’t help but feel intrigued as well.

“Boss, how much for your ice cakes?”

Lu Qin pointed to the price written on his whiteboard and said, “25 yuan per box, and there’s a two-box limit per customer.”

“25 yuan for a small box? That’s a bit pricey!”

“Give me one anyway. If it tastes as good as they say, it’s worth it.”

“I love sweets! Boss, give me two boxes of ice cake and two more cups of mung bean smoothie.”

“Girl, didn’t we agree to go on a diet together? We haven’t even started climbing Huaguo Mountain, and you’re already buying snacks?”

“You need to eat to have the energy to climb!”

“…”

After a wave of customers tasted the mung bean smoothie and ice cake, they couldn’t stop raving.

“This ice cake is amazing!”

“The mung bean smoothie is perfection!”

“The combination of both is absolutely unbeatable!”

Some of the customers who were planning to stroll around the park or take a leisurely walk were now rooted in place, standing in front of Lu Qin’s food stall, unable to move.

Once the first customer showed up at Lu Qin’s stall, more kept coming, and soon enough, a long line formed. The two women selling water next to him were stunned. The tables had turned way too quickly!

Many people even began ordering the fried noodles. Lu Qin dropped the rice noodles into the pan, and soon the rich, delicious aroma spread through the air.

Two friends who had specifically come to the park to exercise, dressed in their workout clothes and carrying their low-fat meals, found themselves in an unexpected dilemma. On their very first day of working out, they had run into Lu Qin’s snack stall. The temptation was too much to resist.

“This fried noodle smells amazing! Should we get a plate?”

“Fried noodles? We just ate lunch not too long ago, and now you’re getting another meal? Aren’t we supposed to be on a diet?”

“Climbing the mountain will burn it off later.”

“Okay… fine. It really does smell good.”

Their diet plan collapsed before it even started.

Meanwhile, the couple who had initially visited the stall had just posted pictures to their social media. It didn’t take long for people to start liking and commenting.

“Those desserts look nice, where did you get them?”

“What is that? It looks so fancy! Is it tasty?”

“Whoa! Is that ice cake?”

“Is that from Lu Qin’s food stall?”

“Where is this? He’s not at People’s Square today? Where did he go? Tell me the location!”

The woman replied, confused, in the comments.

Back at People’s Square, a large crowd was still waiting for Lu Qin’s food stall to show up. They had been waiting all morning, but Lu Qin never came.

Until someone shouted.

“Lu Qin’s stall is at Huaguo Mountain Park today!”

Everyone was stunned, and complaints began pouring in.

“ Ugh ! Why did he move over there?”

“He could’ve at least said something earlier! I’ve skipped breakfast and lunch, waiting for his fried noodles!”

“Huaguo Mountain Park is so far from here. Am I supposed to starve to death on the way?”

“Let’s go! We’re heading to Huaguo Mountain!”

Despite their grumbling, the loyal customers were already on the move, switching locations in a heartbeat.

chapters.title: Chapter 36 - I'm Here to Lose Weight! But I'm Already Full Before Even Starting My Hike!
chapters.content:
Chapter 36 I’m Here to Lose Weight! But I’m Already Full Before Even Starting My Hike!

At Huaguoshan Park, most people came here for a walk or to exercise. But the appearance of Lu Qin’s snack stand disrupted the original balance.

At 2 PM, Around Lu Qin’s snack stand, a crowd of 30 to 40 people had already gathered. Some were standing in line for stir-fried noodles, while others, having already made their purchase, were eating nearby. The scene resembled a bustling food market.

“Hey, old man up front, can you hurry up with your order of noodles? You’re taking forever!”

“Iced mung bean smoothie! Hurry, hurry! Just got down from the mountain, and I need something to quench my thirst!” “Boss, I see stir-fried pork on your menu. Do you have any? If so, I’ll take a plate!” “Oh my gosh! Stir-fried pork? That’s insane!”

“Mung bean smoothie paired with crystal cake! Stir-fried noodles with stir-fried pork!” “This is crazy! I came here to run and lose weight, but I haven’t even started yet. I’ve already wolfed down a plate of stir-fried noodles, a mung bean smoothie, and a box of crystal cake. Screw it, bring on the stir-fried pork too—might as well try everything! It’s just too good!”

“ Ugh , I can’t eat anymore. Two plates of noodles, a box of crystal cake, three mung bean smoothies… I’m stuffed. Boss, will you be here tomorrow? I’m calling it a day for now, but I’ll be back to conquer more tomorrow!”

“Someone lent me a stomach, I’m struggling here! But my mouth still wants more!”

“Huh? Why is my stomach so big? Am I pregnant?”

“Boss, other people’s noodles just cost money, but yours are life-threatening!” “Boss, I think you’re trying to overstuff me so you can inherit my lizard’s payment app debt!”

Half an hour later. A wave of customers from People’s Square rushed into Huaguoshan Park. The already busy snack stand became even more crowded. The park’s security patrol was dumbfounded. How did so many people show up all of a sudden? A mob… eating stir-fried noodles? This crowd is insane!

The security captain quickly instructed his team to move two trash bins closer to avoid environmental issues.

“Captain, those noodles smell amazing!” “Hurry up and get in line! Let’s give them a try!”

Lu Qin looked at the large crowd of customers in front of him and shouted.

“Please line up properly and don’t litter! If the city inspectors come and we can’t set up here anymore, none of you will get to eat.”

His words had an immediate effect, the noise subsided, and the line became more orderly.

“Boss Lu, don’t worry! I know the city inspectors in the west part of town. I’ve already asked them, it’s fine to set up here.” “Everyone, let’s keep it clean and not throw trash around. It’ll affect Boss Lu.”

Lu Qin quickly cleaned the pan and began preparing the next order of stir-fried pork. With a hot pan and cold oil, he unlocked the fresh-preserving temperature box, grabbed a handful of sliced meat, and tossed it into the pan. The freshness of the meat hadn’t changed one bit. As the meat sizzled and crackled in the pan, it was just right. The slices curled slightly, indicating they were releasing the fat. Any longer, and they’d be overcooked.

By now, the rich aroma of the pork filled the air. The surrounding customers all swallowed hungrily. This stir-fried pork was making everyone’s mouth water!

Lu Qin added a large spoonful of Doubanjiang (fermented bean paste), a spoonful of black beans, and some chopped garlic. Over medium heat, he sautéed everything until the red oil surfaced, then tossed in the pork slices for a final stir. Lastly, he threw in some scallions, green and red peppers, a spoonful of cooking wine, a spoonful of sugar, a pinch of salt, and a generous splash of soy sauce. After a few quick stirs, it was ready to be served.

The lucky customer who had first ordered the stir-fried pork couldn’t wait any longer. “Boss Lu, let me have it first! I’ll pay after I finish eating!”

He was practically drooling as he spoke.

Other customers looked on with envy. With the first bite, the flavors exploded in his mouth. The garlic sprouts and pork slices were perfectly intertwined—rich but not greasy, spicy but not overpowering, fatty yet tender. The customer was too focused on eating to say a word. The only sound came from his teeth crunching through the food. All around, the sound of people swallowing their saliva filled the air.

Lu Qin moved on to the second, then the third serving of stir-fried pork. A group of people supposedly on a diet found themselves devouring plate after plate, feeling guilty but unable to stop praising, “Damn, this stir-fried pork is ridiculously good!”

“Boss Lu, you should create a group chat. Let us know where you’ll set up each day by dropping a location pin so we don’t miss out.”

The suggestion came from a chubby young guy in his twenties who looked like a natural foodie. As soon as he said it, the other customers all nodded in agreement.

Lu Qin thought for a moment and agreed. He opened an old messaging app he hadn’t used in years and created a group with a 500-member limit. He wrote the group number on the small whiteboard. Customers scrambled to join the group.

Lu Qin made the chubby guy the group admin to help maintain order. The chubby guy’s name was Zhou Weiwei, a loyal fan of Lu Qin’s snack stand. Lu Qin remembered him because just a few days ago, Zhou had ordered six servings of stir-fried noodles in one go.

Zhou was very familiar with managing groups and immediately posted a group announcement.

“This group is solely for sharing Boss Lu’s food! No inappropriate pictures, political discussions, or advertisements allowed… violators will be kicked out!”

“I feel miserable if I go even a day without Boss Lu’s food!”

“+1”

“+1”

“+1”

…

Within a few minutes, the group surpassed 100 members. In this age of instant communication, the group Lu Qin created quickly spread by word of mouth.

As the last order of stir-fried noodles was packed up, Lu Qin’s food cart was completely cleared out.

In just two hours at Huaguoshan Park, he sold 255 servings of stir-fried noodles, 300 cups of mung bean smoothie, 150 boxes of crystal cake, and 81 orders of stir-fried pork.

His total earnings for the day reached 10,545 yuan, easily breaking the 10,000 mark. The system’s points had accumulated to 1,367. On average, the daily profit from the stall had been around 12,000 yuan for the past few days.

Lu Qin was very satisfied with this income.

At 4 PM sharp, Outside the Ourei International Hotel, Lu Qin parked his three-wheeled food cart in its usual spot by the roadside. The doorman greeted him with a smile.

In just a day’s time, the smiles from both the doorman and the hotel front desk had grown noticeably more genuine. In their private conversations, they gossiped about Lu Qin’s background.

On the surface, he seemed like just another street vendor. But that couldn’t be the whole story. After all, his crystal cake had won over the notoriously picky Mr. Rossi.

There was a good chance that Lu Qin was actually a low-key master chef. Lu Qin waited on the lobby sofa, while a server brought over tea and snacks.

Seven or eight minutes later, a tall woman walked in, holding the hand of a little girl. The girl was adorable, with big, expressive eyes that sparkled with intelligence.

Anyone who saw her couldn’t help but take a second look.

When the girl saw Lu Qin, her face lit up like a flower blooming.

“Daddy~!”

In a sweet, soft voice, she called out and ran into Lu Qin’s arms.

Fang Ya stood to the side, watching enviously. The bond between father and daughter was so sweet.

When Shen Hongda and his wife had dropped off Lu Xiyao, Fang Ya had taken her out to play. Shen Hongda and Jiang Qin had originally planned to meet with Lu Qin, but ultimately decided against it.

They felt a bit guilty towards him.

“Lu Qin, long time no see.”

Fang Ya greeted him with a smile.

Lu Qin stood up.

“Long time no see.”

Fang Ya sized him up. They hadn’t seen each other in over six months.

She noticed that Lu Qin hadn’t changed much—his eyes were still clear, his smile warm.

Such a good man.

Shen Muyan, oh Shen Muyan, you really didn’t know how to appreciate him.

And now, Lu Qin could make such delicious crystal cakes.

For a foodie like Fang Ya, that was a fatal attraction!

chapters.title: Chapter 37 - Lu's Foodie Group
chapters.content:
Chapter 37 Lu’s Foodie Group

Shen Muyan and Lu Qin had divorced, and as Shen’s close friend, Fang Ya normally wouldn’t have any interaction with Lu Qin. But there was still the connection with the little one—she was Lu Xiyao’s godmother. All that care she had shown the kid had now come in handy.

Earlier, while taking her goddaughter out to play, Fang Ya subtly found out that it was indeed Lu Qin who made the amazing crystal cakes. And not only that, he was selling them at a street food stand!

Such a delicious treat, wasted on a street stall. It deserved to be sold at high-end restaurants, where it could easily fetch 288 yuan per serving.

“Lu Qin, I never imagined you had such skills. That crystal cake is unreal!”

Fang Ya gave him a thumbs up.

Before Lu Qin could respond, the little girl chimed in.

“My daddy is amazing! Everything he makes is super delicious!”

Lu Qin smiled, gently patting Xiyao’s head.

Fang Ya said, “Where do you usually set up your stall? I want to come and buy some—I’m craving more crystal cake!”

“I don’t have a fixed spot. If you want, I can make some and bring it to you since we’re friends.”

What the heck?! This man is just too kind! Too warm, too thoughtful!

Shen Muyan, are you hearing this?

Fang Ya quickly waved her hands, saying, “No, no, I couldn’t possibly impose like that. Just tell me where you’ll be, and I’ll come support your stall.”

“Then join our group chat on QQ. It’s mostly for regular customers. I will post the location before I set up.”

“QQ group?”

Fang Ya found this amusing. A food stand with a customer group? But on second thought, given how delicious the crystal cake was, having repeat customers was a certainty—200% guaranteed, no exaggeration.

Fang Ya joined the QQ group, which was named ‘Lu’s Foodie Group’.

She chuckled when she saw it and noticed the group had over 150 members.

Whoa! Quite a few fans!

The group was set to restrict chatting among members because customers were constantly praising Lu Qin’s culinary skills, almost worshipping him. If it continued, they’d soon start a ‘Cult of Lu’.

Zhou Weiwei had suggested that Lu Qin enable the group chat only when the stall was set up and mute it otherwise. Lu Qin agreed with this suggestion.

Shen Muyan had been in a meeting since 3:30 PM and definitely didn’t have time to come down. After saying goodbye to Fang Ya, Lu Qin and his little girl left. The three-wheeled food cart disappeared down the road.

For the first time, Fang Ya realized that even someone riding a tricycle could look handsome. It really depends on who was riding it.

…

In a Chinese-style villa, Chen Dahai sat eating the delicious crystal cake while reviewing some files.

You eat someone’s cake, you help them out.

He already had Lu Qin’s recent situation investigated. Currently living in the southern part of the city in the old neighborhood, Apartment 1702A, a rental. The monthly rent was 3,000 yuan.

He was now working as a street vendor selling stir-fried noodles. He operated his stall during three time slots—morning, noon, and night. Business was booming, but exact earnings were unknown.

His morning stall was usually set up near Sunshine Elementary School, and his late-night stand was located at University Town’s night market street. He often took his daughter along when he worked the night shift.

…

“Hmm… the kid’s certainly hardworking,” Chen Dahai murmured.

“But he’s putting his efforts in the wrong place. Running a food stall isn’t a long-term plan.”

“Business is booming? No matter how busy it gets, can it really be better than a full-time job?”

Chen Dahai glanced through Lu Qin’s education records. He was a top graduate from Tsinghua University and Peking University and had previously worked at Wanwu Technology. His resume was impressive.

So why didn’t he find a proper job and instead chose to run a food stall?

Chen Dahai couldn’t understand. Looking at Lu Qin’s handsome ID photo, he pondered how to help Shen Hongda with this favor. After all, when you accept someone’s kindness, you’ve got to return the favor in style. For now, he decided to get closer to Lu Qin and play it by ear.

Soon, he received a call inquiring about the crystal cake. The caller mentioned that in Jiangcheng, there were crystal cakes, osmanthus cakes, and mung bean cakes, but no one was selling crystal cakes. Even private chefs known for hidden delicacies had no knowledge of it.

This left Chen Dahai disappointed. He didn’t believe Shen Hongda’s claim that the cake was made by a renowned pastry chef abroad. Chen was convinced this crystal cake was a local Chinese snack, no doubt about it.

The thought of having to ask stingy Shen Hongda for more of the crystal cake in the future was something Chen Dahai definitely didn’t want to do.

…

“Daddy, did you miss your little cutie over the past couple of days?”

“Take a guess.”

“I guess you missed me a lot! Daddy, am I right?”

“Yes, you’re right.”

“What’s my reward for guessing right?”

“What do you want for a reward?”

“Can I have an ice cream? Green Mood flavor.”

Lu Qin pointed at the rearview mirror.

“Look at yourself, there’s still chocolate in your mouth.”

“Aunt Fang Ya took you out to play and bought you ice cream, didn’t she?”

Lu Xiyao leaned forward to check the mirror and immediately covered her mouth with both hands.

Oops, busted!

“Hehehe!” She gave her dad a cute little smile.

“Since you ate so many ice creams while I was away, you won’t be having any next week.”

“Waah! Daddy! Nooo, what will I do without ice cream?!”

“You’ll manage.”

Lu Xiyao sulked for a brief moment, then sighed softly.

Being a kid is so tough—you can’t even eat ice cream freely.

“Can I at least have Daddy’s stir-fried noodles, mung bean smoothie, and crystal cake then?”

Since ice cream was off the table, she could at least have Lu Qin’s food. That should satisfy her cravings.

“Sure,” Lu Qin nodded.

They got home.

“Daddy, I’m tired. Can I rest a bit?”

“Yes, go rest on the couch, but take off your shoes first.”

“Okay! Daddy, will you rest with me?”

The two snuggled on the couch. Little Xiyao wrapped her arms around Lu Qin’s, resting her head on his shoulder as they watched TV together. The show was a Chinese cartoon called Haier Brothers.

“When it’s thundering, it’s going to rain, uh-huh! — What’s that?”

“When it rains, you need an umbrella, uh-huh! — Yeah, I know that too!”

The little one sang along with the theme song, clearly familiar with it and not missing a note.

Lu Qin smiled softly, a warm feeling filling his heart. Watching her grow up peacefully, day by day, was one of life’s true blessings. Before long, little Xiyao fell asleep, resting against him.

Lu Qin gently laid her down, covered her with a blanket, and turned off the TV. He then headed into the kitchen to start preparing the ingredients for his night market stall.

…

Meanwhile, Fang Ya was sneaking some food in the hotel kitchen.

“This pea cake is pretty good, but chef, can you make a crystal cake?”

“Crystal cake? No, I can’t.”

The pastry chef with over ten years of experience shook his head.

“Miss Fang, are you sure you don’t mean shui jing gao (water crystal cake)?”

“No, they’re similar but not the same.”

Fang Ya took one more bite of the pea cake before stopping. After tasting Lu Qin’s crystal cake, other pastries seemed to lack flavor.

The chef noticed the expression on Fang Ya’s face and grew curious. If she wasn’t satisfied with his signature pea cake, just how delicious was this crystal cake? He had never heard of it in all his years.

After Fang Ya left, Chef Xiong took out his phone and called his mentor.

“Crystal cake? Yeah, I’ve heard of it.”

“It’s a local specialty from the Northeast. It’s not widely known. I’ve heard it’s a type of rice cake made from sticky rice. The process is quite complex, and to make it taste authentic and delicious, you need a high level of skill.”

chapters.title: Chapter 38 - Boss Lu, Can You Be My Brother-in-Law?
chapters.content:
Chapter 38. Boss Lu, Can You Be My Brother-in-Law?

“Why are you suddenly asking about this?”

Chef Xiong responded, “A customer asked about the crystal cake today. I had never heard of it before, so I thought I’d ask my mentor.”

“I’ve only heard about it too, never had the chance to taste it up north,” the mentor replied.

Chef Xiong continued, “Apparently, we have it here in Jiangcheng. That customer lost all interest in my pea cake after having the crystal cake.”

“Oh? Well, if I get the chance, I’ll definitely have to try it myself.”

After hanging up, Ma Dejun put his phone away, only for it to start buzzing again.

“Who’s bothering me now? Can’t an old man enjoy his weekend in peace?”

“Hello?”

“Old Ma, it’s me, Old Ge!”

“Oh, it’s you. If you’re calling for a drinking session, count me out. I quit long ago.”

“No drinks, just a meal.”

“A meal, huh? You know I’m picky about food.”

“I guarantee it’ll be good.”

“When?”

“Tomorrow morning at 9.”

“For lunch?”

“No, for breakfast.”

“Breakfast?!”

…

Shen Muyan had finally finished her meeting, but she still had department rounds to make. Her diligent, workaholic attitude left Fang Ya speechless.

“When will this workaholic learn to enjoy life?” Fang Ya sighed. It looked like she would be going to the University Town night market alone that evening. She opened the QQ group, but it was still muted, with no updates from Lu Qin about his stand. Fang Ya was craving crystal cake. The last time she only got a small taste before Shen Muyan devoured most of it.

Unable to satisfy her cravings right away, Fang Ya decided to head to the night market by herself, determined to finally try the famed stir-fried noodles.

…

For dinner, Lu Qin made stir-fried pork, and for the first time, little Lu Xiyao was so excited that she nearly bit her tongue.

“Daddy, this stir-fried pork is so good!”

Lu Qin smiled and gave her a chopstick-full of vegetables, reminding her.

“Don’t just eat the meat. You need to eat your vegetables too.”

“I know!” She replied.

She eagerly ate a fatty, tender piece of pork, then shoveled rice and vegetables into her mouth. But in the face of such delicious stir-fried pork, the vegetables and rice felt more like filler than actual food.

After polishing off all the dishes, the father and daughter were stuffed. Xiyao even licked the plate clean, her face covered in grease.

“Daddy, it was so delicious! Can I take some stir-fried pork to kindergarten tomorrow?”

“Sure, but you can’t just eat meat.”

“I know, kids have to eat vegetables too, for balanced nutrition, so they can grow big and strong!” Xiyao said seriously.

Lu Qin chuckled and got up to wash the dishes.

“Daddy, I’ll help you!”

Lu Qin washed, and little Xiyao dried the plates.

At 6:30, they hopped onto their three-wheeled food cart and headed out to set up their night stall.

…

On Changlin Road, at University Town’s night market street, the owner of Hot Spicy Girl Hot Pot stood at the entrance, watching the sea of customers crowding the alley. His heart was racing.

“Wow! This crowd is insane!”

One of the hot pot restaurant’s waiters, just off his shift, snuck out the door to get in line for food.

“Hold up,” the owner called after him.

“Boss, my shift’s over. You’re not making me stay, are you?”

“No, just grab me an order of stir-fried noodles and a mung bean smoothie.”

“Huh?”

The hot pot owner had been craving Lu Qin’s food for days, but the stall was always so busy that he would never get the chance to buy anything. He had been salivating over it for too long.

Lu Qin had only been setting up his stir-fried noodle stand in this alley for three or four days, but its popularity had exploded beyond anyone’s expectations.

Old Liu, who had been running his noodle stand at the entrance for five or six years, was so shaken by the frenzy that he had packed up and moved far away.

Half of the other stalls in the alley had disappeared. None could shake the dominance of Lu Qin’s stir-fried noodle stand.

The number of members in the QQ group had now exceeded 320. Twenty minutes earlier, Lu Qin had lifted the chat restrictions and posted an announcement. It seemed the members had been holding back all day, as the chat window exploded with messages.

…

As Lu Qin’s three-wheeler entered the alley, a round of applause erupted.

“Boss Lu, finally you’re here!”

“I’m about five minutes away from starving to death—hurry and save me!”

“Everyone, step aside and line up properly. Don’t hold up Boss Lu setting up his stall!”

The line quickly straightened out, looking as orderly as a military parade.

Little Xiyao, sitting excitedly on the three-wheeler, waved her small white hands enthusiastically. She wasn’t shy at all and had quite the bold personality.

“Hello, brothers, sisters, uncles, and aunts~!”

“Thanks for waiting~!”

“Thank you for supporting my daddy’s business~!”

“Make sure to eat lots~!”

Her sweet, childish voice melted everyone’s hearts.

“Oh my gosh! This little cutie is too adorable!”

“There’s Boss Lu’s daughter again! She’s as precious as ever!”

“Whose little princess is this? I want to kidnap her!”

“Let me pinch those cute cheeks!”

…

Lu Qin didn’t have to find a spot. His early-arriving customers had already secured one for him—10 meters from the alley entrance. The area was spacious, with no other stalls on either side.

As soon as Lu Qin parked, a few customers came over to help him set up the stall. He barely had to lift a finger.

Once the small whiteboard was hung up, the business officially started.

“Boss Lu, fire it up! Three orders of stir-fried noodles, five cups of mung bean smoothie, and two boxes of crystal cake!”

“I’ll have the same, plus an order of stir-fried pork!”

“Boss Lu, give me five orders of stir-fried noodles, one stir-fried pork, three mung bean smoothies, and two boxes of crystal cake!”

“Hey, you up front, one box of crystal cake is enough! There’s still a line of people behind you!”

“Exactly! Let’s be fair here!”

Lu Qin was used to the banter between customers and didn’t pay much attention. While stir-frying the noodles, he kept an eye on little Xiyao.

She was behaving well, sitting on her small folding stool nearby. Occasionally, she stood up and directed the queue.

“Big brother, stand in line properly! Your butt is sticking out~!”

“Please scan the QR code to pay! We accept Alipay and WeChat~!”

“Big sister, stop staring at my daddy! It’s distracting him from working~!”

Her cheeky comments had the crowd laughing.

“Boss Lu’s daughter is just too adorable, haha!”

“Haha, miss, looks like you’re aiming to be her stepmom!”

“With Boss Lu being so handsome and his kid so cute, being a stepmom sounds like a great deal!”

…

chapters.title: Chapter 39 - Boss Lu, Can You Be My Brother-in-Law?
chapters.content:
Chapter 39 Boss Lu, Can You Be My Brother-in-Law?

At the alley’s entrance, a man and a woman appeared one after the other. They weren’t related, but both were there for the same reason—Lu Qin’s food stand.

The man was Mr. Rossi. He had originally planned to check out of the Euro International Hotel and leave Jiangcheng. To him, the city was an absolute culinary desert.

But after stumbling upon Lu Qin’s crystal cake, it was as though he’d tasted a rare delicacy. He decided to extend his stay and hunt down Lu Qin’s food stand to fully indulge in the chef’s culinary skills.

The woman was Fang Ya. When she saw the long line snaking through the alley, her jaw nearly dropped.

“What on earth?” She muttered in disbelief.

“This is busier than a supermarket clearance sale.”

Despite being a mega-influencer and a food blogger with millions of followers, Fang Ya knew that when it came to food, everyone was on equal footing. She obediently took her place at the end of the line.

But she wasn’t the last to join the queue—more and more customers kept arriving.

“Will there even be enough food left?” Fang Ya wondered, starting to feel anxious. The smell in the air made her excitement soar. The noodles had to be amazing.

“Hey, guys up front, save some for those of us who came late!” She shouted playfully.

“And take it easy with the stir-fried pork, Peng Yuyan wannabes! I brought my own rice!”

Fang Ya was dumbfounded. The customers were practically turning into competitors, fighting over food. It was surreal!

The aroma of stir-fried noodles wafted through the air, sometimes mingling with the rich sauce of the stir-fried pork. The smells were intoxicating, a ten-out-of-ten on the tempting scale. It was pure torture, smelling all that deliciousness but not being able to eat it.

Suddenly, Fang Ya heard a familiar phrase. “Crystal cake.”

She froze for a moment and turned her head, puzzled.

Someone in the crowd was shouting about crystal cake.

“Hey, save some crystal cake for the rest of us!” A voice called out.

But such pleas had little effect. The people in line were resolute—each grabbing two boxes, no one willing to miss out on the famed treat. If there wasn’t a limit on purchases, they would probably buy ten boxes each.

Fang Ya’s eyes widened in shock.

“It really is the crystal cake… no way.”

She stepped out of the line and hurried forward.

What she saw first was a familiar small figure. This afternoon, she had taken her to play the claw machine, ride the kiddie rides, eat ice cream, and devour candied hawthorn. It was none other than little Yaoyao!

Everything felt like a wild coincidence. The stir-fried noodle stand recommended by Douyin users was actually Lu Qin’s!

Fang Ya quickly took out her phone. Sure enough, the QQ group announcement matched the night market’s location. Her heart raced as excitement bubbled up inside her.

At that moment, little Yaoyao was sitting on her small stool, looking like the cutest mascot ever. The customers around her adored the little girl, and her tiny hands were already full of snacks—lollipops, candied hawthorn, jelly, apples, strawberries, bananas, yogurt… She was a hit.

“Little beauty, how old are you?” One customer asked.

“Uncle, it’s rude to ask a lady her age! But I’m definitely not a little three-year-old anymore~!” Yaoyao replied cheekily.

The crowd burst into laughter.

“Little cutie, smile for the camera! I’m taking a picture!”

“Sis, did you turn on the beauty filter?”

“You know about beauty filters? You’re so pretty, you don’t even need one.”

“Who would say no to being prettier? I want to look my best~!”

“Whose kid is this? She’s so adorable, I just want to take her home!”

“Auntie, that’s a dangerous thought~! My daddy will call the police~!”

“Hahaha, I’m just kidding, sweetheart!”

“Little beauty, can I be your stepmom?”

“Does your family own a chain of luxury hotels~?”

Boss Lu’s Ex-Wife is Blind”

“No,” little Yaoyao replied.

“Do you have a mansion?”

“Also no.”

“At least your family must have a huge TV, right?”

“That too, no.”

“Then you can’t be my stepmom!”

The people around were stunned. The little girl’s innocent face was full of sincerity, yet her words held no trace of superficiality or materialism.

People started speculating if the conditions she mentioned were her mom’s requirements. If Lu Qin were to remarry, wouldn’t the stepmom need to at least meet the same standards as the biological mom? Otherwise, why settle for less?

Those young women who had entertained the idea of being her stepmom started backing off. It was a joke, after all—they didn’t have mansions. And looking at how amazing both Lu Qin and his daughter were, it was easy to imagine that Lu Qin’s ex-wife had to have been quite something.

“Boss Lu’s ex-wife must be blind. How could she give up such a great husband and daughter?”

“Exactly! With Boss Lu’s cooking skills, how could she not stick around for a lifetime?”

Hearing the chatter, even Fang Ya couldn’t help but nod in agreement. She blurted out, “Who wouldn’t agree? Boss Lu’s ex-wife must be blind!”

“Right, pretty lady? Great minds think alike.”

“Yeah,” Fang Ya nodded seriously, then took out her phone and sent a WeChat message to Shen Muyan.

Two minutes later, Shen Muyan replied with a single question mark.

Fang Ya followed up by sending a picture of little Yaoyao sitting sweetly on her small stool, holding a pile of snacks. Around her, the customers treated her like a little princess, showering her with affection.

Shen Muyan quickly responded.

Fang Ya, however, decided not to continue the conversation. She put her phone on silent and focused on more important things.

“Yaoyao, sweetie!”

Fang Ya’s sudden appearance caught little Yaoyao by surprise. She looked delighted and exclaimed.

“Auntie Fang Ya, how did you get here?”

Turning towards her father, she called out, “Daddy, Auntie Fang Ya is here~!”

Lu Qin, who was busy packing a few orders of stir-fried noodles, glanced over and responded, “I see her, sweetheart.”

He then looked at Fang Ya with a smile and said, “You’re here? There’s no place to sit except for some small stools.”

Before they could chat any more, the customers in line were getting impatient.

“Boss Lu, please hurry up with the stir-fried noodles! Lives are on the line here! Keep talking while cooking, but don’t stop!”

Lu Qin gave an apologetic smile to Fang Ya and then turned to his daughter, saying, “Yaoyao, please take care of Auntie Fang Ya while Daddy works.”

Fang Ya smiled and said, “Don’t worry about me, Boss Lu. You focus on your work.”

Little Yaoyao patted her chest confidently.

“Daddy, you go ahead. I’ll take care of Auntie Fang Ya.”

With that, Yaoyao went to the food cart and brought out another small stool. She unfolded it and placed it right next to hers, patting the seat invitingly.

“Auntie Fang Ya, let’s sit together~!”

It was as if they were both meant to be the stall’s mascots.

Fang Ya chuckled and sat down. Meanwhile, little Yaoyao went to the foam cooler and retrieved two cups of iced mung bean smoothies and a box of crystal cake.

Fang Ya’s eyes lit up with excitement.

“Auntie Fang Ya, try my daddy’s mung bean smoothie! It’s super delicious~!”

“And this crystal cake is so tasty~!”

Fang Ya swallowed, unable to hide her excitement, and accepted the treats without hesitation.

“Come on, Yaoyao, let’s eat together.”

“Okay~!”

…

chapters.title: Chapter 40 - Tasting the Ice Jelly Cake, I Was Wrapped in Happiness!
chapters.content:
Chapter 40 Tasting the Ice Jelly Cake, I Was Wrapped in Happiness!

Fang Ya inserted the straw and took a sip of the chilled mung bean soup. Instantly, her eyes squinted into little crescents. This mung bean soup—It was too delicious! Incredible!

Fang Ya had never tasted such delicious mung bean soup! Lu Xiyao, watching Fang Ya’s expression, couldn’t help but feel proud and said, “Hehe~! How is it, Auntie Fang Ya? Isn’t the mung bean soup my dad made amazing?”

“It’s so delicious!” Fang Ya nodded vigorously.

Lu Xiyao then opened the ice jelly cake, and the two of them started eating together. The little girl had just had dinner not too long ago, so even though she was craving it, she couldn’t eat two pieces.

Fang Ya, finally tasting the ice jelly cake she had been dreaming about, was enveloped in happiness. Ice jelly cake paired with mung bean soup—it was perfect!

As Fang Ya ate, she also observed the business at Lu Qin’s stall. The line of customers had already stretched out onto the street, and new customers kept arriving. The bustling scene was simply unprecedented.

Meanwhile… In her office, while handling documents, Shen Muyan’s phone suddenly buzzed twice. She stopped what she was doing and picked it up to take a look. It was a message from Fang Ya.

For some reason, Fang Ya had sent a random message about a ‘female blind monk’. Shen Muyan didn’t play games, so she had no idea what that meant. Then, she received a photo of the little girl, which successfully caught Shen Muyan’s attention.

Rarely putting aside her work, Shen Muyan asked where the photo was taken and how Fang Ya had managed to get it. But… there was no response. It was as if the message sank into the ocean. Fang Ya didn’t reply, and when Shen Muyan called her, she didn’t pick up. Shen Muyan furrowed her brows in curiosity.

“Why isn’t Fang Ya answering the phone?”

However, Fang Ya’s phone was on silent, so she didn’t hear it. She was busy playing a ‘Spot the Difference’ game on a tablet with Lu Xiyao, sipping chilled mung bean soup and showing off her ice jelly cake. Life was too good! As for Shen Muyan, she had long been forgotten.

“Daddy~! Auntie Fang Ya hasn’t tried the stir-fried noodles and twice-cooked pork yet~!”

Fang Ya had been sitting with the little girl for an hour. Even though she had already consumed two cups of mung bean soup and two boxes of ice jelly cake, for some reason, she was getting hungrier.

Lu Xiyao was hungry too. The main reason was that Lu Qin, cooking stir-fried noodles and twice-cooked pork nearby, was making everything smell so good that it awakened the cravings inside them. Fang Ya felt a little embarrassed and waved her hand, saying, “Lu Qin, don’t worry about me. Your business is more important.”

The customers were all eyeing him like tigers watching their prey. Even though Lu Qin was already working fast without compromising the taste, the number of customers was overwhelming. He didn’t even have time for a sip of water.

“Boss Lu, maybe you should take a break. You’ve been cooking for two hours straight—no one pushes their animals this hard!”

“Yeah, Boss Lu, if you wear yourself out, we won’t be able to eat your food anymore!”

Quite a few customers, feeling sympathetic towards Lu Qin’s high-intensity work, urged him to rest a bit before continuing. However, Lu Qin didn’t feel tired at all, thanks to the system’s fatigue elimination aura. Occupational diseases? Not something he had to worry about at all.

Lu Qin managed to find time to cook a portion of stir-fried noodles and twice-cooked pork for Fang Ya. The other customers didn’t mind, understanding that Fang Ya was Lu Qin’s friend and that enjoying some special treatment was perfectly normal. Being a friend of Lu Qin was, after all, quite a lucky thing.

The little girl excitedly brought the stir-fried noodles and twice-cooked pork over.

“Auntie Fang Ya, try the stir-fried noodles and twice-cooked pork~! They’re both super delicious~!”

As Lu Xiyao held the plate and spoke, she couldn’t stop drooling. Fang Ya quickly took the plate from her. Looking at the steaming stir-fried noodles and twice-cooked pork, she couldn’t help but drool too.

The stir-fried noodles smelled amazing, and the twice-cooked pork smelled even better. What kind of heavenly dish was this stir-fried noodles and twice-cooked pork? The aroma was too tempting!

As a food blogger, Fang Ya had eaten countless delicacies, but never had anything made her crave like this.

“Yao Yao, sweetheart, did you bring the chopsticks?”

“I did, and I brought two pairs, hehe~!” Lu Xiyao opened her small hand and handed over a pair of chopsticks.

“I can’t wait anymore. Auntie Fang Ya is too hungry. Let me taste the stir-fried noodles and twice-cooked pork your daddy made first.”

Saying this, Fang Ya broke the chopsticks apart and took a bite of the stir-fried noodles.

Even though she had already prepared herself mentally, knowing that Lu Qin’s cooking wasn’t ordinary after drinking the mung bean soup and eating the ice jelly cake, Fang Ya was still amazed the moment the stir-fried noodles entered her mouth.

The taste of the rice flour’s natural fragrance combined with the flavor of stir-frying and pan-searing, rich but not greasy, smooth but not choking. Fang Ya never imagined that simple stir-fried noodles could taste this good.

After two bites of stir-fried noodles, her gaze shifted to the twice-cooked pork on the side. Its golden color made it even more tempting.

Fang Ya picked up a piece and put it in her mouth. In an instant, her tongue and taste buds were conquered. Fang Ya was done for. It was so delicious, she felt like calling the police.

The little one also picked up her chopsticks and started eating the stir-fried noodles and twice-cooked pork. She was so happy that her little white face was filled with joy as she nodded her head rhythmically with delight.

Another half an hour passed. The containers holding the ingredients were gradually emptying. Lu Qin estimated the remaining portions and then shouted loudly.

“To the 16 customers still in line, I’m sorry, please come back tomorrow.”

When the bad news came, the faces of the 16 people in line showed disappointment.

“Boss Lu, hearing that is too hurtful, like announcing a death sentence.”

“Every time I hear Boss Lu say the food is sold out, it feels like failing the college entrance exam.”

There were always a few sad people who had to go home alone every day, with some even posting on social media.

The customers gradually left, and the alley returned to its usual emptiness and tranquility.

Lu Qin began to pack up the stall.

The little one quickly got up and ran over to help.
