“Daddy, I’ll help you~!”

“I’ll help too,” Fang Ya, having enjoyed all the food, felt obliged to lend a hand.

Normally, Miss Fang was very lazy—she wouldn’t even bother to pick up a bottle of soy sauce if it fell. The dishes in her sink would pile up until they started to mold before she’d call the housekeeper to clean them up.

But Lu Qin was very efficient, and in less than ten minutes, everything was packed up. Lu Xiyao and Fang Ya had only helped with folding chairs and stacking food baskets—there wasn’t much work to do.

Today, Fang Ya had experienced Lu Qin’s food stall and gained a new understanding of the term street vendor.

“Lu Qin, I never expected your stall to be this popular!”

“Having booming business isn’t always a good thing. With so many customers, every time I set up the stall, I just want to finish and close up as soon as possible,” Lu Qin replied with a faint smile.

“You must have made quite a bit tonight, huh?”

Before Lu Qin could answer, the little one proudly chimed in.

“Of course! Auntie Fang Ya, we make a lot of money from our food stall~!”

chapters.title: Chapter 41 - Lucky Draw to Earn Attribute Points! Mastering the Skill of Cooking 'Chicken Tofu Flower' at Full Level!
chapters.content:
Chapter 41 Lucky Draw to Earn Attribute Points! Mastering the Skill of Cooking ‘Chicken Tofu Flower’ at Full Level!

Fang Ya wasn’t naïve, she could roughly estimate the sales from the crowd at the night market. She was certain about one thing–Lu Qin’s sales at the stall definitely exceeded ten thousand yuan that evening—just in one night!

Wow!

No wonder Lu Qin didn’t bother looking for a regular job. Working at a job wouldn’t earn him as much as running a food stall. Plus, if Lu Qin did get a job, those foodie customers would probably block the company’s entrance every day.

“Do you need us to give you a ride home?” Lu Qin pointed to his tricycle.

Fang Ya, standing at 1.7 meters tall, felt it would be a bit cramped to squeeze into the tricycle cart. Plus, her home was in the north of the city, about a 20-minute drive away, which wasn’t on the way.

“I’ll take a cab home.”

“Alright, see you then.”

“Auntie Fang Ya, goodbye! Good night!”

The tricycle cart disappeared at the end of the night market street. Fang Ya could still hear the little one’s cheerful singing.

“Over the hills and far away, there’s a group of Smurfs, they are lively and smart, mischievous and nimble…”

In Shen Muyan and Shen Hongda’s eyes, Lu Qin working at a food stall with a child was a very tragic situation. But the truth wasn’t like that at all. They should come and see for themselves how popular Lu Qin’s food stall was. That night, the little one was even happier than when going to an amusement park.

Fang Ya finally got into a cab and turned on her phone, seeing five missed calls from Shen Muyan. She called back.

Shen Muyan’s voice on the other end was cold, clearly in a bad mood.

“President Shen~!”

“Where have you been?”

“At the night market eating stir-fried noodles. I invited you, but you didn’t want to come.”

“What’s the deal with the kid’s picture?”

“Just a coincidence! I happened to visit the stall recommended by some internet users, and it turned out to be Lu Qin’s stall. What a small world, right?”

“You really should have come along. Lu Qin’s stir-fried noodles and twice-cooked pork are absolutely delicious. Didn’t you know your ex-husband is such a great cook?”

There was silence on the other end of the line.

Fang Ya kept going, enthusiastically describing the bustling atmosphere at the stall.

“President Shen! Your ex-husband’s stall makes two to three times more profit in a single day than your salary as a CEO!”

Shen Muyan was speechless.

Fang Ya returned home, tossed her phone aside, and went to take a bath. After spending the entire night at the night market, she felt greasy all over.

Meanwhile, Shen Muyan lay in bed, holding her phone, debating whether or not to message Lu Qin. But she didn’t know what to say.

In the WeChat chat box, she typed.

After typing it out, she held down the delete key and erased the message.

That night, she had a bit of trouble falling asleep…

At 9:30 PM, Lu Qin was helping the little one with her bedtime routine.

“Yao Yao, use this toothpaste,” Lu Qin said, handing her a pink tube with a picture of a little white rabbit on it. This was a children’s toothpaste he had won from a system lottery, one that truly prevented cavities, unlike the exaggerated claims in TV ads.

The little girl’s eyes lit up. She loved the white rabbit. She squeezed a little toothpaste onto her brush and started brushing up and down.

“It smells really nice, like fruit flavor,” she said.

“Dad, I like using this toothpaste.”

She breathed out in Lu Qin’s direction, then giggled.

Lu Qin sniffed, catching the fresh fruity scent in her breath. He smiled and wiped her soft, pale cheeks with a towel.

“Alright, off to bed now.”

“Dad, can I bring stir-fried noodles and twice-cooked pork for lunch tomorrow at kindergarten?”

Lu Xiyao jumped onto the bed, snuggling under the thin blanket with just her cute little head sticking out.

“If you go to sleep now, maybe.”

“I’m sleeping right now! Snore… snore…” She pretended to fall asleep immediately, making exaggerated snoring sounds.

Lu Qin chuckled, shaking his head. He gently patted her until she truly fell asleep, then quietly left the room.

He spent the next 20 minutes in the yard cleaning up the food cart. Before bed, he used over a thousand system points to try his luck at the lottery.

【Spent 100 points, won attribute point: Stamina +0.1】.

【Spent 100 points, won 20 mint candies】.

【Spent 100 points, won full-level Chicken Tofu Flower cooking skill】.

【Spent 100 points, won a Sunny Day Card】.

…

Lu Qin felt a slight thrill—daily lottery attempts always came with rewards. He even gained attribute points! A stamina increase of 0.1 was equivalent to jogging three kilometers a day for three months, instantly earned.

Although his body didn’t feel much different, gaining such points was exciting. Additionally, he had won the full-level skill to make ‘Chicken Tofu Flower’, a dish that was part of the national banquet menu, not something typically served at restaurants. The preparation of Chicken Tofu Flower required exceptional culinary skill. The phrase ‘eating chicken without seeing chicken’ and ‘eating meat without seeing meat’ referred to the way this dish transformed meat into a vegetarian-like appearance, called ‘using meat to support the vegetarian look’.

Lu Qin planned to test out this dish the next day, selling it in limited quantities just like the twice-cooked pork.

The following morning, Lu Qin woke up, washed up, and began preparing the ingredients for the morning food cart. He unlocked the penguin chat group and posted an announcement.

【Morning food stall at 8 AM, location: Sunshine Elementary School entrance】.

As soon as the announcement was made, the group erupted into excitement.

“Battle location confirmed, Sunshine Elementary School entrance!”

“Let’s go, everyone!”

“I’m on the west side of town. I’ll be late to work, but I don’t care—I’m taking a detour just to get a taste of Boss Lu’s stir-fried noodles. Worst case, I lose half a day’s pay!”

“You’re a real warrior, respect!”

“I had quit eating breakfast, but for Boss Lu, I’ll make an exception…”

“Haha! I live in the complex right across from Sunshine Elementary. I knew I bought the right house!”

“So jealous!”

Lu Qin skimmed through the messages. His customers were rowdy but endearing. He closed the chat and checked his system messages—several new customers had requested to join the group.

Looking at the wide array of quirky nicknames and random verification messages, Lu Qin couldn’t help but shake his head and chuckle. One by one, he added them to the group. The group had already reached 468 members.

The group was growing too fast, in another day or two, it would likely hit capacity. He would need to get a membership to expand the chat group to 2,000 members. Just then, the system notification went off again.

Lu Qin opened it to see another new customer requesting to join the group. Their nickname was ‘Thousand Hills and Twilight Snow’. There was no verification message.

He approved the request and then noticed the user had an SVIP Penguin membership. Seeing that, Lu Qin decided to get one himself. The first month was discounted, only 8 yuan.

After purchasing the membership, he upgraded the group to accommodate 2,000 people. Putting away his phone, Lu Qin went to wake up the little one.

chapters.title: Chapter 42 - Everyone Wants to Be the Little One’s Stepmom! What Wishful Thinking!
chapters.content:
Chapter 42 Everyone Wants to Be the Little One’s Stepmom! What Wishful Thinking!

Shen Muyan had gotten Lu Qin’s food stall group chat number from Fang Ya. When she woke up in the morning, she opened her phone and downloaded the Penguin app.

Her Penguin account had been dormant for years, with a mere one-month moon level. There was no personalized signature, and her nickname, chosen randomly years ago, was ‘Thousand Hills and Twilight Snow’.

She clicked the ‘join group’ button, entered the group number, applied, and waited for the group owner’s approval…

A few seconds later, her application was approved. She was in the group. Shen Muyan’s slender fingers scrolled through the member list.

Lu Qin was the yellow-colored group owner, his avatar a cartoon boy, and his nickname was simply ‘Lu Qin’. Fang Ya was also in the group under the nickname ‘Purple Butterfly’, but she wasn’t online, her avatar was grayed out.

Shen Muyan knew Fang Ya’s lazy schedule—she wouldn’t be up until 2 PM. She clicked on Lu Qin’s avatar. He seemed rather indifferent—his profile was completely empty. Shen Muyan pursed her red lips, exited the profile, and opened the group chat window. The group was lively, with messages constantly rolling in.

Shen Muyan quickly caught a key piece of information—Lu Qin would be selling his food at 8 AM at the entrance of Sunshine Elementary School.

But then, her beautiful eyes widened in surprise. The chat kept buzzing, the atmosphere in the group was hot. But why… Why were these nicknames so outrageous?

Shen Muyan stared at the ridiculous nicknames in the group, speechless. The internet is not a lawless land!

Did these people really think they could all be the little one’s stepmom? Wishful thinking!

Closing the group chat, Shen Muyan got out of bed, ready to head to work. Her mood had suddenly soured, so she sent a message to her assistant, Song Xuanxuan.

This was the first time Song Xuanxuan had ever received a message from her boss that wasn’t about work. Instead, it was about breakfast.

When Song Xuanxuan saw the words ‘Ice Crystal Cake’, her eyes lit up.

Wasn’t this the same Ice Crystal Cake she had tried before? If it was…

Without a second thought, Song Xuanxuan checked the time and set off towards Sunshine Elementary an hour early.

…

Meanwhile, Lu Qin was in the kitchen preparing breakfast for Lu Xiyao. The little one was still half-asleep, barely managing to wash her face and brush her teeth before her nose twitched, catching the scent of breakfast.

“Daddy~! I’m ready~!”

Lu Qin glanced at her and noticed she hadn’t washed her face properly.

“Come here.”

He re-wet the towel and carefully wiped her face again. Breakfast was fried eggs, brown sugar buns, and soy milk. The little one ate with enthusiasm, her mouth covered in oil.

Lu Qin handed her a napkin and then packed her lunchbox. Inside were a portion of twice-cooked pork, rice, three kinds of vegetables, and a small serving of stir-fried noodles.

“Daddy~! I promised my friends at kindergarten I’d bring them some stir-fried noodles~!”

“It’s already packed.”

Lu Qin placed another portion of stir-fried noodles into the canvas bag. After finishing breakfast, he dropped off the little one at Starlight Kindergarten.

“Yao Yao, be sure to listen to your teachers and get along with your classmates,” Lu Qin reminded her.

“Daddy~! I know! Your cute little girl—aren’t you confident in me?” Lu Xiyao waved as she walked into the kindergarten, carrying her canvas bag.

The moment she entered, a swarm of children surrounded her from all directions, treating her like a star.

“Lu Xiyao, did you bring lunch today? Do you have an extra portion of stir-fried noodles?”

“Yao Yao, do you want to play with my remote-controlled plane? Can we sit together at lunch today?”

“Lu Xiyao, I just bought some crystal slime. I’ll give you a jar!”

Zhong Shuyi chimed in, “Yao Yao, I asked my family’s chef to make stir-fried noodles and mung bean soup today. Would you like to try some?”

“My grandpa also made stir-fried noodles, but it looks kind of black… not sure if it’s any good!”

Several children eyed the canvas bag in Lu Xiyao’s hand, already salivating, even though they had just finished breakfast.

…

Across from Sunshine Elementary School, a long line had mysteriously formed in an open lot. Strangely, there was nothing at the front of the line.

“What’s going on here? What are you all lining up for?”

“We’re lining up early. Boss Lu is about to set up his morning stall. If you don’t queue up now, you might not get anything.”

“Is it really that good?”

“It’s out-of-this-world amazing!”

A few minutes later, Lu Qin’s tricycle cart appeared in the crowd’s view.

“Boss Lu, over here! We saved a spot for you!”

“I crawled over here! Can you make mine first? It’s a life-or-death situation!”

“Could you come earlier? My boss says starvation doesn’t count as a workplace injury!”

“Boss Lu, stop! Let us help with setting up the stall! You don’t need to do it yourself!”

…

Lu Qin swiftly began cooking the noodles, while the boxes of mung bean soup and Ice Crystal Cake were placed on the side for customers to scan and take by themselves.

As the aroma of the stir-fried noodles filled the air, several pale and skinny customers inhaled deeply, feeling rejuvenated.

The morning customers were in a rush—most were either headed to work or school. They bought their food and left without lingering. Still, some customers were willing to risk being late for the chance to grab a portion of stir-fried noodles, truly living by the saying—A day without it feels unbearable.

After the wave of office workers and students passed, the next batch of customers consisted of retired elderly folks from the nearby neighborhood. They had a slower pace of life, and their usual breakfast staples were soy milk, fried dough sticks, buns, and congee.

They didn’t know what else to eat—until Lu Qin’s stir-fried noodle stall appeared. Now, they all opted for the noodles, leaving other breakfast options tasteless in comparison.

“Little Boss Lu, what’s this? It doesn’t look like a phone number,” an elderly woman asked, pointing at the Penguin group number written on a whiteboard, confused.

“That’s a Penguin group number. The people who join are regular customers. I notify the group where the stall will be set up each day,” Lu Qin explained patiently while cooking.

“This is great! I want to join too. How do I join this Penguin group?” The elderly woman asked, intrigued.

Among the group of elderly retired customers, some were even more tech-savvy than the younger generation. One of them helped the elderly lady register a Penguin account and join the group.

As soon as the elderly lady entered the chat and saw the numerous nicknames like ‘Lu Boss’s Girlfriend’, ‘Lu Boss’s Fiancée’, and ‘Lu Boss’s Wife’, she was taken aback.

Shocked, she exclaimed, “Lu Boss, why do you have so many girlfriends and wives? This isn’t right!”

“Polygamy is illegal!” she added, clearly concerned.

chapters.title: Chapter 43 - You Praised It Without Even Tasting? This Is the Real Ice Crystal Cake!
chapters.content:
Chapter 43 “You Praised It Without Even Tasting? This Is the Real Ice Crystal Cake!”

Faced with the elderly woman’s serious expression, the surrounding customers couldn’t help but burst into laughter.

“What are you all laughing at?”

“Sister, you’ve got to keep up with the times. These Penguin nicknames are just random. They don’t mean anything.”

“Oh, so they’re fake! I was wondering how Lu Boss could have so many wives!”

Lu Qin could only shake his head with a smile. The customers in his Penguin group were too mischievous.

As one wave of customers left, another arrived. Lu Qin’s stall was now so popular that he didn’t have to worry about business at all. Customers often started lining up even before his tricycle cart arrived.

In just an hour and a half each morning, Lu Qin could sell out everything.

At that moment, neighbors from the area near where Lu Qin rented his home arrived. Old Man Ge brought along an elderly man with graying hair.

“Old Ge, it’s rare for you to treat me to a meal, and it’s breakfast, no less!”

“How good can breakfast possibly be?”

Old Man Ge responded mysteriously, “You’ll know after you try it. I’m telling you, Old Mai, even your emperor’s tongue will be satisfied!”

The elderly man, surnamed Mai, was no ordinary person—he had been involved in national-level banquets in the past. Now, he lived a carefree, retired life.

“Oh? I’ll have to see for myself.”

Although Old Mai said this, he didn’t really take it seriously. While street food could be tasty, he had never encountered anything truly impressive from such places. Often, what the masses considered delicious was simply a mix of seasonings, lacking depth upon closer inspection. Street food, in particular, was usually all about the sauce, and the novelty wore off quickly.

Old Man Ge and Old Mai arrived at Lu Qin’s stall.

“Old Ge, you’re here. How many portions of noodles today?”

“Two portions of stir-fried noodles and two mung bean soups. I’ve brought a friend, this is Old Mai.”

“Nice to meet you,” Lu Qin greeted with a smile before starting to cook the noodles.

Old Mai glanced around Lu Qin’s stall and noticed how clean and well-kept it was, even down to the spotless bottom of the wok. People often say that the best food was found in the dirtiest corners. That was nonsense.

For a chef, cleanliness and hygiene were just as important as the cooking itself. If the ingredients weren’t clean, customers wouln’t feel comfortable eating them. Lu Qin seemed to understand this perfectly, earning full marks in this area.

Old Mai was surprised, as he had expected a food stall to be much dirtier. As someone with deep culinary knowledge, Old Mai could tell when someone was skilled. The moment Lu Qin lifted the wok, Old Mai’s casual demeanor disappeared, and his smile faded.

The technique of flipping the noodles wasn’t about brute force but rather a combination of skill and experience. It required finesse.

Lu Qin’s wrist movement was masterful—the noodles flipped gracefully without breaking apart. The precise control of heat and the strength of the flips determined the taste and texture of the stir-fried noodles.

This level of expertise only came with seven or eight years of experience, something Old Mai could instantly recognize.

If another chef were using the same wok, spatula, and ingredients, the result would still taste completely different.

“Young man, your skills are quite impressive,” Old Mai praised sincerely.

Old Man Ge was puzzled.

“Old Mai, you’re already praising him without even tasting the food?”

Given Old Mai’s high standing in the southern culinary world, earning his approval wasn’t easy. Old Man Ge had brought him here because he had complete confidence in Lu Qin’s skills, but even he was surprised that Old Mai was complimenting the food before even tasting it. The stir-fried noodles were ready.

Old Mai first smelled the dish, then inspected its appearance. After saying ‘good’ twice, he finally tasted a bite. The smooth and delicate texture made Old Mai’s eyes light up.

“It’s even better than I imagined.”

He closed his eyes, savoring the taste, and then quickly took a second and third bite…

By the time he finished the entire portion, Old Mai let out a long breath.

“Delicious!”

Mai Dejun looked closely at Lu Qin, this young and rather handsome man, amazed that he possessed such impressive culinary skills.

The world really is full of talented people, he thought.

Seeing Mai Dejun showering Lu Qin with praise, Old Man Ge couldn’t help but feel a bit proud, as if Lu Qin were his own son, receiving admiration for his accomplishments.

“Here, Old Mai, try the mung bean soup made by Little Lu,” Old Man Ge offered.

“Mung bean soup?”

Mai Dejun took a sip, finding it smooth and sandy, sweet without being overly rich.

“This has a very nice texture. You’ve added aged tangerine peel and yellow rock sugar, haven’t you?”

“Yes, you’re absolutely right,” Lu Qin nodded, glancing at Mai Dejun. He could tell that this man was likely a chef as well.

“Old Mai, there’s also the Ice Crystal Cake—now that’s a real treat,” Old Man Ge said, his pride swelling once more.

“Little Lu, even though Old Mai and I came together, each of us can still buy two boxes of Ice Crystal Cake, right?”

“Yes, that’s correct,” Lu Qin nodded.

Old Man Ge paid by scanning the QR code and then grabbed four boxes of Ice Crystal Cake, handing two of them to Mai Dejun.

Upon hearing the words ‘Ice Crystal Cake’, Mai Dejun paused for a moment, feeling a sense of familiarity. He thought for a bit and suddenly realized—his apprentice had called him just yesterday asking about Ice Crystal Cake. He had assumed that it was a rare, local dessert, and not many people knew how to make it. Little did he know, the young man in front of him was the very person making it. What a coincidence!

With high expectations, Mai Dejun opened one of the boxes. He had tried Ice Crystal Cake before, but the memory of it was vague. When he was young, during a trip to a small northern town, he had seen someone selling this treat from a street vendor.

Essentially, Ice Crystal Cake was a type of rice cake made from glutinous rice, with an extra layer of icy skin that gave a cool, refreshing sensation when eaten, similar to a chilled dessert.

However, the Ice Crystal Cake Mai Dejun had tasted back then was only average—overly sweet and sticky. He had bought it once and never again, so it left only a faint impression.

Now, looking at the Ice Crystal Cake made by Lu Qin, Mai Dejun’s eyes lit up. This version was exquisitely crafted—each piece a perfect 4 cm square, translucent like a fine jade. The surface was delicately adorned with osmanthus honey and marigold leaves. Through the icy skin, the inner layers’ intricate textures were clearly visible.

Mai Dejun couldn’t resist picking up a piece. The smooth texture was like polished glass in his hands. He took a bite, and a refreshing chill immediately enveloped his senses. It was nothing like the Ice Crystal Cake he had tasted in his youth.

“This—this is the real Ice Crystal Cake!”

Mai Dejun closed his eyes, savoring the unique sensation. The initial sweetness was cool and light, but after a few chews, a rich, deep flavor unfolded, leaving a lingering fragrance in his mouth. It was unforgettable.

As a national banquet-level chef, although retired, Mai Dejun had spent his life tasting countless delicacies. He had even worked with some of the top pastry chefs in the country, yet none of them had ever produced a dessert that could compare to Lu Qin’s Ice Crystal Cake.

Of the hundreds of types of Chinese pastries Mai Dejun had tried, this Ice Crystal Cake was the only one that left him truly astonished!

After swallowing the first piece, Mai Dejun couldn’t help but ask, “Little Boss Lu, how exactly do you make this Ice Crystal Cake?”

chapters.title: Chapter 44 - Incredible! Lu Qin Is Really Popular!
chapters.content:
Chapter 44 Incredible! Lu Qin Is Really Popular!

As soon as the question left his mouth, Mai Dejun regretted it. Culinary skills weren’t something they could easily share with others, especially niche pastries like the Ice Crystal Cake, which could very well be a proprietary recipe.

Old Mai, the method for making this Ice Crystal Cake is Boss Lu’s business secret. How could he possibly share it with you? Old Master Ge thought to himself, Has Mai Dejun, who’s been in the culinary world for so many years, forgotten the basic rules?

“ Cough, cough… Haha… This Ice Crystal Cake is just too delicious. It’s so good, I’m losing my sense of direction!”

Mai Dejun quickly changed the subject.

Lu Qin simply smiled and didn’t mind. Actually, there wasn’t anything he couldn’t say—it was just that explaining it would be too complicated. The process of making Ice Crystal Cake was too intricate, requiring attention to countless details. It was something he could not just explain easily.

As Mai Dejun continued savoring the cake, he studied Lu Qin, filled with curiosity. Being a chef is different from other professions—anyone could have talent, but the most important thing is dedication and hard work.

Skills like cutting, stir-frying, and dough-making weren’t just a matter of talent, they required a lot of practice and time. Even with a master’s guidance, it took countless days and nights to develop a pair of skilled hands. Lu Qin was so young, not even thirty, yet he had such remarkable culinary skills. It was truly unbelievable!

Old Master Ge was eating his stir-fried noodles while observing Mai Dejun. Something seemed off about the way Mai was looking at young Lu.

It almost felt like he was sizing him up as a potential son-in-law, with admiration and fondness in his eyes. Ge remembered that Mai’s daughter was already married, though… Could it be… divorced?

Old Master Ge wasn’t feeling too happy about it.

“I brought you here to enjoy some good food, and you’re trying to ‘steal him away’? It’s a pity my own son isn’t very ambitious. Otherwise, if I had a daughter, I’d tie someone like Lu up and take him home as a son-in-law.”

Among the neighbors who came for breakfast, Wang Guifen and Liu Dama were the most concerned—and nosiest—about Lu Qin’s personal life. After buying their stir-fried noodles and Ice Crystal Cakes, they just couldn’t leave.

“Boss Lu, about that girl Aunt Wang mentioned yesterday—are you interested?”

“No need to talk about her looks. She’s very pretty, has a great figure, about 5’5″, and is hardworking. She even runs her own clothing store.”

“She’s got great qualities, but Aunt Wang hasn’t been able to find someone who matches her. Boss Lu, you’re a perfect match!”

“She tried your stir-fried noodles and Ice Crystal Cake and took quite a liking to you! As they say, the way to a woman’s heart is through her stomach!”

Faced with Wang Guifen’s enthusiasm, Lu Qin could only smile wryly.

“Aunt Wang, I just got divorced. I’m not ready to think about these things yet.”

“Oh dear, that’s exactly why you need to start dating again! The best way to get over a past relationship is to start a new one. Aunt Wang understands you young folks!”

Liu Dama, not wanting to be outdone, immediately pulled out her phone and jumped into the conversation.

“Boss Lu, take a look at this one. A wealthy beauty—her family runs a big company, way better than Wang Guifen’s girl. If you’re interested, I’ll give you her number, and you can add her on WeChat…”

Seeing these two matchmakers going all out, Old Master Ge and Mai Dejun exchanged glances.

Lu Qin was really popular!

Mai Dejun glanced at the small whiteboard hanging on the food cart, which read.

He quickly asked, “Boss Lu, do you still sell twice-cooked pork and chicken tofu soup?”

“Hey, stop interrupting us! We’re talking about serious matters with Boss Lu!” Wang Guifen and Liu Dama weren’t pleased about being interrupted. If they could successfully set Lu Qin up, it would bring them face-saving glory.

Old Master Ge chimed in, “Don’t bother Lu while he’s doing business. Does he look like someone eager to date right now?”

He sounded serious but was actually just trying to prevent the two women from convincing Lu to go along with their matchmaking. If he couldn’t have Lu, then no one could. Lu Qin belonged to everyone, and no one should be greedy.

To escape Wang Guifen and Liu Dama, Lu quickly replied to Mai Dejun, “I don’t sell twice-cooked pork or chicken tofu soup in the morning stall, and I didn’t prepare the ingredients. I only sell them in the afternoon and evening.”

“Where are you setting up in the afternoon and evening? I have to try your twice-cooked pork and chicken tofu soup,” Mai Dejun pressed.

“How about we exchange contact information?”

Lu Qin doesn’t add customers’ contacts privately, so he pointed to the small whiteboard and said, “Sir, you can join the QQ group. I post the location of my stall every day in the group.”

“QQ group? I don’t know how to use that. Can’t you just give me your contact info?”

“It’s not very convenient,” Lu Qin replied with an apologetic smile. He knew he couldn’t set a precedent for giving out his contact information. If he did, everyone would start asking for it, or worse, his number might get leaked, which would be a huge hassle.

Mai Dejun was taken aback—he hadn’t expected to be turned down when offering to exchange contact info. Old Master Ge stood by, amused, thinking, Old Mai, who would’ve thought you’d get turned down like this?

But then, out of concern for Lu Qin, Old Master Ge spoke up, “Little Lu, Old Mai is from the culinary world too, and he’s quite influential. He’s not just any customer—you should exchange contacts with him.”

Mai Dejun wasn’t just any chef, he was a national banquet chef with apprentices all over the country. Many prominent figures couldn’t even get his contact info, so Lu Qin didn’t realize the importance of this connection. It would be a shame to miss out on such an opportunity.

Mai Dejun coughed, assuming a more imposing demeanor, without saying a word. Now he was trying to play hard to get, hoping Lu Qin would offer his contact information on his own. After all, where would his pride be if he were turned down again?

But Lu Qin remained firm, saying, “It’s better to just join the QQ group, sir. I can help you join it.”

He really didn’t want to add anyone—he wasn’t familiar with Mai Dejun, so there was no need to exchange contacts.

Mai Dejun’s eyes widened, shocked at being rejected a second time.

“ Hmph! Fine, no need then.”

“Let’s go, Old Ge,” he said, hands behind his back as he walked away.

Old Master Ge glanced at Lu Qin, then at Mai Dejun, his expression somewhat strange, but he felt a bit smug inside.

“Alright, Lu, we’ll head off now. We’ll come back tomorrow to get your stir-fried noodles.”

“Okay, take care, Old Master Ge,” Lu Qin replied.

As that group of neighbors left, another wave of customers arrived, and Lu Qin continued running his stall.

“Young man, how much for the stir-fried noodles?” One customer asked.

“Plain noodles are 8 yuan per serving, with an additional 1 yuan for eggs, 2 yuan for ham, and 2 yuan for minced meat.”

“I’ll take two servings with everything added.”

“Alright, please wait a moment.”

Lu Qin poured some oil into the wok and began stir-frying the noodles.

“Young man, you’re quite skilled!” The customer remarked.

“If you’re in this business, you have to be,” Lu Qin replied with a smile.

Chen Dahai, dressed in simple clothes and wearing a pair of old cloth shoes, had a pair of reading glasses hanging around his neck. He looked like a typical old man. Standing at Lu Qin’s stall with his hands behind his back, he watched both the cooking noodles and Lu Qin.

Not bad, Chen thought to himself.

Old Shen’s daughter had a good eye after all. This young man wasn’t bad-looking, tall, clean, and neat—quite pleasing to the eye.

Chen Dahai’s first impression of Lu Qin was much better than those rich young men he had seen before.

chapters.title: Chapter 45 - Shen Muyan's Mood Was Delightful! Chen Dahai Was Shocked!
chapters.content:
Chapter 45 Shen Muyan’s Mood Was Delightful! Chen Dahai Was Shocked!

At the Ourei International Hotel, a cup of skimmed mocha without cream sat on Shen Muyan’s desk. This had been her habit for many years—she had to drink a cup of coffee every morning. But today, she hadn’t touched her coffee. She didn’t want to spoil her appetite.

A moment later, Song Xuanxuan pushed the door open and entered, holding breakfast from Sunshine Primary School. By 8 AM in Jiangcheng, the sun was already blazing, and just walking a few steps outside could make you sweat.

Song Xuanxuan, who was already sweating and a little out of breath, usually solved her breakfast problem at the Western or Chinese-style cafes downstairs from the hotel.

Today, for the first time, she had gone all the way to a place three kilometers away to get breakfast. She had no choice. President Shen wanted food from that place, and Song Xuanxuan had to oblige. Besides, she was craving it herself. She had tried the Ice Crystal cake there before and couldn’t forget it.

When she arrived by taxi at the entrance of Sunshine Primary School, she was surprised to see so many people lined up at a small fried rice noodle stall. She had waited in line for a full twenty minutes before she finally got the food.

“President Shen, I’ve brought the breakfast.”

Song Xuanxuan placed a serving of fried noodles, a cup of mung bean soup, and a box of Ice Crystal cakes on Shen Muyan’s desk.

Shen Muyan’s beautiful eyes lingered on the Ice Crystal cake, and in a cool voice, she said, “I asked for two boxes of Ice Crystal cake.” Then she looked at the other box of Ice Crystal cake still in Song Xuanxuan’s hand.

“President Shen, eating so much for breakfast isn’t good. One box is enough!” Song Xuanxuan made a slightly aggrieved face. She had waited so long in line to get it. She had been thinking about that Ice Crystal cake for a while now! The owner of the noodle stall was quite handsome, but why did they have to impose a purchase limit?

The limit was really annoying. Otherwise, she would have bought four boxes—two for Shen Muyan and two for herself! Now, there were only two boxes, and the boss wanted both? Not leaving one for her? How cruel!

“Who said the Ice Crystal cake was for breakfast? I’m saving it for later when I get hungry,” Shen Muyan said. “And put the other box on the desk too.”

With a tragic expression, Song Xuanxuan reluctantly placed the other box of Ice Crystal cake on Shen Muyan’s desk. Her boss was too heartless! Not even leaving her one box!

Boo-hoo!

She left the office, sat at her desk, and opened the fried noodles. Eating in the office was generally not allowed, but Shen Muyan and her secretary were exceptions since they often worked past mealtime. The fragrance of the fried noodles immediately lifted Song Xuanxuan’s spirits.

“These noodles smell so good!” She quickly grabbed her chopsticks and took a bite. The taste was heavenly! Halfway through wolfing it down, she remembered the mung bean soup and took a sip. It was so good, she could practically feel bubbles of joy popping!

As expected, the handsome owner who made such delicious Ice Crystal cakes also made amazing fried noodles and mung bean soup. But Song Xuanxuan hadn’t anticipated just how good they would taste! If she had known, she would have bought two portions of the fried noodles—one simply wasn’t enough!

Meanwhile, in the office, Shen Muyan hadn’t eaten breakfast for years. Typically, she only had a cup of coffee before diving into her busy workday. She had no appetite for regular breakfast food. Until…

Tasting Lu Qin’s Ice Crystal cake had truly awakened Shen Muyan’s long-dormant taste buds. She opened the box of fried rice noodles, and the rich aroma of sauce and the scent of rice noodles immediately filled the air. Her throat moved as she swallowed hard. Why did his fried noodles smell so delicious? The tempting fragrance made it nearly impossible to resist tasting it.

She picked up her chopsticks and took a bite—absolutely delicious! These were the best fried noodles she had ever tasted! One bite, two bites, three bites… she couldn’t stop eating! Unconsciously, she ate with little regard for her image, taking big bites, her rosy lips glistening with oil.

Paired with the sweet and soft mung bean soup, it felt like her soul was being uplifted. It was an incredible flavor she had never experienced before!

“Burp~!”

In the quiet office, an unexpected sound broke the silence. Shen Muyan’s delicate face blushed slightly. It was the first time in years that she had eaten so much for breakfast, completely absorbed in the delicious food. By the time she finished, her stomach was full. She felt completely satisfied and in a much better mood.

Looking at the two boxes of Ice Crystal cake still on her desk, she felt even happier. When two department managers came in to report, they were astonished to find that the usually cold and serious president was speaking to them with a rare warmth and gentleness. It felt surreal!

Meanwhile, at the entrance, Song Xuanxuan was surrounded by her female colleagues, all offering bribes of milk tea and potato chips, begging her to reveal where she had bought the delicious fried noodles.

Drawn by the irresistible aroma, they had noticed Song Xuanxuan devouring her meal. After much pleading, they each took a small bite, only to discover that these noodles were out of this world!

“Cute and lovely Xuanxuan, please tell us where you got these noodles!”

“Our beautiful secretary, please, I’ll buy you a fresh milk tea, less ice, half sugar!”

“This divine fried noodle is too good! Where on earth did you buy it? Is it near the hotel?”

In no time, Song Xuanxuan became the most popular person around.

At the entrance of Sunshine Primary School, Chen Dahai was eating fried noodles while observing Lu Qin’s bustling business. It was hard to believe that a small fried noodle stall could have such booming business—it was practically a phenomenon.

The key lay in Lu Qin’s extraordinary cooking skills. His food was so delicious that it was impossible to get tired of it, each bite feeling like it was pulling your soul in. Ever since Chen Dahai had tasted the Ice Crystal cake, he had been craving it constantly. He felt an inexplicable discomfort whenever he couldn’t get his hands on it.

For someone as wealthy as Chen Dahai, who had tasted countless delicacies, the fact that he couldn’t stop thinking about this food showed just how extraordinary it was. Now, it wasn’t just the Ice Crystal cake that had won him over—it was also the fried noodles and mung bean soup.

“Heh! This Lu Qin… why is everything he makes so tasty?” Chen Dahai couldn’t help but gossip a little. Old Shen’s daughter must have been out of her mind to divorce such a good husband!

Having met countless people, Chen Dahai could tell that Lu Qin wasn’t the type to betray a relationship. This young man definitely had good character.

Hmm, I’m not wrong about him, Chen Dahai thought, making his initial judgment about Lu Qin. His box of fried noodles was already empty, and he had finished slurping down the mung bean soup as well. His appetite had fully kicked in, and he thought he could easily eat another serving of fried noodles.

The main reason was that Lu Qin’s noodles were just too good—if it were any other food, he wouldn’t have been able to eat even a bite more. As for the two boxes of Ice Crystal cake, he couldn’t bear to eat them yet, he wanted to take them home and savor them slowly.

“Young man, give me another serving of fried noodles, and add all the toppings,” Chen Dahai said, queuing up for another five minutes.

“Sorry, sir, the fried noodles are sold out,” the vendor replied.

“What? Sold out already? Why didn’t you prepare more ingredients?”

“What about the mung bean soup?”

“Also sold out.”

“Ugh!!!”

Chen Dahai was filled with regret. He should’ve ordered two servings of noodles and mung bean soup earlier. The business was just too good!

He had missed out by a minute. If only he had been as smart as the little chubby kid who bought five or six servings of noodles in one go. Smart move!

chapters.title: Chapter 46 - Leave the Rough Work to Us! Your Hands Are Meant For Making Delicious Food, Don’t Hurt Them!
chapters.content:
Chapter 46 Leave the Rough Work to Us! Your Hands Are Meant For Making Delicious Food, Don’t Hurt Them!

As Lu Qin packed up his stall for the day, several customers came over to help.

“Boss Lu, let me take care of it. How can we let you handle something as trivial as packing up the stall?”

“Exactly, Boss Lu. This is hard work, and your hands are precious. What if you can’t make noodles because of an injury?”

“Boss, where will you set up the stall this afternoon? Will you have twice-cooked pork and chicken tofu? I’m already craving it!”

“Boss Lu, I’m spending my whole salary at your food stall each month—you have to take care of me!”

In reality, cleaning up didn’t take long, and Lu Qin didn’t really need their help, but the customers insisted on pitching in. The last few patrons who had just finished their fried noodles and Ice Crystal cakes set their food down and started helping Lu Qin tidy up the stall. They even wiped down the stove.

Lu Qin couldn’t help but smile wryly at how adorable his customers were. Watching this scene, Chen Dahai nodded to himself. People often say, “the customer is king,” but with Lu Qin, it seemed the reverse was true.

“Boss Lu, ride safely and take care on the road.”

“Will we see your daughter this afternoon? I miss her!”

Lu Qin got on his tricycle, waved to them with a smile, and said, “Goodbye, eat the noodles while they’re hot. They won’t taste as good cold. My daughter is still at kindergarten this afternoon, but I’ll bring her with me tonight.”

“Oh, what a shame! I’ll be working late tonight. See you!” One customer lamented.

Just as Lu Qin was about to start pedaling, Chen Dahai stepped in front of him.

“Young Boss Lu, where are you setting up the stall this afternoon? I want to try your twice-cooked pork and chicken tofu!”

Chen Dahai wasn’t just interested in Lu Qin’s food—he also wanted to observe the man a bit more.

“Join the QQ group, I will announce the stall’s location there.”

“I’m an old man, I don’t know much about QQ. Could I just get your phone number instead?”

Chen Dahai had already done some research on Lu Qin and had his phone number saved in his contacts, but he wasn’t sure how to approach him with it. For now, his only identity was that of a customer.

“Sorry, it’s not really convenient. But give me your phone, and I’ll help you join the group—it’s easy.”

Chen Dahai handed over his phone after unlocking it, and Lu Qin added him to the group.

“All done. Keep an eye on the group this afternoon. I’ll post the stall location there.”

“Alright, thanks, Young Boss Lu.”

“You’re welcome.”

Chen Dahai watched Lu Qin ride off and then checked the QQ group. There were already 523 members, and the chat was buzzing non-stop.

Chen Dahai scrolled through the messages, realizing that everyone in the group was a foodie, and it seemed he had become one too. Meeting a chef like Lu Qin was truly a blessing for food lovers.

Lu Qin sped down the road on his tricycle. With his full-level tricycle driving skills, courtesy of his system’s lottery, his efficiency was maxed out.

What used to take him 20 minutes now only took 12 or 13. Of course, he only drove this aggressively when he was alone—if his daughter or fragile ingredients were on board, he prioritized safety.

He parked the tricycle in front of ‘Jingyuan Vegetable Market’. Although the market was small, it had a wide variety of fresh produce, saving Lu Qin the hassle of running around to different places.

“Hey! Young Boss Lu, you’re here!”

It was Boss Zhao, who sold disposable tableware. He was unloading goods at the entrance of the market and, upon seeing Lu Qin, immediately walked over with a smile, pulling a cigarette from his pocket.

“I don’t smoke,” Lu Qin politely declined.

“Ah, I always forget!” Boss Zhao shrugged and lit the cigarette himself.

“I’m heading to Haishi next door for supplies today, so I won’t be able to deliver your tableware and ingredients. I’m afraid you’ll have to come pick them up yourself, Young Boss Lu.”

“You shouldn’t always go through the trouble, Boss Zhao. It’s only right that I come and buy them myself.”

“Don’t be so formal! Every time I deliver, you give me fried noodles and Ice Crystal cakes—I’m the one getting a great deal! I’ve been to your food stall twice, and I couldn’t even get in line. Your business is out of control!”

After exchanging a few more pleasantries, Boss Zhao turned and shouted into his store, “Hey, kid! Bring out the goods and help load them onto Young Boss Lu’s tricycle!”

“Got it!” replied a boy wearing black-framed glasses, walking out with a large box of tableware. Lu Qin recognized him—it was Boss Zhao’s son, a middle schooler who would come to the shop for lunch and help out whenever he could.

“Take it easy,” Lu Qin said, rushing to steady the box, which looked like it might slip.

Boss Zhao waved his hand dismissively. “Don’t worry, Young Boss Lu, these tableware boxes aren’t that heavy!”

Despite his slim frame, Zhao’s son was surprisingly strong and quickly loaded the boxes onto Lu Qin’s tricycle food cart.

“Hi, Brother Lu~!” the boy greeted with admiration shining in his eyes behind his glasses.

“Call him Uncle Lu, not Brother Lu,” Boss Zhao corrected with a light smack to the back of the boy’s head.

Lu Qin smiled and pulled out two boxes of Ice Crystal cakes from the bottom of his foam container.

“These two boxes didn’t sell out—take them and enjoy.”

Zhao’s son immediately brightened up, practically dancing with joy.

“Brother Lu! I love you! I’ve been craving these cakes for so long!”

“You just had them two days ago, you little foodie!”

Boss Zhao shook his head.

“Mom! Brother Lu gave me two boxes of Ice Crystal cakes!”

The boy ran excitedly into the shop, clutching the treats. Soon, an excited voice came from inside, belonging to Zhao’s wife.

Boss Zhao immediately called out, “Wife, son! Don’t finish them all! Save some for me!”

“Young Boss Lu, let me transfer you the money. I can’t keep eating your food for free, especially when I’m still making money on the tableware!”

“No need. You always throw in extra tableware and deliver my ingredients!”

After a brief, polite back-and-forth, Lu Qin refused the payment, and Boss Zhao didn’t press further. He had always prided himself on being a good judge of character, and from the first time he interacted with Lu Qin, he knew he was a decent guy. Time had proven him right.

Most importantly, Lu Qin’s cooking skills were top-notch, and his food stall was always packed with customers. Boss Zhao had even seen Lu Qin trending on Douyin (the Chinese version of TikTok).

It was thanks to his connection with Lu Qin that he could occasionally enjoy those legendary fried noodles and Ice Crystal cakes. Considering how hard it was to get those dishes, he felt like the lucky one.

Lu Qin then entered the market to purchase ingredients for twice-cooked pork and chicken tofu.

chapters.title: Chapter 47 - Lu Xiyao was Crying! Lu Qin Arrived Immediately!
chapters.content:
Chapter 47 Lu Xiyao was Crying! Lu Qin Arrived Immediately!

Lu Qin headed to the pork stall, the same one he used to buy from.

“Mr. Guo, I’m buying pork. I need pork butt—how many pounds do you have?”

Mr. Guo recognized his regular customer, especially one who bought in large quantities, and greeted him with a smile.

“Ah, it’s young Mr. Lu! You’ve come at the right time. I just brought fresh pork from the slaughterhouse, and the pork butt hasn’t even been touched yet!”

Lu Qin took a look, nodded, and said, “Trim off the tip, and I’ll take however many pounds there are.”

“Alright, just a moment. I’ll weigh it.”

It turned out to be 28 pounds of pork butt, tender and fine-textured—a great cut of meat. Mr. Guo offered him the lowest price, 16.5 yuan per pound. Lu Qin scanned to pay, totaling 462 yuan. Next, he needed to buy ingredients for making Chicken Tofu.

According to the traditional Chicken Tofu recipe, it requires a whole free-range, grain-fed hen, from which only half a pound of breast meat is kept, and 20 egg whites. Following this traditional process, a batch of Chicken Tofu for three or four people costs over 200 yuan.

However, with a variety of available ingredients now, chicken breast could be purchased separately, and there was no longer a strict need for free-range, farm-raised chickens.

As for the flavor, Lu Qin’s skill in making Chicken Tofu was top-notch, so he could make up for any ingredient flaws with his technique. As for nutrition, these days, people generally had suboptimal health and didn’t need to focus too much on nutrition, which could lead to overnutrition anyway.

Taste is king.

Fresh, high-quality chicken breast was 15 yuan per pound. After a bit of haggling, Lu Qin purchased 20 pounds at 14.6 yuan per pound. Chicken frames were 5.4 yuan per pound, and Lu Qin bought three pounds. The chicken frames weren’t for eating but for making chicken broth. Rounding off, Lu Qin scanned and paid 308 yuan.

After wandering for another 20 minutes, Lu Qin bought 50 pounds of eggs, rice wine, garlic sprouts, and other ingredients. He loaded everything onto his three-wheeled food cart and was ready to head home.

At that moment, his phone rang. Seeing the caller ID, he was surprised—it was Teacher Li from kindergarten.

He frowned slightly.

Could it be…?

Quickly, he picked up the call.

The entrance of the market was quite noisy, so he couldn’t hear very clearly. However, he was sure he could hear Lu Xiyao’s crying in the background.

Lu Qin’s heart skipped a beat.

He said, “Teacher Li, I’ll be there in ten minutes.”

Then, he sped off on his three-wheeler.

Starlight Kindergarten was about three kilometers from the market. Lu Qin was very anxious, he ran two red lights on his way and rushed to the school. It was lunchtime for the kids when he arrived.

Standing at the retractable gate, Lu Qin immediately called Teacher Li. His mind was racing with worry, wondering if his little one had fallen, hit something, or had an upset stomach—so many possibilities ran through his mind.

When Teacher Li saw the worried expression on Lu Qin’s face, she smiled wryly and shook her head.

“Yao Yao’s dad, Yao Yao is fine. She just accidentally spilled her lunch, and she cried.”

Lu Yao’s dad was so anxious about his daughter that he rushed over without checking the actual situation. But Teacher Li couldn’t help but feel impressed by his deep concern for his daughter. He truly was a good father.

No wonder Yao Yao always seemed happy and carefree every day, radiating joy. Under Teacher Li’s guidance, Lu Qin entered the kindergarten dining hall.

Once inside, a group of kids crowded around a square table in the middle. On the floor, a Doraemon lunchbox had spilled. Stir-fried rice noodles, twice-cooked pork, tomato and egg stir-fry, and green bean soup were scattered everywhere. The rich aroma still lingered in the air. Several kids were eyeing the food on the floor, even itching to pick some up and eat it.

Lu Qin looked over to see his little one surrounded in the middle of the group, her chubby cheeks still streaked with tears. A boy next to her kept his head down, repeatedly apologizing.

“Lu Xiyao, I didn’t mean to spill your lunch! You can have mine. It has beef and fried eggs.”

“But your lunch isn’t as tasty as what her dad makes, Chen Haojie! How could you? Those noodles looked so good!”

“There was twice-cooked pork too! Did you do it on purpose, Chen Haojie?”

“Yao Yao’s portion of noodles fell too—boo hoo, now we don’t get to eat any!”

…

Lu Xiyao didn’t blame the little boy, but losing her lunch had left her feeling down and clearly unhappy. The other kids, however, kept scolding him. Zhong Shuyi, who had wanted everyone to taste the fried rice noodles and green bean soup made by her family’s chef, had tried some earlier but didn’t want a second bite. Compared to Yao Yao’s dad’s cooking, it didn’t even come close.

Why does the same dish taste so different? She wondered as she closed her lunchbox and joined the others in chastising Chen Haojie. If Yao Yao’s lunch hadn’t spilled, maybe she could have shared a bite or two with her friend.

Now there was nothing left for anyone!

“Yao Yao.”

Lu Qin called out to his little girl.

Still upset, she suddenly heard a voice she knew all too well. She looked up quickly and saw Lu Qin.

“Daddy!”

First, she was thrilled. Then, a hint of sadness returned as she ran into his arms. Lu Qin lifted her up, gently patting her back and pulled out a tissue to wipe her nose. Now that he knew it was only a spilled lunchbox, he breathed a sigh of relief, feeling he’d overreacted a bit.

“You’re not three anymore, do you still need to cry over a spilled lunch?”

He playfully tapped her nose.

The moment Lu Qin appeared, her frustration vanished, replaced by happiness. She clung to his neck, not wanting to be put down.

Having Daddy was the best!

The other kids looked at Lu Qin with admiration. A few who were close to Yao Yao politely said, “Hello, Uncle.”

Did Yao Yao’s dad come to bring her lunch? they wondered, with envy in their eyes.

“Everyone, your food’s on the table. If it gets cold, your tummy might hurt. Go eat your lunch,” Teacher Li said, ushering the group back to their seats.

Chen Haojie, blushing, was also hungry but felt bad about spilling Yao Yao’s lunch. He hadn’t dared eat his own meal and had wanted to give it to her. Zhong Shuyi hadn’t touched her own rice noodles, either—they were too dry and greasy. Her family’s chef simply didn’t match up to Yao Yao’s dad.

“Daddy, I’m hungry!”

She looked at Lu Qin with big, sad eyes.

Teacher Li smiled wryly and explained, “Yao Yao’s dad, her lunch fell because of Chen Haojie, and she won’t eat the kindergarten food, so she started crying. I didn’t know what else to do, so I called you…”

Feeling a bit embarrassed, Yao Yao pouted and said, “I only want to eat what Daddy makes.”

Lu Qin ruffled Chen Haojie’s hair and said with a smile, “Go eat your lunch, okay?”

The boy looked up at Lu Qin’s kind smile, and his heart felt calmer. Nodding, Chen Haojie went back to his seat.

“Shuyi, you’re not eating?”

Lu Qin recognized Shuyi as Yao Yao’s close friend, often mentioned by his daughter. Shuyi looked at him hopefully and pleaded.

“Uncle, my rice noodles aren’t any good. I want to eat yours!”

chapters.title: Chapter 48 - All the Kids Wanted to be Lu Xiyao’s Dad’s Friends!
chapters.content:
Chapter 48 All the Kids Wanted to be Lu Xiyao’s Dad’s Friends!

In the Starlight Kindergarten dining hall, while the other children appeared to be eating, their ears were actually tuned in to what was happening with Lu Xiyao.

The moment they heard Zhong Shuyi ask Lu Qin to make rice noodles for her, little eyes lit up around the room.

“Uncle, I want to eat your stir-fried noodles too!”

“Yao Yao’s dad, I want to eat your twice-cooked pork!”

“Me too!”

“Me too!”

“Me too!”

…

A group of kids stopped eating their own lunches, all turning to stare at Lu Qin. Teacher Li, at a loss, gently turned their little heads back to their meals.

“Come on now, eat your own food.”

But after just a couple of bites, all their heads swiveled back in Lu Qin’s direction.

“Daddy! I’ll eat the kindergarten food instead,” Lu Xiyao said. She didn’t want her dad to go through any extra trouble, thinking she could hold off until the evening to eat his cooking.

Lu Qin gently patted her head. She had even shed a few tears just now, which was unusual for his strong little girl, who normally wouldn’t cry over minor bumps or scrapes. It was clear she truly missed his cooking.

He turned to Teacher Li.

“Teacher, my daughter would really like to eat my cooking. Could I possibly borrow the kitchen? I have some ingredients in my cart, and if it’s alright, I’d love to prepare a couple of dishes for her.”

The portable stove in his three-wheeler cart couldn’t do much more than basic heating, and he didn’t even have a proper kitchen knife, so cooking on the cart wasn’t an option. He’d need access to the kindergarten kitchen. Although he knew the school had strict rules, he hoped they might make an exception.

Teacher Li thought about it for a moment. Technically, it wasn’t against the rules, but she wasn’t sure if the kitchen staff would allow it.

“I’ll check with the kitchen. If they agree, you’re welcome to use it,” she said.

“Thank you so much, Teacher Li.”

Excited, Lu Xiyao gave her dad a kiss on the cheek and cheered.

“Daddy, you’re the best!”

Zhong Shuyi, full of admiration, added, “Yao Yao, your dad is really amazing!”

She was envious. Her dad was always busy with work and didn’t even know what she liked to eat, let alone cook for her. Plus, her dad couldn’t cook at all.

Many of the other kids also looked on with envy. At this moment, only Yao Yao’s dad was there; none of their own parents were around. They couldn’t help but feel a bit of jealousy.

“Yao Yao is so lucky to have such a wonderful dad!”

Seeing the looks from the other kids, Yao Yao felt incredibly proud, hugging her dad’s neck even tighter, refusing to let go. Not long after, Teacher Li returned with the good news, the kitchen staff had agreed to let Lu Qin use the space. Yao Yao cheered in excitement.

Lu Qin took out the pork and some seasonings, then followed Teacher Li to the kitchen, with Yao Yao toddling close behind him, determined not to be separated from her dad.

Inside the kitchen were a head chef and three assistants, who had already finished preparing lunch and were leisurely cleaning up. When Lu Qin entered with the ingredients, the staff eyed him curiously. So, this was Yao Yao’s dad? He didn’t look like someone who could cook!

Lately, little Yao Yao had been bringing her own lunch, leaving the other kids drooling with envy. The teachers knew all about it, and word had even reached the kitchen.

They were curious—could stir-fried noodles really be that good?

The head chef nodded at Lu Qin, his face expressionless.

“You can use this stove and the kitchen tools. If you need any help, these three assistants can lend a hand.”

“Thank you. I can handle it on my own; just making a couple of simple dishes for my daughter,” Lu Qin replied as he got to work.

The head chef and his three assistants stood by, watching him closely. The food at Starlight Kindergarten was already known for being quite good, with flavorful, hearty dishes—not like other kindergartens where the meals were plain and oil-free, in the name of ‘healthy carbs, protein, and fiber’.

Here, everything was carefully prepared to be tasty, but even so, the kids were still picky sometimes, especially wanting fried rice noodles and green bean soup that Lu Xiyao’s dad supposedly made. They said it was incredibly delicious. And now, here he was—just like the man in the story of ‘Cao Cao arrives when you speak of him’.

The head chef and his assistants watched Lu Qin intently as he moved through each step. Teacher Li was also observing him cook, while two female teachers stood by the kitchen door, peeking in.

“Daddy! I can help you wash the vegetables!” said Lu Xiyao, eager to help just as she did at home. Though she could only wash the vegetables and wasn’t allowed near anything sharp, she was enthusiastic.

Lu Qin nodded.

“Then wash the garlic shoots and the bell peppers.”

“Okay!” She chirped, looking around before dragging over a small stool to reach the sink and get started.

Teacher Li was taken aback. She hadn’t expected Lu Xiyao to be so proactive and helpful. At this age, most kids were only interested in playing with toys or watching cartoons.

Parents usually treated their children like precious treasures and wouldn’t let them do any chores. Just getting the children to put their plates on the collection tray was already an achievement, and some kids still wouldn’t follow through, running off as soon as they finished.

Yet here was Lu Xiyao, actively helping to wash vegetables—a real surprise. Teacher Li wondered how Lu Qin had managed to teach her so well. Looking at her, everyone felt a sudden desire to have a little helper just like her.

Meanwhile, Lu Qin blanched the pork, cooled it in ice water, and started slicing it. His knife skills were fast and smooth, with no hesitation—like the effortless expertise described in the story of the ‘Oil Seller’.

The head chef, a professional himself, could see immediately that Lu Qin’s knife skills were far from ordinary. Each slice was precisely 2 millimeters thick, perfectly even without any deviation. And he was cutting at an impressive speed! Achieving both speed and precision like this was no easy feat.

Lu Xiyao finished washing the garlic shoots and even shook off the water before handing them over.

“Daddy, all done! Here you go!”

This was all part of what Lu Qin had taught her, and she remembered it all after just one lesson. He took the garlic shoots, cut them into small sections, and prepared the other ingredients. The onlookers were impressed, they now realized that Lu Qin wasn’t being polite when he said he didn’t need any help—he truly didn’t need it. His hands were steady and quick, commanding the kitchen space as if it were solely his domain.

In just eight minutes, he had a plate of garlic-shoot twice-cooked pork ready to serve. The aroma filled the kitchen, rich and mouth-watering.

“That smells amazing!” Teacher Li couldn’t help but exclaim.

The enticing fragrance was so strong it made her salivate unconsciously. The teachers hadn’t had their lunch yet, as they typically waited until the children were done eating. Now, with the smell of twice-cooked pork wafting around, her stomach started to grumble, and she coughed to cover her embarrassment.

The head chef and assistants swallowed, also captivated by the scent. This twice-cooked pork smelled like the ultimate comfort food. If it tasted as good as it smelled, the flavor would be phenomenal.

The head chef, who prided himself on his Sichuan cuisine, including twice-cooked pork, now felt humbled. Watching Lu Qin, he realized there were details in his cooking that he had overlooked. Even without tasting it, he could tell Lu Qin’s twice-cooked pork was on a whole other level.

chapters.title: Chapter 49 - Lu Qin’s Cooking Made the Kids Drool!
chapters.content:
Chapter 49 Lu Qin’s Cooking Made the Kids Drool!

It turned out that when stir-frying twice-cooked pork, they should place the meat slices in a strainer and dip them in the previously cooked meat broth to separate the slices. No wonder Lu Qin didn’t pour out the broth after boiling the meat.

After frying the Doubanjiang (fermented bean paste) to release its aroma, Tianmianjiang (sweet bean sauce) and Douchi (fermented black beans) were also essential. No wonder the aroma was so rich!

The head chef noted down each of these details, realizing they were secret tips only a master would share – these were not things they could usually learn. Originally, the head chef thought his twice-cooked pork was already at about 80 to 90 points out of 100. But in comparison to Lu Qin’s, it now seemed like he hadn’t even passed!

Details determined success or failure. Making a dish taste great had never been a simple task. The three young cooks around the head chef were dumbfounded, their eyes filled with admiration. Lu Qin was about their age, yet his cooking skills were superb. Just this dish of twice-cooked pork had blown them away entirely, leaving nothing to compare.

In the kitchen, sounds of swallowing filled the air.

“Daddy~! It smells so good~! Can I have some~?”

Watching the twice-cooked pork being plated, with its tempting aroma and color, Lu Xiyao was practically drooling with anticipation.

“There are still two more dishes, they’ll be done soon. Just wait a little bit, Yao Yao.”

Lu Qin covered the dish of twice-cooked pork to prevent his little one from drooling into it.

“Alright then~! Daddy~! I’m so hungry~!”

She kept pleading with Lu Qin in her cute way – after all, it didn’t cost anything to act adorable.

“Okay, then Daddy will hurry up.”

“Can I use another pan?” Lu Qin turned and asked the head chef.

“Huh? Oh, sure.”

The head chef was already impressed by Lu Qin’s cooking skills and nodded quickly.

To speed things up and prevent his little treasure from staying hungry, Lu Qin worked on two dishes at once. In one pan, he stir-fried tomatoes and eggs. In the other, he prepared garlic-flavored chicken breast. Teacher Li and the head chef, along with two female teachers standing at the door, were all stunned.

Who knew cooking could be this impressive?

Lu Qin used his left and right hands for different tasks because the seasonings for tomatoes and eggs were different from those for garlic-flavored chicken breast. Almost simultaneously, Lu Qin added seasonings separately with each hand, in varying amounts. Was this the legendary ‘double-handed cooking technique’?

The scene was captivating, and even the little one couldn’t resist clapping her hands. The aroma of scrambled eggs and garlic wafted through the kitchen, causing everyone’s mouths to water uncontrollably again.

Although Lu Qin didn’t have a ‘full skill’ system boost for these two simple homestyle dishes, the principles and techniques were transferable. As a result, both dishes were near perfection.

In less than 20 minutes, three simple dishes were complete. Swallowing sounds filled the kitchen around 89,000 times.

“Teacher Li, have you had lunch? If you don’t mind, would you like to try my cooking?” Lu Qin offered.

There was plenty of food in the large serving dishes.

“I haven’t eaten yet… Are you sure that’s okay?” Although Teacher Li said this, she eagerly went to serve rice without further hesitation. She was practically drowning in her own saliva earlier, but she was too embarrassed to ask directly. Fortunately, Lu Qin extended the invitation, and she happily took the opportunity.

Lu Qin brought the three dishes on a tray and said, “Yao Yao, let’s go to the dining room.”

“Yay~!” Right now, Lu Xiyao’s little head was filled with only one thought: to eat. And to eat Lu Qin’s food.

The two female teachers who had been peeking from the kitchen door nodded and smiled at Lu Qin. Their gaze was unmistakable.

Lu Qin smiled back at them, saying, “I assume you two haven’t had lunch either? If you don’t mind…”

“Haha… we don’t mind at all!” The two teachers smiled so widely that they couldn’t close their mouths.

“We’ll help carry the food!”

The head chef and his three assistants looked on enviously, swallowing repeatedly. Normally, they wouldn’t go hungry in the kitchen, but now they felt a deep craving – they wanted to try Lu Qin’s twice-cooked pork. It was pure temptation.

In the dining room, Lu Qin and his daughter sat with the three teachers.

“Teacher Li, Teacher Gong, Teacher Tian, let’s dig in.”

“Thank you, Yao Yao’s Dad. We won’t stand during the ceremony!”

In the face of such delicious food, the three teachers didn’t hesitate and picked up their chopsticks immediately.

“Yao Yao, let’s start too.”

Even though there were only three dishes, the portions were generous, and they all paired perfectly with rice. The rich aroma wafted through the entire dining hall, making it impossible for the other kids to stay in their seats. They started gathering around Lu Qin’s table.

“Wow!” “Wow! Wow!”

“Wow! Wow! Wow!”

“Is that twice-cooked pork?”

“It looks delicious!”

“Lu Xiyao, is it tasty?”

“Yao Yao, your dad is amazing! His cooking smells so good!”

Lu Xiyao was gleefully munching away, alternating between bites of twice-cooked pork and rice, her cheeks puffed up like a little hamster.

“Of course, it’s super tasty! So delicious!” She replied, her mouth full.

Teacher Li, too, threw caution to the wind and devoured half a bowl of rice in one go.

“Yao Yao’s Dad, this twice-cooked pork is incredible! I’ve never had meat this good before!”

“Absolutely, it’s amazing!”

“My diet plan is ruined! But if I don’t eat this, I’ll regret it for life!”

Lu Qin chuckled softly and reminded his little one to slow down. Obediently, she started eating at a more measured pace.

The three teachers were so engrossed in their meal that they didn’t notice the other children had left their seats and were now crowded around their table. Out of the corner of her eye, Teacher Li noticed and was startled.

“Oh, Chen Haojie! Your drool is dripping onto your shirt!”

Putting down her chopsticks, Teacher Li grabbed a napkin to help wipe the little boy’s chin. It wasn’t an exaggeration, little Chen Haojie’s drool was practically a waterfall, and he wasn’t even aware of it as he stared fixedly at the plate of twice-cooked pork.

“Zhong Shuyi, you’re drooling too—hold it back!”

Teacher Li scrambled to keep up, noticing more and more kids around her beginning to drool. The aroma had even drawn the cleaning auntie and a security guard to the dining hall door, where they peeked inside curiously.

What kind of dishes were on the menu today? Why did it smell so good?

They were met with the sight of a group of kids drooling collectively.

“It’s Yao Yao’s dad’s cooking. I saw him go into the kitchen just now!”

“This young man’s cooking is so good that we could smell it all the way from the security post. Unbelievable!”

“Look! The kids are literally drooling!”

“Honestly, I’m even close to drooling here!”

Someone at the door took out their phone and started recording the scene of the kids drooling as a group.

Seeing the children almost in tears from craving his food, Lu Qin finally put down his chopsticks.

“Kids, bring over your trays, and I’ll give each of you a little portion.”

The children nearly jumped for joy.

“Really, Uncle?”

“That’s amazing! Thank you, Uncle! Your food smells so good!”

“Uncle, you’re the best!”

“Uncle, I’m Luo Xiaohong, Yao Yao’s good friend! Can I have an extra piece of meat?”

“Uncle, I’m Wu Qianwen, I’m Yao Yao’s good friend too!”

“Uncle, I’m Yao Yao’s boyfriend! Can I have two pieces?”

…

chapters.title: Chapter 50 - Lu Qin Goes to the Kitchen For a Second Round! The Kids Turn on Their Acting Skills!
chapters.content:
Chapter 50 Lu Qin Goes to the Kitchen For a Second Round! The Kids Turn on Their Acting Skills!

Lu Xiyao’s boyfriend?

Lu Qin raised an eyebrow, glancing at the little boy with a runny nose.

His daughter, who was in the middle of devouring her meal, heard this and turned her head, rolling her eyes at the boy.

“Li Junjie, stop talking nonsense~!”

“We’re not close at all! And I haven’t even played with your… robot toy dog!”

Having said that, she turned back and continued eating.

The kids excitedly ran over, each carrying their tray. The three teachers barely managed to shovel in a few bites before helping Lu Qin distribute the food to the children.

“Line up, everyone. If you’re not in line or if you cut, you won’t get any!”

Hearing this, a few kids who had been rushing forward immediately stopped in their tracks and obediently got in line.

Watching the other kids about to share her dad’s food, Lu Xiyao quickened her pace. Instead of her usual one piece of meat per bite of rice, she upgraded to two pieces of meat per bite. Lu Qin and Teacher Li both laughed.

But with over twenty kids, there was no way the three dishes Lu Qin made would be enough for everyone. They had to limit the portions. Each child received one piece of twice-cooked pork, a small spoonful of scrambled eggs with tomatoes, and a bit of chicken breast. The main goal was just to let the kids have a taste since their trays already had food.

“Uncle, can I have one more piece of twice-cooked pork?”

Teacher Li intervened, “Nope, just enough to taste is fine. You all need to finish your own meals.”

Some kids, holding their spoons, immediately shoveled Lu Qin’s food into their mouths as soon as it was served.

“Mmm~! So tasty! This meat is amazing!”

Their eyes sparkled with joy.

“Alright, next!”

When it was Li Junjie’s turn, Lu Qin smiled slyly.

“Here, have a bit extra.”

He gave him a big spoonful of green garlic shoots, tomatoes, and garlic cloves—double the portion.

Li Junjie looked disappointed.

“Uncle, where’s the meat~?”

“This is meat!”

Lu Qin pointed at the garlic cloves.

Since the sauce was dark, it was hard to tell the difference between the garlic and chicken breast.

“Really? Wow! So many pieces of meat!”

“Thanks, Uncle.”

Happily, Li Junjie returned to his seat. After taking a bite of garlic, he chewed, feeling something was a bit off, though the sauce was delicious. But it wasn’t quite the same experience as the kids next to him, who were savoring the twice-cooked pork.

By the time half of the kids had tasted Lu Qin’s three dishes, the serving plates were almost empty.

Leaning back in her chair, Lu Xiyao rubbed her now full tummy, satisfied.

“Ah~! I’m stuffed! hiccup !”

Lu Qin helped her wipe her mouth.

The three teachers, on the other hand, were in a bit of a pickle. There were still about ten kids who hadn’t had a taste of Lu Qin’s food. Holding their trays, they lined up, staring at the empty plates with tearful eyes.

In kindergarten, managing kids, whether for eating or playing, has to be done fairly so that everyone gets a turn. Otherwise, those who didn’t get a taste would feel left out. When kids feel unfairly treated, they cry.

Sure enough, a few kids started crying.

“ Waaah~ ! There’s no more twice-cooked pork! I haven’t had any yet!”

“Uncle~! I want your meat~! Boohoo ~!”

“Teacher Li! I want meat! I want meat!”

“Li Junjie got to eat meat, so I want some too!”

A chorus of little voices filled the air as the kids cried in unison.

Teacher Li tried to calm them down, but some only cried louder.

“Wang Xiurui, didn’t you already get a piece earlier? Why are you crying with the others now?”

Teacher Li was at a loss, the scene was descending into chaos!

She had no choice but to turn to Lu Qin for help.

“I’ll head back to the kitchen.”

Lu Qin stood up. Clearly, this group of little food lovers wouldn’t be satisfied today until they had another round of twice-cooked pork.

Lu Qin returned to the kitchen, where the head chef and his team were already aware of the commotion among the children in the dining hall.

Oh boy!

Just how delicious was Yao Yao’s dad’s cooking to have all the kids craving more?

The head chef and his team were now visibly more respectful towards Lu Qin.

“Yao Yao’s Dad, are you making twice-cooked pork again?”

“Mm,” Lu Qin nodded.

The head chef perked up but looked a bit embarrassed.

“Uh, well… Yao Yao’s Dad, would you mind if I recorded you cooking? I’d like to learn from it.”

He took out his phone.

“Sure, feel free,” Lu Qin replied, having no objections.

Although he didn’t have time to formally teach, he wasn’t stingy about others learning by watching.

Efficiently, Lu Qin whipped up another batch of garlic chive twice-cooked pork and garlic chicken breast, making sure the portions were generous again.

As soon as the dishes were brought into the dining hall, the kids’ crying stopped abruptly. Their eyes sparkled as they stared at the freshly cooked twice-cooked pork.

Teacher Li pinched a few kids’ cheeks with a sigh.

“You little drama kings!”

Without being told, the kids were already lined up in an orderly queue.

“Teacher Li, let me help with serving!” volunteered Lu Xiyao, now full and looking for something to do.

Lu Qin figured he could leave now; if he stayed any longer, he’d probably end up making a third trip to the kitchen. He gave his little one a goodbye nuzzle and left the kindergarten.

A group of children ran after him, their little feet pitter-pattering.

“Uncle, are you coming back tomorrow?” “Uncle, I want to eat your twice-cooked pork again tomorrow!”

“Goodbye, Uncle! I’ll take care of Lu Xiyao, and I’ll even share my apple with her in the afternoon!”

“Uncle, where do you sell your food? I’ll tell my dad to buy your fried noodles and twice-cooked pork!”

…

The security guard at the gate was watching, a bit dazed, even forgetting to open the gate as Lu Qin approached.

“Yao Yao’s Dad, you’re amazing! One twice-cooked pork dish, and you’ve won over the entire kindergarten!”

“These kids are pretty cute,” Lu Qin replied with a smile.

…

The guard opened the electric gate, adding, “Where do you set up your stall? I definitely want to stop by!”

“I don’t have a fixed location. Join our QQ group,” Lu Qin replied.

The guard looked surprised.

“What? You have a group? Count me in!”

Lu Qin took out his phone and approved the request. The guard’s eyes widened as he saw the number.

“Five… Five hundred and sixty-seven members!”

“Wow! You’ve got a huge following!”

Then again, he thought, with food as good as Lu Qin’s, it would be odd if he didn’t have a fanbase.

Lu Qin climbed onto his three-wheeler food cart and headed back to his rented place to prepare for his afternoon stall.

For his next session, he added a new item to the menu—chicken tofu blossom.

With a system-maintained freshness and heat-preserving box, he could prepare the chicken tofu blossom at home and sell it as a ready-made dish, preserving its texture perfectly.

The process for making chicken tofu blossom was complex. Though it was a traditional dish in Sichuan cuisine, few people made it anymore. And even fewer could make it well. In fact, it was rare to find chicken tofu blossom on restaurant menus, and many people didn’t even know what kind of dish it was.

To start, Lu Qin prepared a large pot. The first step in making chicken tofu blossom was to brew a high-quality broth from a mature hen. Normally, this process would take five hours. Slow simmering drew out the essence from the chicken meat and bones, showcasing the nutritional value of a mature hen’s broth. However, with today’s easy access to nutritious food, Lu Qin skipped the lengthy simmering.

Instead, he began by rendering the chicken skin’s fat for flavor, then added the chicken frame and seasonings to the pot, allowing it to simmer. This method created an even richer, more fragrant broth.

chapters.title: Chapter 51
chapters.content:
Chapter 51 The Incredible Texture of Chicken Tofu Blossom! Miss Out, And It’s a Fine of Three Days’ Wages!

Lu Qin carefully used a kitchen knife to remove every single white tendon from the chicken breast. Not a trace was left. Chicken Tofu Blossom is made entirely from chicken breast, yet it looks identical to a bowl of plain tofu. Only this part of the chicken can achieve the delicate texture of real tofu when eaten. If the tendons aren’t removed, the chicken would turn into clumps when cooked—not only unsightly but also nothing like the smooth texture of tofu.

With the tendons fully removed, Lu Qin began pounding the meat. Only the back of the knife could be used, not the blade. For the chicken to cook into tofu-like sheets, the meat must be pulverized into a paste, not just chopped.

Once the chicken paste was adequately pounded, the stock in the pot had already turned fragrant, drawing everyone in. Lu Qin cracked open eggs, carefully pouring only the egg whites into a bowl, leaving out the yolks. Though the yolks wouldn’t be used for the Chicken Tofu Blossom, they wouldn’t go to waste either; he planned to make caramel crème brûlée for his daughter, one of her favorites.

With egg whites from twenty free-range eggs in a large bowl, Lu Qin whisked them vigorously until they foamed. Then, he mixed the frothy egg whites with the pounded chicken paste, adding a handful of sweet potato starch and stirring until fully combined.
