This helped the chicken paste bond and achieve maximum elasticity. When steamed, it would quickly solidify into a mesmerizing tofu-like crystal structure, offering the uncanny appeal of a dish that’s vegetarian in appearance but made from meat.

Lu Qin then ladled out the chicken broth, straining out the meat, bones, Sichuan peppercorns, and other solids to keep only the clearest, freshest stock. He put the broth back on the stove to simmer.

Now, it was time to cook the chicken paste. With the heat turned up to a vigorous boil, the rich chicken broth quickly came to a rolling boil, and the paste transformed into tofu-like curds, filling the entire pot. Switching to a low flame, Lu Qin gently pressed down any restless curds with a ladle, occasionally ladling broth over them to soothe them.

Such finesse with heat control relies on intuition. Someone inexperienced could easily mess it up. After no more than 20 minutes, a bowl of fresh Chicken Tofu Blossom was ready. He placed fresh pea shoots at the bottom of a large bowl, then ladled in the mixture, creating a picturesque green and white landscape, with a beautiful, understated elegance.

When they took a bite, it felt like tofu—but chew it, and their mouth filled with the rich flavor of chicken. Take a sip of the broth, and they were struck by a delightful confusion—Was this fake chicken or real tofu?

Lu Qin took a taste and was pleased with the unique, addictive texture. This was a flavor beyond ordinary food, one that could easily become a craving.

He nodded with satisfaction, packaged the Chicken Tofu Blossom in disposable containers, and sealed them in a freshness-maintaining warmer, preserving their just-cooked temperature and texture for 24 hours.

With 28 pounds of chicken breast, he’d made nearly 150 portions of Chicken Tofu Blossom. He also prepared a large tray of sliced pork leg, about 200 servings, which he stored in the container as well. The freshness-locking warmer had ample space, and this was only half its capacity.

Meanwhile, the mung bean paste in the stainless steel pot on the other side of the stove had finished simmering, and the dough for ice-crystal cakes was rising. Lu Qin quickly set to work making the cakes.

With his full array of expert techniques, he was keenly aware of the ideal times for simmering, proofing, and every other detail. With efficient planning, he finished preparing all the ingredients for his afternoon stall in just two and a half hours. He loaded everything onto his three-wheeler food cart and checked the time—it wasn’t yet 3 p.m.

Lu Qin opened his food-stall map app to find the best location. At the top of the list was— Tianhong Plaza, 4.7 km away, foot traffic of 3,000 people.

(A small music festival is currently being held here with celebrity appearances, making it the peak location in Jiangcheng. The best stall spot is 146 meters east of Tianyuan West Road.)

Lu Qin tapped ‘Navigate here’, posted the location in his group chat, and set off on his three-wheeler.

As soon as he posted the announcement, the group chat exploded.

…

In an apartment, Fang Ya just woke up, took a shower, and her stomach started growling. She thought of Lu Qin’s stir-fried noodles and ice-crystal cakes. She quickly opened the QQ group.

…

Chen Dahai, sitting in his Mercedes, saw the group announcement and immediately asked his driver to turn around and head to the music festival at Tianhong Plaza.

…

Ma Dejun, seeing the message, got into his electric tricycle and drove toward Tianhong Plaza.

…

At Xingguang Kindergarten, the security guard saw the blinking notification in the QQ group and immediately pulled out his phone and dialed a number…

“Honey, hurry over to Tianhong Plaza! Yes, the three-wheeler food cart guy I told you about is setting up there at 3:30 this afternoon. Remember, buy as much as you can when it’s your turn, don’t be stingy—it’s incredibly delicious, you won’t regret it!”

…

In the top-floor CEO office of the Ourei International Hotel, Shen Muyan, while reviewing documents, reached over to pick up a piece of ice-crystal cake from a plastic container and took a bite.

She had skipped lunch after a hearty breakfast, but now, feeling hungry, the cake lifted her mood immensely. Across her desk, a hand slowly inched towards the container of ice-crystal cakes.

Slap!

Song Xuanxuan’s hand was smacked by Shen Muyan, who looked at her reproachfully.

How stingy!

Song Xuanxuan thought with a pout. She’d been the one who went to buy these ice-crystal cakes!

Without looking up, Shen Muyan glanced at Song Xuanxuan, then continued reviewing her documents. Her lips moved slightly as she said, “Didn’t you say that was the last piece? Or do you still want more?”

Song Xuanxuan felt a twinge of frustration. After all, it was her boss being stingy. She’d bought two boxes of cakes but was only allowed three pieces—not nearly enough to satisfy her craving.

Shen Muyan thought she’d been generous since these two boxes of ice-crystal cakes counted as her lunch. And now, Song Xuanxuan wanted her boss’s lunch? Was she trying to start a rebellion?

Song Xuanxuan pouted, waiting for Shen Muyan to finish reviewing the documents before she could take them to the department. Secretly, she opened her phone and checked the QQ group.

Tianhong Plaza? A music festival?

Song Xuanxuan’s eyes lit up.

“Boss, after delivering the files, if there’s nothing else, I’d like to request leave.”

“Reason.”

“Boss, there’s a music festival at Tianhong Plaza. I’d like to go.”

Shen Muyan slowly looked up, narrowing her beautiful almond-shaped eyes.

“Requesting leave during work hours to play around? Song Xuanxuan, are you trying to quit?”

“Boss, let me explain! Lu Boss is setting up his food stall there this afternoon, and today he’s got twice-cooked pork and his new dish, Chicken Tofu Blossom!”

Shen Muyan’s expression softened slightly as she opened her phone and checked the QQ group herself. Sure enough, Tianhong Plaza, 3:30 p.m.

She tossed her Maserati keys over to Song Xuanxuan and said coolly, “Take my car. Get one of everything to eat and drink for me. If you don’t get it, it counts as absenteeism, and I’ll dock three days’ pay.”

“If you do get it, no leave will be counted, and expenses will be reimbursed.”

…

chapters.title: Chapter 52
chapters.content:
Chapter 52 Little One Chasing Celebrities? This Place isn’t Good, Don’t Bother Waiting in Line!

Song Xuanxuan was stunned for a few seconds, then suddenly let out a scream. She grabbed Shen Muyan’s car keys and dashed out of the office.

It was already 3:10 p.m. She had gone to line up nearly half an hour early that morning, and still almost didn’t get what she wanted. The music festival was packed, and Lu’s stall was bound to have a long line. Not getting the food meant losing three days’ pay? That would just be adding insult to injury! Shen is such a slave driver!

…

At Tianhong Plaza, a massive stage was set up in the center. Colorful lights, booming speakers, and an energetic crowd created an electric atmosphere. It was a huge scene.

Vendors love occasions like this—business was guaranteed to be good.

Lu Qin arrived on his three-wheeled food cart, heading to the designated spot on his street vendor map, Tianyuan West Road. The street was already lined with stalls selling lemon tea, spicy potatoes, oden, 1-yuan skewers, and spicy chicken.

Whether it was luck or the help of the street vendor map app, there was one spot left, just the right size for Lu Qin’s cart. He parked his cart perfectly in the gap.

He set up his stall. The stall owner next to him, who was selling fried skewers, couldn’t help but glance at him a few times. Lu Qin was selling stir-fried noodles, which made the skewer vendor shake his head.

Who’s going to buy a main dish at this time of day? It’s snacks and drinks that are selling fast! This young man clearly doesn’t know how to do business. Making money from street vending isn’t easy.

But since they were in competition, the skewer vendor wasn’t going to give Lu Qin any advice. Tianhong Plaza wasn’t just filled with snack stalls, there were also fruit stands, dessert shops, a mobile ice cream truck, and more. The merchants weren’t going to miss out on the business opportunities brought by the music festival.

There were still thirty minutes until the festival started. Before watching the show, people were free to enjoy a little ‘food festival’ of their own.

“Boss, give me some fried skewers—enoki mushrooms, potatoes, lamb skewers, and sausages—mildly spicy.”

“Two cups of hand-pressed lemon tea, please.”

“Boss, I’ll have a portion of stinky tofu.”

…

For now, Lu Qin’s stir-fried noodle stall wasn’t drawing much attention. Stir-fried noodles didn’t have the same appeal as grilled sausages, stinky tofu, or spicy chicken. Only a few young women passing by glanced at him, their eyes lighting up at the sight of his handsome face. But when they saw he was selling stir-fried noodles and pork belly, they quickly lost interest.

In this heat, who would eat that kind of food!

Lu Qin wasn’t worried, though. He waited patiently. Just then, his phone rang. It was a video call from Lu Xiyao. He answered the call.

“Daddy~!”

The sweet, soft voice of his little girl filled his ears, and a smile appeared on Lu Qin’s face.

In the video, the table in front of the little girl was piled high with a mountain of snacks. She was holding a chocolate Pocky stick in her mouth and proudly showing off her treats, asking Lu Qin which one he wanted to eat.

Lu Qin chuckled helplessly. This snack haul was surely a reward for him making pork belly for lunch earlier. Right now, his little girl was probably the most popular kid at kindergarten.

“Don’t eat too many snacks. A lot of them have additives, which aren’t good for kids’ health,” he said and reminded her with a smile.

“Okay, Daddy! I’ll stop after finishing this chocolate Pocky!”

Lu Xiyao always listened to Lu Qin. When she said she’d stop, she really would.

“Daddy, where’s your food stall today? Is it really hot outside? If you’re feeling unwell, don’t push yourself this afternoon! Come home and rest! When your little cutie gets out of kindergarten, we can go do the night market together!”

Lu Xiyao was worried about her dad, concerned he might overwork himself.

“The sun’s a bit strong, but I’m inside the food cart selling food, so it’s not too bad. Don’t worry, Yaoyao,” Lu Qin said and reassured her. “Daddy’s at Tianhong Plaza. There’s a music festival here. Look!”

He flipped the phone camera and showed her a huge poster of the music festival. The poster featured several young and trendy singers.

“Wow! It’s Sister Li Xue! I’m a fan of hers! Daddy, can you get her autograph for me?”

Lu Xiyao suddenly screamed with excitement, her eyes lighting up. Lu Qin was a bit surprised. Li Xue? His little girl was chasing celebrities? He had no idea.

“Yaoyao, you’re a fan of Li Xue?”

“Yeah! Zhong Shuyi also loves her!”

“Uh… who’s Li Xue?” Lu Qin asked.

Xiyao rolled her eyes cutely.

“Daddy, you’re so out of touch! Li Xue is a super popular singer! Can you help me get her autograph?”

“I’ll try my best,” Lu Qin said.

But the little girl sighed, “Daddy, never mind. Just take a video of her from afar. She’s a big star, it’s hard to get her autograph!”

…

After chatting for about five minutes, Zhong Shuyi came over to invite Xiyao to play on the slide. Seeing Lu Qin on the video call, Shuyi quickly waved, her eyes full of admiration. It was clear that the dishes Lu Qin had made for lunch had won over all the kids at the kindergarten.

Right after Lu Qin hung up the phone, a large crowd suddenly appeared at his stall. All the nearby vendors were stunned. How could there be so many people? Forty or fifty people, all in a group, and each of them looked anxious. When they spotted Lu Qin’s food stall, they started shouting excitedly.

“Boss Lu, we finally found your stall!”

“You made us search high and low!”

“Next time, can you share your exact coordinates? Tianhong Plaza is huge, we almost starved while looking for you!”

“Pork belly! Pork belly! Pork belly!”

“There’s something new today, chicken tofu!”

“Chicken tofu? You mean ‘ji dou hua,’ a Sichuan specialty!”

“Whatever it is, I have to try it. I can’t wait to taste the chicken tofu!”

“It’s too hot! Let’s get three cups of mung bean slush to cool down first!”

“Boss Lu, did you bring enough ingredients today? I just got paid, and I’m ready to order a ton!”

“Haha! I’m the first one here, jackpot!”

“As soon as I see Boss Lu, I start drooling!”

“Yesterday’s pork belly was amazing! I even licked the box clean!”

…

The crowd quickly formed an orderly line, but it was so massive that it looked like an organized group. The line stretched across the whole street.

The other vendors and passersby were dumbfounded. It was just a stir-fried noodle stall—how good could it be? Was it really worth such a long line? This was insane!

A curious passerby asked one of the guys in line, “Hey, is this food really that good? Is that why there are so many people waiting?”

The guy was caught off guard, then shook his head vigorously.

“Not at all! You should go try the other stalls!”

“???”

The passerby was confused.

What? If it’s not good, why are you so excited to line up?

Up front, Lu Qin had already started cooking, the oil sizzling as he stir-fried garlic and chili peppers, filling the air with a mouthwatering aroma. The crowd was dense, and the fragrance wafted through the area, overpowering the nearby fried skewers and stinky tofu stands.

The passerby finally understood and pointed at the guy, accusing, “Young man, you’re being sneaky! With a smell this good and so many people lining up, it’s definitely delicious! What do you mean it’s not good?”

The guy sighed, “I just want fewer people in line. Less pressure, you know?”

The skewer vendor next to them was so shocked that the chicken wing he was holding slipped back into the oil.

What’s going on? Even customers are feeling the pressure now?

chapters.title: Chapter 53
chapters.content:
Chapter 53 Terrifying Popularity, Overshadowing the Music Festival!

At Tianhong Plaza, the music festival hadn’t even started yet. The staff were still busy setting up and adjusting the equipment. The audience, eager to attend, had already filled the entire plaza. It was bustling, crowded, and full of excitement.

Nearby snack stalls, Snowman Ice City, and fruit stands were doing a booming business. Most of the people attending the music festival were young men and women, the prime customers for snacks and milk tea.

But the hottest spot by far was a stir-fried noodle stall on Linjiang Street. No one really knew why, but somehow, the line to buy noodles had grown incredibly long. The crowd waiting for their food was terrifying, with over 50 people in line!

The vendors selling fried skewers, spicy chicken, and stinky tofu next door were completely baffled. What was going on? They were selling popular, classic snacks—always in demand! How could they be outdone by a stir-fried noodle stall today?

Still, judging by the aroma, the noodles were really something. The young stall owner clearly had some serious cooking skills!

No, strong cooking skills! It seemed he was also frying up pork belly, and the smell was irresistible.

But seriously, who sells such a hearty dish like stir-fried pork belly at a street stall?

The people at the front of the line were seasoned customers, die-hard foodies with an impressive ability to devour meals. They made sure to order everything on Lu Qin’s menu. Especially the limited items like crystal cake, twice-cooked pork, and chicken tofu—those were absolute must-haves!

“So this is chicken tofu? It looks pretty good!” One customer said as they opened their container, and other curious people gathered around to take a look.

Inside the simple plastic box was a dish of tofu, smooth and white like jade, sitting in golden, fragrant broth. The presentation was excellent.

“Hurry up and try it—how does it taste?” Someone urged.

The customer swallowed hard and took a small bite while everyone else watched intently.

“It’s delicious! The flavor is really unique!” The customer exclaimed.

The broth was clear and fresh, like a spring from the mountains. The chicken tofu, with its pure white color, was tender, melting in the mouth with a lingering, exquisite taste. Unable to resist, the customer took a second bite.

“It’s incredibly tasty!”

Eating the chicken tofu, the customer felt like their soul had been elevated. Lu Qin’s cooking skills had reached their peak, and the dish was delicately rich, with a flavor that was hard to come by. This was a rare and extraordinary taste experience!

The expressions of the surrounding crowd said it all—they could tell it was good just by watching. Sure enough, anything made by Boss Lu was nothing short of amazing.

The customer finished their chicken tofu and, licking their lips, still craved more.

“Boss Lu, this chicken tofu is incredible! Can I get another portion?”

“You’re dreaming! It’s one per person!” Someone in line retorted.

“Exactly, you’ve already had yours. Move along, it’s our turn!”

“Hey, wait! I haven’t gotten my noodles and crystal cake yet!” The customer exclaimed.

As soon as the new dish, chicken tofu, made its debut, the crowd waiting in line grew even more excited.

“I want a chicken tofu too!”

“I’ve never had it before. I’m lucky to get to try it today!”

“I saw this on TV! It was on A Bite of Longguo—they said something about ‘eating chicken but seeing no chicken, eating meat but seeing no meat.’ It’s a complex dish to make, but when done right, it’s absolutely delicious!”

“Boss Lu is a genius! Everything he makes is top-tier!”

“Boss Lu is a legend!”

More and more customers gathered at Lu Qin’s stall, while the nearby vendors had fewer and fewer people. Customers from the Penguin group chat were showing up one after another. Meanwhile, the festival-goers, both men and women, suddenly noticed that there was a huge crowd gathered at one corner of the riverside street, and the atmosphere was electric.

“Look over there! There are so many people—could there be a celebrity?”

“Let’s go check it out!”

“Wait, it looks like it’s just a food stall. But wow, it’s really popular!”

As the crowd grew larger, more and more people gathered. To an outsider, it might seem like a celebrity or famous singer had just arrived on the scene. Meanwhile, Lu Qin continued stir-frying his signature twice-cooked pork in the wok, and the enticing aroma was driving the customers in line wild.

“Boss Lu, is it ready yet? I’m starving over here!”

“It’s done,” Lu Qin replied, packaging the twice-cooked pork.

“Let me help with the packing, Boss! You keep cooking—I’ve still got three orders of stir-fried noodles to pick up!” The customer paid by scanning the QR code, grabbed the twice-cooked pork, and then pulled out a container of their own white rice.

“Holy moly! Genius! You can’t eat twice-cooked pork without rice!”

“Hey, young man, can I buy a bowl of your rice? I’ll pay!”

“Boss Lu, I tried to recreate your twice-cooked pork at home after watching the video I filmed yesterday, but it tasted worlds apart!”

“Twice-cooked pork looks simple, but it’s actually very tricky!”

“Ever since I tasted Boss Lu’s twice-cooked pork, I can’t stomach it at any other restaurant!”

Under the bright sunlight, beside the riverside railings, a man took alternating bites of twice-cooked pork and white rice, drawing a crowd of onlookers. Some people even began drooling as they watched. Other street snacks like stinky tofu and spicy chicken skewers suddenly lost their appeal.

Word also spread that Lu Qin’s stall had refreshing iced mung bean drinks that were even better than the popular ‘Farewell My Concubine’ milk tea!

In no time, Lu Qin’s stall completely dominated the street’s business. His stall was so popular that it even overshadowed the upcoming music festival.

With just ten minutes until the performance was set to begin, the host had planned to do some audience interaction but found the crowd sparse. Confused, the host looked around and was stunned.

A massive group of people had gathered around a food stall, stretching all the way down the street. Even the cameraman shifted his focus, aiming the camera at Lu Qin’s stall.

Among the crowd were familiar faces—Mr. Luo Xi, Chen Dahai, Fang Ya, Song Xuanxuan, Ma Dejun, and the wife of Xingguang Kindergarten’s security guard—all waiting patiently in line.

Fang Ya had thought about cutting in, but seeing the sheer number of people, she quickly gave up, knowing it was impossible. Cutting in line would only result in a barrage of complaints from other customers.

At that moment, Shen Muyan, who was in the middle of a meeting, felt her phone vibrate twice. Glancing at it to make sure she wasn’t missing any important work-related messages, she saw that both Fang Ya and Song Xuanxuan had sent her the same photo.

In the photo, taken from above, a long, winding line of people was shown. Their anxious expressions were clear, and some even appeared to be salivating. The message of the photo was unmistakable—This place was on fire.

At the end of the line stood a humble three-wheeler food cart, where a handsome man was stir-frying noodles. This was the first time Shen Muyan had seen Lu Qin at work, cooking in public. He looked so focused, so dedicated. His movements were skillful, exuding an invisible charm that naturally drew people’s attention.

“President Shen?” Someone called out, breaking her concentration. For the first time during a meeting, Shen Muyan had lost her focus. Her flawless, stunning face briefly showed a flicker of change before quickly returning to her usual calm, as if nothing had happened. She resumed the meeting without a hitch.

Back at the music festival, a vibrant young woman wearing a white baseball cap, pink sunglasses, and red-green braided hair appeared in the crowd. She, too, had been drawn over by the irresistible scent coming from Lu Qin’s food stall. Being a true foodie, she couldn’t resist trying out whatever smelled good.

“Looks like I came to the right place!” She said to herself, scanning the long line. “This stir-fried noodle stall must be amazing if so many people are waiting!”

However, the line was ridiculously long, and she estimated it would take at least an hour to get to the front. Unwilling to wait, she stopped a man who had just bought his food.

With a sweet voice, she asked, “Hey, handsome, could you share some of your food with me?”

“What? Share with you? I waited almost half an hour for this! No way!” The man said firmly.

Undeterred, she tried again, this time pulling out her sincerest plea.

“Please, I really want to try it!”

“Want to eat it? Then get in line! Everything’s limited, so I can’t give you any!” The next customer snapped.

She sighed and decided it was time to pull out her trump card. Spotting a girl who had just bought her meal, she approached with a playful smirk. Lifting her cap slightly and lowering her sunglasses, she revealed her face.

The girl gasped, then her expression instantly turned to one of excitement and disbelief.

“Li Xue!!!”

chapters.title: Chapter 54
chapters.content:
Chapter 54 Sign a Photo with WeChat, Just For a Bite to Eat!

A young woman had just bought two servings of fried rice noodles, three cups of mung bean smoothies, two boxes of ice jelly, and one order of chicken tofu pudding from Lu Qin’s food stall. She was in a blissful mood and was just about to find a quiet spot to enjoy her feast.

Suddenly, she was pulled aside by a fashionably dressed girl. Then, something even more delightful happened. The person who stopped her was none other than Li Xue, the wildly popular young pop singer, and the biggest star at the music festival.

The Tianhong Plaza was packed with people, most of them there just to see Li Xue. This young woman, unbelievably, had run into Li Xue by chance — and it was the star who had approached her!

In an instant, the young woman was overwhelmed with joy.

“Mom, I met a celebrity!”

She was about to scream, but Li Xue quickly covered her mouth.

“Shh! Don’t make a scene,” Li Xue whispered, adjusting her sunglasses to disguise herself.

The girl stared at Li Xue, still in a daze. This sort of reaction was nothing new to Li Xue.

She gave a gentle smile and said, “Hey, girl, I snuck out and haven’t had lunch yet. Could you spare me some of your food? I’ll pay you.”

Li Xue’s manager always insisted she stick to a strict diet for her figure — lunch was usually just a plate of salad, which was practically nothing. Normally, she didn’t mind and wasn’t hungry. But today, for some reason, the aroma of fried rice noodles and stir-fried pork in the air had awakened a craving deep in her stomach. She suddenly felt faint with hunger.

“Uh?”

The young woman was taken aback.

You want my food?

She hesitated for a few seconds… It seemed like she was having an intense internal debate.

“Huh?”

Li Xue was equally puzzled. What’s there to hesitate about? Shouldn’t she be thrilled?

I mean, it’s a live celebrity! Surely, she’d be happy to share. Even if she’s not a fan, most people would nod and say yes when they recognize me.

“Well… Li Xue, what would you like? Snail noodles or roasted pork trotters? I can buy it for you!” The young woman said and hesitated again.

Even if it was Li Xue, she was reluctant to part with the food she had worked so hard to get.

Fried rice noodles? Nope, those are for dinner and a late-night snack. Ice jelly? No way, that’s dessert, and she couldn’t sleep without it. Chicken tofu pudding? Absolutely not — it was a new item she had to try, or else her rank in the Penguin chat group would drop.

There were several of Lu Qin’s girlfriend wannabes waiting to overthrow her. Mung bean smoothies? Definitely not. Lu Qin’s smoothies were limited, and three cups were just enough for her. Missing even a sip would ruin the experience.

Li Xue shook her head, she wasn’t interested in anything else. She had specifically come for the food from this popular stall. Li Xue was a discerning foodie who only indulged in truly exceptional food. From the girl’s refusal, it was clear that this food stall’s offerings must indeed be in the exceptionally delicious category. Li Xue’s craving intensified. She had to have a taste.

“I only want the food from that stall, please! Could you share some?” Li Xue asked, using a slightly pleading tone.

“Um…”

The girl smiled awkwardly.

Can’t you buy it yourself?

It was really hard for me to get my hands on this.

“I’ll sign something for you, even with a personalized message,” Li Xue offered, trying to negotiate. For the first time, her celebrity aura seemed to be failing her.

“Uh… uh…”

The young woman was still hesitant. An autograph doesn’t fill my stomach. She was a practical person.

“I’ll sign and take a photo with you — and we can take one where I even give you a shoulder massage,” Li Xue said and sweetened the deal.

“Huh?”

“Now… that I can consider…”

Li Xue was exasperated.

Is it really this difficult?

“It’s just a bite of food, why so stingy?”

“Fine, I’ll give you one serving of fried noodles, but nothing else,” the young woman finally relented.

“What are these?” Li Xue pointed.

“These? Ice jelly and chicken tofu pudding,” the girl replied.

“I want those too.”

“No way, these are limited items and really hard to get.”

“I’ll throw in a signed photo, plus add you on WeChat.”

“Alright… fine. You can have an extra box of ice jelly, but the chicken tofu pudding is off the table. It’s a new release and they only allow one per person.”

“Okay, okay,” Li Xue grumbled, flashing a hint of her cute tiger tooth.

I’m even giving her WeChat, and she’s still so reluctant? Is my WeChat worth less than a box of food?

Li Xue was now convinced that the food must be amazing. In exchange for a serving of Lu’s fried rice noodles and a box of ice jelly, Li Xue signed an autograph, took a selfie, and added the girl on WeChat. Of course, she used her secondary account and warned her in advance that due to her celebrity status, she couldn’t chat frequently, and her busy schedule meant she might only respond occasionally. The young woman left, elated.

Just as Li Xue was about to find a quiet spot to eat, her annoying manager called.

“Yeah, yeah, I’m coming back now.”

Back in the dressing room, Li Xue’s manager, Amy, was horrified to see the fried noodles and ice jelly.

“Oh my god! Fried noodles! And dessert! Eating these will undo two weeks of your weight loss efforts!”

“Stop being so dramatic! It’s just an occasional treat. Do you want me to faint on stage from low blood sugar after a lunch of nothing but salad? End up trending on tomorrow’s news?”

Amy fell silent. She couldn’t argue with that and was genuinely worried about a potential mishap.

“Fine, I’ll get you something else to eat. But not this—it’s too fattening!”

“No way, I’m eating this, or I’ll be lying flat on stage within three minutes!”

“You—”

Li Xue was done listening. There was no way she’d give up the hard-earned delicacies for whatever bland food her manager brought—dehydrated veggies or flavorless chicken and beef that she was sick of.

Tearing open the plastic bag and container, Li Xue snapped apart the disposable chopsticks and took a bite.

“Wow! Delicious!”

Her eyes lit up. She had never tasted fried rice noodles this good. The flavor and texture were absolutely top-notch!

She took several more bites, nearly biting her tongue in excitement. Now, she understood why the young girl had been so reluctant to share. The food was just too delicious to part with.

Amy’s nose twitched as the aroma filled the room. Watching Li Xue devour the noodles, she swallowed hard.

“Alright, that’s too much. You can only eat half — the noodles are way too greasy!” Amy said, snatching the container away, trying to sound stern.

“Give it back! These noodles aren’t greasy at all!” Li Xue protested.

“No way they’re not greasy. Let me taste it.” Amy grabbed the chopsticks and took a bite herself.

“Oh my god!”

The noodles were unbelievably delicious! She never imagined fried rice noodles could taste this incredible.

“That’s my lunch! Have some decency,” Li Xue said, grabbing it back and taking a few more bites before Amy wrestled it away again.

“Stop eating, Xue! You need to maintain your figure!”

“My figure is just fine. I don’t gain weight easily. Now give me back the noodles…”

The two tussled over the single serving of noodles until they broke the disposable chopsticks in the process!

Li Xue stuffed the last bite into her mouth, but still wasn’t satisfied. She glared at Amy.

“Why are you fighting me for it? If you want some, go buy it yourself.”

“After all these years, you still can’t share a serving of noodles with me? You’ve broken my heart.”

“These noodles are non-negotiable. Everything else, maybe.”

“Fine, then I’ll just try this dessert.”

Li Xue’s expression changed instantly.

“Hold on! Not that either! I’ll treat you to tiramisu instead!”

chapters.title: Chapter 55
chapters.content:
Chapter 55 You’re in Luck—I’m Li Xue’s Manager!

There were only 15 minutes left before the official start of the music festival. A staff member came knocking on the door to hurry them along.

“Got it, got it. We’ll be right there.”

Amy closed the door behind her, only to find that Li Xue had already gobbled down three pieces of ice jelly, her cheeks puffed out like a chipmunk.

“Mmm… so good! So, so good!” Li Xue exclaimed with her mouth full.

“Li Xue! Let me have a taste!”

Amy lunged forward, managing to snatch the last piece of ice jelly from Li Xue’s grasp. Holding it in her hand, Amy was a bit surprised. “This dessert is so delicately made!”

Taking a bite, she found it to be soft, smooth, refreshingly cool, with an absolutely exquisite taste and magical texture—far better than any dessert she’d ever tried.

“How could it taste this good? I need another piece!”

But the box was already empty. Li Xue licked the crumbs off her lips.

“I only ate one piece,” she teased.

Amy was craving more.

“If it weren’t for our years of friendship, you wouldn’t have gotten even that one piece,” Li Xue said with a serious tone.

“Where did you get this?”

Li Xue suddenly realized.

“Right, you should hurry and buy more! There’s a food stall by the river—it’s the most popular one there!”

“You go perform, I’ll go buy it!” Amy agreed.

“Get plenty, and don’t forget the mung bean smoothies and chicken tofu pudding. They’re amazing too!”

“Got it.”

The dressing room door swung open, and Li Xue and her manager split up, each on their mission.

…

Outside, the performance had begun. The host on stage was introducing the performing stars and thanking the sponsors.

After five agonizing minutes, the first act was a young male singer performing a not-so-popular love ballad. His voice was decent, but it lacked a bit of emotion. Still, the crowd cheered, took photos, and applauded enthusiastically.

However, by the riverside road, a large crowd remained gathered, contrasting with the audience in front of the stage. Two distinct groups were forming—a smaller one by the stage and a much larger one waiting in line at Lu Qin’s food stall. Despite the music festival’s start, the queue for food remained steadfast.

“So they’ve started rapping and dancing over there, but I still haven’t gotten my food. Ugh , it’s so hard!” Someone grumbled.

“You can hear the music clearly from here, and the sound system’s great, but there’s no real vibe!”

“The show’s already started, and your favorite idol is singing ‘Beautiful Mess’—aren’t you going?”

“Hah! You think I’ll leave now so you can grab Lu’s food before me? Nice try!”

“I’m just a hater!”

“I came for Li Xue, she’s obviously the headliner. I’ll get my fried noodles right before her set starts!”

“I’m here for Lu’s fried noodles first, the performance is just a bonus. Don’t get the order mixed up.”

“I don’t care about singing or dancing—nothing beats a good meal!”

“Same here! Food is life, and Lu Qin is my god!”

…

Backstage, the host, noticing the enormous queue in the distance, asked a staff member, “What’s going on over there? Why is a food stall so popular? People are ignoring the music festival!”

“I heard the food is amazing! If I wasn’t working, I’d be in that line too!”

“This is the first time I’ve seen a street food vendor steal the show like this—it’s genuine street food madness.”

Meanwhile, Amy, Li Xue’s manager, stared at the forty or fifty people in line, feeling overwhelmed.

“How long is this going to take?”

“I’m number 51, you’re number 52! Just wait your turn—it should take about an hour. Lu’s efficiency is pretty good!”

An older man ahead of Amy struck up a conversation to pass the time.

“Lu Qin, huh? No wonder there’s such a crowd. The food he makes is truly delicious!”

“Absolutely! Yesterday, I bought a serving of twice-cooked pork, and oh my God, it was so delicious that I ate three bowls of rice with it. I’ve never tasted such amazing pork before!”

Amy’s eyes lit up.

“Twice-cooked pork? I haven’t tried that yet.”

After twenty minutes of waiting, the line had only moved forward by a third. Just then, a chubby kid holding several servings of fried noodles, mung bean smoothies, ice jelly, chicken tofu pudding, and twice-cooked pork stepped into the middle of the line and shouted to those behind him.

“The next 23 people, you don’t need to wait anymore. Everything’s sold out!”

“What? Already? We’re not even halfway there!”

“What the heck? Who are you to say it’s sold out? I don’t believe you!” Someone protested.

The chubby kid replied, “I’m Lu Qin’s spokesperson. If you don’t believe me, go ask him yourself!”

This sneaky kid who always managed to get multiple servings was Zhou Weiwei, the admin of Lu Qin’s Penguin chat group. Amy’s face turned pale.

“Already sold out!”

But on second thought, it made sense. This tiny food stall was insanely popular. Of course, things would sell out quickly. The crowd dispersed quickly. Some were happy and others disappointed. The lucky ones who got their food walked away beaming, while those who missed out left grumbling — some even lost interest in the music festival, and a few were on the verge of tears.

Determined folks headed to the supermarket to buy a cheap foldable stool for 9.9 yuan and went straight to the night market, planning to line up hours in advance for Lu Qin’s evening stall. They were convinced they’d get his food next time!

Meanwhile, Lu Qin packed up his supplies into his tricycle food cart. That afternoon, he sold 347 servings of fried noodles, 280 cups of mung bean smoothies, 205 servings of twice-cooked pork, and 150 servings of chicken tofu pudding. Total sales reached 20,166 yuan, breaking the 20k mark. After deducting costs, the profit was roughly 18,000 yuan.

The other vendors were dumbfounded. How could fried noodles and twice-cooked pork be this popular? It was like a dream come true. And in less than two hours, Lu was done for the day. Even the last two raw eggs were bought by a customer who said it was a shame to waste time waiting in line for nothing.

…

Back on stage, a dazzling display of fireworks drew everyone’s attention, and the crowd erupted into cheers. A beautiful young female singer burst out of the mist, microphone in hand, her angelic voice filling the air as she sang ‘The Adventurer’s Dream’. The audience went wild with excitement.

Lu Qin glanced over at the stage. The young singer had a great voice. She looked a bit familiar — was she the one on the poster? Could she be his daughter’s idol, Li Xue?

He wasn’t entirely sure since it was too far away. So, he turned to a passerby and asked, “Excuse me, ma’am, is the singer on stage Li Xue?”

Amy, who was standing nearby, gave Lu a puzzled and slightly amused look.

“You don’t recognize Li Xue?” She asked.

“I’m not that into entertainment, to be honest. But my daughter is a fan,” Lu admitted, a bit embarrassed.

“You have a daughter? How old is she?” Amy asked, surprised.

“She’s six.”

A six-year-old, already a fan? Wow, Xue’er, you really are popular! Amy thought to herself.

But wait — this Lu Qin didn’t even recognize Li Xue?

“So… you’re here to get an autograph for your daughter? Or maybe a photo?”

Lu Qin glanced at Amy, a bit confused.

“Yes, an autograph would be great. So, that’s definitely Li Xue on stage, right? I’ll figure something out. I need to give it a try and see if I can sneak backstage. You never know unless you try.”

As Lu Qin turned to leave, Amy called out.

“Wait.”

“Huh?”

“You’re in luck. Do you know who I am?”

“?”

“I’m Li Xue’s manager.”

chapters.title: Chapter 56
chapters.content:
Chapter 56 Who Exactly is this ‘Boss Lu’? Do You Know Him?

“See this?”

Amy proudly showed off her phone to Lu Qin. “Here’s my photo with Li Xue, her ID card, her WeChat — with voice messages, videos, and even demos of her new songs…”

“If I weren’t really Li Xue’s manager, would I have all this information?” She asked, a bit defensively.

“Still not convinced? Want to see some of Li Xue’s private photos?”

Lu Qin quickly shook his head, embarrassed. “No need. I believe you. You’re definitely her manager.”

He hadn’t meant to doubt her, it was just a reflex. After all, if someone randomly approached them on the street claiming to be a celebrity’s manager, who wouldn’t be skeptical?

Seeing Lu Qin finally convinced, Amy nodded in satisfaction.

“So, you’re in luck today. Getting an autograph or even a photo is no big deal.”

“Oh, thank you so much!” Lu Qin quickly expressed his gratitude.

“But there’s one condition.”

“Uh… what condition?” He asked cautiously.

“I want to try your fried noodles and ice jelly. And I’ve heard about your famous chicken tofu pudding — both Li Xue and I want to taste it.”

“No problem, that’s an easy request. But unfortunately, I’ve sold out of everything. I’d have to go back and restock.”

Amy sighed, “Yeah, I know. I stood in line for 20 minutes, and right when I got close, you packed up and left.”

Lu Qin’s food was clearly in high demand.

Amy grabbed his arm and said, “No rush. Let’s wait until after Li Xue’s performance. We can go together.”

“That might not work… I need to pick up my daughter by 5:30.”

“5:30? Don’t worry, you’ve got plenty of time,” Amy insisted and ushered Lu Qin to the VIP front row, where the immersive sound system filled the air.

On stage, Li Xue was dressed in a fresh, blue-and-white printed dress, her face beaming with a sweet smile. Her charming presence captivated everyone.

Lu Qin, who had never attended a concert or music festival before, was genuinely impressed. The atmosphere was electric, with countless young fans screaming.

“Li Xue, I love you!”

Even in the back, there was a middle-aged man in his 40s with a large round face covered in Li Xue stickers, shouting hoarsely, “Li Xue, my wife, I love you!”

This left Lu Qin speechless.

“Birds flying across the blue sky~”

“Gazing down at joyful faces~”

“Bright plants reaching for the sun~”

“Embracing tomorrow’s light~”

Li Xue’s pure, melodic voice echoed around him, and the upbeat song lifted everyone’s spirits. She truly had an amazing voice. Lu Qin took out his phone and snapped several photos, recording a few short videos. He couldn’t help but feel a bit regretful that his daughter wasn’t here to see this up close. She would have been overjoyed.

On stage, Li Xue sang and danced with energy. Her sharp eyes noticed Lu Qin in the crowd — his handsome face stood out and was hard to miss. After performing three songs, Li Xue took a break.

The host seized the opportunity to promote.

“Dive into the world of great stories at Feilu Novels! Recharge now and enjoy a 20% discount. Go, go, go!”

The crowd’s excitement waned during the break. Some people turned to their phones, others ate snacks. The young man sitting next to Lu Qin started scrolling through Douyin and came across a video in the local feed. It showed a group of kids at Starlight Kindergarten bawling their eyes out because they didn’t get any meat during lunch.

The video’s title read—“Handsome young dad’s culinary skills drive all the kids to tears with jealousy!”

The video included clips of the kids devouring meat, and even through the screen, one could tell that the twice-cooked pork was incredibly aromatic. The video had over 10k likes and 3,000 comments.

Intrigued, the young man gave it a like and opened the comment section.

Curious, the young man elbowed the person next to him and asked, “Hey, bro, who exactly is this ‘Boss Lu’? Do you know him?”

Lu Qin turned his head, looking puzzled.

“?”

“Do you know him or not?”

The young man, noticing the person beside him wasn’t responding, asked again. He looked up and glanced at Lu Qin. Something about him seemed familiar…

Wait a minute.

The young man’s eyes widened as he silently raised his phone, comparing the man in the video with the person sitting next to him…

It was him.

The realization left the young man stunned. What are the odds?

“Is it really you?”

“It’s me.”

“Holy crap!”

“…”

“Fried noodles? Twice-cooked pork? Chicken tofu pudding? I want to buy some!”

“Sorry, it’s all sold out,” Lu Qin replied.

“…”

“Well, would you like to join our Penguin chat group? I’ll notify everyone about my next food stall location.”

“Absolutely!” The young man quickly pulled out his phone, eager to join.

Before he could finish, Li Xue returned to the stage.

“It’s truly a pleasure to be here in Jiangcheng. The river view is stunning, the people are so lovely, and I absolutely adore the local cuisine!” She said with a charming smile.

“For my final performance, I’d like to invite an audience member to sing a duet with me. How about it?”

“Yeah–!!!” The crowd erupted in cheers.

“Alright! I’ll randomly choose someone from the audience!”

Li Xue’s sparkling eyes scanned the crowd, then landed on Lu Qin in the front row.

“You, handsome guy in the front row! Come up on stage!” She said with a playful wink.

Suddenly, all eyes turned to Lu Qin. Eight cameras were now pointed directly at him. Lu Qin was caught completely off guard. Before he could process what was happening, Li Xue had already made her way to the edge of the stage, locking eyes with him. A staff member quickly shoved a microphone into his hand.

Lu Qin awkwardly waved his hands in protest. “Uh, sorry… I can’t sing. Could you maybe pick someone else?”

The surrounding audience, especially the guys, started shouting:

“Dude, are you crazy? Why would you say no? Get up there!”

“Even if you can’t sing, just go up and scream a few lines! How often do you get the chance to perform with Li Xue on stage? And on TV, no less!”

“Yeah, seriously!”

“If you don’t want to, let me take your place! Give me the mic!”

“Let me up there! I’m the Karaoke King of Jiangcheng!”

…

Li Xue held her microphone, smiling sweetly at Lu Qin.

“Come on, handsome. Your voice sounds great, so I bet you sing well too. Don’t be shy, come on up!”

Before he knew it, the young man beside him gave him a push, forcing Lu Qin to his feet. He was sweating nervously, feeling like he had no choice but to go along with it.

Reluctantly, Lu Qin walked up to the stage, with the crowd cheering and clapping enthusiastically. There were even jealous shouts and whistles from the audience.

As he and Li Xue reached the center of the stage, the noise gradually quieted down.

With a smile, Li Xue asked, “Handsome, what’s your name?”

“Lu Qin.”

“Mr. Lu, what songs do you know how to sing?”

After thinking for a moment, Lu Qin slowly answered.

“Children’s songs.”

The crowd burst into laughter.

chapters.title: Chapter 57
chapters.content:
Chapter 57 Unexpected Duet: The Boss, the Song, and the Surprise

“Mr. Lu, what kind of songs do you sing?”

“Children’s songs.”

“Huh?”

Lu Qin’s honest answer left Li Xue silent for three seconds. Meanwhile, in the audience, laughter erupted.

“Children’s songs? Haha! Is this handsome guy here to be funny?”

“Haha! This is hilarious!”

“Isn’t that Boss Lu?”

“Wow! It really is Boss Lu!”

“Wait, Boss Lu can’t sing?”

“Turns out, the universe is fair. Boss Lu might be great at cooking, but singing? Not so much!”

“Come on, Boss Lu! You got this!”

“Children’s songs it is, let’s hear it!”

…

Among the crowd, Fang Ya held up her phone, recording Lu Qin as he took the stage. Earlier, she had been at the end of the line and couldn’t get the food she wanted. So, she leveraged her status as Lu Xiyao’s godmother to ask Lu Qin for a special favor. But before she knew it, Lu Qin had vanished, leaving only his little food cart parked on the side of the road. It turned out he had gone to watch the music festival—and even ended up on stage.

Li Xue asked again seriously, “Mr. Lu, are you sure you don’t know any other songs?”

“Nope, really don’t.”

“Alright then, which children’s song can you sing?”

“How about ‘Little Jumping Frog’?”

“Band, can you do ‘Little Jumping Frog’?”

The pianist backstage gave an OK sign. And so, the impromptu finale of the Jiangcheng Rainbow Plaza music festival concluded with a children’s song.

’ “The pond of happiness is planted, dreams turn into an ocean!”

’ “Big eyes, wide mouth, singing just as loud!”

’ “Give me a pair of little wings, and I can fly to the sun!”

’ “I believe miracles are right here!”

’ “La la la la la~”

’ “La la la la la~”

’ “La la la la~”

Li Xue and Lu Qin sang line by line together. At first, everyone thought it was just a funny act. But after a couple of lines, they realized the children’s song was surprisingly pleasant, refreshing in its simplicity. Li Xue’s voice was lively and charming, while Lu Qin’s singing was gentle and relaxed. The two harmonized quite well.

After the song ended, the audience erupted in applause, with some even shouting for an encore. Lu Qin shook hands with Li Xue, returned the microphone, and stepped off the stage. The music festival wrapped up for the day, and the host came on to deliver closing remarks as the audience began to disperse.

Backstage, Lu Qin was taken to a rest area by Amy. Li Xue had changed into a beige casual outfit, with her makeup still on.

“Mr. Lu, you actually sing pretty well.”

“Thank you,” Lu Qin responded with a hint of resignation. “Miss Li Xue, did you call me up on purpose?”

Lu Qin wasn’t naïve—he could tell from her expression that Li Xue knew who he was. She and her manager must have recognized him from his popular food stall.

Li Xue blinked her eyes and smiled sweetly.

“I heard your daughter is a fan of mine?”

“Yep.”

“Autographs and photos are no problem, but Boss Lu, you’ve got to satisfy our stomachs too.”

Li Xue touched her belly.

“Singing is hard work, you know, and now I’m starving.”

Given how delicious Lu Qin’s food was, even Amy didn’t dare mention dieting. After finishing work, all she wanted was to enjoy a good meal.

“Boss Lu, ride in our van with us to pick up your daughter. Tonight’s dinner is on you.”

“I’ll take my food cart. You guys can just follow behind me.”

“Alright, let’s go.”

…

“Boss, I’ve got three orders of fried rice noodles, iced mung bean soup, two boxes of crystal cakes, one serving of twice-cooked pork, and one chicken and tofu dish! All bought and ready!”

Song Xuanxuan boasted proudly about his heroic effort.

“Boss, you wouldn’t believe the crowd—it was a sea of people! The drums were pounding, and my sneakers almost got trampled! It took all my strength to buy these…”

Shen Muyan, with a calm expression, said, “Not bad. I’ll allow you to eat with us.”

Song Xuanxuan thought to himself, After all that hard work, if you didn’t let me eat, I’d be ready to commit seppuku right here!

He laid out the food on the coffee table, waiting for Shen Muyan to finish organizing her work before they could dig in. At that moment…

The office door was knocked on.

Song Xuanxuan went to open it and found her boss’s close friend standing outside.

“Fang Ya, sis!”

Song Xuanxuan greeted sweetly.

“Xuanxuan, you little cutie!” Fang Ya pinched her cheek playfully as she walked in. She took out her phone and headed straight to Shen Muyan’s desk.

“Hey, darling, let me show you something interesting…”

Out of the corner of her eye, Fang Ya noticed the spread of food on the coffee table.

“Huh? How did this happen?”

“When did you get all of this?” Fang Ya was practically drooling.

“Fang Ya, sis, I bought it,” Song Xuanxuan said and pointed to herself proudly.

“You went to Tianhong Plaza too?”

Song Xuanxuan was momentarily confused. “Too?”

Fang Ya pulled out her phone and showed photos of the long line she had taken earlier. It was quite a coincidence. Song Xuanxuan also opened the same photos on her phone. It turned out they had both lined up at Lu Qin’s food stall, but Song Xuanxuan had arrived 10 minutes earlier, so she managed to get her food while Fang Ya left empty-handed.

“Great! Let’s eat together!”

“Uh… we have to wait for the boss.”

“No need to wait for her. She’s a workaholic, skipping meals is her normal routine.”

Just as she finished speaking, Shen Muyan closed her folder and walked over to the sofa.

“Not waiting for me? Do you still want to mooch off my food?”

Shen Muyan glanced at Fang Ya.

“We must wait for CEO Shen!”

Fang Ya and Song Xuanxuan quickly scurried over to sit down.

Song Xuanxuan divided up the three orders of fried noodles and mung bean soup, then brought out three small bowls to share the chicken tofu dish. The twice-cooked pork was placed in the center for everyone to share.

“This twice-cooked pork smells amazing! I’ve never had it before!”

“So, this is chicken tofu? I’m curious about the taste!”

Fang Ya was the first to pick up her chopsticks and dig in. Song Xuanxuan waited for Shen Muyan to take a bite before she started eating as well. Though it was three women, their eating speed was anything but slow.

“Shen Muyan, your Lu Qin’s cooking skills are better than a five-star chef’s!”

Shen Muyan chewed on the twice-cooked pork with a stoic expression, ignoring Fang Ya. But at the mention of ‘your Lu Qin’, a faint blush spread across her fair cheeks.

Song Xuanxuan looked up, puzzled, as if she had stumbled upon some critical information. Fang Ya quickly backpedaled.

“Oops, my bad! I forgot you two are already divorced. Sorry!”

Song Xuanxuan was shocked.

Wait, Lu Boss is the boss’s ex-husband! Oh my God!

Only Fang Ya could get away with saying something like that in front of Shen Muyan. Shen Muyan shot Fang Ya a glare.

“Will some fried noodles shut you up?”

“Yes, absolutely.”

Fang Ya, not wanting to push her luck, focused on eating. The three of them polished off the entire spread, leaving not even the garlic shoots from the twice-cooked pork behind. The plates were completely cleared!

Fang Ya and Song Xuanxuan patted their satisfied bellies. Shen Muyan wiped her mouth with a napkin. She didn’t say anything, but her expression clearly showed that she thoroughly enjoyed the meal.

Grinning slyly, Fang Ya leaned closer and pulled out her phone, opening up a video.

On the screen, Shen Muyan saw Lu Qin on stage, singing a duet with a young woman. But the strange part? The song they were singing was… a children’s song.

chapters.title: Chapter 58
chapters.content:
Chapter 58 Lu Xiyao, Can I Come Home with You Today?

Shen Muyan was staring intently at the video. Fang Ya observed Shen Muyan. Although Shen Muyan’s face showed no expression, Fang Ya could tell that Shen Muyan was very interested in the video.

Song Xuanxuan curiously leaned in to watch, but Fang Ya pushed her back. Before the video ended, Fang Ya put away her phone.

Shen Muyan looked up.

“Want it?”

“What?”

“The video.”

“Send it to me.”

Shen Muyan spoke in a commanding tone.

Fang Ya, who wasn’t her subordinate, smiled and replied, “Red packet.”

A paid exchange.

Shen Muyan sent a big red packet to Fang Ya.

“Wow, Shen always pays generously,” Fang Ya said gleefully as she accepted the red packet and sent the video to Shen Muyan.

_____

Lu Qin had Li Xue’s nanny van follow his three-wheeled food cart.

“Keep up, don’t lose track.”

At first, the driver was dismissive, thinking, Seriously, how can a car like mine lose to a tricycle?

Who’s the real motor vehicle here?

I even have one more wheel than you!

But when the driver saw Lu Qin drifting around a corner with his tricycle, he realized the gravity of the situation. This wasn’t just a tricycle—it was a rocket on three wheels.

The driver straightened up, gripping the steering wheel tightly. Lu’s tricycle weaved effortlessly between electric bikes and pedestrians as if it were a hot knife through butter.

The nanny van had to brake frequently to yield to pedestrians. Sweat began to bead on the driver’s forehead.

Li Xue and Amy exchanged incredulous glances.

“Is Lu’s driving skill seriously that impressive?

Did he use to be a race car driver?”

Lu Qin glanced back and saw the nanny van falling behind, so he waited at a green light. The driver finally caught up, his eye twitching, feeling like his professional pride had been seriously challenged.

_______

At 5:35 PM, outside Starlight Kindergarten, a group of children surrounded Lu Xiyao like they were greeting a royal entourage.

“Lu Xiyao, will your dad come to the kindergarten again tomorrow?”

“Yao Yao, your uncle’s braised pork was so delicious! I had two bowls of rice today!”

“Lu Xiyao, here’s an apple just for you. I saved it!”

“Two chocolate sticks, both for you!”

“This is beef jerky my dad brought back from the city!”

…

The children emptied their pockets. Lu Xiyao held a big pile of snacks in both hands.

“If Chen Haojie doesn’t knock over my lunchbox again, my dad won’t need to come tomorrow.”

Though deep down, Lu Xiyao really wanted her dad to come every day. But she knew how hard Lu Qin worked at his food stall and didn’t want him to run back and forth.

“Chen Haojie, you should drop Lu Xiyao’s lunchbox again tomorrow so her dad can come cook for us again!”

“I-I wouldn’t dare!” Chen Haojie said.

Chen Haojie sneaked a glance at Lu Xiyao. He had made her cry at lunch today and was scared to make the same mistake again.

“Xiao Feng, didn’t you see your mom waiting at the gate? Hurry up and go home!”

“You little rascal, your dad’s been waiting for you forever. Why are you hanging around the girls? Get moving, your mom already made dinner!”

“Dad, can I go home with Lu Xiyao? Her dad’s cooking is so good! I don’t want to eat Mom’s food!”

“Wang Haoyu, why don’t you want to eat the food your mom made?”

“Have you ever seen black fried potatoes and green scrambled eggs?”

“No, but it must taste pretty bad.”

The other kids looked at Wang Haoyu with sympathetic eyes. In contrast, they became even more envious of Lu Xiyao.

Wang Haoyu really wanted to follow Lu Xiyao home and stubbornly refused to leave the school gate.

Until… Wang Haoyu’s dad tucked his shirt into his slacks, revealing a shiny Seven Wolves belt.

Wang Haoyu’s heart trembled, and reluctantly, he waved goodbye to Lu Xiyao and the teacher.

“Lu Xiyao, can I come home with you? I want to eat your dad’s cooking!”

“Yao Yao, I want to come too. When’s your dad coming to pick you up again?”

“Lu Xiyao, I’ll give you my dad, and you give me yours, okay? My dad’s really rich, he has two companies and lots of fancy cars!”

“No way! My dad is irreplaceable!” Lu Xiyao said.

Teacher Li listened to the kids’ conversations, feeling utterly speechless.

“You! And you! Your parents are all waiting at the gate. Go home already! School’s over!”

“Teacher Li, I want to go home with Lu Xiyao!”

“Me too!”

“No, you all have your own homes. How can you go home with Lu Xiyao?”

The parents at the school gate were puzzled. Why did their children all want to go home with that pretty little girl?

“Xiaofeng’s mom, you don’t know this, but Lu Xiyao’s dad’s cooking is amazing. All the kids crave it!”

“Oh? Is Lu Xiyao’s dad a chef?”

“No, he runs a food stall.”

“Why? If he’s such a good cook, why not work at a fancy restaurant? The pay would be better!”

“You don’t understand. You haven’t seen how popular his stall is. I’ve heard even celebrities line up for it!”

“Really?”

Just then, a flashy three-wheeled food cart stopped at the school gate. The parents around knew right away that it was Lu Xiyao’s dad.

“Mr. Lu, you’re here to pick up your daughter from school?”

“Boss Lu, how’s business today?”

“Today, Boss Lu set up his stall at Tianhong Plaza. I went, but I couldn’t even get in line. The crowd was huge—busier than a music festival!”

Before they could exchange a few more greetings, a group of children burst out in a rush.

“Uncle! Uncle!”

“Uncle, are you coming to the kindergarten again tomorrow? I want to eat more meat!”

“Lu Xiyao’s dad, can I come home with you? I want to eat your cooking!”

“Uncle, I want to go with you too!”

The parents and teachers were stunned.

“Xu Ziming, your mom is right there. Don’t you want your mom?”

“I want to go with Lu Xiyao’s dad! I want to eat the braised pork!”

“Your mom can cook braised pork too!”

“It’s not good at all. Only Lu Xiyao’s dad’s cooking is delicious!”

Lu Qin was surrounded by a swarm of children, while Lu Xiyao, with her chubby cheeks puffed up, was squeezed to the outside.

Teacher Li was speechless—she had never seen such a scene before.

It took three more teachers and two security guards to disperse the children and return them to their parents, finally restoring order. One little boy clung to Lu Qin’s leg and refused to let go. His father had to spank him several times before he tearfully let go and was carried away. Holding a big pile of snacks,

Lu Xiyao walked over to Lu Qin, looked up, and with a hint of grievance, said, “Daddy~! Will you abandon Yao Yao for the other kids~?”

“Of course not, Daddy only needs Yao Yao.”

“Hehe~!”

Lu Xiyao grinned, showing her little white teeth.

“Daddy~! Do you want some sachima*~?”

“Alright, I’ll have one.”

“I want one too!”

The nanny van door opened, revealing a sweet smiling face. The girl winked at Lu Xiyao.

“Huh?”

Lu Xiyao looked over, froze for a few seconds, and then exclaimed in surprise.

“Li Xue~! Wow~! It’s Sister Li Xue~!”

At her shout, the teachers, parents, children, security guards, and passersby all turned to look.

“Li Xue!”

“It really is Li Xue!!”

Note: Sachima (沙琪玛) is a traditional Chinese snack made of fried dough pieces bound together with a syrup, somewhat similar to a soft and chewy rice crispy treat.

chapters.title: Chapter 59
chapters.content:
Chapter 59 Li Xue Took Lu Xiyao Away!

A celebrity had arrived at the entrance of Starlight Kindergarten. It was the young singer loved by everyone—Li Xue.

And this singer reached out her hand to little Lu Xiyao, asking for some snacks.

“Mom, look, is that really Li Xue?”

“Li Xue!”

“My idol, Li Xue!”

…

Li Xue’s original songs are mostly uplifting and inspirational, making her popular even among children. Passersby also stopped in their tracks. Teachers and security guards were left gaping.

“Hello, Lu Xiyao! Boss Lu, your daughter is so adorable!”

Li Xue quickly picked up Lu Xiyao and carried her into the car, shutting the door swiftly. Lu Qin drove the nanny van away from the school gate as quickly as possible, leaving behind a crowd of stunned onlookers.

______

Inside the Nanny Van, Lu Xiyao sat next to Li Xue, with Amy sitting across from them. The little one blinked her eyes in disbelief. Meeting her idol suddenly left her mind buzzing. The more Li Xue looked at Lu Xiyao, the more she adored her.

How could there be such an adorable, sweet little girl? Her eyes and nose looked just like her father’s.

“Can I have a piece of sachima?”

Li Xue winked playfully at Lu Xiyao with her right eye.

“They’re all for you.”

The little girl eagerly stuffed all the snacks she was holding into Li Xue’s hands.

“Oh my! I can’t eat this much!”

In just a few minutes, laughter filled the nanny van. With all of them being women, a bond of sisterhood quickly formed.

“So, you came along because you like my dad’s fried noodles?”

Lu Xiyao’s soft, sweet voice melted Li Xue’s heart every time she called her ‘sister’.

Li Xue caressed the little girl’s smooth cheeks and nodded.

“And that ice crystal cake is also delicious!”

“There’s also braised pork~! I haven’t tried his chicken tofu flower yet~! But my dad will make it for us tonight~!”

As soon as she talked about her dad’s cooking, the little girl’s eyes sparkled. Before long, all three of them found themselves swallowing hungrily. Their stomachs started growling.

______

The nanny van followed the tricycle to an old part of town. They entered through a courtyard gate. Lu Qin invited Li Xue, Amy, and even the driver inside.

The driver was momentarily taken aback, touched by the unexpected invitation. Typically, he would have stayed outside during such visits.

“Li Xue, you all go sit in the living room. I’ll get you some water~!”

The little girl eagerly took on the role of hostess. The two-bedroom apartment was small but more than enough for the father and daughter to live in.

The place was meticulously clean. Although the floor was a bit worn, it was spotless. Everything was neatly organized, making the home feel cozy.

“No need to change shoes, just come on in,” Lu Qin said, seeing the three of them standing hesitantly at the door. In reality, the house only had slippers for him and his daughter.

“Alright, you all sit down. I’ll go make dinner.”

Lu Qin turned to Lu Xiyao.

“Yao Yao, you entertain our guests while Daddy makes dinner, okay?”

“Daddy, go ahead! You don’t need to worry about this, I’ve got it covered~!”

The little girl patted her small chest with confidence. The three guests couldn’t help but laugh.

Lu Xiyao poured three cups of warm water, which Lu Qin had boiled that morning and kept warm. After serving the water, she went to fetch some fruit. Li Xue couldn’t resist pulling the little girl over to sit beside her.

“Yao Yao, stop running around and come sit with us.”

“Okay~!”

All three of them had their eyes glued to Lu Xiyao, their gazes filled with affection. This little cutie was so well-behaved and sensible. It was clear that Boss Lu had done an excellent job raising his daughter.

“Yao Yao, do you want to watch some cartoons?”

“Sure~! At this time, Black Cat Detective is already on.”

The little one knew the schedule of all her favorite cartoons by heart.

“Black Cat Detective? I love that show too! The protector of the night, shining the light of justice into darkness.”

“Wow~! Sister, we like the same things~!”

Lu Xiyao’s big eyes sparkled with excitement. Li Xue smiled gently. She used to love watching Black Cat Detective and The Calabash Brothers when she was a child.

__________

In the Kitchen, Lu Qin was busy making braised pork. Today, between his morning and afternoon stalls, he had earned over 30,000 yuan, quite a decent income. The system points had also reached 1,782.

Lu Qin decided to start a lottery draw.

…

Lu Qin continued to draw 17 times, collecting quite a few good items. The mint candies, in particular, were a hit among his customers. The mint candies from the system were highly effective at freshening breath, came in various fruity flavors, and had clean ingredient lists.

Customers who tried them for the first time would always come back to grab a few more pieces. As a result, these free mint candies ran out quickly.

This time, the lottery draw yielded three max-level cooking skills, which were the most valuable prizes.

Not a bad haul this time, Lu Qin thought to himself.

Suddenly, his mind was flooded with the perfect techniques and experience for making three different dishes. Additionally, the muscle memory was instantly ingrained into his body. He plated the braised pork and then started on scrambled eggs with tomatoes.

This dish is a very common home-cooked meal, known for its simplicity. Its sweet and sour flavor appeals to a wide range of tastes. However, the simpler a dish is, the harder it is to elevate its flavor. Perfecting it is quite challenging.

Even before gaining the max-level skill, Lu Qin’s scrambled eggs with tomatoes were already quite delicious. But the max-level version required precise control over the heat, the perfect ratio of seasonings, the speed of stir-frying, and even the size of the ingredients’ cuts. These finer details further enhanced the flavor and texture of the dish.

In just six minutes, Lu Qin finished a plate of scrambled eggs with tomatoes. The dish, cooked with max-level skill, had a brighter, more vibrant color and looked incredibly appetizing.

Lu Qin took a bite with his chopsticks, the eggs were smooth and tender, while the tomatoes were soft and juicy. The texture and flavor were taken to a whole new level. This was a perfect plate of scrambled eggs with tomatoes—there was no better version.

Even the best chefs in a restaurant couldn’t improve this dish by much. At best, they could make it slightly better than the average home-cooked version. But Lu Qin’s max-level version was on an entirely different level.

Those with sensitive palates would especially be able to taste the difference. Next, Lu Qin made a new dish—Imperial Chicken Wings.

“Big Sis, are you hungry? Let me check on how my dad’s dinner is coming along,” Lu Xiyao said eagerly.

Before Li Xue and the others could respond, the little girl jumped off the sofa and ran to the kitchen door. In an instant, a rich aroma wafted into the living room.

Lu Xiyao exclaimed.

“Wow~! It smells so good~!”

chapters.title: Chapter 60
chapters.content:
Chapter 60 Using All Their Strength to Feast!

Everyone took their seats. The dining table was covered with various dishes—braised pork, scrambled eggs with tomatoes, chicken tofu flower, fried rice noodles, Imperial chicken wings, and a sweet green bean dessert. All of these were prepared with max-level cooking skills, offering a peak culinary experience.

Li Xue, Amy, the driver, and Lu Xiyao were deeply captivated by the spread. Even though there were no fancy seafood dishes or fish, Li Xue and the others didn’t underestimate these home-cooked dishes.

Why?

Just the presentation of the dishes was comparable to those in a five-star restaurant. And the aroma—simply irresistible.

Li Xue, being a celebrity, had tasted many high-end dishes in prestigious restaurants, but none of them had such a rich and enticing fragrance. It even felt like if she couldn’t taste it, it would be worse than dying!

The driver quickly covered his mouth, genuinely worried that he might drool and embarrass himself.

“What are you waiting for? Dig in! These are just home-cooked dishes, don’t hold back,” Lu Qin said as he served five bowls of rice and sat down.

“How could we look down on them! I’ve never smelled anything this fragrant!”

“Alright, we won’t be polite then. Thank you for the meal!”

No one held back anymore, they were practically starving and immediately started eating. The Imperial chicken wings were a bit far from the little one, so Lu Qin helped her by placing a piece on her plate.

“Thank you, Daddy~!”

The little girl took a bite, savoring the tender and juicy chicken wings. It was so delicious she could hardly contain her excitement.

“This is so yummy~!”

Oil smeared the corners of her mouth. Li Xue was in a frenzy, grabbing one dish after another. Each dish was a masterpiece, absolutely top-tier, and her hand was getting tired from using the chopsticks so much. But she couldn’t stop.

These dishes were just too delicious! Amy was in awe—she had never imagined that food this good could exist.

“Boss Lu, you’re incredible! Every single dish is so delicious it could make me cry!”

The driver could only give a thumbs-up between bites, not bothering to speak as he focused on eating. He wanted to savor every mouthful, knowing he might never get to eat food this good again.

Li Xue quickly finished a bowl of rice and, without any hesitation, said to Lu Qin, “I’m not full yet. I’ll have another bowl.”

Lu Qin chuckled.

“Help yourself, there’s plenty of rice.”

Anticipating this, he had cooked extra rice. The pot was right there on the table, and Li Xue helped herself. The others, having finished their first bowls, also started helping themselves to a second serving.

Lu Xiyao was eating with such joy that she started bobbing her head. Even Li Xue unconsciously bobbed her head a little. Amy watched in amazement—an adult imitating a child!

During the entire dinner, hardly anyone spoke. They were too busy eating, focusing entirely on the food. Half an hour later, everyone leaned back in their chairs, looking completely satisfied and happy.

They looked like they had been starving for three days and had just had a luxurious feast. The dishes on the table were all wiped clean.

Not even a tiny piece of meat was left on the bones of the Imperial chicken wings. Li Xue had devoured two and a half bowls of rice—something she hadn’t done in years. She was stuffed to the brim.

“Ai, my dear Xue, how could you eat so much? You’re an idol, you have to watch your figure. Hic!”

Amy, who had used all her energy to eat, now looked exhausted from being so full. Li Xue rolled her eyes, too tired to respond. Amy herself had polished off three big bowls of rice.

The driver, feeling a bit embarrassed, had eaten four bowls and now had a slightly swollen belly.

“I’m so full~!”

Lu Xiyao patted her round belly, looking extremely happy. Lu Qin got up to clear the table, and Li Xue also stood up, intending to help.

“I’ve got this, it’s just a few dishes,” Lu Qin said as he took the plates from Li Xue’s hands.

In just a few swift moves, he stacked all the dishes and cutlery neatly. He was very efficient.

Li Xue watched his back and thought to herself, What a good man!

“Yao Yao, I’m so jealous of you. You have a dad who cooks such delicious food!”

“Hehe~!”

Lu Xiyao grinned proudly, her eyes full of joy.

After cleaning up the kitchen, Li Xue and her group were ready to leave. Li Xue had a fan signing event scheduled for tomorrow, and her schedule in Jiangcheng was fully packed for the next few days. She was very busy.

Meeting Lu Qin and his daughter today and having dinner at their place was already a bit sudden. Before leaving, Li Xue signed her name on Lu Xiyao’s favorite sketchbook, writing–[Wishing little Lu Xiyao happiness every day, growing up carefree!].

Lu Qin took out his phone and snapped a few photos of his daughter with Li Xue.

“Boss Lu, let’s add each other on WeChat so you can send me those photos.”

“Sure, no problem.”

The two exchanged WeChat contacts—it was Li Xue’s frequently used account.

Lu Xiyao beamed with happiness.

“Big sister, can I message you in the future~?”

“Of course you can. You can also send voice messages or video calls. If I’m not busy, I’ll always answer,” Li Xue said as she playfully pinched the little girl’s soft cheeks.

“Yay~!” The little girl jumped up and down with joy.

The nanny van disappeared into the night.

Lu Qin gently patted Xiyao’s little head, “Let’s go inside.”

“Okay~!”

Tonight, Lu Qin was not going to set up his food stall, so he sent an early message to the group chat. Immediately, a chorus of groans and complaints erupted in the group. Messages started flooding in rapidly.

A few loyal customers who had already set up their stools and were waiting at the university night market were so frustrated they almost lost their minds. To this, Lu Qin could only apologize, explaining that he had personal matters to attend to.

The customers understood. After all, everyone has unexpected situations from time to time. But tonight, it seemed, a group of customers was destined to be left restless and unsatisfied.

Lu Qin muted the group chat and then closed the app.He spent an hour playing checkers with the little one. When it was time, he sent her off to wash up and get ready for bed.

“Daddy~! Teacher Li said that there’s a parent-child activity at the kindergarten this Friday. All the other kids’ moms and dads will be there.”

Tucked under her blanket, Xiyao poked her little head out and looked up at Lu Qin as she spoke.

Usually, the little girl would say that having her dad was enough.

But seeing all her classmates having both moms and dads to dote on them, she couldn’t help feeling a bit envious. Lu Qin was fully aware of this.

Before the divorce, at least Xiyao could still feel Shen Muyan’s presence. Now that they were divorced, she only saw her mother once a week, leaving her with little maternal affection.

After coaxing the little one to sleep, Lu Qin sat on the sofa in the living room, pulled out his phone, and opened WeChat. He found Shen Muyan’s profile picture and began typing with his slender fingers.

“Do you have time this Friday?” Lu Qin asked.

On the other side, Shen Muyan had just finished a long day at work and was soaking in her bathtub at home. She held her phone, watching the video Fang Ya had sent her earlier.

“Happy little frog, leapfrog,

A happy little frog in the pond,

When it dances, it looks like it’s possessed by a prince,

With cool eyes that no other frog can match,

One day it will be awakened by a princess…”

This was the first time Shen Muyan had seen Lu Qin sing, even if it was… a children’s song. Though he wasn’t as professional at singing as he was at cooking, it was still quite decent.

For the first time, the man on stage seemed a bit… adorable. Unconsciously, a small smile curved on Shen Muyan’s lips.

Ding ding!

Fang Ya sent over an exasperated emoji.

“Lu Qin isn’t setting up his stall tonight!! I was craving a late-night snack!!”

Shen Muyan ignored her and received another WeChat message. It was from a familiar profile picture. She clicked on it.

chapters.title: Chapter 61
chapters.content:
Chapter 61 Shen Muyan Agrees to Go to Kindergarten! Boss Zhao’s Wife Wants to Restart Her Career!

Shen Muyan wasn’t sure why, but when she saw Lu Qin’s message, her first instinct was to reply, Yes, I’m free.

Then she caught herself, her heart skipping a beat. She responded in a calm tone.

Shen Muyan’s heart stirred, and she sent a message to Song Xuanxuan.

Once everything was arranged, Shen Muyan replied to Lu Qin.

After that, the chat fell into a brief silence. Shen Muyan’s slender fingers tapped lightly on her phone screen, but she wasn’t sure what else to say.

Shen Muyan stayed silent for a few seconds.

The chat ended. Shen Muyan put her phone aside and continued soaking in the bath. At that moment, her mood unexpectedly lifted. She closed her eyes slightly and began to hum a tune softly.

“In the pond of happiness, dreams grow into an ocean,

Wide-eyed and big-mouthed, singing aloud together…”

______

The next day, at 5:30 a.m., Lu Qin woke up. Whether it was habit or the fatigue-dispelling effects of his systematic routine, Lu Qin always felt energized when he woke up early, with no desire to sleep in.

The May morning was already bright. After a light stretch, he freshened up. At this moment, the door to the little one’s room opened.

“Daddy~! You’re up so early~?”

The little one, still half-asleep, looked like her soul was still wandering in dreamland.

“Daddy slept well last night and woke up early, so I thought I’d go to the market to buy ingredients and prepare breakfast and set up the morning food stall.”

The little one stretched out her small arms, and Lu Qin picked her up.

“Daddy~! Hugs~!”

Her soft little cheeks rubbed against his face.

“Daddy~! That’s so tiring for you~! Yaoyao feels bad~!”

“No, Daddy’s not tired at all. If I ever get tired, I’ll take a rest.”

“Really~?”

“Really.”

“Why did Yaoyao wake up so early?”

“I need to go potty~!”

After using the bathroom, the little one climbed back into bed and quickly fell asleep again. Lu Qin drank half a glass of warm water, then rode his three-wheeled food cart out of the courtyard.

In the neighborhood fitness area, many elderly residents were already out for their morning exercise, practicing tai chi, walking backward, clapping their hands…

Chen Dahai had quietly rented a place in this old neighborhood and quickly built rapport with Grandpa Ge and the other long-time residents.

“Wow, Old Chen, how many years have you been practicing this tai chi push-hands? Your borrowed force technique is amazing—I’m struggling to keep up!”

Chen Dahai replied smugly, “How about that, Old Ge? Now you see what it means to overcome action with stillness.”

“Wow, impressive!”

As Lu Qin’s food cart passed by, he slowed down.

“Grandpa Ge, practicing, I see?”

Lu Qin glanced at Chen Dahai and found him somewhat familiar.

Chen Dahai smiled and said, “Boss Lu, what’s the matter? Don’t recognize me anymore?”

“I bought fried noodles from your stall yesterday and even joined your WeChat group,” Chen added with a grin.

Lu Qin smiled back.

“No wonder you look familiar.”

“I’m Chen Dahai. Just call me Old Chen.”

“I’ll call you Uncle Chen.”

“Anything works. By the way, what time do you set up your food stall? Just seeing you reminds me of your fried noodles and icy cakes. My cravings are already kicking in!”

“A little after 8 a.m. I drop my daughter at kindergarten before setting up the stall.”

Grandpa Ge walked over, patting Lu Qin on the shoulder with a cheerful smile.

“Little Lu, can you give Grandpa Ge an autograph?”

Lu Qin was confused.

“Grandpa Ge, I’m not a celebrity. What would you need my autograph for?”

Grandpa Ge chuckled, pulling out his phone and opening Douyin.

“At yesterday’s music festival, you stole the show singing with a big star!”

Chen Dahai hadn’t heard about this and curiously took the phone to have a look.
