“Wow, Boss Lu, you really look great on camera!”

Lu Qin felt a bit embarrassed.

“You flatter me.”

Chen Dahai teased.

“You should learn a few more songs. Your voice isn’t half bad.”

Lu Qin gave a helpless smile. He rarely even went to karaoke bars and thought singing a children’s song decently was already an achievement. Besides, he doubted he’d ever have the chance to be on stage again. The other elderly neighbors noticed Lu Qin and came over enthusiastically.

“Boss Lu, what time will you set up your stall? My grandson’s been pestering me to get your icy cakes!”

“Yesterday, I went to Tianhong Plaza for your fried noodles, and the line was so long! Boss Lu, you’re amazing—your snack stall has a fanbase!”

“Make sure to stock up when you shop for ingredients. I’ve missed out a few times because you sold out!”

…

After greeting the neighbors, Lu Qin headed to the market. He spent over half an hour there, purchasing enough rice noodles, eggs, meat, vegetables, and seasonings. He also ordered 4,000 disposable meal boxes from Boss Zhao. Boss Zhao’s wife was increasingly surprised and envious.

“Old Zhao, this is incredible. Look at Boss Lu’s business. From a few hundred meal boxes a day to 4,000 now. How many orders does he sell daily? He makes in a day what we make in a month!”

“Not a month,” Boss Zhao corrected, “probably two months.”

His wife’s eyes sparkled with excitement.

“Are these stalls popular now? Should I restart my old business?”

“Come on, get real,” Boss Zhao retorted. “Boss Lu’s business is booming because of his skills. Do you have what it takes to match him?”

Although Boss Zhao tried to play it cool, it was clear he felt a bit envious. But there was no denying it—Lu Qin’s talent was unmatched.

After finalizing the meal box order, Lu Qin left the market. Unlike other ingredients, Lu Qin bought meat and vegetables daily, never stocking up to ensure the freshness of his food. This shopping trip cost him 1,938.40 yuan.

Yesterday, Lu Qin unlocked a max-level skill for Gan Gao Soup(Liver Soup), but he had no plans to sell it for now. The dish was too complex and time-consuming to make, and its taste wasn’t universally appealing—unsuitable for a food stall.

He also decided not to sell stir-fried tomatoes with eggs. Priced under 15 yuan per portion, the dish wouldn’t generate much profit. Cooking it in large quantities would turn it into a generic bulk dish, ruining the taste. If cooked in smaller amounts, the time investment wouldn’t be worth it compared to higher-profit dishes like twice-cooked pork. In any case, hearty dishes sold better.

chapters.title: Chapter 62
chapters.content:
Chapter 62 I Want Him to be My Son-in-law!

“Daddy~! I had a dream! I was swimming in a strawberry-flavored ice cream cone!” Exclaimed Lu Xiyao, slurping the egg noodles Lu Qin had made for her.

“Weren’t you cold? Ice cream is freezing,” Lu Qin asked with a laugh.

“Daddy~! You missed the point! Don’t you realize how wonderful it is to have an ice cream cone that big?”

“I don’t know about that, but I do know eating too much ice cream can give you a stomachache. If you eat it often, your stomach might even catch a cold.”

“Stomach cold? Can a stomach catch a cold?”

“Of course, so you should eat less ice cream.”

“I think it’s fine if my stomach catches a cold. You can just buy me medicine, right?”

Lu Qin shook his head, smiling. The little one had become quite clever. Whenever he tried to persuade her to eat less ice cream, she always came up with an excuse.

After dropping Lu Xiyao off at the entrance of Starlight Kindergarten, a crowd of kids swarmed around them as soon as Lu Qin’s food cart appeared.

The enthusiastic greetings from the kids created such a lively scene that even the teachers and parents were left stunned.

“Xiyao, did big sister Li Xue go home with you yesterday?”

“Uncle, does your cart have lots of delicious food?”

“Xiyao, I saw you riding in Li Xue’s car yesterday. That’s so cool! Are you two close?”

“Of course we are! She’s my big sister! We watched cartoons together, and she even sang for me!”

The kids were filled with envy, and even the parents couldn’t help but ask:

“Boss Lu, is Li Xue a relative of yours?”

“Can we get her autograph? Our whole family are fans!”

“Will she come to the kindergarten again? I’d love a photo with her.”

Someone else might have been tempted to show off, but Lu Qin spoke honestly.

“She’s actually a customer. My daughter wanted an autograph, and by chance, we invited her over as a guest.”

“Oh, I see! Wow, Xiyao’s dad is amazing! Even celebrities love his cooking!”

“Yeah! Xiyao’s dad is so cool! Li Xue is your fan now!”

…

After exchanging a few words, Lu Qin prepared to head off with his cart.

“Xiyao, Daddy’s going to set up the breakfast stall now. Be good in kindergarten and listen to your teacher, okay?”

The little one waved enthusiastically.

“Got it, Daddy~! Don’t work too hard~! I’ll feel bad for you~!”

“Bye, Uncle!”

“Uncle, where’s your stall? I’ll ask my mom to buy something!”

“Daddy, follow Uncle with the car! I want green bean soup!”

It was undeniable—Lu Qin and his daughter had become the ‘stars’ of Starlight Kindergarten.

As Lu Xiyao was ushered into the school by her peers, Lu Qin’s food cart was trailed by several cars and electric scooters.

When Lu Qin arrived at Sunshine Elementary School and set up his stall, a line of over 30 customers had already formed. It was rare to see such a bustling breakfast stall—Lu Qin’s was the only one this popular. Nearby stall owners were stunned.

One owner stared, dumbfounded, for over ten seconds, accidentally burning the scallion pancake on their griddle.

A customer pointed it out with glee.

“Boss, your pancake’s burnt! Guess I won’t buy it then!”

The customer immediately turned and joined the line at Lu Qin’s stall. Chen Dahai, grinning from ear to ear, bought three portions of fried rice noodles, five cups of green bean soup, and two boxes of icy cakes. With his bags full, he walked happily to the corner of the street, where he climbed into a Bentley sedan.

“Dad, why did you make me come pick you up?” Asked the woman in the driver’s seat, her tone slightly annoyed.

“What, you don’t want to pick up your dad? All those years I raised you for nothing, huh?”

Chen Dahai glared at her before shoving a portion of fried noodles and a cup of green bean soup into her hands.

“Your mom said you’ve been skipping breakfast lately.”

Though his tone was gruff, Chen Dahai clearly doted on his daughter, Chen Yue.

“I just don’t have much of an appetite in the morning.”

“ Hmph! Is it because you think your mom’s cooking isn’t good enough?”

Chen Yue stuck out her tongue playfully.

“You’re one to talk. Aren’t you the one who uses ‘morning exercise’ as an excuse to wander off every day?”

Chen Dahai sighed.

“Your mom loves cooking, but we’re the ones who suffer for it!”

“Dad, is this your breakfast? Let me take you somewhere fancy.”

Chen Yue looked disdainfully at the fried noodles and set them aside.

“You don’t know what you’re missing! Do you have any idea how many people line up for these?”

“I don’t like fried noodles—they’re too greasy.”

“Fine, if you won’t eat them, I will.”

Chen Dahai opened one of the portions and began eating, the rich aroma filling the car.

“So fragrant?”

Chen Yue couldn’t help but swallow hard. Chen Dahai took a sip of green bean soup, his face full of satisfaction.

“Let me try it.”

Chen Yue couldn’t resist opening a box of fried noodles.

Chen Dahai, having anticipated this outcome, glanced at her and said, “Hah, and who just said they wouldn’t eat it?”

Chen Yue ignored him, her full attention on the noodles. She took a bite and found them unbelievably delicious.

“Oh my gosh, this is amazing!”

She started eating in big bites, completely falling victim to the ‘true fragrance law’.

The father and daughter polished off three portions of fried noodles and four cups of green bean soup.

“ Phew! I’ve never eaten this much for breakfast before. But it was so worth it!”

Chen Yue’s preconceived notions were shattered—she’d never expected fried noodles could taste like this.

“I was planning to bring some back for your mom, but… oh well.”

“Dad, what’s in these two boxes?”

“Icy cakes. Haven’t seen them before, have you?”

Chen Dahai opened one box and handed her a piece to try.

“It’s delicious! Soft, chewy, sweet, and a bit icy—better than any dessert I’ve ever had! The flavor and texture are perfect!”

Chen Dahai smirked with pride.

“Of course it is!”

“Dad, where did you buy it?”

“At the school gate.”

The Bentley turned a corner and stopped near the school entrance.

Chen Yue’s eyes widened.

“This stall? Look at that crowd—it’s terrifying!”

By now, there were probably 40 to 50 people in line. Even during peak hours, some of Jiangcheng’s most popular restaurants didn’t have lines this long.

Shaking her head, Chen Yue said, “I was planning to buy more fried noodles and icy cakes, but I guess I was naive.”

Chen Dahai replied, “Told you it wouldn’t be easy.”

The school entrance wasn’t a place for long stops, so the Bentley slowly pulled away. Chen Dahai looked at his daughter with a smile.

“So, what do you think?”

Chen Yue, focused on the road, replied, “Top-tier delicious. Dad, I’ve made up my mind—I’m going to have breakfast from this stall every day!”

Chen Dahai rolled his eyes.

“Who’s asking about the food? I mean the young man.”

Chen Yue was puzzled.

“What young man?”

“The stall owner.”

“I didn’t see him. There were too many customers crowding the place.”

“Then look at this.”

At a red light, Chen Dahai pulled out his phone and opened his gallery, showing her a photo he had sneakily taken of Lu Qin.

Chen Yue glanced at the picture, her eyes lighting up slightly.

“This is the stall owner?”

“Yep. Not bad, right?”

Chen Yue nodded.

“He’s quite handsome.”

Then, as if realizing something, she gave Chen Dahai a wary look.

“Dad, what are you trying to say?”

“I want him to be our family’s son-in-law.”

SCREECH!

Chen Yue slammed on the brakes, staring at Chen Dahai in shock.

“WHAT?”

chapters.title: Chapter 63
chapters.content:
Chapter 63 You Want Me to Pursue Him? Officer, I Need to Report a Case!”

Chen Yue exclaimed in shock.

“Dad, are you really selling out your daughter for a bite of food?”

Granted, the man’s cooking was absolutely delicious. If she could eat it every day, life would indeed be wonderful.

But still…

Chen Yue shook her head vigorously. Forced relationships weren’t an option, after all. Feelings couldn’t be manufactured like business deals.

“I know feelings can’t be forced. I’m not asking you to push it. Just take your time and get to know him,” Chen Dahai said, almost as if he could hear her thoughts.

No one knew their daughter better than a father.

Chen Yue sighed in exasperation. In the past, so many talented young men had failed to catch Chen Dahai’s eye.

But now, a food stall owner?

Had her father lost his mind?

While Chen Yue was deep in thought, Chen Dahai was busy eating icy cakes, one piece after another.

“Dad, what are you…”

Chen Yue turned her head to comment, only to freeze. Here they were discussing serious matters, and he was sneaking bites! One box of icy cakes was down to the last piece!

Speechless, Chen Yue snatched the final piece and stuffed it into her mouth. Then, pulling the car to the side of the road, she declared, “Dad, we’re splitting the second box evenly!”

“Who paid for it? Who’s the dad here? Who said we’re splitting anything?”

Chen Dahai held the second box protectively, like a dragon guarding its treasure. Chen Yue wanted to eat more, but her father wasn’t budging.

“Dad!”

“In front of icy cakes, don’t call me Dad.”

“…”

Chen Dahai glanced at his daughter and shifted the conversation.

“Unless, you consider what I mentioned earlier.”

“What are you talking about?”

“Getting to know that young man.”

“Is this a matchmaking scheme?”

“More or less.”

“Fine, I’ll consider it. But if it doesn’t work out, you can’t push it. Send me his WeChat contact.”

Chen Yue figured she was single anyway, and the guy was decent-looking. More importantly, he could satisfy her picky palate. Besides, she wasn’t impressed with the rich playboys who had been chasing her.

Chen Dahai shook his head.

“I don’t have his WeChat.”

“Then give me his phone number.”

“No phone number either.”

“…”

“Dad, what are you even trying to do?”

Chen Dahai chuckled.

“He doesn’t know me well.”

Chen Yue’s eyes widened.

“Not well!”

Why are you matchmaking strangers, then?

“But I’ve observed him. He’s a good man,” Chen Dahai said with a smile. “You’ll have to take the initiative to get to know him yourself.”

“You’re telling me I have to pursue him?” Chen Yue asked incredulously.

Chen Dahai nodded without hesitation.

“Dad, I’m busy. I need to head back to the office.”

“What about the icy cakes?”

“Hand them over.”

“Nope. Go ask your future boyfriend for some!”

“…”

At Outside Sunshine Elementary School, Maidejun arrived on his motorized scooter, accompanied by his seven- or eight-year-old grandson.

“Old Ge, you’re here so early?”

“It’s you who’s late, Old Mai. Is this your grandson? Such a sturdy little guy!”

Maidejun led his grandson into the queue. This retired national banquet chef had unexpectedly become a fan of Lu Qin’s cooking. Where could one even begin to explain this?

Wave after wave of customers came and went, with more constantly arriving. Lu Qin’s fried noodle stall was growing busier every day. The number of new customers increased daily, almost exponentially.

“Grandpa, is this food really better than what you make?”

The little boy was skeptical. His grandpa often prepared all kinds of delicacies for him, and he had a very picky palate.

“You’ll find out soon enough,” Maidejun said confidently.

Lu Qin’s swift, elegant movements as he stir-fried the rice noodles left everyone amazed. His skill level had clearly maxed out. Some new customers couldn’t help but marvel.

“Boss, every time you flip the wok, the force seems exactly the same! Practice really does make perfect!”

“No wonder the noodles taste so good!”

One customer pulled out their phone to record a video.

“Boss Lu, can I record a video of you cooking so I can try it at home? You don’t mind, do you?”

Lu Qin shook his head, smiling.

“Not at all, go ahead.”

A nearby auntie chimed in, “Recording won’t help. I’ve tried it before, but no matter how hard I try, it never tastes the same. You’ll still end up coming back to buy it.”

A self-proclaimed ‘expert’ customer offered an explanation.

“This stir-fried rice noodle recipe is simple. Lots of people know how to make it. But the reason Boss Lu’s is so delicious is because of his wok-tossing skills and precise control of heat. You can’t just learn that.”

Everyone nodded in agreement. It made sense. Skills like that wok toss couldn’t be mastered in just a few years—it’d take a decade, at least.

Even a young girl was intently filming a close-up video.

A middle-aged man joked, “Miss, you might as well stick your camera right in Boss Lu’s face. That’ll give you the clearest view.”

The girl blushed furiously, quickly putting away her phone and quietly rejoining the line. The atmosphere in the queue was lively, with customers occasionally cracking jokes and chatting with each other.

An elderly man paid for a portion of noodles and a cup of green bean soup via QR code. His expression was stern, and as he left, he gave Lu Qin a suspicious glance.

The old man crossed three streets and arrived at the entrance of the Chengnan Police Station. Squatting by the door, he opened the box of fried noodles and began wolfing it down.

Three minutes later, he’d finished every bite. The green bean soup was gone too. After wiping his mouth, his eyes lit up with certainty—Yes, it’s this taste!

The old man marched into the police station with a resolute expression. As soon as he entered, he declared to the officers,

“Officers, you need to investigate the fried noodle stall below my apartment!”

The officer on duty looked confused.

“Sir, what’s wrong with the fried noodle stall?”

“The fried noodles are just too good!”

Hearing this, the officer couldn’t help but laugh.

He’d heard of food so good it made people cry or rave about it, but this was the first time he’d heard of food so good it warranted a police report.

The officer patiently asked, “Sir, isn’t good food a good thing? Why are you reporting it?”

Another officer glanced at the old man skeptically. Was he perhaps a rival stall owner, unable to compete and resorting to dirty tricks? It wasn’t unheard of.

The old man elaborated, “Ever since I started eating those fried noodles, I can’t stop thinking about them. I’ve been eating there three times a day for four or five days straight, and I never get tired of it!”

“Officers, I’ve lived for decades and have never encountered a food so addictive! I strongly suspect that this fried noodle stall is using illegal additives!”

The old man spoke with such conviction that the officers’ smiles faded. Just then, another officer walked over and commented, “There have been similar cases in other provinces. Vendors have been caught adding poppy powder to cold noodles, making customers crave more. Last year, there were cases of hot pot and braised meat shops doing the same thing.”

The two officers exchanged a glance, sensing that something was amiss. They decided to have the old man lead them to the stall. When the police car, with its flashing red and blue lights, pulled up outside Sunshine Elementary School, it immediately caught everyone’s attention.

“Is that the city management? Oh no, Boss Lu, are they shutting you down? I haven’t gotten my noodles yet!”

“That’s not city management—it’s a police car!”

“What? The police?”

The crowd grew curious, their eyes fixed on the police car.

The old man, followed by two officers, walked straight towards Lu Qin’s stall.

“Officers, this is the fried noodle stall I was talking about!”

chapters.title: Chapter 64
chapters.content:
Chapter 64 Lu Qin Gets Investigated! Loyal Customers Stick Around!

The elderly man led the police officers straight to Lu Qin’s fried noodle stall.

Everyone around—the customers in line, nearby vendors, parents, and students at the school gate—immediately turned their attention to the scene.

“What’s going on?”

“Calling the police over a food stall? Isn’t this a bit much?”

“Isn’t it the city management’s job to handle street vendors?”

“There have been food stalls here for years without any issues. What’s happening now?”

“I don’t know why, but seeing the police always makes me nervous!”

…

Lu Qin handed a packed portion of fried noodles to a customer, who quickly paid via QR code and stepped aside, keeping an eye on the unfolding scene.

The line of customers made way as the elderly man and two officers approached Lu Qin’s stall.

Lu Qin looked up at the police, confusion evident on his face.

“Officers, please investigate,” the elderly man said sternly, pointing at Lu Qin.

The surrounding crowd erupted into chatter.

“They’re not here to shut him down. Did Boss Lu do something illegal?”

“No way! Boss Lu is such a nice guy—he doesn’t seem like the type.”

“Stop guessing! I trust that Boss Lu hasn’t done anything wrong!”

“Yeah, me too. There’s no way he’s a bad person!”

“If anything happens to Boss Lu, we won’t get to eat his delicious food anymore!”

…

The two officers saluted Lu Qin and politely introduced themselves.

“Sir, we’re officers from the Chengnan Police Station. My badge number is 548789.”

“And mine is 470023.”

“We received a report twenty minutes ago alleging that your food poses safety concerns. Please cooperate with our investigation.”

Lu Qin was momentarily stunned, his confusion deepening. The crowd was equally taken aback.

“Food safety issues?”

“No way! Boss Lu’s stall is the cleanest I’ve ever seen!”

“Exactly! His food cart is more hygienic than my own kitchen!”

“The officers are talking about food safety, not cleanliness!”

…

Lu Qin nodded calmly.

“Alright, I’ll cooperate with your investigation.”

He glanced at the elderly man briefly, vaguely remembering him as a previous customer. He couldn’t understand why this man had reported him.

“Please show us your ID and health certificate,” one officer requested.

Since Lu Qin wasn’t operating at a fixed location, he didn’t need a food business license. He handed over his documents.

While one officer examined the paperwork, the other inspected the food cart.

“Mr. Lu, what do you mainly sell at your stall?”

“I started with fried noodles and green bean soup. Later, I added icy cakes, twice-cooked pork, and chicken flower tofu.”

“That’s quite a variety.”

The officer checked all the ingredients and seasonings on the cart, finding only common, standard items and nothing suspicious.

“Where’s the twice-cooked pork and chicken flower tofu?”

“I don’t sell those in the morning. They’re for the afternoon and evening.”

“Got it.”

As they spoke, the officer observed Lu Qin closely, looking for any signs of guilt or nervousness. However, Lu Qin remained completely calm, showing no such reactions. Of course, the officer didn’t rule out the possibility that Lu Qin simply had excellent composure.

“We’ll need to take samples of your ingredients and food items for testing. Is that alright?”

“Sure, go ahead.”

The officers collected samples of the rice noodles, green bean soup, and icy cakes, placing them into evidence bags in the police car.

“We’ll send these for testing. The results should be ready this morning. Mr. Lu, would you mind accompanying us to the station in the meantime?”

Since the lab was free that day, the tests would only take about two hours. The officers planned to bring Lu Qin in—if there were issues, they’d handle it immediately. If not, he’d be free to go after signing off.

“Can I ride my food cart there?” Lu Qin asked.

“That’s fine.”

The elderly man also climbed into the police car, ready to give his statement and, with his strong sense of justice, see the investigation through to the end.

Lu Qin followed the police car on his three-wheeled food cart. But as the police car pulled away, they noticed a group of people trailing behind them—customers, parents, and onlookers.

The police car stopped, and one of the officers got out to confront the group.

“What are you all doing following us?”

“We trust Boss Lu! I’ve been eating his food for days—there’s no way there’s a problem!”

“Exactly! This has to be a mistake!”

The officer tried to explain, “Whether there’s an issue or not will depend on the test results.”

“We’ll wait and see the results!”

“We’re Boss Lu’s loyal supporters!”

“Officers, you better not wrong an honest man!”

The officer sighed, realizing this was going to be a long day.

“We believe in Boss Lu!”

Customers Stand Guard Outside the Police Station

“I trust Boss Lu 100%! I haven’t even had his fried noodles today. Once he’s cleared, I’m buying more!” One customer declared outside the police car.

The officers frowned—this was turning into an unusual case. The senior officer gestured for the other to drive on.

“Let them follow if they want. They can’t get inside the station anyway.”

The police station was less than a kilometer away, and after passing a few traffic lights, they arrived within five or six minutes.

Behind them, the group of loyal customers—some on foot, some riding electric scooters, and others on shared bikes—followed in a grand procession all the way to the station entrance.

The station’s barrier lifted, and one officer stepped out of the car to wave Lu Qin’s food cart through.

“If the food tests reveal any problems or illegal substances, everything on this cart will have to be confiscated,” the officer explained.

As Lu Qin drove his cart inside, the crowd outside called out.

“Boss Lu, we’ll be right here! Just give us a shout if anything happens!”

“Officers, please investigate thoroughly! Boss Lu is innocent!”

“Don’t wrong a good person!”

The officer raised an eyebrow, thinking to himself, What is this, a riot? If he’s guilty, are they planning to storm the station?

More than a dozen people stood vigil outside the station gates. Their presence attracted the attention of other officers.

“What’s going on here? Why are there so many people?”

“They’re all customers of that food stall owner inside. They’re here to make sure we don’t wrong him.”

“Wow, this guy has quite the fanbase!”

The officers glanced at Lu Qin, now seated inside the station.

“Boss Lu,” one officer asked, “should we ask them to leave?”

“No need,” another officer interjected. “The tests will only take about an hour. If the results show no issues, having them here could even help him get back to business faster.”

One officer took the samples to the testing lab, while Lu Qin sat on a bench in the lobby. Opposite him sat the elderly man who had filed the report.

The old man broke the silence.

“Young man, don’t take it personally. I’ve lived a long life and traveled far and wide, but your fried noodles are the only thing I’ve ever found addictive. It’s unusual—too unusual. For the sake of public safety, I had to report it.”

Lu Qin gave him a calm, measured look and said, “You had the right to do so.”

Though he didn’t lash out, Lu Qin wasn’t entirely at peace with the situation. It felt absurd and a waste of time. A female officer brought Lu Qin a glass of water before calling the old man in for questioning.

______

Outside the station, the crowd of supporters remained steadfast. They weren’t just there because they liked Boss Lu—they genuinely adored his food. Many of them had grown so accustomed to his cooking that the thought of losing it was unbearable.

Meanwhile, news of Lu Qin’s situation spread quickly in the group chat for his customers.

The chat buzzed with activity as customers expressed their disbelief and support for Boss Lu.

Inside the station, Lu Qin sat quietly, waiting for the results. His calm demeanor masked the surreal nature of the day—a day that started with cooking fried noodles and ended with an investigation into his recipes.

chapters.title: Chapter 65
chapters.content:
Chapter 65 City Bureau Leader Calls! Test Results Are Clear!

Lu Qin’s investigation by the police had caused an uproar in the Penguin group chat. Within minutes, hundreds of messages flooded in. Shen Muyan, having just finished inspecting the dining department, received a call from Fang Ya.

“My dear goddess, didn’t you see my WeChat?” Fang Ya asked anxiously.

“I was busy. What’s going on?”

“Lu Qin got taken away by the police! Haven’t you checked the Penguin group?”

Shen Muyan’s heart skipped a beat. She immediately ended the call and opened the group chat. Fang Ya wasn’t one to joke about such things.

Sure enough, the group chat was filled with news about Lu Qin being taken to the police station. After quickly piecing together the situation, Shen Muyan found it both absurd and worrying.

An elderly man had reported Lu Qin simply because his food was too delicious, suspecting him of adding something addictive. While Shen Muyan believed Lu Qin was innocent and would likely be released soon, she felt she had to do something.

She scrolled through her contacts and dialed a number.

“Hello, Uncle Qin? I need your help with something…”

_______

Elsewhere, Chen Dahai also noticed the group chat chatter. He immediately picked up his phone and called one of his many connections in the Jiangcheng police force.

“Hello, Director Zhang? This is Chen Dahai…”

Even Maidejun and Mr. Rossi, after seeing the messages, started reaching out to their own networks to see what they could do for Lu Qin.

______

Chengnan Police Station was relatively quiet—it hadn’t seen a case in weeks. The station chief, enjoying the lull, was washing the police car. After finishing, he strolled into the lobby with a cup of tea, where he spotted Lu Qin sitting calmly, drinking water.

Curious, the chief approached him.

“Young man, what brings you here?”

Lu Qin glanced up at the middle-aged man in uniform, noting the two bars and two stars on his shoulder. He recognized him as the station chief.

“I’ve been reported. Someone suspects I’ve added illegal substances to the food I sell, so I’m here cooperating with the investigation,” Lu Qin explained calmly.

The chief was taken aback.

Adding illegal substances to food?

His mind immediately jumped to poppy powder—a substance that had caused several high-profile cases years ago. Unscrupulous vendors had added it to hotpots and barbecues, leaving customers hooked and craving more.

Excessive use of poppy powder could harm the body as severely as drug abuse. If that were the case here, this wasn’t just a food safety issue—it was a criminal offense.

The chief’s expression grew serious. This case needed thorough investigation. However, as he observed Lu Qin, the young man appeared entirely composed.

At that moment, a young officer approached.

“Hey, Xiao Zhang, tell me more about this case,” the chief instructed.

“Sure, Chief,” Xiao Zhang replied earnestly.

“This morning, an elderly man came to the station and reported that this Mr. Lu’s food was suspiciously delicious. He suspected something addictive might have been added to it…”

Before Xiao Zhang could finish, the chief’s phone rang. The caller ID made his expression immediately shift to one of seriousness.

“Hello, Director Zhang!”

The voice on the other end got straight to the point.

“Did your station detain a young man named Lu Qin today? Do you have solid evidence? Make sure you handle this properly—don’t make a mistake.”

The chief’s heart skipped a beat. It was clear from Director Zhang’s tone that he was on Lu Qin’s side. Any mishandling of the case would likely result in blame being directed at the station.

Glancing at Lu Qin, the chief felt a jolt of unease.

This young man hasn’t been here long, and he’s already managed to get the attention of Director Zhang? Who is he?

The speed was so fast!

Could there be some terrifying backing behind it?

Could he possibly be the son of some high-ranking officer?

Then again, the station chief quickly dismissed the thought.

How could the son of a high-ranking officer be running a street food stall?

Unable to figure it out, the station chief stopped overthinking and respectfully said into the phone, “Director Zhang, we didn’t arrest him, just invited him over for an investigation.”

“He hasn’t even entered the interrogation room, he’s sitting in the lobby, drinking tea.”

After saying this, the station chief hurriedly gestured to the young officer beside him, signaling him to prepare a good cup of tea for Lu Qin.

“Hurry up with your process. If there’s no issue, let him go immediately,” came the response.

The station chief promptly agreed, and the call ended. The young officer brought over a cup of tea, clearly surprised.

Who was this Lu Qin? Even a leader from the city bureau was calling to check on him. Both the station chief and the young officer glanced at Lu Qin again.

Lu Qin set the tea cup aside, equally puzzled. He had no connections in the police force, nor did he know anyone like Director Zhang. Just as the station chief put his phone back in his pocket, it rang again after only two seconds.

The station chief looked at the screen and saw that it was Captain Qin from the downtown precinct. Sweat broke out on the station chief’s forehead.

Could Captain Qin also be…

Feeling uneasy, the station chief answered the call.

“Old Zhou, did your station bring in a young man named Lu Qin today? What did he do?”

The station chief’s eyelid twitched. It was as he feared!

“Captain Qin, we didn’t arrest him, just invited him. We’re just conducting a simple investigation with Mr. Lu,” the station chief explained, wiping the sweat off his forehead.

This matter could be either big or small. He immediately called over the two officers in charge of the case.

“What’s the progress of the investigation?”

Seeing the chief’s serious and anxious expression, the two officers quickly replied, “The samples have already been sent to Xiao Zhang in the lab for testing. It’ll take about another hour.”

“It’s not like you’re testing some complicated chemical composition, like opium or something. Testing should take only a few minutes. Go push them to get the report out within 20 minutes,” the chief demanded impatiently.

After all, there were no other urgent cases at the station, and testing some food shouldn’t take an hour.

The two officers rushed to the lab to find Xiao Zhang. To their surprise, Xiao Zhang and a colleague were happily eating iced cakes and green bean paste.

The two officers froze.

“Why are you eating the samples?”

“Why don’t you try some? These snacks are amazing!” Xiao Zhang said, licking his fingers in delight.

“The chief ordered the report to be ready in 20 minutes! Stop eating!”

The mention of the chief snapped them to attention. Actually, the test results were already done, and the food had no issues. But the snacks looked so tempting that they couldn’t resist trying some. And after one bite, they couldn’t stop.

No wonder people reported it for being too addictive—it was truly delicious.

Since they had initially said the test would take an hour, they hadn’t been in a rush. Within 20 minutes, they quickly finalized the report and handed it to the chief.

Relieved, the chief walked over to Lu Qin, personally shook his hand, and apologized.

“Mr. Lu, we’re really sorry for wasting your time and affecting your business. The test results show no issues.”

With the chief’s apology, Lu Qin waved it off and said, “Can I leave now?”

“As soon as you sign the paperwork, you’re free to go.”

The elderly man who had made the report still couldn’t believe there was nothing in Lu Qin’s food.

“Officer, you must have missed something. How can food be so addictive? You have no idea how good his fried noodles are…”

The chief was speechless.

“If it’s delicious, isn’t that a good thing? Or do you prefer bad-tasting food?”

“This is the test report, still warm. Take a look—it can’t lie.”

The elderly man refused to look because he couldn’t understand it anyway.

“I’ve lived so many years and eaten more salt than you youngsters have eaten rice. I can’t be wrong!”

The chief and the other officers finally realized—this old man was as stubborn as a mule and wouldn’t listen to reason.

chapters.title: Chapter 66
chapters.content:
Chapter 66 Lu Qin Showcases his Real Skills on the Spot! The Crowd of Bystanders Suddenly Fell Silent.

“Officer, listen to me. Search the boss, search his home, and you’re bound to find something,” the persistent old man insisted, refusing to back down, convinced that no stir-fried noodles could taste that good without some sort of trickery.

“Sir, you’ve got to trust our police investigation. The stir-fried noodles you ate this morning were freshly cooked by Mr. Lu, right? Did you see him add anything unusual to the pan?”

“No, I didn’t, but there are techniques in the trade—silent and sneaky. They can add something to the pan without anyone noticing,” the old man retorted, steadfast in his belief.

“You should’ve seen how fast the boss’s hands moved, like a machine! It was incredible,” the man added, full of confidence and unwavering in his stance.

The precinct chief and the officers found it impossible to reason with the old man. Even Lu Qin was at a loss for words.

He didn’t want to waste any more time on the matter, so he suggested, “My food cart is right outside, and I still have rice noodles. I can cook a fresh batch in front of everyone. Sir, if the freshly cooked batch tastes exactly the same as what you had earlier, would that prove I didn’t add anything unusual?”

The precinct chief’s eyes lit up.

“That’s a great idea. Sir, with so many of us watching, it’ll be obvious if Mr. Lu adds anything to the noodles.”

The old man furrowed his brows, thought it over, and finally nodded.

“Alright, let’s do that!”

In the open space in front of the police station, Lu Qin set up his food cart. People near the entrance, seeing a group of officers coming out of the station, craned their necks to see what was happening.

“Have they solved the case?”

“What’s going on? Why is Mr. Lu setting up his food cart at the station?”

“This is nerve-wracking. What’s the situation now?”

“I get it!”

“What do you get?”

“Mr. Lu is about to show off his real skills! This is the most convincing way to prove his innocence!”

“Exactly! Actions speak louder than words. Mr. Lu’s about to give them a demonstration!”

“Quick, record this!”

Click, click, click —everyone took out their phones.

In front of everyone, Lu Qin began cooking. He cleaned the wok, turned on the fire, added oil, and sprinkled in the seasonings—all his actions deliberately slowed down so everyone could see every step. Soon, the aroma filled the air. It was mouthwateringly good.

Several officers couldn’t help but swallow their saliva. Even the precinct chief was tempted to order a plate of noodles for himself. Everyone marveled at Lu Qin’s skills. These moves were better than those of a chef at a five-star hotel.

The noodles were done and the portion was generous. A female officer brought out a large bowl and a bundle of chopsticks. Lu Qin served the noodles into the bowl.

The precinct chief couldn’t resist and took a bite first. A heavy smoker, he found it easier to resist cigarettes than these stir-fried noodles.

It was insanely good! The precinct chief took several more bites, his spirits lifted.

“Sir, try these noodles. Do they taste like what you had this morning?”

The old man took a bite. After swallowing, his expression shifted. It tasted identical—just as delicious and addictive as before.

Seeing the old man’s reaction, the precinct chief laughed.

“Sir, these noodles were freshly cooked by Mr. Lu while we all watched. There’s no way anything suspicious could’ve happened, right?”

The old man let out a long sigh.

“Could I have been mistaken after all?”

“Take the ‘could’ out of it—you were mistaken. It’s alright, it’s just a misunderstanding,” the precinct chief said.

“Mr. Lu, sorry for wasting your time. We really apologize.”

“It’s no problem. It’s good to get to the truth,” Lu Qin replied.

Outside, the customers were still recording and began to cheer.

“Everyone says Mr. Lu is a good person. Now you have to believe it!”

“This is wild—good food gets reported to the police? Poor Mr. Lu!”

“Food so good it triggered a police report. I thought that was just an internet joke, but it actually happened!”

“The precinct chief personally apologized to Mr. Lu. The truth is out, and everyone’s happy now!”

“Mr. Lu, hurry up and come out. I still want to order some noodles!”

The precinct chief was momentarily stunned, watching as the large bowl of stir-fried noodles was quickly devoured by the officers.

“Xiao Zhang, Xiao Chen, that was a huge bowl! You didn’t even leave me a bite? Do you even respect me anymore?” He exclaimed, his eyes wide with disbelief.

Among everyone at the station, Xiao Chen and Xiao Zhang were known for having the biggest appetites. Judging by their oily mouths, it was clear they’d eaten the lion’s share. Though it was a big bowl of noodles, splitting it among so many people left everyone wanting more.

A young officer spoke up, “Mr. Lu, you still have ingredients in your cart, right? Can you make us a few more portions? We’ll pay.”

“Yes, yes! Since you’ve already set up, might as well cook us some more. We’ll have an early lunch!”

“Quick, notify the cafeteria not to make lunch. We’re all having Mr. Lu’s noodles today!”

And so, an unusual scene unfolded at the Chengnan Police Station. A group of officers lined up, jostling to buy stir-fried noodles.

“Everyone, stay in line! No pushing! Maintain the image of the police force. I’m the chief, so I’m first in line!” Declared the precinct chief, firmly asserting his position.

Pulling out his phone, he made a call.

“Hello, honey! Do you want stir-fried noodles for lunch? There’s this amazing stall, so good that someone called the police over it… No, I’m not joking, it really happened! Oh, you want some too? Alright, I’ll bring two portions home!”

Xiao Zhang shouted, “I’ll take three portions!”

Xiao Chen chimed in, “I’ll take five!”

Even Old Guo, the chef from the station cafeteria, came out to join the line. The old man who had reported Lu Qin also sheepishly approached him.

“Uh, Mr. Lu, will you still sell to me?”

“Of course. What would you like?”

“Two portions of noodles and two cups of mung bean soup…”

Outside the station, bystanders were recording the commotion inside while waiting their turn to buy noodles. After finishing all the business inside the police station, Lu Qin counted his earnings—1,546 yuan had been added to his wallet.

The precinct chief asked, “Mr. Lu, where can we find you next time we want your noodles?”

“Just join the QQ group. I post updates there,” Lu Qin replied.

And so, the police station’s QQ group gained a dozen new officer members. The existing members, upon noticing this, immediately fell silent. The chat, which had been buzzing with messages, came to an abrupt halt. Though they were all law-abiding citizens, the sudden presence of actual police officers left them too cautious to continue chatting freely.

Pushing his three-wheeled food cart, Lu Qin left the police station, escorted by the precinct chief himself. Elsewhere, Shen Muyan, Chen Dahai, and Mai Dejun all received updates about the incident. It was all a misunderstanding, and Lu Qin was fine. They finally felt at ease.

In total, Lu Qin had spent less than two hours at the police station. Not only was his time not wasted, but he’d also managed to make some sales while he was there.

Shortly after leaving the station, waves of customers began arriving. These were members of his QQ group who had heard about the incident and came to show their support for Lu Qin. They were all loyal fans, and upon seeing that he was alright, they felt relieved and bought noodles as well.

By 10:30 AM, Lu Qin had completely sold out of everything. Packing up, he headed home.

What he didn’t know was that a video titled ‘A food stall’s cooking is so good that an old man called the police, and the result was…’ had quietly gone viral and was now trending online.

chapters.title: Chapter 67
chapters.content:
Chapter 67 Lu Qin Trends Online: The Internet Is Not Beyond the Law!

When Lu Qin was called to the police station for questioning, his customers were initially outraged.

A hardworking street vendor, a culinary artist devoted to his craft, being reported simply because his food was too delicious? Where was justice? Where was the law?

But once the truth came out, and Lu Qin walked out of the station unharmed, having only spent two hours there, the mood shifted.

The precinct chief personally apologized with a handshake, and the officers eagerly lined up to buy Lu Qin’s stir-fried noodles. The customers, no longer angry, were now laughing. Lu Qin’s cooking skills were unquestionable. Even the station cafeteria’s chef couldn’t resist.

Among Lu Qin’s loyal fanbase, there were a few talented video editors. They quickly clipped together multi-angled footage of the incident and uploaded it to Douyin (the Chinese TikTok).

The catchy title, humorous content, and surprising twist made the video go viral in no time. Soon, it hit the trending list.

Online, netizens eagerly joined the conversation.

Lu Qin’s QQ group number also leaked online. In an instant, countless netizens flooded the group with requests to join. Lu Qin wasn’t checking his phone—his group admin, Chubby Zhou Weiwei, was responsible for adding people.

After a flurry of invites, the group size doubled from 600 to over 1,200 members. However, the quality of the new members varied widely.

Some immediately caused chaos.

The group quickly descended into chaos. Weiwei tried his best to kick out the troublemakers, but it was overwhelming.

Suddenly, a video was shared.

The clip, 45 seconds long, showed the interior of a police office. Six uniformed officers stood with their arms crossed in front of a dark blue wall, prominently displaying a large national emblem and the words ‘Jiangcheng Public Security Bureau, Chengnan Subdivision, Jindu Police Station’.

Every frame screamed authenticity. It was a real police station. The group fell silent.

The spammers panicked, leaving in droves.

And just like that, order was restored.

A wave of unruly internet users left the group as quickly as they had joined, retreating in a panic. The group’s numbers dropped by several hundred, returning to a manageable 700+.

The remaining members cheered—the chaos was finally over.

“The police are awesome!”

Even Zhou Weiwei, the chubby admin, let out a sigh of relief. He muttered to himself, realizing his mistake—he shouldn’t have blindly accepted everyone’s join requests. He had almost ruined the group.

_____

Meanwhile, Lu Qin rode his three-wheeled food cart back to the old neighborhood. Waiting by his door were Grandpa Ge and a few other neighbors.

“Lu, we heard about you being called to the police station. Even though it was just a misunderstanding, it’s still bad luck. Stand right there—we’ll help you chase away the bad energy!”

Grandpa Ge pulled out a bundle of pomelo leaves and started lightly swatting at Lu Qin.

Aunt Wang brought over a fire basin and said, “Step over this to get rid of the bad vibes.”

Lu Qin chuckled awkwardly.

“Aunt Wang, it’s not like I was in jail. This really isn’t necessary.”

Aunt Wang responded earnestly, “The police station is the same! Step over the fire basin—it burns away bad luck and brings good fortune. Hurry up!”

Another neighbor, Aunt Liu, chimed in, “You should also drink some water mixed with coal ash to cleanse yourself!”

Lu Qin quickly waved his hands.

“Aunt Liu, no need for that, really!”

Despite their superstitions, the neighbors were incredibly kind.

Noticing that some of them had brought their grandchildren along, Lu Qin said, “Aunt Wang, wait here. I’ve got some mung bean smoothies in the fridge. Let me grab a few cups for the kids.”

“Oh no, no! Those are for selling—we can’t take them,” Aunt Wang protested.

Lu Qin smiled.

“You’re always giving fruit to the kids. I need to return the favor somehow.”

With that, he headed inside. Aunt Wang and the others made a token protest but ultimately didn’t refuse.

Lu Qin’s mung bean smoothies were simply irresistible. They’d tried making their own at home but could never replicate his silky, smooth, and perfectly refreshing texture.

Making a top-tier mung bean smoothie was deceptively difficult. No matter how much mung beans one soaked and blended, the results never matched Lu Qin’s.

After handing out the smoothies, Lu Qin returned home for a short rest before preparing lunch. He whipped up some stir-fried bean sprouts with beef and spicy tofu, eating two bowls of rice until he was completely satisfied.

In the morning, he had spent 925 yuan at the market, buying 50 pounds of chicken wings. That afternoon, Lu Qin planned to sell Imperial Chicken Wings.

The quality of ingredients was key. Lu Qin was meticulous when selecting chicken wings, choosing only those with a natural color and clear, healthy texture. This ensured the wings came out tender, juicy, and flavorful.

Later, Mr. Zhao, a supplier, was scheduled to deliver a batch of utensils along with some fresh ingredients. Lu Qin’s suppliers were all familiar faces. They knew his strict standards, so they always saved the best products for him.

As Lu Qin was washing mung beans, his phone buzzed with a video call. It was from Lu Xiyao, his little daughter.

Answering the call, Lu Qin was greeted by her worried little face.

“Daddy~! Did the police take you away?” She asked, her big eyes filled with concern.

chapters.title: Chapter 68
chapters.content:
Chapter 68 Chicken Wings So Good They Almost Transport Your Soul! “My Mom’s a Rich Lady!”

At 11:55 AM, in Starlight Kindergarten Cafeteria, the kids were sitting neatly in rows, starting their lunch. As soon as Lu Xiyao opened her lunchbox, it immediately caught everyone’s attention.

One by one, little heads turned, spoons and chopsticks in hand, all staring at the food in her box. Teacher Li walked around, gently turning each curious head back towards their own plates.

“Stop eyeing Xiyao’s lunch. Eat your own food,” she said helplessly.

Curious herself, Teacher Li glanced at Xiyao’s lunchbox.

Her eyes lit up.

“Wow, Xiyao, did your dad make something new?”

“Yup! Daddy said this is called Imperial Chicken Wings. They’re super delicious!”

“Really? They look so glossy and vibrant. Very tempting!”

Imperial Chicken Wings?

The little heads that had been turned away all swivelled back again.

“I love chicken wings!”

“Wow, I really want some!”

But with only three wings in Xiyao’s lunchbox, the kids knew they wouldn’t get any, no matter how much they drooled. All they could do was watch, envious, and swallow hard.

“Teacher Li, do you want to try one?”

“Well… yes, I mean no! No, I shouldn’t!”

Teacher Li nodded and shook her head at the same time. She was the teacher, after all—how could she take food from a child? It would be embarrassing if word got out.

But… she really wanted to try one.

“Here, Teacher Li, have one!”

With some difficulty, little Xiyao picked up a wing with her chopsticks. The wings were large, her chopsticks were long, and her tiny fingers didn’t have much strength, so it was a wobbly effort.

Most of the other kids were either clumsily using spoons or fumbling with their chopsticks, dropping half their food along the way—it looked like their chins had holes in them.

Afraid the wing might fall to the floor, Teacher Li quickly took it with her hand.

“Well… okay then. Thank you, Xiyao!”

Taking a bite, Teacher Li’s eyes widened. She felt like her soul was leaving her body. The wing was so juicy!

Tender, flavorful, and with a faint hint of lychee—absolutely perfect. It was so good that Teacher Li nearly chewed up the bone too. The taste was just too irresistible.

Meanwhile, one of the kids, Zhong Shuyi, scooted closer to Xiyao.

“Xiyao, can I try your tomato and eggs?”

“ Mmph… ”

Xiyao, mid-bite, looked up with rice grains and oil around her mouth.

“You’ve got tomato and eggs in your lunchbox too,” she pointed out, glancing at Shuyi’s box, which had tomato and eggs, braised beef with potatoes, and stir-fried tofu with peppers.

“I want to try the ones your dad made!”

“Alright then, just one bite!”

“Ahh-woo!” Shuyi took a bite.

“Wow! So good!”

“Can I try yours?” Xiyao asked.

“Sure, you can have all of it!”

“No, just one bite!” Xiyao tasted Shuyi’s tomato and eggs and immediately frowned.

“This isn’t good at all!”

Shuyi shook her head.

“It’s because your dad is too good at cooking!”

“Okay, I guess that’s just how it is!”

Xiyao continued eating until her lunchbox was clean. Her belly was so full it was bulging.

“Ahh~ I’m so happy!”

She tilted her little head, her face glowing with satisfaction. The other kids looked on enviously, wishing they had a dad like Xiyao’s.

When Teacher Li went to the restroom, she scrolled Douyin for two minutes and saw a trending video. After watching it, she let out a sigh of relief.

“ Phew , so it was just a false alarm. I really thought Xiyao’s dad got arrested.”

“Daddy got arrested? What’s going on, Teacher Li?”

Right then, Xiyao came out of the restroom, overhearing Teacher Li’s muttering. She asked anxiously.

Teacher Li paused to organize her thoughts and gave Xiyao a brief explanation of the situation.

“Why don’t you call your dad and check where he is?”

“Okay!” Xiyao nodded and pulled out her little smartwatch to call him.

Lu Xiyao hurriedly initiated a video call.

“Daddy~! Did the police really take you away?”

“No, sweetie. They just invited me for tea. Nothing to worry about.”

“My daddy is a good person! The police would never take good people away!”

“That’s right. Daddy is at home now, making mung bean smoothies and getting ready for the afternoon. Did you eat lunch?”

“Yes! I ate it all! My tummy is so full! Daddy, Uncle Chef from Zhong Shuyi’s house made tomato and eggs, but it wasn’t good at all. Yours are way better! Did you eat lunch?”

Lu Qin chuckled.

“I ate two bowls of rice. I’m full.”

“Daddy~! I can’t wait for school to end so I can see you sooner!”

“Play with your friends at kindergarten. Time will pass quickly.”

“But don’t work too hard when you set up the stall! If you’re tired, just stay home and rest, okay?”

“If I sleep all day, where will the money come from to buy food for my little foodie?”

“I have New Year money! Lots of it! And I can ask Mom—she’s a rich lady!”

Lu Qin raised an eyebrow.

“Who taught you the phrase ‘rich lady’?”

“The TV! From Destined to Love You, the one with the domineering CEO!”

“….”

Lu Qin paused for a second before replying, “From now on, only cartoons for you.”

Xiyao pouted.

“It wasn’t me! The cartoon ended, and the TV played it by itself!”

“Alright, let’s talk later. You take your nap, and Daddy needs to get to work.”

Ending the call, Lu Qin set the mung beans to cook and then looked into setting up a child lock on the smart TV. Youth mode seemed like a necessity.

At 2:45 PM, Lu Qin finished his preparations and set out to set up his food stall. Before leaving, he used the sunscreen face wash he had won from the system lottery.

After washing, his face felt noticeably refreshed. When he stepped into the sunlight, it didn’t feel harsh on his skin, and he could keep his eyes open without squinting. The system’s products were never disappointing. This sunscreen face wash was both effective and gentle.

Lu Qin checked his Street Vendor Map app. The best spot for foot traffic was at the entrance of Gulin Scenic Area in Yue District, 6 kilometers away, with a flow of 2,000 people.

But he chose the People’s Square pedestrian street instead, with a smaller crowd of 1,200 people but only 2.7 kilometers away. This way, he wouldn’t be late picking up his little girl from school.

He posted his location in the QQ group, noting that today’s menu included the new dish— Imperial Chicken Wings.

As soon as the notification went out, the group’s foodies erupted in excitement. Drivers on the road made sudden U-turns, heading straight for People’s Square. Cyclists pedaled harder, turning their electric scooters into speed demons, hitting 50 km/h.

At an intersection, a traffic cop raised a hand to stop one rider, only to be high-fived as the scooter zoomed past, leaving the officer bewildered. Someone in a restroom hurriedly cut their business short, pulled up their pants, and bolted out the door.

Dog walkers saw the QQ notification and started sprinting, dragging their huskies along. One poor husky smacked its head on the curb, ending up with a dozen bumps.

By the time Lu Qin arrived at People’s Square, he didn’t even need to find a spot. A line of over 50 people had already formed, stretching down the street like an unstoppable force of nature. The scene was so imposing it could be spotted from 100 meters away.

chapters.title: Chapter 69
chapters.content:
Chapter 69 My Wife’s About to Give Birth—Pack Me Five Orders of Stir-Fried Noodles to Go!”

The bustling pedestrian street of People’s Square was buzzing with curious onlookers.

“What’s going on over there? Why is such a big line forming with nothing at the front?”

“Is this what they call lining up for air?”

“Not even supermarket sales attract this many people!”

“This is insane!”

When Lu Qin’s three-wheeled food cart came into view, the waiting crowd erupted into cheers.

“Lu Boss, you’re finally here! Another minute and I’d have blamed you for injuring my stomach and demanded twenty bowls of twice-cooked pork as compensation!”

“Hurry, Lu Boss! My wife’s about to give birth! Pack me five orders of stir-fried noodles to go!”

“I ate at a restaurant yesterday and got food poisoning. The medicine didn’t work—I need some of your chicken tofu soup to fix me up!”

“I heard you’ve got Imperial Chicken Wings. I want the full experience—how much for the whole set?”

“Watch your mouth—there are kids around!”

Lu Qin was used to the banter. Passing by the man with the risqué joke, Lu Qin chuckled and said, “Careful what you say—there might be police officers here. They said yesterday they’d come to buy stir-fried noodles. They might be in line.”

The joker’s face turned pale as he discreetly scanned the crowd and swallowed nervously. The others stifled their laughter, thoroughly enjoying his discomfort. Lu Qin gave the man a playful look that seemed to say, Good luck, and moved forward.

The first customer in line stood like a gatekeeper, taking up a large spot meant for Lu Qin’s cart. Two other customers immediately took the initiative to grab the handles of the food cart and helped park it neatly in place. They expertly unfolded the setup and began arranging baskets of ingredients.

“Thanks,” Lu Qin said with a nod.

“Don’t mention it, Lu Boss! You must be tired from the ride. Have a seat and drink some water. I’ll massage your shoulders, and we’ll start after that, okay?”

“Lu Boss, here, let me tie your apron for you.”

“Lu Boss, is it hot? I’ve got a portable fan—let me cool you down!”

Their enthusiasm left the lemon tea vendor at the next stall dumbfounded.

What on earth?

Who’s the vendor here, and who’s the customer?

“No need, I can handle it myself.”

Lu Qin tied his apron and glanced at the overzealous customers, shaking his head slightly. He knew they wouldn’t last long if he actually sat down for a break—they were already drooling in anticipation.

Lu Qin hung up his menu board and officially started the afternoon business.

“Wow! I’ve loved chicken wings since I was a kid! Boss, are these made fresh or pre-prepared?”

“They’re pre-prepared,” Lu Qin replied as he unlocked a temperature-controlled freshness box. Inside were neatly arranged servings of chicken tofu soup, Imperial Chicken Wings, and thinly sliced pork for twice-cooked pork stir-fry.

The takeout containers were made of high-temperature-resistant, food-safe polypropylene (PP).

“So, this is the Imperial Chicken Wings?”

The first customer didn’t hesitate and ordered a serving. Opening the lid, a rich aroma wafted out, instantly drawing the attention of everyone around.

The glistening, rosy chicken wings looked irresistible, igniting the appetite of the entire crowd.

“I bet these three wings could go for 288 yuan at a five-star hotel, and they still wouldn’t taste this good!”

“Forget the chicken wings—even that twice-cooked pork is worth more than 30 yuan. I’d pay 120 for it! Lu Boss, have you thought about adding a tip jar?”

The suggestion garnered enthusiastic support.

“Those so-called high-end restaurants charge service fees, but what do you get? Some waiter pushing a cart, tossing a few sad lettuce leaves, mixing them up, and charging you 188 yuan with a 10% service fee!”

“Business should be done with integrity, but it’s true—no conscience makes more money. Lu Boss, your conscience is practically shining red!”

The first customer grabbed a wing without even washing his hands. The people behind him were horrified.

“How could you eat it like that? Your hands are sweaty—it’ll ruin the flavor!”

Some even felt it was disrespectful to Lu Qin’s food. Yet, when their turn came, they were so eager they started eating twice-cooked pork with their bare hands. The first bite of the chicken wing was pure bliss.

The translucent meat and tendons were soaked in flavorful juices. With a gentle pull, the meat slid cleanly off the bone. The skin was crispy, the meat tender, and the collagen-rich texture melted in the mouth.

“Wahhh~!”

The customer broke into tears like a child.

“Dude, are you serious? You’re crying?”

“Crying over delicious food is just an expression—how’d you make it literal?”

“Man, your acting is a bit much!”

Wiping his salty tears, the customer explained, “You don’t understand. I’ve loved chicken wings since I was a kid. These Imperial Chicken Wings are so good they remind me of my grandma’s braised chicken wings!”

Lu Qin smiled warmly.

“Your grandma’s braised wings must have been amazing.”

The man shook his head.

“Grandma had poor eyesight and accidentally used vinegar instead of soy sauce. It was so sour I rolled my eyes!”

Lu Qin was speechless.

The customers behind burst into laughter, some letting out snorts that sounded like pig squeals.

“You joker, hurry up and leave after you’ve bought your food! Don’t hold up my turn—I’ve got a wife about to give birth!”

The long line of loyal customers attracted plenty of curious newcomers. Even under the blazing sun, the queue stretched 70–80 meters.

The pedestrian street’s security team had to step in to maintain order, worried that people might start fighting if anyone cut the line.

“Lu Boss, you should put a limit on the stir-fried noodles too. Some of these greedy guys are buying five portions each. If your wok could cook ten at once, they’d probably buy all ten. You believe me?”

Lu Qin nodded, saying he’d consider it. Sure enough, someone tried to cut the line. It was a young woman wearing a sun hat, spaghetti-strap top, and hot pants, with a full face of makeup that made her look quite attractive.

“Hey, handsome,” she purred, her voice sugary sweet. “Mind if I cut in line? I’m in a rush, but I really want to try Lu Boss’s food!”

Her tone was so coquettish it could make any man’s knees weak. Predictably, the man in front of her stepped aside slightly, letting her in.

The other customers immediately began grumbling, just about to protest when the man spoke up.

“Miss, do you have a boyfriend?”

The woman flashed a dazzling smile.

“Of course not! Are you asking for my WeChat? Sure, I’ll add you!” She replied, pulling out her phone with a smug, confident expression.

Suddenly, the man pressed his hand against the back of her head and pushed her out of the line.

“What the hell? No boyfriend and still dare to cut in line? Get lost!”

“You—!!!”

“ Humph . Typical straight guy!”

The woman was furious but had no choice but to storm off to the back of the line, her cheeks puffed out in frustration. The dramatic scene left everyone else in line stifling their laughter.

Pictures of the Imperial Chicken Wings started circulating in Lu Qin’s QQ group. The first customers who bought them couldn’t resist bragging in an over-the-top tone.

Shen Muyan scrolled through the group messages, her elegant eyes lighting up at the pictures of Imperial Chicken Wings . She wanted to try them so badly. Her driver was rushing to deliver contracts with Song Xuanxuan, and it didn’t feel right to ask anyone else to stand in line for her.

She opened WeChat and clicked on Fang Ya’s profile.

“Hey, free to help me grab some food?” Shen Muyan asked.

chapters.title: Chapter 70
chapters.content:
Chapter 70 Lu Boss, Something’s Off About You!

Shen Muyan, craving Imperial Chicken Wings, had to rely on Fang Ya.

“Hey, you mooched food yesterday, so today it’s your turn to stand in line and get stir-fried noodles. I want two iced mung bean smoothies, and the rest is up to you. Don’t forget the Imperial Chicken Wings. They look amazing.”

“Don’t worry about Song Xuanxuan. Just for the two of us.”

Her tone was commanding—something that would’ve prompted Fang Ya to reply with a snarky, Why don’t you send your mom to do it? had it been anyone else.

But this was Shen Muyan, her best friend. The two often spoke this way, and politeness would’ve made them feel like strangers or raised suspicions of a hacked account.

“Call me Sister Fang Ya, and I’ll go, hehe,” Fang Ya said.

“Are you going or not?” Shen Muyan asked.

“Can’t, Boss Shen! I’m in Chang City filming a food tour video. The account hasn’t been updated in half a month, and I’ve already lost 30,000 followers. What can I do, waahhh!”

Fang Ya had already seen the posts in Lu Qin’s QQ group and had drooled over the Imperial Chicken Wings.

Shen Muyan was speechless.

“You go today, and next time, I’ll stand in line. Oh, by the way, I want my noodles to be mildly spicy.”

“Aren’t you in Chang City?”

“Hehe, I’m on the high-speed train now. I’ll be in Jiang City in 30 minutes. Filmed the video this morning, but getting a return ticket was a pain.”

Shen Muyan put down her phone, pursed her red lips, and stood up. She changed outfits, grabbed her car keys, and headed to the underground garage.

When Shen Muyan did something, she did it with efficiency—even when buying stir-fried noodles. Driving her sleek Mercedes sedan, she arrived at People’s Square in just nine minutes. Fortunately, finding a parking spot during the day was still possible.

After stepping out of the car, she glanced towards the street and quickly spotted Lu Qin’s stall. The long queue was unmistakable, starkly contrasting the relatively quiet neighboring stalls. Even Snowman Icy City, known for its affordable and popular drinks, had lost some customers to Lu Qin.

The allure of iced mung bean smoothies—healthier and tastier than milk tea—was too strong, even if the wait was long. Shen Muyan strode forward, her long legs carrying her swiftly into the queue.

The simple yet elegant outfit—tight light-blue jeans, a white V-neck T-shirt, and beige casual shoes—highlighted her tall and perfect S-curve figure. Her attire was plain, but her physique made her stand out effortlessly.

On the street, men’s first glance at women is often directed at their figures, and Shen Muyan’s was nothing short of stunning. Even amidst other scantily dressed young ladies, she stole the show.

Closer inspection revealed her gray baseball cap partially covering her face, but the glimpse of her exquisite features was enough. Her delicate and clean-cut facial features combined feminine charm with a touch of icy elegance—a beauty surpassing that of most celebrities.

The men nearby were awestruck, rubbing their eyes and stealing glances repeatedly. If not for her aloof demeanor, they would’ve likely pulled out their phones to snap pictures.

“Miss, do you want to cut in line? I don’t mind.”

For someone like Shen Muyan, it was no surprise a man would offer his spot. More surprisingly, the other (male) customers behind didn’t protest.

“No need,” she replied coldly, continuing to queue.

Some onlookers silently congratulated themselves—this trip was worth it. Not only could they enjoy Lu Qin’s delicious food, but they also got to admire an absolute stunner.

Stepping to the side slightly, Shen Muyan glanced forward. From afar, she spotted Lu Qin at the front of the line, skillfully tossing noodles in the wok.

Even with her 20/10 vision, the long distance only allowed her to make out his silhouette. Lu Qin’s movements were seamless and smooth, and his serving speed was impressive.

As she moved closer, now eight or nine meters away, she finally got a clear view of his face. The sunlight outlined Lu Qin’s sharp, handsome profile with a faint golden glow. His expression was calm yet focused—he was fully immersed in his work.

“Lu Boss, three stir-fried noodles, two iced mung bean smoothies, one Imperial Chicken Wings, and two boxes of Ice Crystal Cake. How much?”

“124 yuan. QR code on the right, thank you,” Lu Qin replied with quick mental math, glancing briefly at the customer before returning to his cooking. His efficiency impressed her.

When it was finally Shen Muyan’s turn, she pulled her cap lower, her expression slightly nervous.

“What would you like?” Lu Qin asked, raising his head.

He saw a female customer wearing a cap, her head lowered, showing only silky shoulder-length hair and a smooth, porcelain-like chin. Her figure, however, felt oddly familiar. Lu Qin squinted slightly and looked a second time.

“Three stir-fried noodles, mildly spicy, four iced mung bean smoothies, and all the limited items available.”

Her cool, clear voice instantly revealed her identity. Lu Qin was surprised for a moment but quickly composed himself. He decided to treat her as any other customer.

If Shen Muyan wanted to keep things low-key, he wouldn’t push the matter.

Guess my cooking won her over. Otherwise, why would a high-powered CEO personally stand in line?

While cooking, Lu Qin added extra noodles and ham to the stir-fried noodles, gave two extra mung bean smoothies, and doubled the portions of Ice Crystal Cake and Imperial Chicken Wings.

Noticing this, Shen Muyan paused briefly, then pressed her lips together without saying a word. The customers behind her, however, started to catch on.

“Hey, hey, what’s going on? Why does she get extra portions? Lu Boss, this isn’t fair!”

“Wait a minute, Lu Boss, why does this lady’s stir-fried noodles come in such a packed box? It’s about to burst! And weren’t the Ice Crystal Cake and Imperial Chicken Wings limited items? How come she gets extra?”

“Lu Boss, something’s off about you!”

“I’ve been buying food from you for so many days, thinking you were impartial, fair, and just. But now… boohoo , you’ve broken my heart!”

“There’ve been plenty of pretty women here before, and I’ve never seen you treat them differently. Oh, I get it now—it has to be a supermodel-level beauty. Nice eye, Lu Boss!”

“Turns out Lu Boss is just another guy after all…”

The customers erupted in a chorus of teasing remarks, their laughter filling the air. Shen Muyan’s pale cheeks flushed faintly red as she lowered the brim of her baseball cap even further. If it were strangers teasing her, her icy glare alone would’ve silenced them instantly.

But with Lu Qin standing so close—her ex-husband—it made her feel awkward and uneasy. The situation was peculiar and subtle, especially since she, the ex-wife, had willingly come to buy food from her ex-husband.

Just as Lu Qin opened his mouth to explain, a familiar figure suddenly appeared out of nowhere.

A hand lightly patted Shen Muyan’s shoulder, and a slightly out-of-breath voice exclaimed, “ Whew! I almost had to run alongside a train to get here—it was so close!”

The newcomer grinned at Lu Qin, panting slightly.

“Lu Boss, I nearly lost my life for a taste of your food!”

Lu Qin handed her an iced mung bean smoothie with a smile.

“Thanks for the dedication. I’m honored.”

Fang Ya’s eyes lit up as she quickly grabbed the smoothie and started sipping. She gave Lu Qin a thumbs-up.

“You’re such a good man! Always so thoughtful.”

Their friendly chatter left Shen Muyan feeling even more awkward, standing there like an outsider. Noticing this, Fang Ya deliberately waved her smoothie in front of Shen Muyan, a playful smirk on her face.

“President Shen, don’t tell me you don’t have one of these?” She asked, her tone full of mischief, her eyes sparkling.

chapters.title: Chapter 71
chapters.content:
Chapter 71 Oh My God! Lu Qin Earns 1.2 Million a Month! And I’m Lu Xiyao’s Real Mom!

Lu Qin handed Fang Ya an iced mung bean smoothie to quench her thirst, leaving Shen Muyan empty-handed.

Fang Ya couldn’t hide her smugness, thinking, Well, well, Shen Muyan, looks like it’s your turn to feel left out!

Who divorces a perfect husband like this? What a fool!

Imagine living with someone who can cook like this for a lifetime—how amazing would that be!

She deliberately made loud sipping noises with her straw, glancing at Shen Muyan with a mischievous look. Shen Muyan kept her cool, maintaining her icy demeanor, but Fang Ya could see through her facade. Inside, Shen Muyan was probably frustrated and annoyed, but as the composed CEO she was, she didn’t let her emotions show.

Fang Ya clicked her tongue and shook her head.

“Lu Boss, since there are two of us, could you pack two extra boxes of Ice Crystal Cake and Imperial Chicken Wings for us?”

“Man, I starved filming in Chang City, even at their best-rated restaurants. Nothing comes close to your cooking—not even one-tenth as good!”

Fang Ya’s arrival delayed the line by several minutes, and the increasingly impatient customers behind them started complaining.

“Hey, you two up front, hurry up and finish! Buy your stuff and move on. We’ve been waiting forever!”

“Excuse me, ma’am, cutting in line isn’t fair. You can’t just do that!”

“Another pretty lady shows up, and Lu Boss starts playing favorites again!”

“They’re buying five boxes of Ice Crystal Cake and four boxes of Imperial Chicken Wings! I thought there were limits? If they can, so can I!”

“These two must be Lu Boss’s friends. First time’s forgiven, I guess.”

Lu Qin explained calmly, “Sorry, these two are friends of mine, so I’m giving them special treatment. Hope you understand.”

Hearing this, most of the grumbling subsided.

“Alright, alright, just hurry up and move along. Don’t hold us up—there’s a whole crowd starving back here!”

Lu Qin turned to Shen Muyan and Fang Ya. “We’ll catch up later. If I don’t get back to work, this could turn into a public crisis.”

Shen Muyan took out her phone to scan the QR code.

“How much?”

Lu Qin shook his head.

“No need.”

Fang Ya wasn’t having it.

“Why not? Lu Boss, running a food cart is hard work. We’re not freeloaders—just tell us the total, and don’t give us any discounts.”

She pointed to Shen Muyan with a smirk.

“Let the rich lady pay.”

Shen Muyan shot Fang Ya a quick glance but said nothing.

Lu Qin wasn’t one to argue unnecessarily.

After a quick calculation, he replied, ‘289 yuan’. Shen Muyan paid without hesitation. She noticed that Lu Qin didn’t charge for the extra two mung bean smoothies, one box of Ice Crystal Cake, and one box of Imperial Chicken Wings. The realization made her lips curl slightly, her mood inexplicably lifted.

“We’re leaving now. See you next time,” Fang Ya said cheerfully, taking the bag of food and slinging it over her arm.

She playfully squeezed Shen Muyan’s shoulder as they walked away, only for Shen Muyan to brush her hand off coolly.

“While standing in line, I did some rough calculations,” Shen Muyan began calmly. “The average customer spends over 200 yuan. Lu Qin’s cart still has about half of its supplies left. Based on this, his earnings for the afternoon alone should be at least 15,000 yuan.”

She continued, “Adding his morning and evening stalls, his daily income is likely around 45,000 yuan. After deducting costs, his net profit is approximately 40,000 yuan per day.”

Though her tone was composed, waves of emotion surged within her. Her perception had been completely overturned. She had never imagined that running a food stall could generate so much income. But this was clearly an exception—because there was only one Lu Qin, with his unparalleled cooking skills.

What puzzled her even more was that during their years of marriage, she had never discovered this talent of his.

Had she never truly understood her own husband? Or had he deliberately kept it hidden?

But thinking about it now was pointless—they were already divorced.

It was a haunting realization.

Meanwhile, Fang Ya’s mouth hung open in shock, forming a perfect ‘O’.

“Oh my God! Lu Qin earns 1.2 million a month!”

She had always guessed that Lu Qin’s business was profitable, even telling Shen Muyan once that his income might already surpass hers as a CEO—excluding hotel shares, of course.

But now, the numbers she was hearing suggested that Lu Qin’s monthly earnings were nearly equal to what she and Shen Muyan made combined in a year.

Adding to that, Lu Qin had recently gone viral online, and his popularity was still growing. The number of customers would only continue to increase.

Fang Ya clenched her fists.

“Why didn’t you say so earlier, rich lady? If I’d known, we wouldn’t have paid for our food!”

At this rate, Lu Qin was on the fast track to becoming a millionaire—or even a multi-millionaire.

“From now on, we’ll eat for free every day!” Fang Ya declared.

Shen Muyan replied with a faint smile.

“No problem. You’ve got the thick skin for it. Just remember to bring me an order too…”

“ Ugh !”

Fang Ya groaned dramatically.

As they left the queue, Fang Ya suddenly stopped in her tracks.

“What’s wrong?” Shen Muyan asked.

Fang Ya turned her head, her gaze landing on a woman in the queue. The woman had a baby face, fair skin, and a petite, delicate figure. Her makeup was flawless, making her quite pretty.

Sensing Fang Ya’s stare, the woman raised her eyebrows in confusion.

“Do you need something?”

Fang Ya pulled out her phone, opened Lu Qin’s foodie QQ group, and scrolled through the member list. She stopped at a profile with a real photo as the avatar—it was the woman standing before her.

“You’re ‘Yao Yao’s Stepmom’?” Fang Ya asked sharply.

Hearing this, Shen Muyan glanced at the woman, her elegant brows furrowing slightly. The surrounding customers, overhearing, also looked at the woman.

“Hey, it really is ‘Yao Yao’s Stepmom’!”

“She’s pretty, but she doesn’t match up to Lu Boss.”

“True, Lu Boss’s daughter is way cuter. Being a stepmom isn’t an easy job!”

“I think this beautiful lady here would suit Lu Boss much better.”

Someone pointed towards Shen Muyan, but she didn’t notice. Her attention was fixed on the so-called ‘Yao Yao’s Stepmom.’

“What kind of nickname is that? Are you really Yao Yao’s stepmom? Did you even get a marriage certificate?” Fang Ya scoffed, her tone icy.

The woman, realizing Fang Ya wasn’t friendly, puffed up defensively.

“Not now, but that doesn’t mean it won’t happen in the future. What’s wrong with being a future stepmom?”

Fang Ya snorted.

“The future? You’re not qualified.”

“Who are you to say that? You don’t make the rules!” The woman shot back.

“Who am I? I’m Yao Yao’s godmother,” Fang Ya replied, pulling out her phone. She opened her photo gallery and showed several pictures of herself and Yao Yao looking close and affectionate. She shoved the phone in the woman’s face.

The woman’s expression turned embarrassed and irritated. She might have fantasized about being Yao Yao’s stepmom, but in reality, all she’d done was pat Yao Yao’s cheek once while buying noodles—she was miles away from achieving that dream.

Shen Muyan finally spoke, her voice cold and cutting.

“Change that nickname. Don’t ever use something like that again.”

The woman squinted at Shen Muyan, noticing her stunning, otherworldly beauty beneath the brim of her cap.

Annoyed, she asked, “And who are you to say that?”

Shen Muyan enunciated each word with deliberate clarity.

“I am Yao Yao’s real mother.”

chapters.title: Chapter 72
chapters.content:
Chapter 72 What’s Going On? A Beauty Battle? How Amazing is Lu Qin?

When Shen Muyan uttered the words “I am Lu Xiyao’s biological mother,” paired with her stunningly beautiful and cold face, her aura was commanding, and her intimidation was palpable, leaving that ‘stepmother’ speechless and ashamed.

The customers waiting in line didn’t expect to witness such a spectacle. First, there was a godmother, and now the biological mom? Forming a team!

“ Tsk tsk tsk! Boss Lu is in high demand now! Not only is he handsome, but he also cooks so well! Which woman wouldn’t fall for that?”

“Yesterday, over 20 women asked for his WeChat, and today another 10 or so. Wow, he’s a hot commodity!”

“This stunning beauty really does seem to be Yao Yao’s real mom. They look about 70-80% alike!”

“Boss Lu’s taste is impressive—his wife is so nice! No wonder the other women didn’t stand a chance. If his first wife is this high caliber, it’s understandable!”

“Yao Yao’s mom is truly beautiful, but she’s a bit cold and doesn’t talk much. It would be better if the couple communicated more.”
