“Yao Yao’s mom seems a little fierce, and she’s ice-cold. She looks like a strong woman. Boss Lu is so gentle, he must have suffered in the relationship. It’s probably for the best that they split up—he deserves someone better.”

…

The gossiping customers chattered on, and some of their comments reached Shen Muyan’s ears, making her uncomfortable.

Fierce? Strong? Suffering?

Shen Muyan’s face became serious as she pondered and recalled the past.

Fang Ya, standing beside her, observed her friend’s expression with a hint of schadenfreude. The public’s eyes are sharp, their assessment of Shen was quite objective.

…

Another beautiful woman arrived at the fried noodle stand. She stood tall and graceful, a feast for the eyes. This beauty had neat short hair and wore a colorful shirt with a white tee underneath, paired with black denim shorts, looking both stylish and fresh. Her long, slender, fair legs were exposed to the air, and her figure was excellent.

“ Whoa! Another beauty! What’s going on—beauty battle?”

“No wonder Boss Lu’s charm is irresistible!”

“My eyes are dazzled—anyone got eye drops?”

“Beautiful women and delicious food—I’m coming here every day!”

…

Lu Qin smiled at the beauty and asked, “What would you like to order?”

“I want your WeChat.”

Chen Yue was outgoing and carefree, with plenty of men chasing her, and she had pursued men she liked as well.

Although her father, Chen Dahai, had encouraged her to do this, Chen Yue truly found Lu Qin appealing.

Part of it was the instant attraction—simply put, Lu Qin looked good and fit her aesthetic. The other part was that Lu Qin could cook, and he cooked incredibly well.

Even though this was Chen Yue’s first time meeting Lu Qin, she saw no issue with asking for his WeChat.

“Oh—”

“Wow—”

“Ooh—”

The onlookers let out a series of odd cheers.

“She’s asking for his WeChat straight up! Boss Lu is seriously impressive!”

“Man, some guys get no attention, while others are overwhelmed. If just one of these beauties asked for my WeChat, I’d wake up smiling!”

“Lu Boss has it all—he could easily coast on his looks, but no, he has to be an amazing cook too. Where’s the fairness in that?!”

“Uh…”

…

Lu Qin was slightly taken aback but shook his head.

“Sorry, I don’t have one.”

“Hmm, alright.”

Rejected, Chen Yue remained calm and proceeded to place her order.

“Boss Lu, I’ll take three servings of fried rice noodles, no chili, with extra egg and ham cubes.”

“I’ll also take the twice-cooked pork, chicken and bean curd, and crystal cake. How much is it?”

“197 yuan.”

Lu Qin had already started cooking.

After scanning the QR code, Chen Yue kept her gaze fixed on Lu Qin’s face—it was even more attractive when he was cooking.

Five minutes later, in the Bentley, Chen Dahai couldn’t wait to open the plastic bag and took a bite of the twice-cooked pork.

“Mm, delicious! How does Lu Qin make this twice-cooked pork taste even better than what they serve at the Rong Mansion banquets?!”

Chen Yue took a sip of her mung bean soup and said to Chen Dahai, “Dad, he didn’t give me his WeChat.”

Chen Dahai, chewing on a delicious imperial chicken wing, replied nonchalantly, “I expected that. A son-in-law I approve of won’t be that easy to win over. You’ll have to put in effort and sincerity, understand?”

To be honest, Chen Dahai had anticipated Lu Qin’s initial rejection since Shen Hongda’s daughter was indeed more beautiful than his own. But that didn’t mean there was no chance—persistence would lead to victory.

“Prepare yourself for a long battle.”

Chen Yue widened her eyes.

“Why should I suffer through this rejection?”

“Rejection? Just once? That’s nothing. Let me ask you this, is Lu Qin outstanding or not?”

“He’s definitely pretty impressive,” Chen Yue said and nodded.

Having met many elite young men and wealthy heirs, none of them could compare to Lu Qin. But when they talked about excellence, it wasn’t about how much money a man had or how much he could make. With the Chen family’s wealth in Jiangcheng, money wasn’t really a concern anymore.

What made Lu Qin stand out was a mix of his appearance, personality, character, worldview, and skills—basically, his overall qualities. Without a doubt, Lu Qin was flawless. If there had to be a ‘flaw’,it would be that he was divorced with a child. But even that could be seen as a plus.

Divorced men often understood women better and appreciated them more. And as for the kid, who wouldn’t want to have an adorable child like that? It was an added bonus.

“Do you like him?”

“It’s too early to say ‘like.’ At most, I have a good impression of him.” Chen Yue replied.

“You’re not getting any younger. Finding a good man isn’t easy, let alone one in a million like him. You should seize the opportunity.”

Chen Yue fell silent, deep in thought.

Meanwhile, Chen Dahai was digging into his fried noodles when his phone rang. It was Shen Hongda calling, but he silenced the phone, not bothering to pick up. Eating came first.

The ringtone snapped Chen Yue out of her thoughts. Seeing her father about to take a bite of the third imperial chicken wing, she quickly snatched the box away.

“Dad, I bought all this food, and you’re not even saving me the last wing?”

…

“Boss Lu, let us help you pack up!”

“There’s really no need, I can handle it myself,” Lu Qin politely declined the customers’ offer and started packing up his stall.

“Hmm, this imperial chicken wing… he’s better than me at this,” commented Ma Dejun from a distance after taking a bite of the chicken wing he bought.

Standing beside him were his two protégés. One of them was Xiong Tianmin, the pastry chef at the Ourei Hotel. Back when the hotel was facing frequent customer complaints, Fang Ya introduced him to Shen Muyan, who brought in Xiong Tianmin along with a Michelin-starred pastry chef from abroad. Together, they greatly improved the pastry department.

The other protégé was Sui Xiaofu, an expert in Shandong, Sichuan, and Huaiyang cuisines. He was a naturally gifted chef who had climbed the ranks quickly, going from a novice to head cutter, then to head chef, and finally executive chef in just four years.

Hearing Ma Dejun’s high praise, Sui Xiaofu was understandably shocked.

“Master, how is that possible?”

Ma Dejun was a chef at the national banquet level. No matter how talented this young street vendor was, his skills couldn’t surpass Ma Dejun’s.

Xiong Tianmin, on the other hand, was less surprised. He had already known about Lu Qin and had even called Ma Dejun specifically to ask about his crystal cake. Xiong, a pastry chef for 18 years and a national champion in traditional Chinese pastry, was particularly proud of his pea cakes. Yet, Lu Qin’s crystal cake surpassed his pea cakes by at least three levels. He had to admit that Lu Qin’s skills were freakishly advanced.

chapters.title: Chapter 73
chapters.content:
Chapter 73 The Secrets Behind the Imperial Wings

“Both of you try it and give your thoughts,” Ma Dejun instructed.

Xiong Tianmin and Sui Xiaofu each picked up a piece of the imperial chicken wing.

Xiong inspected it first, then commented, “The color and aroma are certainly enticing, I’d rate it a 9.7, but this middle wing section seems like it wasn’t carefully selected. It would have been better to use fresh, plump hens.”

Sui Xiaofu nodded in agreement.

“For a dish like imperial chicken wings, the quality of the ingredients is critical. Ideally, you’d use a special breed like the three-crowned, five-clawed imperial chicken with black and white feathers. Next best would be a well-fed hen. You should select the most tender middle wing for the best texture and flavor.”

Sui Xiaofu was puzzled.

With the wrong choice of ingredients, how could the young man’s imperial chicken wings surpass his master’s?

Ma Dejun chuckled.

“Go ahead, taste it.”

The two chefs exchanged a glance before each taking a bite. As soon as the flavors hit their palates, Sui Xiaofu’s eyes widened in shock. The chicken wing was unbelievably tender, with a rich, smooth flavor. The meat was soft but not mushy, with a hint of lychee sweetness followed by the subtle aftertaste of wine. Every layer of flavor was perfectly balanced, a true culinary masterpiece.

Sui Xiaofu had never tasted anything like it. It was undeniably better than anything his master had ever made. Xiong Tianmin, though equally astonished, had somewhat expected this after tasting Lu Qin’s food before.

Seeing their reactions, Ma Dejun smiled. “There’s no need for further evaluation. Your expressions say it all.”

“You mentioned that the ingredients should be top-tier, yet he used ordinary ones to create a perfect dish. What does that tell you?”

Sui Xiaofu furrowed his brow.

“It means he has fully regained his mastery in cooking technique!”

But how?

That young man was barely in his twenties and had only been working for a few years. To perfect a dish like this, which combines elements of Sichuan and Shandong cuisines, would typically take over ten years of practice. And yet, this was flawless.

Ma Dejun’s expression turned serious.

“No matter how old you get, there’s always more to learn. That’s why I brought you two here today, to remind you that in cooking, there’s no upper limit. You’ve still got a long way to go.”

Xiong Tianmin and Sui Xiaofu exchanged nervous glances, feeling a sudden sense of unease. Were they really expected to keep learning, even after establishing themselves as reputable chefs? One was a pastry master in a five-star hotel, and the other owned a prestigious restaurant. Surely they didn’t need to keep struggling at this point?

Ma Dejun cut through their thoughts.

“Each of you has an assignment—Tianmin, you’ll make crystal cakes. Xiaofu, you’ll recreate the imperial chicken wings. I’m giving you one month.”

“One month later, I’ll taste your results. If they’re subpar… well, you know what’s coming.”

Both chefs’ faces fell, realizing that their rest time had just evaporated.

…

At Starlight Kindergarten, there were still twenty minutes until school let out, but many parents were already waiting at the gate. On the small rainbow-colored playground, the teacher led the children in a dance to the song ‘Rock, Paper, Scissors’.

The young teacher’s smile was radiant, and her movements were sharp and lively. The children danced happily in sync, but a few fathers were more focused on the young teacher than on their kids. No wonder they were so eager to pick up their children now—there were not only young female teachers but also plenty of young single mothers to catch their eye.

Lu Qin parked his three-wheeler and walked over to the fence to watch the children. His daughter, Lu Xiyao, was always quick to learn and had the sharpest dance moves, so the teacher had placed her in the center position. Even if she wasn’t in the center, she’d still be the most eye-catching one.

It wasn’t just her natural beauty, inherited from both Lu Qin and Shen Muyan, but also her lively movements that made her stand out like a little fairy.

Several young single mothers, noticing Lu Qin, brightened up and walked over.

“Boss Lu, here to pick up your daughter?”

“Yao Yao’s dad, your little girl dances so well, like a little pixie!”

Lu Qin smiled.

“Thank you, your kids are great dancers too.”

One of the mothers chimed in, “Your daughter is adorable. I’m so envious! I always wanted a daughter, but I ended up with a son, and he’s such a handful. If I don’t keep him in check for one day, he’ll tear the house apart.”

“Same here! Sons are exhausting, but daughters like Yao Yao are so well-behaved and sweet. She’s like a little angel, always so considerate. Meanwhile, my little rascal is like a mini-destroyer at home—worse than a husky!”

Another mother laughed.

“At least your sons are lively. Look at my girl, Dodo. Her dancing is so stiff, like a little zombie! Sometimes I feel like sprinkling some sticky rice on her to exorcise her bad moves. And then she insists on practicing again at home—pure torture!”

“Hahaha…”

Everyone burst into laughter.

Lu Qin was surrounded by a group of young moms, and bursts of cheerful laughter could be heard from time to time. This scene made the children’s dads green with envy. The music stopped, and the evening exercise dance was over.

“Yaoyao, you danced really well. How about leading tomorrow’s morning exercise dance?”

“Sure!”

Lu Xiyao nodded. She wasn’t shy about leading the dance. School was over.

Parents gathered at the kindergarten entrance, pulling out their pick-up cards.

Lu Xiyao saw Lu Qin, her eyes lit up, and she happily ran over.

“Daddy!”

Lu Qin picked up the little one and kissed her on the cheek. The little girl pouted and kissed Lu Qin’s cheek in return. The warmth and sweetness between the father and daughter made many parents envious and jealous.

“Your dance was great,” Lu Qin praised.

“Hehe! So, can I have an ice cream?”

“Sure,” Lu Qin, in a good mood, agreed.

“Yay! I want a mango yogurt Cornetto!”

Lu Qin bought an ice cream at the kindergarten gate, unwrapped it, and handed it to the little one.

“The first bite is for Daddy!”

The little girl raised the cone and let Lu Qin take a bite. They got on the small three-wheeled food cart and happily headed home.

…

Li Xue had just finished shooting a magazine cover and was scrolling through videos in the dressing room. She hadn’t watched many before coming across a video of Lu Qin on Douyin.

After Lu Qin went viral, many food bloggers in Jiangcheng started emerging. They didn’t bother filming other local foods but focused solely on Lu Qin’s fried noodle stall.

Some influencers even created entire series, filming each dish, from fried noodles to ice crystal cakes to twice-cooked pork, making a video for each one. These influencers understood how to generate traffic.

The food Lu Qin made always looked fantastic on camera, with a built-in filter effect, and had a five-star craving level. As a result, the buzz around Lu Qin’s fried noodle stall kept growing without fading.

Li Xue watched the video of the imperial chicken wings and swallowed her saliva. Only then did she remember that she still had Lu Qin’s WeChat. She directly called him.

chapters.title: Chapter 74
chapters.content:
Chapter 74 Li Xue Invites Them to a Performance! Lu Qin has Already Become an Internet Celebrity!

At home, Lu Qin was preparing dinner, while his ‘vegetable washer’ helped wash the veggies and then obediently sat on the living room sofa, watching Mickey’s Clubhouse. Lu Qin barely watched TV, it was always the little one watching alone.

The TV had been set to kid mode, so she wouldn’t accidentally come across inappropriate family dramas like Temptation of the Countryside or Wife’s Lies.

At this moment, Lu Qin’s phone rang. The little one crawled over to check, her big, grape-like eyes lighting up in excitement. She jumped off the sofa and ran to the kitchen door.

“Daddy! My sister Li Xue is calling through voice chat. Can I answer it?”

Lu Qin, while making braised eggplant, responded, “Sure, you can answer.”

The little one ran back excitedly and answered the call.

“Hello~! Sister Li Xue, it’s Yaoyao. My dad is busy in the kitchen, so I answered for him. Is that okay?”

Her soft, sweet voice made Li Xue melt with affection. Li Xue almost wanted to kidnap this little cutie and keep her for herself.

“Of course, it’s okay, Yaoyao. If you’re free tonight, how about coming with your dad to the cultural square to watch a performance? Would you like that?”

“Of course, I’d love to go! Sister, hold on for a second. I’ll ask Boss Lu!”

The little one jumped off the sofa and ran to the kitchen again.

“Boss Lu! Sister Li Xue invited us to the cultural square tonight to watch a performance. Can we go?”

Lu Qin, while whisking eggs, looked up and said, “Ask her what time the performance starts.”

“Okay!” The little one ran back.

“Sister Li Xue, Boss Lu is asking what time the performance starts.”

“Eight o’clock,” Li Xue replied.

“Got it!”

The little one ran back again but suddenly realized something was wrong.

“Oh no! Silly me! I can just take the phone with me!”

She slapped her forehead, realizing her excitement had made her forget the obvious.

“Hahaha!”

Li Xue’s laughter echoed from the other side.

Without using the speakerphone, the little one didn’t hear it. She carried the phone to the kitchen door.

“Boss Lu! The performance is at 8 o’clock!”

“That works, no problem.”

The little one repeated his words into the phone: “Sister Li Xue, Boss Lu says it’s no problem!”

“Great! Can Boss Lu bring some food? I’ve been craving it for days.”

The little one shouted back to Lu Qin.

“Boss Lu! Can you bring some food? Sister Li Xue has been craving it!”

Lu Qin responded, “Sure.”

The little one relayed, “Sure, Sister Li Xue.”

Li Xue laughed.

“Alright, see you tonight, Yaoyao!”

“See you tonight, Sister!”

After the call ended, the little one was so happy that her tummy started to rumble with hunger. Lu Qin served dinner—three dishes and a soup—braised eggplant, scrambled eggs with tomatoes, garlic lettuce, and winter melon rib soup. The fragrance made the little one’s mouth water.

“Wash your hands, time to eat.”

“Okay~!”

After dinner, the little one insisted on washing the dishes, and Lu Qin had to agree, mentally preparing to buy three new bowls.

Lu Qin, meanwhile, prepared ingredients for his food stall. He still had two hours to set up before the performance started. He wouldn’t have time to make ice crystal cake or chicken tofu, so tonight’s menu would be fried noodles, mung bean soup, twice-cooked pork, and imperial chicken wings.

The little one’s dishwashing skills had improved—she didn’t break any bowls, though she used too much dish soap, filling the sink with bubbles. The dishes, however, were squeaky clean, with no grease left behind.

“Hmm, nice work, Yaoyao. Thumbs up for you.”

Lu Qin gave her a thumbs-up, pressing it lightly on her forehead.

“Daddy~! I think you should give me a real reward, like an ice cream~!”

Lu Qin chuckled.

“How about a brain flick as a reward? Want one?”

“No, thank you!”

Yaoyao rolled her eyes playfully.

Lu Qin rinsed the dishes, placed them on the rack to dry, and wiped down the stove. Once everything was cleaned up, he hung a cartoon water bottle around the little one’s neck, turned off the lights and TV, and they headed out.

…

The cultural square was brightly lit. Beneath the enormous dome-shaped glass wall, the ‘naked-eye 3D’ screen displayed breathtaking, realistic visuals—a deep-sea shark seemingly coming straight at them.

The little one’s eyes were filled with excitement. Although Lu Qin had brought her here before, the lively atmosphere still felt fresh and exciting. It was clear that she was eager to explore.

Lu Qin made a slight miscalculation—tonight, he probably shouldn’t have set up his food stall. Spending time with the little one seemed more important. However, since he had already notified customers in the group chat, many were already heading his way. It wouldn’t be right to suddenly cancel.

“Daddy! Let’s set up the stall!” The little one said, pulling at Lu Qin’s hand, ready to get to work, just as the giant shark on the 3D screen vanished.

At that moment, Lu Qin’s phone vibrated in his pocket. It was a message from Fang Ya.

“Lu Qin, is there a long line? We haven’t had dinner yet. Can you save two portions for us? We’re heading over now!”

Fang Ya was, of course, referring to her and Shen Muyan. The two had become so spoiled by Lu Qin’s cooking that they had little appetite for anything else.

“Sure, no need to pay. It’s on me. The little one really wants to explore the cultural square, so you guys can take her for a stroll while I stay here,” Lu Qin replied.

“Good! Perfect, we’re on our way!”

Putting his phone back in his pocket, Lu Qin hopped onto his small food cart and rode to a secluded spot to set up his stall. The cultural square didn’t have much space for street vendors, and most preferred not to set up there, since the nearby mall’s basement food court was already packed with hungry customers at this hour.

For the first ten minutes, Lu Qin’s stall didn’t attract a single customer. The crowd was large, but everyone hurried past, clearly headed for the mall. But even the little one wasn’t worried about the lack of customers—every time, without fail, their stall was packed, with food selling out fast. That was just the magic of her dad!

The little one grabbed a small folding stool and sat in front of the stall, holding a cup of iced mung bean soup, slurping happily. She was like a living mascot—irresistibly cute!

Several passersby slowed down, unable to resist glancing at her. This little girl was too adorable! Some might even want to kidnap her on the spot!

As the flies started to buzz around under the trees, Lu Qin pulled out a bottle of insect repellent he had won in a system lottery and placed it on the edge of the food cart. The results were immediate—not a single insect dared come within five meters of the stall.

Before long, passersby started recognizing Lu Qin.

“Hey, are you the famous Boss Lu?”

“Wow, it really is the guy from Douyin!”

“Yes, yes! The one who made those kids cry for food in kindergarten! I saw the video on Douyin!”

“He’s the one from the hot search, right? So good it made the police come!”

…

Soon, Lu Qin’s stall was swarmed with people, although he was still unaware that he had become a full-fledged online celebrity.

Just then, little Yaoyao sprang into action.

“Customers~! Please line up in an orderly fashion! One by one, okay? No pushing~!”

“Don’t forget to scan the QR code to pay!”

“Boss Lu! The customers are here! Fire up the grill!”

The little one jumped into the food cart, standing next to Lu Qin, and together, the father-daughter duo began serving their hungry customers.

chapters.title: Chapter 75
chapters.content:
Chapter 75 Daddy, the Rich Lady is Here!

The little one cheerfully announced, and those heading to the mall’s basement food court suddenly paused. The mouth-watering aroma wafting from Lu Qin’s fried noodle stall drew the crowd like a magnet.

From having no one stop by at first, the line now stretched so long that the customers in the back were practically stepping on the heels of those in front.

“Boss Lu, how come you don’t have the chicken tofu flowers today? That stuff is addicting! I love it!”

“This iced mung bean soup is amazing! Just one sip, and I feel refreshed!”

“Haha, it’s great! Too much bubble tea is bad for you anyway, but this mung bean soup is nutritious, healthy, and not too sweet—perfect!”

“Boss Lu, how much for your little cutie here?” One of the customers joked, pointing at the little one.

The little girl looked up, wrinkling her cute little nose, and responded in her soft, sweet voice.

“Uncle, the little cutie’s not for sale! Selling little cuties is against the law! The police will come and arrest you~!”

The crowd burst into laughter. This little girl was just too witty, quick on her feet, and undeniably clever.

“Uncle, your fried noodles and mung bean soup are ready! Are you sure you don’t want to try the Imperial chicken wings? They’re super delicious and a best-seller!”

The customer hesitated for a moment. This little one was quite something—not only adorable but also a natural at upselling. He couldn’t resist her charms.

“Alright then, give me an order of the chicken wings too.”

“Uncle, the twice-cooked pork is also really tasty! Want to try that too? You’ll regret it if you don’t~!”

“Uncle already ate dinner, I can’t eat that much.”

“Okay, then! Uncle, please scan the QR code to pay on your right!”

“How much is it?”

The little one lowered her head and tried to count on her fingers.

“Five plus five is ten, three-eight is Women’s Day, May 1st is Labor Day…”

The little cutie was struggling with the math!

Lu Qin quickly stepped in and announced the price.

“61 yuan.”

The customers couldn’t help but shower her with compliments.

“Boss Lu, your little one is so smart! She’s already got the sales talk down pat!”

“That sweet little voice! If she asked me to buy anything, I would!”

“After seeing this little cutie, I feel like going home and scolding my own kids—the difference is just too big!”

A few customers, after visiting the supermarket, couldn’t resist offering the little one some snacks. Soon enough, she had a banana, an apple, a cheese stick, and a small pack of beef jerky in her hands.

Five minutes later, Shen Muyan and Fang Ya arrived just in time to see the little one expertly helping with the business. Both were momentarily stunned.

Fang Ya nudged Shen Muyan with her elbow, excitement written all over her face.

“Shen, the little one is better at doing business than you!”

“Probably something she learned from her dad,” Shen Muyan responded, glancing up to see Lu Qin completely focused on cooking another order of twice-cooked pork. His concentration was intense, and he didn’t stop for a second.

With so many customers, he hadn’t had a moment’s rest. The stall was squeezed into a corner, and there wasn’t much of a breeze. Sweat was starting to bead on Lu Qin’s forehead.

Shen Muyan reached into her Chanel purse, fingers brushing against a packet of tissues, but then paused. She remembered that they were divorced now, and handing him a tissue would be inappropriate.

Just then, the little one pulled out a tissue herself, her tiny arms raised high.

“Daddy! Let me wipe your sweat!”

Lu Qin bent down, allowing the soft little hand to gently dab his forehead. Afterward, the little one held up her half-finished mung bean soup.

“Daddy! Have a sip to cool down!”

Lu Qin took a sip. It was sweet—sweeter because it was fed to him by his own precious daughter.

Shen Muyan stood there, watching the scene, her heart filled with an inexplicable mix of emotions.

“ Tsk tsk tsk ,” Fang Ya said enviously, “the bond between this father and daughter is so heartwarming.”

Click, click, click.

The tender moment was too beautiful to ignore, and someone in the crowd couldn’t resist snapping a few photos of the sweet scene. With both of them being so photogenic, these pictures could easily be turned into wallpapers after a little editing.

Suddenly, Shen Muyan stepped forward, her cool voice breaking through the crowd.

“Excuse me, could you send me those pictures you just took?”

“Huh? Oh, sure! Of course!”

The young man turned around and was stunned for a second when he saw an incredibly beautiful woman with model-like features talking to him. His brain momentarily short-circuited, and he could only stammer out a response.

By the time he came back to his senses, he felt a rush of happiness. This chance encounter was too good to be true—it was almost like a dream!

He didn’t dare look directly at Shen Muyan, stealing only quick glances. She was just too stunning, with her flawless figure, perfect proportions, and a cold, elegant demeanor. For someone less confident, meeting her gaze would be impossible.

The young man nervously pulled out his phone, while Shen Muyan already had her QR code scanning interface ready. His heart raced. Could this stunning beauty really be in such a hurry to add him on WeChat? Finally mustering the courage to steal a glance at her elegant, flawless face, he noticed that her eyes remained cold and indifferent. Her expression lacked any warmth.

He thought, Maybe that’s just her personality. Regardless, he figured, as long as I add her on WeChat, we can talk later . He quickly pulled up his QR code and watched her scan it. Moments later, he saw the friend request go through.

“Please send the original photos,” Shen Muyan said.

“Oh, okay!”

The young man sent the images over, only to see her turn away and leave without a second glance. Staring at his phone in disbelief, he looked at her profile picture, still feeling like the whole encounter wasn’t real.

Cradling his phone like a treasure, he thought to himself, I’ll talk to her later.

Just as he was about to strike up a conversation, he noticed a red exclamation mark next to his message.

Confused, he read the message.

The young man stood frozen, eyes wide in shock. I’ve been deleted? It dawned on him. I was just a tool! She only wanted the photos!

Nearby, Fang Ya passed by leisurely, shaking her head, so ruthless!

As they approached Lu Qin’s stall, they saw the little girl happily helping out. She was busily packing an order of fried noodles while sweetly promoting the twice-cooked pork to a customer.

“Auntie, you should try my dad’s twice-cooked pork! It’s fresh and delicious. I can eat two bowls of rice with it!”

Her soft, sweet voice, coupled with her adorable, rosy face and sparkling eyes, made her irresistible. Who could say no to that?

The customer smiled and quickly added the dish to her order, bringing the little girl even more joy. Some regular customers, already familiar with Lu Qin’s tasty food, had come planning to buy a lot. However, they couldn’t resist having the little cutie give them her adorable sales pitch, so they deliberately ordered less at first.

Lu Qin let his daughter take the lead, allowing her to develop her little businesswoman persona. The little one seemed to be having the time of her life, thoroughly enjoying the experience of helping her dad at the stall.

Then, Shen Muyan arrived at the stand, wearing a polka-dot chiffon blouse paired with a sleek black pencil skirt with a side slit, which highlighted her long, slender legs. Her presence immediately drew attention from those nearby. She was stunning—her delicate features, icy elegance, and perfect figure were all impossible to ignore.

She wasn’t even wearing much makeup, just a touch of rose-colored lipstick, but she was still effortlessly captivating. If a perfect score was 100, she’d easily score a solid 98.

Some regulars recognized her.

Wasn’t this Yao Yao’s mom, Lu Qin’s ex-wife?

Shen Muyan walked over with her usual cold demeanor, but as her eyes fell on Lu Qin and their daughter, her gaze softened.

The little one looked up, noticing her mother’s familiar figure, and blurted out without thinking. “Daddy, the rich lady is here!”

chapters.title: Chapter 76
chapters.content:
Chapter 76 Shen Muyan is Here! I’m Not a Three-year-old Kid Anymore—If You Have the Guts, Call Me Four!

As soon as she spoke, Lu Xiyao knew she was in trouble. She swore she had meant to say ‘mom’, but for some reason—maybe she was just too happy and excited—the words ‘the rich lady’ slipped out instead.

Hearing those words, Shen Muyan’s eyebrows shot up, and her strict-mother persona kicked in.

“What did you just call me?”

Fang Ya, after a brief moment of surprise, couldn’t hold back her laughter. She walked over and deliberately repeated, “She called you the rich lady! Didn’t you hear?”

Shen Muyan rolled her eyes at Fang Ya. The little one hadn’t expected Fang Ya to repeat her slip of the tongue, and she panicked even more.

“No, no! I called you mom!”

The little one stubbornly tried to defend herself.

Lu Qin reached out and gave her a gentle flick on the forehead.

“Owwww!”

Lu Xiyao clutched her forehead—it really hurt!

“She learned it from TV. I already set the TV to kid-safe mode, so she won’t see those kinds of shows anymore,” Lu Qin explained to Shen Muyan, worried she might think it was his doing.

Shen Muyan wouldn’t suspect Lu Qin, she knew him better than that. But honestly, kids these days really do pick things up fast—especially the bad stuff.

“Yao Yao, sweetheart, come over and let Auntie Fang Ya give you a kiss!” Fang Ya said, pinching the little one’s cheeks and holding her soft little body with delight.

Shen Muyan also wanted to hug her. She hadn’t seen the little one in ages and missed her terribly. But since she had just played the strict mother, changing her demeanor so quickly would be awkward, so she kept up her composed front.

“Have you had dinner yet?” Lu Qin asked Shen Muyan.

“No,” Shen Muyan replied, glancing at the twice-cooked pork in the pot, feeling even hungrier.

Lu Qin packed up the pork for a customer and handed it over. Then, he bent down and opened a foam box at his feet. Inside were two meals he had specially prepared for Shen Muyan and Fang Ya.

“You two go find a place to eat first, then help me watch the little one for a bit. I’ll finish up here and come find you,” Lu Qin said, handing the food to Shen Muyan.

During the handoff, their fingers accidentally brushed. Lu Qin didn’t think much of it, but Shen Muyan couldn’t help but feel something. However, her expression remained unchanged.

Fang Ya, holding the little one, saw the food and swallowed hard.

“Thanks, handsome!”

Lu Xiyao didn’t want to be separated from Lu Qin, so she said, “I want to stay with Daddy! How about we all set up the stall together? It looks so fun!”

Fang Ya pointed to the bustling cultural square, full of fun rides and many kids playing, and said, “Yao Yao, sweetheart, look over there. Isn’t it way more fun? Don’t you want to go?”

The little one turned her head and saw the lively scene—a mini amusement park with lots of kids and their parents playing. She was tempted but torn. She didn’t want to leave Lu Qin alone to run the stall. She wanted to stay and help him.

“Yao Yao, you go with Mommy and Auntie Fang Ya to play first. Daddy will finish up soon and come find you,” Lu Qin said, patting her little head.

“Alright then!”

The little one’s attention had already shifted to the amusement park.

Shen Muyan took her hand, Fang Ya carried the food, and the three of them sat down at an outdoor table in front of a Starbucks to eat first. As soon as they opened the food containers, the delicious aroma wafted out, catching the attention of nearby coffee drinkers.

“ Ugh , I’m starving. Let’s eat!” Fang Ya said and broke apart her chopsticks and dug into the fried rice noodles.

There were also twice-cooked pork and imperial chicken wings, along with chilled green bean soup to drink. Fang Ya declared that no car in the world could tempt her to trade this meal. Shen Muyan started eating too.

The little one had already eaten dinner earlier and had some green bean soup and fruit afterward. Shen Muyan fed her two bites of the noodles, but she really couldn’t eat any more. Still, the little one couldn’t help but drool, though her tiny stomach couldn’t handle much.

A girl at the next table couldn’t resist anymore. She lost interest in her coffee and red bean oat muffin as she stared at the twice-cooked pork and chicken wings on Shen Muyan’s table, swallowing hard.

“Excuse me, where did you get this food?” She asked.

Fang Ya, chewing on a piece of the tender, flavorful pork, pointed in a direction and said, “Over there. The stall with the long line. Look for the handsome guy running it.”

Then she went back to her food.

The girl at the next table left her coffee and hurried off. Soon, others around them got up and started heading towards Lu Qin’s food stall.

Within seconds, the outdoor seating area was empty, leaving only Shen Muyan’s table. The Starbucks staff inside looked out through the glass, puzzled by the scene.

After finishing the meal, Shen Muyan and Fang Ya both let out unladylike burps. The food was all gone, and the green bean soup was finished.

“ Ugh , it was so good! I always eat too much!” Fang Ya exclaimed.

Fang Ya rubbed her belly, a look of pure satisfaction on her face. Shen Muyan was equally pleased.

“Mommy, I want to play!”

Lu Xiyao pointed to the vibrant array of attractions in the center of the cultural square.

“Alright,” Shen Muyan said, taking the little one’s hand and walking towards the area.

“I’m so stuffed, I just want to sleep,” Fang Ya groaned reluctantly as she got up to follow them.

For once, Shen Muyan decided to join the little one in playing. She pointed at a miniature train weaving through the crowd and asked, “Yao Yao, do you want to ride this train?”

The train cars were filled with parents and kids, and everyone seemed to be having a great time.

Lu Xiyao blinked her big eyes and replied in a tone far too mature for her age, “Mommy, I’m not three anymore. This is way too childish.”

Fang Ya chimed in from behind, “Exactly! Our Yao Yao is no longer a three-year-old. Shen, let’s see if you can make her act like a four-year-old instead.”

Shen Muyan’s lips curved into a slight smile. She pinched the little one’s soft cheek and asked, “What about the bouncy castle? You can swim in the ball pit and go down the slides.”

Lu Xiyao waved a small finger dismissively, looking very much like a grown-up.

“Not that interesting~!”

Fang Ya burst out laughing at the little one’s antics, ruffling her hair.

Shen Muyan handed over the decision to the child and asked, “Then what do you want to do?”

“How about that one?” Lu Xiyao pointed upward, her big eyes sparkling with excitement.

Shen Muyan followed her gaze and frowned slightly.

“You want to ride the pirate ship?”

It was a small dragon-headed pirate ship for kids. The swings weren’t too intense, and a few parents were accompanying their children onboard, with occasional squeals of excitement coming from the ride. The pirate ship required a parent to ride along with the child, and Lu Xiyao looked eager to try it.

“How about the carousel instead? Look how pretty it is,” Shen Muyan suggested.

“I want to ride the pirate ship. It’s been so long since I’ve been on one,” Lu Xiyao insisted.

“If Yao Yao wants to ride the pirate ship, then let’s ride the pirate ship. What’s wrong, Shen Muyan—don’t tell me you’re scared?” Fang Ya said and teased, her eyes glinting mischievously.

“Scared? Ha!” Shen Muyan said and let out a small laugh. “Fine, let’s do it.”

She headed off to buy tickets.

“Just get two tickets. I’ll grab some ice cream and use the restroom. Yao Yao, what flavor do you want?” Fang Ya asked.

“Vanilla!” The little one replied.

“Okay!”

Fang Ya made her escape, using the restroom as an excuse—she had no intention of getting on the ride after stuffing herself.

Shen Muyan watched Fang Ya’s retreating figure, gritting her teeth as she purchased two tickets.

chapters.title: Chapter 77
chapters.content:
Chapter 77 Aren’t You Afraid I’ll Get a Stomachache? No worries, I’ll Buy You Some Laxatives!

Shen Muyan was dragged by Lu Xiyao onto a children’s pirate ship. For the little one, stepping onto the dragon-headed pirate ship marked the beginning of an exhilarating adventure.

As the ship’s body slowly rose, the surrounding world seemed to gradually fade away, leaving only the sound of the wind whistling past and the expanding view of the cultural center park below.

Lu Xiyao shouted excitedly.

“Wow! Wow!”

Meanwhile, Shen Muyan, standing behind her, turned a little pale. She was slightly afraid of heights but didn’t say anything, gritting her teeth and getting on the pirate ship. Looking back, she realized she rarely accompanied her daughter on these kinds of activities.

A child’s childhood needs companionship, once these moments pass, they are gone forever.

As the pirate ship reached its peak, it suddenly plummeted as if swept by a violent storm, bringing an unprecedented sense of weightlessness and thrilling speed.

In that instant, all worries and stress vanished, leaving only pure joy and screams echoing in the air. The little one, along with the other parents and children, was utterly delighted. Only Shen Muyan’s face grew increasingly pale.

When the ride ended, the little one stepped off the pirate ship with steady little strides, while Shen Muyan leaned against the railing.

“Mom, let me help you. Are you okay~?”

The little one blinked her big eyes.

“Mom, are you afraid of heights?”

She couldn’t understand why Shen Muyan looked so unwell. After all, even a little child like her was fine—why would an adult feel unwell?

In the past, when Lu Qin accompanied her on the pirate ship, nothing happened to him. The little one didn’t understand the term ‘fear of heights’.

Shen Muyan clutched her chest, feeling a bit nauseous. She had eaten too much for dinner earlier. However, she had finally played something her little one loved. The little one used Shen Muyan’s phone to buy her a bottle of mineral water.

After resting for three minutes, Shen Muyan felt much better. At this point, Fang Ya came over with three ice creams. The little one clapped her hands excitedly. She had just eaten one yesterday, and now she got to eat another today—what a happy and blissful life for this little cutie!

“Yao Yao, darling, the vanilla one is yours, the avocado one is for President Shen, and the chocolate one is Aunt Fang Ya’s.”

The little one took her ice cream cone with both hands and started licking happily.

“Thank you, Aunt Fang Ya~!”

“I can’t eat it. I’m too full,” Shen Muyan said, waving her hand.

Lu Xiyao’s eyes lit up with joy.

“Then can I have two?”

“No, you can only have one. Eating too much will make your stomach hurt,” Shen Muyan said seriously.

“Then who’s going to eat the other one?” Fang Ya asked, holding two ice creams in her hands.

“You eat it,” Shen Muyan replied.

“Aren’t you afraid I’ll get a stomachache?” Fang Ya asked.

“No worries, I’ll buy you some laxatives.”

Fang Ya’s eyes widened.

“What?”

“Slip of the tongue—I meant I’ll get you some montmorillonite powder,” Shen Muyan said.

“That’s more like it,” Fang Ya nodded.

Suddenly, realizing something was off and about to argue, Shen Muyan quickly pulled Lu Xiyao along and walked away. After finishing her ice cream, the little one wanted to play bumper cars.

Sure!

In a large circular arena, more than 20 bumper cars were packed together. Shen Muyan and the little one shared a car, while Fang Ya drove her own. At first, everything seemed normal, and the bumper cars were quite fun.

After a while, several dads spotted the high-profile mother-and-daughter duo in their car and stared wide-eyed, seizing every opportunity to crash into them.

Originally, bumper cars were meant to bump into each other— you bump me, I bump you.

But when seven or eight bumper cars ganged up on one, it became a bit strange.

Shen Muyan and Lu Xiyao’s car was like a ball being tossed around.

“Oops, sorry!”

“Oh no, my bad!”

“Be careful, miss,”

Some bumper car drivers would say with feigned politeness after bumping into them. Their intentions were obvious—they just wanted to grab Shen Muyan’s attention.

Shen Muyan, who had just recovered from her nausea after the pirate ship ride, felt her stomach flip again.

Fang Ya noticed Shen Muyan being ‘ambushed’ and came over to help, but now there were two ‘balls’ being tossed around instead.

In the end, despite the 30-minute time limit, the three of them only lasted 10 minutes before stepping out.

Lu Xiyao, too, seemed quite disappointed with the experience. She hadn’t bumped into anyone herself but was hit so many times she began questioning life.

“Strange, when I played with Daddy, it was so much fun—bumping into everyone!”

“Why is it that when I play with Mommy, so many people bump into us?”

“Beauty brings trouble,” Fang Ya muttered, rubbing her sore neck from the collisions.

“Huh?”

The little one tilted her head, not understanding the meaning.

Shen Muyan pinched Fang Ya.

“What nonsense are you saying in front of the child?”

Later on, the little one dragged Shen Muyan to try out the Phantom Sky VR park. Shen Muyan couldn’t understand why a little girl would be so fond of thrilling and adventurous games. Fang Ya wasn’t interested in playing and sat on the side scrolling through her phone while waiting.

The mother and daughter entered the VR cockpit, put on their headsets, and experienced ten minutes of Phantom Saucer and fifteen minutes of Dark Chariot. Both were similar to 3D roller coasters, but with added elements, making the experience even more intense.

By the end, the little one was as composed as ever, but Shen Muyan was already on the verge of collapse, feeling as if her nausea had reached her throat but unable to throw up.

At that moment, she felt that giving custody to Lu Qin had been the right choice. She was better suited for making money and supporting the family—childcare wasn’t her forte.

At 7:40 PM, Lu Qin finished his street food stall shift, apologizing to over 20 customers who couldn’t get their fried noodles. The frustration of missing out on food was palpable, as if they’d just started dieting, eaten nothing but a cucumber for dinner, gone jogging, and passed by a barbecue stand without a wallet or phone—utter despair.

One grumpy customer shouted in frustration, mimicking a line from a movie.

“Are you kidding me? If Old Wai next door finds out, he’ll think I can’t afford to eat! How’s this little bit of fried noodles supposed to fill anyone up? Bring me a whole sack, one sack per person!”

The people around looked curiously at him, wondering who he was yelling at.

Lu Qin walked over calmly.

“There’s a customer penguin group. We announce the stall location there every day. Want to join and line up earlier next time?”

“Join! Not joining would be stupid!”

The grumpy man nodded without hesitation. “Boss, will you have twice-cooked pork tomorrow?”

“Yes.”

“Great!”

Lu Qin gave him the group number and told him to apply, then went to find Shen Muyan and the others.

His three-wheel food truck was parked in a roadside spot with the keys removed. Surveillance cameras were everywhere, so theft wasn’t a concern.

On the way, Lu Qin received a message—Li Xue had sent the seat numbers for the performance. Lu Qin asked if he could have two extra seats, and Li Xue said no problem.

When he found Shen Muyan and the others, they were at the mall playing with a claw machine. Shen Muyan didn’t look well, standing behind them holding an Ultraman plush toy.

Fang Ya and Lu Xiyao were at the claw machine, moving the joystick. Seeing the claw machine, Lu Qin remembered that one of the system rewards he’d received was a claw machine skill that boosted his accuracy by 80%.

He had won many rewards back then, and this particular skill had been pushed to the back of his mind.

“You okay?” Lu Qin asked while walking over to Shen Muyan.

chapters.title: Chapter 78
chapters.content:
Chapter 78 Stop Pretending! You Must Be An Insider!

Shen Muyan’s face was still pale as Lu Qin approached to check on her.

Fang Ya looked up and said, “She stuffed herself with your fried noodles, then got dragged by the little one onto the pirate ship, played bumper cars, and tried a 3D roller coaster. It’s no wonder her face looks bad—she’s lucky she didn’t throw up.”

“I’m fine,” Shen Muyan said stubbornly.

Lu Qin frowned slightly and turned to Lu Xiyao. “Yaoyao, Mommy gets carsick and is afraid of heights. Next time, don’t make her go on pirate ships or anything similar, okay?”

Shen Muyan shot a glance at Lu Qin but said nothing.

The little one makes me play? Isn’t that reversed?

“Got it, Daddy~!” The little one replied innocently. “But if we don’t do pirate ships, swinging pendulums, or aerial rides, then what do we play?”

“Ride rocking cars, the carousel, or go fishing—something more relaxing, not thrilling,” Lu Qin suggested.

The little one sighed helplessly.

“Fine, I guess. I got tired of those when I was three, but since Daddy says so, I’ll treat it like revisiting my childhood.”

Shen Muyan’s elegant face twitched. She felt subtly disdained but had no evidence. Fang Ya was suppressing laughter with great effort.

“Are you guys done playing? At 8 o’clock, we’re seeing Yaoyao’s idol, Li Xue, perform. I got you seats as well. It’s almost time.”

“Li Xue? You mean the famous young singer, Li Xue?” Fang Ya asked.

“Yes.”

“You got seats? How do you even know her?” Shen Muyan asked.

The little one chimed in, “Sister Li Xue loves Daddy’s fried noodles~!”

Shen Muyan and Fang Ya both nodded in understanding.

Fang Ya then pointed to the claw machine.

“This little one likes that Pikachu. I spent 100 coins and still didn’t get it. Help me find a brick, and I’ll just smash it open!”

Shen Muyan bought 100 coins.

“Let me try.”

Before she could start, Lu Qin took the coins from her, saying nonchalantly, “You won’t make it. Let me do it.”

Shen Muyan stood to the side, speechless.

Why does it feel like I’m being disdained again?

The little one was full of anticipation, leaning on the claw machine, watching intently.

In her eyes, Daddy was always the best.

“Go, Daddy~!”

Fang Ya wasn’t optimistic. While Lu Qin might be better than Shen Muyan, he couldn’t be better than her.

Fang Ya considered herself a decent claw machine player. Quick 360-degree joystick spins, precise timing, and claw tricks—what would Lu Qin know about these?

As she saw it, skill came from experience, and experience came from burning through coins. Fang Ya herself had learned through trial, error, and lots of money.

“Need me to teach you some techniques?” Fang Ya teased, crossing her arms with a smirk.

Lu Qin didn’t reply. He inserted a coin, adjusted the joystick, pretended to aim and wait for the right moment, then hit the grab button.

In reality, the system’s claw machine skill didn’t require any tricks. It simply boosted the success rate to 80%. He only needed to act like he was trying.

Fang Ya watched his clumsy movements and burst into laughter.

“Boss Lu, your technique screams newbie. You’re just handing money to the machine—you won’t catch anything even with $200.”

Before her words settled, reality slapped her in the face.

The claw descended shakily, seemingly unstable, yet it grabbed Pikachu by the leg. It wobbled back up with the toy firmly clamped. Those familiar with claw machines knew that claws were usually loose, either failing to grab or dropping the prize midway.

Fang Ya, with her years of claw machine experience, was 90% certain it would fall.

“It’s definitely going to drop,” she declared confidently.

But —

Clack!

The Pikachu was delivered to the prize slot. The little one’s eyes lit up, and she cheered excitedly.

“Wow~! Daddy, you’re amazing! You caught it~!”

She quickly squatted down and picked up the Pikachu.

“Daddy~! You’re the best~!”

Fang Ya was left dumbfounded.

Shen Muyan’s lips curled into a faint smile.

“No, no, that doesn’t make sense. It must’ve been because I spent so many coins earlier, and you just happened to hit the threshold to win,” Fang Ya insisted.

Shen Muyan gave her a sideways glance.

“Just say you caught it yourself.”

Fang Ya was exasperated.

“You don’t understand. That’s exactly how claw machines work!”

Lu Qin didn’t bother arguing with Fang Ya. “There are still so many coins left. Can we get a refund?”

“Papa~! Just keep going! I want Squirtle~!”

“Squirtle? Sure.”

Lu Qin moved to the next machine, which had Squirtle and Charmander inside. He inserted a coin. He followed the same steps—adjust the joystick, aim, grab. The first attempt failed—just his bad luck hitting that 20% miss rate.

“See? See? It was pure dumb luck before. Grabbing like that isn’t as easy as it looks,” Fang Ya sneered.

He inserted another coin, followed the same process and got success. Squirtle was caught.

“Wow~! Papa~! You’re amazing~! You got it again~!”

The little one now held Squirtle in one hand and Pikachu in the other, showing them off right in Fang Ya’s face.

Fang Ya was dumbfounded.

This… this… Such luck? Seriously?

“If you’re so good, catch another one. I don’t believe it!” Fang Ya challenged, unwilling to admit defeat.

Lu Qin turned to the little one.

“Yaoyao, what else do you want?”

“That one! CJ7 from CJ7!”

“Got it.”

One coin later, CJ7 was in hand.

Fang Ya was utterly flabbergasted. Shen Muyan also found it hard to believe.

Was this sheer luck? Skill? Or a combination of both?

Was he really this good at claw machines? She’d never known.

Lu Xiyao, now holding three plush toys, hopped around gleefully. Other parents and kids noticed the commotion and came over out of curiosity.

Among them were young couples and middle school students, all gravitating towards Lu Qin.

“Papa~! I want the carrot plushie!”

Two coins, one carrot plushie caught.

“I also want the teddy bear!”

Three coins, one teddy bear caught.

Fang Ya was completely numb by this point.

Everyone else was stunned.

“Wow! Mom, look at that uncle—he’s amazing! He caught so many toys!”

“He’s incredible! Dad, can you do that for me too?”

“This guy is insane! He catches whatever he wants!”

“No way, is this guy hacking?”

“He must be an insider! He knows the machine’s secrets!”

“Sir, could you share your technique? We want to catch some too!”

“Yeah, Uncle, you must be an insider! What’s the secret? Is it some joystick combination like left, right, left, right, up, down, up, down? I saw that on TikTok!”

Lu Qin was speechless.

“No tricks, really. I’m not an insider either.”

“Stop pretending! Nobody’s this good—you must be cheating!”

chapters.title: Chapter 79
chapters.content:
Chapter 79 My Dad Runs a Food Stall, He’s Not Some Insider!

Seeing people misunderstanding Lu Qin, Lu Xiyao bravely stood up to explain.

Kids are honest, so her words made the crowd reconsider. Could it be that this guy was just really lucky?

Lu Qin rubbed his nose, realizing that the 80% success rate was indeed a bit overpowered. It could bankrupt these claw machine operators. In the future, if he brought his little one to play again, he decided they’d limit themselves to three toys—no more.

“Alright, Yaoyao, the show’s about to start. Let’s go watch the performance.”

“Okay~!”

The little one handed the carrot and teddy bear plushies to Shen Muyan, gave Squirtle and CJ7 to Lu Qin, and held Pikachu herself before grabbing Lu Qin’s hand.

Lu Qin returned the leftover coins, having only used 8 to win 5 plushies.

He handed 92 yuan back to Shen Muyan—money between them had to be clear. They were divorced, and her money couldn’t stay in his pocket.

Nowadays, most transactions were digital, and Shen Muyan hadn’t used cash in a long time. She glanced at Lu Qin before putting the money in her bag.

Fang Ya followed behind, lost in thought and unable to make sense of what she’d just witnessed.

Was it pure luck? Or did Lu Qin actually have a secret technique?

______

At the cultural square, there was a glass-dome theater called Time Theater, the final stop for Li Xue’s tour in Jiangcheng. Outside, the audience was lined up to have their tickets checked.

Lu Qin didn’t have tickets, Li Xue had already sent him the seat number via WeChat.

He dialed a number.

“Hello, is this Manager Wang?”

“Yes, Mr. Lu. I’m here.”

Hearing the echo in the call, Lu Qin looked up to see a man in a suit with a work badge standing by the passage, waving at him. Manager Wang led Lu Qin and the group through a side entrance for staff, taking them directly to the VIP seats.

The four of them attracted attention with their high looks, especially Shen Muyan, whose goddess-like appearance drew many eyes. Once seated, they found themselves next to Li Xue’s manager, Amy.

“Amy, good to see you again.”

“Hi, Amy~!”

The little one’s sweet greeting melted Amy’s heart. Despite being close in age to Shen Muyan, Amy enjoyed being called ‘sister’, which made her feel young.

Amy smiled broadly and patted the little one’s head.

“Wow, so many plushies?”

“Papa caught them! Isn’t he amazing~?”

Amy nodded.

“Lu Qin, it’s not enough that you’re great at cooking—you’re also a claw machine expert?”

“Just luck,” Lu Qin replied modestly. “This is Yaoyao’s mom, Shen Muyan, and this is Fang Ya, a friend.”

“Hello, I’m Amy, Li Xue’s manager.”

Amy wasn’t particularly nosy about other people’s affairs. She wasn’t the gossipy type but couldn’t help admiring Shen Muyan’s beauty. Lu Qin’s taste was evidently impeccable—his ex-wife was stunning and could easily be a leading lady in the entertainment industry.

No wonder their child, Yaoyao, is so adorable. Strong genes indeed.

“Hello.”

“Hi.”

After exchanging brief introductions, they waited for the show to begin. The little one was the most excited—it was her first time seeing her idol perform.

“Papa~! Can you take lots of pictures later?”

“Of course.”

Amy handed out some glow sticks.

“When the atmosphere picks up, wave or tap them along with the music.”

The prelude started, and a group of energetic backup dancers burst onto the stage, followed by Li Xue, wearing a sparkly blue short-sleeved outfit that radiated youthful charm. The audience erupted into prolonged applause and cheers.

The little one, thrilled, joined in, shouting, “Li Xue! Li Xue!”

Li Xue spotted Lu Qin and Lu Xiyao in the VIP section right away. She made her way to the edge of the stage and waved in their direction.

The little one enthusiastically waved back, holding her glow stick high.

“Good evening, friends from Jiangcheng!” Li Xue greeted warmly.

Li Xue performed eight songs on stage, each of them captivating and worthy of being put on repeat. Combined with her beautiful voice, it was a flawless performance. The ticket price was absolutely worth it.

After Li Xue finished her eight songs, everyone thought the concert was over. To their surprise, Li Xue announced an impromptu interactive segment, inviting audience members to come on stage for a duet.

The crowd cheered enthusiastically. However, instead of selecting a random lucky audience member, Li Xue directly called someone out.

She held the microphone and turned to the VIP section, asking,

“Mr. Lu, would you like to come on stage and sing another children’s song?”

The spotlight landed on Lu Qin, and someone quickly handed him a microphone. All eyes in the audience turned to him. Recognizing his face, the crowd murmured in excitement.

Wasn’t this the guy from the Tianhong Plaza event a few days ago? The one who got invited by Li Xue to sing a children’s song?

At first, everyone thought it was hilarious to see a grown man singing a kids’ song. But to their surprise, it turned out to be quite enjoyable. Still, once was enough. The novelty of a man singing children’s songs didn’t carry the same appeal a second time.

Lu Qin waved his hand to decline the invitation. He wasn’t interested in repeating that peculiar moment, especially since it was clear Li Xue was teasing him.

Unexpectedly, little Lu Xiyao, bold as ever, took the microphone herself and spoke in her soft, adorable voice.

“My dad’s children’s songs aren’t as good as mine! Sister, can I come up and sing with you instead?”

The audience gasped in admiration at the little girl’s confidence. Even Li Xue was caught off guard but quickly descended from the stage to escort the little one up.

Lu Qin didn’t stop her. After all, it was true—his daughter sang children’s songs better than he did.

In her kindergarten, she was the best singer and dancer among her peers. She was talented in many ways—a quintessential ‘other people’s child’.

The song was still Little Jumping Frog. The last time Lu Qin sang it, the humor came from the novelty of a grown man performing a kids’ song. His actual singing ability was average at best.

However, his daughter often sang in kindergarten. Though not professionally trained, she sang with the soft, sweet voice of a child, making it a pleasure to listen to.

______

On stage, Li Xue’s heavenly vocals paired beautifully with the little one’s angelic voice. Their duet resonated throughout the grand theater.

Shen Muyan, holding her phone, switched to 4K video recording to capture every second of her daughter’s shining moment. This was undoubtedly a highlight of her child’s young life.

Fang Ya, watching from the audience, sighed enviously.

“Shen Muyan, your daughter is absolutely amazing!”

“You should consider nurturing her into a singer someday—I think she could make it!”

Fang Ya even joked she’d never dare to have kids, fearing the inevitable comparison. While Shen Muyan focused on recording video, Lu Qin took photos.

Afterward, they exchanged footage, both feeling proud. Their daughter had made them shine in the spotlight. Even Fang Ya, as the little one’s godmother, basked in the admiration of the surrounding audience.

She almost wanted to stand up and announce, I’m Lu Xiyao’s godmother!

Meanwhile, Li Xue’s performance was being live-streamed on Douyin (TikTok).

When Lu Qin and Lu Xiyao appeared on camera, many netizens instantly recognized them.

“Isn’t this the legendary fried noodle stall owner? How’d he end up on TV?”

“His daughter is so adorable and confident—what a talented kid!”

“LOL, Lu Boss’s children’s songs really don’t compare to his daughter’s!”

“OMG! More and more people know Lu Boss now. Tomorrow the line at the fried noodle stall will be insane! I bet I won’t get my twice-cooked pork again!”

chapters.title: Chapter 80
chapters.content:
Chapter 80 Loving Whatever You Do

After the concert, in the theater’s lounge, Amy, Li Xue’s manager, thoughtfully observed the little one and said, “Lu Qin, Yaoyao is truly talented. You could nurture her into a singer someday—she might even become a big star like Li Xue!”

Lu Xiyao looked up with her wide eyes, sparkling with curiosity, and asked, “Really~?”

Amy nodded.

“Absolutely. Trust me, I’m a professional!”

This was no empty boast, her ability to discover and develop talent like Li Xue spoke volumes. The little one, visibly intrigued, seemed eager to try. While she wasn’t particularly interested in being a celebrity, she did love the idea of standing on a stage and singing like Li Xue.

Li Xue gently patted her head, equally enthusiastic.

“Yaoyao is a natural. Her appearance speaks for itself—she’s a beauty in the making. Her voice is lovely, and her stage presence is already apparent. Most importantly, she’s not shy at all. She’s perfectly suited for singing and performing,” Li Xue analyzed with excitement.

“Lu Qin, you should seriously consider it. As for resources, you won’t have to worry—we can help,” she added.

Lu Qin, however, shook his head.

“Yaoyao is still young. We’re not thinking about her future yet. I just want her to enjoy a happy childhood.”

Just as Shen Muyan was about to chime in, she stopped, satisfied with Lu Qin’s response. She shared the same sentiment.

Amy pressed on.

“But you should at least ask Yaoyao how she feels. I think she’s interested.”

The little one looked back and forth between Lu Qin and Amy before nodding eagerly.

“Yes, I am interested!”

Lu Qin crouched down to her level and asked, “Yaoyao, how about running the food stall with Daddy and becoming a little boss when you grow up?”

Her eyes lit up.

“A little boss? Can I be the boss?”

“Of course.”

“That’s awesome~! I want to help Daddy run the stall~! I’ll be the little boss~!”

“Then, do you also want to be a claw machine manager?”

“A claw machine manager? What’s that~?”

“It means you get to manage claw machines and play with them every day, catching as many toys as you want.”

“That sounds amazing~! I want to be a claw machine manager~!”

Lu Qin looked up at Li Xue and Amy, smiling. “See? She’s not particularly interested in singing—she’s just curious about everything.”

Li Xue and Amy were speechless.

Fang Ya leaned over to Shen Muyan and whispered, “Your daughter really has the knack for loving every job she tries!”

Shen Muyan shot her a glare.

At Lu Qin’s Rental House, in front of Lu Qin’s modest rental house in the old district, a Mercedes-Benz van and a Porsche Cayenne were parked.

Lu Qin had invited everyone over for a late-night snack. It was Shen Muyan’s first time at his place—a three-bedroom house with a small courtyard.

The decor was old-fashioned, but the place was immaculately clean and tidy. She checked out the little one’s room. It was filled with pink, cartoon-themed decor—star-shaped nightlights, Winnie-the-Pooh bed linens, and crayon drawings taped to the walls.

Though the room was only half the size of her room in the villa, Shen Muyan felt that the little one might sleep more soundly here.

At this moment, she decided not to pursue custody anymore. Lu Qin and the little one were inseparable. If the little one stayed with her, Shen Muyan couldn’t guarantee she’d always be there for her.

In the kitchen, Lu Qin busily prepared food while the little one acted like the hostess—pouring water, offering fruit and yogurt, and turning on the TV for everyone.

“You can only watch cartoons, though. Daddy set it to youth mode—I haven’t even finished watching The Overbearing CEO’s Unwavering Love yet!”

Fang Ya paused, then asked, “Do you know what an overbearing CEO is?”

“Of course! It’s someone like Daddy—handsome and rich~!”

Fang Ya’s expression turned complicated.

Meanwhile, Shen Muyan found it entirely appropriate for Lu Qin to have set the TV to youth mode. She couldn’t imagine what kind of thoughts the little one would develop after watching an entire series about overbearing CEOs.

“Li Xue, are you leaving Jiangcheng tomorrow~?”

“Yes, we’re heading to Hunan Province for the next show, and after that, to Shenzhen.”

While chatting in the living room, an enticing aroma wafted in from the kitchen. Immediately, everyone put down their drinks and fruit, even turning off the TV.

The conversation ceased as their attention shifted entirely to the delicious smell.

“This must be twice-cooked pork! Lu Qin must be making twice-cooked pork, right?”

The rich aroma of soy sauce and spices wafted from the kitchen, making everyone in the living room swallow their saliva.

In Jiangcheng, the typical late-night snacks included barbecue and crayfish, but compared to Lu Qin’s twice-cooked pork, nothing else came close.

Lu Qin cooked quickly, and in just 30 minutes, the dishes were ready—Imperial Chicken Wings, Twice-Cooked Pork, Tomato and Scrambled Eggs, Minced Pork with Eggplant, Shredded Pork in Garlic Sauce

The last two dishes weren’t enhanced by his system-level skills, but they still surpassed those of high-end restaurants. The dishes were served, and Lu Qin ran out to the nearby convenience store to grab beer and soft drinks.

The little one wasn’t allowed soda, so she happily sipped yogurt instead. After raising their glasses in toast, the group started eating.

At Lu Qin’s dinner table, an unspoken rule seemed to exist—no idle chatter during a meal.

The food was so delicious that everyone was too focused on eating to talk. The only sounds in the room were chopsticks clinking against bowls and plates.

Lu Qin’s drinks were mostly left untouched. After the first glass, everyone avoided drinking too much, unwilling to sacrifice space in their stomachs that could be used for the amazing dishes. The meal also served as a farewell dinner for Li Xue.

A self-proclaimed foodie, Li Xue loved exploring local cuisines in every city she visited. But after tasting Lu Qin’s cooking, she found it hard to be interested in anything else.

By the time they finished, the four guests practically had to lean against the walls to leave.

Each person carried two boxes of Ice crystal cakes that Lu Qin had prepared.

“These can be stored for two days without losing their flavor or texture,” Lu Qin explained.

The thoughtfulness of the gesture left Fang Ya, Li Xue, and Amy touched. They couldn’t help but glance at Shen Muyan.

How could she have let such a good man go?

Under their questioning looks, even Shen Muyan started to doubt herself.

Was I blind back then?

She had failed to notice these qualities in Lu Qin. Now, finding a man who was half as good as him might be next to impossible.

The little one had a day packed with joy—Pretending to be a little boss, Riding a pirate ship, Winning plush toys from a claw machine, Watching her idol perform, Hosting her idol and friends for a late-night snack.

Her happiness in one day could rival another child’s happiness over an entire childhood. Exhausted from the excitement, she fell asleep within a minute of lying down, her soft, steady breathing filling the room.

Lu Qin tidied up the dishes, took a shower, and prepared for bed. Before sleeping, he opened his phone and checked his bank balance. The account showed nearly 300,000 yuan.

His food stall business was thriving, and it seemed that buying a car or a house through his efforts wasn’t far off. He then opened the system interface to check his points balance– 2046 points.

Enough for 20 draws.

Without hesitation, Lu Qin initiated the lottery.

chapters.title: Chapter 81
chapters.content:
Chapter 81 Why Don’t You Just Aim for the Main Artery!

With 2,046 points in his system account, Lu Qin lounged comfortably on his bed, a hint of excitement in his expression as he began the lottery draw.

Lu Qin paused for a moment, feeling something was off. This was supposed to be a food stall system, so why did it offer grappling and combat skills? He was a cook, not a fighter.

The system’s explanation?

It made sense, but so far, Lu Qin had never encountered a dine-and-dasher. After all, this was a society governed by law and civility, with security cameras near his food stall. Pulling off a dine-and-dash wasn’t that easy.

Besides, his fried noodles stall was so popular that anyone trying to flee after eating wouldn’t make it more than a few meters before being pinned down by the queue of waiting customers.

Still, having some combat skills might be useful—if anyone tried to bully Lu Xiyao, he’d be ready to step in without hesitation.

He continued the lottery draw.

He retrieved the fan from the system space and placed it on his bedside table. The sleek white fan stood 48 cm tall, 32 cm wide—compact and portable. Its base had suction cups that could stick to any surface, with adjustable angles.

The highlight was the 5×10 cm solar panel on the base. Thanks to the system’s enhancement, the charging capability was exceptional. It could fully charge in 30 minutes under any light source, regardless of brightness. Once fully charged, the fan’s strongest setting could run for five continuous hours. In short, it was an infinitely powered portable fan.

Lu Qin appreciated the system’s thoughtfulness—this gadget was both practical and convenient. He gladly accepted it.

A Class C license?

That covers small passenger vehicles and light or micro cargo vehicles.

Lu Qin pondered, he only had a three-wheeled vehicle right now. Was this the system’s indirect hint that he should buy a car?

Lu Qin’s heart skipped a beat—finally, a skill related to cooking!

In an instant, his brain, body, and nerves were infused with unparalleled culinary expertise.

From kneading the dough, seasoning the filling, to the precise steaming time and heat control, every detail and image of making crystal shrimp dumplings was etched into his memory. The knowledge seamlessly integrated into his understanding. It was time to introduce a new dish tomorrow. He continued drawing.

After completing 20 draws, Lu Qin began feeling sleepy and drifted off into a peaceful slumber.

The next morning, at precisely 4:50 a.m., without an alarm clock, Lu Qin woke up as if guided by an internal clock. He felt refreshed and energetic. After washing up in the bathroom, he stepped into the courtyard and practiced a set of grappling and combat moves. Each punch and kick carried a lethal force.

Now, Lu Qin could take down ten ordinary adults without breaking a sweat. Even five armed attackers wouldn’t stand a chance against him.

He was strong. But strength wasn’t always necessary. He hopped onto his three-wheeler and headed out for his usual grocery run. At the community activity grounds Several elderly people were exercising.

Mr. Ge was furiously spinning the discs on the Tai Chi wheel. If it weren’t for the screws holding them in place, they’d probably fly off into the sky.

Chen Dahai was performing a set of Five Animal Frolics. His movements looked peculiar, but he carried them out with utmost seriousness.

Seeing Lu Qin riding out on his three-wheeler, the two paused briefly.

“Young Lu, you’re up this early every day. Don’t you ever feel tired?”

“Not at all. I feel great,” Lu Qin replied cheerfully.

Chen Dahai, with his left hip raised and left foot off the ground, mimicked a bear’s paw in mid-motion as he practiced the ‘Bear Frolic’.

“Morning, Lu. Don’t exaggerate. Young people these days don’t get up until noon unless they absolutely have to. You’re only up early because you have to run your stall. Still, your hard work is admirable—really impressive!”

Mr. Ge nodded.

“Young Lu, are you still setting up at the Sunshine Elementary School gate today?”

“Yeah, and I’ll be making crystal shrimp dumplings today. If you want some, come early to line up.”

Chen Dahai, Mr. Ge, and the other elderly folks lit up at the mention of crystal shrimp dumplings.

Crystal shrimp dumplings are a famous Cantonese dim sum dish, a must-order item for morning tea in Guangzhou. They’re also common in Jiangcheng’s tea houses.

Many had tried them before, but the taste was usually just average. Chen Dahai grew excited at the thought of Lu Qin’s version.

He nudged Mr. Ge.

“Stop messing with that thing—you’ve worn the surface smooth. Go home, get ready, and head over early to line up. These shrimp dumplings are bound to be limited.”

Lu Qin arrived at the familiar Jingyuan Market.

At the rice noodle stall, Auntie Zheng greeted him warmly.

“Lu, you’re here! How about a bowl of iced jelly? Just made it—smooth, refreshing, and perfect for cooling down.”

Auntie Zheng was always very polite. When Lu Qin arrived, she didn’t immediately ask how many pounds of rice noodles he needed but treated him like a regular, first exchanging pleasantries.

After all, Lu Qin always bought in bulk—hundreds of pounds at a time. He was a VIP customer, paying a discounted price of 3.8 yuan per pound for the authentic, high-quality rice noodles she sold.

“No need to be so polite, Auntie Zheng. Today I need 250 pounds of Anyang rice noodles. Just deliver them directly to Mr. Zhao at the disposable tableware shop by the entrance. He’ll be passing by to make a delivery later.”

“Got it. I only have 120 pounds here right now. Let me get my husband to fetch the rest from the storage room.”

Lu Qin didn’t need to wait around. He headed straight to the seafood section.

“Boss, how much for the freshwater shrimp?”

“29.5 yuan per pound. They’re delivered fresh—just look at them, every one of them is lively! How many pounds do you want, handsome?”

“Give me 20 pounds.”

“Got it, 2 pounds, right? Wait, what? Did you say 20 pounds?”

The middle-aged man in a leather apron froze for a moment, visibly startled. Then his expression shifted to one of shock—he even dropped the cigarette he had been holding between his lips.

A big customer!

The man quickly broke into a grin.

“Boss, are you sure you want 20 pounds? I’ll give you a discount—28.5 yuan per pound.”

Lu Qin replied, “How about 26.5 yuan?”

The man feigned surprise.

“Young man, you can’t just slash prices like that—it’s like cutting my main artery! 28.5 is already a fair price. If you don’t believe me, check out the other stalls. At Darunfa Supermarket, this same shrimp is 33 yuan per pound!”

Lu Qin chuckled.

“At the Chengnan farmers’ market, the same shrimp goes for 26 yuan per pound. If you don’t believe me, you can go check.”

The man’s expression changed slightly. He glanced at Lu Qin—ah, so this guy had done his market research! Why didn’t he mention that earlier and save me the performance?

“Well, haha, fine, let’s go with your price—26.5 yuan per pound.”

Twenty pounds were worth the equivalent of ten regular customers. A little less profit was acceptable.

The man weighed out 20 pounds of shrimp. “Boss, that’ll be 530 yuan. Here, you can scan my code.”

Lu Qin glanced at the payment QR code stuck on the glass tank, then at the man’s phone displaying his WeChat payment code.

Smiling, he said, “Boss, I’ll scan you directly? Is this your secret stash away from your wife?”

“Shh—!”

The man jumped, quickly gesturing for silence.

“Hey, hey, we’re both men—you understand.”

Lu Qin grinned.

“Then how about we make it 500 flat? Let’s skip the change.”

“What the heck! Young man, you’re taking advantage of me while I’m down!”

The man pulled out a pack of Liqun cigarettes, offered one to Lu Qin, and smiled.

“Running a business isn’t easy. This deal barely makes me a few dozen yuan. If I give you another 30 yuan off, I won’t even have enough for my trouble.”

“Add me on WeChat. Next time, I’ll give you a flat 26 yuan per pound.”

“But the shrimp’s quality and freshness need to be guaranteed. Otherwise, I won’t take them.”

“You don’t need to worry about that. The quality is 100% guaranteed. If it’s not fresh, I won’t even sell it—I’ll eat it myself. I’ve been running this seafood stall at Jingyuan for twelve years. Reputation matters.”

Lu Qin added the man, Hao Boss, on WeChat and transferred 530 yuan to him.

chapters.title: Chapter 82
chapters.content:
Chapter 82 Master of Time Management! Introducing the New Crystal Shrimp Dumplings!

Lu Qin had just bought the necessary ingredients for making crystal shrimp dumplings—wheat starch, tapioca starch, and more.

At his usual vegetable stall, run by an elderly man named Mr. Zhang, Lu purchased six pounds of carrots, five pounds of garlic sprouts, eight pounds of cabbage, three pounds of bean sprouts, ten pounds of green peppers, and two pounds of garlic cloves, spending a total of 102.6 yuan. Mr. Zhang also gifted him a bunch of scallions and two pieces of ginger.

After buying the vegetables, Lu headed to the dry goods store for more supplies.

At 5:45 AM, Lu Qin rode his tricycle back home and unloaded the ingredients into the kitchen. He immediately got to work—setting mung bean soup to boil, blanching pork belly in a cast iron pot, and letting the glutinous rice dough rest.

Juggling multiple tasks at once, Lu Qin didn’t seem rushed but worked methodically and efficiently. He precisely controlled the amount of water, the heat, and the cooking time for each dish.

This mastery of multitasking was the result of advanced skills, granting him superhuman culinary efficiency. It allowed him to prepare a variety of dishes in the shortest, most reasonable time possible.

For the crystal shrimp dumpling wrappers, Lu used wheat starch, also known as clear flour, made from the starchy portion of wheat after gluten removal. Adding a bit of tapioca starch gave the wrappers a pleasantly chewy texture.

To make the filling, fresh shrimp were peeled, deveined, and cleaned. Since shrimp are naturally low in fat, Lu mixed in a third of their weight in fatty pork to make the filling more tender and flavorful.

Using a three-tiered bamboo steamer, with a layer of cloth on each level, he could steam 120 dumplings at once over medium heat for 10 minutes. By the end of it, Lu Qin had made nearly 800 crystal shrimp dumplings.

At 7:30 AM, the mung bean soup, ice crystal cake, and chicken tofu flower soup were also ready. Lu Qin went to wake his little girl, Lu Xiyao.

Still lost in her dreams, her rosy little mouth mumbled something incomprehensible. Lu gently nudged her awake.

“Papa~! Your little cutie is sleeping. Please don’t disturb her~!” She mumbled groggily.

“Alright, I’ll just give you a forehead flick.”

The threat made her spring up immediately, her hair a messy nest.

“Papa~! A forehead flick is too dangerous. Better not, okay~?”

“That depends on your performance.”

“I’ll get dressed and wash up right now!”

Like a flash, she dressed herself, but…

“Xiyao, your shirt is on backward.”

“It looks better this way.”

She stubbornly wanted to leave it as it was, but Lu Qin gently held her head.

“Fix it.”

“Okay~!”

Her little face was full of reluctant resignation. Breakfast was tomato and egg noodles with crystal shrimp dumplings.

Her eyes sparkled as she looked at the dumplings.

“Papa~! What are these?”
