“Crystal shrimp dumplings. They’re new. Give them a try.”

She grabbed one with her hand and took a bite. The dumpling, slightly cooled, wasn’t too hot. The fresh, savory taste instantly captivated her. “Yummy~! So yummy~!”

She immediately grabbed another dumpling, her cheeks puffing up like a little hamster as she tried to chew both at once.

“Papa~! You’re amazing! These dumplings are super tasty!”

“Eat slowly. Don’t stuff your mouth so full.”

“Got it~!”

Out of the six dumplings on the plate, Xiyao polished off three and reluctantly left the other three for her father.

“Papa~! You eat too!” She offered before slurping her noodles.

Lu Qin’s tomato and egg noodles were already a signature dish before he gained his system skills. Now, with added expertise, their taste reached a whole new level.

Xiyao finished her bowl, even drinking the soup to the last drop, and let out a satisfied burp.

Lu packed her lunch—imperial chicken wings, twice-cooked pork, stir-fried celery with tofu, and three taels of rice. While she could only eat one tael of rice from the kindergarten meals, she always managed to finish three when it was her dad’s cooking.

Lastly, he placed two boxes of ice jelly cake in her canvas bag for an afternoon snack, just in case she got hungry later.

Before setting off, Lu Qin took out his phone and posted a notice in the group chat.

As soon as they saw there was a new item, the group chat, filled with foodies, exploded with excitement.

Lu Qin added, “Starting today, stir-fried noodles and mung bean smoothies will also be limited in quantity—each person can only buy three portions.”

The chat instantly filled with comments.

Some protested, while others cheered. The ones protesting were usually from larger families. Families with five or six people wanted to buy five or six portions of stir-fried noodles at a time. Then there were the big eaters—some could eat eight portions of stir-fried noodles in one go!

There was even a competitive eater who once bought eight portions from Lu Qin, squatted by the stall, and devoured them all. They even drank three cups of mung bean smoothie afterward, leaving other customers completely stunned. This person claimed they were only 80% full and queued up again to buy two servings of ice crystal cake.

The ones cheering were often those who arrived late and found there wasn’t much left. Sometimes they didn’t arrive late but found certain conspicuous customers buying ten portions of stir-fried noodles at once. The people at the back of the line would panic, worried that the noodles would sell out.

The record for the largest single purchase was someone who bought 50 portions of stir-fried noodles and mung bean smoothies, claiming their boss at the company cafeteria sent them to buy because no lunch was being prepared that day. The employees all ate Lu Qin’s stir-fried noodles instead.

Having his food widely recognized by customers was a good thing. However, to ensure that everyone could have a taste, Lu Qin decided to implement the purchasing limit after much deliberation.

After all, there were many customers, but he could only make two or three hundred portions each time. Limiting purchases was the fairest solution.

Lu Qin dropped Lu Xiyao off at the Starlight Kindergarten gate, where Teacher Li took her inside. Only after the little girl waved goodbye from the classroom door did he hop onto his tricycle and head toward Sunshine Elementary School.

“Hurry up, handsome Lu! Don’t starve me!”

“You’re a 180-pound baby, almost 300 months old. You could go half a month without starving.”

“Not starving—drooling to death!”

“Boss Lu, I’m looking forward to the crystal shrimp dumplings!”

“I love dumplings! I could devour ten in one bite!”

“Even stir-fried noodles are being limited? Damn it! Luckily, I brought my wife along. That way, we can buy more ice jelly cake—sweet!”

“I brought my kid. That counts as a head too, right?”

“Your kid isn’t even 1.2 meters tall. Doesn’t count.”

When Lu Qin set up his stall, a line of customers was already waiting, craning their necks and peeking toward his stall. Even teachers from Sunshine Elementary School came to buy his stir-fried noodles. The school’s teacher cafeteria had seen its breakfast diners drop by a third.

At the Ourei International Hotel, in the CEO’s office, Song Xuanxuan was called in by Shen Muyan.

“Has President Ouyang from the Shengyang Group arrived?”

“Not yet. The meeting was scheduled for 9 o’clock, and it’s already 8:50.”

Shen Muyan frowned slightly.

“I heard that President Ouyang detests tardiness and is always punctual. He must already be here. Go check with the reception desk to see if they missed him.”

Song Xuanxuan was just about to leave when the restaurant manager, Wang, burst through the door in a rush.

Without knocking, Manager Wang almost collided with Song Xuanxuan. He wiped the sweat from his forehead, disregarding formalities, and quickly reported to Shen Muyan.

“President Shen, President Ouyang arrived twenty minutes ago.”

“He’s here? Where is he?”

“He’s in the Azalea Restaurant on the third floor. He ordered a bowl of liver paste soup, and the kitchen is making it now.”

“Liver paste soup?” Shen Muyan frowned. “What kind of dish is that?”

“It’s a Sichuan dish on the last page of the menu. Hardly anyone orders it.”

“Does Chef Hu have it under control?”

“He said he’d do his best. The preparation for liver paste soup is incredibly meticulous and tests the chef’s skills—it’s a complex dish.”

Before he finished speaking, Shen Muyan was already in the elevator, heading quickly towards the Azalea Restaurant.

chapters.title: Chapter 83
chapters.content:
Chapter 83 No Need to Taste It, This Liver Paste Soup Fails!

At Azalea Restaurant, in Private Dining RoomInside sat a cold-faced young man in a suit, his hair meticulously groomed, and wearing refined glasses. His demeanor radiated authority. Everyone else in the room stood respectfully, leaving the seats empty.

After a short while, Chef Hu entered, carrying a bowl of liver paste soup. His expression was a mix of anxiety and determination.

Although Hu Zhijun was a skilled chef, proficient in Sichuan and Hunan cuisines, liver paste soup was an intricate banquet dish in Sichuan cuisine. While he could prepare it, it was not his forte.

Frankly, among ten Sichuan chefs, finding even one who could make liver paste soup was rare. Whether it met the standard was an entirely different question. Only true masters of Sichuan cuisine could produce an authentic, flawless version of the dish.

The soup was placed in front of the young man, Ouyang Lin. Just as it was set down, a knock came at the door. Shen Muyan entered, followed by Song Yuanyuan and the restaurant manager, Wang.

Upon entering, Shen Muyan immediately sensed the tense atmosphere in the room but said nothing. Her gaze shifted from the liver paste soup on the table to Ouyang Lin, waiting for him to sample the dish.

It didn’t take much for Shen to guess Ouyang Lin’s motive for his unexpected visit to the Azalea Restaurant. Clearly, he was here to evaluate the culinary standards of Ourei International Hotel.

The Ourei Hotel was seeking a major investment from Shengyang Group, so it made sense for Shengyang to conduct an inspection. However, Shen had assumed Ouyang Lin’s focus would be on aspects like the hotel’s cleanliness, room comfort, or staff professionalism. Instead, he had zeroed in on the restaurant’s culinary quality—and given no time for preparation.

Even more puzzling was his choice of liver paste soup, a rarely ordered Sichuan dish. Hu Zhijun was an experienced chef, but based on Manager Wang’s earlier feedback, his “I’ll do my best” comment didn’t inspire much confidence.

This left Shen Muyan feeling cornered, unsure how to salvage the situation. She chose to remain silent for now, waiting to see Ouyang Lin’s reaction.

Chef Hu, on the other hand, was a bundle of nerves. He knew this man was the representative of Shengyang Group, and the hotel’s investment hinged on his assessment. With Shen Muyan personally present, Hu couldn’t help but wonder— if his dish didn’t meet expectations, would Shengyang Group cancel the deal because of him?

The thought made his heart race.

I’m just a chef! How did all this pressure land on me? If he’s unhappy, am I supposed to take the fall?

Hu swallowed hard, trying to steady himself. His palms were sweaty as he began to explain the dish.

“President Ouyang, for this liver paste soup, I used a three-year-old free-range chicken and premium ham to make the broth. I’ve done my best to ensure the soup’s flavor is fresh and rich. Please, have a taste…”

In such high-stakes settings, chefs often explain their dishes. It’s both a way to share the effort behind the meal and a subtle defense mechanism, preempting criticism by showcasing the care involved. After all, food is subjective—what pleases one might not please another.

But before Hu could finish, Ouyang Lin raised his hand, cutting him off.

“No need to taste it. This liver paste soup fails.”

“Ah! What?!”

Hu Zhijun froze, his mind blank. For a moment, he couldn’t even find words.

If anyone else had dismissed his dish without tasting it, he would have snapped back with— Who do you think you are? If you can’t explain why it fails, don’t make me pick up my cleaver!

But this was no ordinary diner. Confronting Ouyang Lin was out of the question. Manager Wang’s face turned pale. For Ouyang Lin to dismiss the dish without even tasting it indicated his deep dissatisfaction.

Shen Muyan frowned slightly and spoke up, trying to salvage the situation.

“President Ouyang, if you’re not satisfied with this dish, perhaps you’d like to try something else? Chef Hu is skilled in many Sichuan and Hunan specialties.”

Ouyang Lin didn’t look up. His voice was calm but carried an undeniable authority.

“This isn’t about the chef’s skill or other dishes. The issue lies with the foundation. The liver paste soup lacks authenticity in its appearance alone. It’s not a question of flavor but of understanding. True Sichuan cuisine requires mastery of tradition. Without that, the rest is irrelevant.”

Hu Zhijun felt as though he’d been struck by lightning. His lips moved, but no words came out. He hadn’t expected such a harsh critique. Shen Muyan’s heart sank further. Ouyang Lin’s words weren’t just a rejection of the dish but an indirect critique of the hotel’s standards as a whole.

Ouyang Lin finally looked up, his gaze sweeping the room.

“If Ourei Hotel wishes to position itself as a luxury establishment, it must hold its cuisine to the same high standard as its service. A dish like this is unqualified, even as a test.”

Ouyang Lin shook his head and got straight to the point.

“President Shen, I have only one requirement. If you want this investment, Ourei Hotel must prepare a liver paste soup that satisfies me. I’ll give you three days.”

With that, Ouyang Lin turned and left without looking back. Manager Wang hurried to open the door for him.

_____

Inside the Private Room, a long silence hung in the air. Manager Wang bent down to examine the liver paste soup closely. The broth was clear and bright, exuding a rich aroma. It was even garnished with high-quality cordyceps, giving it a high-end presentation. Just by the looks of it, it seemed to be an exceptional dish. There was no apparent reason for it to be dismissed without even a taste.

“Chef Hu, are there other ways to prepare liver paste soup? Maybe President Ouyang is looking for a variation that differs from your method.”

“Liver paste soup is made this way. It’s one of the four classic clear soups in Sichuan cuisine. There aren’t any other versions,” Hu Zhijun replied firmly.

As a seasoned chef who had trained several apprentices, Hu Zhijun was confident in his knowledge. If he couldn’t make a dish like this, he might as well quit the industry.

Turning to Shen Muyan, he explained, “President Shen, while I don’t make this dish often, it doesn’t mean I can’t make it. Please, try it yourself.”

Shen nodded, picked up a spoon, and took a sip of the soup before tasting a piece of the liver paste. The broth was fresh and flavorful, the liver paste delicate and smooth. It tasted excellent and could easily be served at any banquet without issue.

Puzzled, Shen Muyan took out her phone, snapped a photo of the dish, and headed to the back kitchen of the Le Xuan Tea Restaurant to find Chef Xiong Tianmin.

_____

At this time, the tea restaurant wasn’t busy. Chef Xiong was experimenting with making ice crystal cake.

“Ice crystal cake?”

Shen noticed the small, four-centimeter-square white cakes with translucent skins neatly arranged on a stainless steel tray. She raised her eyebrows in surprise.

Xiong, aware that Lu Qin was Shen’s ex-husband, felt awkward and self-conscious about being caught trying to replicate one of Lu’s signature creations.

He forced a smile.

“President Shen, if I can achieve 80% of the flavor of Lu’s ice crystal cake, it might be worth adding to the tea restaurant’s menu.”

Shen picked up a piece of ice crystal cake, examining it from all sides. Its appearance was indeed somewhat similar to Lu Qin’s version. She took a bite.

Xiong watched her expectantly.

“How is it, President Shen?”

“Thirty percent,” she said coolly.

The comment hit Xiong like a blow. He was still far from his goal. He quickly took a piece for himself to taste.

“Thirty percent feels a bit harsh. It’s at least 35%,” he muttered, determined to save face.

Not one to waste food, Shen finished the piece. Compared to Lu Qin’s ice crystal cake, the texture and flavor were worlds apart. This moment made her fully appreciate Lu Qin’s skill.

Xiong Tianmin was already an outstanding pastry chef, a protégé of Maidejun, a renowned national banquet chef. Yet even he could only replicate 30% of Lu Qin’s ice crystal cake.

The realization was staggering. Many techniques in desserts and pastries are universal, so a 30% similarity was shockingly low.

“President Shen, is there something you need?”

Xiong started packing the remaining ice crystal cakes into a box, intending to take them home for further analysis while eating.

“I need your help contacting Chef Maidejun.” Shen pulled out her phone and showed Xiong the picture of the liver paste soup, explaining the situation briefly.

Without hesitation, Xiong took out his phone and called Maidejun.

chapters.title: Chapter 84
chapters.content:
Chapter 84 There’s Someone Who Might Be Able to Make Liver Paste Soup!”

Maidejun lived in Tianxin Community, a mid-rise residential area with good facilities, conveniently located not far from the old city. Now retired, he led a leisurely life, running errands and taking care of his grandson.

A Mercedes sedan pulled up at the community gate. Shen Muyan had brought along Fang Ya to visit. Regarding the liver paste soup issue, she might need Maidejun to step in personally, and having Fang Ya as an extra persuader could help.

Fang Ya, clearly dragged out of bed, looked less than thrilled. Being woken at this hour to act as a ‘tool person’ was bound to irritate anyone.

“You owe me. I want stir-fried noodles, ice crystal cake, chicken tofu flower, and imperial chicken wings from Lu Qin later,” she grumbled.

Shen ignored her, not bothering to remind Fang that Lu Qin was no longer part of her life. Carrying a box of fruits, the two entered the community, stepped into the building of Block 16, and took the elevator up.

When they arrived, it was Maidejun’s wife who answered the door.

“Old Mai is out for a walk with Xiao Huzi. Come in and have some tea. I’ll give him a call,” she offered.

“No rush, Auntie,” Shen replied politely.

About fifteen minutes later, Maidejun returned, his grandson in tow.

______

The Problem with Liver Paste Soup

“Hmm, liver paste soup…”

After hearing the situation, Maidejun put on his reading glasses and carefully examined the photo of the dish on Shen’s phone.

“Look here,” he said, pointing at the liver paste in the image. “The surface is pitted with tiny holes. That’s the most obvious issue. Nowadays, people use electric steam ovens instead of traditional steamers. While electric ovens are faster, they’re completely sealed. The trapped steam condenses into water droplets, which fall onto the liver paste and create those imperfections.”

Shen’s eyes lit up with realization.

“So that’s why Ouyang Lin didn’t even taste it and deemed the soup unqualified.”

Maidejun nodded.

“A true food connoisseur evaluates a dish’s appearance, aroma, and flavor. If any of these aspects falls short, it’s a fail.”

Shen immediately asked, “So if we replace the electric steamer with a traditional one, would that solve the problem?”

Maidejun chuckled, shaking his head with a hint of amusement.

“It’s not that simple.”

Fang Ya, munching on a slice of watermelon, chimed in.

“Uncle Mai, why don’t you step in and make the liver paste soup for my dear Shen here? Problem solved.”

Shen quickly added, “Mr. Mai, we’re happy to compensate you generously for your help.”

Maidejun popped a grape into his grandson’s mouth and waved them off with a smile.

“I’m retired. Haven’t held a knife in years. These days, I’m just in charge of grocery shopping, my wife does all the cooking.”

“Uncle Mai, you’re still sharp as ever. Please help us!” Fang Ya pleaded, piling on the flattery.

“It’s not that I don’t want to help,” he replied. “Liver paste soup is a highly technical dish that demands exceptional knife skills. My hands aren’t steady anymore. If I mess it up, not only would I fail you, but I’d tarnish my reputation. It’s not worth it.”

After a pause, he added, “Besides, liver paste soup isn’t even my specialty. Even if I were in top form, I might not be able to meet that Ouyang fellow’s standards.”

Shen frowned. Fang Ya stopped pinching Xiao Huzi’s cheek and asked in surprise, “Not even you can do it? Then what are we supposed to do?”

Shen suddenly recalled something.

“Mr. Mai, I heard you have a very talented apprentice in Haishi, someone with the surname Sui…”

“Oh, you mean Xiao Fu? He’s not up to the task either. His skills are what I taught him, and after graduating, he focused on business rather than improving his craft. He can handle other Sichuan dishes, but liver paste soup isn’t one of them.”

Shen’s disappointment was written all over her face.

“But,” Maidejun said, brightening, “you don’t need to lose hope, Xiao Shen. In Jiangcheng, I know someone who might be able to make this liver paste soup.”

“Who?” Shen and Fang asked simultaneously.

Maidejun pulled out his phone and opened a Douyin (TikTok) video.

“Take a look. This is the person. Not sure if you know him, but his stir-fried noodles stand is wildly popular in Jiangcheng. And he doesn’t just sell stir-fried noodles.”

The video showed footage of Lu Qin expertly cooking stir-fried noodles, followed by close-ups of the finished dish.

“Lu Qin!” Fang Ya exclaimed in shock.

“You know him?” Asked Maidejun, equally surprised.

“Know him? He’s Shen Muyan’s ex-husband!” Fang blurted out.

Maidejun slapped his thigh and laughed.

“Well, isn’t this a waste of time! Why come to an old geezer like me when you’ve got him?”

“Wait a minute…” Maidejun paused, looking puzzled. “Xiao Shen, you and Lu Qin were married. How could you not know your husband can cook? And at a level higher than mine?”

Fang Ya gasped.

“What? Uncle Mai, are you saying Lu Qin is better than you?”

“That’s only natural, I’m a fan of his as well. I’ve been having fried noodles for breakfast for several days now. Little Tiger particularly loves his ice crystal cake, something I can’t manage to replicate.”

Shen Muyan and Fang Ya exchanged glances, their eyes filled with shock.

Maidejun used to be a national banquet master chef, and Lu Qin is even better than him!

Shen Muyan asked, “Mr. Mai, can he make liver paste soup?”

“Twice-cooked pork, chicken bean curd flower, imperial concubine chicken wings—all Sichuan dishes. To elevate them to that level shows his knife skills, experience, and techniques are extraordinary… Wait a second, you’re his wife. You don’t know whether he can make liver paste soup?”

Shen Muyan didn’t know how to respond, it wasn’t something she could explain publicly about their divorce.

Fang Ya stepped forward to cover for her.

“They don’t interfere in each other’s work affairs.”

Maidejun didn’t think much of it and simply said, “If you want to know if he can make liver paste soup, why don’t you just call and ask him directly?”

______

Elsewhere, Lu Qin had finished packing up his morning food stall and was riding his three-wheeled vehicle home. As he passed the Wanda Plaza, he stopped for a moment. He remembered there was a boutique store on the first floor, so he parked his vehicle and went in to buy a few hairpins and hair ties for Lu Xiyao.

After making his purchases and just as he stepped out of the boutique store, a car salesperson approached him, insisting he take a look at a new energy vehicle.

“Sir, even if you’re not buying, take a look at this new M7 model. It just arrived yesterday. It’s a spacious five-seater and comes equipped with a fridge, a TV, a large sofa, and Longguo’s most advanced intelligent driving system—HarmonyOS cockpit. You can come in and experience it…”

Lu Qin was about to say he had other matters to attend to when he noticed that the car’s exterior was impressive, and the interior looked quite nice as well. It didn’t cost anything to take a look, so he got in.

The salesperson had only just started their pitch when Lu Qin’s phone rang.

It was Shen Muyan calling.

“Liver paste soup? Yes, I can make it.”

Lu Qin answered calmly.

On the other end, Shen Muyan’s heart skipped a beat, a trace of excitement flashing in her beautiful eyes.

Maidejun had a look that said, I told you he could make it.

Shen Muyan sent Lu Qin a picture of the liver paste soup made by Hu Zhijun.

“Take a look at the photo I sent on WeChat. Is there anything wrong with this liver paste soup? Can you tell?”

Even though Lu Qin claimed he could make it, Shen Muyan still needed to confirm his skill level. After all, being able to make something and making it delicious were two entirely different things.

Hu Zhijun was a prime example.

Even with Maidejun, who was often praised as a master chef, personally preparing the dish, it was uncertain if it would satisfy Ouyang Lin. This alone showed that liver paste soup was not a dish just anyone could master.

Lu Qin responded quickly.

“There are three problems.”

“First…”

chapters.title: Chapter 85
chapters.content:
Chapter 85 Diagnosed three issues! He’s raising the stakes!

Shen Muyan turned on the speakerphone, and both Maidejun and Fang Ya leaned in closer.

After looking at the picture of the liver paste soup, Lu Qin quickly pointed out three problems. This stunned Shen Muyan, Fang Ya, and Maidejun.

Even Maidejun could only spot one issue, yet Lu Qin found three right away!

“First issue: the surface of the liver paste is pitted, which suggests it was steamed using an electric steamer.”

This matched what Maidejun had already observed.

“Second issue: the pork liver is incorrect. To make liver paste soup, you need to use fine sand pork liver.”

Maidejun’s heart skipped a beat. The pork liver had already been minced into a paste, yet Lu Qin could tell from the picture that the pork liver was wrong? How did he figure that out? Even with reading glasses on, Maidejun hadn’t noticed a thing.

“Fine sand pork liver?” Shen Muyan asked.

Lu Qin explained, “Fine sand pork liver, also known as yellow sand liver or powder liver, is a special variety of pork liver. Its texture is delicate and tender, making it ideal for liver paste soup.”

“Third issue: the broth is slightly too oily and yellowish. This likely means older chickens or ducks were used to make the stock, or the pork was not lean enough. Such broth tends to be too rich and overpowers the flavor of the liver paste.”

After Lu Qin’s analysis, the trio on Shen Muyan’s end were left in stunned silence.

It was as if Lu Qin had diagnosed the symptoms of Hu Zhijun’s liver paste soup with pinpoint accuracy, like a doctor feeling a pulse.

Maidejun felt a bit ashamed. He hadn’t noticed the detail about the broth being too yellow and oily. No—he hadn’t even thought it was a problem, he considered it a normal color.

“ Sigh! Xiao Shen, now it’s clear your husband isn’t just capable of making liver paste soup, his skills go far beyond that,” Maidejun said with a sigh.

Shen Muyan nodded, turned off the speakerphone, and then walked to the corner of the room.

After a few moments of hesitation, she spoke, “Could I trouble you to come and make this liver paste soup? It won’t be for nothing, I’ll give you a red envelope.”

“Sure,” Lu Qin agreed readily. Not for Shen Muyan’s sake, but for the red envelope.

“Can I ask what’s going on? Doesn’t your hotel have plenty of chefs? None of them can make liver paste soup?”

Shen Muyan briefly explained the situation.

“Is it a big investment?”

“Why do you ask?”

“To determine my worth—it affects the size of the red envelope.”

Shen Muyan furrowed her brows slightly.

Did this man love money that much?

“How much do you want?”

Lu Qin turned to the car salesperson.

“How much is this new M7 Ultra?”

“Sir, this new M7 has four versions…”

“Just tell me the price of the highest configuration.”

“Sir, the top-spec six-seat, four-wheel-drive intelligent driving version is priced at 329,800 yuan. This month’s first batch of buyers can enjoy up to 35,000 yuan in purchase benefits, including a 12,000-yuan upgrade package for interior and exterior accessories…”

Lu Qin put the phone back to his ear.

“President Shen, did you hear that?”

Shen Muyan’s beautiful eyes widened slightly. “You want a car?”

A car worth 330,000 yuan!

“A car in exchange for a major investment—you should come out ahead.”

Taking a deep breath, Shen Muyan recalled that she had mentioned offering Maidejun a handsome reward earlier, which would have been at most 100,000 yuan. Maidejun was a national banquet chef, after all.

But this man was asking for 330,000 yuan right off the bat!

“How can you guarantee that the liver paste soup you make will satisfy them?”

“I can’t guarantee it. But if my liver paste soup can’t satisfy them, no one else can either.”

Lu Qin’s confident tone wasn’t just to reassure Shen Muyan—it was to ensure she would buy the car for him.

What he said was also the truth. With his system-provided, max-level liver paste soup skills, any criticism would be nitpicking at best.

“Alright, it’s a deal. When are you available?”

“Anytime. I can start now.”

“Good. Come to Ourei immediately.”

“I need to buy ingredients first.”

“The kitchen has…”

Shen Muyan’s voice trailed off as she remembered Lu Qin mentioning fine sand pork liver earlier. The kitchen definitely didn’t have that.

Lu Qin asked, “Can I get reimbursed for the ingredients?”

With a cold expression, Shen Muyan transferred 1,000 yuan to him.

“This should be enough. Anything extra won’t be refunded, and if it’s short, you’ll cover it. After buying the ingredients, come to Ourei. The broth will take at least two hours to prepare, so set the meeting for 1 PM.”

After hanging up, Lu Qin immediately paid a 5,000-yuan deposit for the car.

On the other side, Shen Muyan promptly asked Song Xuanxuan to contact Ouyang Lin’s secretary.

Fang Ya walked over.

“Sorted? Yaoyao’s dad agreed to make the liver paste soup?”

“Yes.”

Fang Ya clapped her hands, smiling brightly. “See? I told you he’d help you out. This means he still has feelings for you.”

Shen Muyan shot her a glance and said, “The fee is 330,000 yuan—just enough for a car.”

Fang Ya froze.

“What?”

Meanwhile, Ouyang Lin’s secretary received the call and immediately reported it to Ouyang Lin.

Ouyang Lin was surprised. He had just informed Ourei hotel in the morning, and they had already found someone capable of making liver paste soup?

Tapping his fingers on the desk thoughtfully, he instructed his secretary to respond to them.

Lu Qin took a cab to the East City Farmer’s Market, known for its expensive but high-quality and diverse produce. After purchasing all the ingredients needed for the liver paste soup, he headed to the Ourei Hotel by taxi.

At the hotel entrance, Manager Wang from the F&B department was waiting personally. As soon as Lu Qin got out of the car, Wang eagerly took the bags of ingredients from him.

“Mr. Lu, this way, please. President Shen and the others are waiting for you in the kitchen of the Bauhinia Restaurant.”

“Got it,” Lu Qin replied calmly.

Despite his young and striking appearance, which didn’t fit the typical image of a chef, Manager Wang didn’t question him.

When Lu Qin entered the kitchen, it was bustling with people.

Among them were Shen Muyan, Fang Ya, Maidejun, Xiong Tianmin, Song Xuanxuan, and Hu Zhijun, along with a group of chefs Hu had brought. Even other kitchen staff from different restaurant departments had shown up, either to learn or simply to watch the spectacle.

As Manager Wang escorted Lu Qin in, every gaze immediately turned towards him. Many were surprised to see such a young man being brought in to make liver paste soup, especially Hu Zhijun. Even he hadn’t expected Maidejun to join this event.

Shen Muyan stepped forward and said, “If you need anything, just let Chef Hu know.”

Placing the ingredients on the counter, Lu Qin glanced at the crowd in the kitchen and said nonchalantly, “I need everyone who isn’t involved to leave.”

“…”

Hu Zhijun promptly ordered his team and the other curious onlookers out of the kitchen.

The result was a hallway outside the kitchen becoming clogged with people.

Fang Ya approached Lu Qin with a professional handheld camera and aimed it directly at his face.

“Boss Lu, can I record your process of making the liver paste soup?”

“Feel free, it doesn’t matter to me,” Lu Qin replied indifferently.

Then he turned his gaze towards Maidejun and asked, “Mr. Mai? Why are you here too?”

A few people blinked in surprise.

This young man actually knew Maidejun? Impressive.

Even Hu Zhijun, who had initially dismissed Lu Qin, now took him a bit more seriously. What they didn’t know was that Lu Qin wasn’t fully aware of Maidejun’s prestigious background. He only knew him as an acquaintance through an introduction from Uncle Ge, a fellow culinary enthusiast.

They had exchanged contact information, and to Lu Qin, Maidejun was just a retired elder who often went to the market with his grandson and occasionally stopped by Lu Qin’s noodle stall.

Shen Muyan glanced at the ingredients Lu Qin had brought.

“So this is the fine sand pork liver you mentioned?”

Hu Zhijun froze.

“Fine sand pork liver?”

The term caught him off guard—he had never even heard of such an ingredient before.

chapters.title: Chapter 86
chapters.content:
Chapter 86 Look At This, This Is What Professionalism Looks Like! Eating Liver Without Tasting Liver, Impressive!

Fine sand pig liver?

Hu Zhijun’s heart skipped a beat, as though he had gained a crucial piece of information for making liver paste soup.

Shen Muyan observed the fine sand pig liver that Lu Qin had bought, which was also known as ‘yellow sand smooth’. It had a fresh pinkish color with a hint of yellow, making it easily distinguishable from regular pig liver.

Lu Qin noticed Shen Muyan’s curious expression and explained, “Fine sand pig liver has a texture like fine sand—soft and tender. Only pigs that have been raised for over a year under normal conditions can produce this kind of liver. Pigs raised with hormones for rapid growth will never develop the yellow sand smooth liver.”

“Besides its unique texture, yellow sand smooth liver is also more nutritious. This is because the pigs are allowed to grow naturally, without hormones, giving them healthy organs.”

“Fine sand pig liver is the highest quality liver, commonly known as fatty liver. It is the best liver for making liver paste soup, and there is no other better option.”

“The liver paste soup you sent me a picture of was made with regular pig liver. The result was a soup with a strong liver smell, and it was hard to make it smooth and even.”

Picture?

Hu Zhijun was startled. He was sure Lu Qin was referring to the liver paste soup he had made himself. Shen Muyan had taken a photo of it earlier that morning.

What the heck!

Hu Zhijun’s face turned red, feeling a bit embarrassed. But, he had definitely learned a key technique!

Lu Qin’s explanation made everyone around him realize. No wonder Shen Muyan had invited him to help out, this is what professionalism looks like!

At this moment, Fang Ya stepped forward and asked, “Master Lu, what about the steamer? Do we need to use the traditional bamboo steam basket?”

Lu Qin glanced at Fang Ya.

“Call me by my name. I’m not a master. You can use an electric steamer, but make sure to leave a small gap in the door while steaming. This allows the steam to escape, so the surface of the liver paste won’t have indentations. Otherwise, even if it tastes good, it won’t look good.”

Not just talking, but also putting it into practice. Lu Qin looked at the time and began to take action. The others immediately moved aside, giving Lu Qin some space.

Lu Qin laid the pig liver flat on the chopping board and started to pound it with the back of his knife. Liver paste soup is a high-end second-course dish.

What is a second-course dish? It’s the soup served after the second hot dish at a banquet. Liver paste soup used to be a very famous dish, but over time, it became less popular due to its difficulty to prepare. It also became a dish that many Sichuan chefs feared.

As soon as Lu Qin started cooking, he entered a different zone—serious, focused, and stable.

Everything around him became background noise, irrelevant to him. Liver paste soup is a dish that requires skill. The key is to make it delicate.

Lu Qin continuously pounded the pig liver with the back of his knife, while removing all the tendons. The tendons must be completely removed.

Once the liver was turned into a paste, Lu Qin took a large bowl, added cooking wine, green onions, and ginger to marinate, removing the fishy smell. This step took 15 minutes.

Mai Dejun and Hu Zhijun both looked surprised. Hu Zhijun had made liver paste soup earlier that morning, and it had taken him a full half hour to pound the pig liver into paste and marinate it.

This young man’s knife skills… they’re amazing!

The marinating took 30 minutes, and Lu Qin began to make the broth. Liver paste soup not only requires the paste to be good, but the broth also has to be top-notch. Making the broth is key to the success of the dish and tests the chef’s skill. The ingredients used were chicken, duck, ham, and pork knuckle.

As the saying goes, “Without ham, the broth is not beautiful, without chicken, it’s not fresh, without duck, it’s not fragrant, and without pork knuckle, it’s not rich.”

The reason making broth tests a chef’s skill is because it involves blending various flavors and cooking them together, which takes two to three hours, and it cannot be left unattended.

Shen Muyan’s eyes were locked on Lu Qin. She didn’t notice that she was getting a bit absorbed in watching him. Fang Ya, holding a camera, was walking around with professional steps, capturing every angle, even doing close-ups from time to time.

The kitchen staff at the door stretched their necks, eager to get a glimpse. When the broth was nearly ready, Lu Qin filtered the marinated pig liver paste using cheesecloth, turning it into liver juice.

He stirred in egg whites, added pepper and salt, and a bit of soybean flour to make the paste more delicate.

Add soybean flour?

Damn! The details!

Hu Zhijun cursed himself for not taking notes and repeated ‘add soybean flour’ in his mind five times.

Lu Qin set up three bowls, lined them with plastic wrap, brushed them with oil, and poured in the liver juice before placing them in the steamer.

Meanwhile, As the broth neared boiling, Lu Qin chopped five liang of lean meat, making sure there was no fat. He simmered the meat’s freshness into the broth. He filtered the meat paste through cheesecloth, repeating the process three times until the broth was clear. Because he added ham, the dish had a slight tea-colored hue.

At this moment, Everyone could smell the fresh aroma wafting through the air.

“Smells amazing!!!”

The liver paste soup was ready. He made three servings.

Lu Qin wiped his hands with a cloth and removed his apron.

“Done.”

Mai Dejun, who had been silent up until now, spoke up.

“Impressive!”

Someone at the door couldn’t help but exclaim, “Damn! This is like the earliest form of molecular gastronomy!”

“This is high-end Sichuan cuisine—liver paste soup?! Did you notice? Not a bit of MSG or chicken essence, it’s all about extracting the umami from the ingredients, amazing!”

“You can’t afford Sichuan cuisine that’s not spicy!”

“Why is Cantonese soup so famous? Because everyone knows how to make it. But Sichuan soup? Only the experts can truly appreciate it!”

In the private room, a bowl of liver paste soup was served to the table. Ouyang Lin glanced at it and nodded. He took a spoon and tasted a spoonful. His expression shifted as if he couldn’t believe how good it tasted.

Shen Muyan watched Ouyang Lin’s reaction, her heart finally at ease. It seemed like the dish had passed the test.

Indeed, After savoring the taste, Ouyang Lin nodded in satisfaction and marveled.

“The texture is delicate, it has the flavor of pig liver, but no liver smell at all. It melts in your mouth. As they say, ‘Eating liver without tasting liver,’ impressive!”

“Shen, I’m willing to invest an additional 20 million.”

Shen Muyan’s heart soared with joy, but her face remained elegantly calm. Meanwhile, Manager Wang was so excited he almost jumped up.

“Boss Lu is incredible! Not only did this dish secure the investment, but it also added an extra 20 million!”

“Thank you, Ouyang, for your trust. I look forward to our cooperation.”

“Likewise. Can I meet the chef who made the liver paste soup?”

“Of course.”

Shen Muyan instructed, and Manager Wang immediately ran to the kitchen. However, when the people left, the chef was nowhere to be found.

Shen Muyan frowned. He left so quickly!

“Left?”

Ouyang Lin’s eyes widened in surprise.

“He’s not one of your chefs? But a street vendor?”

Shen Muyan replied calmly, “He’s a street vendor. I just hired him to help with this. But, Ouyang, you didn’t say we had to use a chef from our own company, did you?”

Shen Muyan found a loophole.

Ouyang Lin remained silent.

Jiangcheng was known online as the ‘food desert’. How could a street vendor make liver paste soup this good?

Are you serious?

“Shen, you say you know him?”

“He’s the father of my child.”

Shen Muyan said, her face turning red without skipping a beat.

“!!!!!”

Manager Wang’s eyes nearly popped out.

chapters.title: Chapter 87
chapters.content:
Chapter 87 They Might Call the Police! The Taste Buds Were Amazed!

“He’s the father of my child.”

Those words were like thunder in a calm sky. Thankfully, there were only four people in the luxurious private room—Shen Muyan, Ouyang Lin, Manager Wang, and Ouyang Lin’s secretary. Otherwise, tomorrow, the whole company of Euray would be gossiping non-stop.

Manager Wang felt that Shen Muyan’s statement carried a lot of weight, and he didn’t dare process it, afraid of being silenced.

Ouyang Lin’s expression grew a bit strange. This couple was so odd—one was a president of a five-star hotel chain, the other a street vendor. The contrast was too much.

However, he didn’t have time to gossip, so he quickly shifted gears, putting away his smile.

“Shen, let’s get back to business.”

At that moment, Manager Wang and Ouyang Lin’s secretary both left the room.

Ouyang Lin got straight to the point.

“Shen, next weekend is my father’s 80th birthday, and we’re hosting a birthday banquet at Wangjing No. 1 in Haishi. One dish my father always talks about is liver paste soup, so it absolutely has to be on the menu. I need fifty servings of liver paste soup, and I hope your husband can come to Haishi for this.”

Having just added an extra 20 million to his investment, Shen Muyan couldn’t refuse this request.

“I’ll ask him,” Shen Muyan replied, unsure of how to respond right away.

“Feel free to name your price,” Ouyang Lin said, ensuring that Lu Qin wouldn’t be doing this for free.

…

In the kitchen of Zijing Restaurant, Lu Qin rushed in, made the liver paste soup, and then hurriedly left again. To outsiders, it looked like a chivalrous hero coming to the rescue.

But in reality, he had a hungry group in his Penguin group waiting for him. If he didn’t set up his street stall in the afternoon, they might even call the police, reporting a missing person or something.

On the table, two servings of liver paste soup were left.

“Uncle Mai, try this liver paste soup.”

In the kitchen, Mai Dejun was the oldest and most respected, so of course, the first taste was his. Mai Dejun didn’t hesitate and took a spoonful. Everyone watched him.

Just one bite, and Mai Dejun’s taste buds were completely blown away. The liver paste was dense, fresh, tender, and melted in his mouth. The broth was clear, fresh, and smooth, perfectly complementing the liver paste. The two flavors balanced each other perfectly, creating a non-spicy, non-pungent Sichuan dish that was brought to its peak in flavor and texture.

Is this the work of a master-level liver paste soup?

Mai Dejun thought that at his age, being able to taste such an exquisite liver paste soup was enough to make his life complete. He couldn’t resist having two more spoonfuls before noticing that there were still people in the kitchen. He put the bowl down and invited everyone to try it.

Soon, everyone took a spoon and tried a bite.

“Wow! So this is liver paste soup? How can the broth be this fresh?”

“This paste really melts in your mouth! What kind of knife skills does it take to do this?”

“The texture is delicate and delicious, and after the first bite, the aroma of the liver paste slowly spreads. There’s a refreshing and rich aftertaste. This must be top-tier cooking to make such a tasty liver paste soup!”

After tasting, Hu Zhijun felt his taste buds were shaken, and when he compared it to his own liver paste soup, he spat in disgust.

“What is this? What was I thinking?”

Shen Muyan and Manager Wang came into the kitchen, only to find that the two servings of liver paste soup had already been devoured by a group of people. Even the broth in the pot had been scooped up into individual bowls.

Lu Qin returned home on his three-wheeled scooter, preparing the ingredients for his street stall. It was already 4:30 pm.

Shen Muyan couldn’t help but feel a pang of regret. Each bowl of liver paste soup costs 110,000 yuan, and she hadn’t even had a taste.

…

In the Penguin group, the mute setting was lifted, and Lu Qin sent a message out to the group. The stall location was at the People’s Square Pedestrian Street.

Immediately, messages flooded in, many with pictures of people who looked emaciated and starving. Lu Qin chuckled, put his phone away, and set off on his scooter.

When he arrived at the People’s Square Pedestrian Street, the group admin, a chubby guy named Zhou Weiwei, was waiting at the entrance. As soon as he saw the scooter, he rushed over.

“Boss Lu, let’s set up in the busy district, the guys are all waiting!”

“Busy district?”

“Yeah, some of the customers have connections with the Bureau of Culture and Tourism, and they managed to get us a temporary stall spot.”

Lu Qin was led to the busy area.

Today, the queue was unusually long—about a third of the entire pedestrian street.

Passersby were completely confused.

“Hey, buddy, what are you guys lining up for? There’s nothing up ahead.”

“Just follow the line, you won’t regret it. There’ll be more people soon.”

“Don’t talk to me, I’ve been starving since morning. If you say one more word, I’ll pass out from low blood sugar!”

“Do you have any bread? I need something to hold me over.”

“No way, I’m waiting for stir-fried noodles, crystal shrimp dumplings, twice-cooked pork, imperial chicken wings, chicken tofu flower, and I’ll finish off with chilled mung bean soup. I’ve got the whole meal planned, can’t waste any space with bread.”

Surprisingly, many people didn’t even know what they were waiting for. They just followed the crowd, causing the line to stretch far and wide. This spectacle shocked the other food stall owners in the busy area. They rushed out to see the strange scene.

Someone yelled, “Boss Lu’s here!”

And immediately, the entire crowd turned to face Lu Qin.

“Boss Lu, you finally made it! I’m starving, I could eat eight dishes right now!”

“My stir-fried noodles are calling my name, hurry up and give me one!”

“Boss Lu, look at me, I’m so hungry my pants are falling down. The beautiful lady in front thought I was hitting on her and almost called the police.”

“Crystal shrimp dumplings! My crystal shrimp dumplings!”

“Imperial chicken wings, no wings!”

…

Amid countless complaints and requests, Lu Qin set up his stall and started serving food.

“What? Stir-fried noodles are limited?” The first customer asked in surprise, looking at the whiteboard.

“Hey handsome, what would you like?”

“You’re the handsome one, I’m not even gonna compete. If everything’s limited, what should I order? Just give me everything!”

“Okay.”

Lu Qin began to make stir-fried noodles and twice-cooked pork. One wok of noodles made five portions, and one wok of twice-cooked pork made three portions. Customers paid by scanning the QR code and calculated the total themselves to avoid distracting Lu Qin.

After the first customer left, the second customer was about to order when Lu Qin grabbed a plate of stir-fried noodles and started eating.

“I haven’t had lunch yet, just need a few bites to tide me over.”

At first, every customer was in a rush, but when they found out that Lu Qin hadn’t eaten yet, they all felt sympathy.

“Boss Lu, you haven’t eaten and you’re here serving us, I’m so touched!”

“Boss Lu, take your time eating. Get full, rest for a bit. If anyone rushes you, I’ll perform a full set of calisthenics… oh no, I’ll beat them with a combination of martial arts moves!”

“Boss Lu really isn’t easy. Every time he sets up his stall, he has to prepare hundreds of portions of ingredients, and once the morning shift is over, he starts prepping for the afternoon. He works harder than any donkey on a production team!”

“Hey, don’t insult people! How can you compare Boss Lu to a donkey?”

“Boss Lu is such a great guy, raising a kid and earning a living all on his own. Seriously, if it gets too tough, raise the price a little, I’ll be happy to pay more!”

Many people brought snacks and fruit that were originally meant for the little one, but now, they piled the fruits into the empty basket in front of Lu Qin.

One young lady even thoughtfully helped peel apples. Other girls saw this and couldn’t hold back. One peeled apples, another peeled oranges, and yet another chopped dragon fruit. It looked like a scene from a harem competition for favor.

chapters.title: Chapter 88
chapters.content:
Chapter 88 Fair Competition Is Fine With Me, but This Guy Is Cheating—He’s Using Technology!

Lu Qin finished a serving of fried noodles and then drank a cup of chilled green bean soup, feeling completely comfortable.

As for the attention from a few female customers, the fruit they offered, Lu Qin didn’t touch a single bite, and kindly reminded them.

“Isn’t your team supposed to be lining up?”

“Ah!!!”

The girls turned around and realized that they had been away from the line for too long, and now they didn’t know where to get back in.

“This young man, I was in this line just now, can you make some room?”

“Nonsense, when did you line up here? I’ve always been behind the gentlemen, don’t try to cause trouble!”

“Handsome guy, can I cut in?”

“Heh, beautiful lady, can I cut in?”

“Why are you acting like a pervert? Freak!”

The girls couldn’t get in and were left to stand at the very end of the line, feeling helpless. Some people around them laughed in schadenfreude. This was what they deserved for trying to cut in.

Lu Qin continued to run his stall, and the next customer was a woman with a child. As they watched other customers buying and eating, the delicious aroma wafted over, and the child’s mouth watered. The mother quickly wiped his face, trying to keep up.

“Would you like some ice crystal cake? You can let your child have a taste first.”

“Yes, two boxes, please. Usually, this little one is picky and doesn’t like anything, but as soon as he sees your fried noodles stall, his mouth starts watering like a dam has broken!”

The woman quickly opened a box of the ice crystal cake and fed a piece to the child. The line was long, but it moved quickly.

One by one, customers left, satisfied with their food. But the line never seemed to shorten, and new customers kept pouring in. In fact, the number of people leaving didn’t even match the number of new customers.

At the popular section of the walking street, this had never happened before. It wasn’t even evening yet, and the scene was already crazy. At first, curious passersby who didn’t know what was being sold just followed the crowd. Now, they knew.

“I can’t believe it! This fried noodles stall is the one I saw on Douyin yesterday, I’ve been wanting to try it out, and I accidentally ended up in the line!”

“This is the fried noodles guy who made all the kids at the kindergarten drool? I finally see him in person!”

“The double-cooked pork is so fragrant, it’ll make you want to drool!”

…

Other stalls and food vendors saw a sudden drop in customers. The owners were getting restless. One of the beef pancake stalls, which had a decent reputation in the walking street, usually had customers lining up. Now, it had only four customers, and those four were rushing to Lu Qin’s fried noodles stall.

“The beef pancakes are in the oven, you guys can’t leave!” The owner said anxiously.

“We don’t want the pancakes anymore. If we don’t hurry, we might not get any fried noodles!”

“The pancakes are already in the oven!”

“We’ve already paid, boss! You’re buying us beef pancakes, right!”

The four customers left without looking back.

“Damn! Do you still need me to treat you to beef pancakes?”

Nearby, the owner of the shredded ice stall walked over.

“Damn it! Lao Meng, we’re both suffering! I’m just wondering how a fried noodles stall could steal my business, then I found out, guess what? He also sells chilled green bean soup, and the customers say it tastes better than milk tea!”

The beef pancake owner slammed the tongs down, feeling frustrated.

“This fried noodles stall came out of nowhere! What are we supposed to do now, drink air for business?”

“I don’t know, but the fried noodles stall owner has a lot of fans. I saw many customers driving here just to get his food!”

The other stall owners gathered around.

“Damn, what’s going on? That stall is stealing all our customers!”

“The line is ridiculous, and the ones at the back have to wait about an hour. Can people really stand this?”

“These people must be addicted!”

“That stall’s food has got to have something weird in it! Is he using something like science or tech?”

“Poppy powder! It’s gotta be!”

“He wouldn’t, right? There’s even a policeman in the line!”

“Damn! The cop hasn’t even taken off his uniform and he’s already in line, that’s crazy!”

“Let’s report this to management, this guy is disrupting our business!”

“Exactly! If this keeps up, our stores will close for good!”

The group of vendors cursed and marched to the management office, feeling like there was no law left!

When they arrived at the office, they immediately smelled a strong fragrance.

“This smells familiar!”

When they entered, they saw the leader inside enjoying fried noodles, chilled green bean soup, double-cooked pork, and crystal shrimp dumplings.

Seeing the crowd, the leader jumped in surprise, causing some noodles to fall from his nose. He didn’t mind, though, and sucked them back up.

“Old Li, Old Wang… what’s going on?”

“Xiao Gong, that new fried noodles stall…”

Before Old Li could finish, the leader Xiao Gong cut him off.

“Stop! I know what you’re going to say. It’s temporary, just a temporary arrangement. The spots in the busy zone are all fixed, but old Liu’s wife gave birth, so one spot opened up. The guy paid for it and is just setting up for a few days!”

“So what if he paid? He just wants to make some extra money. We’ll pay him to leave!”

“Exactly! Exactly! Since he showed up, our business is gone!”

“Well, his food is really delicious! And hey, the popularity of the busy area has increased because of him, so it benefits you guys as well!”

“You’re just mad because you can’t stand seeing others making money, huh?”

“That’s right! You should reflect on why your food doesn’t compare to his!”

“Exactly! If you can’t beat him, kick him out. That’s a loser’s move!”

“Fair competition is fine with me, but that guy… he’s using cheats! It’s technology!”

“Stop talking nonsense. Do you have any evidence? Also, Old He, aren’t you from the bakery? Is the fried noodles stall really stealing your business?”

“Damn it! He’s also selling something called ice crystal cake. It’s so refreshing! Now, nobody’s buying my best-seller, green grape mousse!”

Xiao Gong’s eyes lit up.

“Ice crystal cake? I didn’t notice that when I was in line earlier. There were too many people. I just grabbed some random stuff. I’m so dumb! I should’ve tried it!”

“Xiao Gong, we’ve been friends for years! Just tell us, will you solve this for us and get him out?”

“…”

“Yeah, get him out!”

“ Cough, cough, alright, alright, I’ll handle it. The fried noodles stall is crazy popular, I’ll deal with it later.”

“Why later? Time’s ticking, Xiao Gong!”

“I need to finish the remaining half portion of fried noodles and shrimp dumplings first. They’ll taste terrible if they get cold!”

“…”

chapters.title: Chapter 89
chapters.content:
Chapter 89 When You Take a Bite, It Can Capture Your Soul – Now This Is What Real Food Is About, You Know?

Xiao, the manager of the pedestrian street, really didn’t want to get rid of Lu Qin’s stir-fried noodles stall. The food this boss made was really delicious—these stir-fried noodles! The twice-cooked pork! These crystal shrimp dumplings!

He felt like he’d wasted decades of his life! Lao Liu’s wife is going to be in labor for several days, and if this stir-fried noodle stall stays here every day, he could eat there every day – how nice would that be!

Moreover, the stall owner was paying the stall fee. And the stall fee was actually paid by the customers, how amazing is that?

But there was no choice, this ‘catfish’ in the busy area was way too overwhelming, leaving no space for other stalls to breathe. It was a big deal, so it was better to restore the area to its original state.

Xiao took the lead, followed by a group of angry snack stall owners, and walked directly towards Lu Qin’s stir-fried noodle stall. But before they could go very far, they were stopped by the customers who were lining up.

“Hey, hey, hey! What are you doing? Don’t cut in line, go to the back!”

“We’re not buying anything.”

Xiao was stunned

Did they look like they were cutting in line?

“Come on, can you come up with a better excuse? Someone tried that before, and guess what happened? Their legs got broken into three pieces!”

Lao Boss: “But people only have two legs, how could it be three?”

Xiao: “We’re not cutting in line. We’re just here to find Lu Qin, the owner of the stir-fried noodles.”

“Then you still need to wait in line!”

Looking for Lu Qin? Don’t tell me you guys know him and are trying to pull strings?!

Damn!

If so many people cut in line, how come I’m still standing here?

I’ll stop them even if I have to!

Hearing the commotion, many customers turned their heads to look.

“These people want to cut in line, just look at their manners!”

Xiao: “We didn’t…”

“Cut in line? No way! Wait your turn like everyone else!”

“I’ve been in line for half an hour already. If you try to cut in line, I’ll beat you up!”

“Who’s cutting in line? Who dares to? I’ll rip them apart!”

A massive customer stepped out. His chest muscles were big enough to crush eggs, and his arms were thicker than most people’s legs. He stood in front of Xiao like a mountain.

Xiao stood in his shadow, unable to stop himself from swallowing. He didn’t dare to say anything anymore. The other stall owners behind him were also silent.

This is terrifying!

If even the stir-fried noodle stall is this intimidating, how can they even negotiate?

“Maybe we should just wait until Lao Liu comes back, then they’ll leave on their own.”

“No way, does that mean we’ll starve for the next few days?!”

“Yeah, we should just shut down!”

Xiao: “Pathetic, can’t you guys come up with some sales promotions? Business is about ups and downs, if their business is good, you can’t just chase them away. You can’t say that either.”

Hearing this, the group of stall owners fell silent for a moment, but then suddenly lifted their heads proudly.

That’s right! We’ve been doing business here in the busy district for so many years. How can we let a newcomer steal everything from us?

We can’t let them be so arrogant!

Discounts! Promotions!

Not just steamed buns—let’s show them!

Xiao observed the expressions of the bosses, saw that they were full of fighting spirit, and quietly slipped back to the office to continue savoring the leftover stir-fried noodles and twice-cooked pork.

“Mmm… It smells so good! Too delicious!”

“Even if all the other snacks were free, I’d still save room for this crystal shrimp dumpling!”

“Huh!”

Xiao suddenly looked up. Could it be that the customers at the stir-fried noodle stall feel the same way?

Xiao’s expression changed subtly. That group of stall owners had eagerly wanted to fight a hard battle, but this battle seemed to be ending quite disastrously!

Niu Ji Beef Pancake Shop.

Niu, the owner, brought out several sheets of white paper and wrote in black marker.

The original price of the beef pancakes was 10 yuan each.

“Straight 50% off, let’s see if you guys come or not. I don’t believe there are still people who don’t like a good deal!”

This price was set with Niu’s teeth gritted. After all, selling at 5 yuan each meant no profit, and the buy two get one free offer was even a loss. Niu stood at the door shouting.

“Special offer today, beef pancakes for 5 yuan each!”

A chubby little kid holding a box of stir-fried noodles stopped in front of the pancake shop.

Niu’s eyes lit up.

“Hey, kid, try one of my beef pancakes! It’s crispy on the outside and packed with meat inside, you’ll taste the oil burst in your mouth! Only 5 yuan each, the original price is 10 yuan.”

“Ugh~!”

The little kid leaned forward to sniff, only to almost gag.

Niu got confused.

Niu’s face turned dark.

“Kid, what’s the meaning of this?”

“Sorry, your meat pancake smells too greasy, I almost threw up!”

Niu was about to curse.

“Damn! How is it greasy? It smells so good!”

The little kid quickly inhaled a few breaths from the stir-fried noodles box to recover.

“Smell this stir-fried noodles, it’s oily but not greasy, light and springy, just look at your beef pancake, with three sheets of paper just to wrap my fingers, and the oil gets on my hands. How can anyone eat that!”

Niu was speechless.

“What’s wrong with a bit of oil? That’s what makes it delicious! You people always like greasy stuff, why would you throw up?”

Niu took a closer sniff of the stir-fried noodles in the box and, to be honest, it did smell better than his own pancakes. He even felt like trying some of the stir-fried noodles.

The little kid quickly closed the box.

“Don’t let your saliva get in there.”

Niu’s face flushed red.

“5 yuan each, don’t you want to try? It’s half price!”

“No, no thanks.”

…

Other snack stalls in the busy area immediately followed suit with promotions and special deals. Grilled skewers, all meat for 3 yuan, vegetables for 1.5 yuan, and free drinks, self-serve.

Seafood hot pot for 48 yuan, two-person set, including large shrimp, octopus, abalone, and clams… with a free serving of spicy soybeans. Pancake family set only for the regular price, plus a free iced soy milk.

Spicy hotpot at 60% off, with a lottery for purchases over 50 yuan, the grand prize being a fruit phone. These promotions were not tricks, they were real discounts, and some were even at a loss.

With such cheap and delicious food, they didn’t believe customers wouldn’t come. But when they waited and waited, the line for the stir-fried noodles was still as long as ever.

The customers heard the shouting, turned around to look, but didn’t hesitate at all. They just kept standing in line to buy stir-fried noodles. The other stall owners in the busy area were stunned.

Had these people gone crazy? They’d rather wait in line for so long to buy stir-fried noodles than come try their delicious, cheap food!

“Hey, come try our grilled skewers! If you wait in line, you’ll be eating them in the dark, don’t you get hungry?”

The grilled skewers shop owner shouted to the customers at the back of the line.

“Well, if your skewers were even half as good as Lu Qin’s ‘Empress Chicken Wings,’ I’d be there already!”

“Unfortunately, they’re not. I’ve tried your skewers, they’re just full of seasoning flavor.”

“Have you ever tried the ‘Empress Chicken Wings’? The meat is tender and falls off the bone, with a hint of sweet lychee and a rich wine flavor. One bite and it’ll steal your soul. Now that is real food, do you get it?”

“How could he understand? Otherwise, he wouldn’t just be selling grilled skewers. Just be patient and wait, getting one bite of Lu Qin’s food will be worth waiting the whole day!”

The grilled skewers shop owner stood there dumbfounded, feeling like his heart couldn’t take it.

What’s wrong with grilled skewers?

Grilled skewers are also food!

Why don’t I understand food?

chapters.title: Chapter 90
chapters.content:
Chapter 90 “Are You Guys Playing a Word Chain Game Over There? Are You Trying to Drive Us Crazy?!”

The owner of the Beef Pancake Shop and the Skewer Shop owner exchanged helpless and bitter glances from a distance.

I want to cry, but no tears are coming!

Damn it!

They’ve slashed prices to the bone—and it’s useless!

The customers just refuse to come, while the line at the stir-fried noodles stall grew by another ten customers.

The drought’s killing the crops, and the flood’s drowning the fields. The situation at the other stalls was the same.

A few scattered customers were considered good, but just as a customer placed an order, they quickly said, “Sorry, boss, I’ll pass on that!”

Before they could finish their sentence, they’d already dashed over to stand in line at the stir-fried noodles stall.

Good grief!

The line stretched out with 70 or 80 people, and new customers kept coming!

What are they doing, playing a game of word chain?

Aren’t they worried about starving halfway through?

This scene had never been seen before in the bustling pedestrian street. Even in other commercial streets, such situations were rare.

Unless it was for a big new store opening with some ‘9.9 yuan for 100’ flashy deals that could attract huge crowds.

And of course, those businesses had to run a lot of ads on platforms like Douyin or Meituan. What was worse, many customers bought bags of stir-fried noodles to eat on the spot, unable to wait until they got home.

Soon, all the resting benches for tourists were filled, and people had to duck into other stores to eat.

What a heartless act!

And they couldn’t even chase the customers away.

Isn’t that infuriating?

“Boss, mind if I use your seat for a while? I’ll leave right after eating!”

“Uncle, can I sit here on your bench? Thanks!”

…

Two young men walked into the shop, found a seat, and immediately opened up their stir-fried noodles boxes. Instantly, the entire hot pot shop was filled with the strong smell of stir-fried noodles!

Well, it smells really good! But…

“Boss, can I get a bottle of water?”

The hot pot shop owner wanted to grit his teeth and kick them out, but in the end, the young men bought a bottle of cheap water for two yuan.

The hot pot owner swallowed the words he was about to say, thinking, These guys are so damn clever, they buy a bottle of water, sit on comfy chairs, eat, and enjoy the air conditioning. They’re taking advantage of me!

But the hot pot owner had no choice but to watch them eat. When the twice-cooked pork box was opened, the owner couldn’t help but feel envious. He swallowed his saliva involuntarily.

No, I can’t look anymore. If I keep staring, something bad will happen.

The hot pot owner immediately took out his phone and opened Douyin to distract himself. But the killer blow came when he saw a Douyin video of the stir-fried noodles stall, filmed by a netizen. The video showed close-ups of the twice-cooked pork and the Imperial Chicken Wings.

Damn it, I’m going crazy!

The two young men scarfed down their food, eating stir-fried noodles like they were in a battle. If you didn’t know any better, you’d think they hadn’t eaten in three days.

Once they were done.

“Boss, we didn’t touch the water, so we won’t take it.”

The two young men put their toothpicks in their mouths and walked out, leaving a table full of messy food containers.

Clatter!

The hot pot shop owner stood there, dumbfounded for a few seconds, and his phone fell to the floor, screen cracked.

“That’s too much!”

The hot pot owner sprang up, furious, and stormed out. Meanwhile, the owners of the other shops were just as disgusted!

“Let’s go find Xiao Gong! This time, we have to get rid of them!”

“Damn it! If this continues, I’ll be dead of anger before the store even closes!”

“If the stir-fried noodles stall doesn’t leave, we’ll all pack up and leave! Let’s see what Xiao Gong has to say!”

“I can’t leave! A few students came into my shop, ate the stir-fried noodles and then sat there drinking green bean soup like it was milk tea. Damn it, they’re probably gonna hang around forever! Some are even playing games! Help me, save me! Damn!”

“Li, you stay and watch the store, we’ll go…”

A group of shop owners hurried to the pedestrian street management office, but when they got there, the office door was locked. Peering through the gap in the door, the lights were off, and no one was inside.

Lao Niu quickly made a phone call and found out that Xiao Gong had already gone home.

“Damn it! Xiao Gong left early!”

“He must’ve escaped early! What a scoundrel!”

“What do we do now? Let that stir-fried noodles stall keep setting up? How can we show our faces after this?”

“Exactly! Lao Zhong, you sell cold noodles and stir-fried noodles, too. You’re his biggest competitor. Say something!”

Lao Zhong thought for a moment.

“As the saying goes, know your enemy and know yourself. I need to go try his stir-fried noodles first, then we’ll come up with a counter-strategy!”

“This is a good idea! Let’s do this—one of us buys a serving, tries it, and then just says, ugh , this is terrible.”

“Are you kidding me? There’s a line of people at his stall! The food must be good!”

“Good food, my foot! As the saying goes, ‘In the eyes of a thousand people, there are a thousand different Hamlets!'”

“That’s ‘In the eyes of a thousand readers, there are a thousand different Hamlets!’ You don’t know your literature!”

Having made up their minds, the group of shop owners stormed to the stir-fried noodles stall and lined up.

“Damn! So many people! How long are we going to wait?”

“This is too abstract! It’s endless! I can’t even see the stir-fried noodles stall anymore!”

“Bro, you bought three servings of stir-fried noodles? Sell me one for double the price!”

“I’ll just give it to you, no charge.”

“Are you serious? You’re not joking, are you?”

“I’m serious. Just now, some guy offered me 500 yuan, and I turned him down!”

“What? 500 yuan for one serving of stir-fried noodles? Are you crazy? You don’t want 500 yuan? Do you think money grows on trees?”

“You wouldn’t understand! Let’s go, I’m done talking to you!”

The other shop owners looked at each other. 500 yuan was nearly equivalent to the profit for a whole day of sales. There was nothing to do, so they just had to queue up honestly.

“Finally, it’s our turn!”

“This is tough! It’s like going to the West to get scriptures!”

“The stir-fried noodles stall owner is so young! He’s pretty handsome, about the same age as my daughter. Young man, are you single…”

“Damn! Did you come here for a blind date?”

“Five servings of stir-fried noodles, please!”

“Two servings of twice-cooked pork!”

“What’s chicken tofu flower? Doesn’t matter, three servings of that!”

…

The shop owners quickly placed their orders.

Lu Qin looked up at them, pointed to the small whiteboard, and said, “Sorry, customers, we have a limit on how much you can buy. Please don’t order too much.”

“Limit!”

“What’s going on here? I don’t get it!”

“Wow! There’s a limit, and you’re still in business? You think you’re too cool for us!”

The customers in the back of the line saw the shop owners lingering and urged.

“What are you doing? Hurry up! Can’t you see how many people are behind you?!”

“The limit is there so that everyone can get a chance to eat. You guys wouldn’t understand!”

“I hate these new customers who don’t know anything. Don’t they check the guides before they go out?”

The shop owners were a bit embarrassed by the remarks from the customers, but quickly placed their orders according to the limit.

After ordering, Lu Qin didn’t start cooking.

“Why are you just standing there? Hurry up and cook the noodles!”

“Why are you looking at your watch? Didn’t you hear our orders?”

“Give us the iced green bean soup first! We’ve been waiting so long, we’re dying of thirst!”

Lu Qin checked the time. It was already 5:10 pm. He apologetically said, “Sorry, everyone, but my daughter will be getting out of school soon. I need to pick her up, so it’ll take about 20 minutes. Please bear with me while I go to kindergarten.”

“…”

chapters.title: Chapter 91
chapters.content:
Chapter 91 “Grass, It’s a Kind of Plant, We Should Cherish It and Not Trample on It!”

A few of the snack stall owners were taken aback when they heard that Lu Qin was going to pick up his daughter from school. They thought it was ridiculous.

“Is this how young people do business now, so recklessly?” One of them muttered.

“Why didn’t he arrange his family matters in advance?” Another questioned.

“And the timing! Just as they finished their orders, he suddenly had to leave!”

They had just been whispering a bit when complaints started coming from the customers.

“Hey, hey, hey! Did you hear that? This young boss is leaving for such a long time to pick up his daughter. Is this normal or not? Hurry up, let’s drown him in spit!”

But to their surprise, none of the customers seemed to mind at all. They even offered to help Lu Qin watch the stall.

“Boss Lu, picking up your daughter is important, don’t be late! Go ahead, hurry!”

“Come on, pick up your little cutie, I miss her so much!”

“Hey, your own daughter is turning into a little madwoman, only looking at your daughter can heal my broken heart.”

“Boss Lu, hurry up, we’re not hungry, we can hold on!”

“Hurry, don’t let Yaoyao wait!”

“How are you going, Boss Lu? Are you taking a taxi? It’s hard to get a taxi here, let me take you!”

The few snack stall owners watched as one customer drove a ‘Don’t Touch Me’ X5 and took Lu Qin to pick up his daughter.

Wow!

“Can someone explain this? Why does a stall vendor get treated like this? Who can tell me?”

They always say that customers are gods, but when it comes to this young boss, it’s the other way around!

They couldn’t do much. The snack stall owners had no choice but to wait since there was no business at their own stalls. They were planning to eat fried noodles and teach this young man a lesson, to show him what it’s like to deal with other people’s turf.

Ugh!

What they really meant was to show him how treacherous people can be!

In less than twenty minutes, Lu Qin returned with his daughter, Lu Xiyao.

“Thank you, Uncle, for driving us!”

The little one was holding Lu Qin’s hand, bouncing along happily.

“No need to thank me, it was nothing!”

The little girl looked at the long line of customers and couldn’t help but exclaim.

“Wow! Daddy, so many people are waiting in line!”

“Daddy is so awesome, so amazing!”

The customers all smiled when they saw the little girl.

“Is this the cute one of Boss Lu? How was kindergarten today?”

“Here, Yaoyao, have an apple and grow tall!”

“Auntie has beef jerky, take a few extra packs!”

“Little cutie, come here, Uncle has some White Rabbit milk candies for you!”

The little girl’s eyes brightened up. She hadn’t had White Rabbit candy in a long time, as Lu Qin was strict with her to prevent cavities.

“Thank you, Uncle! You’re such a good person!”

The adult’s large hand gave her the candy, which she held with both hands.

“Haha, jackpot!”

The little girl’s eyes sparkled.

“Yaoyao, take just two pieces,” Lu Qin’s voice came from behind.

The little girl instantly became discouraged.

“Okay, Uncle, I’ll just take two pieces,” she said, handing the rest of the candy back to the uncle.

“Boss Lu, I don’t want the candy. You can have it,” the customer who had bought the candy said. They genuinely liked the little girl.

“Yeah, Daddy, take it, Uncle doesn’t want it!”

Everyone around laughed, and it was clear that the little girl was loved by all.

Lu Qin pinched her soft little face and stuffed the White Rabbit candies into his pocket. Afterward, as he was busy frying noodles, the little girl rummaged in her own pocket, but her dad just let her be.

The few snack stall owners, who had been standing or squatting at the front of the line, were close to doubting their life choices. When they saw Lu Qin again, they were ready to scold him.

“This is outrageous! Who goes off to do other things when they’re running a stall? Don’t you have any professional spirit?”

“Younger man, not to be rude, but how can you do this? You left all these customers waiting here. Look at the other stalls around here—they’re all focused and dedicated, unlike you, leaving the stall to go pick someone up!”

The few stall owners found a reason to criticize, and one by one, they started berating Lu Qin.

“Grass!”

Old Bull, one of the stall owners, was about to deliver a long string of complaints, but when he saw Lu Qin’s daughter, Lu Xiyao, he froze.

“How is it that such a beautiful little girl exists?! She looks like a little fairy!”

Old Bull’s words stopped abruptly.

“Grass… Ahem, grass is a plant, we should cherish it and not trample on it!”

The little girl, with a pitiful look, spoke up, “Uncles, I’m sorry for making you wait so long. Please don’t blame my daddy!”

“My daddy does everything by himself and runs the stall. It’s really hard work!”

“I don’t want my daddy to be so tired, so sometimes I try to tell him not to run the stall anymore, but he won’t listen. He says the family needs money, so he has to come out to make a living!”

The little girl spoke with such a sad, almost crying tone that the stall owners were left dumbfounded. Before they could say anything, other customers suddenly became upset.

“What’s wrong with you guys? Just because he’s picking up his daughter? It’s only been twenty minutes, and you can’t wait?”

“Boss Lu is raising his child on his own and trying to make a living. What’s wrong with that? Don’t you have any compassion?”

“Running a stall is a free job, you can do it when you want. Boss Lu thought about us, and after picking up his daughter, he came right back to continue working. He could have just closed his stall and left! Such a good guy, and you’re going to say this about him!”

“How can you say that about such a cute and well-behaved little girl? How could you be so heartless?”

The stall owners felt their backs getting cold and their hearts weighed down with guilt. They hadn’t even said anything about the child! They felt a bit ashamed.

“Sorry for making you wait.”

“You ordered three servings of fried noodles, right? And one serving of twice-cooked pork for that uncle?”

Lu Qin fired up the wok and began frying noodles. The stall owners exchanged glances, and they couldn’t help but acknowledge that this young man really had a sharp memory. He even remembered what they had ordered earlier.

With a hot wok and cold oil, he cracked an egg into the pan. Lu Qin’s technique was smooth, and anyone with experience could tell he was a pro.

The stall owners couldn’t help but look at Master Zhong, who ran another stall selling fried noodles. They were comparing. Master Zhong furrowed his brows and felt a sense of pressure building up inside him.

In just two minutes, Lu Qin’s stirring was perfect—208 flips. The high heat and fast flipping were necessary to prevent the noodles from sticking and to achieve that special crispy flavor.

A master!

No, an absolute top-tier master!

Master Zhong broke into a sweat on his forehead. The rich aroma from the wok wafted through the air.

The few stall owners couldn’t help but swallow. It smelled so good! They couldn’t resist.

Lu Qin packed the fried noodles.

“Don’t bother packing. We’ll taste it first.”

Lu Qin handed them the fried noodles, then continued frying the twice-cooked pork.

All three servings of fried noodles were opened, and they shared them with chopsticks.

“Master Zhong, look, this fried noodle breaks apart easily with chopsticks, not greasy, not dry, much better than your fried noodles!”

“Exactly! Master Zhong, I’ve eaten your fried noodles before. They’re all clumped together and not evenly fried. This one’s much smoother! Try it!”

Without comparison, there’s no harm.

Master Zhong silently took the hit.

The truth was too harsh—this young man’s skills were way better than his!

chapters.title: Chapter 92
chapters.content:
Chapter 92 Is It Too Much to Say Something Like That? Will I Be Struck by Lightning?

Several of the bosses were in the food industry, so they could easily analyze whether a stir-fried noodle dish was good or not. After comparing it with Master Zhong’s, they found that Lu Qin’s stir-fried noodles were indeed far superior.

Once they tasted it, each of them was amazed. Master Zhong also tasted it. The fresh, crisp, and charred flavor was something he had never experienced before.

Master Zhong noticed that the soy sauce used in the stir-fried noodles wasn’t ordinary soy sauce but a specially made one, which elevated the flavor to another level. The difference was huge. It was hard to surpass.

Master Zhong, feeling dejected, lowered his head and continued eating the noodles. As he ate, he realized the noodles were actually quite comforting. The other bosses were also praising the dish with exclamations of how delicious it was. Suddenly, one of them had an epiphany.

Wait, we decided earlier that even if it tastes good, we’d say it tastes bad, to teach this young boss a lesson, to let him know the cruelty of human nature… No, we should teach him a lesson!

What’s going on here?

It seems like the ones getting taught a lesson are us every time.

“What should we do? Should we say these noodles taste bad?”
