“Just say the soy sauce is too much, that it’s greasy and cloying, that it’s hard to swallow, completely disgusting!”

“This feels wrong, I can’t say something like that!”

“Will we get struck by lightning?”

The few bosses muttered in low voices.

“Here you go, your twice-cooked pork is ready.”

“Twice-cooked pork!”

“Damn! Forget it! Let’s check the saltiness first!”

“The stir-fried noodles were indeed perfect, no flaws at all. Alright, let’s try the twice-cooked pork, if there’s anything wrong with it, I’ll criticize it to death!”

“I’m a fan of twice-cooked pork, I’m going to be super picky!”

Each of them took a slice of the twice-cooked pork and put it in their mouth.

In an instant, their taste buds exploded. Rich and fragrant, with a touch of spiciness and sweetness, it was fatty but not greasy.

“This… this twice-cooked pork is nothing like the usual ones! It’s so rich, it’s perfect! It’s so good it makes me want to call the police!”

“Oh my god! The taste… salty and flavorful, not dry, not greasy. The twice-cooked pork I had at Jing Shu Ge for 128 yuan per serving wasn’t even half as good as this!”

“Too bad there’s no rice, or I’d eat two plates of this pork with three big bowls of rice!”

The bosses of the small eateries were all praising the food while also feeling impressed by how delicious it was. Just earlier, they wanted to teach Lu Qin a lesson about the cruelty of human nature, but now they were thinking, Why does he make everything so delicious? Is he trying to bankrupt us?

Next came the chicken bean curd, princess chicken wings, and crystal shrimp dumplings, which left the bosses even more in awe. Every dish was incredibly delicious, at the top level.

After the meal, they gave up their shops and stalls and queued up for a second round, even joining Lu Qin’s WeChat group. They had previously been hoping that Old Liu would come back and reclaim the stall.

Now, they were thinking, Old Liu, why don’t you have a second child?

“Yao Yao, are you hungry?”

Lu Qin turned to ask. Lu Xiyao was sitting on a small stool, acting like a mascot.

It was a bit hot, so Lu Qin turned on the electric fan he had won in the system’s lottery, blowing it towards himself and the little one. The breeze felt comfortable, just like natural wind.

Having already eaten two bags of beef and one bag of hawthorn candy, Lu Xiyao looked up with big eyes and said, “I’m hungry.”

“How do you manage to say such nonsense with your eyes wide open?”

Lu Qin asked and shook his head with a smile.

The little one had eaten five crystal shrimp dumplings, then an apple and a banana, followed by some milk candy. After that, she had eaten various snacks. Her belly was already full, how could she still be hungry?

“Heehee~!”

The little one stuck out her tongue. She had wanted to eat a few pieces of twice-cooked pork, but her mouth wanted it, but her stomach refused. There was no way.

At 7:30 PM, Lu Qin packed up his stall. Since he had combined the afternoon and evening sessions, he had prepared enough ingredients for 300 servings as a base. However, many customers still didn’t get food.

This area was a bustling pedestrian street with a large flow of people, and the number of people in the queue was 50% higher than usual. His income was about 37,000 yuan, and system points were 2824.

Lu Qin was just about to take the little one home, give her a bath, and put her to bed, since she had to go to kindergarten tomorrow. But then he remembered tomorrow was Friday, and the kindergarten was hosting a parent-child activity. This reminded him of Shen Muyan.

He still had to remind her. She was always so busy that she might forget. Lu Qin took out his phone and opened WeChat.

Li Laoshi’s avatar had a red dot, indicating a group message.

The message had been sent yesterday evening, and Lu Qin had forgotten to check it.

Parent-child outfits?

Lu Qin opened Shen Muyan’s WeChat and typed.

Shen Muyan replied quickly, probably because she was on her phone.

…

Lu Qin rode his three-wheeled cart back home with the little one. After they washed their faces, Shen Muyan arrived and parked her bright red Porsche Macan in front of the house.

Shen Muyan was wearing a red tight-fitting dress that accentuated her slim waist. The hem of the dress swayed as she moved, revealing her long, fair legs. She wore a pair of red-bottomed high heels that highlighted the elegant curve of her calves. Her hair was tied up in a high ponytail, and her face, though without makeup, was as smooth as snow and incredibly beautiful.

Lu Qin was momentarily stunned, while Lu Xiyao, upon seeing Shen Muyan, ran over excitedly.

“Mom~! You look so pretty today~!”

“Wasn’t I pretty before?” Shen Muyan joked.

“Of course you were pretty~!”

The little one held Shen Muyan’s hand, and Shen Muyan promised to attend the parent-child activity tomorrow, which made the little one very happy.

Usually, at these activities, other kids had both parents, but for Lu Xiyao, only Lu Qin would attend. Tomorrow, they would finally be a family of three.

“You drive, I can’t drive in these high heels.”

Shen Muyan handed Lu Qin the car keys.

Lu Qin took the keys and glanced at Shen Muyan’s feet.

“How did you get here?”

“Without shoes.”

Shen Muyan’s calm voice said, then she led Lu Xiyao to the back seat.

Lu Qin paused for a moment, then sat in the driver’s seat.

If she knew she had to drive, why wear high heels?

The car drove to the underground parking garage of a shopping mall. The mall closed at 10 PM, so there was plenty of time to buy a set of parent-child outfits. The three of them took the elevator to the first floor. Their exceptionally high looks attracted quite a few stares.

“Mom, do you want to play with this 3D illusion space?”

The little one pointed to a few spaceship cabins, excitedly eager to try. The so-called 3D illusion space was just a VR experience setup, with a VR headset and a seat that shook.

Almost every mall has this type of area, with similar games like VR roller coasters or VR skiing. People who get motion sickness, fear heights, or are prone to dizziness couldn’t handle it, like Shen Muyan.

“Yao Yao, are you doing this on purpose?”

Lu Qin rubbed the little one’s face, giving her a slight punishment. He had told Lu Xiyao that Shen Muyan was afraid of heights and couldn’t play games like this. The little one had a good memory and wouldn’t forget.

So, it seemed the little one was doing this on purpose, just to see Shen Muyan’s awkward expression.

But…

It didn’t seem to work. Shen Muyan’s face was flawless, with no expression except for beauty.

chapters.title: Chapter 93
chapters.content:
Chapter 93 the Three of Them Stepping Out Like This Would Surely Attract Everyone’s Attention!

“Yao Yao, let’s go buy the matching family outfits,” Lu Qin said, pulling his little one away from the VR experience zone, which she had been reluctant to leave. While the 3D rollercoaster was exciting, buying the family outfits was important too.

They already had two sets of matching family outfits at home, ones that Lu Qin had bought. However, Yao Yao had always been dissatisfied with his clothing choices, thinking his fashion sense was quite average. When it came to picking clothes, Lu Qin usually stuck to two colors: white and blue. Yao Yao often chose her own clothes instead.

Lu Qin would say that as long as the clothes were comfortable, style didn’t matter much. To him, it was more about being good-looking, and he believed the clothes didn’t matter as much as how one carried them. Every time he said that, Yao Yao would roll her eyes, thinking her dad’s taste was hopeless, and that she would have to handle things on her own.

“What floor should we go to?” Asked Shen Muyan, not familiar with the mall.

Lu Qin, however, was very familiar with these things, so he responded, “Second floor for women’s, third floor for kids’ clothes, fourth floor for men’s and dining. We’ll head to the third floor.”

They didn’t take the elevator but instead rode the escalator. Yao Yao held Lu Qin’s left hand and Shen Muyan’s right. The two hands felt different: Lu Qin’s hand was warm and large, while Shen Muyan’s was delicate, smooth, and slightly cool. Yao Yao was very happy, bouncing along with each hand to hold onto.

They arrived at a clothing store on the third floor, where the family-themed outfit section was clearly visible through the glass. The mannequins displayed matching family outfits, and they were immediately caught by the attention of the store attendants.

The staff quickly greeted them warmly.

“How can we assist you today?”

“Sister, we want the matching family outfits!” Yao Yao said, blinking her large eyes and speaking in a soft, sweet voice. The staff were instantly charmed by her cuteness.

The staff immediately led them to the family outfit section and offered them three glasses of lemonade. Since the family had such high visual appeal, anything they wore would look great on them. They just needed to pick what they liked. Lu Qin was the type who was indifferent to style, always saying ‘anything works’ when it came to clothes. He never really cared about colors or designs, just the fit, quality, and price. Style was not his priority.

From entering the store to checking out, they spent no more than ten minutes. Shen Muyan and Yao Yao ignored Lu Qin and chose the clothes together, leaving him in the lounge area. Lu Qin was more than happy to let them handle it. He sat on the couch, sipping lemonade. If they asked him for his opinion, he’d just say, “This one looks good,” or “That one is nice,” with no real helpful insights.

Yao Yao picked out a cute cotton T-shirt with a little bear hat design and shorts. The material was soft and comfortable, with a good fit and a cute, unique style that could look both sweet and cool. Shen Muyan picked out a men’s set and handed it to Lu Qin, and the three of them went into the fitting rooms to try them on.

Lu Qin and Yao Yao had black and yellow outfits, while Shen Muyan wore a beige and yellow combination. The three of them came out and stood side by side in front of the mirror. A refreshing, innocent vibe radiated from them. Passersby who noticed them stopped to stare, marveling at how good-looking they all were.

The store staff couldn’t help but gush over them, their praise genuine. They even thought if they could, they’d replace the celebrity posters with photos of this family. Every set of outfits looked great on them.

The bear-themed outfit Yao Yao chose was so cute that she immediately bought it. Shen Muyan selected two more sets—one with a white suspender dress featuring a cartoon little cow wearing sunglasses, and another in a beachy style, a chiffon dress with blue and green butterflies, perfect for a trip.

After trying on the other two outfits, the mother and daughter stood in front of the mirror, looking ethereal, as if untouched by the world. They were so beautiful, it was like something out of a dream.

Lu Qin set his lemonade down and snapped a few pictures, but his photography skills weren’t great—he just relied on his phone’s camera settings.

“Daddy, can I wear this outfit now?” Yao Yao asked eagerly, not wanting to take off the outfits she loved so much.

Lu Qin shook his head.

“We have to wash them first, and after they dry, you can wear them.”

“Then let’s take some pictures together!” Yao Yao suggested.

“Sure,” Lu Qin agreed, smiling.

Yao Yao grabbed Shen Muyan’s phone and handed it to one of the store attendants.

“Sister, could you please take a few pictures for us?”

The staff were thrilled to help. After unlocking the phone, the attendant took several shots from different angles. Yao Yao kept posing and changing expressions, sometimes pouting, other times doing the peace sign, each movement full of innocence and cuteness.

Shen Muyan just stood there, exuding an effortless elegance. Lu Qin, though handsome, just did the peace sign or a thumbs-up, and as usual, he became the background.

After the photos were taken, Lu Qin was about to go to the counter to pay, but Shen Muyan had already gone ahead. The total for the three outfits came to over four thousand, which was expensive for most people, but for Shen Muyan, who never looked at price tags when shopping, this was a typical display of wealth.

She paid through the QR code, and Lu Qin waited for the staff to pack the clothes while Shen Muyan went to find Yao Yao. The little one was absorbed in looking at the pictures on Shen Muyan’s phone. Suddenly, Shen Muyan seemed to recall something, and her expression subtly changed. She walked over quickly, but still, it was a moment too late.

Yao Yao was swiping through the photos, and after the ones from the fitting room, the next ones were of Lu Qin and Yao Yao together at the night market stall. There were pictures of Lu Qin focused on cooking fried rice, with Yao Yao helping to pack the food. Then there was one of Yao Yao wiping Lu Qin’s sweat off, the scene full of warmth and happiness.

Shen Muyan reached over and took the phone back, pressing the screen off, and quietly put it in her pocket. Yao Yao raised her head, a playful expression on her face as if to say, “Oh, I know what’s going on.”

Shen Muyan glanced at her, her expression asking, “What do you know?”

At that moment, Lu Qin arrived with the large shopping bags.

“Let’s go,” he said.

“Daddy, let me help you with one of those bags!” Yao Yao offered, not wanting him to get tired.

“No need, Yao Yao, these aren’t heavy,” Lu Qin replied.

Although the clothes weren’t heavy, the bags were large, and Yao Yao would likely drag them on the ground. They left the store and took the escalator down, the three of them walking out together, a perfect family.

chapters.title: Chapter 94
chapters.content:
Chapter 94 Your Little Cutie Can Only Help You up to Here!

“Daddy, I want to play on the ocean ball slide~!”

Lu Xiyao pointed at the naughty castle. Lu Qin and Shen Muyan took the little one to the first floor. They bought matching parent-child outfits, and since it was still early, the little one didn’t want to go back just yet.

Shen Muyan glanced at Lu Xiyao. The last time they came, she had said that the ocean ball area was for three-year-olds and wanted to go on the pirate ship.

But today, she wanted to play again?

Children’s moods change so quickly , Shen Muyan didn’t understand.

“I’ll go play with Yaoyao,” she said.

The naughty castle allowed one adult to accompany, so Lu Qin went to buy the tickets.

“No need, Daddy, you chat with Mommy, I’ll go play with the other kids~!”

Lu Qin bought the tickets, and the little one dove straight into the ocean ball pit. A child who had just struggled out of the ocean balls was thrown back in by Lu Xiyao’s playful dive.

On the bench outside the naughty castle, Lu Qin and Shen Muyan sat next to each other. The little one poked her head out of the ocean balls and, seeing Lu Qin and Shen Muyan sitting together, said casually, “Your little cutie can only help you up to here, good luck~!”

Then she went to play on the slide. The naughty castle wasn’t as fun as the VR phantom space they passed by earlier. The phantom space featured a cute giant spider that suddenly emerged from the dark cavern, scaring the little one each time. It was thrilling and intense.

Shen Muyan’s coldly beautiful face caught the attention of many. A young man walked straight into a trash can, and a middle-aged man froze in place until his wife pulled his ear. Some brave young men wanted to approach her for her WeChat, but Shen Muyan looked so unapproachable that they hesitated.

And they also saw Lu Qin, who was so handsome he could make heads turn, so they decided to give up. Likewise, Lu Qin was very popular. Young women passed by, turning their heads at every step, almost poking their nose with their bubble tea straws.

Even customers at a stir-fried noodle stand, who were casually shopping at the mall, walked a few steps ahead, then returned to confirm it was Lu Qin. They greeted him warmly.

“Mr. Lu, what a coincidence, are you taking your little cutie to the mall? This must be your wife, she’s even more beautiful than a celebrity! No wonder your daughter looks so lovely!”

Long-time customers knew that Lu Qin was a single dad, but new customers didn’t, although even if they did know, they wouldn’t have said “This is your ex-wife, Mr. Lu, she’s gorgeous!” in public.

Lu Qin simply greeted them, and Shen Muyan remained silent, still exuding her cold aura.

“Mr. Lu, where are you setting up your stall tomorrow?”

“Tomorrow’s parent-child activity at the kindergarten, I won’t be setting up my stall in the afternoon.”

“Ah? What a pity, I can’t have your twice-cooked pork and imperial chicken wings!”

But the customer understood. Attending the child’s parent-child activity was more important. After all, when the child grew up, there would be fewer opportunities for that.

When the customer left, Shen Muyan’s stomach growled loudly at the mention of twice-cooked pork and imperial chicken wings.

Lu Qin looked up and saw her face slightly flush.

“Are you hungry?”

Lu Qin suddenly asked.

“I didn’t have dinner.”

Shen Muyan’s voice was soft, and there was a rare hint of grievance. Lu Qin frowned slightly, wanting to say something about her stomach, but he held back.

“The restaurant on the fourth floor is still open. Do you want to go eat something?”

“I want twice-cooked pork and imperial chicken wings.”

After their meal, they drove back to the rented house in the old neighborhood.

“Mom, please come in~!”

“Mom, change your shoes~!”

Lu Xiyao was particularly considerate, as she was very happy. Shen Muyan not only accompanied her to the mall, but also came to their house for a meal. Tomorrow, they could go to the parent-child activity together.

Life was so beautiful!

Although Lu Qin was already in her life, she was even happier with Shen Muyan around. After all, fatherly and motherly love felt different. At home, there were no spare ladies’ slippers, so Shen Muyan wore Lu Qin’s, which were a bit too large.

“You two sit and rest for a bit, I’ll go make the food.”

“Daddy, I’ll help you wash the vegetables~!”

The little one instinctively wanted to help. She was now the family’s vegetable-washing expert.

“No need, just stay with Mommy.”

The little one looked at Shen Muyan, nodded, and decided to stay with her. Lu Qin went to the kitchen, and the little one poured a glass of water for Shen Muyan.

“Mom, Daddy said you didn’t have dinner. If you don’t eat on time, your stomach will hurt. You should eat on time next time~!”

“Okay, Mommy knows.”

Shen Muyan reached out and pulled the little one into her arms, gently kissing her soft cheek, feeling a touch of warmth.

In the past, Shen Muyan had many such opportunities, but for some reason, work was always so busy that she didn’t have time to accompany Lu Xiyao.

Maybe it was because she didn’t know how to cherish it before she lost it. Now, despite still being busy, she could find time for it.

Indeed, time was like water in a sponge. If you squeezed it, you could always find some.

Shen Muyan realized that before, it wasn’t that she didn’t have time—it was just that she didn’t want to squeeze it. She had made a serious mistake.

Lu Xiyao turned on the TV, and a cartoon called Piggy Hero was playing on the children’s channel.

“Mom, can you turn off the teen mode on the TV~?”

“Why turn it off?” Shen Muyan asked.

“I want to watch Billionaire’s Love Affair, the finale hasn’t aired yet~!”

The little one whispered. She had misunderstood that Shen Muyan was easiergoing, unlike Lu Qin, who had been strict with her.

Thinking Shen Muyan would be more lenient, she asked for the change.

Shen Muyan gave her a light tap on the head.

“Wah~! Mommy, I don’t want to watch it anymore. I’ll just watch Piggy Hero~!”

The little one held her head, feeling that life was so hard.

Lu Qin entered with a plate of spicy potatoes and peppers and saw Lu Xiyao holding her head.

He asked, “Yaoyao, were you being naughty?”

Lu Xiyao sat obediently on the sofa, shaking her head.

“No, Daddy, I’m very good~!”

Shen Muyan immediately reported.

“She wanted to watch Billionaire’s Love Affair and asked me to turn off the teen mode.”

“I’ll give her another tap on the head.”

“Okay, no problem.”

Shen Muyan looked at the little one.

The little one immediately ran off five meters away, muttering under her breath, “As expected, you two are a couple, ganging up to bully me, the little one~!”

Three dishes were served on the table, along with a bowl of tomato and egg soup. The little one sniffed the delicious aroma and started eating immediately, hungry and greedy.

“It smells so good, Daddy~!”

Too eager, the little one reached for a piece of secret-recipe spareribs.

Slap!

Slap!

Lu Qin and Shen Muyan both slapped her hand away at the same time.

“Wah~!”

“That hurts~! One slap would have been enough~!”

“I’ll go wash my hands~!”

The little one, feeling wronged, went to wash her hands. Now she realized that having Mommy around didn’t always mean happiness—sometimes it meant double the punishment.

When she came back from washing her hands, she sat down at the table, and just as she was about to pick up her bowl, two pairs of chopsticks came over, both placing a piece of secret-recipe spareribs in front of her.

The little one suddenly felt her hands didn’t hurt anymore. Having Mommy around was still great, especially with double the spareribs—what a treat!

chapters.title: Chapter 95
chapters.content:
Chapter 95 “Mom, doesn’t the meat Dad picks taste better?”

At the dinner table, there were three dishes and a soup, all deliciously prepared and accompanied by fragrant rice. The three of them didn’t say much and just focused on eating.

Although Lu Qin wasn’t very hungry, he sipped on a bowl of tomato egg soup. Even though it was a simple, homemade dish, Lu Qin didn’t make it casually. With his systematic cooking skills, the soup turned out to be rich, aromatic, and perfectly balanced in taste—better than any high-end restaurant’s.

Lu Qin didn’t eat much, occasionally serving food to the little one. Shen Muyan, the goddess-level CEO, usually ate slowly, but when it came to Lu Qin’s cooking, she loosened up, her sensual lips occasionally touching the sauce.

Lu Qin picked up a piece of garlic duck for Shen Muyan, and she thanked him.

Lu Xiyao raised her little face from her bowl, blinking her big eyes, and asked Shen Muyan, “Mom, doesn’t the meat Dad picks taste better?”

Shen Muyan pressed her lips together and didn’t reply.

Lu Qin pressed his little one’s face back into her bowl, saying, “No talking while eating.”

Lu Xiyao continued to show off her ribs. The ribs were made with a secret sauce—marinated with seafood sauce, sesame paste, and star anise powder. The taste was sweet and savory, with a melt-in-your-mouth texture that teased the taste buds.

Actually, Lu Xiyao wasn’t really hungry, having eaten a lot of snacks earlier. But she wasn’t just eating because she was hungry. She ate because she wanted to fill her little stomach as much as she could. And it only counted when it was Lu Qin’s cooking.

At 9:25 PM, Lu Qin and Lu Xiyao finished their late-night snack, while Shen Muyan had finished her dinner. The plates were empty—another clean plate action. Lu Qin washed the dishes, which saved him some effort.

“I’ll do the dishes,” Shen Muyan said, feeling it was improper to eat without helping.

“I’ll do it. You’ve already spent money today,” Lu Qin said, stacking the plates.

Shen Muyan paused for a moment, realizing she had paid for the three sets of parent-child outfits—over four thousand yuan. Though she didn’t mind paying, she couldn’t help feeling a little unbalanced, especially since Lu Qin made more than her now by running a stall.

Why should she be the one to pay for the clothes?

Shen Muyan walked to the living room, long legs gracefully swaying in her red tight dress.

“Mom~! Look at this apple! It’s so big and round, it must be very sweet! Do you want to eat it~?”

Shen Muyan glanced at Lu Xiyao and pinched her smooth little face.

“If you want me to peel it, just ask.”

“Heehee~! Let’s share it,” Lu Xiyao said.

Shen Muyan took the apple and began peeling it, her delicate fingers gently twisting the knife with elegance. The peel came off in one long strip, unbroken.

“Wow~! Mom, you’re amazing! From now on, you’ll always peel the apples!” Lu Xiyao exclaimed.

“….”

After eating the apples, Lu Qin finished washing the dishes and walked out of the kitchen. It seemed like it was time to leave.

“Well, I should head back now. Yaoyao, sleep early. See you at kindergarten tomorrow,” Shen Muyan said, standing up.

“The parent-child outfits will still be good in about 20 minutes. It takes more than an hour to dry them,” Lu Qin glanced at the washing machine, which was turning with a yellow light on.

Shen Muyan thought for a moment. She could have Lu Qin take hers to kindergarten tomorrow and change there. Just as she was about to speak, she heard Lu Xiyao’s soft voice from below.

“Mom~! Can you sleep with me tonight? We haven’t slept together in a long time~!”

The little one pulled on Shen Muyan’s hand, gently shaking it, her big eyes full of expectation and longing.

Both Shen Muyan and Lu Qin were a bit surprised. Before the divorce, Shen Muyan and the little one rarely slept together, and even less so now.

Seeing the little one’s pleading expression, Shen Muyan hesitated. But they were divorced. Spending the night at her ex-husband’s house…

Shen Muyan looked up at Lu Qin.

“Stay, the little one really wants you here,” Lu Qin said, his gaze deep as he looked at Shen Muyan.

Shen Muyan nodded.

“Alright.”

“Yay~! Awesome~!”

Lu Xiyao jumped up excitedly.

To be honest, Shen Muyan was tired and wanted to go to sleep early.

“I didn’t bring my pajamas,” she said.

“Mom~! Wear mine! I have several sets of pajamas~!”

“…”

“It’s too small, it won’t fit.”

“Then wear Daddy’s!”

Lu Qin took out a new bath towel, a white Mickey Mouse T-shirt, and a pair of gray shorts.

“They’re all new, never worn,” Lu Qin said and handed them to Lu Xiyao.

Lu Xiyao eagerly said, “Dad, Mom~! Let’s all take a bath together and then go to bed~!”

…

In the bathroom, Lu Xiyao was holding a children’s toothbrush in one hand, her other hand on her forehead, tears in her eyes as she whimpered, “Ouch~! It hurts!”

Shen Muyan looked down and saw the new toothbrush and cup with toothpaste already applied, ready for use.

At that moment, Shen Muyan felt surrounded by a warm sense of love, sweetness, and happiness. Though the old rental house in the old district was small and outdated, it somehow felt more comfortable than the cold, grand villa.

Shen Muyan used Lu Xiyao’s lemon-scented shower gel. It smelled pleasant. After washing up, Shen Muyan came out of the bathroom wearing the oversized Mickey Mouse T-shirt and shorts, looking less cold and more playful and cute.

Lu Xiyao had already run back to her room and excitedly jumped onto the bed. Lu Qin also took a quick shower and came out of the master bathroom, just in time to cross paths with Shen Muyan.

His black, damp hair was styled casually, and he wore a light gray V-neck sleep shirt, which, combined with his physique—strong, with well-proportioned muscles—made Shen Muyan pause and glance at him. His muscular physique and the elegant lines of his neck made her cheeks flush slightly.

Lu Qin, in his turn, noticed Shen Muyan. Her body, still wearing his oversized T-shirt, made her look even more stunning with her long, white legs and perfect figure. The T-shirt barely covered her curves, and her legs looked like they were crafted by the gods themselves.

The room was quiet. The atmosphere was a little awkward. Even before the divorce, Shen Muyan and Lu Qin had been polite with each other, and such intimate moments were rare.

Now, after the divorce, they both noticed the other in ways they hadn’t before…

They stood in silence for a few seconds.

“Get some rest. Good night,” Lu Qin said, clearing his throat.

“Good night,” Shen Muyan replied, entering Lu Xiyao’s room. The room was pink and cartoon-themed, neat and clean.

On the corner glass shelf were Lu Xiyao’s favorite plush toys, including the Pikachu and Squirtle Lu Qin had gotten last time.

Shen Muyan picked up her phone, opened her chat with her best friend Fang Ya, and typed a message.

She typed the message but didn’t send it, instead holding down the delete key.

“Mom~! Come sleep with me~!” Lu Xiyao called from under the covers, her little head poking out, yawning.

“Alright.”

chapters.title: Chapter 96
chapters.content:
Chapter 96 Antu Sheng’s Fairy Tale! Osmanthus Oolong Tea!

Shen Muyan climbed into bed, holding the little one close to her.

“Mommy~! You smell so nice~! It’s so comfy snuggling with you~!”

The little one burrowed into Shen Muyan’s embrace, her soft, bouncy body pressing against her.

Shen Muyan gently stroked the little one’s smooth cheeks, leaned against the headboard, and softly patted her tiny body.

“Mommy~! Tell me a bedtime story~!”

“I don’t know any.”

“…”

Lu Xiyao rolled her little eyes.

“How about your little cutie tells you a story~?”

“Alright.”

Shen Muyan smiled.

“Once upon a time, there was a little girl who sold missiles. She couldn’t sell a single one during the day. At night, she was so cold she thought of her grandma. So, she lit a missile. That night, the entire village saw her grandma~!”

“How was it~? Mommy, did you like my story~?”

Shen Muyan’s smile froze slightly.

“What do you think? Antu Sheng’s fairy tale?”

Lu Xiyao tilted her head.

“If Mommy didn’t like the story, I can sing a song instead.”

“Go ahead.”

Lu Xiyao cleared her throat.

“Little white rabbit, white and soft,

Lift its ears up high.

Slit its artery, slit its vein,

Now it’s still, oh so cute.

Peel its skin, chop it apart,

Toss it in a wok to fry.

Add some water, close the lid,

Before it’s done, add cilantro.

Grab a bowl, fetch some chopsticks,

Open your mouth, take a bite.

Not too salty, not too bland,

Perfect snack with wine tonight.”

Shen Muyan was speechless.

“Mommy, now I’m hungry~!” Lu Xiyao said.

“Who taught you this?”

“Yuan Jiajie, the boy who sits behind me in kindergarten~!”

“Don’t play with him too much in the future.”

“Oh, but he sticks to me like glue~!”

“Go to sleep.”

“ Zzz !”

Shen Muyan marveled at the little one’s ability to fall asleep instantly. The room was air-conditioned, and though the humidifier was on, Shen Muyan still felt a bit parched and wanted a drink of water.

Suddenly, she noticed a glass of lemon water on the nightstand, covered neatly with a tissue.

On the little one’s nightstand was her cartoon water cup, filled with hot water.

Shen Muyan stared at the blue moon-shaped ceiling light for a moment, her expression complicated. Then, she picked up her phone, edited an unsent message, and pressed send.

Late-night gamer Fang Ya was in the middle of a heated battle in Swordsman World, her guild engaged in a fierce war. The screen was filled with dazzling skills, flashes of light, and chaotic combat.

Her phone buzzed once beside her mousepad.

“Use Flying Stone to break that gap! Focus fire on that bastard vice guild leader—he killed me earlier!”

Fang Ya barked into her headset, glancing at her phone. After a quick look, she put it down to continue the fight.

“Swap positions! Don’t just stand there, you idiot—”

Her voice suddenly stopped. She froze for a moment, then excitedly tossed her mouse and keyboard aside, yanked off her headset, and grabbed her phone.

What did Shen Muyan send?

Fang Ya’s eyes widened with excitement. She hurriedly typed back.

For Fang Ya, their remarriage would be pure bliss—she could visit her goddaughter regularly and enjoy home-cooked meals. Life would take a turn for the better!

“Lady Murong, what are you doing? You’ve disconnected! That vice guild leader killed you again!”

The headset was still on the desk, broadcasting the guild members’ chatter.

“Guys, something came up. I’ll avenge myself next time!”

Fang Ya forcefully powered off her PC and continued chatting on WeChat.

Fang Ya’s typing grew increasingly outrageous. Shen Muyan blushed furiously when she reached the ‘get busy’ part and spat at her phone.

Shen Muyan’s cheeks flushed as she put her phone on silent, cuddled the little one, and went to sleep.

Fang Ya didn’t push further, instead daydreaming about a future where she could openly freeload delicious meals.

______

At the Master Bedroom, Lu Qin hadn’t slept yet. He was in a great mood, perhaps because the little one had her mother with her tonight, making her happy and content. As a result, Lu Qin felt satisfied too.

Thanks to the system’s fatigue-removal perk, Lu Qin’s body clock was fixed at sleeping from midnight to 4:30 a.m. Though short, his deep sleep left him refreshed and energized. He picked up his phone and browsed through the photos taken earlier that day while trying on matching family outfits at the mall.

Shen Muyan, usually the cool and aloof businesswoman, exuded a gentle maternal glow when with her daughter. Shen Muyan looked stunningly beautiful, while Lu Xiyao was mischievous and adorable. The family outfits looked perfect on them.

The photos captured a beautiful vibe. Even though Lu Qin’s photography skills were average, the mother-daughter pair looked amazing in every shot.

After staring at his phone for ten minutes, Lu Qin set it down and opened the system interface.

System points: 2824

Lu Qin initiated 28 lottery draws.

This time, his luck wasn’t great—he ended up with over a thousand mints and five ‘Sunny Day Cards’.

These Sunny Day Cards couldn’t be removed from the system space or manually activated, they were grayed out.

System explanation—[When weather conditions like rain, wind, or hail disrupt outdoor vending, the system will automatically use a Sunny Day Card to restore favorable weather.]

Lu Qin counted his Sunny Day Cards. Two seemed to be missing. No wonder weather forecasts sometimes predicted rain, but it was sunny and pleasant when he went out to sell goods.

Although the draw yielded no new culinary skills, Lu Qin did unlock a max-level skill for a traditional Chinese drink—Osmanthus Oolong Tea.

chapters.title: Chapter 97
chapters.content:
Chapter 97 Reliable Good Man!

In the morning, the next day, at 4:30 a.m. sharp, Lu Qin woke up, went to the bathroom to wash up, and headed to the yard to stretch his muscles.

Today, the family of three had a parent-child activity to attend at the kindergarten. Based on past experiences, these events would take up most of the day.

So yesterday, Lu Qin informed his group chat that he wouldn’t be setting up his street food stall during the day. The chat was instantly filled with complaints.

He also canceled the delivery service for Boss Zhao, knowing no one would be home at noon. He planned to consider setting up his late-night snack stall, depending on how the evening unfolded. Meanwhile, he decided to buy ingredients early in the morning.

Pushing his tricycle food cart, Lu Qin made his way to Jingyuan Market. The three-kilometer trip took twenty-six minutes due to a major road under repair. Half the road was full of potholes, reducing the driving lanes and causing more traffic than usual.

At the market, Lu Qin bought all the necessary ingredients. Pork prices had gone up by fifty cents, but it wasn’t a big issue. Last night, Lu Qin had drawn a max-level skill for making Osmanthus Oolong Tea. He initially wanted to buy the ingredients to try it out, but the market didn’t have what he needed.

Oolong tea required a trip to the tea market, and coffee beans needed a supplier. He also needed dried osmanthus flowers, fresh milk, and butter.

Though sourcing these materials was a hassle, selling this tea could be highly profitable. The cost per cup was around 5 yuan, but it could sell for 28-30 yuan per cup.

With milk tea prices already around 20 yuan per cup, a tea drink made with max-level skills would easily justify the price. As the range of items he sold at his stall expanded, production time became a challenge. It was impossible to prepare every dish and drink every day. Lu Qin was considering rotating his menu.

After returning home on his tricycle cart, Lu Qin found the mother-daughter duo still sound asleep. Carrying the ingredients, he quietly entered the kitchen. He had over two hours to prep. He decided to start by boiling mung beans, which could then be refrigerated and sold as chilled mung bean soup at the night stall.

The landlord’s double-door, 750-liter fridge could store a maximum of 200 servings of mung bean soup. Lu Qin realized he might need a bigger freezer.

Better check with Boss Zhao later. He might know where to get one, he thought.

Lu Qin boiled a large pot of water, adding green onions, ginger, and garlic before placing the pork inside. As it simmered, he marinated chicken wings and began kneading dough.

Each dish he made was limited by storage capacity. The system’s temperature-controlled freshness locker could only hold so much.

Prepared foods like imperial chicken wings, chicken flower soup, and crystal shrimp dumplings had to be stored in the freshness locker to maintain their taste and texture. Leaving them out would compromise quality unless they were made fresh, which wasn’t practical.

Once everything was ready, the freshness locker was packed full. It could keep items fresh for 24 hours—perfect for the night stall.

While preparing breakfast, Shen Muyan appeared in the kitchen doorway, holding a dazed Lu Xiyao by the hand.

“What time did you wake up?” She asked, standing there in a Mickey Mouse T-shirt, her long, fair legs paired with flip-flops, exuding casual elegance.

“Four-thirty,” Lu Qin replied, plating a fried sunny-side-up egg.

“That early?”

Shen Muyan’s eyes widened slightly.

“Every day?”

“Mm-hmm.”

Watching him in his apron making breakfast, Shen Muyan felt a twinge of heartache.

Lu Qin didn’t seem to mind. Looking down at the still-sleepy Lu Xiyao swaying back and forth, he smiled and said,

“Yaoyao, time to wake up. We’ll be late for kindergarten.”

“Okay~!”

The little one instinctively responded but kept her eyes half-closed.

“She’s got quite the morning grumpiness,” Shen Muyan noted. “How do you usually wake her up?”

“By flicking her forehead—three flicks, and she’s wide awake.”

Lu Qin winked at her.

Amused, Shen Muyan curled her finger and brought it to her lips.

“Really? Let me try!”

Suddenly, Lu Xiyao’s long lashes fluttered violently, and she opened her eyes wide.

“Mommy, Daddy! I’m awake! I’m really awake!”

Her dramatic declaration amused Shen Muyan, who smirked playfully.

“I don’t think you are. I think I’ll have to flick your forehead!”

“Nooo! Mommy! I’m really awake! I’ll go wash my face and brush my teeth now!”

The little one covered her forehead and bolted to the bathroom. Lu Qin and Shen Muyan exchanged smiles.

Breakfast consisted of crystal shrimp dumplings, fried eggs, and soy milk. When Shen Muyan noticed a cup of coffee on her side of the table, she paused in surprise.

“What’s this?”

“A mocha, no cream, low-fat. I remember you used to drink this every morning.”

Before the divorce.

A warm, fuzzy feeling grew in Shen Muyan’s chest.

“You made it?”

“No, I bought it from a café while getting groceries.”

It was an Italian-style café that wasn’t usually open so early. The barista made an exception because Lu Qin was so handsome. Sometimes, good looks came in handy.

The coffee tasted average, but to Shen Muyan, it felt special. She decided she’d finish the whole cup.

Lu Qin wasn’t trying to impress her—his meticulous care for their daughter naturally extended to her. His habit of considering everything from all angles made him seem thoughtful, attentive, and utterly reliable.

Which woman wouldn’t be charmed by a man like that?

Thinking back on the divorce, Shen Muyan felt it might’ve been a foolish decision at the time.

After breakfast, both Shen Muyan and Lu Xiyao volunteered to do the dishes.

“Daddy works so hard every day! Let your little cutie handle this!”

“You cooked breakfast, I’ll take care of the dishes.”

The two ladies pushed Lu Qin to the sofa to rest. Shen Muyan even poured him a glass of lemon water. Lu Qin felt a subtle warmth in his heart.

In the kitchen, little Lu Xiyao, who often helped with chores, was much more skilled than her mother.

“Mommy, you’re using too much dish soap! You only need a little—just the size of your pinky!”

“Mommy, rinse off the grease with water first, then scrub with the sponge!”

“Mommy, shake off the water droplets before putting the plates in the sanitizer!”

Shen Muyan felt a bit defeated, needing a child to teach her how to wash dishes. But she was also proud—her daughter’s maturity was clearly the result of Lu Qin’s excellent parenting.

Without him, she doubted Yaoyao would be so cheerful, considerate, and clever.

At 8:15 a.m., the family of three put on their matching bear-themed outfits and left for kindergarten.

“I’ll drive. If you’re tired, nap in the car,” Shen Muyan offered.

“You’ll drive?”

Lu Qin glanced down and noticed she’d slipped on his flip-flops. The high heels she wore yesterday didn’t match the family outfits.

Realizing her mistake, Shen Muyan’s cheeks reddened slightly. She opened the trunk and found a pair of casual shoes.

“I forgot I left a pair in here.”

Lu Qin: “…”

The Porsche Macan’s engine roared as it sped off, leaving the old neighborhood behind.

chapters.title: Chapter 98
chapters.content:
Chapter 98 Lu Xiyao’s Mom Is Here! Father and Daughter Are So Popular?

At Starlight Kindergarten, Cars pulled up one by one in front of the kindergarten as parents arrived with their children for the parent-child activity. Smiles of happiness lit up everyone’s faces.

“Oh! Haohao’s dad, Haohao’s mom, where did you get those matching white and green striped outfits? They look great!”

“Look at that! Yuan Haojie’s family is dressed as three little tigers. How adorable!”

“Zhong Shuyi! Why is your outfit different from ours?”

“This is a traditional Chinese design. I had my mom custom-make it a while ago. See, it even has embroidered red-crowned cranes, my favorite!”

“Wow! That’s amazing!”

“I’m so envious, Zhong Shuyi! Your family’s outfits are so special!”

A group of children circled Zhong Shuyi, their eyes filled with admiration.

Zhong Shuyi’s family was wearing light green, traditional Chinese-style outfits. The mother and daughter looked elegant and ethereal in the flowing design.

However, when it came to Zhong Shuyi’s dad, the look was less flattering. With his bulky figure, square face, and prominent belly, he embodied the stereotypical image of a potbellied businessman.

Zhong Shuyi’s dad muttered with frustration, “If I’d known I’d have to dress like this for the kindergarten event, I wouldn’t have come.”

“It looks fine,” Zhong Shuyi’s mom said, though not very convincingly.

“As long as I and our daughter look good, you’re just the backdrop,” she added.

Zhong Shuyi chimed in innocently, “Mom, isn’t this backdrop a little too round?”

“…”

Just then, a shiny new carmine-red Porsche Macan pulled up at the kindergarten entrance. The children, enchanted by the gleaming red car, burst into exclamations.

“Wow! Mommy, look! That car is so pretty!”

“That’s the new Porsche model this year. It’s stunning!”

“Whose family is this? I’ve never seen this car before!”

Regular parents, familiar with everyone’s routines—what cars they drove, who biked, and who had bought homes in the nearby school zone—were taken aback. The car wasn’t one they’d seen before.

The Porsche zipped through the street and came to a neat stop in front of the kindergarten.

Under the watchful eyes of the crowd, the car door opened. From the driver’s seat, Shen Muyan stepped out.

Wearing an off-white T-shirt and yellow shorts, her long, snow-white, shapely legs emerged first. The visual impact was electrifying, causing the nearby men to instinctively gulp as their hearts raced.

The women, mesmerized too, quickly snapped out of it when they noticed their husbands gawking. Ears were promptly yanked. The outfit itself—casual and homey—might not have stood out on its own. But Shen Muyan’s stunning beauty and cool, regal demeanor drew every eye.

“Wow! She’s gorgeous! Is she even a parent?”

“She’s so cold and elegant, like a goddess! Even prettier than celebrities!”

“Who is she? Someone’s sister or aunt? Today’s parent-child activity just got a lot more interesting!”

From the backseat, Lu Xiyao hopped down, wearing a matching outfit. Shen Muyan walked over, took her hand, and the mother-daughter duo instantly exuded an aura of elegance straight out of a high-end fashion ad. Finally, Lu Qin got out of the car.

Clean-cut and sunny, he looked striking in the same outfit, completing the family picture.

“Lu Xiyao!”

“Boss Lu!”

“Yaoyao’s dad!”

The crowd froze in shock before collectively gasping.

The family of three stood together, their matching bear-themed outfits emphasizing their sky-high attractiveness. They were so stunning that even passersby stopped to look, thinking they might be models shooting a parent-child outfit ad.

The men murmured, “So, that’s Yaoyao’s mom? No wonder her dad never brings her here. Who’d want to show off such a beautiful wife? Better to keep her at home!”

Children and parents swarmed over. Poor Zhong Shuyi, who had been proudly showing off her Chinese-style outfit moments earlier, found her audience vanishing. Even her dad, captivated, stared openly in the new direction.

“Where are your eyes wandering?” Zhong Shuyi’s mom snapped, seething.

“Appreciating beauty is human nature,” he replied cheekily. “It’s not just me—look at all the other dads!”

“Wait till we get home!” She hissed, though he seemed unfazed.

Meanwhile, Zhong Shuyi tugged at her dad’s sleeve, asking, “Dad, isn’t Yaoyao my good friend? Have you ever met her mom before?”

“Nope,” she replied honestly, before running off to join the crowd around Yaoyao.

Her dad, attempting to follow, was promptly grabbed by the collar by his wife.

“Yaoyao, is that your mom?”

“Wow, Yaoyao! Your mom is so pretty!”

“Why didn’t your mom ever come to previous events?”

Hand in hand with her daughter, Shen Muyan remained composed, her frosty demeanor intact, though her mind raced— Is my daughter always this popular at school?

She turned to Lu Qin.

To her surprise, he was surrounded by a group of moms, all chatting animatedly.

Shen Muyan was speechless.

Looks like my daughter isn’t the only popular one.

“That’s my mom!” Yaoyao replied cheerfully. “My mom manages lots of big hotels, so she’s really busy and didn’t have time to come to the kindergarten before!”

“Alright, everyone! Parents and kids, let’s head inside! You’re blocking the road,” Teacher Li called out, clapping to grab attention.

Before Lu Qin’s family arrived, the entrance had been orderly. Now it was chaotic. Inside, disposable slippers awaited parents while children wore their usual ones.

The first activity was family cooking, aimed at letting kids experience the joys of preparing food with their parents—a rare opportunity in today’s fast-paced lives.

Families formed teams, assigned to various tasks like baking cakes, making cookies, or frying rice.

“Mommy, I want roast duck!”

“There’s no duck here. We’ve got flour, rice, pork, and carrots. How about I make you a duck-shaped bun and bake it?”

“…”

chapters.title: Chapter 99
chapters.content:
Chapter 99 Just Because You Can’t Cook! Should We Prepare an Antidote?

Parents and children stood behind the tables, which were set with various cooking tools and ingredients.

Teacher Li announced the rules.

“Dear parents and children, this is a parent-child activity! That means every family member must participate, not just the parents but also the kids. Each family will cook a dish together, and afterward, other families will taste and rate it. The family with the highest score will receive a lovely prize!”

The activity was meaningful, and both parents and children were enthusiastic. However, most of the parents, being millennials, had grown up eating meals cooked by their parents and then transitioned to takeout or dining out as adults. For those craving home-cooked meals, the solution was often a visit to the in-laws.

Kitchens, though renovated into sleek, high-end spaces with imported range hoods costing tens of thousands, were more for show than use. The appliance most frequently used wasn’t the stove but the fridge or microwave.

That said, some parents with years of cooking experience could whip up dishes that rivaled those served in small restaurants.

Lu Qin tapped the pork belly and said to Shen Muyan and Lu Xiyao, “How about we make some Twice-Cooked Pork?”

Both mother and daughter’s eyes lit up, nodding in unison.

“Yes! Daddy’s Twice-Cooked Pork is unbeatable! Let’s make that!”

Shen Muyan smiled slightly. Lu Qin’s Twice-Cooked Pork was leagues ahead of what even the top chefs at her luxury hotel’s Chinese restaurant could produce. Bringing it to kindergarten seemed like overkill.

Other parents’ gazes drifted toward Lu Qin’s table. Many were well aware of Lu Qin’s fame as a street food vendor selling stir-fried noodles, Twice-Cooked Pork, and Imperial Chicken Wings. The flavor was legendary—so good it could make headlines.

If Lu Qin put his skills on display, who could possibly compete?

“Teacher Li, with Yaoyao’s dad cooking, the rest of us might as well give up!”

“Exactly! I’ve tasted his Twice-Cooked Pork before—it’s better than what five-star chefs make. There’s no way we can beat that.”

“Lu Qin’s street food stall is so popular that even showing up an hour early doesn’t guarantee you’ll get anything. His food is that good!”

“Teacher Li, this is unfair! Yaoyao’s dad is like a pro athlete competing with kids!”

The complaints poured in. The consensus—Lu Qin couldn’t cook. It was unfair!

Faced with the uproar, Teacher Li walked over to Lu Qin’s table, smiling apologetically.

“Mr. Lu, your cooking skills are just too good. Even all the other parents combined wouldn’t stand a chance. How about letting Yaoyao’s mom cook instead?”

Shen Muyan froze.

“I can’t cook.”

She wasn’t joking. Asking her to cook was like asking her to climb Everest without preparation—she didn’t even know how to use a rice cooker.

Teacher Li smiled.

“That’s exactly why we want you to cook!”

Lu Qin: “…”

Shen Muyan: “…”

Lu Xiyao: “…”

“Teacher Li, that’s not fair!” Protested Lu Xiyao, pouting.

“Yaoyao, this is how we make it fair. Your mom will take charge, and you and your dad will assist. It’s settled then! Any objections?”

“No objections! Teacher Li is so wise!” Chorused the parents.

“But Daddy…” Yaoyao said with a whimper, “I wanted to eat your Twice-Cooked Pork!”

Shen Muyan ruffled her daughter’s hair. “Mommy will make it for you instead.”

Yaoyao swallowed nervously.

“Do we need to prepare an antidote?”

Lu Qin tied an apron around Shen Muyan.

“Let’s get started. Just follow my instructions.”

“Step one: Turn on the stove and scorch the pork skin.”

“Okay.”

Ten seconds later, Shen Muyan asked coolly, “How do I turn it on?”

Lu Qin was speechless.

“Daddy, maybe I should go help Zhong Shuyi’s family instead!” Yaoyao said.

Over at Zhong Shuyi’s table, things weren’t much better. Her family had a personal chef and live-in help. The concept of cooking was alien to them. Zhong Shuyi’s mom decided to make boiled fish.

Her dad, skeptical, asked, “Are you sure boiled fish means filling a pot with water, tossing in a live fish, adding a couple of scallions, and letting the fish swim around?”

“Isn’t it?”

“Mom, aren’t you supposed to kill the fish first?” Zhong Shuyi asked.

“What does ‘kill the fish’ mean?” Her mom replied innocently.

Teacher Li walked over and said gently, “Mrs. Zhong, I don’t think boiled fish is the right choice for you. How about trying something else?”

Meanwhile, at Lu Qin’s table, Yaoyao was diligently washing vegetables—removing stems from green peppers, trimming garlic sprouts, discarding yellowed parts—while humming a little tune.

“No one in the kids’ world washes veggies better than me. And in the veggie-washing world, no one’s cuter than me. La la la~!”

As Yaoyao finished her task, Shen Muyan finally managed to boil the pork belly.

“How do I take the meat out?” She asked.

In the corporate world, no one was more capable than Shen Muyan. In the culinary world, no one was as clueless. The slab of pork belly, weighing over a pound, was cooked but too large to lift with a spatula, and too hot to handle by hand.

“Use chopsticks,” Lu Qin suggested, handing her a pair.

Chopsticks? Curious, Shen Muyan jabbed them into the meat and managed to lift it out.

“We don’t have ice here, so rinse the pork under cold water,” Lu Qin instructed.

“Why cold water?” She asked, her curiosity piqued.

“It makes the meat firmer and more elastic, enhancing its texture and cooling it down for slicing,” he explained patiently.

Yaoyao placed the cleaned garlic sprouts and green peppers on the cutting board.

“All done! Isn’t your little cutie amazing?”

“Very impressive!” Lu Qin praised generously.

Just then, Zhong Shuyi ran over, pointing at the potatoes on their table.

“Are you using those potatoes? If not, can we have them?”

“We’re making Twice-Cooked Pork—no potatoes needed!” Yaoyao replied.

“Great!”

“Why do you need our potatoes?” Yaoyao asked.

“My mom tried to make sour and spicy shredded potatoes, but they turned out thicker than fingers and sourer than lemons. She said it was too hard and is switching to mashed potatoes now. Fingers crossed it works!”

The spacious classroom filled with the sounds—and smells—of cooking. But not all aromas were pleasant. Alongside the savory notes of frying, baking, and simmering were burnt, charred, and overly sour scents.

Teacher Li and Teacher Zhang exchanged glances and sighed.

Teacher Li remarked, “Parents these days… most can’t even fry an egg. How do they manage to raise kids?”

Teacher Zhang added, “Grandparents probably do most of the work. Some parents can’t even change a diaper!”

chapters.title: Chapter 100
chapters.content:
Chapter 100 Foul Play, Ref! This Is Serious Cheating!

Under Lu Qin’s guidance, Shen Muyan cut the garlic sprouts into sections and sliced the green peppers into diamond shapes. She wielded a fruit knife with precision, peeling without breaking the skin. Her steady hand and meticulous nature made her vegetable cutting look decent.

But when it came to slicing meat, it was a disaster.

“You’re holding the knife wrong, and your hand pressing the meat is positioned incorrectly,” Lu Qin said, pointing out her mistakes.

“You should do it like this…”

Lu Qin took her delicate, fair hand, positioning her index finger and thumb around the blade.

“This grip makes the knife steady.”

“Oh,” Shen Muyan murmured, nodding absentmindedly as her hand tingled from his touch.

“The first joint of your middle finger should touch the knife, and no other part of your hand should make contact. As you slice, your fingers move backward gradually, with the middle finger moving last.”

Lu Qin was thorough in his explanation, even placing his hand over hers to demonstrate.

“Got it now?”

“Mm-hmm.”

A faint blush colored Shen Muyan’s fair cheeks. Having Lu Qin so close, his breath brushing against her ear, made her heart race. Although she said she understood, her hands clearly didn’t. Halfway through slicing one piece, the knife slipped.

Lu Qin couldn’t stand it any longer and corrected her grip again.

“Try again.”

“Okay.”

This time, her meat slices varied dramatically in thickness—some paper-thin, others chunky.

“No, you’re still not holding it right.”

Lu Qin stepped in again, adjusting her hand meticulously, bending her fingers properly against the pork.

“The knife should be perpendicular to the cutting board at a 90-degree angle.”

He guided her hand, demonstrating how to slice a perfectly even two-millimeter-thick piece of meat.

“Now try again.”

“Okay, I’ll give it a shot.”

Shen Muyan managed to slice a piece that was five millimeters thick but at least evenly so.

“That’s better. You just need more practice.”

Lu Qin nodded approvingly.

“Let me help you speed up. Your pace is too slow.”

He moved behind her, placing his hands over hers as they cut together. His head was so close it nearly touched her cheek. Yaoyao blinked her big eyes, staring at her parents. Her gaze turned increasingly suspicious.

She couldn’t prove it, but she was sure her parents were showing off their affection. Several other parents looked up and froze at the sight of the couple cutting meat together.

Foul play! Ref! Serious foul play!

What are they doing?

Cooking or filming a romantic drama?

The rest of us came to make food, and now we’re being force-fed dog food!

Parents exchanged meaningful glances with Teachers Li and Zhang, who turned to look and were equally stunned.

“Hey, Yaoyao’s dad! You’re supposed to assist, not take over! What’s the difference between this and cooking it yourself?” Teacher Li protested.

Lu Qin immediately let go, stepping back.

He genuinely had no ulterior motives. In his ‘cooking mode’, his mind was entirely focused—purely about food. Even if a goddess appeared, his concentration wouldn’t waver.

He glanced at Shen Muyan, who seemed flustered, her porcelain cheeks tinged red.

“?”

Blushing over slicing meat? You’re a mom already—be serious.

“You’re on your own now,” he said, stepping aside.

“Okay.”

But with her nerves unsettled, Shen Muyan’s cuts became erratic, producing slices as thick as shoe soles.

Yaoyao facepalmed. Lu Qin sighed but restrained himself from stepping in.

Meanwhile, chaos reigned at other tables.

One family turned a fried egg into scrambled mush. Another dumped half a bag of salt into their dish. Someone forgot to remove the plastic wrapper from a steak before cooking it. Still, there were exceptions.

Chen Mingming’s mom made a respectable spicy chicken. Zhang Yiming’s dad prepared a decent stir-fried beef with bean sprouts. After many chaotic attempts, Shen Muyan’s stir-fried Twice-Cooked Pork was finally done.

Yaoyao, holding her dad’s phone, snapped several photos of her mom cooking. She intentionally framed the shots to exclude her mom’s beautiful face, focusing instead on her clumsy actions, which reminded her of a monkey in a zoo.

She figured these photos were worth at least two ice creams. If not, she could always share them with her grandparents or Aunt Fang Ya for maximum hilarity.

Looking at the plate of Twice-Cooked Pork, Lu Qin fell into a contemplative silence. He wouldn’t say it was completely unappetizing, but it was… challenging to describe positively.

Teacher Li clapped her hands to gather everyone.

“Now that all the dishes are ready, each family can introduce what they’ve made.”

“Mommy made Winter Melon and Pork Rib Soup! I washed and cut the melon, Daddy chopped the ribs, and Mommy did the cooking.”

“We made Creamy Pudding, but it’s a bit too sweet because we added too much sugar.”

“This is my mom’s specialty—Sweet and Sour Pork Ribs!“

The first few introductions were normal, but things quickly spiraled out of control.

“My mom made Seafood Buns! Be careful not to get pinched by the crab claws.”

“We made… uh, these aren’t coal briquettes; they’re roasted potato chunks. They’re a bit burnt but still edible.”

“My dad steamed huge buns and used one to smash cucumbers. He says they’re also good for cracking walnuts and hammering nails!”

“My dad made Braised Tofu! He cut the tofu with a badminton racket.”

Teachers Li and Zhang struggled to suppress their laughter, wondering if this was a cooking event or a comedy show. Finally, it was Yaoyao’s turn.

“This is my mom’s Twice-Cooked Pork. It looks okay, but I’m not eating it!”

Her blunt honesty drew laughter from the crowd.

“First attempts are already quite impressive,” Lu Qin reassured.

“Really?” Shen Muyan’s eyes sparkled. “Try some and tell me how it tastes.”

“I’m not trying it,” Lu Qin replied solemnly.

“…”

Everyone remembered the rule—families didn’t have to eat their own dishes—they were for others to taste and score.

The families who couldn’t cook collectively sighed in relief. Meanwhile, Zhong Shuyi’s mom brought out her mashed potatoes.

“Everyone, try our mashed potatoes!”

“Why are they brown?” Someone asked.

“I added soy sauce for flavor.”

“Got it. Goodbye!”

Nobody dared taste the dish, fearing they’d regret it. In the end, Zhong Shuyi’s dad volunteered as the test subject.

“Go, Dad!” Cheered Zhong Shuyi.

With a deep breath, he took a bite while everyone watched intently.

“Not bad. Just a bit salty. You can try it,” he said.

Though the mashed potatoes didn’t look great, they were at least edible, earning a score of 40 points.

Zhong Shuyi’s mom was satisfied. For her, the goal wasn’t fame or glory—just completing the activity was enough.

chapters.title: Chapter 101
chapters.content:
Chapter 101 Yuan Haojie’s Mom, Are Your Dumplings Weaned?

Yuan Haojie’s mom brought out the results of an hour and a half of hard work.

“Everyone, please try the dumplings our group made.”

Parents and kids from other groups leaned in to have a look.

“Yuan Haojie’s mom, are these dumplings or garlic cloves?”

“Could they be any smaller? They’re as tiny as a fingernail! Miniature dumplings for sure!”

“Yuan Haojie’s mom, I bet I could eat four figures worth of your dumplings. Do you believe me?”

“Yuan Haojie’s mom, are your dumplings weaned yet?”

“These dumplings look like embryos that haven’t fully developed!”

Yuan Haojie shook his head speechlessly.

“My mom says she’s done her best.”

Lu Qin and Shen Muyan both laughed, while Lu Xiyao wanted to taste one of the tiny dumplings. Shen Muyan quickly stopped her.

If the dumplings were this odd in size, their flavor was probably equally unusual—it wasn’t worth the risk.

The next group presented something that could barely pass as cookies.

“Red-red’s dad, what’s this?”

“Obviously, these are cookies.”

“Obviously, these are lumps of coal!”

“Mr. Li, I can’t accept these cookies. I refuse to try them.”

“Red-red’s dad, your cookies make one feel miserable after the first bite, dazed after the second, and euphoric after the third, ready to ascend the clouds!”

“Red-red’s dad, next time just bake with coal directly. Why go through all this effort?”

The groups that followed only got more ridiculous.

There was green egg cake, Sprite chicken wings, knife-cut noodles, dragon fruit stir-fried with shrimp…

Each dish was more of a culinary nightmare than the last. Other groups dared not taste them. Only the group’s fathers or sons braved a bite, demonstrating their manly responsibility.

The result? Painful grimaces all around. Thankfully, they only had a taste—any more, and they might have become projectile warriors.

“Come, everyone! Try our group’s creative dish! We’ve named it ‘Minesweeper’!”

The last group brought their dish forward.

“‘Minesweeper’? Isn’t this just shredded potatoes? What’s so creative about it?”

“Shredded potatoes stir-fried with ginger strips.”

“Oh, wow! That truly is ‘Minesweeper’!”

“If you’ve got the creativity, why not channel it into figuring out how to cook a proper dish?”

Teachers Li and Zhang couldn’t help shaking their heads. What they had hoped would be a harmonious and joyful cooking activity had turned into a parade of dishes too horrendous to look at, let alone eat.

The fresh ingredients bought by the kindergarten kitchen had been thoroughly wasted. They couldn’t let the kitchen staff see this—or there might truly be an incident involving kitchen knives.

The kids collectively shook their heads, silently thinking how challenging this batch of parents was to handle. Originally, each group was supposed to prepare a dish for everyone to share, solving lunch in the process. Teacher Li had informed the cafeteria in advance not to prepare lunch for today.

Now, things had gotten troublesome. The afternoon still had parent-child activities lined up, and letting the parents and kids starve was not an option.

“Daddy, I’m hungry~!”

Lu Xiyao lifted her little head, looking at Lu Qin with a pitiful, expectant gaze.

“Come, Xiyao, try Mommy’s twice-cooked pork. I’ve made rice as well.”

Shen Muyan promoted her first-ever cooking attempt. Not eating it would be wasteful.

“Thank you, Mommy, but can I not eat it?”

The rice, cooked by Lu Qin, was fragrant and fluffy, which the little one could accept. But the twice-cooked pork? It looked overly salty and greasy—terrifying, really!

Eating rice without the meat it was supposed to accompany was tough.

What a struggle!

“Mommy made this for the first time. Aren’t you going to support her?”

“Mommy, I support you in spirit~!”

“No way! Physical support is a must. Also, no wasting food. Come, recite a poem about cherishing food to elevate your awareness!”

Shen Muyan was unwilling to accept this. After all her hard work, not even one bite? It was too discouraging.

“Alright, kids have sensitive stomachs. Don’t force her.”

Lu Qin stepped in to rescue the little one.

“Let me see if I can salvage this dish.”

Shen Muyan: “…”

Lu Qin washed the wok and then added a bit of cold oil to the hot pan.

The twice-cooked pork was about to get a third round in the pan.

Parents, teachers, and kids all turned their attention to him, faces full of anticipation.

“Not redoing it entirely? Can the twice-cooked pork be saved? That’s Shen Muyan’s first-ever dish!”

“Impossible! Trust me, the prettier the woman, the scarier her cooking. Look at Shen Muyan’s elegance and beauty—do you even need to guess her cooking skills?”

“Well, that’s it. Lu Qin can’t possibly save her twice-cooked pork!”

“Honey, how’s my cooking?”

“Unquestionable! Five-star chef standard!”

“ Hiss —! Take it easy! That hurts!”

“Dare to say I’m ugly again, and you’re kneeling on the washboard tonight!”

“I won’t kneel on the washboard… I’ll kneel on the keyboard, so what?”

Lu Qin tilted the pan, letting the oil drip out of the twice-cooked pork.

Shen Muyan remained calm outwardly but felt slightly embarrassed inside. She distinctly remembered not adding that much oil. Where did it all come from?

A parent joked, “Lu Qin, don’t waste the oil. Pour it into my car’s fuel tank—it might run for two kilometers!”

Shen Muyan’s pale cheeks turned slightly red, adding a touch of charm.

Lu Qin tasted a slice of the twice-cooked pork. It was a bit salty, soft, and greasy. With rice, it could barely go down, but too much would make one nauseous.

He added some minced garlic to the pan for aroma, then tossed the twice-cooked pork back in, stirring vigorously over high heat. Flames erupted in the wok, eliciting gasps from the kids.

“Wow! Look at that! Now, that’s what you call a chef!”

“You don’t get this level of skill without seven or eight years of experience!”

“A man who can cook is truly handsome! Help me—I’m fainting from the sight of this!”

Lu Qin flipped the wok rapidly, adding a bit of sugar, a dash of sweet bean paste, and a small spoon of water. He also threw in a few slices of king oyster mushrooms to absorb the grease.

The twice-cooked pork stayed in the pan for just a minute before being plated again.

“It’s done. Try it.”

Lu Qin sampled it first. The taste was now balanced—not too salty or greasy, with a nice aroma. It wasn’t quite up to his usual standard, but it was good enough for a high-end restaurant.

“Awesome! I want to try!”

Lu Xiyao trusted her dad’s cooking skills. After his improvements, it was sure to taste good.

“Blow on it first, Xiyao. It’s hot.”

“Got it~!”

Lu Xiyao popped a piece into her mouth and started chewing happily.

“Daddy, it tastes really good~!”

“Mommy, you should try it, too. And learn while you’re at it. Hehe~!”

Lu Qin served two bowls of rice for Shen Muyan and Lu Xiyao.

“Aren’t you eating?”

Shen Muyan noticed Lu Qin hadn’t served himself.

“You two eat first. I’ll quickly whip up some tomato and egg stir-fry.”

Lu Qin cracked three eggs into a bowl and whisked them briskly in one direction.

In three minutes, the tomato and egg stir-fry was ready, filling the air with a delicious aroma.

Parents and kids stared, swallowing hard.

It was just tomato and egg stir-fry—how could it smell so good?

Lu Qin’s dish clearly outshone Shen Muyan’s twice-cooked pork.

All three sets of chopsticks kept reaching for the tomato and egg stir-fry.

“Daddy, your tomato and egg stir-fry is amazing~!”

The little one ate with relish.

Even Shen Muyan couldn’t stop her chopsticks.

“Teacher Li, uh, where can we eat? It’s noon, and we’re really hungry!”

Teacher Li awkwardly replied, “I’m so sorry. I thought everyone’s dishes would be shareable, and with so many groups, everyone would have enough to eat. So I didn’t ask the cafeteria to prepare lunch.”

The teachers had initially planned to mooch off the food themselves but had ended up being dragged into the mess.

“I’ve already informed the kitchen staff to start washing, chopping, and cooking. Lunch should be ready in about two hours.”

Parents groaned, their faces falling.

Two hours? They’d starve to death by then!

Meanwhile, Lu Xiyao’s family of three had already started eating.

“Ahem… Lu Qin, do you think our mashed potatoes can be saved? We just need them to be edible!”

Zhong Shuyi’s dad was already starving. His wife was a pro at shopping and beauty treatments, but cooking? A disaster. Even the simplest mashed potatoes she learned from TikTok had been a failure. Left with no other option, he sought Lu Qin’s help.

The alluring aroma of the tomato and egg stir-fry had only made him hungrier.

chapters.title: Chapter 102
chapters.content:
Chapter 102 Even Green Peppers Can Be This Delicious—A Gateway to a New World!

“Sorry, I’ve never made mashed potatoes before, so I don’t have any good suggestions,” Lu Qin said honestly, shaking his head.

Zhong Shuyi’s dad pushed the plate towards his wife.

“Honey, you’ll have to eat this yourself.”

“Are you kidding me? I’m not eating that!”

Zhong Shuyi’s mom was disgusted herself. The strip-shaped, brownish mashed potatoes looked like something a constipated puppy might leave behind. Who could stomach that?

Meanwhile, Zhong Shuyi had already abandoned her parents and wandered over to Lu Xiyao’s table.

“Shuyi, do you want some?”

As a good friend, Lu Xiyao asked, noticing that Zhong Shuyi was clearly starving.

“Mmhmm!”

Zhong Shuyi nodded enthusiastically.

“Dad, can Shuyi eat with us?”

“Of course,” Lu Qin replied, serving Zhong Shuyi a bowl of rice.

“Thank you, Uncle Lu! You’re the best!”

Zhong Shuyi had long been mesmerized by the aroma of Lu Qin’s tomato and egg stir-fry. Ignoring her parents completely, she sat down and started devouring her meal.

Zhong Shuyi’s parents were dumbfounded. Was their child this quick to desert them? As Zhong Shuyi’s dad approached the table, hoping to mooch a bite, he realized the food was almost gone. Even his daughter was scraping the last bit of tomato and egg sauce onto her rice, savoring every bite.

Helpless, Zhong Shuyi’s dad resigned himself to hunger. A few other groups managed to cook one or two dishes and were quietly eating. However, the other twenty-something groups were left watching enviously, regretting not having learned to cook earlier.

When hunger strikes, one truly regrets their lack of skills in the kitchen. The kids began gravitating towards Lu Xiyao’s table. Normally, when Lu Xiyao brought her meals to kindergarten, her delicious food often left other kids drooling. Some were lucky enough to snag a bite or two of her mom’s twice-cooked pork or her dad’s tomato and egg stir-fry. Even a small taste was enough to make them scarf down their own rice.

Now that Lu Qin himself was present, the kids were more tempted than ever, their eyes glued to his table, practically drooling. Unfortunately, the last bit of tomato and egg stir-fry had already been mixed into rice for Zhong Shuyi.

In the kitchen, Chef Hu was frantically trying to prepare food as quickly as possible. The children and parents were starving, and the activity could quickly turn into chaos if hunger wasn’t addressed.

“Why don’t we eat the knife-cut noodles we made?”

“Are you sure those are knife-cut noodles and not ‘knife-hacked noodles’? The pot nearly choked on them!”

“Xiaofeng, let’s eat the pizza we made!”

“Dad, I’m not eating a frisbee!”

…

Finally, Chef Hu brought out a large pot of steamed rice.

The parents and kids cheered in relief.

“Everyone, you can start with rice. The dishes are still being prepared,” Chef Hu said, apologizing. “It’s just me today, and I can’t keep up. If any parents know how to cook, could you lend a hand in the kitchen?”

Just as three parents were about to step forward, a chorus of children shouted, “Lu Xiyao’s dad can cook! He’s amazing!”

“Let Uncle Lu make twice-cooked pork—it’s super fragrant!”

“He can make fried rice noodles too—so good!”

…

Lu Qin’s culinary skills were heavily hyped by the kids.

All eyes turned to him, some familiar with his reputation, others not, but all hopeful he’d step in.

Lu Qin smiled, ruffling Lu Xiyao’s hair.

“Are you all full? Should I make some more food?”

“Yes, Daddy!”

Both Shen Muyan and Lu Xiyao’s eyes lit up. They had already eaten two bowls of rice but were ready for more. Zhong Shuyi nodded eagerly as well.

Lu Qin headed into the kitchen.

Twenty Minutes Later, Chef Hu began serving dishes one after another—twice-cooked pork, tomato and egg stir-fry, spicy shredded potatoes, and stir-fried pork with green peppers.

Chef Hu was beaming.

“Lu Xiyao’s dad is a true master! Watching him cook is a pleasure, but learning from him? Impossible!”

Chef Hu had secretly tasted the dishes before serving them and was nearly tempted to grab a bowl of rice for himself. Years of professional restraint were the only thing that stopped him.

The food was just too ridiculously good. Even with a hundred years of practice, Chef Hu doubted he could match Lu Qin’s skills.

The rice had already been portioned out, with everyone holding a bowl, eagerly awaiting the dishes. The small tables were rearranged into one large communal table. Shen Muyan and Lu Xiyao were invited to sit, while everyone else stood—there simply wasn’t enough space for everyone to sit.

As the dishes were placed on the table, the tantalizing aroma intensified everyone’s hunger.

“Xiyao and her mom, please start,” someone suggested. After all, it was only polite to let the chef’s family begin.

The little one picked up a piece of stir-fried pork with green peppers and said, “Everyone, let’s eat together!”

“Okay!”

Everyone was already drooling but managed to maintain some semblance of decorum, politely taking their share. Otherwise, the scene might have turned into a chaotic feast like at a countryside wedding.

Lu Qin’s dishes were simple home-style meals but better than any delicacy. The room fell silent as everyone focused on eating, mouths too busy to speak. Occasional murmurs of “Mmm, so good!” and enthusiastic thumbs-ups replaced words.

Shen Muyan and Lu Xiyao exchanged proud glances, their eyes shining. Lu Qin was truly incredible. Even picky eaters found themselves cured by his cooking. For the first time, some realized just how delicious green peppers could be—it was like discovering a whole new world.

Teachers Li and Zhang joined in, unable to resist the fragrant food. Even Chef Hu eventually grabbed some utensils, only to find that the large pot of rice was already empty. Many parents and kids were already on their second bowls.

“Chef, is there more rice?”

“Yes, yes, I’ll bring another pot right away.”

As more rice was served, Lu Qin whipped up six additional dishes—braised chicken, garlic duck, stir-fried three treasures, imperial chicken wings…

Even the simplest dishes became divine under Lu Qin’s hands. Parents and kids couldn’t help but cheer, overwhelmed by the flavors. Some joked they were ready to kneel in gratitude.

Even families with private chefs, like Zhong Shuyi’s, were astonished. Zhong Shuyi’s dad, a wealthy businessman who had long lost his appetite for food, found himself devouring a third bowl of rice. Normally, he barely nibbled at meals, but now he seemed capable of eating five bowls.

Zhong Shuyi’s mom was in the same boat, her taste buds completely captivated. She had already finished two bowls of rice, abandoning her usual slow, elegant dining habits.

The scene resembled a competitive eating contest. If someone walked in, they might have mistaken it for a food festival or a great feast competition.

chapters.title: Chapter 103
chapters.content:
Chapter 103 “Outstanding Men Are Always in High Demand! Now I’m Really Interested!”

The last bit of gravy from the plate of imperial chicken wings was snatched up by Xiao Feng’s dad, who mixed it into his rice and cleaned it out completely.

Chef Hu watched as the dishes and plates gleamed spotless, even the soup on the tips of the chopsticks licked clean.

“What’s this? Are you trying to save the kindergarten on dishwashing soap?” He joked.

As he spoke, he scooped up a single grain of rice and a trace of scrambled egg with tomato from his bowl and licked it clean before tidying up the tableware.

One by one, parents and children alike were full and satisfied, belching contentedly. Some even started leaning against the walls for support.

“ Burp~ ! It’s been years since I’ve eaten like this! What a feast!”

“Last month, I visited Beijing and dined at Yi Pin Xuan, enjoying dishes like Crispy Pipa Duck, Spicy Tiger Crab, and Double Pepper Beef with Wild Mushrooms, but they’re nothing compared to the homemade dishes by Lu Xiyao’s dad!”

“Today, I ate four and a half bowls of rice—broke my personal record!”

“The imperial chicken wings were out of this world! If I were given unlimited portions, I could eat enough to bankrupt the chicken farm!”

“You all haven’t tried Boss Lu’s Crystal Cakes—they’re amazing! Easily the best dessert I’ve ever had!”

“Boss Lu’s culinary skills have surpassed those of star chefs. I’ve never tried state banquet-level dishes, but I think he could match the masters who prepare them!”

“Lu Xiyao’s dad is wasted running a food stall. If he joined the culinary world, his future would be limitless!”

“Please, Boss Lu, don’t lose your head. Stay at the food stall. I still haven’t gotten to try the Chicken Bean Curd!”

“Exactly! If he becomes a high-end chef, how would we ever eat his food again?”

Zhong Shuyi’s dad leaned against the wall, listening to the crowd’s chatter, and dismissed the idea he had been mulling over.

Initially, he had intended to offer Lu Qin a high-paying job as the head chef at a hotel he had invested in—so he could enjoy Lu Qin’s cooking for free every day. But now he realized he had underestimated the man.

Lu Xiyao’s dad seemed to be a man indifferent to fame and fortune. With his skills, he wouldn’t need to run a food stall; any top-tier restaurant would eagerly take him. Heck, even preparing meals for high-ranking officials as a state banquet chef was within reach.

Zhong Shuyi’s dad felt fortunate. Choosing not to send his daughter to an elite private school but instead enrolling her at Starlight Kindergarten was undoubtedly the right decision.

At Starlight, many kids had parents who were big bosses or entrepreneurs. He’d heard that Lu Xiyao’s mom was the CEO of the Ourei International Hotel chain.
