Secretly, he felt relieved that his restaurant investments were located in the north of the city. If they were in the south, they would have to compete with Ourei Hotels—and with Lu Qin’s level of culinary artistry, they’d definitely go out of business.

Unsurprisingly, the prize for the cooking activity went to Lu Xiyao’s team, well-deservedly so. Teacher Li handed a beautifully crafted photo album to Lu Xiyao—a custom-made leather-bound album with space for 200 photos. The little girl loved it and thanked Teacher Li.

The afternoon was filled with parent-child activities on the playground. Families participated in games like crab races, fun ping-pong challenges, and tossing and catching games. Everyone had a great time.

Shen Muyan, experiencing her first parent-child activity, genuinely felt happiness and joy.

______

Meanwhile, Shen Hongda finally managed to get through to Chen Dahai on the phone.

“Old Chen, what’s your deal? You haven’t been answering my calls or messages these past few days. I was about to report you missing!”

“Ahaha, I lost my phone recently and just got a new SIM card,” Chen Dahai fibbed.

“Really? Well, what about that thing I asked you to handle after you ate so many of my crystal cakes?”

“Old Shen, after observing these past few days, I feel like you don’t need to push your former son-in-law into any big corporation. Running his food stall suits him perfectly.”

“What? He doesn’t want to work for a company?”

“Of course, he prefers the stall! You have no idea how popular it is. Wherever he sets up, customers flock, and there’s always a line!”

Shen Hongda frowned.

“I know that, but food stall income isn’t stable. He’s a single dad with no house or car—there’s no security…”

Chen Dahai thought to himself, Old Shen must have lost his mind from overthinking. How could Lu Qin’s food stall not be lucrative?

“Income instability? Sure, some days he makes 10,000 to 20,000 yuan, and others, 30,000 to 50,000 yuan—it’s all over the place,” Chen Dahai said sarcastically.

Shen Hongda froze.

“10,000 to 20,000? 30,000 to 50,000? Per day? Are you kidding me?”

“The other day, I had someone estimate roughly. For his morning stall, he sold nearly 300 servings of fried noodles, 250 cups of green bean smoothie, and 200 portions of crystal cake. That’s about 9,500 yuan in revenue, with an 8,000-yuan profit.”

“In the afternoon and evening, he adds dishes like twice-cooked pork at 30 yuan per serving, chicken bean curd at 50 yuan, and imperial chicken wings at 28 yuan. Each dish sells over 200 portions, easily more. Conservatively, afternoon and evening profits are around 40,000 yuan.”

“Actually, how much he earns depends entirely on how much he makes. There are always more customers than food available. No dish ever goes unsold. Old Shen, do you get it now?”

On the other end of the line, Shen Hongda remained silent for a long time. Chen Dahai didn’t rush him, knowing that Shen was still processing the shock. He had felt the same way initially.

Having battled in the business world for decades, Shen Hongda and Chen Dahai understood how hard it was to achieve their current level of wealth and status.

The idea that Lu Qin could earn millions annually just from running a food stall felt almost unbelievable. Perhaps the times really have changed.

This was the age of traffic and flow. Some internet celebrities could effortlessly earn millions through live-streaming and sales. But Lu Qin was different.

Internet influencers relied on luck, hype, and marketing to gain traffic, while Lu Qin’s popularity was built on solid skills. His reputation came from serving one delicious dish after another.

Influencers could face backlash or fade into obscurity. But not Lu Qin. His food stall business would only grow because his culinary skills would keep improving.

After a long silence, Shen Hongda exhaled deeply.

“Old Chen, forget about what I asked you to do earlier.”

“Exactly. No company could pay him better than his food stall does!”

“Old Chen, since you didn’t do anything, make sure to return the crystal cakes you owe me.”

“Shen Hongda, you’re such a scoundrel!” Chen Dahai snapped.

The call ended before he could finish venting. He spat angrily, thinking, Just wait until my daughter Chen Yue wins over Lu Qin. Then you won’t even get a single bite of his crystal cake!

With that thought, Chen Dahai called his daughter.

“How’s it going with Lu Qin? Have you held hands yet?”

Chen Yue, sipping water, nearly choked. “ Cough… Dad! What are you talking about? We’re not even close! He’s so cold. I had to use the excuse of ordering meals for employees just to get his WeChat, and even then, he doesn’t respond when I message him!”

“You’ve got to step up your game. Shen Hongda’s daughter is getting close to Lu Qin—it seems like they’re heading toward remarriage!”

“What? No way! Outstanding men are always in demand! Now I’m really fired up!” Chen Yue’s eyes sparkled with determination.

“Good! Persevere and stay motivated! That’s the spirit of my daughter, Chen Dahai’s bloodline! Seize the moment!”

chapters.title: Chapter 104
chapters.content:
Chapter 104 “The Vase Shen Muyan! Would You Like to Try the Queen’s Seat?”

Chen Yue thought of Lu Qin, a man who captivated her not only with his looks but also with his culinary skills—a double temptation she couldn’t resist.

Comparing him with the wealthy heirs and business elites around her, who all appeared refined but lacked charm, she found none of them as appealing as Lu Qin.

No wonder her shrewd and discerning father, Chen Dahai, praised him so highly. Indeed, he was extraordinary.

Chen Yue took out her phone and sent Lu Qin a message on WeChat.

Using business as an excuse to start a conversation was her strategy. Previously, her simple greetings like “Hello” or “Are you there?” had sunk into the abyss without a response.

Lu Qin had already mentioned he wasn’t considering catering orders for now, but Chen Yue insisted on adding him on WeChat anyway.

Even now, two minutes had passed without a reply. Would calling him directly be too forward? If she did, it might mean sacrificing her dignity entirely after her unanswered messages.

Chen Yue stared at her phone, her manicured nails tapping lightly against the screen. In the end, she decided against calling and put her phone aside.

The saying “a woman pursuing a man is like a veil separating them” was nothing but a myth. That didn’t apply when faced with a resolute and extraordinary man. When anyone meet such a man, pursuing him might feel like crossing a mountain instead.

What Chen Yue didn’t know was that Lu Qin didn’t like adding strangers to his contact list. For potential business contacts like Chen Yue, he used a secondary account.

Moreover, his WeChat notifications for this account were set to a subtle sound that wouldn’t disturb him even if messages came through.

______

By 4 p.m., golden sunlight bathed the entrance of Starlight Kindergarten. The parent-child activity had ended in laughter and cheer, with parents and children alike thoroughly enjoying themselves.

The highlight of the day had undoubtedly been the lunch cooked by Lu Qin—a feast so divine it deeply satisfied everyone’s taste buds and stomachs.

Some parents even suggested to Teacher Li that future parent-child events should replicate today’s arrangement, with one key difference: avoid having the kitchen staff prepare lunch. Teacher Li nodded knowingly. This suggestion was indeed valuable and worth adopting.

At the kindergarten gate, an unusual scene unfolded: parents surrounded Lu Qin as he walked out, while children flocked around Lu Xiyao.

Oddly, the most eye-catching figure of them all—Shen Muyan, a goddess-like woman with stunning looks and an impeccable figure—was the one left unnoticed.

“Xiyao’s dad, the food you made today was so delicious it brought tears to my eyes!”

“Boss Lu, where do you usually set up your food stall? I have to come buy from you! What? You have a QQ group? Add me! Quick, quick!”

“My son stopped being a picky eater after having Xiyao’s dad’s cooking—so impressive!”

Meanwhile, over on Lu Xiyao’s side, other children kept stuffing snacks and toys into her little hands.

“Xiyao, on Monday, can I have a piece of twice-cooked pork? I’ve been dreaming about it!”

“It’s called twice-cooked pork!”

“Lu Xiyao, when is your dad coming to the kindergarten again? His food is amazing!”

Yuan Haojie handed his dad’s BMW car key to Lu Xiyao.

“Lu Xiyao, here’s my dad’s car. On Monday, can I have a chicken wing?”

“Haojie! Time to go home!”

“Okay, Dad!”

“Hey, where’s my car key?”

“Dad, I gave it to Lu Xiyao, but she still wouldn’t give me a chicken wing on Monday!”

“You little brat! Trading a BMW 5 Series for a chicken wing—and not even succeeding! How embarrassing! Go get it back!”

Yuan Haojie ran back.

“Lu Xiyao, this is my dad’s car key. Take it back! And by the way, the story about the girl selling missiles was great, but please don’t tell it again next time.”

“Oh.”

Yuan Haojie took the car key, confused, and handed it back to his father.

“Why are you coming back? Aren’t we going home?”

Yuan Haojie’s dad dragged him into the car. Meanwhile, Lu Xiyao held onto her collection of teddy bear biscuits, chocolates, lollipops, two lychees, and a banana.

She tried to refuse other kids offering toys and milk cartons.

“No more, no more.”

Running to Shen Muyan, she called out, “Mommy, help me carry these!”

“Xiyao, let’s go home.”

The crowd gradually dispersed. Lu Qin waved at Lu Xiyao and Shen Muyan, signaling them to come over.

“Coming!”

The three got into a Porsche. Shen Muyan wondered when this father-daughter duo had become so popular at the kindergarten. Both were so outstanding, yet she, a five-star hotel CEO, felt like she had no presence.

She couldn’t help but feel like a decorative vase.

She couldn’t cook, and she even held back during parent-child games. What was she doing here? Was she just a vase?

Pressing her red lips together, Shen Muyan couldn’t help but reminisce about their married days. Back then, she earned money to support the family, so at least she had a sense of purpose.

As she mulled over her thoughts, Lu Xiyao sneaked her hand towards the milk candy Shen Muyan was holding.

Without turning his head, Lu Qin said, “Xiyao, only two pieces of candy.”

“Daddy, do you have eyes on the back of your head?” The little one asked incredulously.

She didn’t know about the rearview mirror. The car’s Bluetooth automatically connected, and a call came through. Seeing that the call was from an AUTO car dealership, Lu Qin pressed the steering wheel to answer.

The call was on speaker, and Shen Muyan could hear clearly from the back seat.

“Hello, Boss Lu! The M7 Ultra top-tier model you ordered has arrived at the dealership. You can pick it up anytime. When would you like to come?”

At a red light, Lu Qin turned his head to look at Shen Muyan. She sat elegantly, her long legs crossed. Even in a parent-child outfit featuring a bear hat, her regal demeanor was unmistakable.

After a few seconds, she pressed her lips together and nodded, indicating they could pick up the car now. Previously, when she’d asked Lu Qin for help making liver soup, he’d jokingly requested a car worth over 300,000 yuan. It ended up being the world’s most expensive liver soup.

However, Shen Muyan ultimately benefited. Ouyang Lin had thrown in an additional 20 million yuan in investment, sealing the deal smoothly and decisively.

Lu Qin’s culinary skills had become a key to securing investments, and this car was merely a token of gratitude. With her approval, Lu Qin turned right at the next light towards the AUTO dealership in the southern part of the city.

Even near evening, the dealership was bustling with owners picking up their cars, reflecting the popularity of this domestic new energy vehicle.

When the three walked in, still wearing their parent-child outfits, their striking looks and aura caught everyone’s attention.

A staff member hurried over, welcoming them warmly.

“Are you Boss Lu?”

“Yes, I called about ten minutes ago.”

The trio was ushered to a sofa. Water, fruit platters, and snacks were promptly brought over. The service was impeccable.

After confirming the car’s model and configuration, they headed to the garage to see the vehicle.

Lu Xiyao was the most excited, thrilled that the family was getting a new car. She loved its sleek exterior, luxurious interior, and zero-gravity seats.

She darted around the car excitedly, leaving Shen Muyan dizzy.

“Xiyao, you like this car so much? Mommy has lots of cars—why don’t you seem to like those?”

“Mommy, your cars don’t have a fridge, TV, or big sofa!”

“Those are gimmicks. They don’t cost much and are just to fool kids like you. What matters are the three core components.”

“Ma’am, the backseat includes a Queen’s Seat. Would you like to try it?” The staff member offered with a smile.

“Queen’s Seat?”

Curious, Shen Muyan took a look and, unable to resist, gracefully stepped in and sat down.

“It is quite comfortable.”

chapters.title: Chapter 105
chapters.content:
Chapter 105 “What is Installment Payment? I Don’t Understand What You’re Talking About!”

The staff member opened the ‘Queen’s Seat’ in the car for Shen Muyan. The front passenger seat moved forward gently, and a footrest extended automatically.

Despite her tall stature of 1.7 meters, Shen Muyan was able to comfortably stretch her legs, finding it even more relaxing than her sofa at home.

Meanwhile, the little one scrolled through the car’s touchscreen, exploring a variety of animated shows in the kids’ section.

She tapped on one randomly—Boonie Bears.

“My honey, my nuts, my little butterfly~!”

The car’s 21 high-quality speakers, utilizing cutting-edge sound-diffusion technology, delivered an impressive auditory experience.

“Has this car’s nationwide mapless intelligent driving feature been activated?”

“It has,” the staff member confirmed.

“Good,” Lu Qin nodded with satisfaction.

After completing the car inspection, the three of them returned to the main hall. The process of picking up the car was straightforward—confirm the package and insurance, then pay directly via a smartphone app.

“Mr. Lu, would you like to consider installment payments?” Asked the staff member.

“What’s the installment payment?”

Lu Qin glanced at the nearby wealthy lady with an air of nonchalance. His tone carried an unintentional swagger.

The staff member was taken aback.

“Huh?”

Shen Muyan rolled her eyes dramatically but elegantly, then swiped her card to pay for the car in full.

“Ms. Shen is truly generous.”

After about 20 minutes, all the car insurance and documentation were completed. Lu Qin received a temporary license plate and was ready to drive off.

“Xiyao, let’s go,” he called to his daughter.

Handing the Porsche key back to Shen Muyan, he asked, “Will you drive your own car and follow us?”

Instead, she replied that she still needed to change her clothes at Lu Qin’s place. Casually tossing the Porsche key to the staff, she said, “Please call a driver for me.”

Lu Qin was momentarily perplexed.

“Huh?”

The Porsche was left at the dealership, with a hired driver scheduled to deliver it to Lu Qin’s place later. Meanwhile, Shen Muyan decided to fully enjoy the backseat Queen’s Seat.

The little one played a fruit-slicing game on the rear-seat tablet. Lu Qin activated the navigation and tried out the advanced intelligent driving feature.

He didn’t touch the steering wheel or pedals the entire way. The car drove itself like a seasoned driver, smoothly taking them all the way home.

_______

By the time they arrived, it was dinner time. Aromas from nearby kitchens wafted out through open windows.

“Stay for dinner before heading back,” Lu Qin suggested to Shen Muyan.

After all, she had paid for the car without batting an eye, inviting her for dinner was the least he could do.

The meal featured his signature dishes: twice-cooked pork, scrambled eggs with tomatoes, imperial chicken wings, and chicken bean curd. Though similar to what they had at lunch, these dishes never got old.

Shen Muyan devoured two large bowls of rice and let out a satisfied burp. After dinner, she changed out of the parent-child outfit and back into her original red fitted long dress. The dress, combined with her cold demeanor, exuded an air of regal elegance and sensuality.

“I’m heading out. Think about what I said regarding Ouyang Lin,” she said.

“Mommy, bye-bye! Don’t forget to miss me!” The little one called out cheerfully.

Crouching down, Shen Muyan planted a kiss on her daughter’s soft cheek.

“You kiss Mommy, too.”

“Mua~!”

Lu Qin’s gaze briefly swept across Shen Muyan’s exquisite, cool-toned face.

“You looked tired at the kindergarten today. Don’t overwork yourself—go home and rest.”

Stunned, Shen Muyan blinked. She had indeed planned to stop by the hotel, but how did he know she intended to work?

She gave him a glance, an odd feeling stirring in her chest, before getting into the Porsche and driving away.

_____

On her way home, as her slender fingers drummed lightly on the steering wheel, she found herself replaying Lu Qin’s parting words in her mind. Eventually, she changed her route and headed towards her villa instead of the hotel.

_____

At the villa, Shen Muyan’s friend, Fang Ya, had shown up. For dinner, Fang Ya had dined at Shi Wei Tian—a restaurant she used to find quite satisfying. But now, even her favorite foie gras-stuffed mushrooms left her indifferent.

“It’s definitely Lu Qin’s fault,” she muttered. “He’s spoiled my palate.”

When Shen Muyan arrived, Fang Ya greeted her with a playful, “Well, well, the mighty CEO is back!”

“And where did you come from?” Shen Muyan quipped, unlocking the door with her fingerprint.

Fang Ya followed her in, shutting the door behind her. She scanned her friend, dressed in the figure-hugging red dress, her long legs perfectly accentuated. Even as a woman, Fang Ya felt she might drool.

Clicking her tongue, she teased, “You pulled out that dress? Your intentions are as clear as day.”

“I don’t know what you’re talking about,” Shen Muyan replied coolly, retrieving two bottles of water from the fridge and tossing one to Fang Ya.

Fang Ya didn’t beat around the bush.

“When are you two planning to remarry?”

“I don’t understand what you’re talking about.”

Ignoring her friend’s feigned ignorance, Fang Ya continued, “Just hurry up, okay? My happiness in the latter half of my life depends on you two!”

Raising an eyebrow, Shen Muyan replied, “I don’t understand what you’re talking about.”

“Get remarried already! Let Lu Qin move back here. That way, I can come over every day to mooch off his cooking. Right now, I can only eat his food—everything else makes me cough!”

“There aren’t enough bowls and chopsticks in the house.”

“Cut the nonsense! Tell me, how far have you two gotten?”

“No progress. Today was just about attending the parent-child event. Don’t overthink it.”

“ Hmph! A parent-child event, and you wore that red dress!”

Shen Muyan: “…”

“And you even stayed the night!”

“That means nothing. I slept with the little one.”

Fang Ya squinted, thinking to herself, This stubborn friend of mine is hopeless. But seriously, she must regret not recognizing Lu Qin’s value earlier. Now she’s working so hard to win him back. Isn’t that exhausting?

Pulling out her phone, Fang Ya sent Shen Muyan a link.

“What’s this?”

“Tips on rekindling love. This site has tons of books. Take a look.”

“…Thanks, but no thanks.”

“I’m heading to bed,” Shen Muyan announced.

“Let’s sleep together! It’s been ages since we’ve had a sleepover. I’ll even teach you how to win back Xiyao’s dad.”

“Go to the guest room. I can’t deal with your snoring, belching, and teeth-grinding.”

Fang Ya widened her eyes. “When have I ever—”

“Oh, my mistake. It’s just the belching and teeth-grinding I can’t stand.”

Fang Ya: “…”

_____

After her shower, Shen Muyan lay on the wide mattress in her beige silk nightgown. She picked up her phone and sent a message to Lu Qin.

“Are you still going out to run your stall tonight?”

A minute passed with no response. He was probably busy—likely preparing for his stall.

“So he told me not to work, but he’s still running his stall,” she muttered, pouting slightly. Sliding through her phone, she tapped on the link Fang Ya had sent.

The first book that popped up was titled After Divorce, My Ex-Husband Begged Me to Reconcile.

Shen Muyan was speechless.

_____

Meanwhile, Lu Qin was in the kitchen preparing food for his stall. With practiced efficiency, he made 150 servings of crystal cakes, 200 cups of green bean smoothie, and 250 servings of crystal shrimp dumplings.

Tonight, he decided to skip stir-fried noodles and focus on ready-made dishes for quick sales. He planned to sell out and head home to rest early.

After loading the food into a refrigerated container in the trunk of his new car, he notified his eager WeChat group.

The group, ravenous and desperate, erupted into cheers.

chapters.title: Chapter 106
chapters.content:
Chapter 106 I’ve Spent Half My Monthly Salary Here, and You Just Bought This Car?

At Jiangcheng, on the long Lin Road at night, the traffic surged, with cars and traffic lights intertwining like a giant dragon weaving through the city’s streets.

Dynamic lights refracted into colorful reflections, like flowing stars, giving the city a gentle and romantic atmosphere. Inside the car, the air conditioning was on, accompanied by a vanilla-scented fragrance system the little one loved. It was so comfortable, like a mobile home. The little one held a cartoon cup, sitting in the passenger seat and watching cartoons on a tablet.

Lu Qin felt that having a car was indeed very convenient and comfortable. In the congested section of the road, alternating between the accelerator and brake, he turned on smart driving, freeing his feet. The car drove itself, with smooth starts and stops, reacting quickly—better than an experienced driver.

At present, AUTO leads other manufacturers in smart driving technology, even surpassing TSL. This was no exaggeration. When the brand-new, latest top-spec black Wenjie M7 stopped by the roadside, passersby couldn’t help but glance back.

New energy vehicles had just entered the market, they could be seen but were not yet common. Its atmospheric appearance, cool lights, and sporty 21-inch large wheels made it a striking sight on the night market street, drawing people to stop and admire.

“Is this a domestic new energy car? It looks great! If I had money, I’d buy one too!”

“Wow, HW is amazing! This car is so eye-catching!”

“This car is fully equipped, even with air suspension and advanced tech. For the same price, a Mercedes doesn’t even have an electric seat adjustment, and the rearview mirror is manual! Damn!”

“Now everyone’s buying electric cars, but my dumb wife got an electric BMW—so stupid! This car would’ve been perfect!”

“The owner has great taste, anyone would be tempted by this car!”

The pedestrian street was too crowded, making it difficult to drive in. Lu Qin got out of the car, ready to set up his stall right there.

When the onlookers saw him get out of the car, they couldn’t help but exclaim, “This guy is really handsome!”

“A handsome man with a beautiful car—it’s a perfect match. He could be a spokesperson!”

“This guy is so good-looking, even at my peak, I’d have to step aside.”

When Lu Xiyao climbed out of the car, all eyes turned to her as well. The little one also amazed the crowd.

“She’s too adorable!”

“Damn! Handsome dad, cute daughter, and a dream car. What a life!”

The onlookers were filled with envy. If Shen Muyan were here, someone might’ve died of jealousy on the spot.

Lu Qin took out his phone, opened the group chat, and tagged everyone.

Messages rolled in immediately.

“Why can’t it get in? It always could before!”

“The narrow alley spot is ready. Other stalls moved 200 meters away—why change locations?”

“Is the tricycle out of power? Damn, hurry over!”

“Run, run! Let’s go quickly, I’m starving!”

Lu Qin opened the trunk, unloaded two folding tableboards, and set them up.

“Dad, let me help~!”

Lu Xiyao obediently stood beside him, eager to assist.

“Okay.”

Lu Qin smiled and placed a thermal lockbox on the ground. Father and daughter worked together to arrange the packed food boxes on the table. The onlookers finally realized what they were there for—they were setting up a stall!

Times had truly changed. People used to wear straw shoes and carry poles to set up stalls. Now, they drove flashy new cars to do it.

Was this a wealthy family taking their daughter out for a life experience, or did they make so much money from the stall that they bought the car?

However, judging by the young man’s skilled movements, it didn’t seem like he was just experiencing life—he was a pro.

Hiss—!

At this thought, the onlookers couldn’t help but take a deep breath. Was setting up stalls this profitable now? Enough to buy a car worth over 300,000 yuan!

But why bring the car here to set up a stall? Was it showing off?

The license plate wasn’t even on yet!

Before long, a group of customers arrived in a rush.

“Where is it? Where is it? I’m starving!”

“I only had noodles for lunch and skipped dinner. I’ve been waiting for this meal all day—almost had hypoglycemia!”

“My long-awaited fried noodles! I’m so excited, I wasn’t even this thrilled when my wife gave birth!”

The onlookers were startled. Where did these 40 or 50 people come from?

“There! I see Boss Lu, that handsome guy!”

“I see his adorable kid too. Let’s line up!”

“Watch your step! Someone stepped on my shoe!”

“Close your mouths when you run—your spit is flying everywhere!”

The food boxes were neatly arranged on the table, with a QR code in front. Lu Qin and Lu Xiyao sat calmly on two small stools, waiting for customers to come over.

“Dad, the customers are rushing over, they look like they’re about to eat someone~!”

“Your meat probably smells delicious.”

“No, my meat is sour—not tasty at all~!”

The father and daughter joked as the crowd of 40 or 50 people lined up, panting heavily.

The onlookers were stunned. A large group of people buying from this stall?

Some passersby watched with curiosity.

“Just wait, when they see that new car, how will they feel?”

“I’d feel so sour! My money might’ve gone toward that car!”

“The young man needs to keep a low profile. Driving a car worth over 300,000 yuan to set up a stall—isn’t that showing off?”

“Will there still be business?”

The customers also noticed the new car, its open trunk holding a large storage box.

“Wow, Boss Lu, a new car? Great taste! I bought an M5 last year!”

“No wonder you couldn’t get in. I thought it was the tricycle! Driving a car, can you start earlier? I can’t wait!”

“Boss Lu! A new car means we should celebrate—how about a special promotion?”

“It’s just a car. What’s the big deal? Hurry up with my fried noodles; I’m starving!”

By selling pre-packaged food, the customers didn’t have to wait long. Some opened their boxes and started eating nearby.

One middle-aged man, enjoying shrimp dumplings with a satisfied expression, said to Lu Qin, “Boss Lu, this car is only worth 300,000? That’s too low. At least get one for 500,000 and fully load it!”

“This car should be the top spec. Fully loaded. Domestic cars are high value for money!”

“I still think it doesn’t match Boss Lu. Go for a Bentley!”

“Boss Lu, this isn’t fair. I’ve spent half my salary here, and you just bought this car? You’re letting me down!”

“Exactly! This car is too cheap. Don’t tell people, it’s embarrassing for us!”

What?

The onlookers were dumbfounded by the customers’ strange complaints.

What was going on?

The merchant made money, yet the customers thought the car was too cheap?

chapters.title: Chapter 107
chapters.content:
Chapter 107 Master, Translate for Me—What the Hell Is Happiness?

Originally, the onlookers were there to enjoy the drama, but they quickly realized that the script was veering far from a logical path. Everyone was dumbfounded.

Wasn’t it said that watching others make money is more unbearable than losing money yourself?

The stall owner earned their money and bought a new car. Worse, he had the audacity to flaunt it in front of them. This kind of behavior was known as ‘rubbing salt in the wound’.

And yet, they weren’t jealous?

What kind of mental fortitude was this?

Even scarier were a few outspoken customers complaining that the owner’s car was too cheap!

They thought he should have at least bought a Bentley. A car worth over 300,000 yuan was an embarrassment?

The onlookers were left questioning reality, wondering if someone could splash cold water on them to wake them up. Meanwhile, over half of the food on the table had already been sold.

Initially, there were 40 to 50 customers. Later, the number exceeded 100. Customers from the north, east, and west of the city were rushing over, praying fervently that the food wouldn’t sell out too quickly.

Today, the stall only offered limited items—no fried noodles or twice-cooked pork. The efficiency was astounding. Within ten minutes, every customer had their food.

As they ate, they hummed with joy, their faces brimming with satisfaction, even their eyebrows dancing with delight.

Master, translate for me—What the hell is happiness?

If not for the purchase limit, all the food would have sold out within 30 minutes. Hesitating for even 0.1 seconds was considered disrespecting one’s stomach.

However, since the menu was limited to items like bingjinggao (crystal cake) and shuijing xiajiao (crystal shrimp dumplings), it was more of a snack than a meal. It was delicious, yes, but not filling. Customers’ mouths weren’t even satisfied, let alone their stomachs.

To make matters worse, there was a purchase limit!

Despite their frustration, customers understood the logic. Without the limit, some greedy individuals would buy dozens, leaving others empty-handed.

The only solution was to enforce the limit, ensuring everyone got a taste. Lu Qin, as always, was considerate and fair—a truly good person.

Eating food made by Lu Qin was addictive. Missing a day felt unbearable—stronger than any other craving. Some unsatisfied customers began calling reinforcements.

“Hey, bro! There’s a purchase limit. It’s not like the supermarket selling toilet paper—it’s a food stall! Get over here now. I’ll pay, and you can have one shrimp dumpling. Just one, okay? No more!”

“Hello? Uncle! Auntie’s there too? And my cousin? Bring everyone to the night market at University Town. I’ll treat you to some spicy hotpot, but first, help me buy some snacks!”

A smart customer approached a stunned onlooker.

“Hey, buddy, I’ll give you 10 yuan to buy me some food from that stall. One of everything.”

“Really? You’ll give me 10 yuan?”

“Of course.”

The savvy customer immediately scanned the onlooker’s QR code and transferred the money.

“Is the food at that stall really so good? And there’s a purchase limit?” The onlooker asked skeptically.

“It’s just okay, and it’s expensive. But I’m hungry and don’t want to bother.”

The onlooker glanced at the endless line of food stalls just 50 meters away, puzzled but still bought the items. The savvy customer, now with the food in hand, started eating right away.

The onlooker, both skeptical and curious, couldn’t help but swallow as they noticed how exquisite the food looked.

“Not tasty? Then why do those dumplings and cakes look so fancy? They must be good.”

The savvy customer, cheeks full of food, shook his head vigorously.

“I’m just hungry. Honestly, they’re not that great.”

The onlooker rolled his eyes, thinking, You’re full of it. Your eyebrows are practically dancing, and you’re telling me it’s not delicious?

When the onlooker went back to buy more food, Lu Qin smiled politely,

“Apologies, sir, but you’ve already purchased your share. Each item is limited to one per person. Please come back next time!”

The onlooker was stunned.

Looking at the beautifully crafted food on the table, he felt an overwhelming urge to buy more.

Glancing at the satisfied customer, he couldn’t shake the feeling that he’d been played.

Meanwhile, the savvy customer, now full and satisfied, left the night market. Those who hadn’t eaten enough suddenly had an epiphany.

“Damn! Why didn’t I think of that?”

“Hey, sir, I’ll give you 5 yuan if you buy some food from that stall for me.”

“What? You’re not tricking me, are you?”

“Of course not! I’ll transfer the money now!”

The loyal regulars were cunning.

Today, Lu Qin hadn’t cooked fried noodles or twice-cooked pork, so passersby couldn’t smell the enticing aromas and didn’t know how delicious the food was.

If they found out, they’d go crazy, and the line would double in length the next day. For the regulars, keeping Lu Qin’s culinary masterpieces a secret was a priority. The fewer people who knew, the better.

“Miss, could you help me buy some food from that stall? My legs hurt.”

“Auntie, I’ll give you 5 yuan—”

“Make it 20. That guy over there paid me 15 for the same thing. Do you think I’ll settle for less?”

“Wait! Fine, 20 it is. Just go get it!”

The new onlookers were utterly confused, wondering why people were paying others to buy food just a few meters away. Everyone claimed their legs hurt or had trouble walking, yet no one was using crutches or wheelchairs.

Finally, one honest bystander exposed the truth.

“You regulars are too sly! When asked, you all said the food wasn’t good. You didn’t even blink while lying!”

“Everyone, don’t help them buy! If you do, you won’t get any yourself!”

When asked if the food was really that good, another exclaimed, “I swear on everything—it’s out of this world! If it’s not, you can come back and hit me!”

Hearing this, the new crowd immediately stopped helping and rushed to buy their own. The regulars gritted their teeth in frustration but had no choice but to start calling for reinforcements online. However, reinforcements took time, and by the time they arrived, Lu Qin’s food might already be sold out.

chapters.title: Chapter 108
chapters.content:
Chapter 108 There’s No Guidance Here—Only Screams and Cries!

Is it really that delicious?

Skeptical passersby hesitantly approached Lu Qin’s stall to buy food.

To the loyal regulars, these slow-moving newcomers were unbelievably lucky. On a normal day, when Lu Qin sold fried noodles or twice-cooked pork, the wait time would stretch just enough for the overwhelming wave of customers from the group chat to arrive. By then, these clueless newcomers wouldn’t even catch a whiff of the food, let alone get their hands on it.

Some newcomers earned a few dozen yuan by acting as proxies for others. The regulars sneered, thinking, If only they knew that Lu Qin’s food is a rare delicacy, worth every penny and then some. You can’t even buy it with money most of the time.

Many who managed to buy something eagerly opened their boxes.

“Mama mia, what is this divine crystal cake? The exquisite taste dances on my tongue, melting my taste buds!”

One bite of a shuijing xiajiao (crystal shrimp dumpling) nearly made someone bite their tongue.

Inserting a straw into the ludou sha (mung bean smoothie), they took a sip.

“Ahhh! For 3.5 yuan a cup, it beats a 20-yuan milk tea any day!”

Finally, they understood why people were paying others to buy the food—it simply wasn’t enough!

Some onlookers hesitated and asked those who had already eaten.

“How is it? Is it good?”

“It’s so good, I nearly swallowed my tongue with this crystal cake!”

“I eat late-night snacks daily, and I’ve tried everything. But this stall’s crystal cake and shuijing xiajiao are the best—unbeatable! And the ludou sha? It’s the king of all drinks!”

“I swear, I’ll come here every day. Even Jesus couldn’t stop me!”

Those who had tasted the food looked ecstatic, their eyes shining. It seemed like these small delicacies had cured their lifelong ailments, leaving them rejuvenated.

“This is a hidden gem! Hurry and buy!”

“Quickly! Only a third of the food is left. The earlier crowd took most of it!”

As a group of passersby finally moved toward the stall, another wave of customers suddenly appeared from all directions.

“ Phew! Finally made it. I wouldn’t sleep tonight without eating something from Lu Qin’s stall!”

“Damn it, I overslept for a nap! Luckily, I’m still in time!”

“I ran so hard my slippers are at my calves! This is more intense than running the 1,000-meter during college entrance exams!”

“I drove here right after work and even ran a red light!”

These regulars were cursing under their breath, but their actions were swift. They dashed toward the stall, lined up neatly, and relied on a ‘first come, first served’ strategy.

The hesitant onlookers froze for a moment. In the blink of an eye, a line of 20 or 30 people had formed, with more joining. Those who realized too late scrambled to join the queue, while others, slower to react, found themselves at the end of the line. They craned their necks anxiously, watching the boxes of food on the table dwindle at an alarming rate.

Their hearts sank.

Will there be anything left when it’s my turn?

Meanwhile, those who managed to buy food wasted no time. They opened their boxes and devoured the contents ravenously, as if they hadn’t eaten in days. Their reactions left no doubt—this food was extraordinary.

The passersby who had earned a few yuan as proxies felt they had made a terrible mistake. For the sake of a small profit, they had missed out on the opportunity to taste these legendary delicacies. Swallowing their disappointment, they wandered off to find other food in the night market.

One particularly crafty individual even bought a pair of sunglasses from a nearby store and tried to queue again. When it was his turn, Lu Qin handed him a box of crystal cake. The man was about to pay when a soft, sweet voice interrupted him.

“Big brother, didn’t you already buy something earlier? You look familiar!”

Startled, the man stammered, “N-no, this is my first time. Little lady, you must be mistaken.”

“I remember you because of that mole. None of the other customers have it,” Lu Xiyao said confidently.

Lu Qin glanced at the man calmly and said, “If you’ve already purchased, please give others a chance. You can come back tomorrow.”

The man tried to argue, but the other customers weren’t having it.

“Look at you! Obviously, you’ve been here before. Get lost!”

“Don’t make me call the police!”

“Check your payment history if you dare!”

Faced with the threat of revealing his payment records, the man fled, muttering to himself. “Thank goodness for these sunglasses. My face wasn’t exposed. Never doing this again. Damn!”

Ten minutes later, the table was empty, save for a QR code sign and a pack of tissues.

Lu Qin’s tissues were free, but customers rarely used them.

Why?

Because they didn’t want to waste even the smallest bit of flavor! They licked the sauce clean off their lips—wasting it would be a crime.

The free mints were also wildly popular. Lu Qin placed them in a fruit dish each time, and they were always gone by the end of the night.

It wasn’t just that they were free. Even the mints were extraordinary—refreshing in a way that made brushing your teeth three times seem dull. After eating, customers could pop one in their mouths and kiss their partners confidently.

Customers even helped clean up, folding the table and storing it in Lu Qin’s car.

Some joked, “Lu Qin, if you sell more food daily, you’ll upgrade to a Bentley in three months!”

Meanwhile, Lu Qin’s group chat gained new members, swelling to over 800. Some weren’t even local, having joined via online referrals.

The regulars weren’t thrilled.

A newcomer tried to introduce themselves politely.

The response came swiftly.

chapters.title: Chapter 109
chapters.content:
Chapter 109 Why Am I Suddenly Labeled as the “Woman Who Abandoned Her Husband and Child”?

On the way home, Lu Qin activated the smart driving feature of his M7, relaxed his legs, and enjoyed the car’s comfort and quiet.

Lu Xiyao, munching on an apple given by a customer, happily swayed her head as she spoke.

“Daddy, we sold everything today! How about celebrating with an ice cream? Do you think a chocolate-flavored one would be good?”

Lu Qin, keeping his eyes on the road ahead, smiled and said, “I think you can dream about eating one at home.”

“Alright, but if I can’t eat ice cream in my dream, can I have two tomorrow since it’s Saturday?”

The little girl’s big eyes blinked adorably, her expression a mix of innocence and cleverness.

“You’re quite bold, daring to wish for two ice creams, huh?”

“Just one, then?”

Lu Xiyao’s little face now looked pitifully cute.

“Fine.”

“Daddy, you’re the best! Mwah~ !” Lu Xiyao cheerfully praised her father.

“Daddy, let me sing you a song.”

“Sure.”

Lu Xiyao skillfully navigated the car’s touchscreen, selecting a children’s song, “Planting the Sun”, from a music app.

“I have a beautiful wish~”

“When I grow up, I want to plant the sun~”

“Planting just one is enough~”

“It will produce many, many suns~”

…

They reached home at 8:45 PM. Lu Qin bathed the little one, dried her hair, and tucked her into bed. Within two minutes, she was fast asleep. He adjusted her blanket and quietly left the room.

Sitting on the sofa, Lu Qin poured himself a glass of lemon water and slowly sipped it. This lemon water was something Shen Muyan liked when she visited. Lu Qin and the little one usually drank plain water, while Shen Muyan only drank plain water when she had a cold. Otherwise, she preferred flavored water.

Ironically, before their divorce, there were times Lu Qin wouldn’t see Shen Muyan for days, as if her work had completely consumed her. Yet after the divorce, even though her work remained just as busy, her interactions with him and their child became more frequent. Today, they even attended a parent-child activity together.

Lu Qin thought she must have reflected after the divorce and realized she hadn’t spent enough time with the child before, now trying to make up for it. Fortunately, the little one had only missed a tiny bit of maternal love growing up. After all, Shen Muyan had always cared for her but lacked the time to accompany her. Lu Qin, however, compensated with an abundance of fatherly love, ensuring the little one grew up happily and healthily.

The little one had a lively, adorable personality and was bright, talented, and artistic. Even the star Li Xue and her manager praised her potential as a prodigy. Lu Qin felt proud knowing that Lu Xiyao was the kind of excellent child other parents admired.

Taking another sip of lemon water, Lu Qin checked his phone messages. There was a missed call from a woman named Chen Yue, who had once visited his food stall and insisted on adding him on WeChat. Lu Qin had added her using a secondary account, which he rarely checked.

He didn’t plan to reply, as he wasn’t considering delivery services for now, he could barely keep up with stall sales. He opened WeChat to find numerous unread messages.

From Shen Hongda.

Shen Hongda and his wife felt guilty about the divorce and rarely contacted Lu Qin directly, only offering support discreetly.

Upon learning from Chen Dahai that Lu Qin’s food stall was earning millions annually with a steady stream of loyal customers, they felt relieved and proud.

Look at our former son-in-law—impressive, wasn’t he?

However, they often admonished Shen Muyan.

“Why on earth did you divorce him? Do you regret it now?”

“Where can you find such a good man again?”

Shen Muyan found their remarks exasperating.

It wasn’t as if she unilaterally decided to divorce. It was a mutual, carefully considered decision.

Why does everyone think I’m the villain who abandoned my husband and child? Even Dou E wasn’t this wronged!

Lu Qin felt touched by Shen Hongda’s concern and replied, “Thank you. I’ll take care of myself, and you two take care as well. I’ll bring Xiaoyao over this weekend.”

Coincidentally, both Fang Ya and Li Xue messaged him, each sending the same ‘hungry’ emoji. They were friends of Lu Qin’s and fond of the little one. If they visited his stall, he wouldn’t charge them, and if they came over for meals, they were always welcome. The little one was delighted to see them, considering one her godmother and the other her idol.

Lu Qin didn’t reply to them and continued scrolling until he saw Shen Muyan’s WeChat profile picture. He paused—it had changed.

Previously a green landscape, it was now…

He tapped on it.

It was a photo taken today during the parent-child activity at the kindergarten. In the picture, Shen Muyan and the little one were playing an interactive game. She tossed a balloon behind her, while the blindfolded little one held a plastic bucket, trying to catch it as Lu Qin directed them.

All three wore matching bear-themed parent-child outfits. Under the sunlight, their faces glowed with a golden hue, their smiles more radiant than fireworks.

The photo was candid yet perfectly framed and beautifully captured, exuding a high level of artistry. Lu Qin wasn’t sure if it was the photographer’s skill or a natural moment of harmony, but he liked the photo and thought it would look great in a frame.

At around 7 PM, Shen Muyan sent him a message.

Lu Qin replied.

Her response came within seconds.

He joked lightly.

Having spent the whole day together for the parent-child activity, their conversation flowed more easily.

chapters.title: Chapter 110
chapters.content:
Chapter 110 Lu Qin Was Undeniably Impressive, Achieving Financial Success at Such a Young Age!

Shen Muyan found Lu Qin’s joke about earning enough in three months to buy a Bentley entirely reasonable. Based on his current sales, the income from his food stall in that timeframe could indeed make it possible.

She had personally queued to buy his stir-fried noodles and witnessed how overwhelmingly popular his food stall was. Unlike ordinary vendors who might see customers trickling in every ten minutes, Lu Qin’s stall had a continuous stream of eager patrons, with the real concern being running out of stock rather than a lack of customers.

In fact, Lu Qin’s current income had already surpassed her own as the CEO of a corporation managing eight five-star hotels—excluding her stock dividends.

Shen Muyan brought up the matter of Ouyang Lin.

“What do you think about Ouyang Lin’s request?”

Ouyang Lin had promised an investment but hadn’t yet delivered. Shen Muyan knew this delay wasn’t due to unwillingness but was a tactic to pressure her into persuading Lu Qin to prepare the liver paste soup for his father’s birthday banquet.

However, her persuasion wasn’t motivated by the investment, as the contract between their hotel group and Shengyang Corporation ensured the funds would eventually arrive. Instead, she saw it as a significant opportunity for Lu Qin. Shengyang Corporation was one of Haishi’s top financial groups, and preparing the soup at the banquet would be a once-in-a-lifetime chance for any chef.

Not only would it enhance Lu Qin’s reputation, but Ouyang Lin would undoubtedly offer a generous red envelope in return. If she knew how to make the soup, Shen Muyan admitted she’d take on the task herself without hesitation.

After some thought, Lu Qin responded.

“If it’s next weekend, I can make a trip to Haishi. It’ll be a good chance to take the little one on a trip.”

Shen Muyan was delighted.

“Great, I’ll arrange for a car to pick you up.”

“You’re coming too?”

“Of course! I’m the contact person. Do you know how to find Shengyang Corporation or Ouyang Lin in Haishi?”

“Good point. I’ll wait for your call, then.”

“Alright.”

The conversation ended, leaving Shen Muyan in a good mood as she stretched and prepared for a night of beauty sleep.

______

Meanwhile, Lu Qin, after his shower, checked his system points. He had earned 600 points from selling 600 servings of food that day, bringing his total to 624. Not in the mood to use his points just yet, he decided to save them for later.

Lu Qin had already mastered many full-level culinary techniques. However, offering five or six types of dishes per day was sufficient for the stall. Expanding the menu would complicate the process, requiring more effort and diverse ingredients.

In the future, as he mastered more techniques, he considered rotating dishes daily—for example, selling iced jelly on Mondays and crystal shrimp dumplings on Tuesdays. For now, though, it was premature to implement such changes.

______

At 4:30 AM the next day, Lu Qin woke up refreshed. After checking on his sleeping daughter, who was cuddling her carrot-shaped pillow and humming softly in her dreams (likely enjoying imaginary food), he smiled and shook his head before heading out to buy ingredients.

Driving his car instead of his old three-wheeler made the task much more convenient. The vehicle’s spacious trunk easily accommodated his purchases.

When neighbors saw his new car, they couldn’t help but admire it.

“Lu Qin, you got a car? I didn’t realize food stalls were so profitable!”

“It’s not something just anyone can do. Old Liu from Block 16 has been selling skewers for seven years and only saved enough to buy his son a Volkswagen. Lu Qin achieved this in no time!”

“True. His cooking is incredible, and the customers just keep coming. If he doesn’t make money, who does?”

Some added snarky comments about those who had underestimated him.

“Remember Old He’s daughter? She looked down on Lu Qin because he ran a food stall. Bet she’s regretting it now!”

When someone remarked on the car’s price, others chimed in, guessing it cost around 350,000 yuan. While they didn’t believe Lu Qin earned that in just a few days, they agreed he must be making tens of thousands monthly.

_____

As Lu Qin chatted briefly with the neighbors, a woman handed him a box of pineapples for his daughter. Although he initially refused, her insistence made him accept with a promise to add extra toppings to her stir-fried noodles.

The car’s sleek features, like an automatic handle release and illuminated footboard, impressed onlookers, leading to more astonished murmurs.

_____

Nearby, Old He’s wife, Zeng Guiping, who had previously dismissed Lu Qin, spotted the car and inquired about its owner. Upon learning it was Lu Qin’s, her shock was palpable.

One neighbor gleefully showed her a TikTok video of Lu Qin’s bustling food stall, packed with customers, while other vendors’ stalls were nearly empty. Another noted he could earn several thousand yuan in just one day.

Zeng Guiping, realizing her earlier disdain had been misplaced, hurried off in a fluster.

chapters.title: Chapter 111
chapters.content:
Chapter 111 Wenwen, a Man as Good as This, You Must Seize the Opportunity!

Zeng Guiping rushed home in a hurry, almost dropping the cabbage in her hand. As soon as she entered the house, she didn’t even change her shoes and hurriedly knocked on her daughter’s door.

Old He came out of the bathroom.

“What’s the rush? Are you demolishing the house?”

He Huiwen, who hadn’t worked over the weekend and was still sleeping in, was woken up and looked annoyed.

“Mom, what are you doing?”

“Oh my dear daughter, Lu Qin bought a car, a new energy car, just got it, 350,000!”

“Which Lu Qin?”

He Huiwen rubbed her eyes.

Old He, surprised, asked, “The one who’s divorced, has a kid, and sells snacks? He bought a 350,000 car? Did he win the lottery?”

In the old neighborhood of the south city, the wealthy people have all moved to big houses in the city center, leaving mostly elderly people with little money. Forget about 300,000, even if someone buys a car worth 100,000, it would stir quite a commotion. The next day, at the old locust tree in the neighborhood, the ‘information squad’ would gather and spread the news.

He Huiwen, a little more awake now, had a shocked expression.

“He bought a car?”

“Of course!”

Zeng Guiping slapped her thigh.

“Old Ge and Wang Guifen said that Lu Qin sells fried rice noodles and shrimp dumplings at his food stall. He makes several thousand in a day!”

Old He didn’t believe it.

“How could that be? Old Liu, when his fried skewers business was booming, made only about 900 a day. Normally it’s only four or five hundred, and after deducting costs, it’s only a few thousand a month. Lu Qin can make that much in a day? That’s impossible! Fried rice noodles aren’t even as profitable as fried skewers!”

“You can’t argue with the fact that his business is doing well! Wang Guifen said he’s quite popular on Douyin!”

Zeng Guiping asked her daughter to search it up on Douyin.

When they searched, all three were dumbfounded. There were many videos on Douyin showing Lu Qin’s food stall, and they looked very authentic, without any commercial intent.

And the long line of people waiting for food couldn’t be fake. Lu Qin’s food stall had received many labels from local netizens and customers, such as ‘Divine Food Stall’, ‘The Pinnacle of Fried Rice Noodles’, ‘So Good It’s Worth Calling the Police’, and ‘True Crying Over Hunger for Kids’…

It even made it to trending once.

He Huiwen’s heart started racing. She didn’t expect that Lu Qin’s food stall could be this popular. Looking at it this way, it was possible he was making several thousand a day.

He Huiwen’s monthly salary was only 5,500. At that moment, she suddenly thought Lu Qin was handsome and capable, his daughter was sweet and carefree, and he was a perfect match for her.

Zeng Guiping, seeing her daughter’s expression, knew she was attracted, so she seized the opportunity.

“Wenwen, a man like this, you really need to seize the chance!”

“I know, Mom.”

Zeng Guiping nodded. Her daughter was quite pretty, and there were many suitors at her work, but she had high standards and didn’t show interest.

Now, Lu Qin not only looked good but was also making money. If he became popular on Douyin, he could be raking in money. This was a golden opportunity that could grow into a money tree in the future. They needed to make sure to seize it.

He Huiwen, thinking along the same lines as her mother, believed she had great assets—she had a bachelor’s degree, worked in a government job, and had many people pursuing her at school and at work. So, He Huiwen thought Lu Qin would be easy to handle.

She went to shower, did her makeup, picked out her best brand-name clothes that she only wore to parties, and finished off with a bit of perfume and a seductive lipstick. He Huiwen, arm-in-arm with her mother, went downstairs for a walk.

At the community activity center under the old locust tree, the ‘information squad’, led by Wang Guifen, was chatting away. Eight out of ten sentences were about Lu Qin.

“Hey, did you hear? A restaurant owner with a Mercedes came to see Lu Qin a few days ago and offered him a position as a head chef at the hotel with a salary of 500,000!”

“500,000? I never even dreamed of such a sum! But Lu Qin seems to have refused. Looks like he prefers to keep selling food.”

“Selling food gives him freedom, and it’s convenient to look after his little one. Plus, Lu Qin’s food stall income is probably no less than the salary of a head chef!”

“Exactly, Lu Qin is impressive. The food he makes is so delicious. Do you think there’s a secret recipe?”

At that moment, Wang Guifen noticed He Huiwen, dressed up and arm-in-arm with her mother, walking slowly past them.

This was rare. He Huiwen usually didn’t appear until around two or three in the afternoon on weekends. Today, she was out before seven, dressed up as if she had something planned.

“Hey, isn’t that Huiwen? Out early for a walk with your mom? The sun must be setting in the west today!” Wang Guifen said with a sarcastic tone.

The other aunties laughed and covered their mouths.

“I’m just out for a walk because the morning air is nice,” He Huiwen replied.

“Hehe, I’ve never seen anyone wearing high heels for a walk.”

Mother and daughter didn’t bother responding and were both eyeing the direction of Lu Qin’s rented house.

“Hey, what are you two looking at?”

Zeng Guiping nudged Wang Guifen with her elbow.

“Hey, has Lu Qin come back with the groceries yet? My daughter suddenly craves fried rice noodles.”

Wang Guifen rolled her eyes, thinking to herself, It’s not the fried rice noodles she wants, it’s obvious what’s going on in their minds…

“Mom, I saw the car. You guys talk, I’ll go ask him.”

He Huiwen’s eyes lit up when she saw the brand-new, eye-catching black SUV parked by the greenbelt.

At that moment, Lu Qin was making ice crystal cakes. He vaguely heard a knock on the door and quickly walked over. His daughter, Lu Xiyao, was still sleeping, and he didn’t want to disturb her.

He opened the door and saw He Huiwen’s smiling face.

“Are you looking for someone?”

“Hello, Mr. Lu, I’m Zeng Guiping’s daughter from Building 10. My mom said your fried rice noodles are particularly delicious, so I wanted to buy some for breakfast,” He Huiwen spoke softly, with a little flirtation, batting her eyes and giving him a coy look.

Lu Qin, unfazed, replied, “Sorry, I’m still preparing the ingredients. Come back in about half an hour and please line up at the entrance to Sunshine Primary School.”

Just as he was about to close the door, He Huiwen stepped forward.

“Mr. Lu, we’re neighbors, I don’t want to walk that far. Could you make one for me right now? Please~!”

Her tone was sweet, and she leaned in a little, letting him smell her alluring perfume.

“Sorry, I can’t right now,” Lu Qin said quickly, shutting the door.

He Huiwen froze for a moment, frowning. She thought, I’m such a beauty, and you just turned me down so bluntly? Are you blind?

Just then, the gate opened. He Huiwen’s heart skipped a beat.

“See, I knew you wouldn’t be able to resist me.”

But all she saw was Lu Qin’s indifferent face as he said, “Sorry, this is private time. My daughter is sleeping. Please don’t knock again, it will disturb her.”

He Huiwen stared at the closed gate, her mind buzzing. Wang Guifen saw He Huiwen walk back with a bad expression and couldn’t help but sneer, glancing at Zeng Guiping, thinking, That’s a matter of upbringing. Who goes knocking on someone’s door so early on the weekend? Is that polite?

Zeng Guiping pulled He Huiwen over and asked, “Wenwen, what happened?”

He Huiwen glanced at Wang Guifen, who was looking smug, and said, “He said his daughter was sleeping, and it wasn’t convenient, so I should come back later.”

He Huiwen couldn’t admit that she had been turned down. She made up a lie instead. Zeng Guiping let out a sigh of relief and nodded.

“It is a bit early now. You shouldn’t disturb him. Let’s wait until later.”

Wang Guifen didn’t miss the situation. It seemed like Lu Qin directly rejected her with a slammed door!

At that moment, a white Maserati Quattroporte stopped abruptly in front of Lu Qin’s house. Everyone’s attention was immediately drawn to it.

chapters.title: Chapter 112
chapters.content:
Chapter 112 Shen Muyan Came to Mooch Breakfast

A Maserati Quattroporte, a luxury car worth nearly 2 million, appeared at the gate of Lu Qin’s small yard. It immediately caused a stir in the neighborhood’s ‘information squad’. Most people didn’t recognize the Maserati logo, but they could tell it was a very expensive car.

Zeng Guiping and He Huiwen, a mother and daughter duo, both had sharp eyes and recognized the trident logo. Moreover, it was a sports sedan, surely worth over a million. The mother and daughter were first stunned, then puzzled, wondering if the Maserati owner had come to find Lu Qin.

Then, they saw the car door open, and a tall, elegant woman with beautiful features stepped out. She was dressed in a high-end black business suit, her black hair long and straight like ink. The woman had exquisite, stunning features, with skin as pale and flawless as snow, exuding both coldness and elegance.

He Huiwen looked at the woman’s makeup-free but captivating face and felt self-conscious, especially when she saw the woman’s wrist adorned with an extremely valuable Patek Philippe watch. She couldn’t help but clench her fists.

At the same time, she thought to herself, Could this gorgeous, wealthy woman have any connection with Lu Qin?

Then, the woman didn’t knock on the door but took out her phone and made a call. Soon, the iron gate of the small yard opened, and Lu Qin naturally let the woman in.

He Huiwen’s heart was filled with a mix of emotions. Just a moment ago, Lu Qin had told her that the child was sleeping and shouldn’t be disturbed, but now he was letting another woman in. This indicated that their relationship was more than ordinary.

At first, He Huiwen thought that she might be a good match for Lu Qin, but now, seeing the woman who was clearly more suited for him, she felt unworthy. Looking at the man she had set her sights on, she knew she couldn’t have him. It made her feel miserable.

Zeng Guiping also looked very displeased. He never expected that Lu Qin already had a woman, and that this woman was superior to his daughter in every way. It was clear that there was no hope for his daughter.

He regretted not acting sooner and letting his daughter make a move earlier. These days, men were in high demand, especially those who could cook.

Wang Guifen looked at the faces of Zeng Guiping and his daughter, who looked as if they had constipation, and felt secretly pleased. Other neighborhood ladies shared the same feeling of satisfaction, thinking that Zeng Guiping’s daughter wanted to date Lu Qin, but it was a pipe dream.

The woman who had just arrived, looking like a goddess, was the one who was truly deserving of Lu Qin. They were a perfect match, a couple of talent and beauty.

So, the ‘information squad’ began chattering, saying things like, “Only such a woman deserves Lu Qin,” “I’ve never seen such a beautiful woman, she’s like a celebrity,” “She doesn’t even wear makeup, but her skin is as smooth as white jade, so pretty…”

Zeng Guiping and He Huiwen heard all of this, their faces turning dark and green, their hearts pricked as if by needles. They couldn’t bear to listen anymore and hurriedly left.

Shen Muyan’s visit surprised Lu Qin a little.

“What are you doing here?” He asked.

Shen Muyan was dressed formally and grandly.

She explained, “I have an important hotel catering meeting today, and I came to have breakfast here so I wouldn’t starve during the meeting.”

For some reason, ever since attending a parent-child event with Lu Qin the day before, Shen Muyan didn’t feel embarrassed about coming to mooch breakfast. If necessary, she would even pay.

Lu Qin smiled and said, “You treat my place like a breakfast shop?”

“A private breakfast shop, don’t worry, I won’t skimp on the payment,” Shen Muyan replied with a wealthy woman’s attitude.

Lu Qin didn’t mind Shen Muyan coming for breakfast. He thought the little one would be happy to see her mother.

“What time does the meeting start?” Lu Qin asked.

“9:30.”

“Alright, you go wake the little one up, and I’ll make breakfast.”

Lu Qin entered the kitchen and took five bamboo boards from the steamer. The translucent ice crystal cakes emerged, emitting a tempting aroma of rice cakes. The ice crystal cakes couldn’t be eaten just yet. They needed to cool down to develop their soft, sweet flavor.

Lu Qin then started cooking breakfast, putting the second pot of mung bean soup on the stove.

The crystal shrimp dumplings had already been steamed in a large basket, but Lu Qin first put a plate of them aside.

He ladled three bowls of mung bean soup and placed them in the fridge to cool. It was almost the start of summer, and the temperatures were rising. It was best to drink the soup slightly chilled in the morning to help open up the stomach.

Shen Muyan opened the door to the small bedroom, and the little one seemed to be feeling hot. Her white, soft feet kicked off the blanket, and she held a carrot pillow while mumbling something unintelligible.

Shen Muyan didn’t want to wake her up but knew breakfast was the most important meal, so she walked over and gently pinched the little one’s nose.

“Mm… Hoo…”

The little one made a noise, slowly waking up.

“Eh? Rich lady, what are you doing in my room?” The little one asked groggily.

“What did you call me?”

Shen Muyan’s face darkened.

“Aren’t you being a little too cheeky?”

“Rich lady! You bought Daddy a car yesterday,” the little one replied.

“…”

Shen Muyan flicked her forehead.

“Ah!” The little one cried out in pain, and her big eyes slowly became clearer.

“Mama, why did you flick my forehead?” She asked.

“You called me ‘rich lady.’”

Shen Muyan scolded.

“I… I wasn’t fully awake. I was still dreaming!”

“Even in your dream, you called me that. Now, you deserve the punishment,” Shen Muyan said and raised her hand again.

The little one hurriedly slid off the bed and ran barefoot to find her father. She found Lu Qin in the kitchen and immediately hugged his leg.

“Papa, mama wants to flick my forehead!” She cried.

“Well, you have to accept it. She’s a reasonable person, and you must have done something naughty again,” Lu Qin said with a smile as he continued cooking noodles.

The noodles boiled and were rinsed under cold water. Then, minced garlic was added to the pot, creating a sizzle and filling the air with a fragrant aroma.

Gulu~!

The little one’s attention was drawn to the food, and her stomach growled. Shen Muyan, who was also hungry, walked over and leaned against the kitchen sliding door, watching Lu Qin cook.

In the past, Shen Muyan had little appetite in the mornings. She usually just had a cup of coffee for breakfast and couldn’t even stomach a boiled egg. Over time, she gave up on breakfast.

Later, she developed a mild stomach condition, and the doctor advised her to make sure to eat breakfast. That’s when she started eating again, but only a little, as she still didn’t have much appetite. She couldn’t eat things like buns, fried dough sticks, or hot dogs, and could only manage porridge or some corn. It was more of a task than a pleasure.

But after tasting Lu Qin’s ice crystal cake, Shen Muyan’s taste buds opened up. She even enjoyed the fried rice noodles he made for breakfast, and her appetite greatly improved without feeling greasy.

“Yao Yao, go wash your face and brush your teeth, then have breakfast.”

“You also go sit at the dining table; the kitchen is too smoky.”

Lu Qin shooed both mother and daughter out, then closed the kitchen door and turned on the range hood. Breakfast was simple but aromatic chicken noodle soup, which tasted amazing. Both the beautiful mother and daughter ate a large bowl, even finishing the soup.

They also ate a whole plate of shrimp dumplings, about fifteen or sixteen pieces. When Shen Muyan left, Lu Qin gave her two boxes of ice crystal cakes, in case the meeting lasted too long and she got hungry.

Shen Muyan took the cakes, her beautiful eyes fixed on Lu Qin. How could there be no lunch provided for such a big meeting? Still, she felt warm inside. She took out her phone and sent Lu Qin a red envelope.

“I’m not eating for free, I’ve paid.”

Shen Muyan kissed Lu Qin’s daughter, Lu Xiyao, and the little one returned the kiss.

Then, they waved goodbye, and the Maserati Quattroporte drove out of the community.

“We should pack up too and go set up a stall. Today, let’s let Yao Yao be the little boss,” Lu Qin said.

“Little boss? Okay!”

The little one’s eyes instantly sparkled.

chapters.title: Chapter 113
chapters.content:
Chapter 113 Little Boss Lady! If Your Family Doesn’t Make Money, Who Will?

At the gate of Sunshine Primary School, Lu Qin arrived riding a three-wheeled food cart, causing a stir among the customers who had already lined up.

“Boss Lu is finally here! I was just about to starve!”

“So excited, this is my first time arriving early to queue. I can barely wait to eat!”

“Finally, it’s the weekend, and I can have Boss Lu’s breakfast. It’s so far to the north of the city!”

“I haven’t had the ice crystal cake for three days. I’m really craving it!”

…

Many parents of the elementary school children, who had tasted Lu Qin’s food before, came out today since their kids weren’t in school. The first three male customers quickly helped set up the stall, moving fast to prepare and enjoy their meal.

Lu Qin set up a table on the right side of the stall, placing green bean soup, ice crystal cake, and crystal shrimp dumplings out of the fresh-keeping box, neatly arranging them on the table.

“Today, Lu’s Food Stall has added a new mini stall. Please support the little boss’s business!”

Lu Qin smiled at the customers and winked at his daughter, Lu Xiyao, then set up a small stool behind the table.

The little girl’s eyes sparkled with excitement, and she confidently walked to the back of the table, straightened her little chest, and adopted the posture of a small boss. However, she was a cute and naive little boss.

“Dear customers, please queue up properly. The little boss is selling green bean soup, ice crystal cake, and crystal shrimp dumplings. Come buy them quickly if you want!”

Lu Xiyao’s soft and sweet voice rang out. The customers were initially stunned, then they all became amused.

“Oh my, with such a cute little boss, I definitely have to buy something!”

“Wow! She’s in kindergarten and already a little boss. Her future is limitless!”

“Let’s go, go buy something from the little boss first!”

“With such a beautiful little boss, I must support her!”

Thus, the queue split into two: one line for stir-fried noodles, the other for green bean soup and ice crystal cakes. Of course, the stir-fried noodles without green bean soup would lack flavor, and the crystal shrimp dumplings without stir-fried noodles would be incomplete.

After purchasing, the customers were expected to swap places with the other line.

“What would you like, uncle?”

“I’d like five boxes of ice crystal cakes, three orders of crystal shrimp dumplings, and five cups of green bean soup.”

“Uncle, you can only buy a maximum of two boxes of ice crystal cakes, three cups of green bean soup, and one order of crystal shrimp dumplings, right, Daddy?”

Lu Xiyao stretched out her little pinky finger to inform the customer. Lu Qin, throwing some bean sprouts into the pan, nodded at his daughter.

“Yes, the little boss is right.”

With confirmation, Lu Xiyao told the customer, “Uncle, you need to order again, your previous order was wrong.”

The customer laughed and said, “Alright, little boss, I’ll reorder…”

“Uncle, did you scan the payment yet?”

“How much is it?”

“The ice crystal cakes are 25 yuan per box, you’ve ordered two, the crystal shrimp dumplings are 28 yuan per order, you’ve ordered one, the green bean soup is 3.5 yuan per cup, you’ve ordered three…”

A kindergarten kid couldn’t add or subtract double-digit numbers, so the customer jokingly asked, “Little boss, do you need me to help you calculate?”

Lu Xiyao showed an expression of who do you think you’re talking to and pulled out a tablet from behind her stool.

“Xiao Yi, Xiao Yi, can you tell me how much is 28 yuan plus 25 yuan plus 25 yuan plus 3.5 yuan plus…?”

【88 yuan 5 jiao.】

“Uncle, please scan 88 yuan 5 jiao.”

Lu Xiyao looked up, her big, smart eyes sparkling with pride.

The customer was momentarily stunned, then exaggerated, “Wow, little boss, you’ve got Xiao Yi the accountant to help you calculate!”

“ Tsk, tsk, Lu Qin’s daughter is so smart!”

“Clever and cute!”

“Real ‘little boss!’”

“Looking at her more, I just want to take her home!”

“No one stop me! I’m taking the little boss home today!”

…

Seeing the customers complimenting his daughter, Lu Qin couldn’t help but smile happily. Lu Xiyao grabbed a plastic bag, opened it, and packed the items one by one, adding a pair of disposable chopsticks and two napkins before tying the bag. She was already an experienced packager.

The customer scanned the code and said, “Little boss, I’ve paid already.”

The customer stuffed their phone into their pocket, opened the crystal shrimp dumplings, and continued to the left side of the queue for stir-fried noodles.

After completing the first sale, the little boss felt a great sense of accomplishment.

“Next, next~!”

A middle-aged woman shook a lollipop.

“Little boss, want to eat a lollipop?”

“Thank you, auntie… um…”

Lu Xiyao’s eyes brightened when she saw the lollipop, but she suddenly remembered something. After a couple of seconds of blankness, she shook her head.

“No, I’m the little boss now, I don’t want a lollipop.”

“Little boss can’t eat lollipops?” The woman asked.

“Yes, lollipops are for little kids. I’m the little boss now, I’m different!”

“Really don’t want it?” The middle-aged woman continued tempting her. “Little boss can still eat lollipops, just ask your daddy.”

Lu Xiyao turned her head and looked at Lu Qin.

Lu Qin, stirring the pan, smiled.

“You’re the little boss, you decide.”

“Auntie, is it lychee flavor~?”

The middle-aged woman laughed so much that her wrinkles crinkled.

“It’s definitely lychee flavor!”

She bought 88.5 yuan worth of goods, bought as much as she could, and then went next door to buy stir-fried noodles.

With a lollipop in her mouth, the little boss continued her business happily.

“Brother, what would you like?”

The little girl was very polite, greeting everyone.

When she called out “Brother,” the young man felt a sense of comfort, as if it made him younger. The little one’s words were really sweet.

However, the young man continued teasing her.

“Little boss, can you give me a discount?”

Lu Xiyao quickly responded, frowning, “Brother, we run a small business, it’s hard to give discounts. Our food is so tasty, it’s worth the price~!”

“And my daddy gets up early to cook, it’s a lot of hard work!”

The customers couldn’t help but click their tongues.

“Ah! Boss Lu, your little boss is too good at business. If your family doesn’t get rich, who will?”

“Unbelievable, next time we’ll get your daughter a ‘little boss’ certificate to hang around her neck!”

“My naughty kid is still playing with mud, and Boss Lu’s daughter is already making money for the family!”

“This little boss is going viral! The headline is already ready—‘Children Shouldn’t Be Left Behind, Kindergarten Kid Starts a Business on the Weekend!’ Ha ha!”

“Little boss is going to be able to buy a go-kart tomorrow!”

Lu Qin just smiled.

As more and more regular customers came, the ice crystal cakes and green bean soup were quickly packed for fast sales, and a table was set up for customers to queue, scan the code, and pick up their orders. This saved time for both customers and himself.

Some customers didn’t want stir-fried noodles or didn’t have time to wait in a long line, so they could just grab their order from the self-service counter.

This also prevented customers from getting too hungry and fainting from low blood sugar. The little boss’s role was optional but meaningful, letting the little one experience business was quite fulfilling.

chapters.title: Chapter 114
chapters.content:
Chapter 114 Mr. Shen, Is This Tasty? It’s Not Very Good, Just Okay!

The meeting is held at Wangshan Lake Resort. The main attendees include leaders from the Economic Development Bureau, the Cultural and Tourism Bureau, members of the Jiangcheng Hotel Catering Association, and experts, as well as relevant persons in charge from various hotels and catering enterprises in Jiangcheng.

The main content of the meeting was to further understand the development status of the hotel and catering industry in Jiangcheng, provide face-to-face support to solve problems for businesses, and explore new ideas and initiatives for promoting innovation and development in the hotel and catering industry, stimulating market vitality, and promoting high-quality development in the wholesale, retail, accommodation, and catering sectors.

This kind of discussion meeting was held every year. Shen Muyan, who managed the five-star Ourei Hotel, was a leader in the industry, and sometimes she also gave speeches to share experiences and thoughts.

Most of the time, the meeting was rather dull. Shen Muyan’s phone vibrates, and she received a WeChat message from Lu Qin.

She opened it, it’s a video with a message.

Little Boss Lady?

Shen Muyan silenced the phone and, feeling curious, clicked the video.

In the video, Lu Xiyao was handing over a packed piece of crystal shrimp dumplings and says, “88 yuan and 50 cents, scan the code on the left, thank you.”

Of course, since the sound was off, Shen Muyan could not hear what the little one said, but she could roughly guess. Her originally cold face softened a bit, and her eyes brightened. So, she really was playing the little boss lady.

Seeing the little one’s skillful actions, her cute yet slightly crafty expression was just too amusing. Shen Muyan watched the video several times and then replied with one word.

She then forwarded the video to Shen Hongda and his wife, and Fang Ya. Soon, she receives replies.

Shen Muyan smiles slightly, feeling particularly happy. The meeting will last until two or three in the afternoon, and a simple buffet had been prepared for lunch.

They say it was simple, but there were many kinds of dishes, a mix of Chinese and Western, incredibly lavish. However, the CEOs of the companies attending were in the hotel and catering industry, and they had tasted all sorts of food.

What ordinary people would consider a luxurious buffet was, in their eyes, nothing special. But lunch was still a must, how could they attend the afternoon meeting without eating?

Sun Zhi, the CEO of Haoyue Hotel, and Fan Qingfang, the CEO of Shihua Crystal Restaurant, were friends of Shen Muyan, so the three sat together.

Sun Zhi chewed on his steak and complained, “I need to suggest to Old Hao from the resort that they should change the chef at the Western restaurant. This steak, I can’t chew it!”

Fan Qingfang ordered a portion of Gong Bao Chicken and Salt-Baked Cheese Australian Lobster and agreed.

“The meat in this Gong Bao Chicken is a bit tough. It’s not as good as the one in our own restaurant.”

For ordinary people, eating was about eating, but for rich people like Sun Zhi and Fan Qingfang, eating was about enjoying the food. So, their requirements for dishes were definitely higher.

Sun Zhi looked at Fan Qingfang.

“I heard you recently hired a Michelin one-star dessert chef from Spain who does molecular cuisine. He makes dishes that grow out of pots when boiling water is poured on them. Is that true?”

Fan Qingfang took a sip of coffee and smiled. “That’s one of the creative dishes, but when it’s coming from your mouth, it sounds so tacky, a dish growing out of a pot?”

Sun Zhi laughed.

“It’s just a pot! Well, when it comes to making money, it’s still you, Fan. Same vegetables, but with a different packaging, you charge people 2888 for it. Maybe I should get into molecular cuisine too.”

At this moment, Shen Muyan walked over with a bowl of wonton noodles and sat down.

“Shen, you’re just having a bowl of wonton noodles for lunch? But then again, the resort’s kitchen is so bad, you probably couldn’t find anything tasty,” Sun Zhi said.

Fan Qingfang smiled faintly.

“Shen, it’s been a while since you visited our Shihua Crystal Restaurant. If you have time, come try our new dishes.”

“Be careful, Shen. They’ll charge you 288 yuan for a mango pudding shaped like an egg yolk,” Sun Zhi said.

Fan Qingfang glared at Sun Zhi.

“The new dishes are free, no charge.”

Sun Zhi’s eyes immediately lit up.

“Free? Well, I’m in! I love getting things for free.”

“…”
