The wonton noodles were a bit hot, so Shen Muyan put them aside and opened the plastic bag she brought. Coldly, she said, “Fan, you’re talking about molecular cuisine, right? It’s quite popular lately. I’ll try it when I have time.”

Shen Muyan had always paid attention to trends in the catering industry.

Molecular cuisine was quite popular abroad, with reports all over, such as certain high-end restaurants offering molecular dishes and customers flocking to them.

Molecular cuisine was actually just a gimmick, like turning watermelon into beef or foie gras into fruit, focused on surprising or delighting the guests. The taste was subjective, some liked it, some didn’t.

Chefs put all their effort into creativity and presentation, not focusing on the flavor. When a chef didn’t research the taste of a dish, but focused on the art, it was problematic.

Molecular cuisine was expensive, ordinary people could not afford it, and rich people only tried it once because the novelty wore off. They were unlikely to return unless the taste was extraordinary.

But when the food was made to look so bizarre, how good could it taste? So, in the long run, molecular cuisine was destined not to become mainstream, and Shen Muyan wasn’t interested in it.

She opened the plastic box and took a piece of crystal cake, biting into it. The soft, sweet, and wonderful taste spread across her tongue. Indeed, the taste of food was the key.

Lu Qin’s crystal cake was something she could eat every day without getting bored. Fan Qingfang and Sun Zhi both lock their eyes on Shen Muyan’s plastic box.

“Hey, Shen, you brought your own food? What’s this? Looks like something bought from a street stall.”

Only street-side shops had such simple packaging. If it were from a dessert shop or café, it would be in a pretty box or bag.

Fan Qingfang stretched her neck to take a look.

“This looks like some kind of ice-skin cake. Shen, when did you start liking this kind of thing?”

Sun Zhi noticed the delicate look of the cake and asked, “Is this… tasty?”

Shen Muyan seriously shook her head.

“It’s not very tasty, just okay.”

She said this, but then picked up another piece and ate it.

Not tasty?

Sun Zhi squinted his eyes. The cake smelled like a light, elegant fragrance. If it was not tasty, why was she eating it so happily?

Fan Qingfang loved desserts, but she never ate from street-side small shops.

But… the packaging of Shen Muyan’s box looked nice, and she was a bit tempted to try it.

“This cake… what’s it called?”

“Ice Crystal Cake,” Shen Muyan replied, picked up a third piece and took a bite.

“Ice Crystal Cake? Never heard of it. Fan, have you heard of it?” Sun Zhi asked.

Fan Qingfang shook her head.

“Never heard of it. I’ve only heard of crystal cake.”

At this moment, both Sun Zhi and Fan Qingfang were staring intently at Shen Muyan’s ice crystal cake.

Usually, in such a situation, the host would politely ask, “Would you like to try it?”

But Shen Muyan didn’t feel like offering them a taste. There were eight pieces in a box, and the version Lu Qin gave her was the plus edition, with only five pieces in a box. Two boxes were just enough for her.

The bowl of wonton noodles didn’t taste that great, so she would just sip the soup. Two boxes of ice crystal cakes were her perfect lunch.

But…

Sun Zhi and Fan Qingfang were staring at the cakes and starting to swallow.

Shen Muyan helplessly said, “Sun, Fan, do you want to try some?”

“Yes!!”

“Yes!!”

chapters.title: Chapter 115
chapters.content:
Chapter 115 Are You Crazy? A Piece of Cake Costs 100,000!

Sun Zhi had ordered a steak that hurt his jaw while chewing, and Fan Qingfang’s salt-baked cheese Australian lobster didn’t taste good, with too strong a cheese flavor. Neither of them had a satisfying lunch and didn’t feel like trying anything else.

However, they noticed Shen Mu Yan enjoying a Chinese pastry called ‘Ice Crystal Cake’, which had simple packaging. She was eating it with great relish. Yet when asked, she claimed it tasted only “okay,” not particularly good.

Neither Sun Zhi nor Fan Qingfang was fooled. Shen Mu Yan, who could eat piece after piece of the cake, said it was “just okay”? They didn’t believe it.

Though the packaging was simple, the small 4 cm square pieces inside looked translucent and quite appealing. Fan Qingfang, who owned the Shihua Crystal Restaurant, specialized in exquisite private dishes and desserts. She could tell that, based on its appearance, the Ice Crystal Cake looked quite delicious. Sun Zhi also wanted to try.

Both of them stared at Shen Mu Yan’s pastry. Reluctantly, she pushed the opened box of Ice Crystal Cake towards the center of the table.

“Mr. Sun, Ms. Fan, you can only try one piece each.”

Sun Zhi blinked.

“When did you become so stingy, Shen? Fine, fine, I’ll just try a bite. Not that I care about it that much. I don’t really like sweets anyway.”

Fan Qingfang smiled.

“Shen, are you really that reluctant? Only one piece?”

Shen Mu Yan’s face was cold and aloof as she looked at them, her beautiful eyes unwavering. There was no way she would let them take more.

Sun Zhi and Fan Qingfang each took one piece. Shen Mu Yan immediately pulled the box back to herself.

Seeing this move, both Sun Zhi and Fan Qingfang exchanged glances. They thought, Could this Ice Crystal Cake be a gift from someone special to Shen Mu Yan? She’s acting like she’s guarding a treasure.

Sun Zhi and Fan Qingfang simultaneously popped the pieces into their mouths. The moment they bit into it, their eyes lit up.

This Ice Crystal Cake!

It was cool and soft, with a sweet, fragrant, and refreshing taste. It was slightly sweet and sticky but didn’t stick to the teeth. It had a refreshing ice cream-like texture and a rich rice flavor like sticky rice cakes. After a few chews, it released a unique woody fragrance with subtle notes of osmanthus and honey that lingered on the palate, leaving a lasting, delightful aftertaste.

Sun Zhi and Fan Qingfang’s faces were filled with surprise and wonder. It was delicious!

No, it was incredibly delicious!

Sun Zhi had claimed not to like sweets, but he usually took only a bite of desserts or cakes and then stopped. He disliked that cloying sweetness. But this Ice Crystal Cake was sweet without being overwhelming, with a complex and wonderful texture.

Eating it felt like being immersed in a flower field, with the refreshing sensation making the body feel light and filled with happiness. Fan Qingfang had tried many desserts, and whenever her restaurant launched new items, she would test them all. She could honestly say that this Ice Crystal Cake was better than any of the sweet treats in her restaurant.

“Shen, where did you get this Ice Crystal Cake?” She asked.

Sun Zhi had already forgotten his earlier words. He couldn’t resist reaching out.

“Shen, can I try another piece?”

It was so rare to taste something so delicious. He shamelessly asked for another piece.

“Didn’t I say? You can only try one piece.”

Shen Muyan gave him no face.

Fan Qingfang, being a woman and closer to Shen Muyan, also asked to try another piece but got the same result. Sun Zhi and Fan Qingfang were left in an awkward silence.

They had only one piece, but the lingering taste of the Ice Crystal Cake was driving their cravings wild. The desire to eat more couldn’t be controlled.

It felt like ants were crawling on their tongues and in their stomachs, and it was unbearable not to have more. Meanwhile, Shen Muyan finished her first box of Ice Crystal Cake and opened the second box. The cakes were crystal clear, like beautiful jade, exuding an irresistible allure.

Sun Zhi and Fan Qingfang kept swallowing their saliva. They wanted to eat it! But both of them knew that their pride couldn’t let them just snatch a piece.

What could they do?

They could only watch as Shen Mu Yan ate… one piece, two pieces, three pieces…

When there was only one piece left, Sun Zhi could no longer hold back. He was so desperate to eat it, he was overcome with cravings.

“Wait, Shen, I see you’re almost full. How about giving me that last piece?”

Sun Zhi reached out his hand.

“I’ll offer you 50,000. Sell me the last piece of Ice Crystal Cake!”

Shen Mu Yan was speechless.

Fan Qingfang immediately added, “I’ll offer 80,000.”

“100,000.”

“120,000.”

Sun Zhi glared at Fan Qingfang.

“Fan, are you serious? You’re willing to pay 120,000 for a piece of cake? Are you insane?”

Fan Qingfang: “I’m willing.”

Sun Zhi: “150,000.”

Fan Qingfang: “180,000.”

The restaurant had turned into an auction house. People around them, hearing the commotion, turned their heads.

“What’s going on? Why are Mr. Sun and Ms. Fan suddenly bidding?”

“They’re going to spend 180,000 on a piece of cake?”

“Holy crap! Are they crazy?”

“Are they really fighting over it or just trying to show off?”

Some people put down their chopsticks and stood up, gathering to watch the spectacle.

Shen Muyan, a little speechless, thought, They haven’t even gotten my approval, and they’re already bidding?

Noticing the crowd gathering, she quickly intervened.

“Alright, fine. You two are impossible. You can share the last piece, but each of you has to give me 100,000.”

“……”

Sun Zhi and Fan Qingfang looked at each other, then glanced at the onlookers. Their expressions became awkward. They would be spending 100,000 for half a piece of cake—wouldn’t people think they were just fools with too much money?

Sure enough, someone in the crowd said, “Mr. Sun, Ms. Fan, you’ve got money to spare? You’re going to spend 100,000 on a lousy piece of cake? You must be starving, right?”

The crowd burst into laughter.

Shen Muyan coldly replied, “Mr. Sun and Ms. Fan were just acting like children earlier. There’s no way they’d spend 100,000 on half a piece of cake. That was just an excuse.”

“I knew it!”

The crowd dispersed.

Sun Zhi and Fan Qingfang both sighed in relief. But they had to eat the last piece of Ice Crystal Cake. They couldn’t leave without it!

So, Fan Qingfang took a knife and fork and cut it in half, and they each ate a half. As soon as they tasted the delightful flavors again, their faces lit up with indescribable expressions.

“It’s so good!”

“It’s delicious!”

Both of them exaggeratedly licked their forks.

Shen Muyan reminded them.

“Don’t forget to transfer the money.”

“……”

chapters.title: Chapter 116
chapters.content:
Chapter 116 Add as Much Spice as Possible!

Shen Muyan received two notifications of transfers, each for 100,000 yuan. Her mood instantly improved.

She ate a wonton, then pulled out her phone to send a WeChat message to Lu Qin.

After a few seconds, Lu Qin replied.

Shen Muyan smiled faintly, her expression dazzling.

Sun Zhi and Fan Qingfang, seeing Shen Muyan smile so happily, felt even more uncomfortable. They had been impulsive earlier. Although they were wealthy, they weren’t used to spending money like that.

Spending 100,000 yuan on half a piece of ice crystal cake, along with earning the title of ‘big spender’, felt like a complete loss.

Still unsatisfied, they asked Shen Muyan about the origin of the ice crystal cake.

Who made it?

With such craftsmanship, was it a renowned pastry master?

But would a master chef be running a stall?

Was it a traditional craft passed down by an old artisan?

And where did this person sell their goods?

Sun Zhi and Fan Qingfang couldn’t help but get excited. Both of them were in the hotel and catering business. If they could hire the person who made these ice crystal cakes, wouldn’t they strike gold?

They could promote the ice crystal cake as their signature dish, selling it for 588 or even 888 yuan per portion. A money-making machine!

Shen Muyan gave them a group chat ID and told them to apply themselves.

“Don’t you have a phone number?” Sun Zhi asked.

As a prominent CEO, he had no patience for waiting to join a group chat.

“No,” Shen Muyan replied coolly. The meeting was about to resume, so the three of them headed to the conference hall.

During the meeting, Sun Zhi and Fan Qingfang repeatedly inquired about Lu Qin.

At the same time, they had their doubts. Shen Muyan was in the same industry as them, so why didn’t she bring the ice crystal cake maker into her Ourei Hotel? Was she giving up a fortune?

Elsewhere.

After lunch, Lu Xiyao took a nap in her room. Acting as a little boss all morning had been tiring yet thrilling. At just six years old, she was already handling business involving hundreds of boxes.

This little boss was the cutest in the business world and the most business-savvy in the world of cuteness—a true powerhouse. Since it was the weekend, Lu Qin had prepared more ingredients, selling over 500 portions of fried noodles alone.

Breakfast service lasted until just past 10:20, earning him 18,000 yuan. Sitting in the dining area, Lu Qin drank a glass of water and replied to Shen Muyan’s earlier message.

Though he didn’t know the details, he figured it must have involved a lot of wealthy people at that meeting—likely just them showing off.

At noon, Zhao, the disposable tableware store owner, stopped by to make a delivery and brought along a large batch of pre-ordered ingredients for Lu Qin.

The two of them moved the items to the yard together. Zhao was in a rush to his next stop and didn’t stay long. Before leaving, Zhao gave Lu Qin five kilos of boneless plums, saying they had just come into the market and were incredibly crisp and sweet. They were so popular the market was packed with people buying them.

Lu Qin tried to decline, but Zhao insisted, saying it was for the kid and didn’t cost much. Lu Qin had no choice but to accept. Next time he visited Zhao’s shop, he planned to bring some food in return.

After washing a few plums and trying one, Lu Qin found them refreshingly sweet with a hint of tartness. He finished his water and started prepping ingredients for the afternoon, which included three new dishes—twice-cooked pork, chicken tofu, and imperial chicken wings. These required extra time to prepare.

At 3:00 PM, the weather turned pleasant. The sun hid behind the clouds, and a gentle breeze provided cool comfort to those outside.

“Reporting for duty, Daddy! Little boss is ready to head out and start earning money anytime!”

The little one, refreshed from her nap, was full of energy. She stood obediently in the yard with her cartoon water bottle slung over her shoulder. Five plums were in her left pocket, three White Rabbit candies and a chocolate bar in her right—fully stocked and ready for action.

Lu Qin opened a street vending map app and searched for the best spot.

Lu Qin memorized the route, closed the app, and pocketed his phone. Then he hopped on his tricycle food cart and set off with his little boss in tow.

Meanwhile, on Douyin’s live-streaming platform, the hottest hourly broadcast was titled ‘Globe-Trotting Food Journey of a Wealthy South Korean Heir’ with 3 million views. The broadcast’s current location was Jiangcheng. This wealthy heir had previously been in Sweden, where he praised the local beef balls and Valenborg game dishes endlessly.

In his stream, he declared that his palate would suffer in China, as the country had no noteworthy food and couldn’t compare to South Korea. South Korean viewers flooded the chat in agreement, mocking that China couldn’t handle spice and even imported kimchi from Korea.

Chinese viewers were furious and flooded the live stream. The viewership soared to 5 million.

Comments poured in.

At Jiangcheng, Victory River Commercial Street, a young man strolled down the street with a swagger, wearing oversized sunglasses and flashy clothes. He was followed by an assistant, a cameraman, and four bodyguards in suits, gloves, and sunglasses.

Whether this ‘top South Korean heir’ persona or the bodyguards were real didn’t matter—the live stream’s theatrics were spot on. He approached a stall selling beef-stuffed scallion pancakes.

“Annyeonghaseyo! How much is this pancake?”

Through the assistant’s translation, he bought one for 15 yuan and took two bites.

“This pancake is too greasy, and the beef isn’t tender. Not very good,” he said, tossing the remainder to his bodyguards, who devoured it eagerly.

The live chat erupted in laughter.

Soon, he came across a stall selling grilled pig trotters with a long queue.

“People are lining up—this must be good,” he remarked.

The stall owner asked if he could handle spicy food.

“Make it as spicy as possible!” The heir boasted.

“I’m a spice champion in Korea. Chinese spice is no match for me!”

The stall owner silently grabbed two large handfuls of chili powder, coating the pig trotter inside and out. The heir’s eyelid twitched visibly.

chapters.title: Chapter 117
chapters.content:
Chapter 117 The Streamer Insists! So Many People Lining up Must Be Hired!

A bright red roasted pig’s trotters were handed to the second-generation rich young man.

“Here you go, your custom double-extra-spicy roasted pig’s trotters. Watch out for your stomach. Take care.”

Viewers in the Dragon Nation’s live stream laughed hysterically.

Stick Nation viewers in the live stream.

The Stick Nation streamer glanced at the chat, thinking, You all have no idea what you’re asking for while standing safe on the sidelines. But, since it was live, he had to grit his teeth and take a bite.

The Stick Nation streamer hesitated for seven or eight seconds, then bit down. Onlookers watched him eat the red pig’s trotters and felt the spiciness just from watching.

“Wow! Can this young man from Stick Nation handle spice like this?”

“His tolerance is insane! His stomach’s going to suffer tomorrow!”

After taking a small bite of the roasted pig’s trotter, the streamer’s face and neck visibly turned red.

“I’ve seen live streams asking for money, but this is risking your life!”

“…Not spicy… Hiss… Just mild! Hiss ha…”

Dragon Nation viewers.

Saying this, he tossed the pig’s trotter back to his bodyguard like a flicking snot. The red pig’s trotter, which even the bodyguard wouldn’t eat, was eventually thrown in the trash.

This rich Stick Nation streamer went on to eat spicy hot pot, spiced seafood, grilled gluten skewers, and other street foods. Honestly, in such a commercial street, some chain street snacks were average in taste, nothing special, but there were small shops that had been around for years with undeniably good flavors and daily lines of customers.

However, this Stick Nation rich kid would always find some excuse, saying one dish had bad texture and another lacked flavor. Eventually, Stick Nation viewers started mocking the stream.

Dragon Nation viewers couldn’t hold back anymore.

As he kept walking, a long line ahead caught the rich kid’s attention.

“ Whoa, whoa, whoa! What’s this stall? The line’s insane!”

His assistant hurried over to ask someone in line and returned.

“It seems to be selling fried rice noodles, some dessert called ice crystal cake, and stir-fried dishes like twice-cooked pork!”

“I must try this. Let me, the world-traveling foodie, evaluate if it’s worth the hype!” Rich kid said.

“ Whoa! The line is way too long. How long will it take?”

Stick Nation viewers.

Dragon Nation viewers.

The rich kid whispered to his assistant, who then tried to jump the line. The camera followed.

A young, handsome shop owner was stir-frying, flipping the rice noodles mid-air into a fluid-like shape before they landed back in the wok. The scene was mesmerizing.

Next to him was a small stand where a cute little girl was selling items as a mini shopkeeper, soft and adorable.

The live chat exploded.

Stick Nation female viewers.

Dragon Nation viewers felt the same.

The assistant spoke quietly in Mandarin to the shop owner, Lu Qin, suggesting they slip him a tip to cut the line. After all, lining up could take an hour—too long for a live stream.

Lu Qin kept packing fried noodles while shaking his head.

“Nope. Please queue like everyone else. If you’re in a hurry, you can grab ready-made ice crystal cake, chickpea flower cake, or imperial chicken wings and iced mung bean soup from the side stall.”

The assistant discreetly added a red bill. Before Lu Qin could respond, a sharp-eyed customer spotted it and got mad immediately.

“Hey, you! Do you see how big my fists are? Do you know how many queue-jumpers I take down every day?”

“Nice try! You dare jump the line here? We’ve been in this line long enough to turn feral. Smell the hostility in the air?”

“Paying to skip ahead? Flashing your wealth? I’ll get 5,000 coins from the bank and bury you under them!”

“Kid! Watch yourself walking home at night. Don’t let anyone put a sack over your head!”

Faced with one threat after another, the assistant shrank his neck, thinking, Are you customers or gangsters?

The assistant went back to the rich kid and said, “The owner’s tough as nails. No bribes accepted. Let’s just line up—it’s faster than the other places.”

The rich kid reluctantly joined the queue. The crowd’s enthusiasm was a good sign this place was worth a try.

Twenty minutes later, it was finally their turn. They bought one of each item.

A nearby customer kindly reminded them.

“You can buy two boxes of ice crystal cake and three cups of mung bean soup, by the way.”

The assistant translated this to the rich kid, who raised an eyebrow.

“Why would I buy more when I don’t even know if it’s good?”

The customer shook their head and said no more.

Live chat rolled on.

Stick Nation viewers.

Dragon Nation viewers.

…

chapters.title: Chapter 118
chapters.content:
Chapter 118 Chicken Without the Chicken, Tofu Without the Beans!

A few customers who had just bought fried rice noodles opened their lunch boxes, shoveling the food into their mouths while strolling through the street.

Noticing the Stick Nation streamer with his bodyguards, they found it amusing and began eating while watching the scene.

The rich aroma of the fried rice noodles wafted over, and the second-generation rich kid twitched his nose, turning his head towards the smell.

“Anni! Why does that fried rice noodle smell so good?”

His mouth started watering uncontrollably, and he felt an overwhelming craving. He had tried so many gourmet foods before, but none had ever made his mouth water just from the smell. It was strange!

He opened a box of Ice Crystal Cake—a delicately crafted dessert that immediately boosted his appetite.

He took a bite.

Ah-sibah!

He almost bit his tongue!

How could something be this delicious?

“What kind of cake is this?”

“So soft, sweet, and fragrant! It’s cool and refreshing, like eating ice cream!”

“Amazing and delicious!”

The rich kid blurted out without thinking, completely forgetting his original purpose—to feign dissatisfaction with all Dragon Nation cuisine, no matter how delicious.

Stick Nation viewers loved content where Dragon Nation food was belittled, and it was common for them to boost their own nation’s ego while tearing others down.

But as he savored the Ice Crystal Cake, memories of pastries from world-class patisseries in Paris came rushing back—croissants, vanilla mille-feuille, and other desserts that he once thought were divine.

Now, this simple-looking Ice Crystal Cake—a seemingly humble Dragon Nation dessert—was leagues better. The taste and texture were beyond anything he had ever experienced!

One piece, two pieces, three pieces…

After five pieces, he let out a satisfied sigh, savoring the lingering sweetness and coolness in his mouth.

It felt as if a summer breeze had washed over him, refreshing and uplifting. But then his assistant began signaling desperately. The plan was to criticize everything on camera, regardless of taste. The rich kid struggled internally.

He was supposed to trash talk about this food, but how could he lie about something this amazing?

He opened his mouth.

“Uh… This Ice Crystal Cake… Um, it’s sweet… a bit too sweet, yeah… It’s kind of cloying.”

Sweet foods being ‘too sweet’ was the perfect catch-all excuse for criticism.

But as the flavor lingered in his mouth, the rich kid knew he was lying through his teeth. The cake wasn’t cloying at all—it was perfectly balanced, rich yet light. He wanted more!

Just as he reached for another piece, his assistant, in front of the camera, grabbed the remaining cake and handed it to the four bodyguards, implying it was ‘too bad to eat’.

The rich kid shot a murderous glare at his assistant.

Ah-sibah! You’re too quick! This is delicious, and you just gave it to those four oafs?

The bodyguards, who usually preferred meat over desserts, reluctantly took a piece. As one of them bit in, his eyes widened behind his sunglasses, and his body shuddered with delight.

The viewers watched intently.

Stick Nation viewers.

The rich kid glanced at the chat, then at his assistant. With no choice, he lied.

Stick Nation viewers.

Dragon Nation viewers.

The rich kid turned his attention to the other dishes.

“What’s this?”

“Chicken Tofu Pudding,” the assistant awkwardly translated.

“Chicken Tofu Pudding? What a weird name.”

Intrigued, he opened the box.

Inside was a clear broth with a silky, tofu-like substance floating in it.

“Is this chicken or tofu?”

The rich kid had eaten tofu before—its soft, delicate texture could be sweet, savory, or spicy. But what was this?

He brought the box close to his nose. The broth had a light yet enticing aroma.

His assistant asked a middle-aged man in line nearby, “Excuse me, is this chicken or tofu?”

The man, noticing they were foreigners, smirked and replied cryptically, “Chicken you can’t see, tofu made without beans. Get it?”

Then he turned back to the line, leaving them baffled. The rich kid and his assistant were completely confused.

“Chicken you can’t see? Is that some sort of idiom? So is it chicken or not?”

Dragon Nation viewers.

The rich kid scooped a spoonful of the broth and took a sip. The clear, tea-colored soup entered his mouth. As he savored it, he felt like his soul was leaving his body. The seemingly simple broth was anything but ordinary.

It was light yet rich, subtle yet profound. He detected flavors of chicken, duck, pork ribs, and ham—all blending harmoniously into a symphony of taste.

Could it be true that extreme richness leads to simplicity? The rich kid was utterly stunned.

He had often criticized Dragon Nation food for show, but now he was humbled. A nation with over 5,000 years of culinary tradition was no joke.

He set aside his usual arrogant demeanor, replaced by genuine respect, and took another bite. The tofu-like substance was silky and smooth but carried the rich umami of chicken. It was an incredible combination of texture and flavor.

“This… is chicken!”

How could chicken be transformed into tofu-like perfection?

The rich kid, deep in thought, nodded and murmured, “I understand now.”

His assistant blinked in confusion.

“Understand what?”

“‘Chicken you can’t see, tofu made without beans.’ This level of culinary artistry isn’t something just anyone can achieve!”

chapters.title: Chapter 119
chapters.content:
Chapter 119 The Stiffest Mouth in Stick Nation Finally Softened!

The Stick Nation streamer’s previously unshakable ‘tough mouth’ finally gave way, leaving viewers in disbelief.

Dragon Nation viewers in the live stream were shocked.

Meanwhile, the streamer was busy licking the box clean. Not a single drop of broth was spared.

“Leaving even a drop of this Chicken Tofu Pudding would be disrespectful to the dish,” he declared.

“This Dragon Nation dish is truly extraordinary!”

His assistant looked like he’d seen a ghost, thinking, What’s going on? One bowl of tofu, and he’s switched sides?

Even the bodyguards had deserted the team, eagerly lining up to buy Ice Crystal Cake. A single street food stall had dismantled their entire live-streaming crew.

The assistant opened his mouth to protest, but before he could speak, a Imperial Chicken Wing was shoved into it. The rich kid had already finished one and was working on his second.

He’d thought the Chicken Tofu Pudding was the pinnacle, but this chicken wing was so delicious it brought tears to his eyes. Stick Nation’s primary way of cooking chicken wings was frying. But this was a revelation.

The assistant, gnawing on the chicken wing bone, shut his mouth and quietly enjoyed the flavors. It was delicious!

The meat was tender and flavorful, with a delightful texture and captivating aroma. Truth be told, this wing was ten times better than the fried chicken wings from Stick Nation. While fried chicken primarily relies on spicy sauce, this Imperial Chicken Wing showcased the natural freshness and tenderness of the meat.

There were hints of red wine and lychee in the aftertaste—a fascinating and complex flavor. Dragon Nation cuisine clearly had something special. The assistant, too, had now switched sides.

Stick Nation viewers in the live chat fell silent.

Was the food from this street stall really that good? They began to question themselves.

Dragon Nation viewers were equally stunned.

Ice Crystal Cake. Chicken Tofu Pudding. Imperial Chicken Wings. Ordinary street stalls didn’t sell dishes like these. The owner of this stall clearly wasn’t an ordinary person.

Now the streamer and his assistant had joined the queue, but the line stretched endlessly ahead. Suddenly, murmurs of disappointment rippled through the crowd—the Ice Crystal Cake, Chicken Tofu Pudding, and Chicken Wings were all sold out.

The little girl, finally free, happily licked her lollipop. The four bodyguards had managed to snag one last box of Ice Crystal Cake. It contained five pieces. After quickly devouring one piece each, the final piece became the subject of an impending brawl.

The streamer and his assistant attempted to claim the last piece, only to receive two swift punches from the bodyguards. They each ended up with a black eye.

At that moment, the earlier customer who had advised the streamer to buy more food strolled by, laden with bags of food. Spotting the two ‘pandas’, he whistled.

The streamer and assistant’s eyes lit up, staring hungrily at the customer’s haul.

After exchanging a glance, the assistant approached him.

“Sir, I’ll pay 500 yuan for your food.”

The customer replied casually, “Take it. It’s free.”

“Seriously? No joke?”

“You’re joking, right? These aren’t just food. They’re priceless.”

The streamer’s entire team resigned themselves to joining the queue to buy fried rice noodles. Though the line moved slowly, the rich aroma of fried rice noodles and twice-cooked pork kept their appetites raging. No matter the wait, they had to taste it.

After 40 agonizing minutes, it was finally their turn. The streamer eagerly stepped forward to order.

But Lu Qin, the stall owner, greeted them with a polite smile.

“Apologies, we’re sold out. Please come back tomorrow.”

“WHAT?”

Staring at the empty baskets, not even a single rice noodle left, the Stick Nation team was crushed.

The live chat exploded with laughter.

Dragon Nation viewers spammed in delight. However, this stall had clearly become the talk of the town.

“Jiangcheng is supposed to be a food desert, but this stall changes everything!”

“This legendary stall pops up, and even the world’s toughest critic folds!”

“I’m booking a trip to Jiangcheng tomorrow. Victory Road, right? Got it!”

“The owner isn’t just skilled—he’s good-looking too. Since when is street food this competitive?”

The streamer, now humbled, approached Lu Qin as he packed up.

The little girl, blinking her big clear eyes, asked, “Uncle, do you still need something?”

“Will you be here at the same time tomorrow?” The assistant asked.

Lu Qin replied, “Our location isn’t fixed, but you can join our chat group for updates.”

“Add us!”

Eagerly, they pulled out their phones, only to realize they didn’t have the Penguin app, as Stick Nation used their own communication apps like KakaoTalk.

Meanwhile, Lu Qin had finished packing up.

“If there’s nothing else, we’ll take our leave,” he said.

With his daughter perched on the tricycle cart, they rode off into the distance. That day, they had sold over 350 portions of twice-cooked pork. Many customers brought their own rice, devouring everything in sight. The stall’s popularity was unmatched.

The little girl’s eyes sparkled with curiosity.

“Daddy, what are we buying next?”

“Osmanthus Oolong,” he replied.

“Osmanthus Oolong? What’s that?”

“It’s a type of tea from Dragon Nation. You’ve been craving milk tea, right? Daddy will make you an Osmanthus Oolong drink.”

“Really! That sounds amazing! Daddy, you’re the best!”

She clapped her hands in excitement as the cart trundled down the road.

chapters.title: Chapter 120
chapters.content:
Chapter 120 Daddy Loves Me So Much! I Remembered Something Happy!

Lu Qin rode his trusty three-wheeled cart to Cuilu Tea Market, parking it carefully in a designated spot. He pulled out his phone and dialed a number.

“Hello, is this Mr. Li? This is Lu Qin, recommended by Mr. Zhao… Mingying Tea Pavilion? Great, I’ll head over now.”

Navigating the tea market required expertise—it was a place where you could easily get scammed without the right connections. Thankfully, Mr. Zhao had introduced Lu Qin to a trustworthy vendor.

Holding little Lu Xiyao’s hand, Lu Qin made his way to Mingying Tea Pavilion.

Mr. Li, the proprietor, was in his thirties and looked lean and approachable. After some pleasantries, he presented a fresh batch of Osmanthus Oolong tea.

“This is the finest batch,” he said. “Would you like to inspect it?”

“Of course,” Lu Qin replied. For someone who truly understood tea, inspecting the quality was a must.

Little Xiyao couldn’t contain her excitement, piping up, “Daddy’s going to make Osmanthus Oolong milk tea for me!”

Mr. Li chuckled.

“Your dad must love you very much!”

“Mm-hmm! My daddy loves me a lot!” She nodded confidently.

To humor her, Mr. Li handed her an orange, which she happily accepted. Mr. Li carefully unwrapped the tea and spread the leaves out on a bamboo tray, gesturing for Lu Qin to examine them.

High-quality Osmanthus Oolong is characterized by its fragrant aroma without visible flower residues. This is achieved through a meticulous infusion process where tea leaves and osmanthus flowers are layered together. The flowers impart their fragrance to the tea, which absorbs it fully over two cycles of infusion.

Lu Qin sniffed the leaves and rolled a few between his fingers. He nodded.

“The orchid aroma is exquisite. The base tea is authentic Tieguanyin from Xiping, Anxi—exceptional.”

Mr. Li raised an impressed eyebrow.

“You really know your tea!”

Lu Qin continued, “This strong osmanthus fragrance must come from thirty-year-old golden osmanthus trees—a rare and precious material.”

Mr. Li gave him a big thumbs-up.

“Mr. Lu, you’re quite the expert!”

Satisfied with the tea’s quality, Lu Qin said, “How much for ten pounds?”

Since Lu Qin was a friend of Zhao’s and clearly knowledgeable, Mr. Li offered the lowest price without hesitation.

“188 per pound.”

“Deal. Let’s keep working together long-term.”

After making the purchase, Lu Qin and Xiyao left the tea market.

As they walked, Xiyao peeled the orange and handed half to her dad.

“Daddy, let’s go home!”

Lu Qin smiled.

“You’re in a hurry to try my milk tea, aren’t you?”

The little girl giggled, “Daddy, you’re so smart!”

____

Meanwhile, Shen Muyan had just finished a meeting and was hungry. She craved Lu Qin’s fried rice noodles and crystal shrimp dumplings.

Trailing behind her, two executives—Sun Zhi and Fan Qingfang—tried to extend an invitation.

“Ms. Shen, care to join us for dinner at the Swarovski Crystal Restaurant?” Fan Qingfang asked warmly.

“No, I have other plans,” Shen Muyan replied coolly, ending the conversation.

Sun Zhi and Fan Qingfang had been captivated by the Ice Crystal Cake earlier that day. Both had applied to join the stall’s Penguin group, but their requests were still pending.

“Ms. Shen,” Sun Zhi asked anxiously, “Could you ask the group owner to approve us?”

Two high-powered executives, so desperate for street food, would certainly shock anyone who heard about it.

“I’ll see what I can do,” Shen Muyan replied. She took out her phone and messaged Lu Qin.

Lu Qin, who had just arrived home, checked his phone and typed back.

[Are they the ones who’d pay 200,000 yuan for an Ice Crystal Cake?] [Smart guess.]

Shen Muyan responded with a rare smile before pocketing her phone.

Moments later, both Sun Zhi and Fan Qingfang received notifications. They had been added to Lu Qin’s Foodie Group.

The group was muted, but the pinned announcement read.

[Tonight at 6:30, University Town Night Market on Changlin Road. Menu—Ice Crystal Cake, Crystal Shrimp Dumplings, Noble Consort Chicken Wings, Iced Mung Bean Soup, and a new addition—Osmanthus Oolong Drink (handcrafted, free from additives, and nourishing).]

The two executives exchanged thrilled looks. First, they noted the group had nearly 900 members—an astonishing number for a street vendor.

Second, the stall offered not just Ice Crystal Cake but a variety of gourmet dishes and drinks.

“This is insane!” Sun Zhi exclaimed. “I have an important dinner tonight, but I’m canceling it.”

Fan Qingfang added, “The line’s bound to be long. We should get there half an hour early!”

As they discussed logistics, Shen Muyan declined their evening invitation.

“I’m too hungry to wait.”

_____

Back in Lu Qin’s kitchen, little Xiyao peeked over the counter as her dad worked. Spotting a familiar chat icon on his phone, she grinned mischievously.

“Mommy!” She whispered to herself, noticing her dad’s relaxed expression while texting.

“What are you smiling about?” Lu Qin asked, noticing her little smirk.

“I just remembered something happy,” she replied cryptically.

Unbothered, Lu Qin continued cooking. He opened the steamer, releasing a burst of fragrant steam as he revealed a batch of crystal shrimp dumplings.

“Smells amazing!” Xiyao exclaimed, her tiny hands gripping the countertop.

Lu Qin carefully cooled one dumpling before feeding it to her.

“Careful, the filling is hot,” he warned.

Xiyao bit into it, her cheeks puffed out as she savored the delicious filling despite its heat.

“So good!”

Her eyes sparkled with joy as she declared for the umpteenth time.

“Daddy, your cooking is the best!”

Still, she eagerly anticipated the Osmanthus Oolong Milk Tea. She had always longed for milk tea when seeing others sipping it on the street, but Lu Qin had forbidden it, knowing how addictive the sugary, processed versions could be.

Now, with her dad promising a homemade version, her excitement was palpable.

Perched on a small stool, she waited patiently, looking like an adorable little ‘waiting bunny’.

chapters.title: Chapter 121
chapters.content:
Chapter 121 Are You Two Back Together?

At South City Old Street District, 5:40 PM, in the residential neighborhood, the kitchens of every household wafted the aroma of cooking, stirring the appetites of the hardworking people returning home.

At the entrance of Lu Qin’s small courtyard, He Huiwen was there again, encouraged by Zeng Guiping. She wanted to see if there was still a chance with Lu Qin—she didn’t want to miss the opportunity to be with him.

But in the eyes of others, she was clearly being persistent and unwilling to give up. From the courtyard came waves of enticing aromas, far richer and more appetizing than those from the surrounding households.

He Huiwen swallowed involuntarily, thinking to herself, Just for this man’s incredible cooking skills, he’s worth going all in for a second time. Spending the rest of my life eating delicious meals like this is enough to win me over.

Dressed in a long skirt, her appearance radiant and vibrant, He Huiwen hesitated for a moment before finally raising her hand to knock on the gate.

At that moment, a snow-white Maserati Quattroporte suddenly screeched to a halt in front of her, startling her.

Realizing what was happening, her heart sank. No way…

Shen Muyan stepped out of the car, her face cold and breathtakingly beautiful, her tall figure and perfect curves complemented by an air of elegance and detachment. Each of these traits made He Huiwen feel utterly inferior.

Shen Muyan glanced at her briefly, and that alone made He Huiwen lose her courage. Without uttering a word, she turned and walked away.

Shen Muyan paid no attention to He Huiwen. At this moment, the door opened. Lu Xiyao peeked out and saw Shen Muyan. She quickly opened the door wide.

“Mommy, you came just in time! Daddy just finished cooking dinner!”

“I timed it perfectly.”

Shen Muyan pinched the soft, tender cheek of the little girl and, holding her hand, walked familiarly into the house.

Lu Qin was bringing dishes out from the kitchen. He wore an apron with the words ‘Master Chef’ printed on it. His tall figure, defined facial features, and gentle contours gave off an ever-present warmth.

Shen Muyan couldn’t help but be momentarily mesmerized as she looked at him. He really was incredibly handsome when he was cooking.

“Mommy, are you staring because you think Daddy is handsome?”

The little girl’s big, clear eyes sparkled mischievously.

“A little,” Shen Muyan admitted, her cheeks faintly flushed, like a peach blossom blooming, delicate and captivating.

Lu Qin simply smiled, placing a dish of crispy duck on the dining table.

“Wash your hands and eat.”

Drawn by the enticing aroma, the mother and daughter quickly teamed up to wash their hands. Shen Muyan had also come to pick up Lu Xiyao today, Shen Hongda and his wife were missing their granddaughter dearly. Of course, she also came to enjoy the meal.

The little girl did miss her grandparents too, especially the ice cream treats. But more than that, she didn’t want to be apart from Lu Qin. After washing their hands, they sat at the table. There were already four dishes and a soup on the table—simple, homely dishes, yet they tasted far better than anything served at five-star hotels.

As Shen Muyan was scooping rice, her phone rang. It was Fang Ya on the line, sounding weak and listless as though she hadn’t eaten in three days. Fang Ya was calling to find a meal companion and asked Shen Muyan where she was.

Before Shen Muyan could reply, the little girl cheerfully called out, “Aunt Fang Ya!”

Fang Ya instantly perked up, her voice lively, “You’re at Lu Qin’s place, aren’t you?”

“No,” Shen Muyan said.

Of course, Fang Ya didn’t believe her—it was dinnertime, after all!

“You didn’t call me for such an important matter like dinner? I’ll be there in 15 minutes—save me a pair of chopsticks!”

With that, Fang Ya hurriedly hung up. Before the call ended, the sound of a door slamming heavily could be heard.

“Is Fang Ya coming over?” Lu Qin asked.

“Don’t wait for her. Let’s eat everything.” Shen Muyan replied.

“Let’s wait. It’s just 15 minutes,” Lu Qin said.

Shen Muyan shrugged.

“Alright.”

Then, she stuffed a piece of pork liver into the little glutton’s mouth.

Fifteen minutes later, Fang Ya arrived with a bottle of high-end red wine. She felt guilty for always freeloading meals and brought something as a gesture.

“I’m so hungry I could eat a cow!”

Lu Qin accepted the wine and said seriously, “Next time, don’t bring anything. We’re friends, it’s unnecessary and only creates distance.”

Shen Muyan rolled her eyes beautifully and remarked to Fang Ya.

“You’ve eaten and drunk for free at my hotel so many times, and I’ve never seen you bring even a soda.”

“You don’t need it—you’re rich,” Fang Ya said.

“…”

The little girl blinked her big eyes.

“Aunt Fang Ya, you don’t have to bring anything expensive, but you could bring ice cream.”

“Deal.”

“Thank you!” The little girl said.

And then…

Little Lu Xiyao received a gentle flick on the forehead as a reward.

“Ouch…”

The meal was devoured with the clinking and clattering of dishes. By the end, the plates were so clean they reflected light.

Fang Ya sighed contentedly, “That was amazing. I’m stuffed!”

The little girl chimed in, “That was amazing. I’m stuffed!”

Shen Muyan also sighed softly…

Fang Ya and the little girl both turned to her expectantly, waiting for her to follow suit. Shen Muyan, her expression cool, remained silent.

Lu Qin stood up.

“You guys go to the living room. I’ll wash the dishes.”

Shen Muyan: “I’ll wash.”

Fang Ya: “I’ll wash.”

The little girl: “I’ll wash.”

All three stood up at the same time, speaking in unison.

Lu Qin was speechless.

The three women wouldn’t let him wash the dishes, so they played rock-paper-scissors to decide. Fang Ya lost and went to the kitchen.

The little girl began massaging Lu Qin’s shoulders. Though he had been busy cooking all day, Lu Qin didn’t feel tired at all, thanks to the fatigue-relieving effect of his system. Still, the soft touch of the little girl’s hands was quite pleasant.

“That’s enough, Yao Yao. Your little hands will get sore if you keep going.”

Lu Qin’s broad shoulders were indeed tiring for her small hands. The little girl’s clear, innocent eyes sparkled as she said to Shen Muyan, “Mommy, my hands are too small. Can you massage Daddy’s shoulders for me?”

Lu Qin and Shen Muyan exchanged glances, then looked at the little girl before looking at each other again. Neither said anything.

After a few moments of silence, Shen Muyan suddenly stood up. Thinking there was nothing wrong with it—it was just a shoulder massage, nothing more—she calmly walked over. The little girl quickly rolled to the other end of the sofa, making room for her.

“I’m not very good at this. Tell me if it’s uncomfortable,” Shen Muyan said in a cool voice, a hint of softness in her eyes.

Lu Qin hummed in acknowledgment. He wanted to refuse but didn’t know how to. Her slender, fair fingertips touched the skin of his shoulders and neck, soft like silk.

To be honest, Shen Muyan’s technique wasn’t very effective. It wasn’t particularly comfortable, nor was it uncomfortable—more like an exercise in futility.

The little girl lay on the sofa, chin in her hands, watching joyfully as Shen Muyan massaged Lu Qin’s shoulders.

Ten minutes later, Fang Ya finished washing the dishes and earned 5 experience points. When she came into the living room and saw Shen Muyan sitting next to Lu Qin, her hands on his shoulders, Fang Ya’s eyes widened with excitement.

“You two… are back together?”

“?”

chapters.title: Chapter 122
chapters.content:
Chapter 122 Lu Qin is Making Milk Tea—How Should One Respond?

Fang Ya’s face lit up with excitement, as if this was the moment she had been waiting for all along.

For a few seconds, the atmosphere seemed to freeze. Shen Muyan withdrew her hand from Lu Qin’s shoulder, turned around, and sat properly, her expression calm and indifferent.

Lu Qin rubbed his nose awkwardly.

Lu Xiyao blinked her curious eyes and asked, “Big Sister Fang Ya, what are you talking about? My mommy and daddy have been sitting in the living room together this whole time. What do you mean ‘back together’?”

Fang Ya waved her hand, laughing.

“Sweetheart, don’t interrupt. What you mean by ‘together’ isn’t the same as what I mean.”

She then looked at Shen Muyan and Lu Qin, her face full of a knowing look that said, Just admit it already.

Shen Muyan, the formidable CEO, giving Lu Qin a shoulder massage?

Even before their divorce, such intimate gestures were rare between them.

Fang Ya could never figure out why these two had always been so polite and formal with each other—were they just friends?

“It’s just a shoulder massage. Don’t overthink it,” Shen Muyan said as she stood up.

“It’s getting late. I’ll take the little one back to the villa.”

Fang Ya clicked her tongue.

“Oh, come on. My eyes don’t lie. Whether I’m overthinking it or not, you both know the truth.”

Fang Ya pointed a finger at both Shen Muyan and Lu Qin like a playful referee but said no more.

If there’s progress, it’s a good thing, she thought, deciding to hold back and wait for more sweet moments to unfold.

Shen Muyan held Lu Xiyao’s little hand, preparing to leave.

“But I still want to drink Daddy’s Osmanthus Oolong Milk Tea,” the little girl said, her bright eyes brimming with anticipation, as if chanting silently— Daddy, milk tea, milk tea, milk tea…

“Osmanthus Oolong?”

The three adults’ eyes lit up, their faces full of excitement and anticipation.

Lu Qin’s mung bean smoothie had already proven itself leagues ahead of any milk tea on the market. Now that he was making milk tea, what could they do but surrender?

Lu Qin tousled the little girl’s hair and said, “You never forget anything about food and drink, do you?”

The little girl giggled, “Daddy, you always call me a foodie. In a foodie’s world, it’s all about eating and drinking!”

Lu Qin gently tapped her nose.

“You’ve got a whole set of excuses ready, huh?”

“Daddy, hurry up—milk tea, milk tea, milk tea…”

Fang Ya joined in, “Lu Qin, come on—milk tea, milk tea…”

Both turned to Shen Muyan with a look that said–Join the team.

But Shen Muyan ignored them.

“I don’t like milk tea. Can you make it with coffee?” She asked.

Fang Ya teased.

“Shen Muyan, how picky can you get? Milk tea is already good enough.”

Lu Xiyao chimed in, “Mommy, milk tea is sweet and delicious. Coffee is bitter.”

Lu Qin nodded.

“It’s possible to add coffee. I can also use milk, coconut milk, or rice wine. Osmanthus Oolong can be customized with many flavors.”

Hearing this, Lu Xiyao and Fang Ya’s hearts skipped a beat.

“Daddy, I want one with milk and one with coconut milk, so I can try both,” the little girl said excitedly.

Fang Ya added, “I’ll take one with milk and one with rice wine, so I can try both too.”

Osmanthus Oolong Milk Tea, Osmanthus Oolong Coconut Delight, Osmanthus Oolong Rice Bliss—were they about to achieve ultimate milk tea freedom?

Lu Qin burst their bubble.

“I didn’t buy coconut milk or rice wine. We only have milk and coffee.”

“…”

Osmanthus Oolong tea had already been steeped, with hot brewing for winter and cold brewing for summer. Cold brewing enhanced the aroma and flavor, making it perfect for the season.

Each pound of tea could make about 50 cups. For his late-night food stall tonight, Lu Qin planned to limit sales to 100 cups. Not because he couldn’t make more, but because the space in his freshness-locking thermal box was limited and needed to store other food as well. Storing the prepared tea in an ordinary container would compromise its flavor and aroma, which was unacceptable.

Lu Qin went into the kitchen, took out whole milk and coffee beans, and began preparing. Milk tea required more than just milk—it also needed a certain proportion of light cream. The coffee beans were ground to the perfect consistency, as the particle size greatly affected the extraction speed and flavor.

While brewing the Osmanthus Oolong and coffee, Lu Qin carefully controlled the temperature, knowing it directly impacted the taste.

As Shen Muyan watched him work, she couldn’t help but admire his focused and meticulous demeanor—it was captivating. Meanwhile, Lu Xiyao leaned over the back of the sofa, her chin resting in her hands, eyes sparkling with anticipation. It was as if her gaze could summon the milk tea faster. Fang Ya, too, was waiting eagerly. As a self-proclaimed milk tea addict, she had tried countless brands but was confident this would be the best.

Ten minutes later, Lu Qin brought out four cups of Osmanthus Oolong Milk Tea. One of the cups was made with skim milk and coffee—a coffee-flavored drink with Osmanthus Oolong as the base, lightly sweet and wonderfully unique.

The enticing aroma of tea, milk, and coffee filled the room as the three waiting companions couldn’t help but swallow eagerly.

“Daddy, milk tea, milk tea, milk tea!” Lu Xiyao exclaimed, reaching out with her small hands.

Each person received a cup, their ice and sweetness levels predetermined by Lu Qin for optimal taste.

Sip…

Sip…

The tea was rich and smooth, the osmanthus’s floral notes perfectly complementing the oolong’s mellow depth. It was a harmonious dance of flavors—tea infused with aroma, aroma enriched by tea—truly exquisite.

The four of them lit up with delight, their eyes sparkling as if illuminated from within. The refreshing, creamy drink soothed and comforted, leaving them all in awe. It was amazing!

For Lu Xiyao, this was her first time tasting the milk tea she had long dreamed of, and it was her father’s masterpiece. It was so good it made her feel like she was flying! Her little legs swung happily under the table, her mouth open in a bright smile, revealing her pearly white teeth.

Fang Ya, usually talkative, was silent as she drank, fully immersed in the experience. If there were a benchmark for milk tea, this was it—it outclassed every other brand.

Shen Muyan sipped hers slowly, savoring the moment.

“This Osmanthus Oolong Milk Tea is completely different from anything outside. It’s rich, sweet, and incredibly delicious. Milk tea shops often have stronger tea flavors, but they don’t come close to this,” Shen Muyan said, analyzing it objectively.

Lu Qin smiled and explained, “It’s all about the quality of the tea. Milk tea shops rely on mass production and can’t control the quality of the tea leaves. If the tea aroma isn’t strong enough, they add artificial flavoring—the so-called ‘magic of modern science.’ But the artificial aroma can never compare to naturally high-quality tea leaves.”

Shen Muyan nodded thoughtfully.

“You’re a true professional.”

“Daddy is amazing!”

Lu Xiyao declared proudly, even if she didn’t fully understand what he said. To her, her dad knew everything and could do anything.

chapters.title: Chapter 123
chapters.content:
Chapter 123 I Can Still Drink It. Help Me Infuse It Directly Through an IV!

Lu Qin received a call from a neighbor, a woman next door, asking for his help to move her car. Her vehicle was parked in a parallel spot on the roadside, and she couldn’t get it out.

It wasn’t that Lu Qin had parked carelessly, the neighbor simply had poor driving skills. She couldn’t maneuver her car out of the space, even with 50 centimeters of clearance in front and behind. Not wanting to scratch Lu Qin’s new car, she had to call him over.

Inside the house, Lu Qin had only stepped out for a moment when Fang Ya sneaked into the kitchen and brought out a large pot of osmanthus oolong tea.

And then…

“Yao Yao, you can’t drink anymore. You’ve already had two cups,” Shen Muyan said, frowning slightly, stopping the little one from pouring her third.

One more cup, and it’d be time for a forehead flick.

As Shen Muyan spoke, she couldn’t help but hiccup, a faint osmanthus fragrance escaping.

“But Mommy, you’ve already had three cups. I want to drink three, too,” the little girl countered.

“A child’s stomach isn’t the same as an adult’s. If you drink more, your tummy will hurt.”

“No, it won’t. I can just rub it better,” Yao Yao replied confidently.

“No more drinking!”

Meanwhile, Fang Ya, a devoted milk tea fan, had already downed five cups and was pouring her sixth. Despite her stomach being as bloated as if she were three months pregnant, she loosened her waistband to make room for more.

By the sixth cup, the tea could practically overflow to her throat, allowing her to keep goldfish in her mouth and blow bubbles.

Shen Muyan, busy stopping the little one, couldn’t keep an eye on Fang Ya. Both the little one and the grown-up seemed unable to stop drinking milk tea.

When Lu Qin returned and saw the large pot of osmanthus oolong milk tea brought out, it was evident the three had already had several cups. The little one was using her finger to swipe tea residue from her cup and licking it off. Shen Muyan hiccuped again, the aroma of osmanthus oolong wafting in the air.

Fang Ya, determined, was pouring her sixth cup, even though her stomach couldn’t take it anymore. Yet, as a milk tea addict, she insisted, “I can still drink it. Help me infuse it directly through an IV!”

Lu Qin’s face darkened.

Were they drunk on milk tea? Infusing it through an IV?

“Are you three trying to drink yourselves into the hospital?”

Lu Qin, expressionless, walked over and took the large pot of milk tea away. Fang Ya’s sixth cup was poured back into the pot, leaving them no chance to drink more.

Little Yao Yao tattled immediately, “Daddy, I only drank two cups! Mommy drank three, and Auntie Fang Ya drank five!”

Lu Qin glanced at the little one.

“If you drink one more sip, you’re getting a forehead flick.”

Then he turned to Shen Muyan.

“Didn’t you say you don’t like milk tea? Why’d you drink so much?”

Shen Muyan pouted.

“It was too delicious. I couldn’t resist. Besides, I didn’t drink that much…”

Lu Qin sneered.

“Oh, not much, huh? Even your hiccups smell like osmanthus oolong.”

Shen Muyan covered her mouth with her hand and sniffed.

“What osmanthus oolong smell like? I don’t smell anything. Smell again.”

She leaned closer, opening her soft, pink lips slightly and exhaling gently.

Lu Qin stared at her stunningly beautiful face as it inched closer, her long, curled eyelashes framing her features. Her breath carried a faint osmanthus fragrance mixed with a light, elegant aroma. It smelled delightful.

She had indeed drunk too much, but it wasn’t an alcohol buzz. She had overeating at dinner and consumed too much sweet milk tea. Her stomach was full, drawing more blood for digestion, which left less for her brain. This phenomenon is known as a food coma or ‘postprandial sleepiness’.

Lu Qin extended a finger, poking her smooth, jade-like forehead, gently pushing her away.

“Go wash your face and take the little one home.”

Shen Muyan stared at him for a few seconds, then pursed her lips and said, “Okay.”

Fang Ya, still sulking over her unfinished sixth cup, protested, “I wasn’t drunk! I can still drink!”

Lu Qin glanced at her.

“Sure, you’re not drunk. If we drew your blood now, we’d probably get half a cup of milk tea.”

He couldn’t understand how she managed to drink five cups.

Shen Muyan chuckled, “Alright, we’re leaving now.”

Lu Qin warned, “If you sneak milk tea again, both kids and adults are getting forehead flicks.”

Shen Muyan rarely acted playful, sticking out her tongue.

“I dare you next time.”

Lu Qin packed some iced pastries, crystal shrimp dumplings, imperial chicken wings, and mung bean soup for Shen Muyan to take to her parents.

The little one hugged him tightly for a while. “Daddy, I don’t want to leave you. I want to help you at the stall tomorrow and be your little boss lady.”

“Next time, sweetie. Your grandparents miss you,” Lu Qin said, ruffling her hair.

“Daddy, I’ll miss you.”

“Daddy will miss you too. Be good at Grandma and Grandpa’s, okay?”

“Okay, I will. I’m always good.”

“You can have only one ice cream.”

“What? Then I don’t want to go to Grandma and Grandpa’s!”

Lu Xiyao was promptly tossed into the back seat of the Maserati by Shen Muyan. Fang Ya climbed into the passenger seat and waved goodbye to Lu Qin.

Shen Muyan said, “If you’re tired, skip the stall tonight and rest early.”

Lu Qin replied, “Got it.”

…

Sun Zhi and Fan Qingfang arrived half an hour early at the Changlin University Night Market Street, knowing from experience that the queue would be long.

When Lu Qin watched them leave, he tidied up, loaded the food into the trunk of his M7, and drove out of the old town. But when they saw the brightly lit arch at the entrance of the night market street, they realized they were late despite being early.

They were number 34 and 35 in line. Behind them, more people kept joining. The line grew longer and longer. Soon, there were over sixty or seventy people.

At the front of the line, however, the stall was still empty. Sun Zhi and Fan Qingfang exchanged glances.

Incredible!

How could one stall be this popular?

When they ran discounts on hotel buffet vouchers, it wasn’t this crazy. A sleek black M7 turned the corner at the traffic light and entered everyone’s view. The crowd erupted in excitement.

“He’s here! He’s here! He’s brought the iced pastries and crystal shrimp dumplings!”

“Tonight’s new item is osmanthus oolong? I can’t wait!”

“Imperial chicken wings! Imperial chicken wings!”

Sun Zhi and Fan Qingfang, hearing the other customers, were now certain this stall’s food must be extraordinary to draw such a crowd.

Lu Qin parked, stepped out, and opened the trunk. Customers eagerly helped him set up the tables and arrange the food boxes.

He set up a QR code sign and a whiteboard that read.

chapters.title: Chapter 124
chapters.content:
Chapter 124 Even a Culinary God Would Bow Three Times to Chef Lu!

At Night Market Street, The terrifyingly long line in front of Lu Qin’s stall continued to attract curious newcomers. Although many hadn’t tried the food, the sheer crowd triggered their curiosity, prompting them to join the queue.

Some passersby even thought it was a free egg giveaway and eagerly lined up, hoping for a freebie. After all, people tend to follow crowds and join the excitement.

Within five minutes of setting up the stall, the line had already surpassed 100 people. Sun Zhi and Fan Qingfang, seasoned businesspeople, were astonished. The popularity of Lu Qin’s snack stall far exceeded their expectations.

They secretly congratulated themselves for arriving 30 minutes early. Even so, they were lucky to secure spots in the 30s. Had they come just a few minutes later, they would’ve been closer to the 70s or 80s, facing much fiercer competition.

A new customer asked, “Is this stall really good? Why is the line so long?”

A sly regular smirked.

“No, it’s terrible. I’m just a plant hired by the owner to trick you newbies.”

The new customer froze.

“Huh! Really?”

The regular leaned in conspiratorially.

“Why are you still standing here? Go, run! I’m only telling you because I felt bad.”

The new customer stammered, “Th-thank you…” and turned to leave, only to be stopped by a good Samaritan.

“Don’t believe them. They’re sly devils! This snack stall’s food is amazing—so good it’s addictive. By the time you reach the front, you won’t regret it.”

“Really? What’s on the menu?”

“There’s everything—iced pastries, crystal shrimp dumplings, imperial chicken wings, mung bean soup… Oh, and today there’s a new item: osmanthus oolong milk tea. Just thinking about it makes my mouth water!”

“Wow, such variety! Does the owner make it all himself?”

“Absolutely. Chef Lu’s culinary skills are so impressive that even a god of cuisine would bow three times to him. But remember one thing.”

“What’s that?”

“Buy as much as you can. If you buy too little and realize it’s amazing, there might be none left if you rejoin the line.”

The newcomer nodded earnestly.

“Got it.”

“Good luck, and enjoy. I learned the hard way after listening to some jerk in line once. He told me the food was bad, so I left. The moment I stepped out, he moved up a spot. By the time I tried to rejoin, everything was sold out.”

The new customer was stunned.

“People are that devious?”

“Yep. Oh, by the way, this milk tea? A hundred servings only. If you don’t act fast, you’ll miss out.”

Meanwhile, the 100th customer in line was grinning smugly, reveling in their luck.

“I made it! I’ll be the last to get the osmanthus oolong milk tea. Too bad for those behind me!”

“ Ugh , this smug jerk!”

“Look at them acting all high and mighty! Tomorrow I’ll arrive an hour early and show them how it’s done!”

The 100th customer tilted their head back at a proud 45-degree angle, basking in their imagined glory.

“I don’t even know what this osmanthus oolong milk tea tastes like yet, but it’s definitely better than any store-bought version. I can’t wait!”

Their smugness was infuriating to those behind them. At the stall, Lu Qin handed out items one by one, including a cup of the much-anticipated osmanthus oolong milk tea.

One customer, overwhelmed with excitement, fumbled the bag.

Crash!

The cup fell to the ground, spilling milk tea everywhere.

“Ahhhh! My milk tea!!!”

The customer wailed like they had just lost a treasure, kneeling on the ground in anguish. Others reacted with a mix of sympathy and schadenfreude.

“Why cry? It’s your own fault for not holding it properly.”

“That’s such a waste. This milk tea is a labor of love!”

The distraught customer even picked up the broken cup, desperate to salvage even a drop.

Lu Qin stepped forward.

“My apologies. That might’ve been my slip. Let me get you another one.”

He handed over a fresh cup.

The grateful customer, cradling the cup like a newborn, stammered, “Th-thank you! Thank you so much!”

The crowd was full of praise for Lu Qin.

“Wow, Chef Lu is so generous. Even though it wasn’t his fault, he still gave them another cup!”

“Such a kind heart! This is what sets him apart.”

“Chef Lu, I love you!”

Sun Zhi and Fan Qingfang, observing from the line, nodded approvingly.

But the 100th customer was dumbfounded. “What? Now there are only 99 cups left!”

Their faces turned pale as they cursed.

“How can someone be so careless? That was my milk tea!”

The customers behind them immediately mocked them.

“Where’s your smug attitude now?”

“Yeah, keep showing off!”

“See? Karma’s a thing!”

Eventually, Sun Zhi and Fan Qingfang reached the front of the line.

“Chef Lu, we’ll take everything on the menu,” they said excitedly.

“Same here,” added the other.

Lu Qin packed their orders.

“148.5 yuan each. Please scan the code to pay.”

After paying, they didn’t even wait to get back to their car. Following the example of other customers, they sat on a nearby barrier and began eating.

They started with the iced pastries.

“It’s exactly as described—soft, sticky, and sweet. Amazing!”

“Let’s try the shrimp dumplings…”

“Wow! So bouncy and flavorful!”

“These chicken wings… Oh my gosh, they’re incredible!”

“I’m convinced. Totally convinced!”

By the time they finished their milk tea, both were stuffed.

“I’ve never eaten this much before, but it was worth it!”

“Chef Lu is a treasure—a goldmine!”

Suddenly, they exchanged a look and squared their shoulders.

“Fan, we’re reputable people. If we’re going to compete for him, let’s do it fairly!”

“Sun, I’m determined to get Chef Lu!”

“Let’s see about that!”

Both approached Lu Qin with business cards in hand.

“Chef Lu, I’m Sun Zhi, owner of Haoyue Grand Hotel…”

“I’m Fan Qingfang, chairman of Swarovski Crystal Restaurant…”

Typically, people would be flattered and intimidated by such introductions, but Lu Qin merely nodded, set the cards aside, and continued serving customers.

The two executives exchanged a stunned look.

Were they just… ignored?

chapters.title: Chapter 125
chapters.content:
Chapter 125 Boss Lu has many regular customers! Yao Yao has a fever!

Sun Zhi and Fan Qingfang were ignored by a small roadside stall owner?

“Did he not carefully look at our business cards?” Sun Zhi asked.

Fan Qingfang shook her head.

“I saw him look at the cards.”

The business cards had three bold gold characters—Chairman. There was no way the owner didn’t see it unless he was blind.

Since that’s the case, this Boss Lu already knows who they are but still chose to ignore them!

“We need to make our intentions clear,” Sun Zhi said.

Fan Qingfang nodded.

So, the two of them went back over.

“Boss Lu, actually, I’d like to invite you—” Sun Zhi said.

“You want to invite him to be the head chef at your hotel restaurant?”

Sun Zhi froze, turning to see a wealthy middle-aged man finish the rest of his sentence.

“Uh, and you are?”

Sun Zhi found the man somewhat familiar but couldn’t place him immediately.

“Deng?”

Fan Qingfang recognized him. The middle-aged man was Deng Jianping, the owner of Jiangcheng Kaiyuan Grand Hotel.

Deng Jianping’s businesses were even larger than theirs. In addition to running over 17 ‘Fruit Mix Snow’ milk tea stores, he also owned numerous bars and hot pot restaurants in Jiangcheng.

Sun Zhi and Fan Qingfang quickly greeted him politely. Deng Jianping accepted the food, mung bean smoothie, and osmanthus oolong milk tea handed over by Lu Qin with a smile.

“Thank you, Boss Lu. I’ve already paid.”

“Hmm, Deng, this time, you didn’t overpay. Come again soon. Take care.”

Lu Qin smiled faintly. He remembered Deng Jianping because Deng often tipped generously, claiming the food was worth far more than its price. However, Lu Qin didn’t appreciate it, insisting on normal pricing. If Deng didn’t comply, he wouldn’t sell to him. Since then, Deng admired Lu Qin not only for his culinary skills but also for his character.

Sun Zhi’s heart skipped a beat.

“Boss Deng, are you familiar with Boss Lu?”

Deng Jianping shook his head.

“Just a regular customer. Boss Lu has many like me. Even Ouyang from Shengyang Group is one of his patrons.”

“Shengyang Group!”

Sun Zhi and Fan Qingfang were taken aback. That was one of the top groups in Haishi.

“I’ve been rejected by Boss Lu several times already, so you might as well give up and save your energy.”

Deng Jianping took a bite of a crystal shrimp dumpling, his face full of delight.

“Boss Lu is a man of character. He enjoys running his stall, free and easy. Otherwise, Shengyang Group would’ve hired him long ago.”

Sun Zhi and Fan Qingfang fell silent.

Deng Jianping took a sip of osmanthus oolong milk tea, savored it, and suddenly cursed.

“Damn! I knew it!”

Fan Qingfang was startled.

“What’s wrong, Boss Deng?”

“Boss Lu’s osmanthus oolong milk tea is miles ahead of my Fruit Mix Snow! The flavor and texture are unbeatable!”

Deng Jianping stopped after drinking half the cup and carefully packed the rest to take back for his beverage team. He planned to have them try and replicate the taste—even achieving 70% similarity would suffice.

Sun Zhi and Fan Qingfang had also tried the milk tea earlier and agreed it was better than any other on the market.

Who exactly was this Boss Lu?

_____

At 9:15 p.m., the stars twinkled, and the moonlight was like water. The customers gradually dispersed as Lu Qin packed up his stall and sat in the driver’s seat of his M7.

Once again, he’d sold out, over 300 portions of ice crystal cakes, nearly the same for crystal shrimp dumplings, 230 boxes of imperial chicken wings, 400 cups of chilled mung bean smoothies, and 99 cups of osmanthus oolong milk tea. Revenue—28,000 yuan. Lu Qin was satisfied.

Sitting in his car, enjoying the cool air from the air conditioner, he opened his phone to message Shen Muyan.

Lu Qin smiled slightly, put down his phone, and started the car.

“Hello, Xiao Yi.”

“I’m here.”

“Play the song Flowers of Parting.”

“Alright, now playing the song…”

The lyrics rose and fell with emotion, resonating deeply. It was a beautiful song.

When Lu Qin returned home, the absence of the little one’s chatter made the house feel empty. He went to bed early.

The next morning, Lu Qin visited the Jingyuan Market and stopped by Lao Zhao’s shop for disposable tableware.

“Lao Zhao, do you have milk tea cups?”

Lao Zhao’s eyes lit up.

“Boss Lu, you’re making milk tea now?”

So Lu Qin was expanding into milk tea too—an all-out venture!

“Yeah, I tested 100 cups yesterday using 375ml soybean milk cups. I need to switch to 500ml specialized milk tea cups.”

“Of course I have them. Let me show you some samples.”

Lao Zhao soon returned with several options, including transparent, frosted, and printed designs. The prints featured simple, artistic phrases like “All my tomorrows are for you,” “You’re my little happiness,” and “No additives, just love.”

Lu Qin chose a design and placed an order–”Lao Zhao, I’ll take 1,000 milk tea cups. Also, 5,000 rectangular disposable lunch boxes and 1,500 round ones. I’ll transfer the payment via WeChat.”

“Sure, I’ll deliver them later.”

“Thanks,” Lu Qin said.

“Don’t mention it. When are you setting up your breakfast stall? I want to get your fried rice noodles. I missed it yesterday and can’t stop craving it.”

“When you deliver the containers, I’ll fry you three portions.”

“Deal!”

Lu Qin then entered the market to buy various ingredients. At the usual spot near Sunshine Elementary, his breakfast stall disrupted the residents’ sleep as they flocked downstairs to queue. Some came from farther away, yawning in line but waking up at the aroma of fried noodles.

The breakfast service ended near 10 a.m., with earnings of 12,000 yuan. Lu Qin’s system points now totaled 3,054. Just as he rode his food cart back to the old district, his phone rang. It was Shen Muyan.

“Lu Qin, Yao Yao has a fever. We’re at City First Hospital, 9th floor. Come quickly.”

chapters.title: Chapter 126
chapters.content:
Chapter 126 Your Granddaughter Is About to Drool!

The little one had a fever and was hospitalized?

Lu Qin’s heart tightened. He swung the three-wheeled food cart into the yard, then ran to the roadside, got into his M7, switched to sports mode, launched the car, and sped out of the residential area.

A tabby cat in the flower bed was so startled that it jumped a meter high, yowling, “ Wocao nima! ”

Upon arriving at the City First Hospital, Lu Qin pressed the elevator button and went upstairs. He found the pediatric ward, Room 16.

Lu Qin pushed the door open.

“Two little bees, flying into the flower bushes, flying left, flying right, flying—scissors! Flying—rock!”

“Two little bees, flying into the flower bushes, flying left, flying right, flying—clap! Flying—clap!”

“Grandpa, you lost again!”

The little one grabbed a strip of paper and stuck it onto Shen Hongda’s forehead with a slap.

Shen Hongda looked just like Bai Wuchang (a ghostly figure in Chinese folklore), but the elderly man was full of laughter.

Lu Qin: “……”

Was this little one, full of energy like Wu Song fighting a tiger, supposed to be sick?

At that moment, Shen Muyan returned from the nurse’s station. Seeing Lu Qin’s expression, she explained, “Dad panicked. He found the little one’s throat slightly inflamed this morning, took her temperature, and it showed 39.1°C. He got so scared that he rushed her to the hospital. But when the doctor measured it, it was only 36.8°C—no fever.”

“Dad, you probably forgot to shake down the thermometer before using it. Mercury thermometers need to be shaken first.”

Shen Hongda and Lu Xiyao stopped their bee game when they saw Lu Qin.

“Daddy~!”

Seeing Lu Qin, Lu Xiyao’s little face lit up with joy, and she threw herself into his arms.

Shen Hongda took the paper strip off his face and said apologetically, “Lu Qin, I made you worry and even made you come all the way here.”

Shen Hongda wasn’t sure whether he forgot to shake the thermometer or misread the temperature. He had indeed panicked.

“Dad, it’s okay. As long as the little one is fine, that’s all that matters. But if anything happens in the future, please let me know right away.”

Lu Qin didn’t blame Shen Hongda. The old man cared too much about his precious granddaughter.

Hearing Lu Qin call him Dad, Shen Hongda felt a surge of emotion and looked at him warmly. Lu Qin was always his good son-in-law.

In this lifetime, he only recognized Lu Qin as his son-in-law. Then, Shen Hongda shot Shen Muyan a meaningful glance, urging her to win Lu Qin back as soon as possible.

Otherwise, if someone else took him away, she would regret it with no place to cry. Shen Muyan pouted, then gently opened Lu Xiyao’s mouth to check.

“Her throat is a little inflamed. Since they already took blood and an X-ray, let’s wait for the results to rule out pneumonia.”

Lu Qin said, “Make sure the little one drinks more warm water and goes to the bathroom often. She’ll recover faster.”

“Daddy, will drinking bubble tea help me recover faster too?” Lu Xiyao asked.

Lu Qin raised an eyebrow, about to flick her forehead, but after thinking about it, he held back. Given that she wasn’t feeling well, he let it slide.

Seeing she had escaped a flick, Lu Xiyao patted her chest in relief. It seemed like this wasn’t the time to ask for bubble tea—better wait for another opportunity.

Shen Hongda opened the lid of a cartoon water cup, refilled it with warm water, and handed it over.

“Here, Yao Yao, drink more water.”

Lu Xiyao took a big sip, smacked her lips—no taste at all. She missed the chilled mung bean smoothie and osmanthus oolong milk tea her father made.

With a pitiful look, she rubbed her empty belly.

“Daddy, I’m hungry~!”

Shen Hongda suddenly realized.

“Oh dear, I rushed her to the hospital early in the morning, and after all the blood tests and X-rays, it’s nearly lunchtime. I’ll call someone to bring food over right away.”

Saying that, Shen Hongda reached for his phone. Lu Xiyao looked at Lu Qin.

“Daddy, I want to eat the food you make.”

Lu Qin pinched the little one’s cheek and said to Shen Hongda,

“Dad, I’ll go home and cook something for her. No need to order food.”

“You both haven’t eaten yet, have you?”

Shen Hongda and Shen Muyan shook their heads.

“I’ll make extra, then.”

They nodded in agreement.

They also wanted to eat Lu Qin’s cooking. The little one’s mention of food reminded them of their own hunger and cravings.

As Lu Qin walked to the door, he bumped into an elderly man carrying a large plastic bag in one hand and holding a little boy’s hand in the other.

“Please, after you.”

Lu Qin stepped aside politely.

The old man glanced at Lu Qin and smiled.

“Oh, thank you, young man.”

Lu Qin smiled and nodded before leaving the room.

The old man placed the bag on the bedside table of the adjacent bed and turned to Shen Hongda.

“Old Shen, you guys haven’t eaten yet, right? I asked someone to pack food from Yuzun Tower. Would you like some?”

The old man was Liu Hongming. He and Shen Hongda knew each other. Coincidentally, both their grandchildren were hospitalized. Originally, Liu Hongming’s grandson was in Room 15, but he specifically requested to move to Room 16—to keep company.

Liu Hongming’s grandson had a fever and cough. After nebulizer treatment in the morning, he was feeling slightly better.

“Grandpa, I’m hungry.”

The little boy clung to Liu Hongming’s leg, asking for food.

“Alright, alright, Xiaofeng, we’re about to eat.”

Liu Hongming quickly opened the plastic bag, revealing a stack of stainless-steel food containers. As soon as he opened them, a rich aroma filled the room.

“Wow! Smells amazing!”

The little boy sniffed his fingers hungrily.

Liu Hongming turned to Shen Hongda.

“Old Shen, I’m serious, don’t be polite. There’s plenty of food—I told the kitchen to prepare double portions.”

“You know how hard it is to get a table at Yuzun Tower.”

Liu Hongming spoke politely, but his eyes carried a hint of smugness. There was indeed a lot of food. For just him and his grandson, four portions of rice, six dishes, and a soup were unnecessary.

Shen Hongda glanced at Liu Hongming. Of course, he knew that Liu Hongming had deliberately brought extra food to show off how delicious Yuzun Tower’s food was.

Yuzun Tower was a high-end restaurant that opened half a month ago. They had hired a famous chef and promoted it aggressively, so business was booming.

Both of them were in the restaurant business. While they were polite in public, they secretly competed with each other. Shen’s side had Zijing Restaurant and Juxian Tower, but they couldn’t compete with Yuzun Tower at the moment.

That was exactly why Liu Hongming seized the opportunity to flaunt his restaurant’s food.

“No need. Our food is on the way.”

Shen Hongda shook his head expressionlessly. Liu Hongming fed a piece of Kung Pao chicken to his grandson and turned to Shen Hongda.

“Are you sure? This is a rare delicacy. Look at this Kung Pao chicken—crispy, sweet, and savory. Xiaofeng, is it good?”

The little boy nodded vigorously.

“Grandpa, it’s super delicious!”
