“Old Shen, come on, don’t pretend. You’re tempted, aren’t you? Your granddaughter is already drooling.”

“Come on, it’s just food. You usually can’t get this, but I’m not stingy.”

“If you ever come to Yuzun Tower, just let me know—I’ll reserve a table for you.”

chapters.title: Chapter 127
chapters.content:
Chapter 127 Crying Is the Right Reaction! My Son-In-Law’s Food Is So Good It Makes People Cry!

“Putting on an act? Old Liu, what am I pretending about?”

“I’m just not interested in your so-called Yuzun Tower food. No matter how good it is, it can’t compare to the food we have coming.”

Shen Hongda glanced at Liu Hongming, annoyed by his smug expression.

No matter how delicious the food from Yuzun Tower was, could it possibly be better than the dishes his son-in-law made?

Absolutely impossible!

Lu Xiyao turned her head and gave Liu Hongming a puzzled look. She thought, I was drooling because I was thinking about Dad’s twice-cooked pork!

Liu Hongming chuckled, “Old Shen, just keep being stubborn. If you insist, you’re only punishing your own stomach.”

“Come on, grandson, since they won’t eat, let’s enjoy this ourselves. Try this golden coin chicken paired with stuffed chicken wings.”

“Wow! Grandpa! These chicken wings are really delicious too!”

Shen Muyan glanced at the grandfather and grandson next to them with a strange expression.

Are they performing a rehearsed skit?

In fact, to outsiders, Yuzun Tower’s dishes did seem incredibly tempting. But for those who had tasted Lu Qin’s cooking, their palates had already been spoiled. Other food rarely amazed them, let alone made them crave it.

At that moment, the attending pediatrician entered the room with a nurse carrying a report sheet.

“Lu Xiyao, there’s nothing serious—just a bit of inflammation… huh? What smells so good?”

The doctor and nurse couldn’t help but glance towards the adjacent bed and swallow.

“Wow, such a lavish meal?”

Their eyes widened.

Palace-style Kung Pao chicken, braised chicken wings, steamed perch, braised lion’s head meatballs, lily with celery, scallion oil bok choy, and a corn and pork rib soup.

Wasn’t this too extravagant?

Shen Hongda frowned slightly.

“Doctor, you haven’t finished talking about my granddaughter’s condition!”

“Oh… cough, cough … your granddaughter is fine, just mild inflammation. She doesn’t even need medication, just drink more water to flush it out.”

Liu Hongming offered, “Doctor, nurse, would you like to try some? We have extra chopsticks.”

“Uh… no need.”

Although they were tempted, they felt embarrassed.

“There’s nothing to be embarrassed about. There’s plenty of food, and my grandson and I can’t finish it all. Yuzun Tower’s food is excellent.”

“Yuzun Tower? The one on Qinghe Road? That place is always packed! I’ve seen so many cars parked outside when I drive by!”

The doctor seemed excited. No wonder the food smelled so good! That was a Michelin-starred restaurant. Expensive, but worth every penny. The doctor was visibly tempted.

Liu Hongming handed over two pairs of chopsticks.

“If someone insists on being stubborn and won’t eat, let them be. Doctor, nurse, you should try some. Don’t hold back.”

While speaking, he cast a smug glance at Shen Hongda.

But the latter remained expressionless.

“Well, since we’ve been making rounds all morning and only had a tea egg for breakfast… we’ll just have a small taste.”

“Eat! Enjoy!”

Liu Hongming encouraged them generously.

The doctor and nurse each took a few bites, and their eyes lit up. The food was indeed delicious—perfectly cooked, fragrant, and flavorful. Sounds of satisfied chewing echoed through Room 16.

Although Shen Hongda, Shen Muyan, and Lu Xiyao were hungry and craving food, they had no interest in the dishes next door. They patiently waited for Lu Qin to return.

A dozen minutes later—Lu Qin pushed open the ward door.

“Daddy~!”

The little girl was the first to see him and instantly perked up.

“Your little cutie is starving~!”

“You must be hungry. Go wash your hands before eating.”

Lu Qin patted Lu Xiyao’s head. Shen Muyan took Lu Xiyao to wash her hands, while Lu Qin placed the food he had brought on the bedside table. This time, he had made extra since he hadn’t eaten lunch himself.

Shen Hongda had been waiting eagerly and immediately helped open the containers. Imperial-style chicken wings, crystal shrimp dumplings, twice-cooked pork, tomato scrambled eggs, stir-fried pork with green peppers, and a seaweed egg drop soup.

Liu Hongming puffed out his chest and glanced over, noticing that the food on Shen Hongda’s side was packed in plastic containers. He assumed it was just some cheap takeaway, and his smugness grew.

Old Shen must be starving if he’s willing to eat fast food like that.

“Old Shen, which fast-food place did you buy that from?”

Shen Hongda, Lu Qin, and Shen Muyan all turned to look at Liu Hongming.

Before Shen Hongda could respond, Lu Xiyao proudly announced, “My daddy made this! And it’s super delicious!”

“Oh?”

Liu Hongming looked at Lu Qin skeptically.

Delicious?

Could it be better than the dishes from Yuzun Tower?

The doctor and nurse, who had just been praising the Yuzun Tower dishes, suddenly froze.

As soon as Shen Hongda removed the plastic lids, a wave of even more intense aroma filled the room, completely overpowering the scent of the Yuzun Tower dishes.

Liu Hongming, his grandson, the doctor, and the nurse all stopped eating and turned their heads.

Could it be… that the food next door smelled even better?

The plastic containers held dishes that looked ordinary compared to the beautifully plated Yuzun Tower meals, which even had decorative carrot carvings.

But!

The aroma was too tempting!

It was mouthwatering.

Shen Hongda and the others had already started eating. Lu Qin divided the rice, and they dug in.

Eating Lu Qin’s food had one rule—no talking. Talking would only slow down the eating process. The fragrance next door was unbearable. Liu Hongming’s grandson was the first to break.

“Grandpa, I want to eat those dumplings!”

The little boy was sitting on the bed, and as soon as he turned his head, he saw a box of crystal shrimp dumplings facing his direction. The dumplings were translucent, and through the wrapper, he could see the pink shrimp filling inside. They looked exquisite and delicious.

Suddenly, he lost all interest in the Yuzun Tower dishes in front of him and stared intently at the dumplings.

“Ah? This…”

Liu Hongming felt embarrassed. He couldn’t understand why the food next door smelled so much better. But after all the mocking he had just done, how could he ask Shen Hongda to let his grandson have a taste?

Yet, before he could think of a solution, Shen Hongda casually picked up a fresh pair of chopsticks, grabbed a shrimp dumpling, and held it out.

“Here, didn’t you want to try one? Go ahead.”

The little boy opened his mouth wide and chomped down. He chewed eagerly.

Liu Hongming, the doctor, and the nurse all watched him closely, curious about how it tasted.

“Is it good?” Liu Hongming asked.

“It’s delicious! So, so delicious!”

The little boy lacked the words to describe it, but his joyful expression said it all.

The dumpling was small, but its texture was silky smooth, and its flavor was rich. Lu Qin’s mastery of dough and fillings had elevated it to perfection.

One wasn’t enough. After the first, the craving for a second, a third, a fourth naturally followed.

The little boy was completely hooked. Then, Shen Hongda fed him a piece of twice-cooked pork.

The boy swayed happily as he ate. But after two bites, Shen Hongda turned back and resumed eating his own meal. That was enough for a taste. But the little boy couldn’t resist. He wanted more.

“Grandpa, I want more dumplings and pork! Waaaah~ I just want to eat them!”

Now, Liu Hongming finally understood.

Shen Hongda hadn’t refused his food out of stubborn pride—he had better food. He had been wrong all along!

Liu Hongming looked deeply at Lu Qin.

Does this young man really have such exceptional culinary skills?

The little boy cried even louder.

They were in a hospital. No matter how hard Liu Hongming tried, he couldn’t calm him down.

Biting his lip, Liu Hongming turned to Shen Hongda.

“Old Shen, the child is crying… could you…”

Shen Hongda turned around.

“He’s crying? That’s the right reaction! My son-in-law’s food is so good it makes people cry.”

…

chapters.title: Chapter 128
chapters.content:
Chapter 128 Does Your “B” Brand With Wings Have a Queen’s Seat?

Liu Hongming raised an eyebrow and thought, Old Shen, you’re twisting words. The “so delicious it makes people cry”—are you referring to my grandson crying?

My grandson is crying because he desperately wants to eat it!

Are you going to give him some or not? Don’t make me beg you!

Looking at the presentation, the crystal shrimp dumplings and twice-cooked pork from the other side were indeed tempting, but his Yuzun Tower dishes had an even better presentation and looked more appetizing.

Since he hadn’t tasted the other food, he couldn’t judge. But seeing his grandson’s intense reaction, it must be incredibly delicious.

“Daddy, here, have a piece of meat~!”

Lu Xiyao picked up a piece of twice-cooked pork, lifted her tiny arm, and fed it to Lu Qin’s mouth.

“Alright, thank you, Yao Yao.”

Lu Qin ate the piece of pork, feeling warm inside.

“No need to thank me, Daddy. Cooking is hard work.”

The little girl was very considerate of Lu Qin.

“Here, Mommy, have a piece of green pepper.”

The little girl picked up a green pepper from her own bowl and held it up to Shen Muyan’s mouth.

Shen Muyan’s gaze remained cool. She asked, “Why are you giving me a green pepper?”

The little girl’s eyes were pure as she replied, “Mommy, because my bowl only has green peppers left. The last piece of meat went to Daddy~!”

Shen Muyan pursed her lips, just about to take a bite when the little one added.

“And… I don’t like green peppers.”

Hearing that, Shen Muyan pulled her head back and sat up straight.

Oh, so the meat she likes goes to Daddy, and the green peppers she doesn’t like come to Mommy.

Alright. Very clear thinking.

“Children need balanced nutrition when eating. You can’t just eat meat—finish your green peppers.”

Shen Muyan took on the role of educator, speaking in a commanding tone while picking up four or five more green peppers and putting them into the little one’s bowl.

“That’s too much, Mommy! Waaaah~!”

Lu Xiyao’s face fell.

Shen Muyan raised a slender finger and shook it.

“Not too much at all. Eat them all.”

“Ohhh~!”

Lu Xiyao ate the green peppers as if she were taking bitter medicine.

Actually, Lu Qin’s stir-fried pork with green peppers was excellent. The pork was tender and fragrant, and the green peppers had absorbed the sauce wonderfully. He had even added Shaoxing wine to enhance the flavor. The green peppers were just as tasty as the meat.

But Lu Xiyao had disliked green peppers since childhood and instinctively resisted them. Seeing this, Shen Hongda secretly helped her by eating two green pepper slices. The little girl gave him a grateful look—Grandpa was the best!

Shen Muyan, however, pretended not to see. Liu Hongming’s grandson cried for a while, realized it was useless, and quietly went over to the edge of the other bed, staring longingly at their food.

Liu Hongming felt awkward. His earlier arrogance had completely vanished. Just one shrimp dumpling and a piece of twice-cooked pork had made his grandson cry with craving. This was a serious problem.

At that moment, Shen Hongda finally picked up a small bowl, took a little bit of everything, and handed it to Liu Hongming’s grandson.

“Wow! Thank you, Grandpa Shen!”

The little boy’s eyes lit up with joy.

“Mmm… so delicious!”

He started devouring the food ravenously. Meanwhile, Liu Hongming took a bite of his own Yuzun Tower dish. It still tasted good, but somehow, it didn’t feel as satisfying.

“Xiaofeng, let Grandpa have a bite.”

“No!”

“Hey, you little ingrate! Grandpa gave you so much delicious food earlier, and now you won’t even let me try a bite?”

Liu Hongming forcibly took the last piece of twice-cooked pork from his grandson’s bowl and ate it. The little boy pouted in displeasure.

But as Liu Hongming chewed, his eyes widened in shock. This twice-cooked pork… was better than what Yuzun Tower’s head chef, Master Qin, made!

More fragrant! More flavorful!

Perfectly seasoned—salty, savory, rich yet not greasy.

The taste and texture were flawless!

Perfect!

Unable to contain his curiosity, Liu Hongming asked, “Old Shen, this is your son-in-law?”

He looked at Lu Qin, surprised that such a young man could cook so well. Master Qin at Yuzun Tower was already over fifty, with decades of experience.

This… didn’t make sense.

Shen Hongda grinned proudly.

“Of course! Handsome, isn’t he?”

“Very handsome! He’s a perfect match for your daughter!”

Liu Hongming praised, but his heart felt sour. His own son was divorced. His ex-daughter-in-law was too demanding, always gambling, and never took care of their child.

Lu Qin and Shen Muyan exchanged glances but didn’t say a word. They weren’t going to explain their divorce to outsiders.

Lu Xiyao was happily eating, feeling that this was the perfect family moment. After finishing Lu Qin’s food, the little girl declared that her throat no longer hurt, meaning she didn’t need medicine and could go home.

Lu Qin examined her tonsils. They were slightly red but not severe.

“Your throat really doesn’t hurt anymore?” He asked.

Lu Xiyao nodded.

“Daddy, it doesn’t hurt at all! I can even eat ice cream!”

Lu Qin chuckled.

“Since it doesn’t hurt, then let’s go with a knuckle flick instead. Earlier, I didn’t do it because you weren’t feeling well.”

Lu Xiyao: “???”

As they left the hospital, Shen Hongda invited Lu Qin to their house.

“Your mother-in-law has been practicing cooking lately. Master Chef Lu, care to give some guidance?”

Lu Qin couldn’t refuse and followed them to the villa for dinner.

“I want to ride in Daddy’s car!”

Shen Hongda frowned.

“Is Grandpa’s B-brand with wings not good enough?”

Lu Xiyao asked, “Grandpa, does your car have a 15-inch LCD screen?”

“No, it doesn’t.”

“Does it have zero-gravity seats?”

“Also no.”

“Does it have a drone?”

“A drone? Which car comes with a drone?”

“My Daddy’s car does!”

“…”

The little girl climbed into the backseat of the M7, expertly opened the built-in tablet, and started playing the cartoon Little Bunny Feifei. Shen Muyan also got into the backseat of the M7.

Shen Hongda: “You too?”

Shen Muyan responded coolly, “Does your ‘B-brand with wings’ have a Queen’s seat?”

Shen Hongda: “…”

“Old Zhong, step on it. Leave that black SUV far behind.”

“Boss Shen, I can’t. That’s an electric vehicle, and its acceleration is almost as fast as our Bentley—around four seconds.”

“What? You’re saying a 3-million-yuan Bentley isn’t better than an electric Chinese car?”

Shen Hongda suddenly felt like his Bentley was lacking everything.

“What car is that?”

“The new AITO M7. Comes with a fridge, TV, and a large sofa. Uses Huawei’s technology—HarmonyOS smart drive, leading the way.”

“Huawei?”

Shen Hongda took out his Huawei Mate X5 top-tier edition phone.

“Then I’ll get one too, to match my phone,” Shen Hongda said.

“Boss Shen, if you want to fire me, just say so.”

“Huh? When did I say that?”

“That car has smart driving. It makes me useless.”

Shen Hongda stroked his chin.

“Is that car really that good? My son-in-law has great taste!”

Lu Qin set the navigation for the villa, activated smart driving, and relaxed in the air-conditioned, fragrant cabin.

Lu Xiyao reclined on the backseat, resting her head on Shen Muyan’s long legs. Shen Muyan activated the multi-point massage seat in the back, closed her eyes, and enjoyed the ride.

chapters.title: Chapter 129
chapters.content:
Chapter 129 Mommy, Can You Help Yao Yao Change Her Slippers?

Since Lu Qin was going to Shen Hongda’s villa for dinner, he decided not to set up his food stall that afternoon. As soon as he sent a notification in the group chat, the chat was instantly flooded with wails of despair.

“Boss Lu, are you abandoning your children? Are you just going to watch us starve on the streets?”

“Boss Lu, you go ahead and take care of your business, I totally understand! Go, I promise not to eat anything and wait for your stall at night! But if you don’t set up at night either, I’ll have to starve until morning!”

“It’s over. I have to eat my wife’s dark cuisine tonight. I don’t want to eat burnt mantou with sparks flying from it. Someone save me!”

“It’s just a few hours! Tighten your belt and punch a few more holes in it! If you’re a real man, you’ll only eat Boss Lu’s food!”

…

Shen Muyan originally had some files to work on in the evening, but she decided not to work overtime, giving her mother some face by trying her cooking.

At Junsheng Villa, Lu Xiyao hadn’t expected to see her dad today, so she was super happy, bouncing into the villa excitedly.

“Daddy, come here, let me change your slippers for you.”

The little cotton-padded jacket (a Chinese metaphor for a daughter being thoughtful and warm) brought out a new pair of slippers for Lu Qin and helped him change into them.

“Mommy, can you help Yao Yao change her slippers?”

Shen Muyan: “?”

“I think you’re getting a little too bold.”

Shen Muyan reached out her finger, ready to flick her forehead.

“Waahh~! Mommy, I was just joking~!”

The little one quickly changed her slippers herself and ran inside, throwing herself onto the sofa.

Both Shen Hongda and Lu Qin laughed.

Shen Muyan was speechless. Lately, this little one has been getting bolder in front of me, especially when Dad and Lu Qin are around.

Jiang Qin saw Lu Qin and immediately pulled him aside to chat, just like in the past, asking about his well-being and catching up on family matters.

As they sat in the living room chatting, Jiang Qin carefully observed Lu Qin and said with some concern.

“Lu Qin, you’ve lost weight recently. Don’t overwork yourself at the stall. Since you’re doing freelance work, take a break when you’re tired.”

“It’s not tiring. Every day feels fulfilling, and life has something to look forward to.”

Lu Qin replied with a smile.

Shen Hongda noticed that Lu Qin was different from before. He seemed more energetic, more confident. He liked Lu Qin even more now.

Shen Muyan curled up on a single-seater sofa, scrolling through her phone. Occasionally, she would glance up at Lu Qin before looking back at her screen.

The phone screen showed a chat with Fang Ya.

“Shen Boss, let’s go out for some fun! Take me to Lu Qin’s place for a meal,” Fang Ya said.

Shen Muyan secretly snapped a picture of Lu Qin sitting diagonally across from her and sent it over.

“Damn! Are you two getting back together? Are you bringing Lu Qin home to get your dad’s approval?”

“What nonsense are you spouting? The little one had a mild throat infection, so he came to check on her,” Shen Muyan said.

“ Tsk, tsk. Ultimate dad material. That little one is so lucky.”

“Are you coming or not?” Shen Muyan asked.

“Nah, I’d feel awkward.”

“Since when did you get shy?”

“It’s your family dinner. I don’t want to intrude. But next time you go to Lu Qin’s place for food, you have to invite me! If you don’t, I’ll unfriend your alt account.”

Shen Muyan was speechless.

The housekeeper took some ice cream from the fridge and handed it out to everyone. Lu Xiyao was lazily lying on Lu Qin’s stomach watching TV. When she saw the housekeeper bringing ice cream, she instantly sat up like a zombie, her eyes shining.

“Thank you, Auntie!”

Just as she was about to take the ice cream, Lu Qin stopped her.

“Yao Yao, you’ve used up your ice cream quota for this week. It resets on Tuesday.”

“Waaah~! Daddy, can I have just one? I won’t have any next Tuesday, I promise!”

The little one put on her most pitiful face.

“No.”

Lu Qin was firm with his rules.

“But I really want to eat ice cream.”

Lu Xiyao put on her best tragic expression, playing the suffering child role.

“Too much ice cream isn’t good for your health. Just bear with it.”

As he spoke, Lu Qin opened his own ice cream and took a bite of the chocolate-vanilla flavor. The little one stared at him, longing for a taste.

Shen Hongda, Jiang Qin, and Shen Muyan all started eating their ice cream too. Her willpower was slowly being crushed.

I’m not a three-year-old anymore. I can resist…

But just as she was about to cry from holding back, Lu Qin finally gave her a small bite. The little one didn’t break down completely.

Jiang Qin went into the kitchen to cook and invited Lu Qin in for some guidance. Since they had a housekeeper, Jiang Qin rarely cooked.

But every time her granddaughter visited, she barely ate half a bowl of rice and only picked at the dishes. The housekeeper’s food was great, but the little one had been spoiled by Lu Qin’s cooking.

Jiang Qin wanted to learn a few signature dishes so she could cook for her precious granddaughter.

“Mom, to make the beef and potatoes flavorful, the trick is to mince the doubanjiang (fermented bean paste) before adding it.”

“For minced pork eggplant, there are two key points. First, buy tender eggplants—the ones with thin skin and no black spots in the middle. Second, before stir-frying, lightly coat the eggplant with oil.”

…

As Lu Qin gave instructions, every word was useful and insightful.

Jiang Qin had an aha moment and nodded repeatedly. She hadn’t realized even a simple stir-fried vegetable dish had so many details to consider.

The housekeeper, while washing vegetables, listened carefully. Many of these cooking techniques were new to her. She learned a lot and felt excited.

For example, the order of seasoning.

For stir-fried vegetables: salt → sugar → vinegar.

For stir-fried meat: sugar → cooking wine → vinegar → salt → soy sauce.

For stewing and braising: cooking wine → sugar → vinegar → salt → soy sauce.

Getting the order wrong could change the taste. If it weren’t for the son-in-law stepping into the kitchen today, they might never have known these tricks.

One hour later, one by one, the dishes were placed on the dining table. Dinner was quite the feast.

Shen Hongda glanced at Jiang Qin and asked, “Did you make all of this? Lu Qin didn’t help?”

Lu Qin smiled.

“I just gave instructions. Didn’t lift a finger.”

Shen Hongda looked disappointed.

I was hoping to eat his food…

Jiang Qin raised an eyebrow.

“Are you disappointed?”

Shen Hongda quickly shook his head.

“No, no! I’ve been eagerly looking forward to my dear wife’s cooking!”

His survival instincts were strong. Since Lu Qin hadn’t cooked, Shen Muyan and Lu Xiyao weren’t very excited. But out of respect for Jiang Qin, they acted interested.

Dishes included—Braised Beef with Potatoes, Stir-fried Bamboo Shoots with Pork, Minced Pork Eggplant, and a Three-Delicacy Stew.

As everyone ate, Jiang Qin asked, “How does it taste?”

“Delicious.”

Shen Hongda, Shen Muyan, and Lu Xiyao nodded in unison. Jiang Qin sensed something was off.

They’re all just being polite.

So she turned to Shen Hongda and asked, “Which dish is the best?”

Shen Hongda thought for a moment, then pointed to the Garlic Pork Ribs in the center.

“These ribs are really good. You have chef-level skills!”

Hearing this, Shen Muyan and Lu Xiyao also nodded and took another piece. Out of all the dishes, only the ribs were outstanding.

“Honey, when did you learn to make this?”

Jiang Qin said expressionlessly, “The garlic ribs were made by Lu Qin.”

Shen Hongda froze.

“Didn’t you say he didn’t cook?”

“Only this dish was made by Lu Qin,” Jiang Qin said.

Shen Hongda was speechless.

chapters.title: Chapter 130
chapters.content:
Chapter 130 If Grandpa and Grandma Don’t Want You, Daddy Will Take You In!

Shen Hongda thought to himself, I’m doomed. I completely messed up that compliment.

Why?

“Haha… cough , I was just thinking, how could these garlic pork ribs taste so good? It couldn’t possibly be you who made them. I know your cooking skills all too well…”

As he spoke, he realized something was off. His voice gradually trailed off until he completely shut his mouth. He gulped and reluctantly looked up, only to see his wife shooting dagger-like glares at him. His heart skipped a beat.

It’s over. I’m sleeping on the couch tonight.

Shen Muyan pursed her lips, trying to hold back her laughter. When she looked up at Lu Qin, she saw that he was also suppressing a smile. The two exchanged a glance and silently lowered their heads to focus on eating.

“Grandpa, Grandma’s cooking is really good! This potato and beef dish is delicious! I’ve already had three pieces!”

Lu Xiyao fluttered her innocent big eyes, unintentionally delivering another blow to Shen Hongda.

His eyelid twitched as he noticed Jiang Qin’s gaze growing even colder. He cast a resentful glance at his precious granddaughter, thinking, Why are you speaking such blunt truths? Now I’m definitely sleeping on the couch tonight.

Jiang Qin, on the other hand, beamed with joy at the little one’s praise.

“Right, Yao Yao? This is my favorite dish too! The beef is so tender. Here, have another piece!”

“Thank you, Grandma.”

Lu Xiyao happily munched on another bite. She really liked beef. Shen Muyan clicked her tongue, shaking her head.

Dad, even your flattery skills aren’t as good as Yao Yao’s.

Lu Qin placed two big pieces of eggplant and potatoes into Lu Xiyao’s bowl.

“Yao Yao, don’t just eat meat. Have some vegetables too.”

“Oh, okay, Daddy.”

The little one obediently ate them.

After finishing her vegetables, she reached out with her chopsticks for the last piece of garlic pork rib—only to see Shen Muyan take it first.

Seeing her disappointed expression, Lu Qin smiled and picked up the piece he had purposely saved in his own bowl, placing it into hers.

“Here, Yao Yao. Daddy saved this just for you.”

Her eyes sparkled with emotion.

“Wow! Daddy, you’re the best!”

She didn’t eat it right away, though. Instead, she glanced at the rib in Shen Muyan’s bowl and then asked, “Mommy, do you want to eat garlic pork ribs?”

Shen Muyan was surprised.

She looked at the little one’s rib and thought, My daughter rarely offers me food. This is touching.

She shook her head.

“Mommy doesn’t want it. You eat it, Yao Yao.”

“Okay! Thanks, Mommy!”

And then—

The little one reached out with her chopsticks, took the rib from Shen Muyan’s bowl, and put it into Lu Qin’s bowl.

“Daddy, this is for you. Now we each have one. Mommy said she didn’t want it.”

Shen Muyan: “…???”

Her fingers twitched.

This little rascal is really testing my patience these days! She’s dancing on the edge of a forehead flick!

She seriously wondered if this child was really her biological daughter. Did she have any love for her mother?

Oh, wait—earlier at lunch, she did give her that one piece of green pepper she didn’t like.

Thinking about that, Shen Muyan felt a bit sour. Now she had no pork ribs. She silently glanced at her daughter, her eyes sending tiny daggers her way.

Shen Hongda and Jiang Qin saw this and exchanged knowing smiles. The father-daughter bond between Lu Qin and Yao Yao was truly strong.

As for Shen Muyan? Oh well.

She noticed the way her parents were smiling and felt even more bitter.

After dinner, everyone sat in the living room, watching Detective Conan, with a fruit platter on the coffee table. Lu Xiyao picked up a piece of dragon fruit with her fork. First, she fed a bite to Lu Qin. Then she picked another piece and ate it herself.

Shen Hongda sipped his tea and asked Lu Qin,

“Are you going to set up your stall tonight?”

“I try to go whenever I can. I have a lot of regular customers now. Every time I set up, they’re the first ones there.”

Shen Hongda nodded.

To take a street food business this far is impressive. That’s the ceiling of the industry. He had previously asked Chen Dahai to find Lu Qin a stable job, but now he realized it was completely unnecessary.

Jiang Qin looked at Lu Qin, her eyes full of pride.

“Yao Yao, your daddy is truly outstanding!”

The little one immediately nodded.

“Of course, Grandma! No doubt about it!”

Time to Leave

After sitting in the living room for half an hour, Lu Qin stood up to leave with the little one.

“Grandpa, Grandma, Mommy, I’m going home with Daddy now. Later, I have to help as his little boss!”

“Lu Qin, drive carefully,” Jiang Qin and Shen Hongda reminded, looking reluctant to see them go.

“Okay. I’ll visit you both again soon.”

Lu Qin bid them farewell and turned to Shen Muyan.

“I’m leaving.”

“Mm,” Shen Muyan said.

Shen Hongda was displeased with that casual Mm.

Ungrateful child!

Jiang Qin pushed her daughter towards the door.

“Yan Yan, go walk Lu Qin out.”

Then, with a bam, she shut the door behind her.

Shen Muyan: “…???”

Lu Qin’s car was parked just a few steps away.

Why did she need to escort him?

And—she didn’t have her house keys!

“There’s no need to walk me out. Go back inside.”

“Mm, don’t stay out too late setting up the stall. Get some rest.”

She turned to press the doorbell.

A few seconds passed—no response.

“?”

She pressed again.

Still no response.

Shen Muyan: “…?”

She took out her phone and called Jiang Qin. The call was declined. She called Shen Hongda. Also declined.

“I… what the hell?”

She was speechless.

What are those two old folks up to?

“Mommy, did Grandpa and Grandma abandon you?”

Lu Xiyao asked excitedly.

Shen Muyan looked at her.

This little one—does your father know how hurtful your words can be?

“Mommy, don’t worry. If Grandpa and Grandma don’t want you, Daddy will take you in.”

Then she turned to Lu Qin, blinking her big adorable eyes.

Am I not the cutest little thing ever?

Lu Qin chuckled.

“Alright, then you’ll sleep with Mommy tonight.”

“Excuse me.”

Shen Muyan climbed into the passenger seat. Meanwhile, the little one sat in the back, playing on her tablet. Lu Qin drove them back to his rental.

“You two rest. I’m going to prepare the food for the stall.”

He placed a pitcher of lemon water on the coffee table. Shen Muyan hesitated for a moment, then stood up, her gaze firm. She was dressed in casual home attire—a beige cotton T-shirt paired with khaki casual slacks. Her long, slender legs were elegantly crossed.

Her outfit was loose-fitting, yet it still highlighted her graceful figure. Her cool, stunning face, combined with a simple high ponytail, made her look both elegant and slightly youthful.

“I’ll help you.”

The little one quickly turned off the TV and ran over.

“I’ll help too! I’m a diligent vegetable washer!”

Lu Qin nodded and accepted their help.

With helpers, he could save some time. He handed a bowl of mung beans to the little one.

“Wash them five times. They may look clean, but they’re actually quite dirty.”

He assigned Shen Muyan to prepare the shrimp.

“Trim the whiskers and legs first, then remove the vein before peeling the meat.”

He demonstrated once, then observed her do it.

After confirming she was fine, he moved on to kneading the dough.

“Be careful not to cut yourself.”

“Mm, got it.”

And so—

The three of them busied themselves in the kitchen together.

chapters.title: Chapter 131
chapters.content:
Chapter 131 Daddy, Put Mommy’s Finger in Your Mouth to Stop the Bleeding!

Twenty-five pounds of mung beans—Lu Xiyao used her tiny hands to scrub them for a long time. Then, using all her strength, she drained the water and rinsed them repeatedly.

After carefully washing the mung beans five times, the little one finally finished.

“Daddy, I’m done!”

Meanwhile, Lu Qin had finished kneading the dough and left it to rest on the counter. He had precisely timed the resting period to the second. Without using a timer, he glanced at his phone and memorized the time.

“Yao Yao, I’ll handle the rest.”

Lu Qin poured the mung beans into a stainless steel heating pot, adding six times the amount of water, yellow rock sugar, and lemon slices. Finally, he took a large amount of ice from the freezer and added it to the pot.

Adding ice helped the mung beans soften faster during cooking. Just as he started cooking the mung bean soup, he suddenly heard a soft gasp.

“Ah!”

Lu Qin quickly turned around and saw Shen Muyan frowning slightly, holding her finger.

“What happened?”

“Mommy, what’s wrong?”

Both Lu Qin and Lu Xiyao hurried over.

“I got pricked by the shrimp head’s sharp point. I think I’m bleeding.”

It was just a minor cut, but it still stung.

“Daddy, quick! Put Mommy’s finger in your mouth! It will stop the bleeding!”

The little one’s mischievous mind sparked with an idea.

Lu Qin: “…”

Shen Muyan: “…”

“Yao Yao, you need to stop watching those weird TV shows. A lot of what they say isn’t true.”

“Oh.”

Lu Qin gently held Shen Muyan’s injured finger. Her skin was delicate and cool to the touch.

“It’s not a big deal. These shrimp are quite clean, but to prevent any bacterial infection, I’ll squeeze the blood out.”

“Okay.”

Shen Muyan trusted him completely. She had never peeled shrimp before and had never been pricked by one. She thought she could just slap on a band-aid and be fine.

“ Hiss… it hurts!”

“Bear with it… done.”

Lu Qin carefully squeezed out a few drops of blood from her fingertip. Then, he took a bar of soap and washed her hand thoroughly before drying it and applying a Pikachu-themed band-aid.

Shen Muyan looked at Lu Qin’s sharp features.

He was so careful and focused that it warmed her heart. Her entire hand was wrapped in his. She could feel his warmth.

A faint blush appeared on her fair cheeks. Under the kitchen lights, her face glowed beautifully.

“Haah~ Haah~”

Lu Xiyao gently blew on her mom’s finger.

“Mommy, I’ll blow on it for you. It’ll heal faster!”

Shen Muyan ruffled the little one’s hair.

“Alright, you two won’t be able to help with the rest. Go rest in the living room.”

With that, she and Yao Yao were kicked out of the kitchen.

In the living room, Lu Xiyao handed her mother a glass of lemon water.

“Mommy, have some water.”

Shen Muyan took a sip—sweet and tangy, perfectly balanced. Lu Qin’s lemon water tasted different from any she had before. Everything he made was special.

“Mommy, I heard that eating ice cream makes minor injuries heal faster. Do you want to try it?”

The little one’s big, sparkly eyes shined with expectation.

“Who told you that?”

“A little fairy.”

“This little fairy… is her name Lu Xiyao?”

“Hehe~!”

“If you call me a fairy, I’ll get you an ice cream.”

“Fairy~!” The little one immediately called out.

“Now say, ‘Mommy, you’re the best mommy in the whole world.’ Let me hear it.”

“Mommy, you’re the best mommy in the whole world~!”

The little one shouted enthusiastically.

“Alright, I’ll go get it for you.”

Shen Muyan got up and walked towards the fridge.

“Awesome~!”

The little one was pleased with herself.

With just a few sweet words, she had tricked her mom into getting her an ice cream.

“Mommy, I want vanilla-flavored. It’s on the top shelf of the freezer.”

“Got it, wait here.”

Shen Muyan walked to the fridge, but instead of opening it, she slid open the kitchen door.

“Lu Qin, your daughter wants ice cream. I wouldn’t let her have it, and she called me a bad mommy. What should we do?”

She reported the crime in all seriousness.

Lu Xiyao: “???”

Lu Qin turned off the stove and walked out with a serious expression.

“Lu Xiyao! No ice cream for you next week!”

Lu Xiyao: “!!!”

“Waaah~!”

Sneaky Mommy! I’ve been tricked!

Lu Qin went back to the kitchen, while Shen Muyan happily took out a vanilla ice cream for herself and sat on the couch, enjoying it.

The little one pouted, swallowing her saliva.

“Sweetheart, do you want some?”

Shen Muyan teased.

The little one nodded vigorously.

“Oh no, my legs feel a bit sore. Maybe I walked too much and got tired.”

She stretched her long, fair legs onto the coffee table. The little one got the hint right away and started massaging her mom’s legs with her tiny hands.

As Shen Muyan enjoyed her ice cream and a massage from her daughter, she felt incredibly content.

“Mommy, eat slower. Save some for me.”

In the end, the little one traded five minutes of leg massage for one-third of the ice cream. She ate it happily, even licking the stick for a long time.

In the kitchen, Lu Qin finished making—Mung Bean Soup (from 25 lbs of beans), 312 boxes of Bingjing Cakes, 850 Crystal Shrimp Dumplings, 200 cups of Osmanthus Oolong Milk Tea and 80 lbs of Anyang Rice Noodles, packed in clean baskets and covered with gauze. Everything was ready. He loaded everything onto his three-wheeler food cart.

“You drive Yao Yao. I’ll ride the three-wheeler. We’re heading to University Town Night Market.”

“Let’s go!”

The little one pumped her tiny fist in excitement.

At the Night Market, when they arrived, Shen Muyan finally realized how popular Lu Qin’s food stall was. A huge crowd of eager customers—easily 80 to 100 people—were already waiting in line.

They weren’t even open yet, but the customers had arrived early!

“Boss Lu! Finally, you’re here! Do you know how close I was to suing you for intentional starvation? You owe me ten orders of fried rice noodles as compensation!”

“Boss Lu, is this the little boss and the lady boss? Wow, they’re both beauties!”

“That must be Mrs. Lu! Damn, with this face and body, only a woman like her could match Boss Lu!”

“I’m starving! If I spit now, I’d probably find twenty hunger bugs in my saliva!”

“Boss Lu has it all—savory, sweet, main courses, snacks, drinks. It’s a one-stop shop for night market cravings!”

Regular customers quickly helped set up the stall—unfolding tables, arranging trays of food. Lu Qin stir-fried the rice noodles while Shen Muyan and the little one sat on small stools, selling the desserts and drinks.

Customers split into two lines, smoothly taking turns. Boxes of food were selling so fast, they couldn’t even keep up with packaging.

“Boss lady, forget the packaging! I’ll just grab it and go!”

Many couldn’t wait another second. They scanned the payment QR code and ran off with their food, finding a spot nearby to eat immediately.

Watching the boxes of food disappear at lightning speed, Shen Muyan was stunned. Even though this was retail, it was selling faster than wholesale!

Her phone wallet balance kept rising.

At that moment, she realized—Selling street food was way more satisfying than an office job!

Office paychecks came once a month.

Here? The money poured in every second!

chapters.title: Chapter 132
chapters.content:
Chapter 132 Why Drink Milk Tea for Sore Hands? This Man Is Showing Off!

Shen Muyan had her first experience as the big boss lady, and it felt pretty good. The only problem? She wasn’t great at packaging food. She was even slower than the little one with her tiny hands. She turned her head and saw Lu Qin continuously stir-frying rice noodles with a completely relaxed expression.

Is flipping a wok easier than packing food? That is impossible.

It could only mean one thing—Lu Qin is amazing.

A man who can cook is so attractive!

Shen Muyan stared at him for a few seconds, her heartbeat quickening.

Stop looking! Any longer and I might fall for him again.

Some regular customers didn’t even bother waiting for them to pack their food. They just scanned the payment code, grabbed their orders, packed them themselves, and left.

“Mommy, do you want a cup of milk tea? My hands are sore.”

“Why drink milk tea for sore hands?”

“Because milk tea makes me happy, and when I’m happy, my hands won’t feel sore anymore.”

“Boss Lu, the little boss wants milk tea. Can she have one?”

“Sure. Looks like I’m taking a loss tonight—hired two boss ladies, they didn’t do much work, and now I’m giving away milk tea.”

Lu Qin glanced at the mother and daughter with a playful smile. Shen Muyan and Lu Xiyao exchanged a glance and giggled.

Shen Muyan grabbed a cup of osmanthus oolong milk tea, poked a straw into it, let the little one take a sip first, and then took a sip herself.

The icy, sweet, and smooth milk tea flowed through her mouth and down her throat—it was incredibly satisfying!

At 9 PM—Everything Sold Out

Tonight was a bit later than usual, but they had also prepared more food. Most of the customers got what they wanted, leaving very satisfied. Lu Qin saved two portions of stir-fried rice noodles—one for himself and one for the mother and daughter to share as a late-night snack.

They had already had two cups of milk tea and a serving of shrimp dumplings, so they couldn’t eat too much at night.

On the way back, Shen Muyan calculated the night’s earnings for Lu Qin.

She wasn’t expecting much, but when she finished counting, she was shocked.

413 orders of stir-fried rice noodles, 312 boxes of bingjing cakes, 378 cups of mung bean soup…

She tapped on the phone calculator.

“The total is… 24,796 yuan!”

She was stunned—this was just one night!

The little one clapped her hands excitedly.

“Wow! So much money! Can we buy lots and lots of ice cream?”

“It’s okay, after deducting costs, the net profit is about 20,000,” Lu Qin replied casually.

Shen Muyan pursed her lips and looked at him.

This man is totally showing off!

But… he’s really impressive.

“Alright, you two go wash up. I’ll clean up the food cart.”

“I’ll help.”

“Daddy, I want to help too!”

They knew that Lu Qin was always the most hardworking one.

Most of the time, they were just there for fun. They couldn’t help with everything, but they could at least do some cleaning.

“No need. I’m fast. I’ll finish in twenty minutes. If you help, it’ll actually slow me down.”

“Yao Yao, you have kindergarten tomorrow. You need to go to bed early.”

Shen Muyan wanted to insist, but when she heard that, she gave up.

“Daddy, can I skip kindergarten tomorrow? I want to be the little boss lady!”

“My fingers are suddenly feeling itchy,” Lu Qin said with a playful smile.

“Waaah~! I’ll go to kindergarten tomorrow like a good girl.”

The little one held her forehead in panic as Shen Muyan dragged her off to wash up.

Lu Qin changed into slippers, rolled up his sleeves and pants, and turned on the outdoor faucet. He attached a hose and tossed it into a large stainless steel basin, beginning to clean the pots, pans, baskets, heating containers, and folding tables.

The dish soap and cleaning cloth he won from the system were incredibly useful—three to four times more effective than regular ones and much easier to use.

Fifteen minutes later, Lu Qin hung the washed apron out to dry and locked the door before heading inside. He poured himself a glass of water, drank it, and walked towards the bathroom.

The bathroom was occupied. It was Shen Muyan. She had just finished showering and was blow-drying her hair. She was wearing his Mickey Mouse T-shirt and a pair of blue shorts.

Her long, flawless legs stretched down gracefully. Her black hair swayed slightly under the hairdryer as a faint, elegant fragrance drifted towards Lu Qin.

He was about to leave when she turned off the dryer, making the bathroom suddenly silent.

“I’m done. You can use it now.”

She hung up the hairdryer and brushed past him. Her soft, silky hair grazed his ear—it smelled really good.

Lu Qin steadied himself, shook his head slightly, and walked over to the sink. Then he noticed something. His toothbrush already had toothpaste on it. The cup was already filled with water.

Meanwhile, at the door, outside the bathroom, Shen Muyan leaned against the door. She pressed a hand against her chest. Her heart had started beating faster just now. After calming herself, she entered Lu Xiyao’s room.

Lu Qin quickly washed up and got ready for bed. Before entering his room, he checked on the little one. He knocked gently and pushed the door open.

The mother and daughter were still awake. The little one was rolling around the bed non-stop, and Shen Muyan couldn’t get her to settle down.

So—she started tickling her.

“Ahahaha~!”

The little one laughed uncontrollably and begged for mercy.

“Daddy! Save your little cutie! She’s going to die of laughter!”

Lu Qin chuckled.

“Go to sleep now. It’s getting late.”

At that moment, Shen Muyan’s loose Mickey Mouse T-shirt shifted slightly, revealing her tiny, firm waist. Her waistline was incredibly attractive. Her long legs were casually crossed, and her bare feet glowed under the warm light. Lu Qin felt his composure slipping.

“Yao Yao, cover yourself properly with the blanket. Don’t catch a cold.”

He adjusted the air conditioner to 27°C, set the fan to the lowest setting, and turned on the humidifier in the corner.

“Daddy, we’re going to sleep now. Good night!”

“Lu Qin, good night.”

“Good night. Sweet dreams.”

Lu Qin turned off the lights.

“By the way, don’t tell the story of the little girl selling missiles again before bed. She already ran off with the whole village to find her grandma.”

Late Night—System Rewards

Lying in bed, Lu Qin opened his system panel.

System Points: 4097

He started drawing rewards.

【Used 100 points: Won 100 mint candies.】

【Used 100 points: Won advanced fruit peeling skills.】

【Used 100 points: Won advanced fruit cutting skills.】

【Used 100 points: Won a 75L Fresh-Lock Thermostat Box.】

【Used 100 points: Won max-level Mapo Tofu cooking skills.】

After 40 draws, he obtained many useful rewards. The Mapo Tofu and Kimchi-making skills were the most valuable. The Fresh-Lock Thermostat Box was also a great find.

Lu Qin took it out from the system—it was fully charged from the factory.

Perfect.

He also won stat boosts in Strength, Stamina, Agility, and Intelligence—though only 0.1 points each, they added up over time. His physical condition was gradually approaching that of an athlete—even without working out.

Lu Qin dumped the mint candies onto his nightstand and decided to organize them tomorrow.

That night, Lu Qin dreamed of a beautiful day. He was driving along a scenic coastal highway. The view was breathtaking—but the most beautiful sight was in the backseat.

Shen Muyan and Lu Xiyao were playing a fruit-cutting game together. The car was filled with laughter and joy. The journey was lighthearted and full of warmth.

chapters.title: Chapter 133
chapters.content:
Chapter 133 Good Morning, Husband, Mwah!

Lu Qin had a wonderful dream and woke up feeling refreshed the next morning. He packed the peppermint candies from his bedside table into a bag and carried the second fresh-locking thermostat box to the kitchen.

With two fresh-locking thermostat boxes, the storage space doubled, meaning he could prepare and sell more food at his stall at once. Since the kitchen only had two burners, Lu Qin planned to add a simple gas stove in the courtyard, increasing the number of burners to four.

Now, he could simultaneously prepare crystal ice cakes, crystal shrimp dumplings, and mung bean paste without having to wait. Yesterday, he won a lucky draw and obtained max-level Mapo tofu and kimchi recipes. He planned to buy the necessary ingredients in the morning to try out these new dishes.

He also needed to buy a gas stove, shelves, and kimchi jars. Even with his car, it wouldn’t all fit, so he had to trouble Boss Zhao from the disposable tableware shop again. After freshening up, Lu Qin fried three portions of stir-fried rice noodles and packed them, preparing to head out.

Just then, Shen Muyan walked out of her room. Her snow-white skin, alluring figure, and stunning beauty made her look breathtaking, even though she was only wearing a Mickey Mouse T-shirt and shorts.

She looked drowsy, her eyes still half-closed, clearly not fully awake yet. Yawning slightly, she walked towards the bathroom.

Upon seeing Lu Qin, she naturally greeted him.

“Good morning, husband, mwah!”

Smooch!

After saying this, she leaned in and kissed Lu Qin’s cheek before walking into the bathroom to use the toilet. Everything happened so naturally, as if Shen Muyan and Lu Qin were already a loving married couple.

Lu Qin stood frozen in place.

What just happened?

Inside the bathroom, Shen Muyan, eyes half-closed, sat on the toilet in a daze for a few seconds as her mind slowly cleared. Suddenly, her beautiful eyes widened in shock.

What had she just done?

That wasn’t a dream!

It was real!

She had woken up, got out of bed, and went to the bathroom!

Which meant…

She had actually kissed Lu Qin in real life!

She had even called him “husband”!

And even added a “mwah”!

Swoosh!

Her fair cheeks turned red, blushing all the way to her ears.

Oh no!

That was definitely not her line!

This was beyond embarrassing!

Shen Muyan covered her face with both hands, frozen in place, unsure of what to do. Outside, Lu Qin had a strange expression on his face.

“Was she… still half-asleep just now?”

The words husband and mwah left a ripple of emotions in his heart. But he shook his head. That scene just now didn’t match Shen Muyan’s usual persona. She must have been confused from just waking up.

Deciding it was best to pretend it never happened to avoid awkwardness, Lu Qin adjusted his emotions and drove off. Inside the bathroom, Shen Muyan didn’t dare to move.

She listened carefully and heard the door close.

He… had left?

She carefully cracked open the bathroom door, confirmed that Lu Qin was really gone, and only then stepped out. Her cheeks were still burning red, making her look incredibly adorable, a stark contrast to her usual cold and aloof demeanor.

At that moment, Lu Xiyao also woke up, rubbing her eyes.

“Mommy, I need to use the bathroom too. Huh? Why is your face so red?”

“N-nothing! The bathroom was just too hot.”

“Oh.”

Lu Xiyao didn’t suspect anything and ran into the bathroom.

Shen Muyan, her mind a complete mess, returned to her room and flopped onto the bed. Last night, after falling asleep, she had dreamt of living happily with Lu Qin and their little one.

Lu Qin cooked for her every day, a thoughtful, gentle, and handsome man—every woman’s dream. And she had him. She felt incredibly happy, but that happiness didn’t last long because she woke up.

She had woken up because she needed to use the bathroom after drinking too much lemon water and osmanthus oolong milk tea before bed.

Then, she got up in a daze, half-asleep, mistook the real Lu Qin for the one in her dream, and…

Burying her face into the pillow like an ostrich, she had no idea how she would face Lu Qin later.

Just thinking about it made her want to die of embarrassment.

_____

Meanwhile, Lu Qin drove to the market in a good mood.

“Boss Zhao, did you find the outdoor gas stove I asked about?”

Lu Qin walked up to the disposable tableware store and placed the three packed rice noodles on the table.

“You always bring food when you come by! Haha, but I love it.”

Although stir-fried rice noodles weren’t expensive, they were delicious. Boss Zhao never turned them down. He could just give Lu Qin a few extra takeaway containers in return—it was a win-win for their long-term cooperation.

“Of course, I found it for you! Here, take a look at the pictures.”

Boss Zhao pulled out his phone and showed Lu Qin the images.

“A stainless steel portable model—traditional clay stove on the left, gas stove on the right. Comes with a big iron wok and a blower. What do you think? I checked the quality for you.”

Lu Qin was satisfied.

“How much?”

“Internal price—1,500. They usually go for 1,800, but I got you a deal.”

Lu Qin nodded. The price was a steal, without connections, it would be impossible to get such a good deal.

“But it’s a bit bulky and hard to transport.”

“It’s detachable and easy to assemble. Don’t worry, Lu Qin, I’ll deliver it to you at noon and help set it up.”

“Alright, I’ll treat you to lunch then.”

“Haha, now that’s a deal!”

Lu Qin also bought some disposable meal boxes and asked Boss Zhao to help him find kimchi jars.

“That’s easy! Just down the next street. Do you want glass or ceramic?”

“Ceramic. Medium size. I need five.”

“Got it, I’ll bring them over when I deliver the stove.”

After chatting a bit more, Lu Qin headed into the market for groceries. At Jingyuan Market, he was a familiar face, and since he always bought in bulk, the vendors welcomed him warmly.

“Lu Qin, you’re here! What do you need today?”

“How much are the yardlong beans?”

“New recipe, huh? Normally 4.2 per pound, but for you, 4.”

“I’ll take 5 pounds.”

Yardlong beans were for making kimchi. Lu Qin also bought white radish, cabbage, ginger, garlic, and chili peppers—all kimchi ingredients—spending 181.50 yuan in total.

He then bought 50 yuan worth of coagulated tofu, 5 pounds of beef, 2 pounds of garlic sprouts, and 3 bottles of Pixian broad bean paste for Mapo tofu.

For his usual dishes, he stocked up on Anyang rice noodles, mung beans, pork leg meat, and chicken breast—but in 50% larger quantities. Thanks to his second fresh-locking thermostat box, he could now store more food.

After loading his car with multiple bags of ingredients, vendors even gifted him fresh lychees and grapes at wholesale prices.

Lu Qin gladly accepted—it was all part of good business relationships.

______

Back in the old neighborhood, the mother and daughter were still asleep. Lu Xiyao was sprawled across the bed, sleeping soundly. Shen Muyan, however, hadn’t slept at all.

She covered the little one with a blanket, ensuring her tummy and feet were warm, then sat in a daze. Her face was still flushed, as if she had a fever.

Not wanting to disturb them, Lu Qin quietly carried the ingredients inside. He then noticed something in the system inventory—a glass jar of powder he had received when he won the Max-Level Kimchi Making Skill in the lucky draw. He had been too busy to check before, but now he finally did.

The system explained.

Lu Qin took a deep breath and uttered one word.

“Awesome!”

chapters.title: Chapter 134
chapters.content:
Chapter 134 Making Kimchi! Do You Like Daddy?

At 5:30 AM, Lu Qin started preparing various foods for his street stall. He ground glutinous rice into flour, mixed it with water, white sugar, and other ingredients, kneaded the dough, oiled it, and let it rest—this was for making crystal ice cakes.

While the dough was resting, he prepared the dough for crystal shrimp dumplings. Using 3,500 grams of starch and 1,500 grams of wheat starch, he mixed them with warm water at about 45°C. The key was the mixing technique—there couldn’t be any lumps.

After letting the slurry sit for two minutes, he poured some starch onto the counter and started kneading. The dough for the crystal shrimp dumplings needed to be smooth and elastic while looking transparent—only then would the dumpling skins be soft, chewy, and translucent.

The dumpling dough was also left to rest. Next, he washed and soaked mung beans for mung bean paste. While they were cooking, he started making kimchi.

Lu Qin carefully washed fresh, plump vegetables—yardlong beans, cabbage, radish—then let them air dry slightly to remove excess water.

After cutting the radish, ginger, and cabbage, he washed the kimchi jars, then layered the cut vegetables inside, adding chili peppers for flavor, garlic for sterilization, and Sichuan peppercorns for aroma.

The next step was adding old brine, which acted like yeast in dough fermentation. It inhibited unwanted bacteria while allowing lactic acid bacteria to thrive. This transformed the vegetables’ natural sugars into lactic acid, giving kimchi its signature tangy flavor.

Since this was Lu Qin’s first time making kimchi, he had no old brine. Instead, he mixed salt and cooled boiled water at a 1:50 ratio in a stainless steel basin. New brine wasn’t ideal, but with a little system-grade fermentation accelerator, it would work even better than old brine.

After sealing the jars, he poured water into the rims to block air and bacteria. The kimchi was now ready—he just had to let the microbes work their magic.

Thanks to the system’s scientific boost, the kimchi would be ready by tomorrow instead of the usual 15 days. He made five jars of kimchi and placed them under the eaves outside.

By then, the mung bean paste was nearly done. He stirred it with a long spoon, then left it to cool before portioning it into cups and sealing them.

______

At 7:15 AM, Lu Qin had finished making crystal ice cakes and crystal shrimp dumplings. Today, Lu Xiyao didn’t stay in bed. As soon as she woke up, she ran outside on her little legs to find Lu Qin.

“Daddy, hug me!”

Lu Qin smiled, wiped his hands, and lifted the little girl into his arms. She pressed her soft cheek against his for a while until she was fully awake.

“Daddy, I’m hungry.”

“Go wash up first. Breakfast is stir-fried rice noodles and mung bean paste.”

“Okay, Daddy~!”

She ran off.

Shen Muyan had also gotten up. She hadn’t slept at all after last night—her mind was buzzing. When they met, Lu Qin acted naturally, pretending nothing had happened, making it less awkward.

Meanwhile, Shen Muyan avoided his gaze, her fair cheeks still flushed.

“You should wash up too. Breakfast is ready.”

“Oh.”

She nodded quickly and disappeared into the bathroom.

_____

After washing up, mother and daughter came out together.

Lu Xiyao kissed Lu Qin’s cheek again.

“Daddy, does it smell nice? It’s white peach-flavored toothpaste.”

“Mmm, very nice. Breakfast is on the table, eat slowly.”

“Wow, smells so good~!”

The little girl climbed onto the chair and started eating happily.

When Shen Muyan saw her daughter kiss Lu Qin, she immediately remembered last night—her face turned even redder.

____

After breakfast, Lu Xiyao had to go to kindergarten. Lu Qin packed her lunchbox—Stir-fried rice noodles, Crystal shrimp dumplings, Mapo tofu, Mung bean paste drink and Crystal ice cake as a snack. It was perfectly prepared.

She pointed at the container with Mapo tofu and asked in her soft voice.

“Daddy, what’s this?”

“Mapo tofu, a new dish.”

“Wow! Can I try it?”

Her eyes sparkled, and her little mouth watered.

“Sure.”

Lu Qin scooped up a piece, coated in glossy red chili oil, blew on it to cool it down, and fed it to her.

Traditionally, Mapo tofu is best eaten hot and spicy, but that could hurt a child’s stomach. So, Lu Qin had toned down the spice—keeping the chili’s fragrance while making it milder.

The little girl ate it enthusiastically, squinting her eyes and sticking out her tongue.

“ Phew~! It’s spicy! But it’s so delicious~! Daddy, this Mapo tofu is amazing~!”

Lu Qin smiled.

“It’ll be milder when you eat it with rice at lunch.”

“Okay!”

Then, he noticed Shen Muyan swallowing hard, staring at the Mapo tofu like a hungry cat. Lu Qin scooped up another spoonful and offered it to her.

“Here, try it.”

The little girl tilted her head.

“Daddy, you forgot to blow on it. It’s hot.”

“…”

Lu Qin blew on it, then fed it to Shen Muyan.

She took a bite.

“…Mm, it’s delicious!”

She was amazed—the Mapo tofu was rich, fragrant, and spicy, instantly whetting her appetite.

“Did you make lunch for me too?”

She shamelessly asked. Even though her Ourei Grand Hotel had multiple fine-dining restaurants, none could compare to Lu Qin’s cooking.

“Yeah, here.”

Lu Qin handed her a bag containing the same meal as their daughter’s.

“Thanks.”

She looked genuinely happy and touched.

______

At that moment, Lu Xiyao clapped her hands and asked mischievously, “Mommy, is Daddy good?”

Shen Muyan blushed and nodded.

“Good.”

“Is Daddy’s food delicious?”

“Yes.”

“Then will you come back next time?”

“Yes.”

“Then… do you like Daddy?”

“…”

Lu Qin: “…”

______

Since Shen Muyan hadn’t driven last night, Lu Qin let her take his car to drop their daughter off at kindergarten. He would get it sent back later. He himself rode his three-wheeler food cart towards Sunshine Elementary School.

At Starlight Kindergarten, many parents were surprised to see Shen Muyan instead of Lu Qin.

“Oh? It’s Mommy dropping her off today?”

“Yes.”

She responded in her usual cold CEO demeanor. Seeing this, the parents didn’t try to chat and quickly left after sending their kids inside.

Meanwhile, Lu Xiyao was thrilled—her mom hadn’t taken her to school in a long time.

“Drink lots of water, listen to the teacher, and eat your lunch properly.”

“I will, Mommy~!”

“Give Mommy a kiss, then go in.”

Smooch!

Shen Muyan watched her daughter happily walk into kindergarten, then rolled up the window and drove towards Ourei Grand Hotel.

______

Meanwhile, Lu Qin sent an announcement in the customer chat group.

The chat exploded with excited messages. Many office workers, even those working in the north of the city, detoured to the south just for breakfast.

Being late to work meant getting scolded by the boss, but missing Lu Qin’s breakfast meant suffering all day!

Residents of the old town—dog walkers, grocery shoppers, parents dropping off kids—all changed direction, flocking to Sunshine Elementary School.

“Wait! I think I see Lu Qin’s car!”

A customer on an electric scooter spotted a black M7 at a red light.

“What’s the license plate? 4997?”

“Yes! That’s Lu Qin’s car!”

“Where did you see it?”

“Baishui Road, near the leather factory! He’s heading toward Sunshine Elementary! Follow him—I can already smell the stir-fried rice noodles!”

The scooter riders sped up, more excited than seeing a celebrity.

chapters.title: Chapter 135
chapters.content:
Chapter 135 The Customers Followed the Wrong Car! But You’re Different—You’re a Rich Lady!

There were quite a few traffic lights on Nanhua North Road, so Shen Muyan was driving in a stop-and-go manner. Little did she know that behind her, eight electric scooters and two small cars were closely following.

These customers were chatting in a group chat.

After waiting through several red lights, a long line of customers had already gathered behind Shen Muyan’s car!

Shen Muyan glanced in the rearview mirror and felt a bit strange. It seemed like… she was being followed by a group of people. Some of the electric scooters looked familiar—they had been following the same route as her.

She figured it might just be a coincidence since she had been driving straight without making any turns. So she didn’t pay it much mind. As soon as the light turned green, she stepped on the accelerator and drove off.

After passing two more intersections, she turned right. Another 1.4 kilometers straight ahead, and she would arrive at the Ourei Grand Hotel.

“???”

“What’s going on? Weren’t we heading to Sunshine Primary School? Why did she turn right?”

“Did Boss Lu get lost? Is he just driving randomly?”

“Impossible. Boss Lu isn’t new to this route. He wouldn’t get lost. He must be stopping to refuel.”

“Yeah, that’s very likely. His car is a hybrid—probably forgot to charge it last night.”

“Just keep following him. We need to buy breakfast and get to work—we’re running late.”

The group analyzed the situation in the chat and decided to keep following the car.

Until…

The car stopped in front of the Ourei Grand Hotel. A hotel valet saw Shen Muyan get out of the car. He was momentarily stunned but quickly walked over to take her keys.

“President Shen, new car? I’ll park it in the garage for you.”

“Wash it first.”

“Understood.”

Meanwhile, twenty-something electric scooters rushed to catch up, arriving at the entrance of the Ourei Hotel, along with five or six cars… and two ‘Steamed Fish Heads with Chopped Chili.’

The security guard was utterly confused.

“What are you guys doing here?”

A whole crowd like this? There was no way they were here to dine at the hotel.

Hearing the commotion, Shen Muyan and the valet turned around to look. Both of them were equally puzzled.

The security guard cautiously said, “President Shen, it seems like… these people all followed you here.”

Shen Muyan looked at them, frowning slightly. “You’ve been following me the whole way?”

The group of customers suddenly realized that the person in the car wasn’t Boss Lu. They were completely dumbfounded!

“Wait… it’s the boss’s wife?”

Last night at the night market, everyone had seen Lu Qin with this incredibly beautiful lady. Before, they had always wondered what kind of genes could produce such a stunningly cute daughter. The moment they saw the boss’s wife last night, they understood.

The crowd panicked. They had followed the wrong car! If Boss Lu wasn’t driving his car, he must have been riding his tricycle food cart. By now, he was probably already near the entrance of Sunshine Primary School.

Damn!

It felt like they had been lured away on purpose!

“We thought you were Boss Lu! We were following him to his food stand!”

Shen Muyan: “…”

“I knew something was off—the route was all wrong!”

“Damn! Who was the first one to start following? They led everyone astray!”

“It was that guy! The one with the nickname [So Handsome, People Want to Attack Me] in the group!”

A young man with acne, wearing a yellow helmet and riding an electric scooter, looked nervous and stayed silent.

“I don’t know if he’s handsome, but getting beaten up seems inevitable!”

“Enough talking! We need to get to Sunshine Primary School!”

…

Reminded of their mistake, more than thirty people hastily turned their vehicles around and rushed off in the other direction. The security guard and the valet exchanged bewildered glances.

What exactly just happened?

Who was the real boss?

And who was President Shen’s husband?

Such explosive gossip!

They sneaked a glance at the goddess-like President Shen, but she maintained her cold and indifferent expression, saying nothing as she turned and walked into the hotel.

…

Meanwhile, Lu Qin arrived at Sunshine Primary School on his tricycle food cart and found no early customers waiting. He thought it was a bit odd. But he didn’t dwell on it. As soon as he set up his stall, the residents from the nearby community rushed over to buy breakfast.

“Wow! I’m the first one here today—what good luck!”

“Weird, not many people today! First come, first served!”

“Yes! Finally, I don’t have to wait in line. I’ll have three portions of stir-fried noodles, two cups of mung bean soup, and two ice crystal cakes! Do you have crystal shrimp dumplings? I’ll take a serving!”

“Boss Lu, I’ll have three servings of fried rice noodles and three cups of mung bean soup! Is the mung bean soup hot? I prefer it cold.”

“Drink it hot in the morning—cold food is bad for your stomach. Health comes first so you can enjoy Boss Lu’s food for longer!”

“Boss Lu doesn’t sell cold drinks in the morning? He’s looking out for our health—such a good man!”

A few minutes later, a group of ‘lost sheep’ finally arrived, grumbling.

“Damn! Boss Lu, we followed your M7 all the way to the Ourei Grand Hotel! You should’ve told us you weren’t in the car!”

“Boss Lu, just so we’re clear—I have no interest in your stunning wife! I’m only interested in your food!”

“This was exhausting! Two servings of fried noodles and one mung bean soup—takeout! I’m late for work!”

Hearing the complaints, Lu Qin was momentarily stunned before realizing what had happened.

“Next time, just follow the notifications in the Penguin group,” he said with a smile.

Despite the small hiccup, his food stand remained busy, with a constant flow of customers and no idle moments.

Lu Qin’s food stand had already gained some fame in Jiangcheng, and he had a large group of loyal customers in the group chat. His only problem was running out of stock, never struggling to sell.

At 9:45 AM, Lu Qin packed up his stall and counted his earnings—16,455 yuan.

At noon, Zhao, the owner of a disposable tableware store, arrived in a small pickup truck to deliver food containers, an outdoor gas stove, and some miscellaneous ingredients. Together, they carried everything into the yard.

Assembling the gas stove was straightforward. Lu Qin and Zhao finished setting it up in twenty minutes. Zhao also brought over a large bag of coal. The left-side stove was for burning firewood, but these days, even in rural areas, most people used coal instead of wood—it wasn’t environmentally friendly to chop trees for fuel.

“This is the coal supplier’s contact. Just call when you need more, and they’ll deliver it to your door,” Zhao said, having thought of everything.

“Thanks. I’ll cook lunch—let’s have a simple meal together,” Lu Qin said and invited.

Zhao didn’t refuse, swallowing his saliva in anticipation. Twenty minutes later, Lu Qin served the dishes.

“I’ll help you bring them over,” Zhao said eagerly.

Lunch consisted of twice-cooked pork, imperial concubine chicken wings, and tomato scrambled eggs. All made with maxed-out cooking skills—fragrant and irresistible.

“Dig in, Zhao. No need to be polite,” Lu Qin said.

“Alright,” Zhao said, finishing three bowls of rice but hesitating to get a fourth, worried about seeming rude.

Lu Qin refilled his bowl with a smile.

“Eat as much as you want—it means you appreciate my cooking, and that makes me happy.”

Zhao took the bowl, praising, “Boss Lu, your cooking is unmatched. Your wife and daughter are truly lucky!”

chapters.title: Chapter 136
chapters.content:
Chapter 136 Fusion Cuisine Tasting!

Fang Ya sent an address: Jingtai Grand Hotel. Lu Qin explained to Fang Ya that he wasn’t a professional food critic and could only do his best. After all, he wasn’t a gourmet, he was just a chef.

Fang Ya didn’t see it that way. Back when Hu Zhijun from Zijing Restaurant made a liver paste soup, Lu Qin had only looked at a photo and, with his sharp eyes, immediately pointed out three fatal flaws. So, in Fang Ya’s eyes, Lu Qin was absolutely incredible.

The tasting was set for 1 PM. Lu Qin tidied himself up and prepared to take a taxi since his car had been taken by Shen Muyan. Unexpectedly, Shen Muyan had already arranged for someone to return his car—and it had been washed too. The one who drove the car back was Shen Muyan’s lovely young secretary, Song Xuanxuan.

Song Xuanxuan, a self-proclaimed foodie, looked at Lu Qin with eyes full of admiration—almost sparkling. Having finally gotten the chance to meet Lu Qin in private, Song Xuanxuan shamelessly asked if there was anything to eat—anything at all, even a pot lid, she joked.

Lu Qin hadn’t cooked anything fresh yet, and his fridge only had a box of ice jelly cake and a cup of mung bean smoothie.

“There’s also some osmanthus oolong tea as the base. Want milk tea?”

“Yes! I love milk tea!”

Song Xuanxuan’s eyes practically shot out laser beams—she was overjoyed! She had always wanted to try Lu Qin’s milk tea but never had the chance.

Lu Qin packed up the items and handed them to Song Xuanxuan, who insisted on paying, pushing a red bill into his hands.

“That’s too much,” Lu Qin said.

“No worries! I can get it reimbursed by President Shen.”

Song Xuanxuan got into a taxi and left. She was so ecstatic that she almost bumped her head before getting into the car. Lu Qin shook his head with a smile, opened the car door, and got into the driver’s seat.

Even though the car had been washed, a faint, elegant fragrance still lingered inside. The driver’s seat had been adjusted—clearly to Shen Muyan’s preferred position.

Lu Qin saved it as Seat Memory 2 and then pressed Seat Memory 1 to restore his own setting. In the central armrest’s cup holder, he found a purple hair tie—no doubt left behind by Shen Muyan. He placed it in the storage compartment, switched to drive mode, and stepped on the gas toward Jingtai Grand Hotel.

______

Jingtai Grand Hotel was a well-known dining establishment in Jiangcheng. Its main restaurant, Rongxuanji, specialized in contemporary fusion cuisine, had already earned a two-star Michelin rating, and was aiming for a third.

Now, Lu Qin understood why Fang Ya had invited him. Rongxuanji wasn’t just some small eatery. As a food influencer with millions of followers, Fang Ya couldn’t afford to make mistakes—especially when it came to professional culinary knowledge.

After parking his car, Lu Qin entered the hotel lobby and called Fang Ya.

“Damn, it’s totally gridlocked here! Lu Qin, I’m so sorry, but I’ll be late. You go ahead and wait in… wait in Private Room 3092. I’ve arranged everything—you can order whatever you want to eat or drink, and I’ll cover the bill…”

Fang Ya’s signal was bad, crackling over the line. After hanging up, Lu Qin checked with the front desk and then headed towards Private Room 3092.

“3092, right?” Lu Qin muttered to himself.

The phone call had been unclear, but it should be 3092. He took the elevator and found the room.A young woman in professional attire stood at the door—not a hotel staff member, more like a corporate employee.

As Lu Qin approached and was about to speak, the woman glanced at the documents in her hand and asked, “Are you Mr. Lu?”

“Yes.”

“You’re the last one we were waiting for. Please, come in.”

She opened the door, and Lu Qin stepped inside. The large private dining room was filled with over a dozen men and women. The moment Lu Qin entered, all eyes turned towards him.

He was taken aback. He had assumed he would be the only one helping Fang Ya with the tasting. He hadn’t expected her to invite so many people.

Was this really such a big deal?

Not knowing what was going on, he simply took the only available seat.

In front of each person was a pen and a sheet of paper labeled ‘Feedback Form’.

Lu Qin thought to himself, Why so formal?

Meanwhile, in a luxurious office inside Jingtai Grand Hotel, the chairman, Chen Jingrong, sat in his executive chair. Standing before him was the young woman from outside Room 3092.

“Manager Zhu, has the last chef arrived?”

“Yes, Chairman. He’s here.”

“Good. Inform the kitchen to start serving the dishes. The first assessment is on taste, the second on knife skills, and the third on cooking technique. A good chef must excel in all three. I will personally review each feedback form after the assessment.”

“Yes, Chairman.”

The restaurant manager, Zhu Li, went to make the arrangements.

Five minutes later, Zhu Li returned with waiters pushing serving carts into Private Room 3092. Plates were placed on the table—each guest received the same dish. On the plate was a sauce-dressed portobello mushroom stuffed with a type of meat.

As a chef, Lu Qin’s sense of taste was more sensitive than an average person’s. Just from the aroma, he could tell the stuffing was beef. This was a fusion dish—visually appealing, with vibrant colors and elegant plating. The plate’s rim was even decorated with viola flowers.

Lu Qin had mastered a vast array of cooking techniques, with expertise spanning multiple cuisines. Even when cooking a dish outside his expertise, his flavors were still top-tier. His tasting ability was also exceptional, just shy of a master’s level.

Once the waiters finished serving, they left the room.

Zhu Li announced, “You have ten minutes. After that, the feedback forms will be collected.” Then, she exited as well.

Lu Qin found it strange—why the time limit? It felt like an evaluation. However, the other attendees remained serious and focused, showing no other emotions. So, Lu Qin suppressed his curiosity for the time being. He picked up his spoon, took a bite of the dish, and tasted the sauce.

The portobello mushroom was delicious, and the beef was tender, with the cooking temperature well-controlled. However, the basil flavor was a bit overpowering. Fusion cuisine often relied on multiple seasonings, resulting in strong and complex flavors.

By contrast, traditional Chinese cuisine emphasized bringing out the natural taste of the main ingredient. As for the sauce, it had a lot going on.

After savoring the dish for a few moments, Lu Qin had already made his judgment. Within a minute, he jotted down his feedback. In contrast, the others furrowed their brows in deep concentration, carefully tasting not just the sauce but also the mushroom and beef multiple times.

Lu Qin thought to himself, Isn’t this a bit much? Should I take another bite just to fit in?

But his palate was sharp enough to detect everything in one go, so further tasting wouldn’t change his conclusions. So, he sat back, waiting. This kind of fusion dish didn’t really excite him. While it was decently made, it still fell short of his own cooking.

Everyone else spent at least five minutes analyzing the dish before finally writing their feedback. Afterward, they occasionally stole glances at Lu Qin. This gave Lu Qin the distinct feeling of being the first student to finish a test in class.

chapters.title: Chapter 137
chapters.content:
Chapter 137 Joking Internationally—Four Ounces of Pork Loin Can Yield 3.7 Ounces?

The ten-minute time limit was almost up. Some had just finished filling out their tasting feedback and let out a sigh of relief. Some had been frowning throughout the tasting process, treating it as if they were answering a college entrance exam, even sweating on their foreheads.

This made Lu Qin wonder—wasn’t this just helping out with a dish tasting? If you could provide feedback, great; if not, then just don’t. Or were they afraid that with so many people present, not coming up with something insightful would make them lose face?

Thinking about it, Lu Qin figured that might actually be the case. At that moment, Rongxuanji Restaurant’s manager, Zhu Li, came in and collected the fourteen feedback forms. Then, she led everyone to the kitchen.

The kitchen had been cleaned, leaving only a long table in the center, with fourteen cutting boards placed on it. Each cutting board had a piece of pork loin. Lu Qin had initially thought there would be more dishes to taste, but judging from the setup, were they supposed to cut meat?

What was Fang Ya up to?

A staff member set up two tripods at opposite corners of the kitchen and mounted two DV cameras. Lu Qin suddenly realized—they were recording the meat-cutting process?

Showing off skills?

That was the thought that came to his mind.

Sure enough, Zhu Li spoke, “Second task—knife skills.”

“The pork in front of you weighs four ounces. Please demonstrate your knife skills.”

After saying that, Zhu Li left the kitchen.

Lu Qin glanced at the others, everyone had a serious expression. Typically, showcasing knife skills involved carving radishes into flowers, slicing potatoes into thin threads, or cutting cucumbers into paper-thin slices. However, cutting pork into fine strips was far more challenging.

And since this was a skills demonstration, the meat strips had to be far more refined than what one would normally cut for cooking. The requirements included length, thickness, uniformity, and even quantity—how many strips could be cut from four ounces of pork loin? This was the real difficulty.

A few people picked up their knives, checked the blades, then scooped up some water with their hands and sprinkled it over the blade before starting to cut.

Well, since he had agreed to help, Lu Qin switched into serious mode and prepared to showcase his knife skills. Pork loin, also known as pork tenderloin, is the meat located along both sides of the pig’s spine, running the entire length of the vertebrae.

This part of the meat is tender, with distinct muscle fibers, and contains some fat and connective tissue. If knife skills were being tested with meat strips, pork loin was the perfect choice.

With his maxed-out cooking skills, cutting meat into strips was second nature to Lu Qin. As expected, he finished the fastest. And, of course, his cuts were top-notch. Once again, the others glanced at him in unison. Lu Qin finishing so quickly seemed to put pressure on them.

After everyone finished cutting, they placed their meat strips into individual stainless steel bowls filled with water. With the meat strips floating in water, their quality was clear at a glance.
