Zhu Li instructed two staff members to take down the DV cameras and film close-up shots of each bowl of meat strips.

The participants also observed each other’s work. Zhu Li examined each batch—there was a wide range of skill levels. Some participants weren’t trained in traditional Chinese cuisine and had barely practiced knife skills.

Some were self-proclaimed fusion chefs, blending Chinese and Western techniques but mastering neither. Their knife skills were weak. Some focused on creative fusion cuisine and hadn’t invested much time in honing their knife skills.

As a result, some had only managed to cut two ounces of meat into strips, wasting half of the meat—if they were working in a restaurant, the owner would go bankrupt from such losses!

Some had cut the strips too thick—they weren’t strips but meat chunks. Some had inconsistently sized cuts, many of which were broken or uneven. Zhu Li shook her head in disappointment.

Knife skills are a fundamental part of Chinese cuisine, representing a chef’s expertise. For Rongxuanji to earn a Michelin three-star rating, it couldn’t rely solely on fusion dishes—it needed to incorporate technically demanding Chinese cuisine.

Clearly, just based on knife skills, 70% of the participants would be eliminated. The remaining ones were barely passing. Zhu Li arrived at the last participant. She took a look and immediately recognized him—it was Mr. Lu, the last one to arrive.

Handsome, with a casual demeanor, yet his gaze was firm, giving off a sense of reliability. When Zhu Li saw the meat strips in Lu Qin’s stainless steel bowl, her eyes widened.

Visibly, the strips were uniform in thickness and length, with an even texture. The strips floated in the water, making their consistency unmistakable. His knife skills were incredibly refined. Zhu Li suddenly felt a surge of excitement.

She turned to a staff member and said, “Bring a digital scale.”

Lu Qin smiled slightly and said, “Four ounces of pork loin—removing 0.2 ounces of connective tissue, yielding 3.7 ounces of meat strips.”

Zhu Li’s body stiffened, and she looked at Lu Qin in shock.

Was he joking?

Four ounces of pork yielding 3.7 ounces of strips with almost no loss?

Impossible!

Under normal circumstances, according to the high standards of Shandong cuisine, four ounces of pork loin, after removing connective tissue, could yield about 3.4 ounces of meat strips—that was already exceptional.

3.7 ounces!

That was an insane difficulty level—only about 0.01% of chefs could achieve that!

It was nearly impossible!

The others murmured among themselves.

“Four ounces of pork loin yielding 3.7 ounces of strips? That’s a joke! I trimmed off 0.3 ounces just removing the connective tissue!”

“I’ve practiced knife skills for six years—3.2 ounces is my limit!”

“I don’t know… I glanced at that handsome guy while he was cutting. His hands were ridiculously steady, and his knife work was incredibly fast! It made me feel immense pressure!”

“No way he got 3.7 ounces. I know a top chef who’s been practicing for over 20 years, and the best he can get is 3.5 ounces. There’s always some loss—just what sticks to the knife is more than 0.2 ounces!”

The digital scale arrived.

The meat strips were drained and placed on the scale. Everyone held their breath. The result—precisely 3.7 ounces!

Four ounces of pork loin, with 0.2 ounces of connective tissue removed, yielding 3.7 ounces of meat strips!

Good grief!

This was god-tier knife work!

This…

This was outright insane!

Zhu Li’s heart pounded. She took a deep look at Lu Qin—this young man was a rare talent!

His knife skills were extraordinary!

All eyes in the room were on Lu Qin—some were in shock, some in envy, some in admiration, and some in disbelief…

This guy was a master!

An absolute top-tier expert!

With him here, why were they even bothering to compete?

They were just extras at this point!

Only Lu Qin remained clueless—he still thought this was just something Fang Ya had set up in advance. To confirm, he had even messaged Fang Ya midway through, and she replied, Room 3092. So, there was no mistake.

Zhu Li suppressed her excitement and announced the third task.

“Prepare a Sichuan dish—Mapo Tofu.”

Lu Qin rubbed his nose and thought, Well, this is easy. My Mapo Tofu skills are maxed out.

This was his specialty!

With limited stovetops, the fourteen participants were split into two groups. Of course, the second group had to wait outside while the first group cooked—no peeking. Unsurprisingly, Lu Qin finished first again.

Zhu Li paid special attention to him. He completed the dish in about eight minutes with three rounds of thickening—a sign of true expertise. The rich aroma made her involuntarily swallow. It smelled incredible!

The taste had to be phenomenal!

As soon as Lu Qin plated the dish, Zhu Li had it sent to the chairman’s office for evaluation.

Lu Qin remarked, “This Mapo Tofu isn’t perfect.”

Zhu Li asked, “Why? Did something go wrong?”

“The doubanjiang isn’t quite right.”

“But it’s all Pixian doubanjiang.”

“There are different grades of Pixian doubanjiang—the taste and saltiness vary.”

Zhu Li: “…”

chapters.title: Chapter 138
chapters.content:
Chapter 138 All Correct! This Lu Peng, He Got Everything Right!

At Jingtai Grand Hotel, Chairman’s Office, Chen Jingrong frowned as he flipped through the feedback forms, feeling deeply dissatisfied.

Why was the level of these chef applicants so poor?

And this was after a citywide recruitment drive in Jiangcheng!

They could only identify basic flavors like oil, salt, sauce, and vinegar in the portobello mushroom sauce?

Anyone with a tongue could do that!

Standing beside him, Zhu Li noticed the chairman’s displeasure and, worried that his blood pressure might spike, quickly reminded him.

“Chairman, perhaps you should check the last feedback form—from Mr. Lu Peng.”

“Lu Peng?”

Chen Jingrong tossed aside all the other feedback forms in frustration—not a single one had impressed him. Then, he picked up the last form.

The handwriting was neat and composed in standard Chinese script.

“The beef is tender, the heat control is well-executed, and the sauce for the portobello mushroom is well-balanced. The sauce is the key to this dish, containing pumpkin, taro, verbena, thyme, and European hornet honey. However, the basil flavor is a bit overpowering.

The butter used is not ordinary butter—its flavor suggests that the cows were fed fresh grass rather than processed feed, as there is a hint of herbal undertone. The perfect wine pairing for this dish would be a Sauvignon Blanc from Blanqué.”

Chen Jingrong’s eyes widened in astonishment, and he exclaimed excitedly, “All correct!”

“This Lu Peng, he got everything right!”

Zhu Li was also shocked.

“He even pointed out that the basil flavor was too strong and could detect the herbal notes in the butter. That’s practically supernatural!”

Chen Jingrong took a deep breath to calm himself and hurriedly asked, “What about the second task?”

He was eager to know how this Lu Peng had performed in the knife skills test!

“Chairman, he cut 3.7 ounces of meat strips from a 4-ounce pork loin, almost zero waste. And the strips were identical in thickness and length—you could barely distinguish them with the naked eye.”

After speaking, Zhu Li showed the DV recording to the chairman.

Chen Jingrong watched in stunned silence.

“And the final dish—the Mapo Tofu?”

A true test of a chef’s skill, this Sichuan dish had piqued Chen Jingrong’s curiosity. What kind of surprise would this Lu Peng bring?

“It’s already here.”

Zhu Li had a cart brought in, but naturally, all the other applicants’ Mapo Tofu dishes were ignored—only Lu Qin’s was left.

As soon as the lid was lifted, a wave of steam billowed up. Chen Jingrong swallowed his saliva and gasped.

“The aroma!”

He examined the dish—it looked fantastic. “There’s minced beef and finely chopped garlic sprouts—this is authentic Mapo Tofu!”

“There’s an old saying—-‘Tofu should be eaten hot, and wives should be married plump.’”

Chen Jingrong picked up a spoon and took a bite.

“Hu-chi hu-chi~!”

“Hmm!!! Hot, numbing, and spicy!”

He immediately took two more bites.

“Absolutely satisfying!!!”

Even as he furrowed his brows from the heat and spice, his expression was one of joy and immense satisfaction.

“Xiao Zhu, come try this.”

Zhu Li, having already been tempted by the rich aroma, eagerly picked up a spoon the moment the chairman permitted it. She took a bite of the tofu, enveloped in spicy oil.

“The numbing and spicy flavor is intense, yet incredibly delicious!”

“This is amazing!”

As a lover of Sichuan cuisine, Zhu Li had never tasted a better Mapo Tofu—this was the best, no exaggeration.

“Good, isn’t it?”

Chen Jingrong grinned, taking another bite, his face radiating delight. Now this was authentic Sichuan cuisine! All the Mapo Tofu he had eaten before felt inferior in comparison!

“Quick-fried tofu, slow-cooked fish.”

“Look, the sauce is still steaming—it’s genuinely hot!”

As an experienced gourmet, Chen Jingrong had the highest praise for this Mapo Tofu. He could confidently say—it was master-level!

“Xiao Zhu, bring this Master Chef Lu Peng to me at once.”

Without a doubt, this Master Lu Peng was qualified to be the head chef of Rongxuanji Restaurant!

Zhu Li hurried off to fetch him.

______

Meanwhile, the fourteen chefs had returned to Private Room 3092, waiting for the results. While drinking tea, several people took the initiative to strike up a conversation with Lu Qin.

“Dude, you’re amazing! Who did you train under?”

Given his exceptional knife skills—cutting 3.7 ounces of meat strips from a 4-ounce pork loin—it was clear this guy wasn’t an ordinary chef. He had to be someone significant! So, they immediately asked about his teacher.

“I never had a master.”

“No master?”

The group was puzzled—was he self-taught?

A natural talent?

“Then which famous hotel did you work at before?”

With skills like his, he must have worked at one of Jiangcheng’s top hotels or restaurants.

“I ran my own food stall.”

“……”

“A food stall?”

Everyone was dumbfounded.

What kind of joke was this?

With such incredible culinary skills, he was running a stall? What kind of logic was that? Was he bluffing?

Just then, Lu Qin’s phone rang.

It was Fang Ya.

“Lu Qin, I’m here. I don’t see you!”

“I’m on the third floor, Private Room 3092.”

“I’m in 3092 too!”

“?”

“Oh! Lu Qin, it’s VIP 3092, not a regular private room!”

“???”

Lu Qin hung up, thought for a moment, and replayed the events in his mind.

I messed up.

This was a chef recruitment event, wasn’t it?

Lu Qin glanced around at the other thirteen applicants and confirmed his suspicion. But one thing still confused him.

Why did Manager Zhu initially ask if he was “Mr. Lu”?

Could it be that someone else shared his surname?

What were the odds of that happening?

Fang Ya had said, “Everything is arranged”—which likely meant she had booked a VIP private room with top-notch service and unlimited food and drinks.

Not some elaborate culinary assessment!

She hadn’t even arrived—how could there be a tasting session?

Lu Qin was speechless.

What an absurd mix-up!

No time to dwell on it—he needed to leave before anyone caught on. Otherwise, they’d think he was an imposter causing trouble!

So, using the classic “bathroom escape” tactic, Lu Qin quietly slipped out of the regular 3092 private room. He didn’t bother asking the staff where the VIP rooms were—he just needed to get away first.

After turning a couple of corners, he spotted a VIP sign on a wall-mounted light fixture. At the same time, he encountered three people in the hallway. They were hotel and restaurant investors—Deng Jianping, Sun Zhi, and Fan Qingfang.

The three of them had just stepped out of a VIP private room. Previously, they had all tried to recruit Lu Qin as their head chef and had personally lined up at his food stall.

Lu Qin had a great memory—he recognized them immediately. The three men were equally surprised and delighted to see him.

“Boss Lu, what are you doing here?”

“I’m meeting a friend.”

“Oh? We didn’t get to eat your food yesterday—we’ve been craving it. Will you be setting up your stall this afternoon?”

“I won’t be there this afternoon, but I’ll open earlier in the evening.”

…

After a brief chat, Lu Qin learned that the three of them were investors in Rongxuanji Restaurant. However, not the Jiangcheng location, but a branch in Haishi, where they had all invested.

“Well, since you’ve got plans, we won’t keep you. We’ll come by your stall tonight.”

With that, the three headed for the elevator.

Meanwhile, in the Chairman’s Office—Chen Jingrong frowned and asked, “What do you mean he’s missing? Where is he?”

“He said he was going to the restroom, but he never came back. I sent staff to check all the restrooms—he’s nowhere to be found,” Zhu Li said.

“How could this happen?”

“Did he leave?” Zhu Li asked.

Chen Jingrong shook his head.

“Why would someone at his skill level walk away from an interview he was sure to pass?”

“Bring me his application.”

Zhu Li found the file, but upon seeing the ID photo, she muttered, “Huh?”

Chen Jingrong frowned.

“What’s wrong?”

“The photo looks nothing like him. In the picture, he’s wearing glasses.”

Chen Jingrong studied the file.

“Age… thirty-eight?”

Zhu Li shook her head.

“No way—he’s young, probably not even twenty-eight.”

Both were utterly bewildered.

“The resume is fake?”

Zhu Li quickly pulled up the DV footage from the kitchen and played it. Chen Jingrong compared the footage to the resume.These were two completely different people!

What the hell was going on?

chapters.title: Chapter 139
chapters.content:
Chapter 139 Your Husband Is About to Be Snatched as Head Chef!

Chen Jingrong compared the ID photo on the resume with the young man in the DV recording.

Well, damn.

They weren’t the same person at all!

Could someone actually fake their identity just to apply for a chef position?

“The resume has this Lu Peng’s phone number. Xiao Zhu, call him right away and ask what’s going on.”

“Yes, sir.”

Zhu Li pulled out her phone and dialed the number on the resume. Two minutes later, she put her phone away.

“Boss, I’ve confirmed it—this Lu Peng didn’t send anyone to impersonate him. He actually gave up on this interview but just forgot to notify our HR department.”

Chen Jingrong was even more confused.

“Then who is the guy in the DV footage?”

Zhu Li shook her head—she had no idea either.

At that moment, Deng Jianping, Sun Zhi, and Fan Qingfang knocked on the door and entered the office.

“President Chen, have you chosen the head chef for Rongxuanji Restaurant?” Deng Jianping asked with a smile.

Noticing Chen Jingrong’s deep frown, Sun Zhi asked, “What’s wrong, President Chen? Something happened?”

“It’s like this…”

Zhu Li quickly explained the situation.

“Oh? That actually happened?”

The three investors were equally intrigued.

The chef who was supposed to interview had forfeited his spot, yet another Mr. Lu took his place instead. But after acing the assessments, this replacement chef just… disappeared.

How bizarre!

The most shocking part?

This unexpected Mr. Lu had crushed every stage of the assessment, showcasing exceptional culinary skills—far beyond the other candidates.

“I have to see who this Mr. Lu is!”

Deng Jianping took the DV footage, and Sun Zhi and Fan Qingfang leaned in to watch. One look, and they nearly jumped out of their seats.

“Wait a minute! Isn’t this Boss Lu?”

“He came to apply for the head chef position at Rongxuanji!”

The three of them were completely stunned.

Chen Jingrong was equally surprised.

“You know him?”

“Know him? That’s an understatement…”

Deng Jianping and the others immediately sang Lu Qin’s praises, explaining just how insanely talented he was. They swore they weren’t exaggerating.

Chen Jingrong and Zhu Li were both speechless.

“Wait… he sells stir-fried rice noodles at a food stall?”

Chen Jingrong was in disbelief—then immediately felt what a waste of talent. Seeing his expression, Deng Jianping raised an eyebrow and said, “President Chen, don’t look down on food stalls. Boss Lu’s customers are just as many—if not more—than yours at Rongxuanji.”

“If you don’t believe me, search ‘Jiangcheng’s Legendary Stir-Fry Stall’ on Douyin. He’s literally the king of Jiangcheng’s street food scene!”

Chen Jingrong had Zhu Li look it up—and what they saw left them in stunned silence for several seconds.

In the Douyin videos, an endless crowd lined up in front of Lu Qin’s food stall—at least a hundred people at any given moment. And as the footage played, more customers kept arriving, meaning the queue never actually shrank.

Even when he sold out, dozens of customers were still waiting, hoping for more. That was insane. Chen Jingrong thought back to the incredible Mapo Tofu he had just eaten.

No wonder people lined up for hours. That level of taste was worth the wait!

“It seems… this Mr. Lu is no ordinary person!”

Deng Jianping nodded.

“The real question is—are you planning to hire him, President Chen?”

“Of course! There’s no better choice for Rongxuanji’s head chef!”

Chen Jingrong’s eyes lit up—he had made up his mind.

Regardless of how Lu Qin had ended up at the interview—whether by mistake or pure luck—he had gone through the assessment and excelled beyond expectations.

Since that was the case, he had to be recruited!

“President Deng, you said you saw him earlier? Near the VIP private rooms?”

“That’s right.”

…

Chen Jingrong stood up, determined to meet Lu Qin personally.

Meanwhile, in VIP Room 3092, Lu Qin and Fang Ya were casually drinking tea.

“Damn! How did you end up in a job interview by mistake?”

“You told me Room 3092, and coincidentally, one of the candidates was also a ‘Mr. Lu’.”

Fang Ya shook her head in amusement and disbelief.

“Damn, that’s crazy.”

Just then, her phone rang—it was Shen Muyan. Fang Ya held up her phone and grinned at Lu Qin.

“Look, it’s President Shen calling.”

“If I answer this, the first thing she asks will definitely be about you. Wanna bet?”

Fang Ya’s expression was full of mischief.

“Uh…”

She picked up the call.

Sure enough—first sentence out of Shen Muyan’s mouth.

“How’s the dish tasting going? What did you try?”

Fang Ya shot Lu Qin a ‘Told you so’ look, while Lu Qin just rubbed his nose and kept quiet.

“It hasn’t started yet—the dishes aren’t even here. I got stuck in traffic and just arrived…”

After chatting for a bit, Fang Ya hung up.

She set down her phone, smirking at Lu Qin.

“ Tsk tsk , President Shen is getting more and more interested in you.”

Lu Qin just smiled.

At that moment, someone knocked on the door.

Thinking it was the waiter bringing food, Fang Ya casually said, “Come in.”

Instead, several big-name figures from the restaurant industry walked in. Fang Ya recognized Chen Jingrong and Fan Qingfang. As soon as Chen Jingrong entered, he locked eyes on Lu Qin, walked straight over, and excitedly extended both hands.

“Mr. Lu, welcome to Rongxuanji Restaurant! From now on, the kitchen is in your hands. Annual salary: 800,000. Are you satisfied?”

Lu Qin: “?”

Fang Ya: “???”

800,000 annual salary—straight to the point!

Chen Jingrong already knew Lu Qin had rejected previous job offers from Deng Jianping and the others. So, he cut to the chase, showing his utmost sincerity with a generous salary offer.

Lu Qin had accidentally gone through the assessments, but now that he had, Chen Jingrong was determined to keep him. 800,000 a year was already a top-tier salary in Jiangcheng’s restaurant scene.

Chen Jingrong refused to believe that Lu Qin wouldn’t be tempted. Lu Qin stood up as Chen Jingrong enthusiastically shook his hand.

He was dazed—how had this escalated to being hired as the head chef?

He was pretty sure he never even wrote down his name during the interview process. Then, Lu Qin spotted Deng Jianping and the others—all smiling at him.

Ah… so that’s how they found me.

Just as Lu Qin was about to politely refuse, Chen Jingrong pressed forward aggressively—having Zhu Li bring over a contract, pen, and ink pad.

“President Chen, actually, this is a misunderstanding…”

Before Lu Qin could fully explain, Fang Ya secretly messaged Shen Muyan.

Shen Muyan had long wanted to recruit Lu Qin as the head chef for her hotel restaurant but never brought it up. Later, when she realized Lu Qin made more money at his stall than she did as a five-star hotel CEO, she gave up on the idea.

But now, Chen Jingrong was trying to steal him!

Shen Muyan immediately replied.

Fang Ya nudged Lu Qin.

Chen Jingrong’s face changed instantly.

He gritted his teeth.

“1.05 million!”

Fang Ya snickered and relayed the bidding war to Shen Muyan.

Over the phone, Shen Muyan’s icy voice rang out.

“1.2 million.”

Lu Qin was speechless.

He suddenly felt like he was an item being auctioned off.

chapters.title: Chapter 140
chapters.content:
Chapter 140 This Is Impossible—How Can a Street Food Stall Make That Much Money?

When Shen Muyan, over the phone, calmly and decisively raised the salary offer to 1.3 million, Chen Jingrong gave up.

“Alright, President Shen, you win.”

If Chen Jingrong hired Lu Qin at 1.3 million per year, even if Lu Qin’s culinary skills were undisputed, the other employees would feel unfairly treated. After all, such an astronomical salary was more than ten times that of a regular employee—it would definitely crush morale.

Fang Ya let out a victorious “Yay!”, clearly enjoying the moment. Chen Jingrong shot her a look and frowned.

“This isn’t some bidding war. Since President Shen has offered 1.3 million, that means Mr. Lu is now the head chef of the Ourei Hotel.”

Lu Qin sighed in exasperation.

Why were they all ignoring the fact that he—the actual person involved—had a say in this?

At that moment, Lu Qin spoke up in a calm tone, “Alright, enough. Thank you all for your appreciation, but I have no intention of becoming a restaurant’s head chef.”

Silence.

Chen Jingrong and the others looked stunned—completely dumbfounded.

“Mr. Lu, we’ve already gone up to 1.3 million—there’s no reason for you to refuse. The Ourei Hotel’s restaurant is no worse than Rongxuanji.”

Deng Jianping also chimed in.

“Boss Lu, don’t turn your back on money. That annual salary is among the highest in even first-tier cities like Shanghai or Beijing!”

Sun Zhi and Fan Qingfang both nodded in agreement. To be honest, some of the restaurants they operated didn’t even generate 1.3 million in profit per year!

Zhu Li, meanwhile, felt deeply envious. As the restaurant manager of Rongxuanji, she had the highest salary below the vice president—even higher than the head chef.

Yet now, the salary being offered to Lu Qin was four times hers. How could he not accept such an incredible opportunity?

On the other end of the phone, Shen Muyan remained silent. She had bid against Chen Jingrong not because she actually wanted Lu Qin as her head chef, but simply because she couldn’t allow another restaurant to take him.

Why let talent go to an outsider?

But deep down, she already knew—Lu Qin was never going to accept a head chef position anywhere. Because his food stall made way more money. It wasn’t something anyone else could replicate.

At this moment, Fang Ya stepped forward, cleared her throat, and exuded an air of self-importance—as if she were about to drop a truth bomb.

“Even at 1.3 million, Boss Lu still won’t take the job. Do you know why?”

She paused for dramatic effect, then continued,

“Because his street food stall makes more than 1.3 million per month.”

“Think about it, dear bosses—do you really think he would work for you?”

Chen Jingrong: “!”

Zhu Li: “!!!”

“That’s impossible! There’s no way a street food stall can make that kind of money!”

At first, nobody believed it. It sounded utterly absurd. But then, Deng Jianping, Sun Zhi, and Fan Qingfang—who had all visited Lu Qin’s stall—began calculating.

A plate of stir-fried rice noodles was a dozen or so yuan, A box of ice jelly cake was 25 yuan, A serving of crystal shrimp dumplings was 28 yuan…

Multiply that by the number of customers.

And the more they calculated…

The more shocked they became.

Holy sh*t.

Lu Qin was easily making tens of thousands per day!

Even though Lu Qin’s food wasn’t expensive, it was so delicious that customers couldn’t stop buying. People weren’t just buying one or two items—most were spending over 100 yuan per visit.

And unlike restaurants, where customers sit for an hour or more, his food stall had a constant flow of new customers, money pouring in non-stop. Even at peak hours, a packed restaurant like Rongxuanji would never serve as many customers as Lu Qin’s stall.

Deng Jianping, Sun Zhi, and Fan Qingfang exchanged looks, their expressions shifting dramatically.

My God.

As veteran restaurant owners, they had never realized that a food stall could generate this kind of revenue. If this was possible, why even run a restaurant? Why not just start a food stall instead?

But then, they quickly dismissed the thought. This was a once-in-a-generation anomaly. Only Lu Qin could pull it off—his culinary skills were simply beyond anyone else’s reach.

Deng Jianping pulled Chen Jingrong aside and whispered something to him. A few minutes later, Chen Jingrong’s expression changed multiple times. Finally, they all sighed in defeat.

“…We were mistaken. Apologies for the disturbance.”

Then, they left the private room, visibly disappointed. Before leaving, Chen Jingrong handed Lu Qin his personal business card, only after repeatedly insisting that he accept it.

Fang Ya picked up the card and inspected it closely.

“Gold trim, huh?”

“Boss Lu, you’ve really got some serious clout.”

She was teasing, but it was also a fair assessment. If so many major players in the restaurant industry were desperate to hire him, it just proved how valuable he was. Even she herself had invited him here today for his expertise.

______

The first dish was ‘Premium Beef Cubes’.

Fang Ya took out her phone and started recording.

“Chef Lu, please taste it.”

She ceremoniously handed him chopsticks with both hands. Lu Qin took a bite.

Fang Ya took a bite as well.

Her impression? The beef was tender and delicious. But she waited for Lu Qin’s professional critique.

Lu Qin observed the dish for a moment, then commented.

“The beef has been stir-fried over high heat, creating a strong wok aroma. The cut of beef is excellent—lean with no connective tissue, making it tender and smooth. The seasoning includes high-proof liquor and black pepper. Overall, it tastes good. The chef’s skill is decent, and their heat control is on point. The only flaw is that the scallions were sliced too thin and over-fried—but it’s a minor issue. I’d say this dish deserves a positive review.”

Fang Ya nodded seriously.

Her eyes lit up with admiration. Now that was a professional critique!

______

Over the next three or four dishes, Lu Qin evaluated each one in detail. After completing the food review, Lu Qin prepared to leave. Fang Ya, however, insisted on treating him to dinner.

“What are we eating?”

“Your food.”

“I’ll buy stir-fried rice noodles, twice-cooked pork, imperial chicken wings, chicken tofu soup… from your food stall.”

Lu Qin: “…”

_____

That evening, after returning home, Lu Qin took a sip of water, then headed into the kitchen to prepare for his night market stall. He checked his customer group chat—since he hadn’t set up shop that afternoon, people were complaining.

After estimating the time, he posted an update.

Immediately, the group chat exploded with excitement.

Lu Qin put his phone away and focused on ingredient prep. For Mapo Tofu, the best choice was gypsum tofu—but brine tofu had the best flavor. However, brine tofu was tricky to cook—too much heat, and it would become tough and dry.

Only high-level chefs could control it properly. But Lu Qin had max-level Mapo Tofu skills. He wasn’t just any chef.

Pricing? 40 yuan per portion.

Lu Qin carefully prepped the tofu, beef, and garlic sprouts, neatly portioning them into disposable containers for easy cooking at the stall.

chapters.title: Chapter 141
chapters.content:
Chapter 141 Father and Daughter Are Super Popular! The Kids’ Admiration Level is Maxed Out!

In the afternoon, since he wasn’t setting up his stall, Lu Qin had plenty of time to prepare more ingredients and make more food.

At 5:10 PM, Lu Qin packed all the items for the stall into two large insulated freshness-preserving boxes—350 portions of Twice-Cooked Pork ingredients, 150 portions of Mapo Tofu ingredients, 200 portions of Chicken Bean Curd, 300 portions of Imperial Concubine Chicken Wings and 400 portions of Crystal Shrimp Dumplings

The Ice Crystal Cake and Mung Bean Smoothie didn’t need to be stored in the insulated boxes. Ice Crystal Cake could be stored at normal room temperature without affecting its taste. Lu Qin had already bought two large 200L PET plastic boxes, in which he packed 500 boxes of Ice Crystal Cake.

A 190L insulated box contained 600 cups of iced mung bean smoothie and 180 cups of Osmanthus Oolong Milk Tea. Additionally, 120 pounds of An Yang rice noodles had been soaked and drained, ready for use.

With four large boxes of ingredients and food, plus 15 basins of rice noodles, it took Lu Qin about seven to eight minutes to load everything onto his three-wheeled food cart. He stacked everything up to the brim.

Originally, there was still space for one adult inside the three-wheeler, but now, there was no room left at all.

Looks like I need a bigger food cart.

Lu Qin thought to himself that the three-wheeler might need to be upgraded to a four-wheeled fast-food cart. He pulled out his phone and messaged Boss Zhao, asking if he had any leads on a new cart.

Boss Zhao hadn’t replied yet—probably busy. Lu Qin wasn’t in a hurry, so he got on his three-wheeled food cart and rode out of the neighborhood.

______

At 5:30 PM, Lu Qin arrived on time at Starlight Kindergarten. Many parents were already waiting outside, mostly grandparents and mothers picking up their kids. The mothers gathered in small groups, chatting.

They frequently brought up Lu Qin’s family—ever since the last parent-child activity, the family of three had left a strong impression.

“Yao Yao’s mom is so lucky! She has such a great husband and daughter. Especially her husband—his cooking skills are amazing! His food tastes even better than big restaurants!”

“ Ugh , why are other people’s husbands always so outstanding? My husband doesn’t even know how to use a rice cooker, doesn’t do any housework, and just smokes and drinks all day.”

“Mine just plays video games all day when he’s home. But at least he doesn’t fool around outside.”

“I can only be jealous. Why couldn’t I meet a perfect husband like Yao Yao’s dad?”

“Even if you wanted to, you’d need Yao Yao’s mom’s looks and figure! I heard she’s the CEO of a chain of high-end hotels! Their whole family is amazing!”

“I’m so jealous!”

As they were chatting, Lu Qin appeared at the kindergarten entrance. Even though he was only wearing a simple T-shirt and jeans, he was seriously handsome—with deep eyes, a tall and lean physique, standing out like a crane among chickens, immediately drawing the attention of many people.

Several familiar parents greeted him enthusiastically.

“Yao Yao’s dad, you’re here!”

“Hey, Yao Yao’s dad, why didn’t you set up your stall this afternoon? I was craving your Imperial Concubine Chicken Wings! My son, Ting Ting, was crying because he wanted to eat the food Yao Yao had for lunch!”

“Same here! My daughter barely ate her own lunch because she was so envious!”

“Yao Yao’s dad, here, have some longan! These are premium Shi Xia longans—crispy flesh, small pit, super sweet!”

“Yao Yao’s dad, take these yogurt drinks—two bottles, one for you and one for Yao Yao!”

Some parents had bought fruits and snacks for their kids but generously shared them with Lu Qin.bHe politely declined as much as he could, but in the end, both of his hands still ended up holding two yogurt drinks, an apple, and a handful of longan.

It was obvious—many parents genuinely liked Lu Qin and his daughter, Lu Xi Yao. At this moment, the teachers led the kids out of the kindergarten.

Parents took out their pick-up cards and brought their children home. Little Lu Xi Yao had, once again, made the other kids jealous with her Mapo Tofu at lunch—so much so that she had been surrounded by admirers all afternoon. Now that school was over, she walked out like a little princess, surrounded by fans.

When she reached her dad, she held out both hands, filled with snacks—all gifted to her by her little friends. There were so many snacks that she couldn’t even return them—because she had no idea who had given what!

Meanwhile, Lu Qin’s hands were also full of food. Seeing this, Teacher Li chuckled.

“Yao Yao’s dad, you and your daughter are both super popular!”

A few parents and children didn’t leave immediately—they waited until Lu Qin picked up his daughter and then approached him.

“Yao Yao’s dad, are you heading home or going to your stall?”

“Not going home. Straight to the stall. I didn’t open in the afternoon, and a lot of customers have been complaining that they’re starving.”

The parents laughed.

“Great! We’ll just follow you there!”

Several children jumped with excitement.

“Uncle Lu! Do you have Mapo Tofu?”

“Yes.”

The parents and kids swallowed their saliva in anticipation. So, Lu Qin rode his three-wheeler in front, followed by a convoy of electric scooters and cars. These parents thought that since they were coming directly with Lu Qin, they’d be the first in line and could buy food before others.

However, when they arrived at the night market, they were shocked. The stall wasn’t even set up yet, but there was already a long line of customers waiting!

The parents were dumbfounded…

“Wait, people are already lining up… even though the stall isn’t even open?”

“One, two, three, four, five… Oh my god, there are already 37 people in line?!”

“No, not 37—now it’s 40! Three more just arrived! This is crazy!”

“When did these customers even get here?”

“You don’t know? Lu Qin has a customer group—almost a thousand people! The moment he sends a message, customers rush over!”

“A thousand-member customer group? Damn, that’s impressive!”

“Well, Lu Qin’s food is just that good!”

…

Parents hurriedly lined up behind the growing queue. When Lu Qin finally arrived, his regular customers started their usual playful banter.

“Lu Qin! Hurry up! We’re starving over here!”

“Lu Qin, you’re five seconds and 3.15926 milliseconds late! I’m watching you!”

“Can’t you start earlier? Starving like this is too cruel! If you want, I can lend you my Range Rover as a food truck!”

“I heard you have a new dish—Mapo Tofu! Nice! Fast updates! Love it!”

“I’ve loved tofu since I was a kid!”

“I want to eat Lu Qin’s tofu!”

The moment Lu Qin stopped the cart, a few male customers rushed over and helped set up the stall with expert-level efficiency. The stall was up and ready in no time, with all the food neatly displayed.

Meanwhile, Lu Xi Yao brought over a small stool, sat down, and started acting as the little boss.

“Little Boss! Give me some Chicken Wings, Chicken Bean Curd, and two Ice Crystal Cakes!”

“Uncle, please scan the QR code to pay first~!”

“What? You think I’d eat for free? You’re such a little money-grubber!”

In the blink of an eye, she had already made 211.5 yuan. The parents behind in line felt a mix of emotions. Look at Lu Qin’s daughter—so skilled at selling food!

Meanwhile, their own kids were still sucking their thumbs and throwing tantrums just to get snacks. The difference was just too big!

The other kids stared at Lu Xi Yao in awe.

“She’s amazing!”

chapters.title: Chapter 142
chapters.content:
Chapter 142 Is Boss Lu’s Daughter Really This Smart?

“Xiao Nuo, take mommy’s phone and go line up at Yao Yao’s stall to buy one order of Imperial Concubine Chicken Wings, one order of Chicken Bean Curd, and a cup of Osmanthus Oolong Milk Tea.”

A parent, seeing how capable Lu Xi Yao was, decided to send their own kid to experience buying things independently.

The little girl named Xiao Nuo responded with an “Oh” and ran over. It wasn’t clear if she fully understood, as she looked a bit confused. Other parents saw this and decided to do the same. If Lu Xi Yao could sell things so well, then at least their own kids should be able to buy things properly, right?

They couldn’t let the gap be too big!

One by one, little kids holding their parents’ phones lined up at Lu Xi Yao’s stall.

“Mommy, I’m scared to go.”

One child hesitated, feeling socially anxious and unsure of how to move forward without their mother holding their hand.

“What’s there to be scared of? Look at Lu Xi Yao! She’s chatting and laughing with complete strangers, and she just casually made someone buy an extra Ice Crystal Cake. Go and learn from her!”

“Wow! Boss Lu’s daughter is incredible! She even knows how to act cute to get more sales! Who can resist that? You’d have to open your wallet willingly!”

Several little kids were pushed forward by their parents and soon joined the queue to buy food. When it was Xiao Nuo’s turn, she timidly said:

“Lu… Lu Xi Yao, I want to buy…”

Lu Xi Yao interrupted her with a serious expression.

“Xiao Nuo, when we’re outside, call me ‘Little Boss Lady’.”

“Oh, okay… Li-Little Boss Lady! I-I want Chicken Bean Curd, Chicken Wings, and Milk… Milk Tea.”

From the back, Xiao Nuo’s mom frowned.

“Why is my daughter stuttering?”

“Do you want Ice Crystal Cake? It’s delicious! And how about some Crystal Shrimp Dumplings? Just one order, they’re super tasty!”

“Okay… I’ll take both.”

“Got it!”

Lu Xi Yao carefully picked out each item, packed them into plastic bags, and tied them securely. Then, she used the tablet’s voice assistant to calculate the total price. The parents watching were amazed.

“Wow, so smart! Is Boss Lu’s daughter really this capable?”

Even other customers were impressed by this smart little boss.

“Damn! She’s only in kindergarten, hasn’t even learned double-digit addition yet, but she already knows how to use tools to help her! That’s genius!”

“That’ll be 176 yuan. Scan the QR code over there and pay.”

Xiao Nuo froze, looking clueless.

“I… I don’t know how to scan.”

Her mom stomped her foot in frustration.

“ Ugh! How can you be this clueless?! You’ve watched me buy things so many times! I even taught you right before coming here!”

Lu Xi Yao sighed, took the phone, and scanned the QR code for her.

“It’s 176 yuan in total. Xiao Nuo, double-check it. Now enter your password.”

Xiao Nuo scratched her head awkwardly.

“P-Password? I forgot it! Wait! Let me go ask my mom!”

Lu Xi Yao facepalmed, looking utterly speechless.

“What do you mean go back and ask? Just take the phone to your mom and let her pay!”

“Oh, oh, okay!”

“And don’t forget your food!”

“Huh? Oh! Right!”

Xiao Nuo quickly grabbed her bag of food and ran back to her mother. Everyone watched her as she left.

Xiao Nuo’s mother felt embarrassed.

“They’re in the same kindergarten, but the difference is huge!”

Boss Lu’s daughter was running a food stall like a pro, while her own kid couldn’t even figure out how to buy food properly!

Even worse, the QR code had to be scanned for her, and she had just been taught how to do it!

The gap was just too big!

Meanwhile, a customer decided to test Lu Xiyao.

“Little Boss Lady, I’ll trade you five lollipops and three White Rabbit candies for an Ice Crystal Cake, okay?”

He deliberately tempted her—because kids are usually easily swayed by candy. He spread out his hands, revealing a colorful assortment of candies, making it very enticing.

Nearby, Lu Qin was busy stir-frying Twice-Cooked Pork, but he glanced over and smiled, letting his daughter handle it herself. Some things, he had already taught her.

For example, she knew exactly how much a lollipop costs and the price of a bag of White Rabbit candy.

The customer thought, For a little kid, this should seem like a good deal.

But unexpectedly.

“Uncle, lollipops cost 50 cents each at the convenience store, and a bag of White Rabbit candy costs 10 yuan at the supermarket. There are 38 pieces in a bag—I counted! My Ice Crystal Cake costs 25 yuan per box. If I trade with you, I’ll lose big time!”

“Besides, I only sell my Ice Crystal Cake for money. I don’t do trades.”

She blinked her big, intelligent eyes and analyzed the deal seriously.

The customer was stunned.

“Wow! This little boss is way too smart!”

He laughed heartily and handed her the candies anyway.

“Alright, alright! You win! The candy is a gift! Now, give me two boxes of Ice Crystal Cake, one order of Imperial Concubine Chicken Wings, and one Chicken Bean Curd.”

Lu Xi Yao shook her head.

“I’ll just take two White Rabbit candies. Thank you, Uncle! I don’t need the rest. Eating too much candy causes cavities. Besides, even if you give them all to me, my dad will take them away anyway.”

She spoke in a soft, sweet voice.

The customer burst out laughing and patted her head.

“This little cutie is way too clear-headed! And she’s so polite! I almost want to take her home with me!”

The people around them also laughed heartily.

“Boss Lu’s daughter is seriously smart!”

“She’s adorable! I suddenly want a second child—I need a daughter! My firstborn son is a total mess, not obedient at all!”

Lu Qin watched his daughter’s performance and smiled proudly.

“Looks like she’s officially qualified to get her ‘Little Boss Lady’ certificate!”

A customer who had just ordered Mapo Tofu couldn’t help but sigh.

“Boss Lu, your little boss lady is too smart! And way too cute!”

“If I had a daughter like this, I’d wake up laughing every day!”

Lu Qin smiled and continued cooking Mapo Tofu—adding the perfect blend of pork fat, beef tallow, and rapeseed oil to keep the dish sizzling hot and enhance the fragrance.

He stir-fried the beef quickly, adding cooking wine, Sichuan Douban sauce, fermented black beans, and chili flakes. As he tossed the wok, the rich aroma filled the air.

“That smells amazing!”

Customers were already drooling!

He then added tofu and sauce, simmering it for three minutes over medium heat. In the final steps, he sprinkled pepper powder and garlic shoots, then thickened the sauce twice before finally finishing it with a touch of Sichuan peppercorn powder.

The customers watched with anticipation.

“Boss Lu, you’re seriously a pro!”

“This is real Mapo Tofu! It smells incredible!”

“I can’t take it anymore!”

One customer couldn’t wait, grabbed their serving, and immediately dug in.

“Ahh! So hot! But so good! It’s so numbing and spicy!”

They were blown away by the taste—although it was just tofu, it was even more flavorful than meat!

“Eat slowly, don’t burn your tongue,” Lu Qin reminded.

“Ahh! Ouch!”

The customer did burn their tongue but kept eating anyway!

“Boss Lu! It’s my turn! One Mapo Tofu and one Twice-Cooked Pork!”

“Got it, one moment.”

After making two more orders, Lu Qin announced a 10-minute break. He and his daughter hadn’t eaten dinner yet.

chapters.title: Chapter 143
chapters.content:
Chapter 143 We Are Professionals—We Can Even Make Flour Taste Like Milk Powder!

At 6:23 PM, the bustling night market was as lively as ever, filled with neon lights and vibrant energy. Beside him, the little boss lady was so busy running the stall that she hadn’t even complained, “Daddy, I’m hungry.”

Lu Qin grabbed two portions of rice noodles, tossed them into the wok, cracked in two fresh eggs, and added diced ham and lean pork, stir-frying everything over high heat.

Once cooked, he plated the food and called out, “Little Boss Lady, dinner time!”

But the little one didn’t hear him. She was still busy selling tea to a young woman.

“Sister, this milk tea is really healthy! Drinking it is good for you! My daddy carefully selected the best osmanthus oolong tea leaves from the tea market just for this. Are you sure you don’t want a cup?”

The young woman gently pinched Lu Xi Yao’s soft cheek. She already had two cups of premium tea from a popular chain.

“Ba Wang Tea Ji,” in her hand.

She really didn’t plan on buying more, especially since she had already bought three cups of iced mung bean smoothie earlier. But this little girl was just too cute!

“How is there such an adorable child on this planet?”

“Alright, fine, I’ll take a cup! But only because of you, Little Boss Lady~!”

“Thank you, sister! Once you try my milk tea, you won’t drink any other brand, trust me!”

“Oh? Is that so?”

The young woman scanned the QR code and paid, then took the cup of milk tea. Just then, her best friend arrived, carrying two servings of spicy hot pot.

“Babe, why are you still here? What’s taking you so long…?”

She looked at the adorable little girl and froze.

“Whoa! This little cutie is so beautiful!”

Then her gaze shifted to Lu Qin.

“Wow! The stall owner is super handsome!”

She turned to her friend.

“Gorgeous friend, is this little cutie yours?”

“Duh! Obviously! Can’t you see the resemblance? I even bought milk tea from them!”

“Wait… Didn’t you already buy from ‘Ba Wang Tea Ji’? Why are you buying more?”

“The Little Boss Lady used her ultimate cuteness technique. I was powerless against it.”

“Oh come on! You’re drinking so much milk tea, you’re gonna get diabetes!”

“It’s osmanthus oolong flavor. I’ll just take a sip and toss it if it’s not good. It’s a street vendor’s drink, after all… But there’s a huge line, so it must be decent.”

She took a sip— and her eyes instantly lit up.

“Mmm! This is delicious! The osmanthus fragrance is so rich! The milk flavor is strong, but it doesn’t overpower the tea’s aroma—it’s perfectly balanced!”

“Really? Let me try… Whoa! It’s actually better than ‘Ba Wang Tea Ji’!”

The two girls started sharing sips, and before they knew it, they had finished the entire cup on the spot.

“So good!”

“Let’s go buy another one!”

____

Meanwhile, Little Boss Lady finally felt her hunger kicking in. She turned her head just in time to see Lu Qin placing two plates of stir-fried rice noodles on the table.

“Daddy, your timing is perfect! The Little Boss Lady needs to refuel!”

She grabbed a small folding stool, set it up, and sat down with Lu Qin to eat dinner together.

“Daddy, how about a cup of iced mung bean smoothie?”

“Sure.”

She opened the insulated box, took out two cups, and inserted a straw in her father’s cup first before inserting her own. Three bites of rice noodles, one sip of smoothie— pure bliss.

Under the watchful eyes of their customers, this stunning father-daughter duo was basically live-streaming their dinner. But no one was impatient.

They knew how hard Lu Qin and his daughter worked, so they willingly waited for them to eat in peace. Besides, watching them eat was surprisingly entertaining!

“Take your time, Boss Lu! We’re in no rush tonight.”

“Babe, look at that little girl. She even put the straw in for her dad! You’ve never even opened a bottle for me!”

“I can crack open your skull if you’d like.”

“ Ugh! How did I ever fall for you, you insensitive jerk?!”

“I swear, the Little Boss Lady should be a food vlogger. Even watching her eat stir-fried noodles is adorable!”

“ Ugh , the more I watch, the hungrier I get!”

Lu Qin mentally noted that while his daughter loved stir-fried noodles, she had already eaten them for lunch and dinner. Next time, he’d bring rice to change things up. After they finished eating, Lu Qin wiped the grease off his daughter’s mouth, and they continued selling food.

____

By 9:00 PM, most of the food had sold out. There were still 21 customers in line, the fewest they had ever had. Among them, seven or eight were repeat customers, back for second servings. For once, there were fewer complaints.

“Boss Lu, you really stocked up well today! Usually, you sell out by 8 PM.”

“Great job, Boss Lu! Keep up the good work!”

“Little Boss Lady, we’re heading out! See you tomorrow!”

“Bye-bye, Uncle~!”

“Goodbye, Big Brother~!”

The Little Boss Lady was exceptionally polite.

Lu Qin had always taught her—’Manners never hurt anyone’. She remembered it well.

Some customers offered to help clean up, but Lu Qin declined.

“No need, really. There’s nothing left to clean.”

Since everything was sold out, there really wasn’t much to do. One customer wanted to help pack up the tables, but Lu Qin had already handled it. They simply waved and left.

“Little Boss Lady, time to go home!”

“Okay, Daddy~!”

Before they could get on the food cart, several customers ran over.

“Boss Lu, wait! Here, take some fresh pineapple—we just cut it!”

“This is old-style yogurt—take two bottles!”

“Hawthorn snacks, for the Little Boss Lady!”

Lu Qin sighed.

“You guys are too generous. You really don’t have to.”

These were customers who had finished eating and gone shopping at the night market. Seeing that Lu Qin was still here, they brought back snacks for him and his daughter.

“You have to take them! It’s your fault for making such delicious food!”

“Take the yogurt—no saying thanks!”

“Boss Lu, is it true that many hotel owners have invited you to be their head chef?”

Lu Qin smiled.

“I just enjoy running my stall.”

After all, he had a system that rewarded him with points, and his earnings were excellent.

“I love being the Little Boss Lady!”

Lu Xi Yao added.

Hearing this, the customers felt relieved. High-end restaurants were too expensive, and if Boss Lu worked there, his amazing food would be even pricier.

Finally, the customers left, and the father-daughter duo was loaded with fruits and snacks. The tipping function had been disabled, but now customers had activated the gift-giving feature instead.

As their three-wheeled food cart disappeared into the bustling night market, a middle-aged man carrying several takeout boxes reached the end of the street.

This was Old Liu, the owner of a struggling fried noodle stall. His business had lost all its customers to Lu Qin.

“If people want fried noodles, they go to Lu Ji. If they can’t get it today, they’ll come back early tomorrow. They won’t even consider other vendors.”

Inside a newly rented shop, two young men were waiting. Old Liu set the food down.

“Taste it again. Can you replicate it?”

One of them took a bite and sighed.

“Damn, this is insanely good. Hard to believe there’s no artificial flavoring. That Lu guy is a real pro.”

Old Liu scowled.

“Stop praising him! Just tell me—can you copy it? I only need 80% of the taste!”

The tall young man finished his plate, burped in satisfaction, and grinned.

“85%! Don’t worry, Old Liu—we’re professionals. We can even make flour taste like milk powder!”

“Good. Get it ready tonight. We open tomorrow!”

chapters.title: Chapter 144
chapters.content:
Chapter 144 Shen Muyan, You’re Going to Be Ridiculously Happy for the Rest of Your Life!

At Ourei Grand Hotel – CEO’s Office, after finishing her overtime, Shen Muyan glanced at her phone—it was already 8:50 PM. She rubbed her stomach. So hungry. She pulled out her phone and dialed Fang Ya.

“Come out for a late-night snack.”

Fang Ya, who was in the middle of a gaming session, tilted her head to hold the phone while playing. The moment she heard food, she got excited, immediately abandoned her team, and shut down her computer.

In the game, her teammates were confused.

“???”

“Captain? Where did you go? Did you disconnect?”

“WTF! You just ditched us in the middle of a full-team mission!”

While changing clothes, Fang Ya asked over the phone, “Are we going to your husband’s stall? I’m craving his fried noodles!”

Shen Muyan replied, “No. At this time, he’s probably just finished packing up. He must be exhausted. Let’s go somewhere else.”

Fang Ya’s excitement dropped by half.

“ Tsk, tsk. Look at you, being all considerate. How about giving him a massage?”

“Are you coming or not? If not, I’m going home to sleep.”

“Fine! I’m coming!”

______

At ‘Ding Shuan Tian Xia’ Hotpot Restaurant, Shen Muyan ordered, “One portion of Australian grain-fed beef chuck. By the way, your latest video was good—high quality. 1.45 million likes and lots of comments.”

Fang Ya, pleased, ordered, “One portion of half-meter-long dried mustard greens. Wait, what? Shen Muyan, you’re actually complimenting me? No way—are you indirectly praising your husband?”

Shen Muyan remained unfazed.

“One portion of white jade mushrooms. I don’t know what you’re talking about.”

Fang Ya smirked.

“One portion of Iberico pork meatballs. Don’t play dumb! You never used to watch my videos. This time, you watched because your husband gave me his professional advice, right?”

Ignoring her, Shen Muyan continued, “One portion of fish roe shrimp paste and crispy konjac. That’s all for now. If it’s not enough, we’ll order more.”

The server confirmed, “Understood. Please wait a moment.”

As soon as the server left, Shen Muyan turned to Fang Ya.

“You’re paying.”

Fang Ya raised an eyebrow.

“Why?”

Shen Muyan calmly replied, “You asked me to get in touch with Boss Lu. I did. Shouldn’t you repay me?”

Fang Ya burst into laughter.

“I could’ve reached him myself. I just gave you an excuse to talk to him—to help you rebuild your relationship. If anything, you should be paying for me!”

Shen Muyan ignored her and focused on the steaming hotpot broth.

“It’s been a long time since I had hotpot. I’m going to eat to my heart’s content.”

Fang Ya immediately realized—she was getting a free meal today.

Smirking, she asked, “Should I get dipping sauce? Same oil-based sauce as always?”

“Mm.”

“ Ugh! Sesame sauce is the real king! Why do you still go for oil-based?!”

As the spicy broth started bubbling, the aroma of Sichuan peppercorns filled the air. They had been starving—and quickly dropped ingredients into the pot and started eating.

But after a few bites…

They both paused, looking at each other.

“The meat is good, but for some reason, I’m not really into it,” Shen Muyan said.

“Your husband has spoiled your taste buds! Even fried noodles seem better than this hotpot!” Fang Ya said.

“Should we go to his place and mooch some food?”

Shen Muyan shook her head.

“No, he needs to rest. Plus, the little one has school tomorrow.”

Fang Ya teased.

“Wow, listen to you, already protecting your man.”

Shen Muyan dismissed her.

“Let’s just ask him how to make a good dipping sauce. Your oil-based sauce isn’t great.”

Fang Ya’s eyes lit up.

“Great idea! I want to try his sesame sauce! Oil-based dipping isn’t even my specialty!”

Shen Muyan texted Lu Qin. Fang Ya leaned in to peek, but Shen Muyan pushed her away.

“Are you asleep?” Shen Muyan asked.

“Not yet. Just got out of the shower,” Lu Qin said.

At the time, Lu Qin had just finished packing up his food cart, taken a shower, and was relaxing in the living room with a yogurt while checking his system points.

“Is the little one asleep?” Shen Muyan asked.

“Just went to bed,” Lu Qin replied.

“Fang Ya and I are having hotpot. Are you hungry? Want to join us?”

“Nah, I’m not hungry. I’ll sleep soon. Enjoy your meal.”

“Master Chef Lu, we need your wisdom—how do we make the best dipping sauce?” Shen Muyan asked.

Lu Qin explained in detail.

“For oil-based dipping sauce: 3 parts sesame oil, ½ spoon vinegar, 1 spoon oyster sauce for umami, minced garlic, scallions, and small chili peppers.

For sesame paste: Use a “2:8 blend” (2 parts thick sesame paste, 8 parts thin), with sesame oil making up ⅕ of the ratio.”

Shen Muyan forwarded the recipe to Fang Ya.

Fang Ya jumped up excitedly and ran off to remake their dipping sauces.

“Wow! This sesame sauce is amazing!”

“My oil-based sauce is also way better now! I suddenly have an appetite again!”

The two immediately ordered more food—two more plates of meat and a mixed vegetable platter.

Fang Ya sighed dramatically.

“Your husband is literally a god at everything! Shen Muyan, you’re going to be too happy for the rest of your life!”

_____

Meanwhile, Lu Qin started using his system points to draw prizes.

With 2,700+ points, he started spending.

100 points → 80 mint candies (Ugh, the most common consolation prize…)

100 points → Traffic violation waiver coupon

100 points → Customizable street vendor uniform

100 points → Multi-purpose chef’s knife (permanently sharp)

After spending 1,700 points on 17 draws, he still hadn’t unlocked a top-tier skill—a bit disappointing.

Just as he was about to continue, the system triggered a new feature.

【System Lottery: 300 total draws reached! Advanced Lottery Unlocked! Each draw costs 1,000 points.】

Lu Qin’s eyes widened—an Advanced Lottery must mean better prizes!

Without hesitation, he activated it.

“Surely, this won’t be full of mint candies, right…?”

【1,000 points spent. Advanced Draw Result: Mint Candy Mega Pack】

Lu Qin: “…”

“This is a scam!”

Opening the Mega Pack, he found a massive bag of 500 mint candies—all different flavors. original, watermelon, peach, grape, green apple, mango…

He sighed.

“Forget it. I’m going to sleep.”

_____

The next morning, Lu Qin followed his usual routine—waking up, washing up, and heading to the market. Yesterday’s pickled vegetables were ready. He opened the jar, took a bite, and found them crispy, tangy, and perfectly spicy. He packed several small containers to share and cooked three portions of fried noodles before heading out.

In the neighborhood, people were already exercising, shopping, and walking pets. Seeing him, neighbors greeted him warmly.

“Boss Lu, up bright and early as always! Hurry up with the morning stall—I’m already craving your food!”

Even Elder Ge and Chen Dahai, who were practicing tai chi, perked up. Chen Dahai, who had rented an apartment nearby and showed no intention of returning to his villa, asked, “Boss Lu, what’s in your bag?”

Lu Qin smiled.

“Pickled veggies. Want to try?”

Instantly, a crowd gathered.

“Pickled vegetables? I want some!”

“Me too! Boss Lu’s pickles? Count me in!”

Even Auntie Wang, who was 50 meters away, turned back immediately—forgetting about her grocery shopping.

chapters.title: Chapter 145
chapters.content:
Chapter 145 Dreams Are Opposite, So That Means No Milk Tea in Reality Either!

Lu Qin gave each neighbor a box of pickled vegetables, and they were absolutely thrilled.

“This is authentic Sichuan pickled vegetables!”

“Wow! It has long beans and tender ginger—perfect for porridge!”

“There’s crispy radish, my favorite! Amazing!”

“Can’t wait to taste it—Boss Lu’s pickled veggies must be top-tier!”

Elderly folks in their 60s, 70s, and 80s had grown up eating porridge for breakfast—and they loved pairing it with traditional sides like fermented tofu, salted duck eggs, preserved mustard greens, and pickled vegetables. Elder Ge took a bite of the pickled long beans and immediately praised them.

“Wow! These are perfectly sour, crisp, and tender! It’s super appetizing! Would be amazing with rice or noodles!”

Chen Dahai, not one to be picky, grabbed a piece of tender pickled ginger with his hands and popped it into his mouth.

The crunchy texture and tangy flavor made him pause for a moment—this was insanely good!

“This is amazing! Better than the ones made by restaurant chefs!”

Chen Dahai’s own restaurant’s Sichuan chef made pickled vegetables, but compared to Lu Qin’s, the taste and texture were simply on a whole different level.

Auntie Wang took a bite of the pickled long beans and nodded in approval. But instead of eating more, she closed the lid tightly.

“This is too good. I’ll save it and stir-fry it with minced pork later to eat with rice!”

Others also treasured their pickled vegetables like gold.

“Boss Lu, do you sell these? I want to buy more—they’re too delicious!”

Lu Qin smiled.

“Not selling them yet, but if you like them, come by my place at noon and grab some. We’re all neighbors, no need to pay.”

These elderly folks were always kind to Lu Qin and his daughter. They often brought fresh vegetables, fruits, and eggs for them—not expecting anything in return, just because they genuinely liked Lu Qin and adored his little girl.

Hearing Lu Qin’s words, they liked him even more. Pickled vegetables weren’t expensive, but making them wasn’t easy—especially in large quantities.

“That won’t do, Little Boss Lu! We have to buy them. This kind of delicious pickled vegetables is something we’ll always need in our kitchens! Perfect for porridge, drinking, stir-frying… Just thinking about it makes me drool!”

“Boss Lu, will you keep making these? I want to place a long-term order!”

“Me too! My wife loves pickled vegetables!”

Lu Qin had expected his top-tier pickles to be good, but he hadn’t anticipated such overwhelming demand. Clearly, five jars wouldn’t be enough—many people would want them in the future.

Meanwhile, Chen Dahai was even more impressed with Lu Qin. He immediately dialed his daughter, Chen Yue, waking her up from sleep.

“Yue Yue, listen—pay more attention to Lu Qin!”

Half-asleep, Chen Yue grumbled, “Dad, why don’t you just kidnap him and make him your son-in-law? I’m too tired. Let me sleep!”

Before Chen Dahai could say anything, she hung up on him. He sighed in frustration. His daughter had already made several advances, but Lu Qin had never reciprocated.

Was it because he wasn’t interested in her? Or was he still hung up on Shen Mu Yan?

So frustrating…

Still chewing on a tangy pickled radish, Chen Dahai watched as Lu Qin drove off, smiling contentedly.

_____

At the market, Lu Qin arrived at Jingyuan Market—only to find no parking spaces available. Just as he was looking around, Boss Zhao stepped out of his shop, about to buy breakfast.

When he spotted Lu Qin, he immediately abandoned his breakfast mission and ran over.

“Boss Lu! No parking? Come, use my spot!”

Without hesitation, he drove his own small pickup truck onto the sidewalk, clearing the space for Lu Qin. Lu Qin parked, then carried three servings of fried noodles and two boxes of pickled vegetables into the shop.

“Boss Zhao, I need more takeout containers—same as last time, another 3,500 pieces. Also, five more pickle jars.”

As he set the food down on the table, Boss Zhao nodded absentmindedly, eyes locked on the food.

“Boss Lu, is this pickled vegetables?”

“Yep, brought some for you to try.”

Boss Zhao’s excitement skyrocketed.

“WIFE! SON! GET OUT HERE!”

His wife and kid rushed out, greeting Lu Qin enthusiastically.

“Good morning, Uncle Lu! So glad you’re here! The buns next door taste awful!”

“Morning, Xiao Hai! Boss Zhao, Sis, enjoy. I’m heading to buy groceries.”

Meanwhile, Boss Zhao had already started devouring the fried noodles, speaking with his mouth full.

“Go ahead! I’ll bring your stuff over at noon!”

Lu Qin left the shop and entered the market.

Back at the shop, Boss Zhao’s family was busy enjoying their meal, constantly praising the flavors.

After shopping for over 40 minutes, Lu Qin had loaded up his car with fresh ingredients.

His trunk—already legendary for its storage capacity—was packed to the brim. Before heading home, he stopped by the dealership where he had bought his three-wheeled food cart.

With his menu expanding and food sales increasing, it was time to upgrade to a four-wheeled food cart.

After a 10-minute discussion, he reached an agreement with the dealer. His current three-wheeled cart, bought for 3,600 yuan just over two weeks ago, was still in great condition and could be resold for 2,500 yuan.

As for the new four-wheeled mobile food cart, there were no display models—they had to order directly from the manufacturer. Boss Zhao showed him photos and videos of the latest models.

These modern electric food carts weren’t just affordable—they were also spacious, well-designed, and highly functional. Lu Qin picked a purple one, with a rainbow LED sign on top and ample interior space—almost like a small food truck.

“For the sign, put ‘Lu Ji Snack Stall.’ For the front and back, print: ‘So Delicious You’ll Cry’ and ‘You’ll Regret Not Eating This!'”

He also requested a customized layout—Two main serving windows, two gas stoves, no ice cream machine and two extra storage compartments.

To make sure everything was clear, he sketched a detailed blueprint and handed it to the dealer.

“Can you make it exactly like this?”

“Of course! Anything for our customers! We’ll make it perfect!”

The total cost? 5,000 yuan.

Lu Qin found the price reasonable and paid a deposit. Since the manufacturer was local, the cart would be ready by tomorrow.

Lu Qin returned to the South City Old Street and went home. Inside, Lu Xi Yao was still fast asleep. Lu Qin quietly began prepping ingredients for the morning stall.

Lu Qin woke up his daughter. She groggily sat up—but her soul was still in bed.

“Yao Yao, wake up! Time for ice cream—your favorite Chocliz!”

The moment she heard ice cream, she instantly activated full power mode, eyes wide open.

“Daddy, did you say ice cream?”

Lu Qin smirked.

“You must be dreaming. Who eats ice cream in the morning? Get up, wash your face, and eat breakfast before school.”

He pinched her chubby cheeks playfully. Little Yao Yao’s big round eyes blinked in suspicion.

“But I heard you say ice cream!”

Lu Qin chuckled.

“That was probably in your dream. And dreams are the opposite of reality. If you had ice cream in your dream, it means no ice cream in real life.”

She pouted.

“But Daddy… in my dream, I was drinking milk tea, not eating ice cream!”

Lu Qin, amused, replied, “Dreams are opposite—so that means no milk tea in reality either!”

Little Yao Yao: “…!!!”

chapters.title: Chapter 146
chapters.content:
Chapter 146 Group Dining! President Shen Washes Dishes to Pay Off the Debt!

Lu Xiyao felt like she had been tricked by Lu Qin, but she had no evidence. Her mind wasn’t working too well, and she groggily brushed her teeth and washed her face.

“Daddy, what’s for breakfast? Can I have ice cream and bubble tea for breakfast?”

“A forehead flick is also quite tasty. Do you want to try it?”

“Wuuu~! Never mind then.”

At the dining table, there was pickled vegetables, and breakfast consisted of porridge and tea eggs.

“Hu~! Ha~! Hu~! Ha~!”

“Daddy~! The porridge is too hot~!”

“Eat from the edge of the bowl first. It’s not hot there.”

Lu Qin used his chopsticks to gather the porridge along the bowl’s edge before taking a bite. The little one copied him, and sure enough, it wasn’t hot.

Lu Qin peeled a tea egg and fed it to her. She opened her rosy little mouth and took a big bite, her eyes glancing at the pickled vegetables.

“Daddy, what’s that?”

“Pickled vegetables.”

The little one didn’t like eating vegetables, and she didn’t like pickled ones either, so she didn’t reach for them.

“Here, have a piece of tender ginger. ‘Eat radish in winter, ginger in summer, and stay away from the doctor’s prescriptions.’”

Lu Qin picked up a piece of ginger and placed it in her bowl. Her little face showed clear resistance—ginger and garlic were both terrifying, and she didn’t dare eat them.

“Daddy, can I not eat it? How about adding some sugar to my porridge? I want it sweet.”

“Sure, Daddy won’t force you.”

“Daddy, you’re the best~!”

“Actually, Daddy was thinking, if Yaoyao wasn’t picky with food, I’d reward you with an ice cream. But I guess never mind.”

Lu Qin sighed deliberately.

Hearing the words ice cream, the little one’s eyes immediately lit up.

After struggling for a moment, she said, “Daddy, Yaoyao isn’t picky! I’m very obedient! I’ll eat this ginger.”

“Don’t force yourself.”

“Not at all.”

After saying that, she took a big bite of the ginger, treating it like medicine.

Chewing twice, she realized—it was crunchy, slightly sour, with only a mild ginger flavor, actually quite tasty.

“Daddy, this is actually pretty good!”

She alternated between bites of ginger and sips of porridge, looking very satisfied.

Lu Qin smirked, “Try this garlic next. It’s also delicious.”

“Okay~!”

Before, she would’ve fought to the death rather than eat these things. After breakfast, they got ready to head out. The older one set up his breakfast stall, and the little one went to kindergarten. Lu Qin packed her lunch and added two boxes of pickled vegetables.

“Yaoyao, give this to Teacher Li to try.”

“Got it.”

At the Gate of Starlight Kindergarten

“Bye, Daddy.”

“Bye, Yaoyao. Listen to your teacher.”

“Mm-hmm.”

Lu Xiyao was led into the kindergarten by Teacher Li.

“Teacher Li, my daddy made this pickled vegetable for you. Try it! If you like it, I’ll bring more next time.”

“Pickled vegetables? Your dad made them?”

“Yeah! I already ate some, and it’s super tasty!”

“Thank you, Yaoyao! I’ll thank your dad when I see him!”

Teacher Li smiled happily and didn’t hesitate to accept it. Feeling great, she took the two boxes of pickled vegetables to the office.

Inside, Teacher Wang and Teacher Chen were having breakfast—one was munching on corn, the other on a steamed bun.

“Teacher Li, I left your spicy noodles on your desk.”

“Thanks.”

She opened the meal box—this spicy noodle shop wasn’t great, but the alternatives nearby were even worse. If only Yaoyao’s dad set up his stall near the kindergarten. My stomach would be in heaven.

She opened the box of pickled vegetables.

“Teacher Li, where’d you get the pickled vegetables?”

The other two teachers leaned in curiously.

“Yaoyao gave them to me. Her dad made them.”

“Yaoyao’s dad made them? I have to try!”

“Me too!”

The three of them each took a bite and simultaneously gasped. Their view on pickled vegetables changed completely.

“This is amazing! So refreshing!”

“I never knew pickled vegetables could taste this good! It’s unbeatable!”

“This even makes my plain steamed bun taste incredible!”

All three teachers agreed—even if it was paired with coarse bran, it’d still be delicious!

Teacher Li quickly hid one box in her drawer— this one’s for my family after work .

The other box? It disappeared in no time during breakfast.

_____

At the Gate of Sunshine Elementary School, before Lu Qin even arrived, a long line of customers was already waiting eagerly. When his three-wheeled food cart appeared, the queue got even more excited.

Today’s breakfast menu included stir-fried rice noodles, chicken tofu soup, mung bean smoothies, ice jelly cakes, and crystal shrimp dumplings. He had also prepared 50 boxes of pickled vegetables for sale—8 yuan per box, around 300 grams. A very fair price, considering hotel versions cost 38 yuan per plate but weren’t even as good.

“Boss Lu, new item? Is this pickled vegetables?”

“Yeah, 8 yuan per box. Only 50 boxes, one per person.”

“I’ll take one to try! And three portions of fried rice noodles, three mung bean smoothies, and one order of shrimp dumplings.”

“Got it.”

Lu Qin fired up the stove for the stir-fry. Pre-packed food was on the side table—customers could grab their own, with prices clearly marked on a whiteboard.

After paying, the first customer immediately dug into the shrimp dumplings—devoured three in a row—then took a bite of the pickled vegetables…

“Damn! Boss Lu, your pickled vegetables are incredible!”

The 50 boxes were gone in no time. Customers behind the first 50 started grumbling.

“Boss Lu, only 50 boxes? That’s way too little!”

“Yeah! 50 isn’t enough! Now the stall next door thinks we can’t afford pickled vegetables! Next time, one box per person at least!”

“Damn, I wish that rich second-gen punk would show up again. I’d shove a spoonful of this in his face and tell him—‘Boss Lu is your ancestor when it comes to pickled vegetables!’”

Lu Qin chuckled, “Actually, I have more, but I didn’t have time to cut and pack them this morning.”

“Boss Lu, need a helper? I don’t need pay—just feed me. I can work hard, plus I’ll give shoulder massages and serve tea.”

The moment someone offered, others joined in. Some even offered to pay just for the chance to be a helper.

Lu Qin could only shake his head and laugh. The breakfast rush ended in just an hour. Though he had prepared a lot, the customers’ buying power was insane.

_____

Lu Qin didn’t check his earnings right away. Instead, he looked at his points—1399. Yesterday, he had spent 1000 points on a high-level lottery spin, only to get a giant bag of peppermint candy, which still annoyed him. Now that he could spin again, he decided to save up for a bigger pull.

As Lu Qin was about to eat, Shen Muyan and Fang Ya showed up uninvited, dragging along Song Xuanxuan.

Fang Ya greeted him with—“Lu Qin, Shen Muyan is missing you—”

After a dramatic pause, she added, “missing your cooking.”

That instantly made Shen Muyan blush, but she kept her cold, indifferent expression, pretending nothing happened.

Originally, Lu Qin was just going to whip up some simple fried rice noodles, but with three beauties mooching, he made three proper dishes—Twice-cooked pork, Mapo tofu and Tomato scrambled eggs

The three freeloaders ate like they were starving, praising every bite. After the meal, Fang Ya and Song Xuanxuan each snatched a cup of bubble tea.

Shen Muyan, feeling bad for Lu Qin’s hard work, asked Fang Ya to pay.

“Wow, you’re already budgeting together like a couple?”

Fang Ya teased while sipping her drink. Then, without warning, she shoved Shen Muyan into Lu Qin’s arms, grabbed Song Xuanxuan, and bolted.

“President Shen will wash the dishes to pay off the debt! We don’t eat for free! Bye!”

chapters.title: Chapter 147
chapters.content:
Chapter 147 Straightforward Lu Qin! Other Vendors Are Trembling!

Shen Muyan was suddenly pushed by Fang Ya, stumbling straight into Lu Qin’s arms. The culprits, Fang Ya and Song Xuanxuan, ran off like spinning tops, leaving only the male and female leads in the room. The air froze for a moment. It was very quiet.

Only the sound of their heartbeats could be heard. Ten seconds later, they separated, both looking slightly awkward.

“I’ll go wash the dishes. You rest for a bit.”

Shen Muyan lowered her head, her delicate face tinged with a light blush, and walked into the kitchen. Today, President Shen was wearing a black irregular-cut round-neck blouse, slightly tight-fitting, accentuating her graceful curves. Her lower half was a simple white short skirt, highlighting her slim, straight, and fair legs.

She put on an apron, her usual cold and elegant aura softening slightly in Lu Qin’s presence—less aloof, more gentle, exuding an indescribable charm. Shen Muyan wasn’t very skilled at washing dishes. It wasn’t that she was incompetent, but her busy schedule had never given her a chance to enter the kitchen.

Lu Qin watched through the glass kitchen door for a while, then went to the living room to peel some fruit. Feeling his departure, Shen Muyan let out a quiet sigh of relief.

After finishing the dishes, she walked into the living room. Lu Qin had already prepared a fruit platter—a beautifully arranged mix of strawberries, green dates, blood oranges, small Thai mangoes, and dragon fruits…

These fruits were all gifts from customers and neighbors—there were so many that he couldn’t possibly finish them all.

Seeing the fruit platter, Shen Muyan’s eyes lit up. Whether it was knife skills or presentation, this platter looked way better than the ones served at her restaurant.

She took out her phone and snapped a few photos—planning to show her fruit-cutting chef for inspiration. Lu Qin had master-level fruit-cutting skills—his casual cutting was already top-tier.

He could even carve fruits into the twelve zodiac animals—though that would be unnecessary. Shen Muyan mistakenly thought Lu Qin had specially prepared the fruit platter for her. Feeling a bit embarrassed but also secretly happy, she said, “You didn’t have to peel so much—I just ate lunch, I can’t eat that much.”

Lu Qin, ever the straightforward man, replied, “I’m taking these for my stall later. If you can’t eat much, just eat less.”

“Oh.”

Shen Muyan nodded, picked up a fork, and started eating—finishing almost half the platter. The two of them chatted casually, mostly about Lu Xiyao.

They were once on the same household registry, but now, talking about work or life felt inappropriate—their only connection was the little one.

Lu Qin sent recent photos of the little girl to Shen Muyan—some were cute, silly, clingy, and some showed her acting like a little boss…

Each one was adorable, and Shen Muyan saved them all. Before leaving, Lu Qin gave her two boxes of ice jelly cake and a cup of osmanthus oolong milk tea.

At this point, she had also joined the ranks of those who ‘eat and take’, but she didn’t want to refuse—it was too delicious, too irresistible. Besides, Fang Ya and Song Xuanxuan only got milk tea, while she got extra desserts.

After frequently eating Lu Qin’s food, Shen Muyan realized she had gained two pounds. Although it wasn’t noticeable—she still had her perfect S-curve, looking slim in clothes and curvy underneath—she couldn’t let it slide. So, she added morning runs to her routine.

_____

After Shen Muyan left, Lu Qin continued cooking Imperial Concubine Chicken Wings and boiling mung bean soup. At 2:30 PM, he sealed up 300+ cups of osmanthus oolong milk tea and neatly packed them into a fresh-lock insulated box.

Milk tea couldn’t be left outside for too long, or its taste would degrade, so it had to be stored properly. After loading all the food and ingredients onto his tricycle food cart, he set off.

Checking his Stall Location App, the best vending spot today was Huaguo Mountain Park—a large influx of tourists meant high foot traffic.

Since it was a weekday, most of his loyal customers from the Penguin chat group couldn’t rush over. They flooded the chat with crying emojis.

_____

Lu Qin chose a spot at the foot of the mountain, where the park entrance and square intersected. This area was shaded and occasionally had cool summer breezes. Many people were wandering around.

Because this wasn’t his usual vending spot, loyal customers couldn’t wait in advance—they had to wander aimlessly looking for him. After setting up his stall, Lu Qin snapped some location photos and sent them to the Penguin group.

Seeing the update, the scattered customers in the park shouted in excitement and rushed over.

There were a few other stalls nearby—a drink stand, toy stall, grilled sausage cart, and skewers booth.

None of them realized that in just a few minutes, a massive wave of customers would descend upon the area, bringing an unexpected fortune. However, Lu Qin’s Stall App had already predicted this surge—he was about to make a quick fortune.

Nearby, the drink stall had some business, but the other vendors were barely selling anything. Most of them were just elderly vendors killing time—better to do something than be idle.

Within minutes, over twenty customers found Lu Qin’s stall and rushed in. His previously quiet stall suddenly had a long line.

People around stared in curiosity—how did this food cart suddenly blow up?

“Boss Lu, three portions of stir-fried noodles, one milk tea, and an order of Imperial Concubine Chicken Wings!”

“Give me the menu—I’ll take everything!”

“I’ll take one of everything!”

“Bro, stop talking nonsense. Even if there were no limits, buying everything would take at least two full bowls!”

“Excuse me, I’ll just take twice-cooked pork, shrimp dumplings, a box of pickled vegetables, and a milk tea.”

“I only had one egg for lunch—been waiting all day for this! One of everything for me!”

“One chicken tofu soup, one Imperial Concubine Chicken Wing, three iced mung bean smoothies, and an osmanthus oolong milk tea!”

…

The ordering frenzy left onlookers dumbfounded.

This stall sold the weirdest combinations—pickled vegetables with milk tea!
