Each customer ordered in bulk—spending over 100 yuan per order. Was the food really that good?

Nearby vendors were green with envy. Selling water and grilled sausages barely made any profit, and they struggled to sell even one or two items in a long while.

But this young guy’s stall was something else entirely—ice jelly cakes sold for 25 yuan per box, milk tea for 28 yuan, twice-cooked pork for 28 yuan…

High prices? Fine. But why were customers still buying in droves?

Some even bought everything on the menu, spending over 200 yuan at a time. Since when did street food stalls turn into mini gourmet restaurants?

At first, they thought it was just young people with poor budgeting skills—blowing all their cash in one go. But then they noticed—middle-aged and elderly customers were also buying, happily sipping 20-yuan milk teas without hesitation. This street food stall was absolutely insane!

The smell of stir-fried noodles, twice-cooked pork, and Mapo tofu wafted through the air, drawing in more and more customers. The other vendors nearby started trembling.

chapters.title: Chapter 148
chapters.content:
Chapter 148 Sorry, Your Takeout Spilled a Bit!

“This Penguin chat group is too toxic! I can’t open it!”

“These guys don’t just post food pictures at midnight, they do it in broad daylight!”

“I’m doomed! Seeing all this food is making me starving and craving like crazy!”

“I’m at work, I can’t eat Boss Lu’s food. This feels like finally getting to visit a long-distance girlfriend, only for her to be on her period—pure torture!”

“Stop posting! One more food pic and I’m reporting you for posting NSFW content! Where’s the NSFW? Look at that golden fried noodles and Imperial Concubine Chicken Wings—shining with a golden glow!”

…

No matter how much customers in the group begged, ranted, or cursed, the food pictures kept coming. It was as if the group had turned into a food photography competition—whoever had the best shots, posted the most drool-inducing photos.

It was like saying—“Haha, I got to eat Boss Lu’s food today. Feeling superior and proud. Just had to show off a little!”

One poor office worker was stuck in a meeting, listening to his boss ranting and scolding everyone. Already fuming with frustration, he glanced at the Penguin chat—only to see more food pictures popping up. Now he was not only angry but also starving.

Frustrated, he exited the chat and glanced at his home screen—there, he saw a yellow icon.

Meituan Takeout.

A light bulb flashed above his head—why didn’t I think of this sooner?!

He immediately opened Meituan, found a nearby Wallace (fast food restaurant), and ordered the cheapest meal combo—but with a long note in the special instructions.

_____

Nearby, a Meituan delivery rider in his signature yellow uniform was idly sitting on his e-bike, playing Honor of Kings because there hadn’t been any orders for 20 minutes. Just as he entered a match, a new order popped up.

“Damn it! I wait 20 minutes for nothing, then the moment I start a game, an order comes in. So unlucky!”

He checked the order details—the pickup location was just nearby at Wallace, but the customer note was long.

“ Ugh , another one of these ‘special request’ customers.”

The rider hated these kinds of orders.

Ordering a 10-yuan meal but expecting extra favors—like grabbing bottled water, buying cigarettes, or even throwing out their trash.

If you don’t do it, they threaten to give you a bad review.

Absolutely disgusting behavior!

He braced himself and read the note.

The rider was about to lose his temper— What kind of ridiculous request is this?

But then he saw the last line.

The curse words that were about to leave his mouth stayed inside.

Wait a minute… I get a free meal AND an extra 10 yuan?

Is this for real?

This might actually be worth it. Plus, the delivery fee itself was another 5-6 yuan!

Huaguo Mountain Park? That’s just across the street!

No hesitation—he grabbed the Wallace meal, put on his helmet, and sped off.

Upon reaching the park, he was about to ask for directions, but then he saw it—At the foot of the mountain, a food stall with a massive line of people.

Riding closer, he took one look at the line and gasped.

“Holy crap, this line is ridiculous!”

He immediately called the customer.

“Hello? The line is way too long. I can’t wait that long. Find someone else!”

“No way! I’ll add 20 more yuan and give you a five-star review! Will you do it now?”

“…Alright, fine! But I’m only waiting 30 minutes. If it takes longer, you’re adding more money! Also, confirm the order now, or I might run out of time.”

“Money’s not an issue! I’ll pay whatever it takes!”

The rider hung up, sighed, and got in line. To pass the time, he reopened his game and started another match.

But just two minutes in, something caught his nose.

“What is that smell? Why is it so good?”

He stretched his neck to look ahead—Boss Lu was cooking twice-cooked pork.

“Damn! Just stir-frying pork can make me drool? That’s insane!”

He realized he wasn’t the only one drooling—other customers in line looked just as hungry and desperate.

Now he understood—No wonder someone was willing to pay extra to get this food.

No wonder there was a huge line.

This stuff must be amazing!

His stomach growled. Giving in, he opened the Wallace meal and started eating.

The Order is Ready!

Twenty minutes later, he finally reached the front.

Seeing the limit of one per item, he had no choice but to buy only one extra fried rice noodles for himself, while everything else was for the customer. He took a picture of the food and sent it to the customer.

The reply came fast.

Oh crap!

The rider panicked—how did the customer notice a missing portion so quickly?

He had originally planned to keep a cup of iced mung bean smoothie for himself, but now… he had to let it go.

He quickly lied.

Packing everything up, he sat down and devoured his fried rice noodles.

“Holy crap! This is the best fried rice noodles I’ve ever had!”

In just a minute, he finished it all, still craving more. But the customer called again, urging him to hurry up—so he rushed off to make the delivery.

At Xusheng Electronics, a glasses-wearing office worker was waiting anxiously outside. Finally, the rider arrived.

“Finally! I’m starving! Hand it over!”

“Uh… Sorry, the food spilled a bit on the way.”

The office worker paused, then shrugged, “No worries, it’s normal for some soup to spill.”

The rider handed over a single plastic bag.

“???”

The office worker stared at it.

After checking multiple times, he screamed in horror.

“You call this ‘spilled a bit’?! YOU ATE IT ALL!”

The rider: “Uh… My bad. I’ll refund you.”

The office worker was furious but helpless—it wasn’t like he could beat up the rider.

But then—he noticed something suspicious.

There were crumbs on the rider’s lips.

“…Wait a minute.”

He took a closer look.

“YOU HAVE ICE JELLY CAKE CRUMBS ON YOUR FACE!”

“YOU ATE MY FOOD!”

“What?! That’s impossible! No way! I didn’t— hic!” The rider said.

“Damn it! EVEN YOUR HICCUP SMELLS LIKE OSMANTHUS OOLONG TEA! YOU LIAR!”

Enraged, he slapped his own face twice, leaving red handprints. He then took multiple selfies as evidence.

“WTF!”

“You either pay up and buy it again, or I’m filing a complaint!”

The rider, terrified, raced off to buy the food again—praying it wasn’t sold out.

chapters.title: Chapter 149
chapters.content:
Chapter 149 One Bite Means the End of Me!

At the entrance of Huaguo Mountain Park, seven or eight tour buses slowly pulled in, each bearing the ‘Frog Travel Group’ logo.

After parking, crowds of tourists poured out, all wearing little yellow hats and waving small red flags. The large group moved forward in an orderly fashion.

“Huaguo Mountain? Is this where the Monkey King lives?”

“No, that’s in Yun’gang City. This Huaguo Mountain just has the same name.”

“The scenery looks nice. Can we pick peaches here?”

“I heard Jiangcheng is a ‘food desert’—nothing here is worth eating. Let’s just look around and wait until the next stop in Haishi for good food.”

“Haishi’s clay pot chicken and oil-poached fish are amazing!”

“Stop talking about food! You’re making me hungry! This place probably doesn’t have anything good to eat.”

“If nothing else, we can just eat KFC or something.”

…

With nearly 300 tourists, the group followed their guide and scattered throughout Huaguo Mountain Park.

“A true hero climbs to the top! Let’s conquer the summit and enjoy the breathtaking view!”

The tourists were full of enthusiasm and ambition.

Since they were here, they had to climb the peak—otherwise, the trip would feel wasted. Huaguo Mountain had rugged peaks and diverse scenery, standing 1,077 meters above sea level. There was no cable car—only leg power could take you up. Whoever made it to the top was truly a hero.

“Everyone, gather at the base and stock up on water and snacks. There are no supplies on the mountainside—only a small shop at the summit.”

The tour guide warned them. He had seen this situation too many times—tourists always started off acting tough, thinking they could climb Everest, only to collapse one-third of the way up, panting like dying dogs.

“There’s a small shop at the base! Follow me!”

“Is there food? I need to eat something first.”

“I’m starving! We really should have eaten before climbing. No energy otherwise!”

“This place probably only sells biscuits and bread.”

The crowd in yellow hats swarmed towards the base of the mountain. Soon, they spotted a long, winding queue at the foot of the mountain.

At the front of the line, there was a bustling snack stall, packed with people—a stark contrast to the deserted nearby stalls.

“It smells amazing!”

“I can smell stir-fried rice noodles!”

“Didn’t expect to find a snack stall here! And look at that long line!”

“Guide, is this place famous? So many people lining up—it must be a local specialty! Do you know about it?”

“…No idea. Never heard of it.”

The guide looked confused, shaking his head as he led the group forward.

“Hey, young man! Is the food here any good? What did you buy?”

The young man shook his head vigorously, saying, “Not good at all. I didn’t buy anything.”

But as he spoke, he was holding a huge bag of food and quickly walking away.

“???”

“If it’s bad, why did he buy so much?”

“That guy is lying! He was practically drooling while saying it’s not good!”

“Hey, buddy! You bought a lot—what kind of snack stall is this? Is it some online-famous place?”

“…Aba aba aba…”

“…”

“Excuse me, miss, you bought so much—what’s the best thing to eat here?”

“Miss?”

The middle-aged woman thought she misheard, but when she realized someone called her ‘miss’, she was instantly delighted—such an appropriate title!

“Are you from out of town?”

“Yeah, we’re tourists.”

“Good, then I don’t have to worry.”

“Worry about what?”

The woman explained.

“This stall’s food is ridiculously good. The line is always insanely long, and customers are really competitive. Regulars don’t want new customers—they see them as enemies!”

“That’s a bit exaggerated, isn’t it?”

“Not at all! Ask anyone, and they’ll say it’s ‘not good’—just to stop you from lining up. Supplies are limited. If you get some, it means someone else might not. Get it now?”

“Then we HAVE to try it! Thank you, miss!”

“Hehe, you’re welcome.”

“I’m getting in line! You guys go climb the mountain first.”

“Me too! It smells too good!”

“What about climbing? Where’s your enthusiasm?”

“Eat first, climb later!”

“This line is ridiculously long—how long will it take?”

“Everyone else is lining up. I can’t let my stomach down!”

In an instant, a huge portion of the tour group abandoned their climbing plans and joined the queue. The line stretched from the base of the mountain all the way to the central flower bed in the park square.

“Holy crap! This line is terrifying! The last person might starve to death before reaching the front!”

Passersby started taking photos—some posting on social media, others uploading to Douyin (TikTok).

Scenes like this were rare!

“Forget it, I’m not lining up. This queue is scary.”

“That young vendor can handle this? His wok hasn’t stopped moving for a second—he’s like the ancestor of Parkinson’s disease! Insane skills!”

“How much is he making today?”

As satisfied customers left with their food, bad news started spreading:

“Osmanthus Oolong Milk Tea is SOLD OUT!”

“Mung Bean Smoothie is SOLD OUT!”

“Crystal Shrimp Dumplings are SOLD OUT!”

The tourists panicked—they hadn’t even gotten their turn yet, and items were already disappearing!

“Nooo! I’ve been waiting 25 minutes!”

“Finally my turn! One order of stir-fried rice noodles, please! Smells amazing!”

“I want Ice Jelly Cake!”

“Chicken Wings! Imperial Concubine Chicken Wings!”

Tourists who originally thought Jiangcheng was a food desert had no expectations—but now, they were glued to this snack stall at the base of Huaguo Mountain.

“This stir-fried rice noodles is incredible! I’ve never had anything this good!”

“My Ice Jelly Cake is amazing! The best dessert ever!”

“Oh my god, I just tasted the ancestor of Twice-Cooked Pork!”

“It looks so good—let me have a bite!”

“NO! One bite means the end of me!”

“This handsome vendor has skills! His cooking is five-star restaurant level!”

“Five stars? His food is even better than a five-star chef’s!”

“The true masters are hidden among the common folk!”

…

“This is too good! I suddenly don’t want to leave—I want to stay in Jiangcheng for a few more days!”

“Me too!”

“Are we still climbing?”

“Forget climbing! This Chicken Tofu Soup is more important! It’s fresh, fragrant, tender, and smooth—but it’s limited to one per person!”

“This Jiangcheng trip was worth it! I’ve found my dream food—no regrets in life!”

“Totally worth it!”

…

The once-enthusiastic mountain climbers had completely surrendered to food.

Even the tour guide, munching on chicken wings, thought, This is the easiest tour I’ve ever led. Just eat amazing food all day and the job’s done.

“Wait… When did Jiangcheng get such an incredible snack stall? How did I not know?”

“They have a night stall at University Town’s Night Market. Wanna go?” Tour guide said.

“YES!”

…

As Lu Qin closed shop, half the tourists still hadn’t gotten their food, looking like they were about to cry.

One guy even chased Lu Qin’s tricycle, panting.

“Swallow! Swallow! How do I live without you?”

“His name is Lu, you drama king!”

“I didn’t get any! I had to steal half a piece of Ice Jelly Cake from the tour guide—it was SO GOOD!”

“We HAVE to go to the night market!”

chapters.title: Chapter 150
chapters.content:
Chapter 150 Liu’s Snack Shop!

The yellow-clad delivery rider rushed back to Huaguo Mountain Park—only to find that the snack stall was gone. All he saw were crowds of tourists in yellow hats.

“Shit! They sold out? The stall is gone?”

A cold sweat dripped down his face. Panic set in as he tremblingly pulled out his phone and called the customer with glasses.

“C-Customer… T-the snack stall… is gone! Sold out!”

From the other end, came an ear-piercing roar, loud enough to be heard even without speaker mode:

“YOU XX! YOU STUPID XX! I’M GONNA @￥%*# YOU! JUST WAIT! I’M REPORTING YOU! YOU STOLE MY FOOD—”

The delivery rider’s heart sank to rock bottom.

Damn it. I should’ve never taken a bite!

But… he couldn’t resist!

The food was just too damn good!

Whatever! Let him complain!

That evening, Lu Qin worked non-stop until 7:30 PM, preparing a huge amount of food and ingredients. Meanwhile, Lu Xiyao learned a new skill—sealing drink cups.

“Daddy, this sealing machine is so fun!”

“Be careful not to burn or pinch your hand.”

“I know, Daddy! You’ve said that five times now!”

She enthusiastically placed a cup of mung bean smoothie into the sealing ring.

With a press of the start button, the ring automatically retracted, and the sealing film was applied. The fully automatic sealing machine adjusted for different cup sizes on its own. The sealing film was pre-loaded, so all the little one had to do was—Place the cup inside, press the start button and take out the sealed drink. A simple three-step process—almost foolproof.

Even though the risk was low, Lu Qin still repeated his warnings multiple times. Kids were curious and mischievous—who knew if she might stick her hand into the heating ring and burn herself?

“Daddy, I’ve sealed 30 cups! Am I awesome?”

“Very awesome! Our little boss lady is getting more and more skilled.”

“Hehe~!”

She beamed with pride. Every sealed cup felt like an achievement. But soon, she noticed something—the number of unsealed drinks kept piling up.

As Lu Qin sped up his cup-filling pace, the marble countertop was soon covered with dozens of mung bean smoothies.

“Daddy, slow down!”

The little one tried to keep up, but her tiny hands weren’t fast enough. Eventually, Lu Qin took over, sealing cup after cup with lightning speed.

Next up—milk tea…

“Daddy, you’re amazing! You’re like a ninja!”

Lu Qin’s movements were swift and precise, showing off his mastery through repetition. Once all the food and drinks were packed, they loaded up the tricycle cart and set off. The little boss lady was as excited as ever.

“Afternoon at Huaguo Mountain Park, over 200-300 tourists showed up. Instead of sightseeing, they just stood in line and bought out everything! Now they say they’re heading to the night market!”

“Shit! Even out-of-towners are joining the hype! Can the alley even fit more people?”

“They didn’t join the group, so they don’t know the exact time. We should go early—by the time they show up, all they’ll get is table scraps!”

“Great idea! What time should we go?”

“Lu Handsome said he’ll start around 8 PM. Let’s go at 7!”

“Shit! That early? What do we do for an hour?”

“It’s just an hour! One match of PUBG and it’ll be over!”

“I’m going at 6:30 to secure first place!”

“6:00 for me! I’ll be the REAL No.1!”

“WTF! You guys are INSANE!”

A narrow alley behind Spicy Girl Hotpot,

Most other food stalls were gone.

Because of Lu’s Snack Stall, other vendors had been driven away. Normally, food stalls competed fairly, surviving by quality and popularity. But Lu Qin’s stall was on a whole different level—a one-sided domination.

Setting up near him?

Pointless.

Not a single person even glanced at other stalls.

Their hearts, minds, and stomachs were fully committed to Lu’s food.

No exaggeration!

The few who tried to compete nearby?

They left in utter defeat.

At 7:00 PM, over 30 customers were already in line. Even the owner of Spicy Girl Hotpot was lining up.

______

At the end of the night market street, a new shop had opened—Liu’s Snack Shop. Red banners, giant flower arrangements, and a small red carpet decorated the entrance.

A large ‘Grand Opening: 20% Off’ sign attracted passersby. Inside, Master Liu stood with a confident smile.

“Don’t just stand there! Come inside!”

“Boss, what do you sell?”

“It’s all listed here! Take a look!”

The glass windows and door were covered with big photos and descriptions.

“Stir-fried rice noodles, Ice Jelly Cake, Twice-Cooked Pork, Crystal Shrimp Dumplings, Mung Bean Smoothie, Imperial Concubine Chicken Wings…”

“Whoa! That’s a lot of variety!”

“Doesn’t matter how much variety there is—what matters is whether it’s GOOD!”

“Hah! With that kind of confidence, I HAVE to try it!”

A few curious customers stepped inside, ordering food.

Master Liu started cooking—Stir-fried rice noodles, Twice-Cooked Pork, Ice Jelly Cake, Mapo Tofu…

The kitchen was hidden behind a curtain, where Master Liu had been studying Lu Qin’s food for days. He had eaten every dish multiple times, and combined modern food science tricks to replicate the taste.

“Wow! Boss, your stir-fried noodles are really good!”

“This Ice Jelly Cake is so sweet and refreshing!”

“The Twice-Cooked Pork is nice and spicy!”

Soon, the small shop filled with customers.

Master Liu grinned from ear to ear—business was booming. Meanwhile, Liu Weiwei—a chubby foodie was rushing to the night market after working overtime.

“Damn capitalists! Giving us extra work at the last minute! If I don’t get to eat Lu Qin’s food today, I’ll spray paint my boss’s house tomorrow!”

Riding his beloved scooter, he sped through the streets.

SCREECH—!

Right before reaching Spicy Girl Hotpot, he suddenly braked hard in front of Liu’s Snack Shop.

“Stir-fried rice noodles… Ice Jelly Cake… Twice-Cooked Pork… Mung Bean Smoothie…?”

“…Why do these dishes look so familiar?”

Then, he checked the prices.

Ice Jelly Cake: 24 yuan (Lu Qin: 25 yuan)

Twice-Cooked Pork: 27 yuan (Lu Qin: 28 yuan)]

“…Why are the dishes exactly the same?

And why is everything just 1 yuan cheaper than Lu Qin’s stall?”

A bad feeling crept over Liu Weiwei.

chapters.title: Chapter 151
chapters.content:
Chapter 151 Counterfeit Version of Lu Qin! A Wild Boar Can’t Appreciate Fine Bran!

The chubby boy, Zhou Weiwei, parked his little donkey and stepped into Liu Ji Snack Shop. The shop had just opened, offering a 20% discount on all items. Though the space was small, all eight tables were filled with customers.

“What would you like?”

Master Liu wiped the table with a greasy rag, haphazardly clearing away the mess left by previous customers before sweeping all the used meal boxes into the trash.

Zhou Weiwei frowned. The rag in Liu’s hand was visibly dirty, clearly used on multiple tables without being washed. After wiping the table, it left streaks of oil and water stains.

Being a clean freak, Zhou Weiwei had always avoided street food—until he discovered Lu Qin’s snack stall, which was exceptionally hygienic. It was the gold standard of cleanliness among street vendors.

So, the scene before him filled him with disgust.

But he couldn’t just walk away. He needed to figure out what was going on with this Liu Ji Snack Shop.

He sat down without touching the recently wiped table.

“One order of stir-fried rice noodles.”

“Just one order? You can try some of our other dishes. Our twice-cooked pork and crystal cake are also great,” Liu suggested.

Zhou Weiwei shook his head.

“Just the rice noodles.”

“Oh.”

Seeing that the chubby customer only ordered one dish, Liu’s attitude visibly cooled. He turned away and disappeared behind the curtain.

Zhou Weiwei took a deep breath. What kind of service was this? Order less and get treated poorly? How rude!

He took out a tissue to wipe the table himself. The tissue turned yellowish-brown from the grease. Disgusted, he tossed it into the overflowing trash can, which was surrounded by scattered napkins and meal boxes.

The hygiene here was atrocious! And this was supposed to be a grand opening? They couldn’t even use the excuse of being too busy—hiring an extra hand or simply emptying the trash bin wouldn’t take much effort.

Still, most street food customers weren’t too picky about cleanliness. A bit of dirt was just part of the experience. Soon, the stir-fried rice noodles arrived, steaming hot.

The aroma was slightly weaker than Lu Qin’s. The presentation was not as good either. A single lift with the chopsticks pulled up a massive clump of noodles. The ingredients were identical to Lu Qin’s—egg, diced ham, and lean pork. But the price was 12 yuan, one yuan cheaper than Lu Qin’s.

Zhou Weiwei was convinced this pricing was deliberate, meant to undercut Lu Qin. He took a bite and was instantly shocked.

It was incredibly similar—almost identical to Lu Qin’s!

As a die-hard Lu Qin fan and a moderator of Lu’s foodie group, Zhou Weiwei had been a loyal customer since Lu Qin’s first day of business. He knew Lu Qin’s flavors inside out. This Liu Ji version was very close in taste, but the texture was off. The balance of oil and seasoning wasn’t quite right—it lacked the same refreshing aftertaste. Still, the flavor was impressively replicated. A high-quality knockoff.

The ability to mimic Lu Qin’s taste so well was remarkable. No wonder the place was packed despite being new. It wasn’t just the discount. Even more suspiciously, the menu was identical to Lu Qin’s—clearly an attempt to steal customers.

Zhou Weiwei ordered a crystal cake next. Again, the taste was very similar, but the texture was lacking. Ordinary customers weren’t food critics. Most weren’t overly picky. As long as the food tasted good, minor differences in texture were tolerable. After all, this was street food, not fine dining. Who could afford to be so selective?

Without trying anything else, Zhou Weiwei could already guess—everything on the menu was just a slightly inferior copy of Lu Qin’s offerings.

This shop should’ve been called “Lu Qin Snack Shop (Counterfeit Version).”

After Zhou Weiwei left, other customers began noticing as well.

Someone posted in the Penguin chat group.

…

A wave of customers rushed to Liu Ji Snack Shop. Seeing the influx of people, Liu Ji’s boss grinned ear to ear. These must be customers stolen from Lu Qin!

His plan had worked—successfully diverting traffic from Lu Qin’s bustling stall. That place had way too much foot traffic…

“Come in, come in! We have air conditioning!”

Liu even grabbed extra stools to accommodate the growing crowd.

“Haha! I’m gonna get rich!”

Liu gleefully went back to frying noodles.

Meanwhile, long-time customers started discussing.

“The taste is pretty good! Similar to Lu Qin’s, just a little greasier.”

“I don’t mind. The line at Lu Qin is unbearable. Liu Ji is slightly worse but way more convenient!”

“It’s really close in flavor. I’m not picky!”

“This fried rice noodle dish tastes kind of strange… is it just me?”

“Maybe we’re just too used to Lu Qin’s version. The mung bean dessert feels off too, but I can’t pinpoint why.”

“Whatever, as long as it tastes good. Better than waiting in line forever.”

…

Back in Lu Qin’s foodie group, discussions heated up.

…

For true loyalists, Lu Qin’s food was flawless. But there was one big problem—those insanely long lines.

Many customers opted for Liu Ji instead. A knockoff was better than waiting. And clearly, Liu Ji’s owner was no amateur. His ability to replicate Lu Qin’s flavors meant he had serious cooking skills. So before Lu Qin’s stall even opened for the day, a third of his customers had already been lured away by Liu Ji.

chapters.title: Chapter 152
chapters.content:
Chapter 152 These Things Seem to Have Additives!

At 8:10 PM, Lu Qin’s food cart appeared in sight, and most of the customers reacted with excitement as usual.

“Boss Lu, you’re finally here! I’m starving!”

“The little boss is here too! She’s even cuter today! Here, have an apple!”

“I’ll help set up! I know how to do it already!”

Lu Xiyao, lively as ever, greeted the crowd, “Good evening, uncles, aunties, brothers, and sisters! You must be hungry—buy more later, okay~?”

“You really are the little boss! Even your greetings come with a sales pitch. Your dad makes so much money—has he bought you ice cream?”

At the mention of this, the little girl pouted pitifully.

“Nooo, I haven’t had ice cream in so long, I don’t even remember what it tastes like~!”

“Boss Lu, this is unacceptable! How could you not buy ice cream for such a cute little girl? That’s too much! If you don’t buy it, I will!”

The little girl’s eyes lit up instantly.

“Really?”

Lu Qin chuckled, knowing the customer was joking.

“She just had some two days ago. Kids always crave sweets and cold treats.”

“If you buy her one, she’ll dare to ask for a second. She’s quite bold.”

He ruffled the little girl’s hair affectionately.

The customers laughed.

How adorable!

And it made sense. How could Lu Qin refuse his precious daughter ice cream if she wanted it? Even these customers, who weren’t family, wanted to spoil her! Boss Lu was right, though—too much cold food wasn’t good for kids. Most customers happily chatted with the father and daughter. However, some looked concerned.

They couldn’t help but ask, “Boss Lu, have you noticed there are way fewer customers today?”

“Did you hear? A new shop called Liu Ji Snack Shop opened on the east side of the night market. They sell the same dishes as you—and worse, each one is priced one yuan cheaper. That’s clearly intentional!”

“Boss Lu, did someone steal your recipe?”

“Don’t worry, Boss Lu! I’ll always be a loyal customer—I’d never betray you. I’ll only eat your food for the rest of my life!”

“Me too!”

“Me too!”

“Liu Ji is just a cheap knockoff—no comparison!”

“Boss Lu, what you make is cuisine. What they make is just food.”

Some customers were very passionate, acting as if they were the ones losing business. But Lu Qin remained unfazed. With a business as popular as his, it was inevitable that someone would try to copy him.

He smiled and said, “It’s fine. I just need to focus on my own work. Competition is a good thing. We can’t stop others from doing business.”

“Look at that mindset! Boss Lu is truly admirable!”

“If it were me, I’d be cursing them out by now!”

“I don’t just love Boss Lu’s food—I respect him as a person. And I love the little boss, too! No way am I buying from Liu Ji.”

Chubby Zhou Weiwei snorted, “Liu Ji is straight-up plagiarism! And the nerve of him—undercutting Boss Lu by exactly one yuan per dish!”

“What’s really weird is how Liu Ji’s food tastes so similar to Boss Lu’s…”

“Enough about Liu Ji—let’s just order already! Boss Lu’s business is still booming!”

“If some people leave, that just means we don’t have to wait as long in line!”

Meanwhile, at Liu Ji Snack Shop…

“Boss, hurry up! Clean this up!”

“Coming, coming…”

Liu Ji carried two plates of stir-fried rice noodles to a couple. The girlfriend’s face instantly twisted in disgust—she had just seen Liu Ji’s untrimmed, dirty fingernail poke into the noodles.

She didn’t say anything aloud but felt deeply nauseated. Liu Ji moved to another table and wiped it down with an oily, unwashed rag.

“Dude, at least wash that rag—it’s disgusting!”

This table of customers had enough.

“I’m busy, okay? Here, I’ll use a napkin instead.”

He grabbed a tissue and haphazardly wiped the table. But the napkin turned yellow with grease.

The customers lost their appetite completely. Then they saw him using the same greasy hand to grab the noodles directly.

NOPE.

“We’re not eating here. This place is too unsanitary!”

Three customers got up and left without looking back. The couple also wanted to leave, but they had already ordered.

“I’m not eating this. The hygiene is horrible.”

“Let’s go, babe! Never coming here again.”

The boyfriend quickly paid, grabbed his girlfriend’s hand, and left.

“…Tch.”

Liu Ji realized two tables had left before he could stop them.

“Whatever. I don’t need you guys.”

Grumbling, he returned to the kitchen. He still had plenty of customers.

“I have to admit, Liu Ji’s food tastes similar to Boss Lu’s, but it’s just not the same—Lu Ji’s food is still better.”

“It’s just that Lu Ji’s line is too long. Liu Ji is the second-best choice.”

“It’s not even that clean. And it’s a new shop—there’s already rice noodles on the chairs!”

“Eh, whatever. Street food is all like this.”

“Boss Lu’s cart is spotless. He even wears a plastic mask, and his hands never touch the food directly.”

“Yeah, I trust his cleanliness. And I get to see the little boss—haven’t fed her snacks in two days, and I’m feeling withdrawal!”

At that moment, a massive group of tourists arrived.

“Where’s Lu Ji’s food stall?”

“I’m starving!”

“We missed it earlier—if I don’t get to try it now, I’ll go crazy!”

The tour guide checked their notes. “It should be down this narrow alley behind Spicy Girl Hotpot. Follow me!”

When the tourists reached the alley, they were shocked.

“So many people!”

“I knew it! This place must be amazing!”

“…Wait, why are some people leaving?”

“Hey, handsome! You’re not buying food?”

“Nah, there’s another place—Liu Ji’s Snack Shop. Their food tastes the same, and there’s no crazy long line. I’m gonna try that instead.”

“For real? If it’s the same, I’m going too! If we wait in line here and don’t get any, we’ll regret it!”

So, a bunch of tourists defected to Liu Ji’s stall.

Liu Ji was ecstatic. His gamble had paid off. Lu Qin had the long queue. Liu Ji had the short queue.

Liu Ji had never had this many customers before, not even during peak holiday seasons.

“This was genius! I’m a damn genius!”

Meanwhile, some tourists who bought Liu Ji’s food returned to compare.

“This is Liu Ji’s stir-fried noodles? Huh… why does it look worse?”

“It’s kinda oily. And slightly burnt. How’s the taste?”

“It’s good! Pretty solid.”

“Let me try a bite.”

People started comparing Liu Ji’s food with Lu Qin’s.

Some even brought their Liu Ji dishes to Lu Qin.

At first, Lu Qin just smiled, indifferent. But then, he looked closely—and his expression changed.

His eyebrows furrowed.

“These things…seem to have additives.”

“?”

chapters.title: Chapter 153
chapters.content:
Chapter 153 I Trust Boss Lu’s Integrity—He Has a Conscience!

“Additives?”

Customers who had bought food from Liu Ji froze, then quickly panicked.

“Boss Lu, don’t scare me like that!”

“No way… Are you serious? Damn!”

All eyes turned to Lu Qin.

He was dead serious—this was no joke. Next to him, little Lu Xiyao looked up, her big eyes blinking in confusion. She didn’t fully understand, but she got the gist.

The food from that other shop had bad stuff in it.

So scary!

She made up her mind—she’d never eat food from outside again. Only Daddy’s cooking was safe!

Lu Qin never cared much about competition. But food safety? That was another matter. He had to let everyone know the truth. Zhou Weiwei was the first to react, his chubby face turning red with anger.

“I knew there was something wrong with Liu Ji’s food! I was wondering how it tasted so close to Boss Lu’s—turns out, they cheated with technology!”

Some customers were skeptical.

“Boss Lu, do you have X-ray vision or something? How can you tell just by looking?”

“Come on, Boss Lu is a professional! Experts can spot things we regular folks can’t.”

Lu Qin had always been known as a reliable and honest businessman. Most people trusted him.

“Boss Lu, explain it to us! How did you figure this out?”

Curious, the customers gathered around. Lu Qin handed out three packed orders, turned off the stove, and took two minutes to explain.

“I didn’t even need to taste it. I could tell from the color and the smell.”

“For example, this stir-fried rice noodle dish…”

He picked up the portion from Liu Ji and separated the noodles with chopsticks.

“The color is darker than it should be. That’s not from soy sauce—it’s from an additive called Dichlorvos.”

“What’s that?”

Some customers had never heard of it.

“Dichlorvos is a chemical additive. When mixed into flour, it makes noodles appear whiter, improves texture, and extends shelf life.”

“Some unethical vendors use it to cut costs by buying cheap, chemically treated noodles and rice.”

The crowd’s expressions shifted from concern to outrage.

“Damn! Even stir-fried noodles aren’t safe!”

“These shady vendors will do anything for profit!”

“I can’t believe this! What the hell are we even eating?”

Someone quickly googled it.

“Holy sh*t! At temperatures above 60°C, Dichlorvos breaks down into harmful chemicals—formaldehyde, sulfur dioxide, hydrogen sulfide! It can cause headaches, fatigue, loss of appetite, and even nasopharyngeal cancer!”

The moment the word cancer came up, the customers freaked out. Especially those who had already eaten at Liu Ji—their faces turned pale.

“I’M CALLING THE POLICE!”

“That Liu Ji owner is a criminal! This is insane!”

Lu Qin took another sniff and continued analyzing.

“This stir-fried rice noodle dish also has artificial flavor enhancers—specifically, concentrated umami powder.”

“Normally, chefs use things like MSG and oyster sauce to enhance flavor, but no natural seasoning can make food this intensely flavorful.”

“The overpowering umami completely masks the taste of the rice noodles themselves.”

“Consuming too much of this stuff makes you thirsty.”

At this, someone slapped their thigh.

“Yes! I ate Liu Ji’s noodles earlier, and my throat got insanely dry—I drank a whole bottle of water, but it didn’t help!”

The anger in the crowd exploded.

“No wonder the food tasted too good to be true! That bastard dumped a ton of artificial enhancers!”

“I was wondering how his food was nearly as good as Boss Lu’s—turns out, it’s just a bunch of chemical tricks!”

Lu Qin glanced at the chili peppers in the dish and shook his head.

“The spiciness is too intense—they must’ve added capsaicin extract.”

“Wait, what?! Even the chili is fake!”

“This is next-level fraud! The Liu Ji owner deserves jail time!”

“I ate his stir-fried noodles, mung bean dessert, and twice-cooked pork—do I need to go to the hospital?”

“This is terrifying! I don’t think I’ll ever eat at street stalls again!”

“Boss Lu is the only one I trust. I saw him at the market once—he only buys fresh ingredients. He won’t even touch low-quality ones!”

“I believe in Boss Lu’s character—he has a conscience!”

“LET’S GO CONFRONT THAT SCUMBAG!”

“YEAH! DESTROY HIS SHOP!”

“Call food safety authorities—get this place shut down!”

A group of furious customers stormed out of the alley, heading straight for Liu Ji’s snack shop. Meanwhile, those who stayed loyal to Lu Qin’s stall stood tall, feeling like victors.

See? We told you so. Boss Lu’s name is golden for a reason!

But soon, the angry mob returned.

“Boss Lu, we need solid evidence—Liu Ji might deny everything if we just accuse him. How do we prove it?”

The place was packed—some customers had come straight from Lu Qin’s.

“Boss Lu’s twice-cooked pork is still better—this one’s too greasy. But whatever, it’s edible.”

“The crystal cake is nothing like Lu Qin’s—it lacks that unique richness. A knockoff will always be a knockoff.”

“It’s one yuan cheaper? Who do they think they’re fooling? If this tasted like Lu Qin’s, I’d gladly pay ten yuan more!”

“This owner has zero hygiene. He doesn’t even wear a mask! I just saw him smoking in the back!”

“The shop is full, though.”

“These people probably have never tried Lu Qin’s food. They think this is good—I can’t stop laughing!”

“I’m never coming back. My wife can settle for less—I won’t compromise on good food!”

____

10 minutes later, a mob of angry customers stormed Liu Ji’s shop. Two of them had their phones recording from their shirt pockets. Another had already called food safety authorities.

Catching a fraud needs hard proof. They had planned their strategy on the way over.

Step one: Obtain evidence.

Step two: Expose the fraud.

Four people entered first.

Liu Ji wiped his hands and came over.

“What’ll you have?”

“Two stir-fried rice noodles, two crystal cakes.”

“Also, we want a raw portion of rice noodles, to take home and cook ourselves.”

Seeing them pay first, Liu Ji didn’t suspect a thing. He bagged up the raw noodles and brought them out. The entire process was recorded.

The four customers nodded subtly—Step One: Success. Then, they signaled the rest of the group.

BOOM.

A wave of people flooded into the shop. Liu Ji’s gut twisted. He turned off the stove and stepped out.

“What’s going on? What do you want?”

Someone stepped forward.

“We’re here for JUSTICE.”

“We’re here to EXPOSE YOU.”

“We’re here to BLOW YOUR FRAUD WIDE OPEN!”

chapters.title: Chapter 154
chapters.content:
Chapter 154 Everything He Made Was Just ‘Technology and Tricks’!

“Counterfeit!”

“What counterfeit? I run an honest business! Did I offend you somehow?”

“Oh, I get it! Some other shop is jealous of my success and sent you to stir up trouble, huh?”

Liu Ji’s face darkened, his voice a mix of shock and anger.

“If you’re not buying, get out! Don’t cause trouble here!”

Inside the shop, other customers stopped eating and turned into spectators. This was gonna be good!

An entire mob had barged in—clearly, something big was going down!

“Honest business? If you’re an honest businessman, then there are no shady vendors in the world!”

“Everyone, stop eating! Everything here is full of chemical tricks!”

As soon as those words landed, all the customers froze, dropping their chopsticks. They stared hard at their plates—suddenly, the food didn’t look so appetizing.

One of them spoke hesitantly, “Now that you mention it… something does taste off. Boss, what exactly did you put in this?”

Liu Ji’s face darkened further. He clenched his fists, trying to suppress his panic.

How the hell did they find out?

The shop only just opened!

“I added nothing!”

“They’re slandering me!”

“Hey! You better watch what you say, or I’ll sue you for defamation!”

“You added nothing? Then why is there Dichlorvos in your noodles?”

“Maybe some of you don’t know what that is—it’s a chemical preservative. It stops rice noodles from spoiling, meaning the noodles you’re eating could be months old.”

“If you eat these noodles, best case, you’ll get diarrhea and vomiting. Worst case, you’ll end up in the ICU!”

At the mention of ICU, the customers lost it. Their faces turned pale. Some even threw their chopsticks onto the table. All eyes turned to Liu Ji.

“That’s a lie! My noodles are freshly bought and soaked every day!”

“I don’t even know what ‘Dichlorvos’ is!”

Liu Ji pointed at them defensively, his voice loud but shaky.

“You can’t lie your way out of this! Your food also has artificial umami powder and chili extract!”

“LIES! Do you have evidence?”

Liu Ji tried to keep his cool, but his heart was pounding.

How did they figure everything out?

How did they get my whole playbook?

“Of course we have evidence!”

“This bag of raw noodles was just bought from you.”

A customer placed the sealed package on the table. Then he pulled out another bag.

“And this one is from Boss Lu’s stall.”

Liu Ji’s eyes widened in shock.

“Oh, we’re not just talking—we’re doing a science experiment.”

Just then, a breathless customer rushed in.

“Got it—the Dichlorvos test kit!”

He slammed a small testing kit onto the table.

Liu Ji’s face turned white.

Oh no.

They were about to prove it. Phones popped up all over the shop.

Live recording mode: ON.

Two disposable cups were placed on the table. Both bags of noodles were cut into pieces and soaked in purified water. The countdown began.

Ten minutes later, a few drops from each cup were placed into Dichlorvos test tubes.

Click! Click! Click!

Phones zoomed in for a close-up. The test strips were compared against the color chart.

Truth: EXPOSED.

Boss Lu’s noodles? Color reading: 0.

Liu Ji’s noodles? Color reading: MAX (2000+).

“Holy sh*t! The scale only goes to 2000, and it’s maxed out!”

“Your noodles are off the charts with chemicals!”

“You sick bastard! You’re literally poisoning people!”

“People could get cancer from eating this sh*t!”

Panic spread through the customers. Some people clutched their stomachs in horror. Others started dry heaving.

“Someone call an ambulance!”

“Boss, pay for our medical bills!”

“You monster! GIVE US OUR MONEY BACK!”

“Call the police—get this guy arrested!”

Liu Ji’s hands trembled.

He tried to protest.

“You… you switched the samples! This isn’t my rice noodles!”

“Hah! We recorded everything.”

“The whole video is unedited. You handed this over yourself!”

Right at that moment—Government inspectors walked in.

Liu Ji’s soul left his body. He collapsed into his chair, defeated. The inspectors raided the kitchen. Within minutes, they uncovered everything.

An entire box was carried out—Illegal flavor enhancers, Chemical seasoning powders, Synthetic aroma boosters and the worst—opium husk powder.

That was right. He had been adding addictive substances to his food. The inspectors knew this was serious. This wasn’t just any street vendor—this was a night market hotspot.

Thousands of customers could be affected. They immediately alerted their superiors. Customers watched in horror.

“Oh my God… this much poison?”

“If I ate here often, I’d probably be dead in a few years!”

“Liu Ji used to sell noodles in the alleyway. When Boss Lu showed up, he got jealous. Instead of improving his skills, he used chemicals to copy the taste.”

“If you can’t beat someone in talent, you cheat?”

“Well, look where it got him now!”

“Thank God Boss Lu warned us—otherwise, we’d have kept eating this garbage!”

“I swear, from now on, I’m only eating at Lu Qin’s!”

“This whole market is shady! Inspectors, check every stall!”

The inspectors knew this wasn’t just about Liu Ji. They called for backup.

Within minutes, three white vans rolled in. A full investigation team stepped out. They started inspecting every single vendor—hotpot stalls, barbecue joints, snack stands.

The results?

Shocking.

Several other vendors were caught using illegal or excessive additives. Fines were issued on the spot. Even the legal food additives scared customers away. That night, many vendors swore off chemicals forever.

Some honest vendors—like the guy selling baked flatbread—put up signs.

“100% Natural—No Additives!”

They saw an opportunity to win over customers.

And Then, They Reached… Lu Qin’s Stall

“Whoa… this place is packed!”

“Damn, it’s clean.”

“I’ve checked so many places—this is the only stall that looks trustworthy.”

The inspectors checked the ingredients.

Lu Qin cooperated fully.

“These veggies are super fresh!”

“Bought them this afternoon—I never use overnight stock.”

The inspector nodded in approval. Then he clapped his hands.

“Hey, all you vendors, get over here!”

“THIS is how you do business!”

“Look at this place—fresh ingredients, immaculate kitchen, and not a single fly!”

“Learn from him!”

“This is the gold standard of street food.”

“I’m giving this stall a 120/100.”

chapters.title: Chapter 155
chapters.content:
Chapter 155 Five Shining Words: “Our Competitor Is a Fool!”

Lu Qin’s snack stall was the cleanest the inspectors had ever seen in their entire careers.

His personal hygiene habits were impeccable—every detail meticulously handled.

Organized workstation.

Fresh ingredients sorted properly.

Separate areas for raw and cooked food.

Clean, insulated storage boxes.

Disposable gloves for handling food.

Anti-spit face shield.

The lead inspector called over all the other vendors.

“Take notes, people! This is how it’s done!”

Meanwhile, Lu Qin’s loyal customers beamed with pride. They felt like they were part of something great. Usually, when inspectors checked food vendors, they always found problems.

But tonight? Every other vendor on Night Market Street had some issue or another except Lu Qin. Not only was everything perfect, but they gave him a record-breaking 120/100 score!

A rare moment in history!

“It’s Not Just the Hygiene—It’s the Taste!”

One inspector commented, “No wonder so many people line up here! It’s so clean, you can eat with peace of mind.”

A customer chuckled, “Inspector, you got it wrong. People don’t line up here because it’s clean—they line up because it’s delicious!”

“Oh? Is that so?”

At that moment, the tiny boss made her move.

“Uncle, my daddy’s stir-fried rice noodles are the best. Want to try some?”

The inspector looked down and found himself staring at the cutest little girl ever. His heart melted.

“Well, our inspection is done. Guess it’s time for a midnight snack.”

And just like that—a whole squad of food safety inspectors lined up at Lu Qin’s stall.

The other vendors? Sweating bullets.

When had anyone ever seen government inspectors eating at a street stall?

The moment Lu Qin’s wok fired up, the aroma exploded in the air. The inspectors gulped—it smelled insanely good.

As they waited, little Lu Xiyao ran up, holding several cups of iced mung bean soup.

“Uncles, you must be thirsty! Have some mung bean soup—it’s on the house!”

Her round little face, big blinking eyes, and innocent kindness made everyone smile.

What a smart kid!

She was adorable, but also knew how to do business. Even grown-ups couldn’t resist her charm. The inspectors ruffled her hair, laughing.

Of course, they didn’t take it for free—they insisted on paying.

One sip.

Eyes lit up. The natural sweetness, the fine, smooth texture, the refreshing coolness—It was perfection.

One cup and the summer heat was gone.

“This is amazing!”

“How is this mung bean soup so delicious?”

Then, their stir-fried rice noodles were ready. Plates were served, plastic stools were brought over. The inspectors sat down—no need for formalities.

One bite.

BOOM .

The soft yet chewy rice noodles, the fresh vegetables, the tender ham bits—It was an explosion of flavor. The inspectors ate faster and faster. The surrounding customers smirked knowingly. This reaction was normal.

Even a delicate noblewoman would devour Lu Qin’s food like a starving beast. After finishing their noodles, the inspectors bought even more food—Crystal cakes, shrimp dumplings, imperial chicken wings, and even milk tea. They couldn’t stop.

“Boss Lu, your food is too good. We’ll be back for more!”

“Are you here every night?”

“Clean, delicious, and safe—no wonder business is booming! Hope you get even more customers!”

Before leaving, the inspectors patted little Lu Xiyao’s head one last time. Then, they boarded their official vehicle and left.

The lines re-formed at Lu Qin’s stall.

But every other vendor?

Some were shut down for violations.

Some were self-inspecting, scrambling to fix issues.

That night, Night Market Street underwent a major transformation.

Hygiene standards rose.

Expired food was thrown out.

Every vendor checked their health permits.

Why?

Because they heard the inspectors say they’d be back tomorrow for more of Lu Qin’s stir-fried rice noodles. So now, everyone took food safety seriously. Not just for show—but for real.

As for Liu Ji’s fake snack shop?

Closed for good. Liu Ji was taken away by the authorities. He was heavily fined—there was no escape. And to make things worse, the entire incident went viral online.

With video proof, the government had no choice but to severely punish him. This time, public outrage wouldn’t let it slide.

The Biggest Winner?

Lu Qin’s Snack Stall, getting praised by the government meant everything.

It sent a clear message.

“Boss Lu is honest and trustworthy.”

Food safety? Guaranteed.

Quality? Best in the city.

The inspectors themselves became loyal customers.

And that was the best free advertisement ever. Lu Qin’s business was already number one.

Now?

Even more customers. Liu Ji tried to steal his success, but instead, handed it over on a silver platter.

Five Words to Summarize the Situation?

“Our Competitor Is a Fool.”

_____

At 9:30 PM—Sold Out. Tonight’s huge wave of tourists had completely blocked the alley. And just like in the daytime, many people left empty-handed. Some split one plate of noodles between three people—just for a taste which only made them crave more.

“I’m not leaving. I’m coming back tomorrow for more!”

“Me too! If I leave, I won’t be able to eat this again!”

“I take back what I said—this city is a food paradise!”

The tour guide sighed.

“We have other places to go tomorrow. If you skip them, there’s no refund.”

“DON’T CARE.”

_______

“Yao Yao, time to go home. It’s late.”

“Okay~!”

The little girl’s arms were full of snacks and fruit, barely able to carry them all. Lu Qin chuckled, packing them into a bag.

Customers loved the little boss—giving her food had become a daily ritual. Of course, Lu Qin always told them not to go overboard.

But customers insisted.

“Boss Lu, your food is too good. If we can’t tip, at least let us give gifts!”

Lu Qin could only smile helplessly. At least, running the stall was great training for Yao Yao—She learned social skills and had fun doing it.

When they got home, she immediately crashed into bed. Lu Qin cleaned up, showered, and checked his bank balance.

610,000 yuan in less than half a month. That was insane. But it was also exhausting. If not for his system’s fatigue-reduction bonus, his body would have collapsed by now.He glanced at his system points. 4,128 points accumulated. He decided time to roll for rewards.

chapters.title: Chapter 156
chapters.content:
Chapter 156 Max-Level Sichuan Cuisine Skills! Assistant Xiaomi!

Lu Qin twitched the corner of his mouth. Was this advanced lottery such a scam?

Draw again.

Lu Qin’s heart skipped a beat!

Max-level Sichuan cuisine?

All Sichuan cuisine?

In an instant, countless top-tier Sichuan cooking techniques flooded his mind, spreading through his entire body.

Douhua Beef, Dongpo Pork Knuckle, Kung Pao Shrimp Balls, Garlic Pork, Husband and Wife Lung Slices, Camphor Tea Duck…

A culmination of a thousand years of Sichuan culinary mastery—each dish with its own unique flavor, a hundred dishes with a hundred different tastes. Three grains of Sichuan pepper, five layers of flavor balance, seven combinations of eight delicacies.

Lu Qin could now masterfully wield the twenty-four flavors and cook any Sichuan dish!

Authentic!

Top-tier!

Traditional!

Lu Qin was instantly wide awake. The advanced lottery had finally paid off!

With the entire Sichuan cuisine repertoire consisting of over a thousand dishes, Lu Qin had now mastered them all. The knowledge infusion alone took a full ten minutes.

If he had relied on normal lottery draws, it would have taken him decades to acquire all these max-level skills. Probability was truly unpredictable. He still had two more advanced draws.

Draw again!

With an increase of just 1 strength attribute point, Lu Qin could already feel the power in his punches.

Draw again!

“Assistant?”

Lu Qin was puzzled and checked the system space.

Inside, there were 12 【Sunny Day Cards】, a 【Mint Candy Gift Pack】, and… a doll?

A palm-sized doll in the shape of a young girl lay in the system space. Lu Qin examined the system’s description of this doll.

【Assistant ‘Xiaomi’: Upon use, you will permanently obtain a female assistant. She possesses qualities of sincerity, kindness, and diligence. Her purpose is to assist you in cooking. She will follow your instructions as long as they do not violate ethical principles. She has a comprehension level of 100, possesses basic life skills, is fluent in Mandarin and English, and has normal adult human physiological functions. She is immune to diseases and has a theoretically infinite lifespan.】

Infinite lifespan?

Lu Qin raised an eyebrow.

Could she outlive him?

With a thought, Lu Qin activated the ‘Xiaomi’ doll.

In the room, a beam of light shone on the floor at the foot of the bed, and a young girl suddenly appeared. She looked to be in her early twenties, about 1.6 meters tall, with well-defined facial features, tanned skin, and shoulder-length black hair tied into a simple ponytail, giving off a capable and hardworking vibe.

“Hello, Boss. Xiaomi is here to serve you permanently!”

Xiaomi bowed and greeted Lu Qin.

She even lowered her voice, as if she already knew the little one next door was fast asleep.

“Hello, Xiaomi. Do you have an ID card?”

Xiaomi seemed to understand Lu Qin’s intent. She took out a phone and a lady’s wallet from her pocket. Inside the wallet, there was an ID card and two bank cards.

“Boss, rest assured, Xiaomi’s identity is officially registered.”

“Let’s exchange phone numbers and WeChat. I’ll transfer 10,000 yuan to you. Tonight, find a hotel to stay in, and tomorrow, rent an apartment nearby.”

“Understood, Boss. Please give me your phone, I’ll handle it.”

Lu Qin unlocked his phone and handed it to Xiaomi. She saved her number in his contacts and added his number to her phone. They also exchanged WeChat contacts.

“Boss, transferring 10,000 yuan. Please enter your password.”

Xiaomi handed the phone back to Lu Qin. Lu Qin entered his password, and the money was transferred.

“Boss, I won’t disturb your rest. I’ll go find a budget hotel for the night. What time should I come tomorrow?” Xiaomi asked respectfully.

“Come at 5 a.m. Oh, by the way, do you know any self-defense techniques?”

“No.”

Xiaomi shook her head.

“Never mind, I’ll drive you to a nearby hotel.”

It was past 10 p.m. Although the area had good public security, to be safe, Lu Qin decided to drive her himself—after all, she was a young woman.

“Thank you, Boss.”

The night outside was serene, and the residential area was mostly empty. Lu Qin started the car, and Xiaomi sat in the back seat.

Lu Qin glanced at her and asked, “Why aren’t you sitting in the front?”

Xiaomi smiled and replied, “Boss, the front passenger seat is reserved for the lady of the house.”

Lu Qin hadn’t expected Xiaomi to not only have basic life skills but also understand social etiquette. He dropped her off at a ‘Good Luck’ budget hotel.

Xiaomi entered the hotel, took out her ID card, and checked in. Before going upstairs, she waved at Lu Qin, who was still sitting in the car, then took the elevator. Lu Qin started his car and headed home to sleep. This advanced lottery was definitely worthwhile!

______

The next morning, at 5 a.m., Xiaomi arrived punctually at Lu Qin’s door. Grandpa Ge and Chen Dahai saw Xiaomi and exchanged glances, full of curiosity.

“That young girl is looking for Lu Qin?”

“Could she be his girlfriend?”

Chen Dahai frowned, “Lu Qin has such good qualities. Of course, women would flock to him!”

Grandpa Ge chuckled, “Coming so early? Are relationships this competitive nowadays?”

Everyone in the community knew that Lu Qin was divorced and raising a child. They had also seen his ex-wife, Shen Muyan—an exceptionally beautiful and elegant woman, who was also a CEO.

His ex-wife often came by to visit. Whether it was just to see their daughter or for other reasons, the neighbors speculated that a reunion was very possible. They looked compatible, after all.

But this young girl? She didn’t quite match up. Compared to his ex-wife, she was far from being on the same level. Sure enough, Lu Qin clarified that Xiaomi was an assistant he hired because he was too busy.

Chen Dahai heaved a sigh of relief and joked, “This young lady seems quite diligent, showing up so early for work.”

Xiaomi smiled politely and greeted Grandpa Ge and Chen Dahai. Lu Qin checked the time and didn’t delay. He took Xiaomi to the market to buy ingredients.

At the market, Lu Qin introduced her to all the vendors. In the future, he planned to let Xiaomi handle the shopping, saving him time to cook more dishes.

He also taught her how to select the best ingredients. For example, fresh pork should be bright red, as it means it was freshly slaughtered. Cucumbers with more spines are fresher and juicier.

Pinkish tomatoes are better for eating raw, while deep red ones are better for cooking. He also explained market pricing trends and price fluctuations. This was when Xiaomi’s 100% comprehension ability shone.

She grasped everything instantly and could even infer additional knowledge on her own. Lu Qin nodded in satisfaction. They bought a large amount of food, carrying multiple bags. Despite her seemingly slender arms, Xiaomi was incredibly strong. She efficiently loaded everything into the trunk in two trips.

Lu Qin didn’t lift a finger—he wanted to test her abilities. Without a doubt, Xiaomi’s strength and efficiency rivaled that of a young, fit man.

chapters.title: Chapter 157
chapters.content:
Chapter 157 Xiaomi, the Smart and Capable Assistant!

“Xiaomi Sis, buy it secretly, and Daddy won’t find out!”

After buying all the ingredients with Xiaomi, Lu Qin drove back home.

“Boss, I’ll start washing the vegetables. If you have any other instructions, just call me.”

As soon as she got out of the car, Xiaomi opened the trunk, grabbed several large bags with both hands, and carried them inside.

Since Xiaomi was specifically hired to assist Lu Qin in cooking, she didn’t need constant instructions—she knew what needed to be done and took the initiative.

Lu Qin stroked his chin and wondered, If I manage to draw two more assistants, would I only need to supervise while they handle everything?

Walking into the kitchen, he saw Xiaomi skillfully washing and preparing vegetables. He observed for a while and noticed that she cleaned the vegetables thoroughly with minimal waste and turned off the tap promptly to avoid water wastage.

Lu Qin nodded in satisfaction—Xiaomi was undoubtedly the most competent assistant.

“Xiaomi, after you finish washing the vegetables, rinse the 30 pounds of mung beans in the corner three times. The sink in the kitchen is too small, so wash them outside in the small courtyard.”

“Also, take this pork outside and use the stove fire to sear the skin. The unpleasant smell in pork mainly comes from the sweat glands in the skin. Stir-frying alone isn’t enough to remove it, but searing it will add a nice charred aroma. Afterward, scrape off the burnt layer with a knife.”

“Understood, Boss.”

Lu Qin started kneading dough in the kitchen. Once Xiaomi finished the assigned tasks, Lu Qin checked the time and estimated that she was only slightly slower than if he had done it himself.

Satisfied, he began teaching Xiaomi how to make mung bean paste.

“You can’t just throw the mung beans straight into the pot to cook. First, soak them in hot water for 10 minutes, then freeze them before boiling. That way, they will break down into a smooth paste easily. Add five slices of ginger to reduce dampness and coldness, and some dried lily bulbs…”

“Got it, Boss.”

Xiaomi nodded, her clear eyes filled with intelligence. Lu Qin left her in charge of the mung bean paste and went to let the dough rest while blanching the pork.

By the time the pork was blanched, Xiaomi had already started simmering the mung bean paste. With her 100% comprehension, Xiaomi realized that freezing the mung bean paste would take three to four hours, so she used ice cubes from the freezer and chilled it in ice water, cutting the process down to just a few minutes.

While the mung bean paste was cooking, Lu Qin taught Xiaomi how to chop ingredients, knead dough, and make fillings. Xiaomi picked up the skills quickly.

In a little over two hours, the two of them had made over 300 portions of ice crystal cake, more than 350 pounds of mung bean paste, and over 1,200 crystal shrimp dumplings…

The total amount of food produced was nearly 50% more than when Lu Qin worked alone.

“Xiaomi, how much do you eat?”

“Boss, I eat like an average girl.”

Lu Qin nodded. He thought she might have a huge appetite since she worked so efficiently. He prepared breakfast, adding an extra portion for Xiaomi.

Xiaomi helped out voluntarily.

“Xiaomi, fry an extra egg and heat three cups of milk. I’m going to wake up my daughter.”

“Understood.”

Xiaomi took the spatula.

Lu Qin gently pushed open his daughter’s bedroom door. The little one was sprawled out on the bed in all directions, her blanket kicked onto the floor.

Lu Qin called her name twice, but she didn’t respond. He nudged her gently, and she just turned over and continued sleeping.

“Yao Yao, wake up. There’s ice cream for breakfast.”

“Ice cream?”

The little one squinted as she sat up, only to see Lu Qin looking at her with a half-smile.

She pouted her chubby face and mumbled, “Daddy, this time I really heard it. I’m not dreaming!”

“I want ice cream!”

“Alright, after kindergarten today, Daddy will buy you some.”

Lu Qin picked her up from the bed.

Just as Lu Xiyao was about to cheer, she suddenly realized something was off.

“Daddy, today is an ice cream day anyway! It’s Tuesday, Thursday, and Saturday!”

Lu Qin blinked, “Yeah, I never said you couldn’t have it.”

“Uh…”

Her little brain buzzed in confusion as Lu Qin pushed her into the bathroom to wash up. As she brushed her teeth, she pondered, It’s really hard to get an extra ice cream out of Daddy!

After washing up, Lu Xiyao ran into the dining room, only to find an unfamiliar young lady there. She looked Xiaomi up and down with a puzzled expression.

“Huh?”

“Sis, who are you?”

“Hello, Yao Yao. I’m Xiaomi, your dad’s new assistant.”

Xiaomi was very polite to the little one. Lu Qin walked out of the kitchen and patted his daughter’s head.

“Yao Yao, this is Xiaomi. Daddy was too busy, so I hired her to help me.”

“Oh, hello, Xiaomi Sis.”

For some reason, Yao Yao felt an instant sense of familiarity with her.

“Daddy, does having Xiaomi Sis help you a lot?”

“Of course!”

Lu Qin laughed.

“Xiaomi Sis, work hard to help Daddy. Daddy works very hard. If you do a good job, I’ll tell Daddy to give you a raise!”

She spoke like a little boss, sounding mature beyond her years. Lu Qin chuckled, rubbing her chubby cheeks.

“Understood, Miss Yao Yao. I’ll do my best to assist the boss.”

Ding!

“The milk is heated. Boss, Miss Yao Yao, please take your seats, I’ll bring the milk over.”

Watching Xiaomi’s back, Yao Yao said in her soft voice, “Daddy, Xiaomi Sis is really capable. Not bad!”

“Do you like her?”

“Yes~!”

Lu Qin finished breakfast first and began loading food into the car. Now that he had Xiaomi, she could drive the three-wheeled food cart while he drove the car.

Both vehicles could carry plenty of food. Xiaomi helped Yao Yao break apart her toast. Yao Yao’s big eyes darted around, and she suddenly smiled sweetly at Xiaomi.

“Xiaomi Sis, you’re so nice.”

“Miss Yao Yao, it’s my duty.”

“Then… could you buy me ice cream sometimes? Don’t worry, I won’t eat it for free. I’ll tell Daddy to give you a raise.”

Yao Yao felt so clever—now she could eat as much ice cream as she wanted!

Xiaomi looked easygoing and nice.

“No, Miss Yao Yao.”

Xiaomi refused immediately.

Yao Yao: “…”

“Xiaomi Sis, buy it secretly. Daddy won’t find out.”

“No.”

“Just one time, okay? Never again after that.”

“Miss Yao Yao, if you keep asking me to buy ice cream, I’ll tell the boss.”

“……”

“Xiaomi Sis, I don’t like you as much anymore.”

…

After finishing breakfast, Xiaomi washed the dishes while Lu Qin packed lunch for Yao Yao. Lu Qin drove the car while Xiaomi rode the three-wheeled cart.

At the entrance of Xingguang Kindergarten.

“Daddy, be on time to pick me up in the evening!”

Thinking about her ice cream, Yao Yao’s eyebrows were full of excitement.

“Okay, be good in kindergarten.”

“Got it~!”

She kissed Lu Qin on the cheek, waved her little hand, and ran into the kindergarten. Lu Qin then drove towards Sunshine Primary School.

chapters.title: Chapter 158
chapters.content:
Chapter 158 Record-Breaking Income! New Dish: Pickled Chili Chicken Feet!

At the entrance of Sunshine Primary School, a long line stretched from across the street all the way to the school’s front gate. The school’s security guards and teachers, after welcoming the students, also joined the end of the long queue.

There was still some time before classes started, and the teachers silently prayed they’d have enough time to buy breakfast from Boss Lu. The line was ridiculously long.

Boss Lu hadn’t even arrived yet, and people were already queueing up!

It was seriously competitive!

Three minutes later, a three-wheeled food cart turned the corner and drove down the street. The customers, upon seeing the cart, got excited—stomachs rumbling, cravings intensifying.

If they had drums and gongs, they’d probably be banging them in celebration, welcoming the cart like a festival parade. A young girl got out of the food cart, causing the crowd to murmur in confusion.

“Huh? Who are you? Where’s Boss Lu?”

“Hello everyone, I’m Xiaomi, Boss Lu’s new assistant.”

“Boss Lu is dropping his daughter off at kindergarten and will be here shortly.”

Xiaomi parked the cart and started setting up.

Some customers stepped forward to help.

“Boss Lu finally hired someone! That’s great!”

“Xiaomi, take good care of Boss Lu, he works so hard!”

The customers all cared about Lu Qin. Running a food stall while taking care of a child was exhausting, and demand for his food was overwhelming.

Even a workhorse wouldn’t be pushed this hard.

Xiaomi nodded.

“Yes, my job is to assist Boss Lu.”

“For now, you can buy ice crystal cakes, crystal shrimp dumplings, and mung bean paste.”

“Stir-fried rice noodles will be available once Boss arrives.”

Xiaomi pulled out three large insulated containers and two fresh-lock storage boxes. A strong young customer helped her unload them.

“Wow! There’s so much stock today! Having an assistant really makes a difference!”

“Haha! I think Boss Lu should hire even more people! Expand production!”

Xiaomi hung up the small whiteboard that Lu Qin had prepared. It had the food prices written in black marker, with some minor adjustments to purchase limits.

Ice crystal cakes: max 2 boxes per person

Crystal shrimp dumplings: max 2 servings per person

Mung bean paste: max 5 cups per person]

The customers, after seeing the whiteboard, cheered.

“Five servings of rice noodles? Awesome! There are five people in my family, just right! And I can get mung bean paste too!”

“Two servings of crystal shrimp dumplings? Perfect! One was never enough!”

“I’ll take three cups of mung bean paste, two boxes of ice crystal cakes, and two servings of crystal shrimp dumplings—I’m starving!”

“I’m maxing out on everything!”

“Me too, getting the full limit!”

…

Xiaomi worked quickly, serving over 30 customers in just six or seven minutes. She also carefully checked each customer’s payment records.

At this moment, Lu Qin arrived by car. The roadside parking spaces were all taken.

“Boss Lu, park here!”

The school’s security guard enthusiastically moved a tricycle onto the curb, creating an empty parking spot.

“This is the school canteen’s vegetable delivery tricycle. It’s fine to move it.”

The security guard laughed as he explained.

“Thanks.”

“No problem, Boss Lu, just keep selling your food here every day.”

“Ever since I started eating your food, I can’t go back to anything else.”

Lu Qin smiled, skillfully maneuvered the car, and parked in one smooth motion. He got out and walked to the food cart.

Xiaomi stepped aside.

“Boss, should we split the sales?”

“Yes, you handle those three items, and I’ll do the stir-fried rice noodles.”

As soon as Lu Qin tied on his apron, a group of customers immediately lined up. Their speed was even faster than students forming ranks during military training.

“Boss Lu, can we really buy five servings of stir-fried rice noodles? That’s amazing! I’ll take five!”

“Me too!”

…

This time, Lu Qin had prepared over 150 pounds of fresh rice noodles, each pound making about four to five servings. The portions were generous. Meanwhile, Fang Ya arrived at Sunshine Primary School, dark circles under her eyes, looking exhausted.

She had stayed up all night playing a battle strategy game, planning to sleep all day. But before that, she needed a good meal. She intended to skip the queue but suddenly noticed a young girl standing next to Lu Qin.

Immediately, she felt like she had stumbled upon something interesting. Fang Ya paused, joined the line, and pulled out her phone to message Shen Muyan.

“Your Boss Lu might be up to something. Yellow alert.”

On the other end, Shen Muyan had just woken up. Wearing a light silk nightgown, she walked elegantly to the bathroom, her figure curvy and graceful.

As she brushed her teeth, she scrolled through work emails on her phone. Just then, Fang Ya’s message popped up.

At this hour, Fang Ya was usually still asleep. She opened the message, raised an eyebrow, and replied with a simple: [?] [Fang Ya: Let me investigate first.]

Now fully awake, Fang Ya sneakily observed from the line. Watching their interactions, the girl seemed to be an employee.

Did Lu’s Snack Stall finally hire staff?

Fang Ya had always trusted Lu Qin’s character. However, Shen Muyan’s man was too outstanding, and as the saying goes, it’s not the thief you fear but the one who keeps thinking about stealing.

After observing for a while, Fang Ya noticed that Xiaomi only looked at Lu Qin with respect—nothing else. False alarm, situation under control.

Hiring an employee was actually a good thing—it would ease Lu Qin’s workload. Shen Muyan, still waiting for an update, was about to call when Fang Ya sent another message.

Shen Muyan examined the picture.

The young girl had tanned skin and was barely passable in looks—certainly not a beauty.

No threat at all.

She didn’t give it another thought, wiped her face, and replied, “Bring me a serving of stir-fried noodles and crystal shrimp dumplings.”

Fang Ya: “OK.”

…

At 9:40 AM, the breakfast rush ended. Today’s sales hit a record high: 24,745.50 yuan. System points accumulated to 2,788.

After packing up, Lu Qin had Xiaomi ride the tricycle back home and gave her the rest of the day to find an apartment and buy clothes and daily necessities. He had transferred 10,000 yuan to her yesterday. Rent in the old district was cheap, so it should be more than enough.

Lu Qin, on the other hand, headed to the vehicle dealership. The four-wheeled mobile food truck should be ready for pickup. When he arrived, the dealership had already parked the custom electric food truck at the entrance. It was exactly the one he had ordered.

After parking his car, Lu Qin walked over and inspected the food truck for a few minutes. The materials were solid, and the quality was excellent. The dealership owner saw Lu Qin checking the truck and approached with a smile.

“Boss, you’re here? Everything look good? No worries—if you have any issues after taking it back, just let us know.”

“Yeah, the truck is fine. Can you deliver it to this address?”

“Of course, we offer home delivery!”

The dealership owner quickly agreed and noted down the address. Lu Qin paid the remaining balance.

The dealership owner then asked, “Boss, are you selling your old tricycle?”

“No, I’ll still need it.”

Now that Xiaomi was around, she could use it for transportation or for grocery shopping.

On the way back, Lu Qin stopped by the market and bought ingredients for pickled vegetables. Old Zhao also gifted him five extra jars, so he decided to fill them all. He also bought 30 pounds of chicken feet.

A new dish—Pickled Chili Chicken Feet—was coming soon!

chapters.title: Chapter 159
chapters.content:
Chapter 159 What If There’s Not Enough Stomach Space? Can I Install an Extra One?

Lu Qin returned home and started simmering the mung bean paste while kneading 10 pounds of dough to let it rest. Next, he began preparing Pickled Chili Chicken Feet (Pao Jiao Feng Zhua).

The process was actually quite simple. He cut along the backs of the chicken feet, brought a pot of water to a boil, and then added the chicken feet, skimming off the foam. Boiling for 24 minutes, he then transferred them into ice water to cool completely before deboning them.

At this stage, they couldn’t be pickled immediately. Instead, he kneaded them with flour first—this step helped remove excess grease from the surface. In a glass jar, he added purified water, salt, sugar, vinegar, Sichuan peppercorns, and other seasonings.

The key technique of Pickled Chili Chicken Feet was the precise ratio of ingredients. With max-level mastery, Lu Qin could measure each component down to the gram, ensuring the perfect flavor and texture.

Normally, Pickled Chili Chicken Feet needed to be refrigerated for three days. But, just like with his pickled vegetables, the system conveniently provided a pickling accelerator. Sprinkling in a mysterious gray powder, the chicken feet would be ready in just two hours.

Just as Lu Qin finished preparing them, Xiaomi rode back on the three-wheeled food cart. Checking the time—she had only been gone for less than an hour.

“Xiaomi, did you rent the house? Got all your essential supplies?”

“Boss, everything is done.”

Lu Qin nodded. Xiaomi was incredibly efficient. As soon as she finished her personal errands, she rushed back to work. Xiaomi poured herself a glass of water and handed one to Lu Qin as well. Then, she showed him the rental contract and shopping receipts.

Renting a place in the old district was easy since many people had moved to the city center.

Xiaomi rented a one-bedroom small house for 1,000 yuan per month. She showed pictures—it was clean, tidy, and comfortable for a single person.

She also bought three sets of clothes and three pairs of shoes from a budget store near the supermarket, spending less than 200 yuan per outfit, including shoes. Her bank balance still had over 8,000 yuan, but since this money technically belonged to Lu Qin, she wouldn’t spend it recklessly.

After finishing her drink, Xiaomi helped Lu Qin with work. He began teaching her how to make Ice Crystal Cakes. With 100% comprehension ability, one lesson was enough for her to master it.

And he didn’t have to worry about ‘teaching an apprentice and starving the master’—Xiaomi was his loyal assistant.

“Xiaomi, next, wrap the crystal shrimp dumplings. I’ll steam them when I get back. Also, blanch the pork hind leg meat, slice it, and store it in the insulated fresh-lock box.”

“I’m heading out for a bit.”

“Understood, Boss.”

Lu Qin drove to an advertising shop to make three name tags.

【Boss】 (“Boss, the real one”)

【Employee Xiaomi】

【Little Boss Lady】

Big companies have corporate culture—why not his food stall? A little humor made it more fun.

He also ordered matching uniforms in dark brown, with ‘Lu’s Snack Stall’ embroidered on the left chest. From now on, whenever they set up shop, they would wear matching outfits—creating a professional and unified look.

For takeout packaging, he didn’t need custom containers, but he ordered plastic bags and drink sealing films printed with ‘Lu’s Snack Stall’.

The total cost at the advertising shop was 700+ yuan.

Lu Qin also added the shop owner on WeChat since he’d need a steady supply of branded packaging in the future. After some thought, he ordered another name tag.

【Boss Lady】

Before leaving, he also bought 20 meters of warm-colored LED strip lights. These would decorate the new food truck, making it stand out at night markets and attract even more customers.

(Of course, Lu’s Snack Stall didn’t really need help attracting customers.)

When Lu Qin got home, Xiaomi was wrapping crystal shrimp dumplings—600+ dumplings already filled the table. She had also finished all the other tasks he had assigned—her efficiency was top-notch.

She even cut and packaged the pickled vegetables—400+ neatly packed portions stored in white plastic containers. Lu Qin was very satisfied. Xiaomi’s presence saved him a ton of work.

Together, they steamed the crystal shrimp dumplings, with Lu Qin explaining key steaming techniques to avoid mistakes.

“Leave at least 2cm space between dumplings to prevent sticking.”

“Once the water boils, switch to medium heat. Since we made 30g dumplings, the optimal steaming time is about six minutes.”

Xiaomi nodded, memorizing everything.

After steaming 500+ dumplings, production had

skyrocketed compared to when Lu Qin worked alone.

At 2:30 PM, Lu Qin told Xiaomi to pack everything up, then went to the courtyard to remove the canopy from the three-wheeled food cart. From now on, the tricycle would be Xiaomi’s personal vehicle for commuting and grocery shopping.

With a 110km battery range, it was more than enough for daily needs. At this moment, the new mobile food truck arrived. Lu Qin opened both sides of the iron courtyard gate. Once the large food truck was driven in, the courtyard instantly felt cramped.

After receiving the invoice, he carefully inspected the vehicle—everything was in perfect condition. He then installed the LED strip lights—by afternoon, Lu’s Snack Stall’s upgraded food truck was officially ready for business.

At 3:15 PM – Tianhong Plaza

When the new food truck rolled into Tianhong Plaza, customers were in awe.

“Boss Lu got an upgrade! Awesome! The business is growing!”

“Bigger is better! Now we can buy more food!”

“【Boss】? Hahaha, that’s hilarious!”
