“Boss Lu, can customers get name tags too? I want one that says ‘Proud Lu’s Snack Stall Customer’!”

The food truck had openable sliding glass windows, which could be set up as two or three serving windows. Lu Qin stir-fried noodles on the left, while Xiaomi handled sales on the right.

“Boss Lu! You’re selling Pickled Chili Chicken Feet now? Amazing!”

“Insane! You release new dishes so fast—I love it!”

“There’s too much good food! I can’t keep up! But I still want EVERYTHING!”

“Wait, what if my stomach isn’t big enough? Can I install an extra one?”

“This Pickled Chili Chicken Feet is delicious! Flavorful, tangy, spicy, and crunchy—absolutely amazing!”

…

Late afternoon, since Tianhong Mall was air-conditioned, many people stayed inside, shopping, drinking milk tea, or eating ice cream. But once Lu’s Snack Stall appeared, crowds swarmed the food truck.

Within minutes, the mall became nearly empty. The mall manager was dumbfounded. A food stall this popular? Completely unreasonable!

As the temperature rose, Lu Qin turned on the solar-powered fans. With 360-degree adjustable oscillation, he set the airflow to cool both the food truck and the queueing customers.

Crowds kept growing. Seeing many people squatting on the roadside to eat, Lu Qin paused sales for a few minutes to buy 30 foldable stools.

“Boss Lu, you’re so thoughtful! Thanks!”

“Boss Lu is a great guy!”

Customers were polite, folding up the stools neatly after eating, making them available for the next person.

As more and more customers arrived, Lu Qin bought 30 more stools.

At 5 PM, by this time, stir-fried noodles were completely sold out, leaving only 50 portions of pickled vegetables. Lu Qin told Xiaomi to finish selling those, then drive the food truck home while he picked up his daughter.

At Starlight Kindergarten, Lu Xiyao walked out, her big eyes glowing with excitement—thinking about ice cream. She had waited so long for this moment.

Lu Qin had already bought a vanilla ice cream and stored it in the car fridge. She kissed him happily.

“Thank you, Daddy!”

As they drove home, she ate ice cream while watching cartoons in the backseat.

Noticing a box with uniforms and name tags, she found one labeled 【Little Boss Lady】 and her eyes lit up.

“Daddy, is this mine?”

“Yes, you’re the Little Boss Lady! You can wear the uniform when we set up shop tonight. And we got a bigger food truck!”

“Wow! That’s amazing!”

Her eyes sparkled with excitement.

chapters.title: Chapter 160
chapters.content:
Chapter 160 Little Tiger, Want to Race with Uncle?

Lu Xiyao licked her ice cream stick clean, then excitedly put on her ‘Little Boss Lady’ name tag.

“Daddy, look! Does it look good?”

Lu Qin glanced quickly in the rearview mirror, smiled, and played along.

“Looks amazing.”

“Hehe~!”

Lu Xiyao rummaged through the box of uniforms, finding that she had two sets of her own. She loved them and couldn’t wait to wear them at the food stall—only then would she truly be a Little Boss Lady!

At the bottom of the box, she found another name tag.

【Boss Lady】

Her big round eyes spun, and the clever little girl immediately thought of her wealthy mommy. This must be hers. So, she sneakily tucked the name tag into her pocket, then glanced up at Lu Qin with a mischievous look.

Seeing that her dad was focused on driving, she relaxed. Her brilliant little mind hatched a genius plan— With this ‘Boss Lady’ name tag, I should be able to trade it for five ice creams with my rich mommy, right?

Thinking about this, Lu Xiyao giggled to herself.

“Yao Yao, what are you laughing about?”

Lu Qin glanced at her.

“Nothing, Daddy. I’m just happy.”

_______

As the car entered the neighborhood, the streets were lively with kids and adults. Lu Qin slowed down and rolled down the window.

Many neighbors were familiar faces—Grandpa Ge, Chen Dahai, Wang Guifen, Grandma Liu… Even Ma Dejun was out with his grandson today. It would be rude to just drive past without acknowledging them. So, Lu Qin lowered his speed and greeted them with a smile.

Lu Xiyao poked her little head out, greeting everyone in her sweet, babyish voice. Seeing her, the neighbors’ faces lit up with joy.

“Yao Yao, you’re back from kindergarten!”

“Here, have an apple! It’s a real Aksu apple—super crisp and sweet!”

“Thank you, Granny Li! One is enough! Thank you~!”

“Yao Yao, wanna come out to play later? My little Tiger will take you for a ride in his Mercedes-Benz electric car!”

As she spoke, a chubby little boy drove up in a brand-new ‘Mercedes’ electric toy car, making a loud puttering noise. He even honked his horn—but instead of a normal beep-beep, it played a cute cartoon melody, just like those kiddie rides at supermarket entrances.

A crowd of kids followed behind, eyeing the car with envy. Little Tiger sat proudly in the driver’s seat, looking very smug.

He patted the passenger seat and said in his childish voice, “Yao Yao, wanna go for a ride with me later?”

Other kids eagerly tried to negotiate a turn.

“Little Tiger, let me sit for a bit, please?”

“I’ll trade you crystal slime for a ride!”

“I’ll give you my jumping candy if you let me drive!”

Little Tiger shook his head at each offer. Then, he turned back to Lu Xiyao.

“Yao Yao, I’ll only let you ride!”

Lu Qin narrowed his eyes, watching Little Tiger silently.

“I don’t want to ride. I’m going to help Daddy wash vegetables and work at the food stall tonight to make money.”

Lu Xiyao lifted her tiny chin confidently.

“I don’t play with toys anymore. Those are for little kids.”

Little Tiger froze.

“But… aren’t you a little kid?”

“Nope! I’m a Little Boss Lady!”

She proudly flashed her name tag.

【Little Boss Lady】

Grandpa Ge chuckled.

“Oh wow! Yao Yao, you’re moving up in the world! Now you’ve got an official name tag!”

“Amazing! Yao Yao, you’re the youngest boss lady I’ve ever seen!”

While amused, the neighbors also felt a deep sense of amazement. This contrast was staggering. Lu Qin’s daughter was incredibly mature!

She was only in kindergarten, yet she was already helping with chores and working at a food stall—a true Little Boss Lady!

Meanwhile, their own kids? Still playing with mud.

Wang Guifen smiled and asked, “Yao Yao, how much money did you make from yesterday’s food stall? Enough to buy an ice cream?”

Lu Xiyao tilted her head, did some mental math, and then proudly announced:

“Yesterday, I sold 421 cups of mung bean paste, 357 boxes of ice crystal cakes, and sooo many crystal shrimp dumplings—I lost count! Plus, pickled vegetables, chicken tofu soup, and Mapo tofu… In total, we made 29,000+ yuan!”

She beamed with pride.

She didn’t fully grasp how much 29,000 yuan was, but she knew it was A LOT.

Grandpa Ge: “…How much?”

Wang Guifen’s voice shook.

“Twenty… nine thousand!”

Grandma Liu gasped in disbelief.

“Yao Yao, are you sure? Maybe you got the numbers wrong? Maybe it was just 290 yuan?”

Lu Xiyao shook her head, pulled out her tablet, and activated the voice assistant.

“Xiao Yi, Xiao Yi! How much did Little Boss Lady make yesterday?”

The AI assistant responded immediately.

“Hello, Little Boss Lady. Yesterday, you made a total of 29,726.50 yuan.”

She held up the tablet for everyone to see.

“See? We really made that much!”

The little one was very skilled with her tablet, even though she didn’t fully understand money—but she knew how to use tools!

(This was something Lu Qin had taught her.

“Use your tools wisely!”)

Even the AI assistant’s greeting, “Little Boss Lady,” was customized—and she loved it.

The neighbors were utterly stunned. For a moment, their hearts pounded violently—they couldn’t process it.

Damn.

The Little Boss Lady made more money in one night than their monthly pensions combined!

At first, they thought the gap between children was shocking. Now, it felt like the adults weren’t even on Yao Yao’s level.

_____

Sure, Lu Qin did all the cooking, and business was booming. But Yao Yao was only a little kid, yet she was already exposed to business and real work—far beyond her peers. Their own kids still couldn’t even figure out kiddie rides, and yet Lu Qin’s daughter was making tuition money.

Forget earning ice cream money—she could buy enough ice cream to last three years. The neighbors, who were previously smiling, suddenly looked stiff. Childhood was supposed to be about playing, having fun, and being carefree.

But seeing Yao Yao so happy, successful, and still enjoying herself…

How were they supposed to argue with that?

…Wait.

Could they take back that apple?

They suddenly felt like their own grandkids needed it more!

The kids nearby tilted their heads, looking at their parents’ strange expressions.

Did they do something wrong?

Lu Qin saw their faces and smirked.

Damn, I really raised a top-tier smurf account.

She’s already outshining everyone in the newbie village. Then, he looked down at Little Tiger and grinned.

“Little Tiger, wanna race with Uncle?”

Little Tiger: “???”

Lu Qin smiled mischievously, then slammed the gas pedal.

The car roared forward, leaving behind a gust of wind—almost flipping the Mercedes toy car over!

chapters.title: Chapter 161
chapters.content:
Chapter 161 I can give you five brain bursts!

Lu Qin parked the car and the father and daughter entered the yard. Lu Xiyao hopped and skipped, very happy. One, the kindergarten was over; two, she got the ice cream she had been craving for; three, she could be the little boss again tonight.

Inside the house, Xiaomi had already started cooking the mung bean paste for the night. The kitchen was filled with the fragrance of the mung beans. The mung bean paste had to be cooked first because it needed to chill for a while.

“Boss, Miss Yaoyao, you’re back?”

“Hello, Xiaomi(sister Xiaomi), do you need help? I am the best at washing vegetables in the world.”

The little one loved washing vegetables.

“I’ve already washed them all,” Xiaomi said while starting to knead the dough.

She had already mastered the dough kneading technique that Lu Qin taught her, and her skill level was only second to Lu Qin.

Lu Qin noticed that Xiaomi seemed to have a fatigue-erasing aura, otherwise, working non-stop like a donkey in a production team would definitely make her tired. But Xiaomi wasn’t tired, her movements were still smooth and fluid.

“Wow, Xiaomi is so impressive!”

Lu Xiyao complimented.

Lu Qin didn’t let her do the other tasks in the kitchen, fearing she might get hurt or burned since she was just a child. At most, he let her use the sealing machine when packing things.

“Yaoyao, go watch cartoons in the living room. Leave the rest to me and Xiaomi.”

“Okay~!”

“How much water have you drunk today?”

Lu Xiyao shook her cartoon water cup, and it was empty.

“I drank two cups!”

Lu Qin nodded.

“Go ahead and sit on the sofa to watch, don’t stand in front of the TV, it’s bad for your eyes.”

“Got it, Boss Lu!”

Lu Xiyao skipped away to the living room to watch TV. Lu Qin took the work clothes from the box and handed them to Xiaomi.

“Xiaomi, these are two of your work clothes, one to wash and one to change into. You need to wear them when setting up the stall. Also, don’t forget to wear the name tag.”

“Okay, Boss.”

Xiaomi took the clothes. Lu Qin rummaged through the box and noticed something was missing. It was the ‘Boss Lady’ name tag. Lu Qin searched the bottom of the box but couldn’t find it.

He thought it might have fallen out, after all, it was a small item. He didn’t think much about it, especially since the boss lady wasn’t around. Lu Qin didn’t waste time and rolled up his sleeves to start working. The more food he made, the more money he could earn.

Although the daily income was decent, there was still a long way to go before he became wealthy. Lu Qin knew he still had a lot of work to do.

In the living room, Lu Xiyao was eating an apple and watching the cartoon ‘Peter Pan’. After finishing the apple, she secretly glanced towards the kitchen and saw Lu Qin busy, then picked up her tablet to video call Shen Muyan on WeChat.

The video connected in a few seconds. Shen Muyan’s beautiful, cold face appeared on the screen.

“Yaoyao.”

Lu Xiyao appeared on the screen sideways, smiling mischievously.

“Hello~! Mama~! What are you doing?”

Shen Muyan felt her mood lighten upon seeing her cute daughter.

“Working and making money.”

“Are you making money to buy ice cream for your little darling?”

“Is there anything in your head besides ice cream?” Shen Muyan laughed lightly.

“Of course! There’s also the delicious food Daddy makes, ice crystal cake, stir-fried rice noodles, imperial chicken wings, twice-cooked pork… Oh no, I can’t say more, or I’ll start getting hungry!”

Shen Muyan felt like she might start getting hungry too after hearing that.

“Mama~! Daddy made foot-soaked chicken feet today. It’s so delicious, you’ve never had it, right? When are you coming to eat?”

Lu Xiyao had just been watching from the left side of the screen, but now she switched to the right.

“Is it pickled chicken feet?”

Shen Muyan pursed her lips, wanting to get some food from the little one. In the camera, the little one’s face disappeared, and the screen showed her chest. On her chest was a name tag.

It had the logo ‘Lu Ji Snacks’ and said ‘Little Boss Lady’.

Shen Muyan became a little interested.

“Did Dad make this name tag for you?”

“Yes, Dad and Xiaomi both have one too.”

“Dad also changed to a bigger food cart, it’s huge, like a little house.”

Lu Xiyao stretched her little hands as wide as possible to demonstrate.

Upon hearing ‘Xiaomi’, Shen Muyan’s eyes flickered slightly.

“Mama, can you buy me five ice creams?”

“I can give you five brain bursts,” Shen Muyan said and teased with a mischievous face, as she always mentioned ice cream.

Lu Xiyao wasn’t scared. She secretly took something from her pocket.

“Mama, can this be exchanged for five ice creams?”

Lu Xiyao shook the name tag in front of the camera.

Shen Muyan saw the ‘Boss Lady’ name tag, and froze for a moment. Then, her eyes shone with light.

Shen Muyan squinted.

“Where did this come from?”

“Heehee, I can’t tell you, Mama, are you going to exchange it?”

The little one had a sly look in her eyes, her big eyes sparkling with cleverness.

“Five ice creams is too much, one for the thing you have in your hand.”

“Four ice creams, no less~!”

“Two.”

“Three.”

“Deal.”

“Yay~!”

Three ice creams, the little one was satisfied…

“Put it away, Mama is coming to steal food.”

“Okay, I’ll tell Daddy to add more dishes.”

“Go ahead.”

After hanging up the video call, Lu Xiyao put the “Boss Lady” name tag in her pocket, then ran to the kitchen.

“Daddy, Mama said she’s coming to steal food.”

Lu Qin fed a piece of tomato to the little one.

“Did she call you?”

“Umm… it was sour~! Yeah, I just video chatted with Mama.”

“Alright, I know, you go outside, it’s smoky in the kitchen.”

“Okay~!”

Lu Qin added two dishes, spicy boiled fish and thirteen-spiced braised shrimp. Around 6:00 PM, a white Maserati Quattroporte stopped at the gate.

Shen Muyan knocked and entered the house, and Lu Xiyao ran to open the door.

“Mama, you’re here?”

The little one looked at Shen Muyan and exclaimed, “Mama, you look so beautiful today~!”

Her eyes were sparkling.

Lu Qin came out of the kitchen holding two plates of food, and when he saw Shen Muyan, he paused slightly. She was dressed in light-colored attire, simple and refreshing, a white printed T-shirt paired with a beige skirt. It was casual yet subtly sexy, the hem tucked into the waist to highlight her figure, and a matching belt added a sense of flow.

She wore matching shoes that added harmony, comfortably and casually. Her black hair was gently curled, draped over her shoulders, and her red lips and fair, delicate face radiated an alluring glow.

She… was wearing makeup?

Lu Qin felt that she had dressed up deliberately today, a bit different from her usual casual style.

Though she was beautiful without makeup, with a little makeup, she looked even more stunning.

Lu Qin was momentarily dazed.

It wasn’t until the temperature of the plates transferred to his hands that he snapped back to reality and said, “You’re here? Wash your hands and eat.”

Lu Qin placed the plates on the dining table. Shen Muyan slightly pressed her lips together. Lu Qin’s reaction just now made him seem like a fool. She looked around but didn’t see Xiaomi.

“Where’s Xiaomi?”

“Mama, Xiaomi went back to her own house.”

“Ah.”

Shen Muyan’s face didn’t show much emotion. She changed into slippers and took Lu Xiyao to wash her hands. Lu Xiyao secretly shoved the name tag into Shen Muyan’s hand.

“Mama, don’t forget your promise!”

Lu Xiyao held up three little fingers.

“Okay.”

Shen Muyan pinched the little one’s soft face.

“What promise?” Lu Qin asked in confusion.

“Daddy, your ears are so sharp!”

chapters.title: Chapter 162
chapters.content:
Chapter 162 No Need to Browse Anywhere, Just Head to Lu Ji!

At the dinner table, Shen Muyan was eating the spicy boiled fish, her eyes sparkling.

“This fish is really delicious.”

She had already eaten several pieces. Her husband’s cooking skills were becoming more and more impressive. Shen Muyan thought, if she could hire him as the head chef at her hotel, customers would likely flock in.

She believed that within a year, the name ‘Oury Hotel’ would not only be famous throughout Jiangcheng and Haishi, but perhaps the entire province. However, Shen Muyan couldn’t afford to hire Lu Qin.

Moreover, Lu Qin only wanted to run his food stall, carefree, and didn’t want to work as a head chef in any restaurant. It was rather ironic. At first, Shen Muyan thought it was ridiculous for Lu Qin to set up a stall with the child. After all, he had a degree from a prestigious university—any job would be better than running a food stall. But now, it seemed laughable to her.

If a person is truly outstanding, no matter what they do or which industry they choose, they can shine brightly. In every industry, there are top achievers.

Lu Qin had undoubtedly perfected the food stall business and reached the peak of the industry. And he did all this in such a short time. Now, no matter where the Lu Qin food stall set up, it was incredibly popular. He was truly impressive!

While Shen Muyan enjoyed the delicious food, she glanced at the man eating with his head down. His chiseled features, deep dark eyes, high nose bridge, and moderately thick lips—despite Shen Muyan’s discerning gaze, she was still a little mesmerized.

Her heart raced slightly, and she had to eat a few more pieces of the boiled fish to calm herself down. Lu Xiyao, enjoying the food, shook her head in delight.

“Daddy, this fish is super good, and there are no bones at all!”

Though Lu Qin had purposely reduced the spiciness, the little one still ate with delight.

“Yaoyao, if it’s too spicy, eat less fish and have some other dishes that aren’t spicy.”

Lu Qin picked up a piece of scrambled eggs with tomatoes for her.

“It’s not spicy at all, Daddy!”

“Sizzle! Sizzle!”

The little one stubbornly claimed it wasn’t spicy, even though it was, because it tasted so good! After finishing dinner, Shen Muyan had to go back to work, so she left.

Shen Muyan was a bit busy today. She actually had a dinner party in the evening, but she canceled it to get the ‘Boss Lady’ name tag and specifically came over. Of course, eating Lu Qin’s cooking was also very important.

And of course, it was necessary to see Lu Qin and the little one. Before coming, she had dressed up, initially wanting to assert her dominance in front of Xiaomi, but Xiaomi wasn’t there, so she could only charm Boss Lu instead. Shen Muyan realized that she had many reasons to come to Lu Qin’s place.

…

In the evening, Xiaomi came to open the food truck and head to the night market street, while Lu Qin took Lu Xiyao in his own car. Lu Qin Food Stall’s business was booming, with customers increasing exponentially.

The new pickled chicken feet were selling very well. There were four or five stalls selling lemon chicken feet on the street. These stalls were very similar, selling “Internet-famous lemon chicken feet” along with pickled chicken feet and sometimes pickled radishes.

The entry barrier was low. The stall owners might have just taken a short online course and bought a food cart to set up a stall. The taste of their lemon chicken feet varied, and the competition was fierce with a stall every so often. Their business wasn’t great.

Now that Lu Qin had started selling pickled chicken feet, the situation had worsened for them. At the east side of the university gate, several female students were out for a late-night snack.

“I’ll go get milk tea. You guys bring me a main dish.”

“I’ll go get a portion of lemon chicken feet for everyone.”

“I’ll go buy dessert, let’s meet at the usual place later.”

One of the girls walked towards a narrow alley and found the area crowded with customers.

“Never mind, this place is too crowded. I can never get in line.”

The girl gave up. She had never tried Lu Ji’s food, but had heard it was really good. The problem was, there were so many people, she had tried several times but couldn’t buy anything. So she stopped coming.

Maybe, for her, it was just something that sounded good. Just like with milk tea, everyone said Ba Wang Cha Ji’s milk tea was great, but when she tried it, it was only slightly different from other places, just a different flavor. But nothing special.

She thought the same about this food stall. But when she walked a few more steps, a strong fragrance wafted over. The girl’s eyes lit up.

“Wow, what’s that smell… Is it Mapo Tofu?”

Mapo Tofu was her favorite dish. She was from Sichuan and had been living in Jiangcheng for college, but she hadn’t had any good Mapo Tofu in a long time. Although there were restaurants outside, none were as authentic as the ones back home.

“Smells so good?! It must be delicious!”

The girl turned around and walked into the narrow alley. It turned out that the crowded customers had split into three lines. The middle line was slower because it was for freshly cooked food. The lines on the left and right were quicker, selling pre-cooked food.

“Before, there was just one long, slow line. Every time I came, I couldn’t get anything. Now there are three counters, and the speed is much faster. I’ll definitely be able to buy something now!”

The girl started to queue in the middle line, wanting to try the Mapo Tofu. As she watched customers walking back with all sorts of food, she was stunned. She remembered that this stall only sold fried rice noodles and a Chinese dessert called ‘Ice Crystal Cake’.

But now, they even sold milk tea?

The girl quickly took out her phone and sent messages to her roommates.

“Come to the narrow alley next to the Hot Girl Hot Pot restaurant. I’m in line here!”

“Is it the popular fried rice stall with the handsome boss? Nah, the line’s too long, I’ve never managed to try their fried rice!”

“I can get in line! It’ll take about 15 minutes, there are about fifteen people ahead of me. There are two other lines too, they’re really fast! Trust me!”

“I’m not coming. I’m getting milk tea at Ba Wang Cha Ji.”

“This place sells milk tea too, and lots of people are buying it! It’s gotta be good! I just asked a computer science major, and she said this milk tea is amazing!”

“Seriously? I’m coming over!”

“I’ll check it out after I get my lemon chicken feet!”

“The handsome boss also sells pickled chicken feet! They’re almost sold out! Hurry up and come!”

“Wait, I’ll come see! Does the lemon chicken feet stall not have business because everyone’s over at the handsome boss’s stall?”

…

A group of girls lined up at Lu Qin Food Stall.

“Lu Qin Food Stall?! I remember it used to be a tiny three-wheeled food cart, but now it’s a big one!”

“Oh my god! Fried rice noodles! Mapo Tofu! Imperial Chicken Wings! Chicken Tofu Flower! Ice-cold Mung Bean Paste! Osmanthus Oolong Milk Tea… How is the handsome boss selling so many things?”

“I’m so excited! Finally, I can try their food!”

…

Twenty minutes later, the girls sat down at their usual place, opening the bags of food from Lu Qin.

“Wow, it smells so good!”

“Wow, this Osmanthus Oolong Milk Tea is incredible! Compared to Bai Wang Cha Ji’s milk tea, this one is in a league of its own!”

“Try the Mapo Tofu, it’s even better than the Mapo Tofu at Ju Xian Lou, a hundred-year-old restaurant back in my hometown! So amazing!”

“Wow! This Ice Crystal Cake is unbeatable! It’s like an ice cream cake—icy, sweet, and refreshing, but with the texture of glutinous rice cake, not overly sweet, and has a special rich flavor. I’ve never had anything like this before! So good!”

“The handsome boss is incredible! Everything he makes is amazing!”

“I regret not coming here sooner!”

“From now on, we don’t need to go anywhere else, just head straight to Lu Ji!”

chapters.title: Chapter 163
chapters.content:
Chapter 163 Only Lu Qin’s Food Can Be Called Gourmet! Daily Income Breaks 60,000!

“I’m not working tomorrow, I’m going to have a blast, so comfortable!”

“I’m not working tomorrow, I’m going to be as lazy as I want!”

“I’m not working tomorrow, no need to care when the alarm rings!”

…

Lu Xiyao thoroughly enjoyed being the little boss lady. Her tablet was playing music randomly, and Lu Xiyao thought this dialect song was very catchy and the melody was addictive.

“Cool, wow-wow~!”

She also sang along for a couple of lines.

The little one’s soft, sweet voice singing Sichuan dialect lyrics was quite entertaining. The customers couldn’t stop laughing. Lu Qin heard the song and raised an eyebrow, then gently ruffled the little one’s head.

“Kids listening to children’s songs.”

Lu Qin opened the tablet, switched to the children’s song section, and played “Little Jumping Frog.”

The little one didn’t mind and started singing along to the song.

“Happy ponds planted, dreams turn into oceans”

“Big eyes and big mouths, singing loud and clear”

…

Lu Xiyao was selling food while happily shaking her little hips. She was overjoyed. Lu Qin smiled watching the little one so happy, his face constantly carrying a smile as well.

Lu Xiyao was selling iced mung bean paste, Osmanthus Oolong milk tea, and Ice Crystal Cake. When they first started selling, she was already craving, so she asked Boss Lu for a cup of Osmanthus Oolong milk tea.

The milk tea sold outside was 550 milliliters per cup, but Lu Qin didn’t let the little one drink so much, especially at night. He only let her have half a cup, leaving the other half for Xiaomi.

Half a cup of milk tea was enough for Lu Xiyao, and she happily accepted. The early summer night market, lights dimming and flashing. The narrow alley filled with the strong aroma, and the bustling Lu Qin food stall was full of customers.

On the Jiangda campus forum, there were many posts about Lu Qin food. Students who had tried Lu Qin food couldn’t resist sharing this ‘divine’ food stall outside the campus. Other stalls’ food could only be called food, but Lu Qin’s food could be called “gourmet.” And it was divine gourmet!

Tonight, a hot post was pinned to the top, and the cover picture was of Lu Qin’s food truck. The sign of the “Lu Qin Food Stall” with neon lights was eye-catching, and the lights decorating the food truck were both fashionable and creative, setting the perfect atmosphere.

Inside the open window, the handsome boss, the cute little boss lady, and a hardworking employee, the picture was beautiful as a painting. Such a food truck was hard to resist attracting customers.

Moreover, Lu Qin Food Stall had already become famous on the university town night market street. Customers flocked there. This post had a high discussion rate.

“Lu Qin has a new product, pickled chicken feet. It’s the best pickled chicken feet I’ve ever had. I drank all the sauce!”

“Damn! Can’t even get it! I ran here right after my evening self-study, but the pickled chicken feet were already sold out!”

“Classmate upstairs, you’re too daring! I skipped the first class of self-study to run here! Still didn’t get the pickled chicken feet! But I got the Imperial Chicken Wings and iced mung bean paste!”

“The business is too hot! I only got one serving of fried rice noodles, and my six roommates had to share it. We fought over the last bite, two of us even broke up!”

“I bought two boxes of pickled vegetables! Tomorrow for breakfast, I’ll have porridge with pickled vegetables! So tasty!”

“Are the pickled vegetables for sale? I’ll pay double!”

“Hey, I’ll buy a box of pickled vegetables for 100 yuan!”

“Not even for 1000! I might agree, but my mouth won’t! Even if my mouth agrees, my stomach won’t! No deal, no deal!”

“Boss Lu is so handsome! I’m crazy about him!”

“The chef Lu Qin is even more handsome! He’s too hot!”

…

At 9:15 PM, most of the food was sold out. There were about ten servings of fried rice noodles left, and although there were still more than thirty customers in line, Lu Qin decided to close the stall.

The little one had school tomorrow and needed to go to bed early. When the words “closing the stall” were announced, the customers looked disappointed.

“Boss, you can go, I’ll tidy up and take the food truck back.”

Xiaomi moved the folding stools into the food truck.

Lu Qin thought for a moment and nodded. “Okay, we’ll leave first.”

He carried the little one into the car and started the engine. After the stall closed, the little one started getting sleepy, her eyelids drooping, and she dozed off in the car.

After all, she was just a child, and running a stall was tiring. After a couple of hours, her energy was nowhere near what it was at the beginning. Plus, she had been singing, dancing, and rapping the whole time, so it was no surprise she was tired.

Once home, Lu Qin helped the little one squeeze toothpaste, and she sleepily brushed her teeth. Lu Qin wrung a towel and wiped her face and washed her feet, then carried her to bed. He tucked her in, and the little one was already sound asleep.

After Lu Qin finished showering, Xiaomi arrived at home with the food truck.

“Boss, you go ahead and sleep. I’ll clean the food truck and wash the food containers.”

“Okay, just don’t stay past 10 o’clock. You can leave some for tomorrow too. When you’re done, ride the tricycle. It’ll be your transport from now on.”

“Got it.”

Lu Qin reminded Xiaomi to lock the door when she left, then went to sleep. The next day was Friday. The business was even better than the day before, and the food was prepared in abundance. The daily income broke 60,000 yuan.

With the assistant Xiaomi, Lu Qin felt empowered, and the extra time allowed him to spend with the little one instead of being stuck in the kitchen all day. Lu Qin spent 7000 points on a high-level lottery.

He wanted to draw another assistant, but it wasn’t that easy. Seven high-level draws resulted in five bags of mint candy, a powerful cleaning set, and proficiency in various regional dialects. Mint candies were the least valuable.

He had gotten cleaning sets before, so he didn’t really need them, but it was better than nothing. Proficiency in dialects was not very useful either. 98% of customers spoke Mandarin, and only occasionally would elderly customers speak dialects. Even without dialect proficiency, one could usually communicate with gestures.

This time’s high-level lottery didn’t yield anything useful. Maybe next time. By the weekend, Lu Qin almost forgot that he had promised to go to Haishi to make 50 servings of liver soup for Old Mr. Ouyang’s birthday banquet at Shengyang Group. Thanks to Shen Mu Yan’s reminder, Lu Qin remembered.

The reason he agreed was that he wanted to take the little one along for a short trip while doing the job, killing two birds with one stone. The birthday banquet was on Sunday, but the preparations had to be done the day before.

Making the liver soup required careful selection of ingredients, so Lu Qin had to do it himself. Before leaving, Lu Qin made a sufficient amount of pickled chicken feet, pickled vegetables, Ice Crystal Cake, mung bean paste, and Osmanthus Oolong milk tea, enough to sell for the day.

The Osmanthus Oolong milk tea had to be stored in a temperature-controlled box to preserve its freshness, as its taste would deteriorate after half an hour at room temperature.

While Lu Qin was gone, Xiaomi sold the food from the stall, and the sales were still calculated into the system’s points.

“Boss, Boss Lady, Miss Yaoyao, don’t worry, leave everything to Xiaomi.”

…

chapters.title: Chapter 164
chapters.content:
Chapter 164 The Milk Tea You’ve Had! Mom is Cold!

Xiaomi’s single word, ‘Boss Lady’, caused Lu Qin, Shen Muyan, and even Lu Xiyao to look at her with wide eyes. After thinking for a moment, Lu Qin realized that Xiaomi must have heard the little one call Shen Muyan “Mom,” and from that, she concluded that Shen Muyan was the “Boss Lady.”

Logically speaking, this assumption wasn’t wrong. Xiaomi only understood basic social etiquette and wouldn’t delve too deeply into reading people’s emotions. Otherwise, she would have noticed that although Lu Qin and Shen Muyan were the child’s parents, they didn’t live together.

They also didn’t display any intimate gestures, so it would have been clear to her that they were probably separated, and “Boss Lady” wasn’t a title one could use casually. But Lu Qin didn’t correct Xiaomi; it was just a name, no need for an explanation.

Shen Muyan, on the other hand, observed Xiaomi again. Previously, she had noticed Xiaomi’s eyes were clean and pure, a girl without much pretense.

Now, hearing “Boss Lady” from her so naturally made Shen Muyan like her even more. This girl really knew how to talk—she had a “bright future.”

Shen Muyan considered giving her a red envelope later on. Shen Muyan’s mood lifted, and she smiled. Lu Xiyao tilted her little head and looked at Xiaomi, her big eyes sparkling.

Hearing the “Boss Lady” made her realize there was “business potential.” Last time, the “Boss Lady” name tag got her three ice creams, so if she called Mom “Boss Lady” this time, wouldn’t that make her happy?

If she’s happy, would she buy her ice cream?

If one “Boss Lady” didn’t work, maybe two would. If that still didn’t work, she could call her in front of Dad. Thinking this, Lu Xiyao seemed to see ice cream raining down on her.

“Yaoyao, what are you grinning about?”

Lu Qin glanced at the little one, who was grinning so widely her mouth almost reached her ears, her eyes a little mischievous.

“I’m thinking about going to Haishi, I’m so happy~!”

Lu Xiyao climbed into the back seat of Lu Qin’s car, eager with excitement. Lu Qin shook his head with a smile, gave Xiaomi some instructions, loaded the suitcase into the trunk, and took the driver’s seat.

Shen Muyan didn’t sit in the passenger seat but went to the back. Because of the “Queen’s Seat,” it was very comfortable to lie down. Xiaomi placed a bag of food containers in the back seat, containing fruits, milk tea, mung bean paste, ice crystal cake, and crystal shrimp dumplings for the road.

“Thanks, Xiaomi, bye-bye~!”

“Goodbye.”

…

The car drove along the ring road, heading north onto the highway.

“Yaoyao, we’re on the highway now, close the window.”

Lu Qin reminded her, setting up the navigation and turning on the smart driving mode.

“Okay.”

The little one pulled her head back inside and began playing with the tablet. Shen Muyan lay half-reclined in the “Queen’s Seat,” stretching her limbs as much as possible, revealing her beautiful, alluring curves.

Today, she was wearing a black pleated sequined pencil skirt with a pure white fitted Chanel T-shirt on top. It was a casual yet fashionable outfit with a touch of youthful energy—pure and regal at the same time.

Shen Muyan’s long, slender, fair legs were stretched out, resting on the footrest behind the passenger seat. The car was quiet, with soothing music playing, and despite being on the highway, the road noise was minimal.

The ‘Queen’ was enjoying some longan, peeled by the little one, feeling quite content. She had always focused on work, and work was endless, leaving her with only exhaustion.

Wealth? Status? A sense of accomplishment?

Shen Muyan gradually realized that none of those compared to having a warm, cozy dinner with her family and child.

When Shen Muyan shared this thought with Fang Ya, Fang Ya clasped her hands in prayer, almost bowing to her and said, “My God, you finally get it!”

…

Lu Xiyao peeled a longan for herself and then one for Shen Muyan, acting sweet and obedient.

In the little one’s mind, flattering a rich woman had to be done well, and ice cream was a must.

“Daddy, here’s a longan I peeled for you~!”

Lu Xiyao set a small bowl of longans on the cup holder of the center console. The little one was very thoughtful, knowing that Lu Qin was the one doing the hardest work driving, so she made sure to provide the needed supplies. Lu Qin ate a few longans, they were sweet and juicy.

After finishing the fruit, Shen Muyan placed a cup of milk tea in the cup holder. Lu Qin took it and had a sip, noticing that it was already half-drunk. He turned his head, thinking it was the little one who had drunk it, but then saw that she was happily sipping from her own cup of milk tea.

Lu Qin glanced at Shen Muyan, who looked calm but had no expression. Lu Qin didn’t say anything and took another sip of the milk tea before continuing to drive.

Shen Muyan appeared calm on the surface, but her heartbeat quickened slightly. Five minutes later, Lu Qin couldn’t hold back and said to the little one, “Yaoyao, there’s a thin blanket behind you. Could you help cover Mom’s legs? She’s cold.”

“?!”

Shen Muyan, who had been resting with her eyes half-closed, opened them and looked at Lu Qin. Because the “Queen’s Seat” was reclined, the passenger seat had moved forward. Then, a pair of snow-white, delicate legs were stretched out, and every time Lu Qin looked in the rearview mirror, he could see this beautiful and tempting sight.

The jade-like toes were together, unpolished, clean, and white. Any normal man would find it hard to resist. Shen Muyan noticed that Lu Qin’s gaze was unconsciously drifting towards her legs. She subtly raised the corner of her mouth.

Lu Xiyao put down her milk tea, picked up the thin blanket, and asked, “Mom, are you cold?”

“A little,” Shen Muyan said and nodded slightly, though she wasn’t actually cold.

The little one covered Shen Muyan’s legs with the blanket and then went back to sipping her milk tea. Two and a half hours later, the car exited the highway and drove towards the city.

Another twenty minutes later, the car reached a five-star hotel under Sheng Group. Ouyang Lin had some business to attend to, so he sent the hotel’s general manager to greet them.

The manager, recognizing these guests were important, didn’t dare be careless. He called two bellboys to help park the car and carry the luggage.

After a quick observation, the manager understood that this was a family of three because the super cute little one kept calling them “Dad” and “Mom.”

He kindly prepared a luxurious suite for them. He escorted them to the 18th floor, showed them to their room, left his contact information, and then left, allowing the guests to rest. The suite was indeed large, with a floor-to-ceiling window offering a stunning sea view. The décor was luxurious, clean, and tidy, with a massage bathtub.

But…

There was only one large bed. Lu Qin and Shen Muyan were both silent. The manager had been very considerate, and asking for another room felt inappropriate. Lu Xiyao, on the other hand, was extremely excited. She ran over to the huge floor-to-ceiling window, looking out at the beautiful sea view, feeling a sense of wonder.

“Dad, Mom, look! There’s a ship on the sea~!”

chapters.title: Chapter 165
chapters.content:
Chapter 165 This Is No Longer Being Well-Behaved; This Is Knowing How to Be a Good Person!

After finishing his work, Ouyang Lin arrived at the hotel to meet Lu Qin and his family.

Lu Xiyao obediently called out “Uncle,” and Ouyang Lin pulled out a thick red envelope from his pocket and handed it to the little one. It was Ouyang Lin’s first time meeting Lu Xiyao, and he marveled at how cute she was.

Lu Qin’s family was truly enviable—aside from the couple’s perfect match in appearance, their child was also amazing. This was truly a case of excellent genes all around!

Ouyang Lin had learned that they were a family of three, so he had prepared a gift for the child in advance. He had considered giving something valuable, but worried it might not be accepted, so he thought the red envelope would be the most appropriate choice.

“Uncle, I can’t accept this, thank you for your kindness~!”

Lu Xiyao shook her little hand to refuse. Normally, other children might turn to their parents for guidance in such situations, and if the parents said “accept,” they would, and if they said “don’t accept,” they wouldn’t.

But Lu Xiyao was a clever child. Lu Qin had told her before that when unfamiliar people give gifts, it was best not to accept them. She had never met Ouyang Lin before, so naturally, she couldn’t accept the red envelope.

Ouyang Lin smiled and ruffled her head. “Yaoyao is really well-behaved. But, Uncle and your dad are good friends, and this is our first time meeting. This gift is for you to accept. You don’t call me Uncle for nothing.”

Ouyang Lin was asking Lu Qin for a favor, and for him, it was important to observe proper manners. So, Lu Qin told the little one to accept it. As Ouyang Lin went to arrange for the car, Lu Xiyao had no place to put the big red envelope, so she secretly opened it for a peek. Inside were bright red bills—many, many of them.

The little one wasn’t interested in money, but she knew that red bills could be exchanged for lots of ice cream. Her eyes instantly lit up. Just as she was about to hide the envelope, she noticed that her hands were empty. A slender, fair hand had taken the red envelope from her.

Lu Xiyao pouted.

“Mom, I think that’s mine~!”

“Mom will keep it safe,” Shen Muyan said (calmly).

“No need, Mom, you can have it all~!”

The little one had long known that the phrase “keep it safe” was a lie. During the holidays, all the red envelopes had been taken by Shen Muyan, and she had never seen a single cent of it—losing out on many, many ice creams.

Shen Muyan raised an eyebrow and glanced at Lu Xiyao, thinking, has this little one become more sensible?

“Mom, that red envelope can buy so much ice cream, right? Can you fill our fridge with ice cream?”

Just as she said that, Lu Xiyao’s little head was rubbed by Lu Qin’s big hand.

“Why is your mind always on ice cream?”

After Ouyang Lin finished his call, not long after, a luxury business car arrived.

The group got in the car. A few minutes later, the car stopped outside a more remote courtyard.

“We’ll have a simple lunch here, and in the evening, we’ll host you properly,” Ouyang Lin said politely.

Although he called it a simple meal, it was anything but. This place was a VIP private kitchen, and Shen Muyan knew that such places only served special guests—ordinary people couldn’t even get in the door.

Shen Muyan said, “President Ouyang, you’re too kind.”

The group entered and passed through a uniquely designed courtyard before arriving at a house in the style of a traditional Jiangnan residence. Inside, the Chinese décor exuded a sense of traditional charm and elegance, making the atmosphere warm and comfortable.

This private kitchen didn’t have a menu; the chef prepared whatever he chose for the guests. Lu Xiyao had been holding a canvas bag the whole way, which was bulging with a few box-like items.

Ouyang Lin couldn’t help but ask, “Yaoyao, what are you carrying?”

“It’s delicious food,” Lu Xiyao replied honestly.

Lu Qin laughed and explained, “President Ouyang, it’s food we brought on the road. We’ve always taught the little one not to waste food, so she brought it with her to eat. Sorry for the joke.”

Ouyang Lin waved it off and complimented, “You’ve really done a great job in educating your child!”

Ouyang Lin also had a daughter about the same age as Lu Xiyao. His family spoiled her so much that she had become a little pampered. Ouyang Lin had always thought this wasn’t a big problem, as many families in his circle raised their children the same way.

And, after all, a daughter should be raised with luxury, given anything she wanted, so she wouldn’t be wronged. But after seeing Lu Qin and Shen Muyan’s daughter, Ouyang Lin could see the difference.

From the initial politeness and education to her lack of shyness in a new environment, even knowing not to waste food, Lu Xiyao’s performance was incredibly pleasant. She was quiet, well-behaved, occasionally lively and fun—she was hard not to like.

Ouyang Lin had originally thought of bringing his own daughter to let the two little ones play together. But now, he was glad he didn’t. Ouyang Lin thought that if he had brought his daughter, it would have been embarrassing.

His daughter certainly wouldn’t be as well-behaved as Lu Xiyao. She would probably get bored in just two minutes and start asking for a phone, tablet, or to play with her dolls.

At mealtime, she would be even worse, picking at everything, refusing to eat this or that, and needing to be coaxed to eat. And when eating, she insisted on holding a toy, a bad habit she had developed over time.

It was a relief that he hadn’t brought her, Ouyang Lin thought. Otherwise, the difference would have been too obvious.

…

Ouyang Lin was still lost in thought when he saw the little one running over with a teapot. She climbed onto a chair, refilled her own cup, and then refilled Lu Qin’s and Shen Muyan’s cups before returning to her spot.

At that moment, Ouyang Lin was truly stunned, forgetting to even say “thank you,” standing there in shock. Lu Qin and Shen Muyan’s child was just too impressive!

This wasn’t just being well-behaved, this was knowing how to be a good person!

Ouyang Lin thought of his own daughter. If it had been her, not only would she not be refilling anyone’s tea, but she probably would have knocked over the cup.

It just goes to show how important good education from a young age is!

On this point, Ouyang Lin felt ashamed, realizing that Lu Qin was more impressive than him. Ouyang Lin made a mental note to start taking his own daughter’s education more seriously after the old man’s birthday banquet was over.

After drinking some tea, the dishes began to be served. The dishes in this private kitchen were known for their unique flavors and creativity. To put it simply, these were dishes they couldn’t find elsewhere.

Lu Qin looked at the dishes being brought out, all red and green, so visually appealing that just the presentation looked high-end and exquisite. The chef himself brought the dishes and briefly introduced each one.

“This dish is [Vegetable Juice Wild Rice with Ginseng]. After steaming the wild rice, we use fresh asparagus and chicken broth to make the juice, then simmer the ginseng in the broth. This green sauce is made from asparagus…”

As the chef introduced the dish, his tone was slightly proud, clearly showing that these private dishes were his proud creations.

chapters.title: Chapter 166
chapters.content:
Chapter 166 Is Gan Gao Soup the Best Dish Lu Qin Can Make?

The private kitchen’s head chef introduced his meticulously crafted dishes with a hint of pride. Vegetable Juice Wild Rice with Kanto Sea Cucumber, Spicy Beef Short Ribs, Black Truffle Abalone with Conch, Nourishing Snow Swallow Stuffed Tree Tomato, Crystal Leek and Mushroom Bun…

Ouyang Lin, sitting nearby, nodded in satisfaction. The chef of this private kitchen was well-known in the industry for his exceptional culinary skills.

Every dish here was made with high-quality ingredients, carefully paired to create a refined and delicious experience. Whenever Ouyang Lin hosted important guests, he often chose this place.

Among today’s dishes, there were two that even Ouyang Lin hadn’t tried before. He was the first to pick up his chopsticks, smiling as he invited his guests.

“The food here is excellent. Mr. Lu, President Shen, please help yourselves.”

“Thank you, then we won’t be polite.”

Lu Qin and Shen Muyan also picked up their chopsticks and took some dishes in front of them.

After taking their first bite, Ouyang Lin expected to see expressions of delight on their faces. However, Lu Qin and Shen Muyan showed little reaction, only nodding slightly.

Seeing this, Ouyang Lin was puzzled.

Were today’s dishes not as good?

To confirm, he took a bite of the Surf Bamboo Fish in front of him. As he chewed, a look of enjoyment appeared on his face.

The fish was tender, infused with a subtle bamboo aroma—delicious. He figured that Lu and Shen were simply maintaining dining etiquette, suppressing their reactions even if they found the food delicious.

With that thought, Ouyang Lin smiled and turned to Lu Xiyao.

“Yaoyao, eat whatever you like. Make yourself at home.”

“Okay, thank you, Uncle Ouyang~!”

When the Surf Bamboo Fish turned to her, Lu Xiyao took a piece.

“Is it good?” Ouyang Lin asked with a smile.

“It’s nice~!”

She nodded her little head but didn’t take a second bite. Ouyang Lin was momentarily surprised. If a child truly liked something, they would usually exclaim “So yummy!”

But Lu Xiyao’s “nice” sounded more like an indifferent remark to him. Moreover, her face didn’t show any excitement. Kids don’t hide their expressions.

Could it be that the dish was just average? That didn’t seem possible—Ouyang Lin thought it was delicious. As he wondered about it, he saw Lu Xiyao pointing to a few takeout containers they had brought along and turning to Lu Qin.

“Daddy, can I take these out?”

The containers held some dishes Lu Qin had prepared in the morning for their lunch. The trip from Jiangcheng to Haishi took about three hours. Since they left at around 9 a.m., they had expected to eat lunch before arriving.

But along the way, the mother and daughter kept snacking non-stop. Even Lu Qin had been fed plenty of snacks and fruit. As a result, they barely touched their packed lunch—only the Imperial Concubine Chicken Wings had been opened and finished as a snack.

Lu Qin shook his head.

“There’s already a lot of food on the table. Let’s not take them out.”

Before he finished speaking, Ouyang Lin waved his hand.

“It’s fine. We just talked about not wasting food. If we leave it until dinner, it won’t taste as good. Let’s take them out.”

Bringing their own food to such an occasion was a bit unusual. But since it was for a child, perhaps there was something she liked inside. Ouyang Lin personally helped Lu Xiyao open the containers and placed them on the rotating table among the restaurant’s exquisite dishes. The head chef came in with a fruit platter.

When he glanced at the table and saw the extra takeout boxes, his expression turned strange. In all his years of hosting guests, this was the first time someone had brought their own food.

What’s this?

They think my food isn’t good?

He took a quick look at the dishes inside the boxes.

Spicy Boiled Fish, Twice-Cooked Pork, Tomato Scrambled Eggs, Mapo Tofu, Kung Pao Chicken…

Just some simple, common dishes.

These looked no different from what anyone would find in any casual diner. Placing such low-end dishes on a table full of fine cuisine—what were these guests thinking?

Ouyang Lin noticed the chef’s awkward expression but said nothing. As a guest, there was no need for him to explain. Although the chef was displeased, he didn’t say much either. He simply muttered, “Enjoy while it’s hot,” and left.

Looking at the assortment of elegant dishes with those “out-of-place” takeout boxes in the middle, Ouyang Lin admitted that it did ruin the visual appeal. Still, it didn’t affect the meal, so he continued eating the fine private kitchen dishes. The food Lu Qin had prepared was still slightly warm, only losing a bit of its ideal texture. But the flavor remained just as good.

After eating for a while, Ouyang Lin noticed something unusual. Lu Qin and his family barely touched the exquisite restaurant dishes. Instead, they kept picking from their own packed food.

Lu Xiyao was eating the Spicy Boiled Fish enthusiastically, her little mouth glistening with oil. Ouyang Lin was stunned. When she ate the Surf Bamboo Fish, she had shown no such enjoyment…

Could it be that this Spicy Boiled Fish was incredibly delicious?

That was impossible, right?

“Uncle Ouyang, why aren’t you eating my dad’s cooking? It’s really good~!”

Lu Xiyao stuffed a bite of Tomato Scrambled Eggs into her mouth.

“These are all Mr. Lu’s dishes?”

Ouyang Lin asked. Until now, he hadn’t touched any of the food from the takeout boxes, assuming they were just cheap takeout.

“Yes! He made them at home this morning.”

Lu Qin nodded.

“President Ouyang, you should give it a try.”

“Oh? Mr. Lu cooked these?”

Ouyang Lin had thought that Gan Gao Soup was the only impressive dish Lu Qin could make. After all, Lu Qin was quite young.

For a chef so young, mastering even one dish as complex as Gan Gao Soup was already remarkable.

But now…

He looked at the Twice-Cooked Pork rotating toward him and picked up a piece with his chopsticks. The moment he bit into it, he was struck by an explosion of flavor. The lean meat was aromatic, the fatty part rich but not greasy, and the pork skin had a delightfully chewy texture. The meat was crisp outside, tender inside—soft yet flavorful.

The fresh green garlic sprouts added a crunchy, refreshing bite, complementing the spicy, savory notes of the dish. A deep, smoky aroma filled his mouth, leaving a lingering, satisfying aftertaste.

This…!!!

It was incredible!

Ouyang Lin had never had such delicious Twice-Cooked Pork before. Lu Qin’s version completely redefined the dish for him.

So this is what real Twice-Cooked Pork tastes like!

With a spoonful of rice to go with it, all of life’s worries seemed to melt away. It was just that good!

Deeply moved, Ouyang Lin said, “Mr. Lu, this Twice-Cooked Pork is truly amazing!”

Lu Qin smiled.

“They say if you visit Sichuan but don’t try Twice-Cooked Pork, it’s like you never went at all. This is the perfect time of year to eat it. If you like it, President Ouyang, please have more.”

Ouyang Lin eagerly took a few more bites and finished an entire bowl of rice in no time.

So satisfying!

No wonder Twice-Cooked Pork is the king of Sichuan cuisine—it’s unbeatable!

Ouyang Lin had thought Gan Gao Soup was Lu Qin’s best dish. But now, here was this Twice-Cooked Pork!

At that moment, Lu Xiyao’s sweet voice chimed in.

“Uncle Ouyang, try this Spicy Boiled Fish! It’s super tasty and so exciting~!”

chapters.title: Chapter 167
chapters.content:
Chapter 167 No Wonder My Own Dishes Were Untouched—I’ve Just Encountered the Pinnacle of Twice-Cooked Pork!

Ouyang Lin remained silent. He had tasted every one of the five dishes multiple times. The flavor was beyond words!

To put it simply and bluntly—delicious!

Indescribably delicious!

Ouyang Lin had originally thought that for someone of Lu Qin’s age, making such an exceptional liver paste soup was already an impressive feat.

But why did even a simple tomato and scrambled egg dish taste so unbelievably good?

For a moment, Ouyang Lin doubted whether all the tomato and scrambled egg dishes he had eaten before were even real.

And this spicy poached fish—turned out, blackfish and spicy broth were the ultimate match!

All those so-called “innovative fusion dishes”, like the flashy “Surfer Marble Goby”, weren’t even a tenth as fragrant and flavorful as this spicy poached fish!

There was no real comparison without contrast. After tasting Lu Qin’s cooking, Ouyang Lin realized that the gap between “good” and truly good was far wider than he had ever imagined.

Fusion cuisine, which attempts to combine and innovate flavors from different regional cuisines, was still lacking compared to traditional dishes. After all, mastering multiple regional cuisines is incredibly difficult for any chef.

And if a chef is only slightly familiar with each one, how could they possibly elevate fusion cuisine to its full potential?

Of course, these thoughts only crossed Ouyang Lin’s mind after tasting Lu Qin’s cooking.

Lu Qin’s mastery of Sichuan cuisine had reached an unparalleled level—utterly flawless.

Although fusion cuisine is an emerging trend, for now, it still falls short compared to traditional dishes.

The eight meticulously crafted private dishes remained mostly untouched.bMeanwhile, the food from Lu Qin’s takeout containers was completely devoured. Ouyang Lin even ate three full bowls of rice, breaking his personal record.

“Mr. Lu, I always thought your liver paste soup was your specialty, but now I realize every dish you make is top-notch!”

“Had I known your culinary skills were this incredible, I would have made more trips to Jiangcheng just to get a free meal!”

Ouyang Lin joked as he rubbed his stomach.

Lu Qin simply smiled while gently wiping Lu Xiyao’s little mouth.

Hearing Ouyang Lin’s praise, Shen Muyan felt a deep sense of satisfaction—she loved hearing others compliment Lu Qin.

A truly remarkable man.

“Mr. Lu, I’ll be counting on you for the liver paste soup at the Old Master’s birthday banquet tomorrow.”

“Let me know what ingredients you need, and I’ll have someone prepare them.”

Lu Qin thought for a moment and replied, “Mr. Ouyang, I’d prefer to select the ingredients myself. Liver paste soup requires very precise ingredients.”

“Then I’ll trouble you with it. I’ll send a few people to assist you.”

It was the best outcome—Ouyang Lin also worried that if the ingredients weren’t up to standard, the soup might not turn out right. After settling the bill, they left the private kitchen restaurant.

When the waitstaff cleared the table, they noticed that most of the dishes had barely been touched. Seeing this, they reported it to the head chef. As a seasoned chef, he often analyzed the leftover food to gather feedback.

If a dish remained mostly uneaten, it usually indicated a problem that needed improvement.

“Which dish was it?” The chef asked while walking towards the dining area.

The waiter hesitated before whispering, “Um… it seems like… all of them?”

The chef stopped in his tracks and frowned.

“All of them? What do you mean?”

“I mean… none of the dishes were eaten much. Not a single dish was even a third finished.”

“That’s impossible!”

The chef was shocked.

In all the years since the restaurant had opened, this had never happened.

“But they ate a lot of rice.”

“Oh, and they finished half of the fruit platter too.”

The chef was speechless.

When he finally reached the table, he saw it with his own eyes—nearly untouched dishes.

His eyelids twitched.

What the hell happened?

Without hesitation, he grabbed a pair of clean chopsticks and sampled each dish.

“Everything tastes fine… what’s going on?”

The waiter also took a bite and looked equally confused.

“Chef, these dishes are really good…”

The chef frowned.

“Then why did this happen?”

After a moment’s thought, the waiter suggested, “Maybe they just weren’t very hungry? Maybe they came mainly to discuss business?”

That seemed possible—their restaurant was well-known for its secluded ambiance, perfect for private discussions.

But even during business meetings, people still eat. Especially when presented with such high-quality dishes. Then the chef’s eyes landed on the empty wooden rice container.

“If they weren’t hungry, why did they finish all the rice?”

At that moment, the chef noticed something. On the table were five empty takeout containers. They brought their own food. This was a first—who brings food to a fine dining restaurant?

Curious, the waiter picked up one of the containers and sniffed it. His eyes lit up.

“Smells amazing!”

Inside, a single leftover slice of twice-cooked pork clung to the container wall—a missed piece. The waiter reached out to taste it, but the chef stopped him.

“Wait, I’ll try it.”

The chef picked up the small piece and popped it into his mouth. The moment it hit his tongue, his taste buds exploded.

This twice-cooked pork…!!!

It was on another level!

The chef himself was very skilled at making twice-cooked pork. But compared to this—his version was a joke!

No wonder his own dishes were untouched.

He had just encountered the pinnacle of twice-cooked pork!

“Chef! There are two pieces of spicy poached fish left in this soup!”

“One for each of us!”

The chef took a bite. The waiter took the other. The moment the fish entered their mouths, their eyes widened. This was the best spicy poached fish they had ever tasted!

The tender fish, the layered spicy flavors, the way the heat lingered—it was perfection. For the first time, the chef felt humbled.

“If this dish were freshly served, it would taste even better.”

The waiter nodded in agreement. Fusion cuisine was supposed to surpass traditional dishes. But now, the chef realized he had been narrow-minded. There was always a greater chef out there.

A mountain beyond a mountain, a man beyond a man.

Meanwhile, on the way back, Lu Qin noticed his system points increasing. Even though he wasn’t in Jiangcheng, Xiaomi was still selling pickled vegetables and marinated chicken feet, boosting his points.

Nice.

Back at the hotel, they settled in. The TV played Pokémon. Shen Muyan and Lu Xiyao were already asleep, curled up on the bed. Lu Qin smiled. He was the one driving all morning—why were they the ones so exhausted?

Adjusting the AC to 27°C, he gently covered them with a blanket. Shen Muyan, half-asleep, peeked at him, then curled up like a lazy cat. Just then, Lu Qin’s phone vibrated. It was a call from Ouyang Lin. His men had arrived.

Efficient.

chapters.title: Chapter 168
chapters.content:
Chapter 168 This twice-cooked pork dish is a no-go—take it off the banquet menu!

Ouyang Lin sent three strong young men to serve as Lu Qin’s drivers and assistants for carrying ingredients.

“Mr. Lu, if you need anything, just say the word. We’re at your service.”

The three young men stood respectfully.

“First, take me to check out the kitchen at Wangjing No.1, then we’ll head to the market.”

“Understood.”

The car was a luxurious black Mercedes MPV with a highly conspicuous license plate: Hai A·000001.

Someone opened the door for Lu Qin—this was full VIP treatment. He insisted on inspecting the kitchen first. A craftsman must sharpen his tools before he can do his job well. After all, making forty servings of Liver Paste Soup was no small task, and proper preparation was essential.

______

Wangjing No.1 was located on a hillside in the northern part of Qinglian District, Haishi. Nestled among trees, it looked like a secluded mountain retreat—exuding an air of exclusivity and luxury.

When the car arrived at the entrance, the electronic gate opened automatically—clearly, the license plate had been registered in advance.

For the past two days, Wangjing No.1 had been fully booked by the Shengyang Group to exclusively serve the birthday banquet of the group’s patriarch, Ouyang Peng.

The kitchen was massive—nearly 800 square meters, with designated sections for washing and cutting, hot cooking, stir-frying, workstations, refrigerators, steam cabinets, and nearly ten cooking stations. The kitchen staff numbered over thirty people.

In the hot kitchen, a group of chefs was preparing a dish. Ouyang Lin was sampling a plate of twice-cooked pork. Twice-cooked pork was a common dish and wasn’t originally on the banquet menu. However, Ouyang Lin had suddenly requested that it be added, so it had to be tested first.

Chef Gu Tielin, an expert in Sichuan cuisine, prepared the dish. Twice-cooked pork was one of his specialties—a staple dish for any Sichuan chef.

Ouyang Lin took a bite, then shook his head.

“Nope, still far from good enough!”

His blunt statement stunned Gu Tielin and the other kitchen staff.

“Did Mr. Ouyang just say that Chef Gu’s twice-cooked pork is ‘not good’?”

This couldn’t be right!

Chef Gu’s skills were well recognized. Gu Tielin frowned. What exactly was wrong with my dish?

He had never received such a harsh critique before. Gu Tielin had trained under the Sichuan cuisine master Yang Huaian, inheriting the authentic techniques.

Although he had since explored fusion cuisine and modern styles, his foundation in traditional skills remained solid. Hearing Ouyang Lin’s critique, he held back his temper.

“Mr. Ouyang, what’s wrong with this dish?” He asked, unconvinced.

Ouyang Lin put down his chopsticks and explained.

“The lean meat isn’t fragrant enough, the fat isn’t delicate enough, the skin isn’t chewy enough, the spiciness isn’t bold enough, and the aftertaste is lacking.”

He spoke calmly. His assessment was based on the twice-cooked pork he had tasted earlier—made by Lu Qin. Even though Lu Qin’s dish had been made that morning—with much of the wok aroma already faded—it still outshone the freshly cooked version from Gu Tielin.

Gu Tielin was stunned.

“Is he just nitpicking?”

He frowned, took another bite, and tasted carefully.

It was delicious!

Where was it lacking? The lean meat was fragrant, the fat was delicate, and the aftertaste was rich!

This was clearly just finding fault for no reason!

Irritated, he said, “Mr. Ouyang, I’ve tasted it myself, and there’s nothing wrong with this dish.”

To prove his point, he had the other chefs try it too.

“It’s delicious! Spicy and aromatic!”

“Absolutely tasty. Chef Gu’s skills are as great as ever!”

“Fantastic dish!”

The kitchen staff gave honest opinions—no flattery. The dish was genuinely good.

Gu Tielin looked at Ouyang Lin, thinking, Is your taste different from everyone else’s?

But Ouyang Lin simply sighed and patted his shoulder.

“I know your skills, Chef Gu. But this dish won’t be on the banquet menu.”

Compared to Lu Qin’s version, there was still a clear gap. Liver Paste Soup was already a time-consuming dish. Since Lu Qin had to make so many servings, he wouldn’t have time for twice-cooked pork.

Otherwise, Ouyang Lin would have definitely asked Lu Qin to cook this dish and spicy boiled fish—which would have stunned everyone at the banquet.

Face-Off?

Gu Tielin frowned.

As a top chef, his skills were rarely questioned—aside from his mentor, who would critique him for self-improvement. Now, this outsider, Ouyang Lin, had dismissed his dish outright.

But Ouyang Lin didn’t seem to be deliberately insulting him. Plus, he was still entrusting him with the banquet. So, what was really going on? Gu Tielin was confident that his dish was good enough for the banquet.

“Mr. Ouyang, if you think my dish isn’t good enough, then find someone better and have them make it!”

He refused to back down.

At that moment, Lu Qin called the lobby manager—a number provided by Ouyang Lin.

“Mr. Lu, let me show you around Wangjing No.1 to familiarize yourself with the layout.”

The lobby manager knew that Lu Qin was an invited chef, so he needed to be familiar with the venue. However, the manager hadn’t expected him to be so young.

“Mr. Lu, you’re truly impressive at such a young age! Even our top Sichuan chef, Chef Gu, couldn’t master Liver Paste Soup!”

“Not at all,” Lu Qin said humbly.

“Wangjing No.1 has been operating for twenty years. We have eight banquet halls, fifty-six private dining rooms, and our largest room accommodates sixty guests…”

“All our ingredients are carefully selected and sourced from special suppliers across different regions…”

Walking through the lavish hall, the manager introduced the restaurant with a smile. Lu Qin nodded. There was no restaurant in Jiangcheng that could match Wangjing No.1’s scale. The manager led him to the kitchen.

“This is the main kitchen. Up front is the stock-preparation station, where we cook broths over low heat. These are gas stoves, and here we have the large wok station… Over here, we have double-wok setups with thirteen burners…”

After familiarizing himself with the kitchen, Lu Qin asked, “I need to speak with the head chef. I’d like to see if I can get three burners reserved for my use.”

The kitchen was large, so securing three burners shouldn’t be an issue—but with such a big banquet coming up, it was best to confirm in advance.

“Of course, Mr. Lu, right this way. I’ll introduce you to the head chef.”

Most of the kitchen staff were in the prep area, working on ingredients for the banquet. Meanwhile, in the hot kitchen.

“Chef Gu, your twice-cooked pork is already quite good. But I’ve tasted an even better version—that’s why I’m being so critical. No offense.”

Ouyang Lin spoke sincerely.

After trying Lu Qin’s version, other versions just didn’t compare. But his words caused a stir in the kitchen.

“A better twice-cooked pork?”

Gu Tielin raised an eyebrow. At that moment, the lobby manager entered with Lu Qin. Ouyang Lin’s eyes lit up.

“Speak of the devil!”

chapters.title: Chapter 169
chapters.content:
Chapter 169 Cold Water for Soup, Boiling Water for Flavorful Meat!

Ouyang Lin claimed he’d had better Twice-Cooked Pork before, even using exaggerated phrases like “not even close” and “way off” to describe Gu Tielin’s dish.

Gu Tielin refused to accept that. They could say he wasn’t skilled in other cuisines, like Cantonese or Shandong dishes—but when it came to Sichuan cuisine, he had every reason to be proud. He didn’t believe anyone could make a Twice-Cooked Pork that was significantly better than his.

Just then, the restaurant manager walked in with a strikingly handsome young man. Ouyang Lin was first surprised, then delighted, and eagerly went to greet him.

“Mr. Lu, I thought you were coming tomorrow.”

In the kitchen, all eyes were on Lu Qin. Gu Tielin, already annoyed, didn’t wait for an introduction and directly asked, “President Ouyang, is this young man the one you said could make a better Twice-Cooked Pork than me?”

Gu Tielin crossed his arms and stared at Lu Qin, his posture resembling the classic arrogant antagonist in web novels—one who was about to be put in his place. Lu Qin glanced at Gu Tielin with an indifferent expression.

It seemed like Ouyang Lin had eaten his cooking at lunch, and now his taste had become more refined—more demanding.

Sigh.

Wasn’t this just creating more trouble for him?

Sure enough, Ouyang Lin smiled and said, “Mr. Lu, this is Chef Gu Tielin, the head Sichuan cuisine chef at Wangjing No.1. Why don’t you two have a little competition? Here, this is his Twice-Cooked Pork.”

He pushed a plate towards Lu Qin, signaling him to taste it.bLu Qin glanced at Ouyang Lin.

Showing off costs extra, you know.

He picked up his chopsticks, took a bite, and gave an honest, straightforward review.

“The lean meat isn’t dry and fragrant enough, the fat isn’t tender enough, the skin lacks chewiness, the spice isn’t strong enough, and the aftertaste is weak.”

Lu Qin looked up at Gu Tielin.

“You made this?”

As soon as he said that, Gu Tielin’s face turned red and then pale. Everyone around was wide-eyed.

Wait… wasn’t that exactly what President Ouyang said earlier?

Could Chef Gu’s Twice-Cooked Pork really be that bad?

No way!

They had all tasted his cooking before—his skills were highly regarded in Haishi. Ouyang Lin, however, nodded with a smile. Compared to Lu Qin’s Twice-Cooked Pork, this assessment was spot on.

Gu Tielin snorted.

“Yes, I made it. Mr. Lu, was it? Then let me try yours. Please—!”

He gestured towards the kitchen.

Lu Qin had no reason to hold back—making a dish of Twice-Cooked Pork would only take about ten minutes. With Chef Gu’s personality, he wouldn’t be convinced until he was humbled.

Lu Qin stepped forward, raised his hands, and suddenly exuded a strong presence. The staff instinctively stepped back, clearing space for him.

Ouyang Lin gave a look to one of the assistants, who quickly caught on and ran to fetch an apron, tying it around Lu Qin. Lu Qin selected a piece of second-cut pork belly. He had to admit—Wangjing No.1 used top-tier ingredients.

Every cut of pork looked excellent.

As the water was coming to a boil, word spread that an outsider was challenging Chef Gu. Curious kitchen staff began sneaking over to watch. Before long, the kitchen entrance was packed.

“Who’s this kid? Daring to challenge our Chef Gu? Must be clueless. Probably from out of town.”

“Actually, yeah—he’s from Jiangcheng.”

“President Ouyang said his Twice-Cooked Pork is way better than Chef Gu’s.”

“No way! For real! That’s a bold claim! Chef Gu is one of the top five Sichuan chefs in Haishi!”

“Jiangcheng is known as a ‘culinary desert’—I once ate at a famous Sichuan restaurant there called Lou Wai Lou, and honestly, it wasn’t as good as Chef Gu’s cooking.”

“He’s young—how skilled could he be? I’ll give him credit for bravery, though.”

…

Whispers filled the room. The water came to a boil. Lu Qin placed the meat in, skin-side up, meat-side down.

Ouyang Lin, unfamiliar with cooking, asked curiously, “Isn’t the meat supposed to go in cold water? Why boiling water?”

Lu Qin smiled.

“President Ouyang, cold water for soup, boiling water for flavorful meat. Cold water brings out the freshness of the broth; boiling water enhances the meat’s aroma.”

“Oh, I see,” Ouyang Lin nodded.

Lu Qin added, “Also, fresh meat can be blanched in boiling water, but if it’s feedlot meat or frozen, you have to use cold water—otherwise, the blood won’t be fully extracted.”

Ouyang Lin gave a thumbs-up.

“I never knew there were so many techniques in cooking, and this is just the start.”

He couldn’t help but gain a newfound respect. The surrounding chefs, hearing how clearly Lu Qin explained things, put aside their initial doubts.bThis guy knew his stuff.

Some even realized they’d been doing it wrong all along—always putting meat in cold water. Gu Tielin narrowed his eyes. What Lu Qin just explained wasn’t something you could easily learn outside—his mentor had taught him personally. He had never taken on a disciple, so he never shared these details.

In a competitive kitchen, knowledge was power. Teaching others only reduced his own edge. Yet Lu Qin openly revealed it without hesitation. Lu Qin skillfully twisted a bunch of scallions into a bundle and tossed them into the pot.

Using a ladle, he skimmed off the scum and added scallions, ginger, garlic, cooking wine, and Sichuan peppercorns. He adjusted the heat and flipped the meat over. Gu Tielin watched intently, now completely serious. Many chefs thought blanching meat was just about cooking it through. But that was a huge mistake!

The level of heat was crucial. Too high, and the meat cooked too fast, becoming dry. Too low, and the meat wouldn’t firm up properly. And the meat shouldn’t be fully cooked—it needed to be half-raw in the center and fully cooked on the outside to achieve the signature “lamp-shadow” texture when sliced. Even he couldn’t always control this level of precision.

But—!

The way Lu Qin handled it with such confidence and ease shocked him. This guy was legit. Once the meat cooled, Lu Qin began slicing. Everyone stretched their necks to watch his knife skills.

Among chefs, knife skills were a major test of ability. As Lu Qin started slicing, people’s expressions gradually shifted to astonishment.

“Whoa! He’s so fast! That’s real skill!”

“This level of knife work takes at least a decade to master!”

“The slices are so even! I bet the thickness difference between each piece is less than half a millimeter. I kinda wanna grab a coin to compare!”

“Damn! He might be even better than Chef Gu!”

In just over three minutes, the meat was perfectly sliced.

Gu Tielin’s heartbeat quickened.

He was more than twenty years older than Lu Qin, but when it came to knife skills—Lu Qin had the upper hand!

This young man was terrifying!

Then came the stir-fry.

Lu Qin’s technique was the embodiment of one word—professional.

In no time, the dish was plated. A rich aroma filled the air. Everyone instinctively swallowed.

Damn.

It smelled better than Chef Gu’s!

chapters.title: Chapter 170
chapters.content:
Chapter 170 Dad, flick her, not me!

A crowd of people at the entrance surged inside. Everyone wanted to see up close what kind of twice-cooked pork could taste even better than the one made by Chef Gu!

Ouyang Lin swallowed hard and was the first to pick up his chopsticks and take a bite.

“Mmm… so good! This is the flavor!”

“Hey, bring me a bowl of rice.”

A nearby kitchen staff member was stunned for a few seconds before reacting—he had never seen someone ask for rice while taste-testing! Ouyang Lin just couldn’t resist!

He had only eaten lunch a short while ago—and had three whole bowls of rice, no less. Yet here he was, ready to dig in again.

Gu Tielin’s expression darkened. He had a bad feeling about this, but still, he picked up his chopsticks and tried a piece. After swallowing, there was no doubt—he had lost. The lean meat was chewy, the fatty part was tender and fragrant, and the crisp, spicy garlic sprouts tied it all together perfectly.

Gu Tielin’s heart pounded. This twice-cooked pork wasn’t just a little better than his—it was on a whole different level. Before Lu Qin appeared, he would never have believed that twice-cooked pork could reach such heights.

If his own skill level was that of a seasoned expert, then the young man in front of him was a true master. Fear crept into Gu Tielin’s heart. He had watched Lu Qin cook the dish from start to finish. He had seen every detail clearly. Yet he knew—he could never replicate that taste.

The precise control of the heat, the knife work, the balance of seasonings… Knowing the techniques wasn’t the same as executing them. This was where the gap lay.

Gu Tielin’s usual pride disappeared. He fell silent. Everyone could tell from his expression—the head Sichuan chef of Wangjing No. 1 had lost.

And he had lost to an unknown young man. Luckily, this young man wasn’t here to challenge them. Otherwise, Gu Tielin’s defeat would have meant the end of his career at Wangjing No. 1. The crowd turned their gaze to Lu Qin, full of curiosity.

Who was this young man?

In all of Jiangcheng and Haishi’s culinary circles, they had never heard of a famous chef named Lu. Meanwhile, Ouyang Lin was completely immersed in his meal, showing none of the demeanor of Shengyang Group’s CEO. He was just a hungry man, devouring his food with zero restraint.

Around him, the sound of people swallowing their saliva echoed. After finishing a whole bowl of rice, stuffed to the brim, Ouyang Lin finally put down his chopsticks and let out a satisfied burp.

Wiping his mouth with a napkin, he said contentedly, “I’ve never had such a good appetite before.”

People like him, accustomed to the finest delicacies, rarely got excited over food.

But Lu Qin’s cooking elevated cuisine to a whole new level. Eating his food was a true pleasure!

Ouyang Lin patted Lu Qin on the shoulder.

“Mr. Lu, after the birthday banquet, you must stay in Haishi for a few days and let me be a proper host.”

Stuffed to the point of discomfort, Ouyang Lin had to get up and walk around to digest. As soon as he left, someone couldn’t resist sneaking a taste of the remaining twice-cooked pork. Once the first person tried it, the second quickly followed.

Then, the entire kitchen staff swarmed in with their chopsticks. Even the restaurant manager couldn’t help but take a bite. After tasting it, they finally understood—Ouyang Lin and Lu Qin’s earlier critique of Chef Gu’s dish had been completely fair.
