No fancy words could truly capture how delicious this twice-cooked pork was. It was simply incredible!

The restaurant manager was in awe and praised, “Mr. Lu, no wonder Mr. Ouyang specifically invited you from Jiangcheng to make Elder Ouyang’s requested liver pâté soup. Your culinary skills are extraordinary!”

Hearing this, Gu Tielin, who had been silent, suddenly looked up in shock.

“So, you’re the one Mr. Ouyang found to make the liver pâté soup?”

Elder Ouyang had insisted on adding a special dish—liver pâté soup—to the birthday banquet menu. Gu Tielin had tried making a version for Elder Ouyang to taste. But Elder Ouyang had been completely dissatisfied.

Gu Tielin had wanted to ask his long-retired master, Yang Huaian, for help, but Yang had fallen ill and needed two weeks to recover. So, Ouyang Lin had gone to Jiangcheng to find someone who could make the dish.

Gu Tielin had assumed the only person Ouyang Lin could have invited from Jiangcheng was the retired national banquet-level chef, Master Mai. He never expected it to be a young man. A terrifyingly skilled young man!

“Yes, it’s me.” Lu Qin smiled calmly.

He wasn’t trying to show off, but if an outsider wanted to cook in a prestigious kitchen like Wangjing No. 1’s—especially a complex dish like liver pâté soup—earning the respect and cooperation of the kitchen staff was necessary. Now, their attitudes had clearly changed.

The restaurant manager spoke up.

“Chef Gu, Mr. Lu will be preparing the liver pâté soup tomorrow. We’ll need your full cooperation.”

Gu Tielin had already lost face. Though he was reluctant, he had no choice but to comply. He just hoped this person would leave for Jiangcheng as soon as he finished making the soup.

“What do you need?” Gu Tielin asked.

“Reserve three stove stations for me tomorrow. I’ll need one assistant chef and one prep cook.”

“No problem.”

“I also need to check the available ingredients.”

“Of course.”

Gu Tielin took Lu Qin to the ingredient storage room. It was huge—over 200 square meters. Most of the ingredients met Lu Qin’s standards, except for two things.

The pork liver wasn’t fatty liver, and the old chicken and duck for the broth weren’t aged enough.

The restaurant manager said, “Mr. Lu, I’ll have the purchasing department get the fatty liver right away. Is that correct?”

“Yes, fatty liver. I’ll go myself.”

Lu Qin didn’t want to risk any mistakes with the ingredients, so he insisted on handling the purchase personally. The restaurant manager arranged for Master Sun from the purchasing department to accompany him to the market.

After Lu Qin left, the remaining kitchen staff stared at the last bit of twice-cooked pork with glowing eyes.

…

Suddenly, they all pounced at once, scrambling for it. Gu Tielin’s eye twitched.

Had these people never eaten twice-cooked pork before?

“Does no one have work to do?”

With a single shout, the crowd scattered like birds. But the plate of twice-cooked pork was already spotless—not even a piece of garlic sprout remained!

…

Lu Qin followed Master Sun to Haishi’s largest market, spending an hour selecting ingredients before returning to the hotel. By the time he arrived at his room, Shen Muyan and Lu Xiyao were already awake.

Shen Muyan had just closed her laptop, likely finishing some work. The little girl was sitting on the bed watching TV. As soon as she saw Lu Qin return, she lit up with excitement. She ran over with tiny steps and jumped into his arms.

“Daddy, hug! I missed you!”

She had only been apart from Lu Qin for half a day, but she already missed him. Lu Qin took a deep sniff.

“Hmm, vanilla-chocolate flavor.”

The little girl covered her mouth in shock. “Daddy, your nose is amazing!”

“Were you allowed to have ice cream today?” Lu Qin narrowed his eyes, curling his fingers as if ready to flick her forehead.

“Mommy let me!”

She immediately pointed at Shen Muyan, betraying her without hesitation. Shen Muyan shot her a sharp look. She had agreed to let the little one have three ice creams, and there was no taking it back now.

“Oh? Was today not allowed? I must’ve remembered wrong.”

Shen Muyan quickly changed the subject.

Sensing an opportunity, the little girl egged Lu Qin on.

“Daddy, flick her! Flick her, not me~!”

And then…

She got a forehead flick from the “rich beauty” herself. Holding her forehead, she finally behaved.

chapters.title: Chapter 171
chapters.content:
Chapter 171 Persian Pastry! Excitement Maxed Out!

The afternoon in Haishi was a bit hot, and the three of them stayed in the hotel room without going out. Little Lu Xiyao, who had just gotten a flick on the forehead, was a bit sulky. Her pouting lips looked like they could hang an oil bottle.

“Yaoyao, want to watch Detective Conan?”

The previous cartoon Tom and Jerry had just finished. Shen Muyan held the remote, unsure of what to play next. But the little one was still upset about getting flicked. Her face was stiff, and she didn’t look like she could be coaxed without at least one popsicle.

“Hmph hmph hmph~!”

The little girl hugged Lu Qin’s arm, curled up on the sofa, and gave Shen Muyan a glance, her stubborn little temper flaring. She never got mad at Lu Qin, but as for Shen Muyan—well, she figured it was fine to act a bit wild around her.

Seeing Lu Xiyao’s intentionally scrunched-up face and her grumbling noises, Shen Muyan couldn’t help but laugh. Her smile bloomed like a flower. This made the little one even more upset. She kicked her little feet and shook Lu Qin’s arm.

“Daddy, look at Mommy~!”

She was still angry, and Mommy was laughing. Lu Qin smiled. He also thought her pouty expression was funny, but he gave Shen Muyan a knowing look. Kids just needed a little coaxing.

“Alright, Yaoyao, don’t be mad. Mommy will take you shopping tonight.”

“Hmph hmph hmph~!”

The little one wasn’t very interested in shopping. Lu Qin quietly took out his phone and sent a WeChat message to Shen Muyan.

Shen Muyan glanced at her phone and then said to Lu Xiyao with a smile, “Haishi has a local orange soda in glass bottles, it’s pretty good. Yaoyao, want some?”

Orange soda?

The little girl’s eyes lit up, and her pretend unhappiness instantly disappeared.

“Yes~!”

Lu Qin and Shen Muyan exchanged smiles. When it came to their little girl, nothing couldn’t be solved with one popsicle. If one didn’t work, then two. Or you could swap popsicles with other snacks or drinks. Shen Muyan made a call to the front desk. The hotel’s dining department had the orange soda. Soon, a server delivered three bottles.

The soda came in glass bottles and was not like the artificial ones from the 70s and 80s that cost 50 cents a bottle. This local Haishi brand was made with real fruit juice and even had orange pulp inside. The ingredients were clean. A small 300ml bottle cost 12 yuan. All three were ice-cold. The soda was tangy and sweet with a fizzy kick—pretty tasty.

“This orange soda is pretty good. We should bring some back,” said Lu Qin after taking a sip.

Xiyao happily gave him a kiss on the cheek.

“Bring more! Yaoyao loves it~!”

Lu Qin playfully tapped her little nose.

Shen Muyan usually didn’t drink sugary beverages, but lately, their little girl had been obsessed with osmanthus oolong milk tea. Even though Lu Qin made it himself, it still had some sugar, which wasn’t ideal for a child.

So Shen Muyan often took half of it to reduce the load. Eventually, she developed a taste for drinks too. But only the ones Lu Qin made—like the osmanthus oolong or iced mung bean smoothie. She didn’t like store-bought ones. This orange soda was okay, but not as good as Lu Qin’s drinks.

After finishing the soda, the two adults watched an episode of Detective Conan with the little one. It was the “Mountain Villa Bandaged Man” episode—a childhood nightmare. It scared the little girl so much she covered her face and buried herself in Lu Qin’s arms.

“Daddy, it’s so scary~! Boo hoo hoo~!”

After twenty minutes, the culprit was caught, and she finally stopped being scared. But then her tummy grumbled.

“You hungry?”

Lu Qin looked down at her.

“Mm~!”

She nodded.

“I’m hungry too,” Shen Muyan said coolly.

Lu Qin thought for a moment and asked, “How about Persian pastry?”

“Persian pastry?”

“Is it new?”

Both Shen Muyan and Lu Xiyao’s eyes lit up.

It already sounded delicious!

The little one blinked and asked, “Daddy, what’s Persian pastry~?”

“It’s a dessert. You mix lard into flour, wrap it with sweet filling, and fry it until crispy. Eat it hot with rock sugar white fungus soup—tastes amazing.”

As soon as Lu Qin said that, mother and daughter instantly craved it.

Persian pastry… sounded so good!

Anticipation was maxed out!

Lu Qin called the manager of the Kailuo Hotel—who was the one that had welcomed them to Haishi. Since the three were guests of Ouyang Lin, the hotel manager was especially attentive.

“Manager Xu, could you check if your kitchen has the ingredients—high-gluten flour, candied dates, and rose preserves? My daughter wants to eat something I’ll cook. I’d like to borrow the kitchen to make a dessert.”

As soon as he heard it was just borrowing the kitchen, Manager Xu happily agreed. He called the kitchen, then quickly called Lu Qin back and told him it was all arranged—just head to the third-floor kitchen.

“You two wait here. I’ll go make the Persian pastry.”

“Okay~!”

Shen Muyan and the little one nodded like obedient bunnies. When Lu Qin arrived at the kitchen, a young staff member was already waiting. It wasn’t meal time, so the kitchen wasn’t crowded. Besides prep and cleaning, only two chefs were present—one pastry chef and one dessert chef. The ingredients Lu Qin needed were common, so the kitchen had them.

As soon as Lu Qin stepped in, a bunch of staff came to watch him. The chef circle was small. Word spread fast. Especially since Lu Qin had just impressed everyone at Wangjing No. 1.

People heard that the chef Ouyang Lin brought from Jiangcheng to make liver paste soup was actually a young guy named Lu. He had made a classic Sichuan dish—twice-cooked pork—and outshone Head Chef Gu. In just half a day, Lu Qin’s reputation had spread.

“Chef Lu, please come in.”

“Manager Xu said you’re making dessert for your daughter? What a great dad!”

The two chefs welcomed Lu Qin in. They were excited to see his skills. They’d only heard about him before—now they could witness it.

Lu Qin noticed them all watching and asked, “You all know me?”

“Haha, we heard what happened at Wangjing No. 1. Chef Gu is one of the top Sichuan chefs in Haishi, and you beat him so easily—amazing!”

Hehe, just lucky, Lu Qin thought, So that’s why.

All the ingredients were already prepared on the counter.

The pastry chef smiled and said, “Chef Lu, here are the ingredients you asked for—high-gluten flour, sugar, candied dates, rose preserves, and lard shortening.”

“Need anything else, just say the word.”

Lu Qin nodded.

“You’re the pastry chef, right? Can you help me steam the dates?”

He had noticed some flour on the middle-aged man’s clothes—clearly a pastry chef.

“My pleasure,” the man said and rolled up his sleeves at once.

Being experienced, he knew that if the dates were to be used as filling, the pits had to be removed before steaming. So he got to work.

Lu Qin began preparing the dough.

chapters.title: Chapter 172
chapters.content:
Chapter 172 “I can’t learn this! There’s no way I can learn this!”

In the back kitchen of Kaile Hotel, the previously busy staff all stopped what they were doing to admire Chef Lu’s skill. The pastry chef was removing the pits from the honey dates and putting them in the steamer.

Lu Qin reminded, “Medium heat is enough, don’t overcook them.”

“Got it, Chef Lu.”

Although the pastry chef had been in the industry for over ten years, he still spoke to Lu Qin with respect, always calling him “Chef Lu.” No other reason—Lu Qin’s skills spoke for themselves.

“Chef Lu, what dessert are you making?” The pastry chef asked.

Even though all the ingredients were laid out in the open, he still couldn’t tell what kind of dessert it would be.

“Persian honey cake,” Lu Qin replied.

“Persian honey cake?”

The crowd was shocked.

“Is it the legendary Persian honey cake? A dessert from Sichuan!”

“I heard it really tests a chef’s skills!”

“Any rare Sichuan dish is bound to be difficult to make!”

“I’m getting excited! I want to see Chef Lu’s technique!”

…

Another pastry chef also stepped in to help and asked, “Chef Lu, is 500g of high-grade flour enough?”

Lu Qin said, “That’s enough, should be good for about 20 cakes.”

“Chef Lu, could you make a few more? We’d love to try some too.”

Lu Qin smiled.

“Sure.”

The pastry chef added another 1000g of flour.

“High-grade flour” refers to specially milled top-quality wheat flour, using only the core part of the wheat. It’s high in gluten, low in impurities, and very white in color. Basically, it’s high-gluten flour—commonly used for toast, bread, muffins, dumpling wrappers, noodles, and so on. The water in the pot came to a boil.

Lu Qin stirred the boiling water with a rolling pin while pouring in the flour. The speed and force of stirring had to be precise—this step required real skill and fundamental technique. The kitchen staff watched closely, whispering among themselves.

Even the first step—scalding the dough—was already too much for many people. The pastry chef and the assistant were both highly focused, borderline trying to learn by watching. Once the flour was fully cooked and non-sticky, Lu Qin evaporated the excess water, then placed the dough on the counter to cool. Meanwhile, the dates had finished steaming.

The pastry chef handed the honey dates to Lu Qin. He used a scraper to mash the dates into a fine paste, mixed in rose preserves, and then added melted lard and sugar. Lu Qin’s hands moved skillfully, and he knew the exact proportions of each ingredient by heart. To everyone else, it looked like his hands were moving in fast-forward.

“Damn! This is insane! Like watching a video at double speed!”

“No way I can learn this! Not in a million years!”

Lu Qin shaped the date filling into uniform balls and set them aside. Then he began kneading and pressing the dough, gradually incorporating five batches of rendered lard into the dough.

Once the oil and dough were fully combined and the texture became soft and non-elastic, he divided the dough into small portions. His technique in tearing the dough portions was so fast it stunned the onlookers. It was like watching kung fu—faster than Wing Chun!

The quality of a pastry chef’s skills could be judged just by how they divided the dough. And Lu Qin’s dough portions? All identical in size and cross-section—just like they came out of a machine. But he did it by hand, and at such speed. The pastry chef and assistant were both floored.

Chef Lu’s skill was outrageous. Stealing his technique? Dream on.

Were they just too old now? Or were the younger generation just absolute monsters? Their whole worldview was crumbling.

Lu Qin then stuffed each dough portion with the date filling, flattened it into a round cake with his palm, and placed them into a deep ladle to fry. During frying, he had to keep shaking the ladle to create a web-like pattern on top—this was the “Persian threads” effect.

Of course, controlling the oil temperature was also crucial. The kitchen staff watched in one wave of shock after another. Every step of making the Persian honey cake required technique. Just watching wouldn’t help—they couldn’t even hope to replicate it!

Lu Qin finished frying them all. Soon, the kitchen was filled with a rich, fragrant aroma.

“I’ll help with the plating!” The pastry chef said.

He grabbed a plate, laid each golden, crispy cake on mulberry leaves, then decorated them with dill flowers and carrot carvings.

With that plating, the whole presentation instantly looked high-end. That plate of ten cakes could easily sell for 1288 yuan!

“You all can try them, just leave 25 for me. I’ll make some white fungus soup to go with it.”

The pastry chef quickly said, “Chef Lu, I actually cooked a pot of white fungus soup earlier—you don’t have to make it.”

“Okay,” Lu Qin nodded.

He packed 25 honey cakes and three bowls of the soup, then went upstairs. Once he left, the kitchen staff swarmed over and stared at the remaining cakes, swallowing hard. The pastry chef and assistant were the first to try—one bite, and their souls trembled.

The crispy threads on the outside paired with the sweet rose-date paste inside—crispy outside, tender inside, absolutely delicious!

So good!

Unbelievably good!

The pastry chef was the most shocked—he had made desserts for years, mostly repeating the same recipes, but breaking through to another level had always been difficult. Especially for traditional Chinese sweets, which are far more complex and demanding than Western pastries.

Lu Qin’s Persian honey cake was on a whole other level—absolutely the pinnacle. The others also tried it—every one of them showed the same amazed reaction!

“Chef Lu is amazing! This Persian honey cake he casually whipped up—best dessert I’ve ever had!”

“Insanely good! Chef Lu is now my idol!”

Everyone praised Lu Qin enthusiastically. No one dared touch the beautifully plated set of cakes—it was just sitting there like a work of art.

Everyone went back to work. In a quiet moment, when the kitchen was empty, a front-desk server walked in.

Three VIP guests had gotten hungry and wanted something to eat quickly. The server came to the kitchen looking for something ready-made. That was when he saw the beautifully plated Persian honey cake and the pot of white fungus soup.

Without asking anyone, he just took it out. The three VIP guests were sitting in the dining room. Since there were no other customers at this time, it was just them. When the server brought out the dishes, the guests lit up at the sight of the beautifully presented honey cake.

“What’s this?”

The server scratched his head.

“It’s a dessert made by our kitchen’s pastry chef—I’m not sure of the exact name.”

“I see… alright, you may go.”

The three guests were indeed hungry, so they immediately started eating.

“Mmm… this is delicious! So crispy and fragrant!”

“Really tasty!”

“Didn’t expect the hotel to have such a skilled pastry chef. Impressive!”

They sipped the white fungus soup, which paired perfectly with the cake—a golden combo.

“Though, the soup is kind of average.”

“Yeah, the soup’s just okay. If it were better, this would be a perfect combo!”

chapters.title: Chapter 173
chapters.content:
Chapter 173 Conan Little Detective, Solve the Case Yourself!

At Kaile Hotel, three VIP guests had just finished eating Persian honey pastries and were still craving more. On the table, three bowls of white fungus and lotus seed soup were left half-eaten. It was unclear whether the taste was lacking, or the three had picky palates.

“These pastries are really something — crispy on the outside, soft and glutinous inside, sweet and fragrant. I’ve had quite a few and still want more!”

“Old Zhu here has lived for over half a lifetime, and this is the first time I’ve eaten this good!”

“Bros, shall we order another round?”

“Absolutely!”

They called for a server. The elder surnamed Zhu said, “Hello, could you please call out your pastry chef from the hotel kitchen?”

Soon, the pastry chef came. When he looked at the plates in front of the guests, he froze. He looked around for the Persian honey pastries but couldn’t find them — turns out the three VIP guests had eaten them all.

“Chef, what kind of pastry is this?”

“Persian honey pastry, a sweet snack from Sichuan.”

“It tastes amazing, we’d like another round!”

The pastry chef looked a bit awkward.

“That wasn’t made by me — it was made by a guest staying at the hotel for his daughter…”

After explaining, the three VIP guests didn’t push further.

“What a shame we don’t know that young man. We really wanted to try the pastries again!”

The chef said, “Guests, how about I prepare you some other desserts? If you’ve finished the white fungus soup, I can bring more.”

“No need. That’ll be all.”

The three guests returned to their rooms.

No wonder the soup didn’t taste as good — it wasn’t made by the same person. So, they figured the chef’s other desserts probably weren’t worth trying either. While waiting for the elevator, Mr. Zhu got a phone call from Ouyang Peng.

“Old Zhu, you three just got off the plane. I know airplane food’s terrible, so I had something prepared for you. I’ll send someone over to pick you up?”

“No need, we’ve already eaten at your Kaile Hotel. We’re heading back to the room for a nap.”

“Oh? Then rest well. I’ll make arrangements for tonight.”

After hanging up, Ouyang Peng called Manager Xu at Kaile Hotel to check in. Hearing that the three guests from Beijing were satisfied with the food, he was relieved — he had been worried the hotel kitchen wouldn’t meet their standards.

____

At Kaile Hotel, Room 8032, 8th Floor, Shen Muyan and Lu Siyao were enjoying Persian honey pastries, in a great mood.

“These pastries are delicious! So different from crystal cakes, but equally tasty!”

Shen Muyan’s eyes sparkled. Leisurely moments and tasty food made life feel perfect. Even though she was a CEO, and this wasn’t a real vacation — after the birthday banquet, she’d have to discuss investment details with Ouyang Lin. She snapped a photo of the pastries and sent it to Fang Ya.

“What’s that? Looks so yummy!!” Fang Ya said.

“Persian honey pastries,” Shen Muyan said.

“What the hell! Your Lu chef made another new treat? But Shen Muyan!! Why would you send a picture? I could forgive you for sneaking a bite, but this level of temptation is just cruel!”

“I like seeing you drooling but unable to eat it.”

“You’re the worst! We’re done. Deleted, friendship over!”

Shen Muyan curved her lips into a smile, glanced at Lu Qin sitting across from her, and felt even more joyful.

Lu Xiyao licked the crumbs off her fingers and eyed the last pastry on the plate, torn between wanting it and holding back.

She looked so adorably hesitant, anyone watching would shout, “Give it to her already!”

“Yao Yao, have you heard the story of Kong Rong letting others have the bigger pear?”

Shen Muyan narrowed her eyes at the little one, thinking stealing it from her would make it taste better. Both Lu Qin and Shen Muyan had told the child the story — teaching that generosity is a virtue.

Lu Xiyao nodded, picked up the last pastry, and handed it to Lu Qin.

“Daddy, you ate the least. This one’s for you!”

Shen Muyan was stunned. Her plan failed.

She underestimated her daughter’s loyalty—clearly Lu Qin ranked higher in Yao Yao’s heart, so she gave the last pastry to him.

“Thank you, Yao Yao.”

Lu Qin took it and pinched her cheek lovingly. He was about to eat it, but the little girl stared at it longingly. Though she gave it away, her eyes and the gulping showed she really wanted it.

“Yao Yao, you eat it. Daddy’s full,” Lu Qin said with a helpless smile.

“But Daddy, you can’t be full. I counted — you only had six. Mommy and I had nine each! You eat it~!”

Yao Yao cared about her dad. She was tempted, but insisted he eat it. Lu Qin wasn’t hungry anyway—he’d had a good lunch. He took a small bite, then pretended to shake his head.

“Yao Yao, Daddy’s really full. I can’t eat more. You have it.”

Yao Yao blinked and looked at him for a moment, then believed he was really full. She swallowed a bit of drool.

“Really? Okay then, I’ll eat it~!”

The little girl opened her mouth and leaned in to take a bite.

“Yao Yao, look outside! There’s a plane!” Shen Muyan called out, pointing at the window.

“A plane?”

The little one’s attention was momentarily stolen. She turned to look out the window. Shen Muyan immediately braced herself on the coffee table, leaning forward—her stunning face only inches from Lu Qin. He caught a whiff of her soft, delicate scent.

She lowered her head and quickly popped the entire pastry into her mouth—the one Lu Qin had just bitten. Her tongue even accidentally touched his finger. Then she swiftly sat back down. She knew she’d brushed against his hand but kept a cool expression, cheeks slightly pink, and quickly chewed and swallowed the pastry.

“Where’s the plane~?”

Lu Xiyao turned back, but the pastry in Lu Qin’s hand had vanished. Lu Qin had a strange look on his face. The little girl stared at him, then turned to Shen Muyan.

“Mommy, was it you?”

Shen Muyan shook her head.

“No.”

Yao Yao’s expression slowly turned into a pout.

“It must have been you~ humph !”

“Do you have any proof?”

She turned to ask Lu Qin, “Daddy, was it Mommy?”

Lu Qin laughed and said, “Detective Conan, solve it yourself.”

The little girl jumped off the sofa and ran to Shen Muyan. She studied her face carefully. Suddenly she shouted, “Mommy, it was you! You’ve still got crumbs on your mouth!”

chapters.title: Chapter 174
chapters.content:
Chapter 174 Leave the Food Truck Here, If I’m Still Hungry I’ll Just Gnaw on It!

Lu Xiyao climbed onto the sofa and stretched out her chubby little hand to scrape off a bit of pastry crumb from the corner of Shen Muyan’s mouth, then popped it into her own mouth. She couldn’t get the last bite, so all she could do was lick the crumbs.

“Mama, you’re a grown-up. How can a grown-up fight with a kid over food~!”

The little one spoke in a soft, sweet voice, ready to scold Shen Muyan in the name of justice. Lu Qin always let her have the good stuff and saved the tastiest bites for her. Of course, she would save good food for Lu Qin too.

But Shen Muyan? She never let her off easy. And sure, she was always the most mischievous when around Shen Muyan. Shen Muyan had a “Yeah I stole your bite, what are you gonna do about it?” look on her face. The little one couldn’t argue.

Still, the more she thought about it, the more wronged she felt. It didn’t feel right unless she cried a little—she owed it to herself. So with a pitiful pout, she quietly curled up into Lu Qin’s arms and started brewing tears. Our little cutie was about to cry.

“Yao Yao,” Lu Qin called softly.

Just hearing his voice was like emotional fuel—she really wanted to cry now. But no matter how much she tried to work herself up, the tears wouldn’t come. Instead, she puffed out a big snot bubble.

“HAHAHAHAHA~!”

Shen Muyan couldn’t hold back her laughter. Lu Qin chuckled too and grabbed a tissue to wipe her nose.

“Waaahhhh~!”

And that did it—the little one finally burst into tears. She was this sad, and yet they still laughed?!

In the end, a popsicle calmed her down. With tears still on her face, she giggled again, snuggling into Lu Qin while watching Detective Conan.

________

At 5 PM, Beijing time, Lu Qin received a WeChat message from Xiaomi reporting on the day’s street vending. After they left in the morning, she rode a three-wheeled cart to the South City farmers market to set up the stall.

They were selling pickled chicken feet, pickled vegetables, crystal jelly cake, mung bean slush, and osmanthus oolong milk tea. Lu Qin had prepared a full day’s worth before leaving. But he underestimated the customers’ appetite.

By 3 PM, everything was sold out except some pickles. The farmers market was usually the turf of old grannies and grandpas, but once the Lu Ji snack cart showed up, it threw everything off balance.

A bunch of loyal customers rushed over after seeing the update in their group chat. There were other small stalls nearby selling homegrown vegetables, pancakes, cold noodles, and sticky rice. When they saw the fancy snack cart, people were curious.

At first, the elders weren’t impressed. They didn’t like new things. For breakfast, they only trusted their usual soybean milk and fried dough shop. For groceries, only their favorite butcher or veggie vendor would do. But soon, a line formed in front of Lu Ji’s food cart.

There were young couples and grandparents with kids.

They were grumbling while lining up.

Why?

Because Lu Qin wasn’t there. There were no staple foods. All they could buy were pickles and pickled chicken feet. Sure, they tasted amazing…

But the regulars who relied on the Lu Ji food stall for all three meals? They couldn’t live off pickled chicken feet alone!

“Come on, man! Milk tea and pickled feet don’t fill you up! Can’t Lu Boss prep some rice or noodles ahead of time?”

“Let’s just get more pickles! His pickles are the real deal. We never used to eat porridge at home—now three pickles per bowl, and we’re scarfing it down!”

“Lu Boss isn’t here! Damn! Good thing there’s crystal jelly cake, or I’d die of sadness!”

“Where’s the fried noodles? No shrimp dumplings either?! Only pickles and chicken feet? Lu Boss better show up tomorrow, or someone’s gonna die! I’m serious!”

Despite the grumbling, people still bought what they could—because the pickles really were that good with porridge. Some curious shoppers finally stopped and wandered over.

“What are they selling? Pickles? Mung bean slush? What’s this crystal jelly cake?”

“Can we try it?”

“3.5 yuan for mung bean slush? That baozi shop over there only charges 2 yuan!”

The folks who got up early to shop were frugal types. They’d compare prices and sizes, not buy impulsively. Xiaomi offered samples to the skeptical aunties and uncles. She’d prepared a tray of bite-sized pickle pieces with toothpicks.

“Please help yourselves,” she said.

With free samples on offer, they dove in.

“Wow! These are spicy and tangy! Delicious!”

“How did they make these so tasty? How much per box?”

“12 yuan per box! And it’s not even a big portion!”

“Can’t you make it cheaper?”

“Honestly, it’s worth it. I’ll take five boxes!”

“You can only buy two! It’s on the sign!”

New customers hesitated at first—trying to bargain, complaining about quantity, or griping about the purchase limit. Then they saw the regulars paying without a word, moving fast.

Watching stock fly off the shelves, the hesitant ones panicked. Some had only paused for a few minutes—next thing they knew, the crystal jelly cake was gone. They gasped and rushed to buy whatever was left. Everything tasted incredible.

Even the 3.5-yuan mung bean slush was way better than the 2-yuan version elsewhere. It was clear why it cost more. At 3:30 PM, Xiaomi reported everything had sold out. They stayed at the same spot all day.

Without Lu Qin there and with fewer hearty food options, some customers were left unfulfilled. So they joked.

“Leave the cart here, I’m still hungry—I’ll just eat the whole thing!”

To calm the crowd, Xiaomi handed out a big bag of mint candies.

“You think a few mint candies can make this right? If Lu Boss isn’t here tomorrow, I’m taking the tires off this cart and steaming them for dinner!”

One customer pocketed a handful of mints, cursed under their breath, and walked off with some pickled goodies.

______

Lu Qin chuckled as he read Xiaomi’s detailed update. They’d made about 25,000 yuan from the morning’s sales. Noticing someone watching him, he looked up—it was Shen Muyan.

“Who are you chatting with?”

“Xiaomi. She’s reporting from the stall. Everything was sold out. Customers were mad there was no main dish—said they’d eat the cart next.”

Shen Muyan laughed.

What a bunch of characters. Lu Qin opened the Penguin group chat—customers were spamming it with memes of skinny, starving people and animals.

____

Half an hour later, Ouyang Lin called and invited Lu Qin and the others to dinner. The dinner was at the restaurant on the 6th floor of Kaile Hotel. Ouyang Lin would bring his wife and daughter.

Tomorrow was Grandpa Ouyang’s birthday banquet, so technically Ouyang Lin had a ton to do. But he really wanted to eat Lu Qin’s cooking and let his family try it too. So he left everything to his assistants and brought the family along.

_______

The two families greeted each other and exchanged pleasantries.

Lu Xiyao sweetly greeted everyone.

“Hello Uncle Ouyang,” “Hello Auntie,” “Hello Sister.”

Ouyang Lin’s daughter was a year older than Lu Xiyao, so Xiyao called her sister. The girl was dressed like a little princess in a white dress, hugging a teddy bear. Maybe because she was shy, she didn’t really respond to greetings.

Ouyang Lin and his wife felt a little awkward. But Lu Qin and Shen Muyan didn’t mind. Soon after sitting down, Ouyang Lin’s daughter got bored and started whining to go home. Lu Xiyao took out her tablet and put on a cartoon—they watched together, and that finally calmed the ‘little princess’ down.

Ouyang Lin’s wife, Wu Rui, only spent a few minutes with Lu Xiyao before falling head over heels. Maybe it was because she was always exhausted from dealing with her own spoiled daughter—who she couldn’t bear to scold—that she felt like she was raising a tiny empress.

Now, comparing her daughter to Lu Qin and Shen Muyan’s?

Lu Xiyao was sweet, polite, and adorable in every way!

She was even a year younger than her own daughter, but still more sensible and likable. Wu Rui couldn’t help but hug her and shower her with affection and questions, eyes full of joy.

The result?

Her own ‘little princess’ got jealous—and burst into loud wails.

chapters.title: Chapter 175
chapters.content:
Chapter 175 Are all kids like this? Why is your daughter so well-behaved!

Ouyang Lin played a little trick. He said he was inviting Lu Qin’s family of three for a meal, but once they arrived at the private dining room, he immediately pulled Lu Qin to the kitchen to “choose dishes.”

Lu Qin was confused. The restaurant on the sixth floor wasn’t the kind where you picked dishes yourself — it had a set menu. When they reached the kitchen, he realized all the ingredients were already prepared.

Plates and baskets of food had been washed, chopped, and neatly arranged with all kinds of seasonings and spices ready to go. Lu Qin shook his head with a helpless smile. So it wasn’t about choosing dishes, it was about cooking them.

Letting a guest cook for the host — the reason Ouyang Lin, the CEO of Shengyang Group, could get away with such an “impolite” thing was simple: he really wanted to eat Lu Qin’s cooking. But Lu Qin couldn’t really refuse.

For someone with maxed-out cooking skills, whipping up a few dishes was no trouble at all.

“All right, Mr. Ouyang, go wait in the private room. The kitchen’s full of smoke.”

“No need, I’ll give you a hand,” Ouyang Lin said, pretending to roll up his sleeves. But his two assistants had already prepped everything, and he really couldn’t help with anything.

The real reason he stayed was because watching Lu Qin cook was a pleasure in itself. The two young kitchen helpers had never seen Lu Qin before. Seeing he was about the same age as them but was calling the shots, they felt a bit unconvinced.

But since Ouyang Lin gave the order, they had no choice but to obey. Once Lu Qin finished a plate of Twice-Cooked Pork, the two helpers were left stunned. This guy was definitely a professional chef!

His cooking skills were insane. His knife work was top-tier, his techniques incredibly smooth—they couldn’t even compete with a bullet train. The dish was bursting with rich fragrance, making mouths water. Then Lu Qin made another dish—Imperial Concubine Chicken Wings.

By this point, the two assistants were looking at Lu Qin with nothing but admiration. Ouyang Lin started getting restless. It smelled too damn good. If he stayed any longer, he’d drool himself to death, so he returned to the private room.

As he pushed the door open, he saw his daughter crying loudly, looking like she’d suffered a huge grievance.

“Duoduo, what’s wrong?”

Ouyang Duoduo was sobbing, snot bubbles forming, while pointing at Wu Rui. Ouyang Lin pulled his daughter into his arms to comfort her and turned to look at Wu Rui—only to find his wife holding someone else’s child like she was her own!

Ouyang Lin was stunned and glared at Wu Rui.

What, you’re ditching our daughter now? Granted, the other kid was adorable, but she wasn’t theirs!

“Yaoyao, come here,”

Shen Muyan called out.

Lu Xiyao ran over happily.

Wu Rui, realizing her mistake, reached out to comfort her daughter. But Ouyang Duoduo slapped her hand away, crying and muttering, “Bad mommy!”

Duoduo had clearly been a bit spoiled. Despite Ouyang Lin and his wife trying to coax her for over ten minutes, she kept crying. The private room remained noisy with her sobs and screams.

Ouyang Lin looked awkwardly at Shen Muyan. “Sorry, President Shen. What a mess.”

Shen Muyan shook her head.

“It’s okay, kids are like that.”

Ouyang Lin glanced at the well-behaved Lu Xiyao and twitched his lips.

Kids are like that?

Then why is your daughter so obedient?

Shen Muyan leaned down and whispered something in Lu Xiyao’s ear, making the little girl’s eyes light up. She nodded repeatedly. Soon, the waiter came in with two vanilla ice creams.

Lu Xiyao took them and said loudly, “Wow! It’s my favorite — vanilla ice cream!”

Her cheerful tone drowned out Duoduo’s crying.

Duoduo had been crying for a while and was getting tired, but she was stubborn and didn’t want to stop. Now interrupted, she hiccuped and looked over in a daze. She saw Lu Xiyao open one ice cream and start eating happily.

“So yummy~! Mommy, have a bite too!”

Lu Xiyao held the ice cream up.

Shen Muyan took a bite and smiled blissfully, “Mmm, so sweet and cold.”

Duoduo’s eyes turned red with envy.

She usually got whatever she wanted, but that didn’t mean she cherished it. Now seeing another kid eating it made her crave it even more. She ran over and stared longingly at Lu Xiyao. She thought Xiyao would just give her the other ice cream.

But Lu Xiyao ignored her and kept enjoying her own, looking satisfied. Duoduo got anxious, but she wasn’t close with Shen Muyan or Lu Xiyao, so she didn’t dare grab it. She turned to look at her parents with pleading eyes that said, “Duoduo wants ice cream too.”

Ouyang Lin was just about to speak when Shen Muyan waved at Duoduo.

“Duoduo, do you want ice cream?”

Duoduo nodded.

“Yaoyao has another one. Do you want it?”

“Yes!”

“But it’s Yaoyao’s. If you want it, what should you say?”

Duoduo froze for a few seconds and bit her lip. She didn’t know what to say — she hadn’t been taught well. Shen Muyan was sure the Ouyang couple had tried, but clearly hadn’t taught her effectively. Not every kid was like hers—smart and quick to learn.

So Shen Muyan said gently, “Duoduo, you should say, ‘Yaoyao, can I please have one of your ice creams?’”

“Go on, say it nicely.”

She gave Duoduo a light push. Caught off guard and tempted by the treat, Duoduo didn’t throw a tantrum. She trotted over and awkwardly said, “Yaoyao, can I please have one of your ice creams?”

Lu Xiyao licked her own and replied generously, “Sure! Here you go.”

She handed the other one to Duoduo. Duoduo put down her stuffed bear and took the ice cream, trying to unwrap it. Ouyang Lin and Wu Rui finally understood. It wasn’t about coaxing—one well-timed treat and good guidance had solved the whole issue.

Ouyang Lin gave Shen Muyan a grateful look. No wonder Lu Xiyao was so well-behaved. It was all in the way she was raised.

Ouyang Lin quickly added, “Duoduo, what does Daddy always tell you? When someone gives you something, what do you say?”

Duoduo had a little moment of realization and whispered, “Thank you.”

Lu Xiyao nodded while licking her ice cream, “You’re welcome, Duoduo-jiejie. Eat it quickly before it melts.”

Being a level-four ice cream expert, Xiyao noticed Duoduo struggling with the wrapper and offered, “Want me to help?”

Duoduo nodded.

“Yes, thank you.”

Xiyao expertly unwrapped the ice cream and handed it to her.

“Wanna watch cartoons?”

“What kind of cartoons?”

“Conan.”

“Sure, let’s watch.”

And just like that, peace returned to the room. The two little ones sat quietly, eating their ice creams and watching Detective Conan. The Ouyang couple couldn’t help but admire Lu Xiyao more and more. Their daughter was a year older, but clearly far behind in maturity.

Wu Rui was especially envious of Shen Muyan—not only was she stunning, but her daughter was also adorable and clever. Wu Rui looked around and suddenly realized—wait, where was Shen Muyan’s husband? Didn’t Ouyang Lin drag him away to order food?

Why was Ouyang Lin back alone?

She was about to ask when the door opened. Lu Qin walked in wearing an apron, followed by two servers pushing food carts.

Wu Rui froze and exclaimed, “Mr. Lu, why did you go cook?”

chapters.title: Chapter 176
chapters.content:
Chapter 176 A Husband Like This Is Hard to Find, the Nation’s Ultimate Good Husband!

When Wu Rui saw Lu Qin return to the private room wearing an apron, she reproachfully looked at her husband, Ouyang Lin.

“Mr. Lu is our guest. How could you make him cook?”

Wu Rui knew that Ouyang Lin had invited Lu Qin over to make liver soup for Old Master’s birthday banquet. However, tonight, they were the hosts, so how could they let the guest cook? That was too rude!

Lu Qin took off his apron and smiled.

“I volunteered to do it. My daughter loves the dishes I make.”

Ouyang Lin nodded in agreement in his heart, thinking Mr. Lu was very good at handling social situations and knew how to speak appropriately.

They all sat down, and the waiter began bringing out the dishes.

Soon, Wu Rui smelled the rich aroma of the food. Her eyes lit up, and she smiled, saying, “Mr. Lu’s cooking is really good. These dishes must taste amazing!”

Ouyang Lin thought to himself, it’s not just amazing!

Twice-cooked pork, imperial chicken wings, mapo tofu, spicy chicken cubes, fish-flavored shredded pork… all classic and familiar Sichuan dishes that looked very appetizing. The two little ones, smelling the food, stopped watching cartoons. Without anyone telling them, they eagerly ran to the table.

Ouyang Duoduo was much quieter today, likely influenced by the example set by the other child sitting nearby.

“Mom, I’m hungry!”

Ouyang Duoduo tugged at the edge of the table, looking at Wu Rui. Wu Rui was pleased to see her daughter taking the initiative to eat. Usually, they had to chase after her to feed her.

“Duoduo, let’s wash our hands. After washing, we can eat,” said Shen Muyan, standing up.

Wu Rui, gazing at the beautifully presented dishes on the table, swallowed her saliva, already unable to wait to dig in. But since Shen Muyan had taken the children to wash their hands, she had to wait for them to return. Two minutes later, they were back. Finally, they could eat.

Lu Xiyao and Ouyang Duoduo sat together. Ouyang Lin saw how well-behaved his daughter was at the dinner table. She didn’t even hug her teddy bear anymore. He thought it was truly not easy. Before, she would always have to play with her toys or watch TV while eating.

Children really shouldn’t be indulged with such habits. Now, this was much more pleasant to watch. Everyone began eating. Wu Rui took her first bite of Lu Qin’s cooking and was deeply impressed. It was so delicious!

After two or three bites, she could no longer maintain her dignified demeanor and started eating with abandon. Ouyang Lin had anticipated that his wife would behave like this. No one could resist the temptation of such delicious food. Wu Rui finished a whole bowl of rice before slowing down.

Ouyang Lin noticed that his wife was eating faster and more than he was, and chuckled.

“It’s good, right? You’ve never had anything like this before, right?”

Wu Rui rolled her eyes at Ouyang Lin, ignoring his teasing, and said to Lu Qin and Shen Muyan, “Sorry, Mr. Lu, but your dishes are just too delicious!”

“If you think it’s good, feel free to eat more,” Lu Qin replied.

Wu Rui nodded.

One bowl of rice wasn’t enough. At least two bowls. She took a bite of the spicy chicken, and the perfect combination of spicy and fragrant chicken meat, along with the rich seasonings, made it the best spicy chicken she’d ever had. Every dish Lu Qin made was so delicious. His culinary skills were extraordinary!

Wu Rui was even more envious of Shen Muyan. A husband like this was nearly impossible to find, a national treasure of a good husband. Looking at her own husband, aside from having some money, he didn’t seem to have much else.

The two little ones ate quietly, very focused. The food was so delicious that they couldn’t stop. From time to time, Lu Xiyao would pick up food for Ouyang Duoduo, taking care of her sister.

Ouyang Duoduo wasn’t quite skilled with chopsticks yet. She wobbled as she tried to pick up some fish-flavored shredded pork, but only managed to get a third of it in the bowl. It was a mess. If she tried to pick up a chicken wing, she’d probably have to use her hands to help.

At seven years old, she should have mastered using chopsticks by now, but Ouyang Duoduo hadn’t quite gotten the hang of it. This could only be attributed to the fact that the parents had been too indulgent, feeding her whenever she asked.

In contrast, Lu Xiyao was already very skilled with chopsticks, not wasting any food at all.

Wu Rui admired her, saying, “Xiyao, you’re really good at using chopsticks.”

Wu Rui exchanged a glance with Ouyang Lin. The couple both realized they hadn’t been good parents. Others had raised an excellent child, while their own child still needed a lot of work and had a rather big temper. The comparison was too obvious.

Meeting Lu Qin’s family had given Ouyang Lin and Wu Rui a strong sense of awareness about their own child’s education. Duoduo had to be taught well, with Lu Xiyao as a model.

After the meal, the food was all finished, and the plates and bowls were so clean that they could practically reflect light. The waiter came to clean up, surprised to see the dishes gleaming.

It was the first time Ouyang Lin and Wu Rui had seen their daughter eat so much, a whole and a half bowls of rice, along with a lot of dishes, without needing to be fed or coaxed.

Without a doubt, Lu Qin’s cooking had magical powers. After dinner, Wu Rui couldn’t help but ask Lu Qin and Shen Muyan for advice on child-rearing. They didn’t ask for much. They just wanted Duoduo to be as well-behaved and sensible as Lu Xiyao. They talked for almost half an hour.

Ouyang Lin and Wu Rui were shocked to find out that Lu Xiyao had mostly been raised by Lu Qin. Shen Muyan, on the other hand, was often busy with work and didn’t spend much time with the child.

Shen Muyan felt a bit awkward and regretted not spending more time with Xiyao in the past. Ouyang Lin couldn’t help but give Lu Qin a thumbs up. In terms of fatherhood, he had to learn from Lu Qin!

As for cooking, there was no way he could learn it—he could never match that level. Ouyang Lin looked at the time and apologized.

“Tomorrow is Old Master’s birthday banquet, and I still have some things to arrange. The liver soup tomorrow will be entrusted to you, Mr. Lu…”

“Got it,” Lu Qin nodded.

Ouyang Duoduo had developed a little friendship with Lu Xiyao and felt reluctant to part with her. After some back-and-forth, they were finally coaxed into leaving by Ouyang Lin and Wu Rui.

Before they left, Ouyang Duoduo even gave her toy bear to Lu Xiyao. This surprised Ouyang Lin and Wu Rui, as the little bear was Duoduo’s favorite toy.

______

After Ouyang Lin and Wu Rui left, it was still early. Lu Qin and Shen Muyan took their little one out to enjoy the night scenery of Haishi.

The best view of Haishi’s night scenery was on “Moon Island.” Lu Qin drove the M7 and took the mother and daughter to the destination. After parking, they walked along the riverbank, holding hands. The night breeze was gentle, and the moonlight shimmered on the river, blending with the lights, creating a beautiful scene.

Many pedestrians passed by, and the high-profile looks of Lu Qin’s family caught the attention of a lot of people. Many were envious, thinking that this family was so dreamy, almost like something out of a manga or novel.

Passing a boutique selling tea and alcohol, they happened to have high-quality osmanthus oolong tea. Lu Qin went inside and bought two pounds. It was a bit expensive, over five hundred a pound, and the gift box probably cost around one hundred. However, as the boss, Lu Qin wasn’t short on money. He bought the tea to make milk tea for his little one tomorrow.

After more than an hour walking around Moon Island, the three of them returned to the hotel. When they got back, they realized the hotel suite only had one large luxurious bed. Both Lu Qin and Shen Muyan were a little embarrassed.

Shen Muyan grabbed her clothes from the luggage and took the little one to shower first. Half an hour later, both mother and daughter came out of the bathroom, smelling fragrant and looking fresh.

Shen Muyan wore a bathrobe, with a black silk camisole underneath. She hadn’t expected to be sharing a room with Lu Qin, so she wore her usual sleepwear. It was quite seductive, given her figure.

Lu Qin dared not look too much. He grabbed a towel and his clothes to head to the bathroom. But before he could close the door, Shen Muyan rushed in.

“What’s wrong?”

“Nothing.”

Shen Muyan, with a flushed face, quickly took down a set of black lace lingerie from the shower curtain rail. She had forgotten that Lu Qin still had to shower after her.

“I didn’t see anything,” Lu Qin quickly said.

“…”

chapters.title: Chapter 177
chapters.content:
Chapter 177 “Mom, why is your face so red?”

Lu Qin really didn’t see it, he had just entered the bathroom. As an honest person, he said an honest thing. However, in Shen Muyan’s ears, it meant the opposite.

Lu Qin received a beautiful eye roll, and a fragrant breeze passed by as the bathroom door closed. Lu Qin locked the bathroom door to prevent the two ladies outside from accidentally barging in and seeing his eight-pack abs.

Inside the bathroom, the scent of a small orchid-scented shower gel lingered, fresh and slightly sweet, like a morning garden. He filled the tub with water and got in to shower. This luxury suite also had a massage bathtub.

After the water filled up, Lu Qin comfortably lay in it, his posture relaxed. After showering, Lu Qin came out into the room. The big CEO and the little cutie were already lying in bed, wrapped in an air-conditioned blanket, watching cartoons on a tablet.

When Lu Xiyao saw Lu Qin come out, she happily shouted, “Daddy, time to sleep~!”

The little one was carefree, and it was rare for both her mom and dad to sleep on the same bed, which made her feel very happy. Lu Qin nodded slightly but didn’t make eye contact with Shen Muyan. He grit his teeth and lay down on the bed.

Though there was a little one between them, they could still indirectly feel each other’s body warmth. Shen Muyan’s heart started to race, and the slender, jade-white fingers of one hand clutched the blanket tightly.

Although both of them knew nothing would happen with the little one in between, it was still an unusual feeling to be lying on the same bed. After watching a few minutes of the cartoon, Lu Qin relaxed. He was a gentleman, and even with a stunningly beautiful woman lying next to him, he wouldn’t do anything inappropriate.

He had a clear conscience, focusing on watching “Detective Conan.” The episode was about the legend of the mist dog, which scared the little one so much that she didn’t dare to open her eyes.

But Shen Muyan wasn’t in the mood for the scary cartoon. Her mind was blank, and her heart was in turmoil. This feeling was very strange. After so many years of marriage, she had never experienced such a feeling. She had always focused on her work.

She trusted Lu Qin to take care of the child at home. She used to be all about work, but after the divorce, she realized something was missing in her life without Lu Qin and the little one.

As she sought to fill the void, she gradually discovered the goodness of Lu Qin and the little one. Her work-focused brain gradually got eaten away by the little zombie, and her love-driven brain started to bloom. Now, her heart felt like a peaceful lake disturbed by a stone, sending ripples she couldn’t calm.

Although Shen Muyan knew nothing would happen that night, she couldn’t help but hope that something would, even just a little bit. Maybe they could send the little one to the front desk for the night and return her in the morning. That would leave the luxurious bed to her and the boss…

Her inner thoughts gradually became vivid, and Shen Muyan’s exquisite, cool face flushed.

“Mom, why is your face so red~?”

The little one, sensing something unusual, raised her little head and noticed Shen Muyan’s abnormality.

Lu Qin also looked over. Shen Muyan felt awkward but acted calm on the outside:

“This wolf in this episode is really scary, Mom was frightened.”

Lu Xiyao, with clear eyes, said, “But Mom, that wasn’t a wolf, it was a mist dog, and clever Conan solved the case. The legend of the mist dog is made up.”

“Oh, it wasn’t a wolf, it was a dog! Then I’m not scared anymore.”

Shen Muyan casually brushed it off, her mind erasing the blurred images. They watched one more episode of Conan. The little one yawned, sleepy.

Lu Qin and Shen Muyan decided to let the little one sleep early. They turned off the light. The little one nestled into Lu Qin’s arms, closing her eyes comfortably. It didn’t take long before she fell asleep. Her soft little feet, however, were pressing against Shen Muyan’s flat belly.

The sleeping position was quite touching. Shen Muyan was speechless. This little one, her head facing Lu Qin, but leaving her rear end for Shen Muyan!

Shen Muyan gently swatted the little one’s bottom and closed her eyes. Her sleep was light, and it took a long time for her to fall asleep. But for some reason, perhaps because Lu Qin was lying beside her, she fell asleep quickly that night.

Lu Qin didn’t have to set up a stall, and his biological clock shifted later. The next day, he woke up at 7 AM. He slept really well. Lu Qin opened his eyes slightly and sniffed a faint, pleasant fragrance, like flowers just blooming—fresh and enchanting.

It was the smell of body scent mixed with a bit of shampoo. It smelled wonderful.

A bit intoxicating.

The little one didn’t have this scent.

Lu Qin gently raised his head and saw a soft, black shiny head of hair resting on his chest. What was originally the little one’s spot had been taken over.

Where was the little one?

Lu Qin noticed the little one had moved to the foot of the bed, hugging Shen Muyan’s smooth, delicate calf, her little face rubbing against it. She must have been dreaming of a delicious, fragrant chicken drumstick.

The beautiful, rich woman lay like a lazy little cat in Lu Qin’s embrace, her long, slightly curled lashes fluttering, probably dreaming too. One of her hands was caressing Lu Qin’s abs, a chocolate that had been fondled for a long time.

Lu Qin: “…”

Shen Muyan lay like a piece of smooth, white jade on top of Lu Qin. Her figure was sculpted according to the golden ratio by the goddess Nuwa, and how alluring and seductive it was didn’t need to be said.

Lu Qin, in his prime, found this scene harder to control than an AK47. There were important things to do today, and Lu Qin had to get up.

He pushed against the bed with both hands, trying to get up, but as soon as he raised himself by 45 degrees, the lazy cat climbed back on top.

Lu Qin’s defined abs provided excellent friction, and her delicate hand climbed back up like it was chocolate. Her head rested on Lu Qin’s chin, and her seductive red lips whispered, “Don’t move, I’m still sleepy~!” before lazily continuing to sleep.

“Don’t move?”

Staying still is like being a turtle!

Lu Qin had important things to do today.

This rich woman was testing him like this?

What man could withstand such a test?

Lu Qin raised an eyebrow and, on impulse, smacked her perfectly curved backside. After doing so, he realized he had overreacted. That was a crime!

Amitabha, I’ve sinned.

The rich woman, feeling the odd sensation of pain, slowly opened her beautiful eyes, her slender, curled lashes trembling.

At that moment, Lu Qin had already gently placed the little one’s hand on her delicate backside. It had to be framed as an accident. The case was a bit complicated.

The rich woman woke up and found herself on top of Lu Qin, holding onto his abs, unwilling to let go. Her eyes met his deep, dark gaze for a moment, and her face immediately turned red.

This was definitely not intentional, she had fallen asleep, and her body was no longer under her control. Shen Muyan never imagined that she had rolled onto Lu Qin. But the sensation of his abs felt really good.

The atmosphere became a little awkward. They were both adults, and more importantly, they had once been married, so thinking about it, it wasn’t a big deal. Besides, there was nothing going on. They had to widen their hearts and open their minds. Yeah, everything was for the little one.

Shen Muyan’s temperature gradually cooled, but then, there was a slight fiery feeling. It felt like she had been slapped. Shen Muyan raised her head in confusion, looking at Lu Qin.

Lu Qin’s expression remained normal, but he made a gesture with his mouth, indicating that she should look for herself. Shen Muyan then saw the little hand pressing on the spot.

“Stop moving while sleeping!”

Shen Muyan stretched out her hand and lightly slapped the little one’s bottom, taking her revenge.

chapters.title: Chapter 178
chapters.content:
Chapter 178 Boss Lu’s Tactics are Deep!

Lu Qin dragged the little one to the correct position and covered her with the blanket. The two adults had lost their sleepiness and were getting ready to get up.

Shen Muyan had just propped herself up when the air-conditioned blanket slipped off. She was sleeping without her robe, wearing a black silk spaghetti-strap nightdress, her snow-white, delicate skin exposed, making the air around her seem to vibrate.

Lu Qin found that his gaze was no longer under his control, as it wandered freely and unabashedly, appreciating the sight. Her smooth black hair cascaded down her jade-like shoulders, the perfect curve of her breasts on either side, like hands beckoning him, saying, “Come over here, come over here.”

And that slender, delicate waist of the rich woman, tsk tsk, how could anything withstand such temptation?

Sensing the intense gaze from Boss Lu, Shen Muyan realized something was off and instinctively pulled the blanket up to cover herself. But it only covered the top, while her legs, smooth and white like jade, slipped out from beneath.

The silk nightdress barely covered her shapely figure, leaving just enough fabric behind – truly, such a small piece of material for a country to save. Lu Qin managed to control himself but felt the blood rush to his head. He quickly turned and rushed to the bathroom.

Puchi!

Seeing Lu Qin’s flustered state, Shen Muyan felt a sense of victory, even though her face was as red as a monkey’s butt.

Half an hour later, Lu Xiyao was picked up by Lu Qin and sent to the bathroom to wash up. Breakfast was delivered to their room by a hotel staff member.

VIP treatment.

The three of them sat at the dining table to eat breakfast. As for the events before getting out of bed, both Lu Qin and Shen Muyan tacitly chose to pretend nothing had happened. Everything seemed normal.

“Daddy, this is not as good as the crystal shrimp dumplings and green bean paste you make.”

Lu Xiyao scooped up some yogurt and oatmeal, speaking softly and sweetly.

“This cranberry bread isn’t very good either. I want stir-fried noodles.”

Shen Muyan was also picky. The five-star hotel breakfast was nothing compared to Lu Qin’s stir-fried noodles.

“I don’t have time to make it today. Just make do with this,” Lu Qin said, peeling two eggs and handing them to Shen Muyan and the little one.

“Eh~? Mom, you have eggs today too~?”

Lu Xiyao, being perceptive, noticed that in the past, Lu Qin would only peel eggs for her, but today he had also peeled one for the rich woman.

Shen Muyan raised an eyebrow and pinched the little one’s cheek.

“What’s wrong? Don’t I deserve to have an egg peeled for me?”

The little one stuck out her tongue and lowered her head to eat the yogurt and oatmeal. Shen Muyan felt inexplicably upset. She glanced at Lu Qin, wondering why he never peeled eggs for her before but was doing it now.

Can’t this man be consistent?

Lu Qin: ……?

After breakfast, Lu Qin took out a piece of paper and gave the little one forty addition and subtraction problems with numbers less than 20.

Spending some time on learning every day was a good habit. The little one had already outgrown counting with her fingers and had some basic arithmetic skills, using methods like borrowing and making tens to solve simple problems.

“Lu Xiyao, same as usual. If you get them all right within 15 minutes, you get an ice cream.”

Lu Qin turned on the stopwatch on his phone, ready to time her.

“Okay~! I’m going to eat ice cream~!”

“Good luck~!”

Lu Xiyao cheered herself on and began answering the questions without distraction. Motivated by the ice cream reward, the little one answered the questions very seriously, checking her answers once she finished.

The checking step was crucial, if she made one mistake, the ice cream was gone.

“Daddy, I’m done~!”

Lu Qin checked the time. It took her 13 minutes and 43 seconds. Hmm, she had improved by 30 seconds compared to the last time. The little one was improving quickly.

Lu Qin turned off the stopwatch and switched to the photo album, opening a screenshot of the stopwatch that had been prepared in advance.

“Ah, what a shame, Lu Xiyao, this time it took you 15 minutes and 26 seconds. So close to getting the ice cream!”

The little one glanced at the phone screen, showing a disappointed expression, but she didn’t get discouraged.

“I’ll try harder~!”

Lu Xiyao encouraged herself, all for the ice cream!

Shen Muyan’s lips parted slightly, her face showing a strange expression as she glanced at Lu Qin.

Boss Lu’s tactics are deep!

Then, Shen Muyan asked the little one to recite a poem.

After math, Chinese couldn’t be left out.

“Lu Xiyao, let’s see if you can recite a Tang poem. If you can’t, I’ll reward you with an ice cream~!”

Lu Xiyao blinked her clear, naive eyes, nodding her little head.

“Mom, give me an easier one.”

“Alright, let’s go with Wang Wei’s ‘Deer Park.’”

The little one’s eyes lit up with excitement.

“I know this one! ‘Deer Park,’ by Wang Wei, Tang dynasty, ‘In the empty mountains, no one is seen, but the sound of human voices is heard. The returning light enters the deep forest and shines again on the green moss.’”

“Yay~! I did it~! Is it right~? Mom, where’s the ice cream~!”

Shen Muyan smiled.

“Lu Xiyao, you’re great! You recited it really well!”

“Ice cream?”

“If you had failed to recite it, then you’d get an ice cream. But since you recited it well, what do you need the ice cream for?”

Lu Xiyao: “?????”

…

At 10 AM, Ouyang Lin personally came to invite Lu Qin. Before the birthday banquet, Ouyang Peng wanted to try Lu Qin’s liver paste soup. Ouyang Lin had already briefed Ouyang Peng on Lu Qin’s situation.

But when Ouyang Peng saw Lu Qin, he couldn’t help but comment on how young he was. He truly couldn’t believe that such a young chef could make the top-tier Sichuan dish, “liver paste soup.”

“Thank you, Master Lu,” Ouyang Peng said politely.

In the kitchen, it was crowded. Everyone wanted to see Master Lu make the liver paste soup—it was a rare sight. Gu Tielin was also among them. Initially, the liver paste soup was assigned to him, but after two failed attempts, and one partial success, Ouyang Peng tasted it and shook his head in disappointment.

He said, “You’re not at that level yet.”

That one sentence made Gu Tielin feel deeply ashamed. But Gu Tielin also knew that making the liver paste soup was a difficult task. It was a national banquet dish, so it wasn’t shameful to fail.

Ironically, now a young man had come to make the liver paste soup. Gu Tielin couldn’t help but feel a little slapped in the face, a second time. Although embarrassed, Gu Tielin secretly took out a small notebook and pen, planning to take notes on the key points and details when Lu Qin made the soup.

Since Lu Qin hadn’t said it was forbidden to watch, it was fine to learn secretly. Others in the kitchen also did the same, some even filming the entire process with their phones, using 4K resolution and one billion pixels. A good phone with a large memory was really impressive!

Once Lu Qin started preparing the broth, he began handling the pig liver. The cleaver came out with full force!

Wow, the mystery was solved!

Master Lu was definitely not a chef—he was the number one knife master in the martial world!

Chop, chop, chop!

The cleaver moved so fast that it was invisible to the naked eye!

All anyone could see were the flashes of the blade. Lu Qin chopped up a whole block of pig liver weighing 1.2 kg into liver paste, and it only took as long as a high-speed blender!

chapters.title: Chapter 179
chapters.content:
Chapter 179 Details! It’s All About the Details!

Seeing this scene, Gu Tielin, the head chef of the Sichuan cuisine at Wangjing No. 1, felt his eyelids twitch uncontrollably. He leaned in closer and saw, in just a few minutes, Lu Qin had finely minced an entire piece of pig liver into delicate liver paste.

Even when making minced pork, it was never that fast. What was more, there was also the task of removing the membranes from the pig liver. Gu Tielin knew that this level of knife skill could not be developed through time alone, it required talent.

Yesterday, when he saw Lu Qin’s knife skills demonstrated in the making of twice-cooked pork, he thought that was all he was capable of.

That level of skill made him feel ashamed.

But now, seeing the incredible knife skills displayed in the liver paste soup, alright, he dared not compare himself to him anymore. He could only look up in awe. It was terrifying!

Master Chen, the Cantonese cuisine chef, was equally astonished. Initially, he thought that hiring such a young chef from Jiangcheng to help with the banquet was a desperate, last-minute decision. But now, it was clear—this young man was a true master.

While he was just cooking food, this man was making art. Lu Qin filtered the liver paste twice, added chicken broth infused with spring onions and ginger to season it, and then added salt and pepper.

In another bowl, he mixed egg whites and bean powder into a paste and poured it into the liver paste. Lu Qin was fully focused, unaware of the rustling sounds around him as people took notes and several others secretly filmed with their phones.

This liver paste soup, representing the pinnacle of Sichuan cuisine, was a rare opportunity to see the full process. Truly, they felt fortunate. But learning to make it was another matter entirely.

Lu Qin opened the steamer, and the smooth, jelly-like liver paste lay in the clear broth, looking absolutely beautiful. Gu Tielin’s heart raced. So this was the success he had been chasing—the liver paste soup that looked infinitely better than the failed version he had made.

The liver paste soup was served to Ouyang Peng. Ouyang Peng eagerly tasted a spoonful, and then, he gasped in admiration.

“Mmm, this is it!”

“The texture is as delicate as tofu, incredibly tender, silky, and rich. The broth is crystal clear, and it’s absolutely breathtaking!”

Ouyang Peng looked at Lu Qin.

“This liver paste soup is even more delicious than I expected. Master Lu, you’re truly a gifted talent. I’ll leave the banquet tonight in your hands!”

Ouyang Lin had already told the old man about Lu Qin’s background. Lu Qin had never formally studied cooking, his culinary skills were self-taught, which was truly impressive. Even chefs like Gu Tielin, with recognized titles, could not make the liver paste soup. That was why Ouyang Peng said Lu Qin was extraordinarily talented.

Lu Qin simply nodded lightly.

“It’s no trouble.”

Although he hadn’t discussed payment for helping with the liver paste soup, considering the wealth of Shengyang Group, the tip would certainly be generous. Ouyang Peng wanted to add the liver paste soup to the banquet menu for a reason.

When he was young, without money, he was shivering outside a restaurant. Originally, he was destined to eat scraps, but a chef took pity on him and gave him a bowl of liver paste soup.

As everyone knew, non-spicy Sichuan cuisine was extremely expensive, and ordinary people couldn’t afford it.

But in his time of poverty, Ouyang Peng had actually gotten to eat liver paste soup—it felt like a dream. Therefore, Ouyang Peng had developed a special attachment to liver paste soup.

For lunch, the little one wanted stir-fried rice noodles, and the rich woman wanted Mapo tofu. Lu Qin instructed a staff member from the kitchen to prepare the ingredients.

“Alright, Master Lu, I’ll get on it right now.”

The kitchen staff now revered Lu Qin. After hearing his instructions, they excitedly began preparing the ingredients.

They bumped into other people and said excitedly, “Master Lu asked me to prepare the rice noodles, tofu, and pork… He must be making something else! I can’t wait to see!”

“We’ve already seen his twice-cooked pork and liver paste soup—those were incredible! I wonder how his other dishes compare. I’m so excited!”

“If his other dishes are at the same level, he’ll be unbeatable! I’m going to kneel before him!”

The ingredients Lu Qin needed were neatly arranged on the cutting board. The kitchen quickly became crowded again as people wanted to witness the master at work.

“What are you all doing? Don’t you have work to do?”

A sudden loud shout made everyone shrink back. Gu Tielin and Master Chen, the two head chefs, entered the kitchen with cold expressions. The staff dared not say a word—the head chefs were in charge, and they had to obey. There was no more looking allowed. Everyone scattered and got back to work. The crowded kitchen suddenly emptied.

Once the staff had been sent away, Gu Tielin and Master Chen changed their expressions and began watching Lu Qin cook with great interest.

Lu Qin nodded to the two chefs. He had always adhered to open cooking—anyone could watch, and anyone could learn. If they learned, that was their own skill. But Lu Qin knew that even if someone learned, making food with the same level of skill, with the same flavors, was impossible. Because he was operating with technology.

It was what people called “playing with cheats.”

How could they catch up?

Soon, two servings of stir-fried noodles were ready. The rich, smoky flavor from the wok made both Gu Tielin and Master Chen swallow involuntarily.

These two had been in the kitchen for decades and had smelled every type of aroma, but when Lu Qin’s stir-fried noodles came out, their mouths watered uncontrollably. It was ridiculously good!

Even if they added a pound of fragrance-enhancing powder, it wouldn’t smell this good. Once the stir-fried noodles were plated, there was still a little left, so Lu Qin put it in a small bowl.

Then, he made a dish of Mapo tofu—one of the most popular dishes from Sichuan, a true gift to the world’s best comfort food rankings. Gu Tielin saw Lu Qin’s master-level Mapo tofu and became anxious. He trembled as he took out his notebook and began jotting down notes.

“Mapo tofu must use soft tofu. Tofu made with pickled mustard greens doesn’t taste as good, and using old tofu makes it too tough…”

“Before adding tofu to the pan, put in some salt to remove the smell and make the tofu more firm…”

“Details, it’s all about the details!”

Master Chen, watching Lu Qin’s culinary art, suddenly nudged Gu Tielin with his elbow.

“Hey, Old Gu, Master Lu is using soft tofu. Why are you using old tofu?”

A line was drawn through the notebook. Gu Tielin grumbled, “Soft tofu is easy to break, while old tofu holds together better. The texture is just a bit worse, you don’t get it.”

“Oh, I see. So, Old Gu, your skills aren’t up to par. Well, never mind.” Master Chen chuckled.

Gu Tielin nearly spat blood, glaring fiercely at Master Chen.

But watching Lu Qin cook had been an eye-opening experience. He had learned many small details and techniques that could raise his own level of cooking. As for control of heat, seasoning ratios, and wok skills… that was beyond him.

Lu Qin had initially planned to make twice-cooked pork, but he worried it would be too filling and leave the rich woman and the little one too full. So, he opted for sour and spicy shredded potatoes and made a bowl of liver paste soup with the ready-made broth. Lu Qin called over a waiter.

“Please take this to room 888.”

The waiter walked out of the kitchen with the tray. Lu Qin took off his apron and nodded to Gu Tielin and Master Chen before leaving as well. On the cutting board, there was still a small bowl of stir-fried rice noodles, steaming hot.

Gu Tielin and Master Chen exchanged glances and excitedly walked over. A few seconds later, the kitchen echoed with loud exclamations.

“This is ridiculously good!”

“I can’t believe it! Even stir-fried noodles taste so good!”

The waiter walked out of the kitchen and into the dining hall. It was now mealtime, and customers were streaming in. The tray held Mapo tofu, with an aroma so tantalizing, and the rich, flavorful stir-fried rice noodles, all drawing the attention of the nearby diners. They couldn’t help but turn their heads to look.

“It smells so good! I want the tofu! And the stir-fried noodles!”

chapters.title: Chapter 180
chapters.content:
Chapter 180 Cute and Well-behaved Are Nothing Compared to Wealth!

“Wow, it smells so good! I want that tofu! And the stir-fried rice noodles!”

“Waiter, I want the same!”

Wangjing No. 1 was fully booked by Shengyang Group, and lunch was being served to some guests who had arrived early for the birthday banquet.

These guests had come ahead of time, as the banquet was in the evening, so they were just having a light meal at Wangjing No. 1 in the afternoon. The waiters glanced at the dishes on the tray.

“Guests, we have Mapo tofu, sour and spicy shredded potatoes, and stir-fried rice noodles…”

These dishes were fairly ordinary, ones that customers usually didn’t order at Wangjing No. 1. After all, these dishes could be found at any regular restaurant.

But since the guests had made specific requests, everything was prepared according to their instructions. The waiters quickly notified the kitchen to prepare them. The dishes—Mapo tofu, stir-fried rice noodles, and shredded potatoes—were served.

The guests smelled the dishes and frowned. “This doesn’t smell the same as the one we had earlier. It’s not as fragrant.”

After tasting a bite, they felt the flavor was just average, nothing exceptional. They could say it was good, but it didn’t stand out as being truly delicious. These guests, being invited to Ouyang Peng’s birthday banquet, were no ordinary people.

They were business magnates and social elites, used to eating luxurious meals. Naturally, their palates were refined and discerning. They called over the waiter to reorder.

Room 888

Shen Muyan and Lu Xiyao shared a plate of stir-fried rice noodles, while Lu Qin ate a separate serving. A bite of stir-fried rice noodles, followed by a spoonful of spicy and crispy Mapo tofu—everything was perfect.

“Daddy, can I eat your food all the time~?”

The little one slouched in her chair, patting her round tummy. She had eaten a lot and was feeling stuffed.

“Sure, but only if you’re well-behaved,” Lu Qin said, taking a sip of liver paste soup.

“I’m very well-behaved~!” The little one said confidently.

In the world of cuteness, she was the most well-behaved, and in the world of being well-behaved, she was the cutest.

“Then can I eat your food all the time?” Shen Muyan spoke coldly.

Lu Qin and Lu Xiyao turned their heads to look.

The rich woman spoke again, “I’m very rich.”

Lu Qin smiled and nodded.

“Of course.”

In the face of wealth, cuteness and obedience mean nothing. The rich woman was just that—a rich woman, and when she spoke, it was all about flaunting her wealth.

After lunch, Lu Qin had to get busy preparing for the evening’s banquet. Forty servings of liver paste soup required three large pots of broth and would take an entire afternoon to prepare.

Before heading to the kitchen, Lu Qin made osmanthus oolong tea for the rich woman and the little one. The rich woman had coffee, and the little one had milk added to hers.

The two pounds of osmanthus oolong tea leaves bought from Moon Island were too much to carry back and forth, so Lu Qin simply brewed all of it and sealed it in thermos flasks, which the waiters placed in the tea area of the lobby to serve the guests.

Of course, Shen Muyan was also invited to the evening’s banquet, seated at one of the business elite tables. Around two or three o’clock, guests began arriving one after another.

Shen Muyan recognized some familiar faces from the hotel’s dining scene, as well as a few people who had appeared in newspapers and magazines, so she went over to exchange business cards.

Lu Xiyao was looked after by a young lady in a qipao from the tea area. The little one wasn’t shy and, relying on her cuteness, charmed the tea ladies in qipaos.

“Yao Yao, don’t run around, stay here and be good.”

“Little beauty, would you like some tea?”

Lu Xiyao shook her head.

“Sister, I don’t drink tea~!”

“Then what would you like to drink? How about some orange soda?”

Lu Xiyao’s eyes lit up.

“Yes, I want a bottle! Thank you, sister~!”

“Haha, no need to be polite, you’re so cute!”

The tea lady fetched two bottles of orange soda. The little one pocketed one bottle and drank the other. She held the straw in her mouth, and as she looked at the thermos flask, she pointed excitedly.

“This is the osmanthus oolong tea my daddy made~!”

“Osmanthus oolong tea? Your daddy made it?”

“Mm~!”

One of the tea ladies opened the lid, and an intense, pure tea fragrance immediately wafted out.

“Wow! What a fragrant tea!”

“This tea is really fragrant!”

The tea ladies couldn’t help but praise it. Their job was to brew tea, so they were quite familiar with it and professional. But they had never smelled such a rich tea aroma before!

It was so refreshing!

Lu Xiyao proudly said, “My daddy’s tea is the best!”

The tea ladies poured the tea into cups, each taking one to taste. The reason the tea smelled so good was because Lu Qin had boiled it three times, bringing out the fullest fragrance of the tea leaves.

Boiling tea for too long could slightly affect the taste, but Lu Qin’s mastery of osmanthus oolong tea allowed him to control the ratio of water to tea leaves, along with the precise temperature, which enhanced the flavor and compensated for any slight shortcomings.

In short, the tea Lu Qin made could elevate the flavor to levels higher than the tea itself. The tea ladies were amazed. This was truly good tea—an exceptional kind of tea!

The texture in the mouth was thick like drinking jam, with the fragrance and taste of the tea transforming on the tongue. The tip of the tongue and the surface of the tongue presented different flavors!

It was smooth with a bit of bitterness, but it quickly turned sweet, and after swallowing, the sweet, refreshing, and comfortable sensation lingered for a long time. This tea was amazing!

“Your daddy is so incredible!”

“Not only is the tea good, his tea-brewing skills are top-notch!”

The tea ladies couldn’t help but praise, “Yao Yao, would you like to have a sip?”

“I’ve already had it, daddy added milk, so I’m drinking milk tea~!”
