“That’s impossible. I just checked the Lotus District official WeChat, they’re still recruiting booth owners!”

“It must have been rejected on purpose, do they look down on our Lu Ji Snacks?”

“Blind Lotus District! How can they not see the quality of our Lu Ji Snacks!”

“We definitely need to teach Lotus District a lesson!”

“Boss Lu, go smash the place up!”

“First snack stall in Jiangcheng, yet they won’t let us at the food festival, Lotus District, you’re amazing!”

“Lotus District, you’re really something!”

Under the strong insistence of customers, Lu Qin reluctantly went to the Lotus District to set up a stall.

____

No stall at the food festival? Fine, no problem.

Our Lu Qin Snacks will set up next door!

One stall will steal the whole food festival’s traffic!

Let’s see!

“Boss Lu, remember to make plenty of food!”

“We’ll help you promote it for free.”

Lu Qin felt somewhat troubled.

“I don’t think this is necessary.”

“Don’t be afraid, Boss Lu!”

“I’m afraid we’ll steal all the customers from the food festival and make Lotus District look bad.”

“Hahaha! Boss Lu, so confident!”

“The food festival starts at 2:30 this afternoon!”

Lu Qin had Boss Zhao bring a carload of ingredients over, and at 2 PM, they headed to Lotus District’s Wanmin Street.

____

Qin Hui watched the stalls being set up at the food festival site, nodding in satisfaction.

“President, many people from Haishi and Fengshi have come to the food festival!”

“Is the official live broadcast team ready?”

“We’ve already started warming up, the Douyin live stream is up.”

“Great!”

At the food festival site, the official live broadcast team of Lotus District, hosted by the beautiful Wu Yiyi, was interviewing people coming to the festival.

“Hello, miss, which foods do you like the most at our Lotus District food festival?”

“Hmm… stinky tofu, beef cakes, Grandpa’s water-cooked…”

“Hey, handsome, where are you from? What foods do you like?”

“I’m from Annan District, I like fried rice noodles, ice crystal cakes, mung bean paste, and Bosi oil cakes…”

Wu Yiyi turned to look and wondered, “Senior, what are these things you mentioned? Ice crystal cakes? Bosi oil cakes? I don’t see any stalls selling those! Fried rice noodles aren’t exactly a special dish, they’re too common!”

“Lu Qin fried rice noodles, haven’t you tried it?”

“Lu Qin fried rice noodles? Never heard of it.”

Wu Yiyi thought for a long time but couldn’t recall any stand called Lu Ji Fried Rice Noodles…

“You’ll know soon,” The young man said mysteriously, then disappeared into the crowd.

Wu Yiyi shook her head and didn’t pay much attention to the man’s words, continuing to interview the next person. The live stream audience began to grow.

“Lotus District is holding a food festival, support it!”

“I’m here, the stalls are set up, can’t wait to eat!”

“I’m on my way! Love food festivals! So much to eat!”

“Many famous food stalls from Jiangcheng are here, food paradise! Foodies are in for a treat!”

____

Half an hour later, a large crowd of food lovers poured into the food festival, and the entire street became bustling. Shouting and calling out from all sides. Meanwhile, on another street, Lu Qin also started setting up his stall. Old customers rushed over.

This street usually didn’t have many stalls, but now, Lu Qin was the only one. Other stalls that used to set up here had all gone to the food festival, hoping to catch some heat from it, even if they couldn’t get in.

Old customers bought food from Lu Qin, then went to find a place to sit at the food festival. Two young people sat at the public table, proudly displaying their fried rice noodles.

There were so many people at the food festival that this table was quickly occupied by others. A couple came over and immediately smelled the fragrant fried rice noodles. Their braised meat rolls and spicy hotpot suddenly didn’t seem as tasty.

“Hey, bro, where did you get this fried rice noodles from? It smells so good, is it tasty?”

One person kept eating without responding.

The other looked up, smugly saying, “Take off the ‘ma’ and add ‘xiangdang,’ then you’ll know how good it is!”

“Where did you get it? We’ve walked around and didn’t see any fried rice noodle stall!”

“On the other street, it’s not here, you need to hurry, there’s a long line!”

The couple exchanged looks and quickly ran over. When they reached the other street, they saw a long line in front of the ‘Lu Ji Snack Stall’ food truck.

Could this be another area of the food festival?

Without thinking, the couple rushed over and joined the line!

The same thing happened in several places at the food festival. Customers specifically went to the food festival to get food from Lu Ji Snack Stall.

No shouting needed, the people just came because they smelled the fragrance.

“Excuse me, what’s this you’re eating? It looks so delicious!”

“Bosi oil cakes.”

“Where did you get it?”

“On the other street.”

chapters.title: Chapter 192
chapters.content:
Chapter 192 The Disappearing Guests!

“Handsome, what kind of pastry are you eating? It looks so delicious!”

“Ice Crystal Cake.”

“Where did you buy it? Can I have a piece?”

A young lady batted her eyes, trying to charm her way into getting a free piece of the ‘Ice Crystal Cake.’

“At the stall of Lu Qin on the next street.”

The young man didn’t even glance at the lady, Lu Boss trains foodies who only have eyes for food.

“You want to eat my Ice Crystal Cake?”

“Can I?”

The young lady blinked her large eyes adorned with colored contact lenses.

“No…”

The young man hadn’t even finished speaking when the lady snatched it.

“You…”

He glared at her. But the lady’s attention was entirely on the cake. She brought it to her mouth and took a bite.

“Wow! So good!”

Her friend approached.

“Jiaojiao, you must try this ‘Ice Crystal Cake.’ It’s cold and sweet, so refreshing, it’s so delicious!”

“Really? Let me try… Wow! It’s really good!”

The octopus balls they were holding suddenly didn’t taste as good.

“Handsome, where can I find Lu Qin’s stall? Can you take us there? Pretty please!”

The two of them used a coquettish tone.

“Alright, I’ll take you there!”

The young man stood up. He must take them there!

Once they buy the Ice Crystal Cake, the piece that was eaten will definitely be brought back.

…

“Huh? What smells so good?”

“That’s twice-cooked pork! It smells amazing!”

“Excuse me, where did you get that twice-cooked pork? I’ve walked around and haven’t seen it!”

“Next street, at Lu Qin’s stall!”

____

“Where can I find iced green bean soup?”

“Next street, at Lu Qin’s stall!”

____

Soon, everyone knew about Lu Qin’s food stall on the next street. People flocked there.

“Wow, so many people are queuing up at Lu Qin’s stall? Is this part of the 27th Food Festival?”

“Definitely not, it’s on the next street!”

“So many people! The stalls in the food festival aren’t even this crowded!”

“Lu Qin’s stall! This place sells really delicious fried rice!”

“Fried rice? I thought they sell Ice Crystal Cake?”

“Don’t they sell green bean soup?”

“I came for the twice-cooked pork!”

Some people looked at each other, until they saw the menu and were shocked.

Does Lu Qin’s stall sell so many kinds of food?

“Do we need to queue?”

“Of course! There are so many people already in line! You can tell just by looking that it must be really delicious!”

____

Half an hour later, more food festival guests were drawn to Lu Qin’s stall!

“Quick, go to Lu Qin’s stall on the next street! The flavor of the royal chicken wings is just amazing!”

“Lu Qin also has osmanthus oolong milk tea! It beats some other tea brands hands down! I heard they only use zero-added natural cane sugar, truly no burden!”

“Bosnian oil cake is delicious!”

“Lu Qin’s stall has everything! And every single thing is absolutely delicious!”

____

Twenty minutes later, another group of guests headed to the next street.

“Don’t eat the boiled dishes anymore! Lu Qin’s stall on the next street has spicy chicken feet, and it tastes amazing!”

“Hey, have you tried Lu Qin’s milk tea? I heard it’s even better than the Bawang Tea Sister! The stall owner’s secret recipe—osmanthus oolong milk tea! The taste is incredible!”

“Lu Qin even has chickpea flower! Can you believe it?! Chickpea flower! The legendary non-spicy Sichuan dish! Let’s go try it!”

Customers who were about to buy the boiled dishes put their plates down and followed their friends to the next street.

The boiled dish stall owner was left in confusion, wondering, “Lu Qin’s food stall? What’s going on?”

The owner turned and noticed the same thing happening at the neighboring Dalao’s sesame cakes and the He Ji Ice House. Customers who were placing orders were suddenly pulled away to what seemed to be Lu Qin’s food stall.

Each stall owner was filled with questions.

“Lu Qin’s food stall on the next street?”

…

Douyin, Live Stream of the Lianhua District Food Festival.

Host Wu Yiyi: “Everyone, look at how lively our food festival is! It’s absolutely packed!”

“This year, we have over 150 stalls at the food festival, offering a variety of specialty foods. Food lovers are in for a treat!”

“Everyone, there are group purchase coupons in the yellow cart. Feel free to take them! Bawang Tea Sister, Xiao A Qiao Seafood Spicy Marinade, Sha City Stinky Tofu, Fang Jie’s Fried Skewers, Zhang Hezi’s Spicy Hot Pot…”

Wu Yiyi was eagerly hosting the live stream, unaware that the crowd behind her was moving toward the exit.

Wave after wave. Soon, the traffic in the food festival area visibly decreased. The staff behind the live stream noticed this and tapped Wu Yiyi on the shoulder, pointing behind her.

“What’s going on behind me?”

Wu Yiyi turned around in confusion, only to see wave after wave of people heading toward the exit. She quickly grabbed the microphone and went to interview them.

“Wait a minute, the food festival just started an hour ago, and you’re leaving already?”

“Where are you headed, handsome?”

“Miss, why did you just throw away the spicy hot pot you just bought?”

Wu Yiyi asked several people and received the same response:

Lu Qin’s food stall.

“Is there a Lu Qin food stall on the next street?”

“It seems to be really popular! Everyone’s heading over there!”

“Do you know about it?”

Wu Yiyi asked her staff.

The staff shook their head, wearing the same confused expression.

“Let’s go, check it out!”

Wu Yiyi was stunned as she watched the foot traffic at the food festival drastically decrease. She had to find out why. The camera followed Wu Yiyi. The live stream audience noticed the excitement and began sending messages.

“Lu Qin’s food stall? Sounds familiar…”

“There used to be a Lu Boss who sold fried rice in the college town night market, is it the same one?”

“I only tried Lu Boss’s fried rice once, and it was delicious! But it was so hard to get! The place was always packed, and I never got to buy it again!”

“Lu Qin’s food stall is definitely Lu Boss’s, but I don’t know how they ended up in Lianhua District!”

“Wow! Amazing! Lu Boss’s food stall vs. all the vendors at the food festival!”

“Host, go check it out!”

____

Wu Yiyi arrived at the next street, where the first thing she saw was a sea of people. Everyone had really come here. It was just one stall, but it had drawn the crowd away!

The line was so long—Wu Yiyi had never seen anything like it!

Why?

How?

Wu Yiyi immediately went to interview people.

“Handsome, is Lu Qin’s food really that good?”

“It’s delicious! I’ve never had fried rice this tasty!”

Wu Yiyi looked at what the handsome guy was holding in his hands, confused.

“You’re holding fried rice?”

“The fried rice is long gone! This is twice-cooked pork and mapo tofu! Both are delicious! I’m taking them home to eat with rice!”

“…”

“Hey, hey! Beautiful, wait a minute! What did you buy at Lu Qin’s stall?”

“Fried rice, Ice Crystal Cake, Bosnian oil cake, Osmanthus Oolong Milk Tea, and iced green bean soup.”

“Is it good?”

“Of course! Otherwise, why would I buy so much?”

A girl replied as she ate a Bosnian oil cake.

“Can I try some?”

Wu Yiyi was curious about what tasted so good that made so many people flock to it.

“No…”

The girl was about to refuse, thinking, I’m not sharing, I don’t even know you!

But then she saw the camera.

“Uh… are you live streaming?”

“Yes, we’re the official live stream of the Lianhua District Food Association.”

“Okay, I’ll give you a Bosnian oil cake to try!”

Although the girl was reluctant, she didn’t want to look stingy on TV.

“Thank you!”

Wu Yiyi took the Bosnian oil cake, which was still a bit hot. She blew on it a few times before taking a bite. A few seconds later, Wu Yiyi was completely stunned!

This oil cake was SO GOOD!!!

The outside was crispy, and the inside was soft!

The texture was perfect!

Who would have thought that this oil cake, which looked like a loofah, could taste so amazing?

After finishing the Bosnian oil cake, Wu Yiyi was totally hooked. She praised Lu Qin’s food stall non-stop on the live stream.

“Oh my god! Family, this Bosnian oil cake is absolutely amazing! Crispy on the outside, tender inside, sweet and delicious! Absolutely, absolutely! You’ve never had such a good oil cake!”

“No wonder Lu Qin’s food stall has a line that stretches down the street. I’m sure everything else is delicious too!”

Wu Yiyi’s eyes were sparkling, completely forgetting she was the host of the food festival. The staff tried signaling her, but Wu Yiyi didn’t notice. She grabbed the camera and rushed straight to the stall.

“What are you doing? No cutting in line!”

“We’re not cutting in line, we’re just looking!”

Wu Yiyi explained as she squeezed her way through. Lu Qin’s face appeared on the live stream.

“It’s Lu Boss!”

“Really! Lu Boss came to Lianhua District! I’m nearby, I’m heading over now to buy!”

“Lu Boss upgraded from a tricycle to a food truck, and even hired staff! His business is growing, but the crowd is still as large as ever!”

When Wu Yiyi saw Lu Qin, she froze in place.

Oh my god!

The owner of Lu Qin’s food stall is so handsome!

“Family! Lu Boss is too handsome!”

The live stream comments were scrolling rapidly.

“Of course, Lu Boss’s looks are so stunning, even in my prime I would step aside!”

“Lu Boss is handsome, his food is delicious, and his stall is clean and hygienic! It’s literally my dream stall! I wish I could go there every day!”

“I’m already on my way! Lu Boss, please come to Lianhua District more often! Annan District is too far away!”

“Is Lu Boss just joining in on the fun at our Lianhua District Food Festival?”

“Ah! I’m torn! If I go, it’ll probably all be sold out! It’s happened to me so many times before! Just thinking about it makes me scared!”

____

Wu Yiyi stared at Lu Qin’s face like a lovesick fool.

“Please go to the back of the line and don’t cut in!”

Xiaomi reminded her.

“Oh, uh? Ah! Sorry, I’ll go to the back!”

Wu Yiyi snapped out of her trance and immediately rushed to the back of the line.

A feast for the eyes!

Absolutely a feast for the eyes!

The staff could no longer stand it and grabbed Wu Yiyi’s arm.

“You’ve lost your mind! Why are you going to stand in line? We’re doing a live stream for the food festival!”

“As the host, you’re running over to stand in line? Really?”

Wu Yiyi went, “Ah!” and finally woke up.

The audience in the live stream was laughing hysterically. Wu Yiyi ran back, but the foot traffic in the food festival had clearly dropped by half. And more people kept heading to the next street.

This time, Lu Qin had stocked up properly, and with each person limited to one portion, the food wouldn’t run out so quickly. They were doing their best to ensure everyone could get a taste of Lu Qin’s food.

At the food festival, several stalls had been doing well in the beginning, with good sales in the first hour. At this rate, they could make a fortune by the end of the day. But suddenly, the customers disappeared.

In broad daylight, the “disappearing guests” happened!

After some inquiries, it turned out that Lu Qin’s food stall on the next street had stolen all the customers!

One stall?

Taking all the customers from the entire food festival?

Is it really that powerful?

Yes, it is that powerful!

There was a stall selling crystal shrimp dumplings, and in the time since it started, they had sold only three servings, making less than 30 yuan in total.

Why?

Because the crystal shrimp dumplings at Lu Qin’s stall were even better!

Their dumplings were so thin, the filling was tender, and they tasted incredibly fresh—so delicious. This stall got completely wiped out. The customers were still running to the next street. They kept running!

By around 4 PM, the food festival’s foot traffic had dropped to a mere 300 people. It had originally been over 1,000. Now, everyone had gone to the next street.

In the office, Qin Hui was giving a report to the leaders of the cultural tourism bureau.

“Yes, that’s right, in less than an hour, the foot traffic exceeded 2,000, and it’s still increasing in the afternoon. By evening, it will only go up…”

“Thank you for the compliment, if the response is good, this food festival could last more than a week…”

Qin Hui’s assistant had been waiting in the office for more than ten minutes, looking very anxious. Qin Hui was on the phone, signaling for the assistant to wait. Finally, Qin Hui hung up the phone. He received a compliment from the leader and was in a good mood.

He glanced at his anxious assistant and said slowly, “You really can’t sit still, you want to tell me everything even before I finish talking.”

“Didn’t you see I was talking to the leader?”

Qin Hui took a sip of tea, then asked, “What’s the matter?”

“Ah! President, the foot traffic at the food festival has dropped to only 300 now, and it’s about to go down to less than 200!”

Qin Hui’s hand shook, and he quickly looked up.

“What are you joking about?”

chapters.title: Chapter 193
chapters.content:
Chapter 193 Isn’t This Just Making Clothes for Others?

In the office, Qin Hui almost dropped his teacup, staring at his assistant, feeling like this was some kind of joke. At the start of the event, the food festival was bustling with visitors, and the promotion had been successful, attracting many food lovers.

Given the current momentum, by the time the neon lights lit up in the evening, the Lianhua District night market would have been packed. Although it was only the first day, attracting over 10,000 people was a piece of cake.

Now, you’re telling me that with over 2,000 people at the beginning, there are only 300 to 400 people left?

Where did everyone go?

Did an earthquake scare them off?

The assistant hurriedly explained, “They, they all went to the next street!”

“The next street?”

“Yes, there’s a food stall called Lu Qin’s on the next street, and most of the visitors are lining up there!”

“Lu Qin’s food stall?”

Qin Hui was momentarily stunned, feeling that the name sounded familiar. He furrowed his brows and asked, “How many stalls are there on the next street?”

“Only one?”

“What?”

Qin Hui found it hard to believe.

“So many people are lining up at just one stall?”

How many people would that be?

The assistant answered, “I didn’t count them closely, but just looking quickly, it has to be at least 500 people. The line stretches from one end of the street to the other!”

Qin Hui’s mind was buzzing.

“What’s this stall selling? Dragon meat?”

Suddenly, Qin Hui remembered something. Lu Qin’s food stall had applied for a space at the food festival, and at the time, Qin Hui had dismissed it because the stall offered a variety of common dishes that didn’t seem special, so he passed on it. He didn’t expect it to end up on the next street.

“Let’s go, check it out.”

With a serious expression, Qin Hui left the office. The assistant hurriedly followed him. To be honest, Qin Hui didn’t believe a small food stall could take business from so many vendors at the food festival.

They first went to the food festival exhibition area. Initially, when Qin Hui had checked it out, it had been full of people, the atmosphere was lively. But now, it was a completely different scene.

There were only a few hundred people walking around, browsing the 150+ stalls. It felt like the peak season had suddenly turned into the off-season. Many stalls had no customers, and the owners stood there, dumbfounded, wondering where all the people had gone!

The food had already been prepared, but no one was buying. Qin Hui stood with his hands on his hips, looking around, beads of sweat beginning to form on his forehead. Just then, a group of customers entered through the entrance.

Qin Hui thought they were returning, but surprisingly, these customers had already bought food and were sitting in the public dining area. The umbrellas and tables were free for customers at the food festival, but now they were bringing food from elsewhere.

Was this just making clothes for others?

Qin Hui was frustrated and immediately ran to the next street to check it out. Turning the corner, Qin Hui and his assistant could see a food truck parked under a big tree. The line of people waiting extended all the way to the end of the street.

The line was so long, and the customers were patiently waiting. This was surprising, considering that many people today are impatient. If they have to wait too long for food, they would choose to leave and go to another stall. But at Lu Qin’s stall, this wasn’t the case.

The assistant gulped nervously.

“This food must be really good, people are lining up for so long!”

Qin Hui didn’t believe it and called out to a few people. After asking, everyone said the food was amazing, and the wait was worth it.

How good must it be?

“Can I buy the food you have in your hand? I’ll pay double the price!”

The assistant tried to buy the food from one of the customers, but honestly, they knew they wouldn’t get it easily.

“Are you kidding? Not selling.”

“Triple the price!”

“Not selling, I’ve been waiting for so long,” the customer impatiently walked off.

The assistant was speechless.

“Not selling, even for triple the price? Are you not making money?”

What he didn’t realize was that, in the eyes of food lovers, delicious food is priceless. The assistant didn’t give up and pulled out two red bills, trying to buy the food from a customer again. But he got a cold shoulder, and the customers looked at him as if he were a fool. The assistant was infuriated.

Qin Hui stood with his hands on his hips, observing Lu Qin’s food stall. To his surprise, the stall owner was a handsome young man, skillfully tossing food in the pan. In recent years, many young people have entered the food stall business. They were creative, selling all kinds of food, some making a profit, and some not.

But this young owner of Lu Qin’s food stall was the most impressive Qin Hui had ever seen. He sold simple, common foods—fried rice, twice-cooked pork, green bean soup… nothing special, yet he had drawn such a huge crowd. It had to be due to his excellent cooking skills!

“Xiao Jiang, come here!”

Qin Hui saw one of his employees, Xiao Jiang, who had also come to buy food from Lu Qin’s.

“President!”

Xiao Jiang had slipped away during work hours to buy food, and now he was caught in the act by his boss. He secretly cursed his luck. What was worse, he had gone to the next street instead of the food festival! Wasn’t this sabotaging his own event?

At first, Xiao Jiang thought he was going to get a lecture, but instead, Qin Hui asked, “What did you buy?”

“Fried rice, Ice Crystal Cake, mapo tofu, pickles, and milk tea!”

Xiao Jiang watched Qin Hui’s expression and saw he wasn’t angry, so he answered honestly.

“Is it good?”

“Definitely! I asked several people, and they all said it’s great!”

“Leave two portions for me to try,” Qin Hui looked at the fried rice and mapo tofu, secretly swallowing his saliva. He had already smelled the enticing aroma, and it was indeed mouth-watering!

“What? Two portions?”

Xiao Jiang immediately felt uncomfortable. “President, this is my dinner.”

“It’s just two portions! You’re so stingy. There are so many stalls at the food festival, go buy whatever you want. I’ll reimburse you,” Qin Hui generously said, then snatched one portion of fried rice and mapo tofu from Xiao Jiang.

Xiao Jiang immediately felt disheartened.

“How can the food from the other stalls compare to Lu Qin’s? It’s not even in the same league.”

Otherwise, why would everyone be flocking to Lu Qin’s?

But since Qin Hui was his boss, he didn’t dare complain. He could only endure.

“Well… Xiao Jiang, heh, do you have anything else? Can I…”

The assistant looked at the remaining food in Xiao Jiang’s hands, eyes shining like a wolf eyeing a chicken.

“No, absolutely not!”

Xiao Jiang hid the remaining food behind him and quickly ran away.

The assistant was stunned.

“It’s just a little food, so stingy.”

Back in the office, Qin Hui eagerly opened the plastic food containers, pulled out the disposable chopsticks, and gave them a quick rub before digging in.

“Mm… delicious!”

The fragrant fried rice immediately impressed Qin Hui. He thought to himself, no wonder so many people are lining up, the flavor of Lu Qin’s fried rice is truly different from others.

It’s much better!

Qin Hui finished most of the fried rice before stopping, sipping his tea, then looking at the box of mapo tofu.

chapters.title: Chapter 194
chapters.content:
Chapter 194 Dare to Do an Eating Live Stream on the Next Street? Dare to Hit the Rocket and Cannon!

Qin Hui scooped a spoonful of mapo tofu with a plastic spoon and tasted it. The spiciness, the numbing sensation, the freshness, the fragrance, the heat… a delightful mix of flavors spread across his mouth. It was absolutely satisfying!

Qin Hui ate quickly, making his assistant beside him almost drool with envy.

“President, can I try it? I’m really curious about the taste.”

Qin Hui initially didn’t want to share, but then thought that as a leader, he couldn’t seem too stingy, so he pushed the small portion of fried rice over.

“Use a new pair of chopsticks and try a bite.”

The assistant was overjoyed, quickly tasting a bite.

“Wow! My god! This fried rice is amazing!”

The assistant couldn’t believe how delicious this street stall’s fried rice was—it was oily yet not greasy, fresh and fragrant, beating any other fried rice he’d had.

“I’ve never tasted fried rice like this!”

The assistant kept devouring it, downing several bites. Qin Hui immediately widened his eyes and snatched it away.

“We agreed you’d try a bite, but you’re finishing the whole thing?”

The assistant, holding the chopsticks in his mouth, was reluctant to stop.

“President, I need to use the restroom.”

Qin Hui waved him off, not even looking up, and continued eating the mapo tofu.

As soon as the assistant left, he dashed out the door, heading straight to the Lu Qin food stall to join the queue. Meanwhile, the official live stream of the Lianhua District Food Festival was filled with comments like ‘Lu Qin’s food stall,’ ‘Lu Boss,’ ‘Fried rice is so delicious,’ and ‘Bosnian oil cake is amazing…’

Wu Yiyi was overwhelmed.

There were over 3,000 viewers in the stream, and many comments urged her to go live at the Lu Qin food stall on the next street. Some even asked if the host dared to do an eating live stream, with rockets, planes, and cannons ready if she went.

The staff around Wu Yiyi were sweating profusely. They knew that if the live stream wasn’t shut down soon, it would blow up!

The original backdrop of the live stream was bustling with crowds, but now, there were just a few lonely stalls with no customers. Finally, the live stream was turned off.

Wu Yiyi let out a sigh of relief, then handed the equipment to her staff and rushed to the next street to queue.

Staff: “…”

In Lu Boss’s food lover group, a member posted a picture.

At 5 PM, Lu Qin took off his apron to pick up his daughter from school.

“Lu Boss, hurry back! There are so many people waiting for your fried rice!”

“Also the twice-cooked pork, mapo tofu!”

“Lu Boss, I’ll go pick up your kid for you! You just need to fry the rice!”

“Hey! Hey! I can go! Where is the kindergarten?”

The customers almost started competing to help. To this, Lu Qin just smiled. There was no way he’d let a stranger pick up his daughter. What if something went wrong?

Even if there was no ill intent at first, after seeing his little cutie, who knew what might happen?

Lu Qin couldn’t take any risks. When they reached the car, he was about to unlock it automatically. Just as he was about to get in, a middle-aged man walked up—it was Qin Hui.

“Hello, Lu Boss, I’m from the Lianhua District Food Association. We’re the organizers of this food festival.”

Qin Hui smiled and extended his hand.

“Hello, what’s the matter?”

Lu Qin glanced at his watch—he could only spare two minutes before he’d be late to pick up his daughter. It was a big deal.

Qin Hui had overheard Lu Qin saying he needed to pick up his daughter, so he cut to the chase. He couldn’t let Lu Qin get away with this; otherwise, this food festival would fail.

It was around dinner time, but there were less than 300 people in the food festival area. He had promised the higher-ups that the foot traffic would break 10,000 by the evening. This time, Qin Hui had misjudged things.

Later, he checked into Lu Qin’s food stall. And he was shocked. Lu Qin’s stall was already well-known in the Annan District University Town Night Market. Not only that, but during a music festival at Tianhong Plaza in Jiangcheng, superstar Li Xue even became a fan of Lu Boss!

Lu Qin’s food stall had even been featured on the local news when an old man called the police because he got hooked on Lu Qin’s fried rice.

All these signs showed that Lu Qin’s stall was an absolute powerhouse!

With such popularity, they were a must-have for the food festival.

“Lu Boss, we sincerely invite your stall to join the Lianhua District Food Festival. We’ll offer you a spot in the prime location, right in the center, and there will be no stall fees!”

Lu Qin checked his watch, thought for a moment, and said, “Let me think about it. Sorry, I need to pick up my daughter now.”

Qin Hui eagerly opened the car door for Lu Qin. He had already offered the best deal—no stall fees, which was a rare offer. Lu Qin picked up his daughter.

Qin Hui was still waiting there. He saw Lu Xiyao and praised, “Lu Boss, your daughter is so cute!”

“Thank you.”

“Have you thought about what we discussed?”

“If you waive the stall fees, I’ll consider it, but I’ll move tomorrow, not today.”

Qin Hui gritted his teeth and nodded. He had expected that Lu Qin’s stall wouldn’t be moving tonight—there were too many customers in line, so no time to waste. Tonight, the food festival was doomed.

He could only hope that Lu Qin’s stall would sell out quickly, so the food festival could get some traffic back. But just as Qin Hui was about to leave, he saw Lu Qin unloading two big boxes of ingredients from the trunk. One of them seemed to be filled with rice noodles.

Qin Hui’s eyelid twitched. Well, forget about getting any traffic back. It seemed like Lu Qin would be staying open until 9 PM. The food festival’s stalls would just have to pack up early tonight. It was such a blunder!

They should’ve approved Lu Qin’s food stall’s application yesterday—they could have brought in a ton of traffic!

Lu Qin made his daughter a plate of fried rice and a serving of chickpea flower for dinner.

“Dad, can I have a cup of milk tea?”

“One cup is too much, you can only have half a cup.”

“Okay~! Thanks, Dad~!”

It wasn’t good for little kids to drink too much tea at night, so Lu Qin had Xiao Mi pour a cup of milk tea and split it into two cups. He and his daughter each drank half.

The little girl cleverly drank a big sip from Lu Qin’s half first, then handed the cup back to him.

“Little foodie!” Lu Qin scratched his daughter’s nose. “Eat slowly, no milk tea during meals, wait half an hour after eating.”

“Got it, Dad.”

The space inside the food truck was large. It had a small table board, and the little girl sat happily on the stool, showing off her fried rice. Lu Qin added a piece of royal chicken wings for her.

After she finished eating, Lu Qin handed her a wet wipe to clean her face. The little girl wiped her face, then put on a “Little Boss” name tag and started working. The third window of the food truck opened.

Customers saw a cute little head poking out, and they all melted in adoration.

“Lu Boss, your daughter is so cute!”

chapters.title: Chapter 195
chapters.content:
Chapter 195 The Pregnancy Cravings and Full Mastery of Chinese Pastry Making

Lu Xiyao’s bright eyes, white milk-like skin, and adorable appearance made everyone who saw her want to pinch her cheeks and shower her with affection. How could such a cute creature exist in the universe?

It had to be said, Lu Boss’s genes were outstanding!

One couldn’t help but wonder, what does her mother look like?

She must be a stunningly beautiful woman, right?

“I’m Lu Qin’s little boss lady! Big brothers, sisters, uncles, and aunties, if you want to eat Ice Crystal Cake, Chickpea Flower, or Chilled Green Bean Soup, come to my spot and line up!”

Lu Xiyao’s soft, sweet voice echoed, incredibly pleasant to hear, making many customers want to rub her little head.

Swipe, swipe, swipe!

A large group of customers immediately headed over to line up for her. Lu Qin smiled slightly at the sight. The little boss lady was already used to the routine.

Meanwhile, in the food festival area next door, there were only a few customers left. The owner of Chen Ji’s crayfish stall was smoking a cigarette, gazing at the sky with a hopeless look on his face.

He had paid over 1,000 yuan in stall fees and prepared hundreds of pounds of marinated crayfish, expecting to sell them well and promote Chen Ji’s crayfish in Lianhua District. But unexpectedly, a stall named Lu Qin popped up next door and stole all the customers. Now, his sales were barely 400 yuan, not even enough to cover the stall fees. He was losing big.

“Old Chen, it’s over! There’s no business today!”

“Pack up early. Tomorrow, Lu Qin will come over to this side, and once the customers return, our business will pick up!”

Upon hearing this, Chen Boss’s mood lifted slightly. The crayfish he had marinated today could still be sold tomorrow. Reluctantly, he packed up, planning to fight again tomorrow.

“Mr. Ge, how did you all come over here?”

Lu Qin was surprised to see that the old neighbors from his street had come all the way to Lianhua District, which took about half an hour by car.

“I couldn’t stand not eating your food today. My old man wanted to try a trendy snack and came for a late-night treat!”

“I said we could take a cab, but you insisted on taking the bus, which took an extra twenty minutes. Now, there’s barely any fried rice left from Little Lu Boss!”

Chen Daqi peered into the food truck, noticing several ingredient boxes were empty. Business was booming. Chen Daqi was an interesting character. Despite owning a mansion, he preferred living in the old street district.

Originally, he was helping Shen Hongda by getting close to Lu Qin. Later, realizing how impressive Lu Qin was, he thought he could marry his daughter, Chen Yue, to Lu Qin.

He thought it would be easy for a woman to pursue a man, but to his surprise, Lu Boss remained unmoved. Chen Daqi thought Lu Qin must still prefer his original partner, so he gave up. But he still visited Lu Qin’s morning stall every day.

Now, living in the old district and practicing Tai Chi, his health had improved, so he hadn’t moved out.

Wang Guifen spoke up, “Taking a cab would cost over 40 yuan, but the bus only costs 2 yuan. The money saved could buy two boxes of Ice Crystal Cake!”

As she spoke, she handed a bottle of yogurt to the little one. The yogurt was quite expensive, about ten yuan a bottle, locally produced in Jiangcheng. The milk content was over 90%, and there were no additives except for probiotics.

Food safety concerns were high, so people were becoming more careful, especially when buying food for their children.

The little one chirped, “Thank you.”

Ge Daqi and others gave the little one some snacks and fruit. Lu Qin didn’t stop them. These neighbors genuinely liked the little one, and if he didn’t let them feed her, they would be upset.

In return, Lu Qin would give them extra fried rice, Ice Crystal Cake, pickles, and so on. They gladly accepted. Some customers noticed this “feeding” feature and wanted to try it too.

“I’m going to buy snacks right now!”

Lu Qin quickly stopped them. The little one didn’t lack food, and this “feeding” behavior would spread and lead to waste.

Even though customers were doing it out of affection for the little one, it wasn’t necessary. The old street neighbors bought their food and left.

For these familiar people, Lu Qin would give them extra food and allow them to buy without limit. Other customers watched, envious.

Ge Daqi and Wang Guifen couldn’t help but feel superior.

“We’re Lu Boss’s neighbors. We see each other every day, so of course, we get special treatment.”

In the food festival area next door, most of the stalls had already packed up.

What could they do? All the customers had been taken away!

Some stall owners, after packing up, came over to check out Lu Qin’s stall.

“Is Lu Qin’s food really that good?”

“One stall has practically taken all the customers from the whole festival?”

“How is this possible?”

They joined the queue. They bought the food. They tasted it. They silently walked away. It was good, really good!

And not just good, it was extraordinary!

How to describe the difference? Sure, they were street stalls, but Lu Qin’s food had the level of a five-star hotel chef.

It was a pure dimensionality reduction strike.

“Dear, how are you feeling?”

“Disgusted, want to throw up, but also hungry!”

Not far away, a young couple walked over. The wife was pregnant and had severe morning sickness.

“You haven’t eaten anything all day. You must eat something. You used to love fried chicken. Should I get you some?”

The husband patted his wife’s back, anxious.

“ Ugh… don’t mention fried chicken, just thinking about it makes me want to throw up. I can’t eat anything!”

“What do you feel like eating? Hotpot? Barbecue?”

“ Ugh… no, I don’t want that either!”

The husband scratched his head, thinking, How about crab pot? Cold noodles? Spicy skewers?

“ Ugh… can’t eat! Can’t eat anything!”

The wife was in agony but still hungry.

It felt like having a severe stomach illness. The husband scratched his head. What could he do? The foods his wife used to love now made her nauseous.

What could she eat?

“How about some stir-fried rice? Darling, bear with it, just eat a little. The baby needs food too!”

“I said I can’t eat, why are you asking me to eat?!”

The wife glared at her husband, annoyed that he couldn’t understand. As they walked past Lu Qin’s food stall, the strong smell of oil and spices wafted over.

“You led me here. It’s a street stall, the oily smell is too much. You’re going to make me sick!”

The wife was feeling miserable, thinking if she had known pregnancy would be this hard, she would have never gotten pregnant. The husband could only silently endure. Pregnancy was the biggest issue.

Just as he was about to cover his wife’s nose and move away from the stall, he saw her stop in her tracks.

“Wait, I think I’m not that nauseous anymore. What does this stall sell?”

The husband was confused, quickly ran to take a look, and then ran back.

“Dear, they sell many kinds of food, and the boss is making mapo tofu. It smells really good!”

“Mapo tofu?”

The wife took a deep breath, and magically, she felt an appetite.

“I want to eat this mapo tofu!”

“You’re hungry now? You want to eat!”

The husband was overjoyed. Finally, she wanted to eat something!

“Wait here, I’ll go queue up!”

“Hurry up, I’m starving!”

“Coming! Coming!”

The husband hurriedly ran to join the queue but soon realized the line was… insanely long!

How long would this take?

At least 30 to 40 minutes! Wouldn’t his wife starve before that?

“Excuse me, could I cut in line? My wife hasn’t eaten all day, she’s really sick, and she’s finally hungry. She can’t wait that long! Please, I beg you!”

The young man earnestly requested.

Many customers had noticed the young couple’s struggles, and feeling sorry for them, the person at the front of the line let them through.

“Thank you, thank you…”

The young man was immensely grateful and ordered a portion of mapo tofu.

The customers behind him reminded, “Lu Boss’s food is all great, you should order more. It’s tough waiting in line.”

“Really? Oh! Okay!”

The young man glanced at the small menu board and added Ice Crystal Cake, Crystal Shrimp Dumplings, and Green Bean Soup to his order. After paying, the husband rushed back to his wife’s side.

“Dear, the mapo tofu is hot, eat slowly!”

“Give it to me fast.”

The wife was starving, finally having something she wanted to eat. Her stomach began to growl.

She devoured several bites of the mapo tofu, and finally, she felt like herself again.

“This mapo tofu is amazing!”

The spiciness and numbing sensation instantly opened her appetite.

“Dear, try this Crystal Shrimp Dumpling!”

“This Crystal Shrimp Dumpling is amazing!”

The wife then tried the Ice Crystal Cake and was blown away!

It was magical!

She didn’t know why, but before, she couldn’t eat anything, but now she could eat everything without feeling sick!

The customers around gave a thumbs up to Lu Qin.

“Lu Boss, you’re incredible! Your food even cured the morning sickness of a pregnant woman!”

“Yeah, I saw that young mom, she was so nauseous earlier, and just hearing the name of food made her want to throw up. But she ate your mapo tofu in large bites! That’s amazing!”

“I’m reminded of Stephen Chow’s movie God of Cookery, where the urine cow ball cured a person’s loss of appetite. Lu Boss’s food can cure appetite loss too!”

It may sound exaggerated, but the truth was in front of everyone. Everyone knew that when the morning sickness hit, pregnant women couldn’t eat anything. But that young mom could eat Lu Boss’s food without any issues—it only meant one thing—Lu Boss’s food was truly incredible!

The young wife drank the last of her Green Bean Soup in one go, and with a satisfied burp, said, “Ah, I haven’t eaten this well in a long time!”

She didn’t leave a bite for her husband and ate everything herself.

“Dear, is your morning sickness gone?”

“I don’t know, but this stall’s food is really good!”

“If it’s good, we’ll come back tomorrow!”

The young man ran to ask and found out that Lu Qin’s stall would still be at the food festival tomorrow. He was relieved and added Lu Boss’s food lover group.

After the news that Lu Qin’s food could cure morning sickness spread, the stall’s popularity soared. By 9:10 PM, the food was almost sold out. Lu Qin left three portions of fried rice, a box of Ice Crystal Cake, and three cups of Green Bean Soup as midnight snacks.

There were still dozens of customers who didn’t manage to buy anything, but with everything sold out, they could only leave disappointed. Some ran to the convenience store to buy instant noodles or bread for supper, looking a bit miserable.

Lu Qin turned off the sign lights on the food truck, and the surrounding area quieted down. The three of them finished their midnight snacks inside the truck, then cleaned up and headed home.

Back at home, Lu Qin helped his daughter wash up, while Xiao Mi cleaned everything, then rode the tricycle back.

“Good night, Daddy! Mwah~!”

“Good night.”

Lu Qin turned off the lights and exited the room. Today, their income not only broke 100,000 but also exceeded that—125,800 yuan. Just from fried rice alone, Lu Qin had made 700 to 800 servings. Then there was the twice-cooked pork and mapo tofu.

Fortunately, the system’s fatigue elimination aura was in effect, or he would have developed a career-related illness after working for so long.

Lu Qin checked his system points: 6,563. He could do six advanced draws.

His luck was good—he drew the [Full Mastery of Chinese Pastry Making Techniques].

Mooncakes, Qing Tuan, Lotus Root Pastry, Date Paste Cakes, Osmanthus Cakes, Dragon Beard Pastry…

He had mastered over a hundred kinds of Chinese pastries!

“Not bad.”

Lu Qin planned to add two new Chinese pastry items tomorrow. For the remaining five advanced draws, besides a few bags of mint candy, he also received [Fluency in Eight Languages].

Fluency in languages?

It didn’t seem that useful now, but having more skills never hurt.

Just as Lu Qin was about to sleep, he heard a notification on his phone—It was Shen Mu Yan. He opened the message and was shocked to find a selfie of the rich lady in a nightgown!

But this wasn’t an ordinary nightgown. It was a wine-red ice silk, thin and seductive!

The ice silk fabric was soft, light, and smooth…

But even smoother was the delicate white skin beneath the nightgown.

Lu Qin’s throat went dry.

chapters.title: Chapter 196
chapters.content:
Chapter 196 New Lotus Blossom Pastry and Layered Oil Cake! Rich women in their friend circle are all envious!

Before going to bed, President Shen sent a picture to Boss Lu. It was a picture of a rich woman wearing a light, thin nightgown, exuding a sense of coolness.

The silk fabric clung to her snowy white skin, leaving one wondering whether she was preparing for a study or a romantic encounter. She was both noble and pure. Her skin was white and smooth. The art was striking, too artistic.

The image was, of course, only visible to Boss Lu. So, after Boss Lu stared at it for a few seconds, he took a deep breath and felt an urge to do push-ups. Just when Boss Lu was dry-mouthed and still didn’t feel satisfied, the image was retracted.

Boss Lu stared at the screen for a few seconds, then typed and sent.

Shen Muyan hummed in dissatisfaction and didn’t reply. Her trusted strategist, Fang Ya, messaged Shen Muyan.

Shen Muyan, with both beauty and body, is a wealthy woman with everything in place. If she wasn’t so cold towards other men, the line of suitors would stretch across the Pacific Ocean.

The strategist, Fang Ya, wetted her finger, flipping through a book called “The Love Guide for Pursuing Men.”

_____

The first ray of morning sunlight shone through the window curtains. Lu Qin opened his eyes. The night before, he had won a high-level lottery and obtained [Full Level Chinese Pastry Skills].

Lu Qin decided to add two new products: Lotus Blossom Pastry and Layered Oil Cake. Pastry foods don’t need to be freshly made, and once prepared, they don’t require special storage, making them perfect for stall-based selling.

Lu Qin got up early to purchase new ingredients. His assistant, Xiaomi, had already been busy in the kitchen since around 4 AM. By now, Xiaomi was able to independently prepare green bean paste, ice crystal cakes, and even knead the dough and make fillings for crystal shrimp dumplings.

And she never needed Lu Qin to tell her what to do. She worked without complaint or taking breaks, making her the perfect employee in the eyes of her boss.

The best part? She wasn’t paid a salary—only basic living and food needs were covered. Such an assistant was undoubtedly the most amazing reward from the advanced draw. Unfortunately, he hadn’t won anything like that again.

The production of the Lotus Blossom Pastry was quite complicated, mainly because it required a variety of ingredients. It also demanded special attention to its shape.

Why was it called “Lotus Blossom Pastry”? Because the pastry looked like a lotus flower. Authentic Lotus Blossom Pastry required extremely delicate craftsmanship, almost like a beautiful painting.

The filling for Lotus Blossom Pastry was either made from carrots or white lotus paste. There were two main color options—the flower’s center was yellow, made with pumpkin, and the petals were red, made with dragon fruit juice.

The dough contains lard to help it flake, much like the method for making layered oil cakes, so Lu Qin made sure to buy enough pork lard as it’s needed for both pastries.

Lu Qin wandered through the market, purchasing a variety of ingredients. The hardest ingredient to find was the green and red shredded toppings for the Layered Oil Cake.

In some street shops, the toppings were made using mustard and food coloring, which, while not harmful, weren’t good for health. Lu Qin certainly didn’t want to buy such low-quality toppings.

The better toppings were made with ingredients like dried tangerine peel, carrots, orange peel, papaya, and radish. But the most authentic ones were made from green apricots and rose preserves. Due to price and labor costs, these were more expensive. In the past, only royal families or wealthy households could afford them.

After asking around for over an hour, Lu Qin finally found a small shop selling such high-quality shredded toppings. It was a small shop run by an elderly couple, a family business that had been around for decades.

Though the shop was small, it was clean, and they used traditional wooden and bamboo tools instead of machines. Lu Qin happened to see the elderly couple making kumquat and candied dates.

On a wooden rack, they had glass jars filled with green and red shredded fruits, preserved fruits, and candies. Lu Qin entered to negotiate, and after tasting the products, he was very satisfied with the quality.

The reason the shop had been around for decades was its reputation and quality. Business was tough for the elderly couple, so Lu Qin didn’t haggle and bought four jars of green and red shredded fruit, one jar of kumquat candied dates, and one jar of winter melon sugar cubes.

These natural handmade treats were healthier than the mass-produced candies made by machines. Lu Qin drove back to the old neighborhood, and as soon as he arrived at the small courtyard, Xiaomi immediately ran out to help carry the items.

Lu Qin walked into the kitchen, where the dough for ice crystal cakes and crystal shrimp dumplings was almost ready. He washed his hands, dried them, and started getting to work.

After the ice crystal cakes and crystal shrimp dumplings were placed in the steamer, Lu Qin began kneading the dough to make the lotus blossom pastries and layered oil cakes. Xiaomi helped on the side.

Lu Qin gave Xiaomi a list of addresses.

“Xiaomi, from now on, you need to add layered oil cakes every day. The steamer won’t be enough, so after the morning stall, go to this address and buy two stacks of steamers.”

“Got it, boss.”

Xiaomi committed it to memory.

The lotus blossom pastries, like the layered oil cakes, are fried in oil. But the layered oil cake is steamed in a steamer.

Lu Qin instructed Xiaomi to keep kneading the dough and explained in detail the proportions of lard and sugar needed in the medium-gluten flour. The amount of water added to the flour varies depending on whether it’s summer or winter.

A seasoned master would only say “appropriate” when asked how much water to add for every pound of flour. But how much is “appropriate”? It all depends on experience and feel.

Beginners would use a measuring cup, but Lu Qin, with his full-level skills, could tell exactly by feel. Xiaomi had a hundred percent of the intuition for it, so after Lu Qin demonstrated once, Xiaomi deeply understood the concept of “appropriate.”

Lu Qin let Xiaomi knead the dough while he prepared the filling for the lotus blossom pastries. After stir-frying the carrot filling, Lu Qin began making the layered oil cakes.

Layered oil cake was one of the two famous dishes of Fuchun, a renowned food place in Yangzhou with a history of over a hundred years. The other specialty was jade-colored shao mai.

As the Napoleon of Yangzhou’s morning tea, layered oil cakes were famous for their snow-white layers that resembled the delicate unfolding of lotus petals. The cake had 64 layers, each as thin as a cicada’s wing.

After Xiaomi kneaded the dough, Lu Qin rolled it out, then folded it, brushed sugar oil on it, and folded it again. The dough was layered upon layered, requiring meticulous craftsmanship.

Why was the layered oil cake called one of the two specialties? Because it used a semi-fermentation technique, allowing one pound of flour to absorb six to seven ounces of water.

After steaming the dough in a steamer, Lu Qin began preparing the lotus blossom pastries. Making lotus blossom pastries was a complex process, and if the details were not precise, the pastry could easily fail.

Moreover, the petals and the flower center must not break. If any petal broke, it affected the appearance and was considered a failure. The dough was divided into red and yellow. The red dough was made by adding dragon fruit juice to form the lotus petals, and the yellow dough was mixed with pumpkin to form the lotus center.

Lu Qin rolled out the dough to show the layered effect of the petals, just like the layered oil cakes, and folded it.

Both the petals and center required a “one-open-four” technique.

After every fold, small holes were pricked in the dough with a toothpick to release air bubbles.

Why do air bubbles form?

Because the dough had gluten, and after kneading, air bubbles inevitably formed. The dough needed to rest, and it was simple to do so—just cover it with plastic wrap and wait for ten minutes.

Lotus blossom pastries needed to be not only tasty but also beautiful, so a lot of time was spent shaping them. However, for Lu Qin, a full-level expert, it took no time to form the dough into small balls with a small knife—quicker than any machine could.

Half an hour later, the countertop was filled with little dough balls that looked like lotus blossoms, red wrapping yellow. For those who suffer from trypophobia, this might be a bit much.

A large pot of hot oil was ready, with the temperature controlled at 130 degrees Celsius.

Ordinary masters would use a thermometer to measure the oil temperature, but Lu Qin only needed to dip a chopstick into the oil and check the bubbles or simply use his hand to control the temperature…

Using a thermometer was a sign of lacking experience. As the pastry dough hit the hot oil, the petals of the lotus pastry unfurled, and the flowers bloomed in the hot oil, creating a wonderful and beautiful sight.

When lifting them, simply use chopsticks, but steady hands were essential. Lu Qin demonstrated it three times, and Xiaomi finished frying the remaining dough. Lu Qin then busied himself with other tasks.

It was 7:20 AM. Lu Qin called for the little one to wake up. Ten minutes later, breakfast was ready. A knock came at the door.

Shen Muyan was back for breakfast again.

“Mommy~!”

“Good morning, Boss Lady!”

Shen Muyan was wearing a black and white professional suit today, her long and slender legs perfectly straight. Her hair was simply styled, and she wore no makeup, yet her beauty outshone that of many actresses.

Lu Qin and Shen Muyan exchanged a glance and greeted each other with a single word. “Morning.”

Although neither said much, their eyes spoke volumes. Recently, Shen Muyan had been making the moves, while Lu Qin had been retreating and holding his ground, unsure how much longer he could resist.

Breakfast consisted of fried eggs, ham, crystal shrimp dumplings, and hot milk. Xiaomi also brought over a plate of lotus blossom pastries and layered oil cakes. Both Shen Muyan and Lu Xiyao were impressed.

Lu Qin had created new products again. The lotus blossom pastries looked like delicate sculptures made from flour. The layered oil cakes were diamond-shaped, with a soft, translucent texture, topped with red and green shredded fruits, making them visually refreshing and delightful.

Little Boss Lady exclaimed, “Dad~! They’re so pretty! I don’t even want to eat them!”

Though she said that, her mouth was already watering.

The big boss lady, excited, took out her phone. “Don’t touch them, I need to take a picture.”

Because the lotus blossom pastries and layered oil cakes looked so beautiful, Shen Muyan, who rarely took pictures, managed to capture them in a way that still looked artistic, despite her lack of technical skill.

She rarely posted on social media but made an exception this time.

【Heartwarming Breakfast】【picture】【picture】

Not long after, the post got several likes and comments.

_____

Shen Muyan, reading all the comments full of admiration, was in a great mood.

“Dad, can I eat this lotus flower pastry?”

The little one took her finger out of her mouth, pointing at the lotus blossom pastry but hesitant to pick it up.

“Of course, this is a type of Chinese pastry called lotus blossom pastry. Take one and eat it.”

There were four lotus blossom pastries and layered oil cakes on the plate, so everyone could have one. The little one took a bite of the lotus blossom pastry, her eyes squinting into crescent moons, nodding in approval.

Shen Muyan also tried a bite, finding the flaky layers clear and light, with a sweet and savory fragrance, a unique flavor. She then tasted the layered oil cake.

The layered oil cake was well-defined in layers, soft, sweet, and smooth, just as exceptional in taste and texture. The rich woman looked at Lu Qin with sparkling eyes.

Lu Qin really was impressive, everything he made was top-tier. It seemed likeher admiration for him was only growing.

After breakfast, the rich woman didn’t hesitate to eat and take more with her. Lu Qin learned she was taking it to Shen Hongda and his wife, and naturally, he was more than willing. He packed a few more boxes.

“You two go to the stall. I’ll take the little one to kindergarten.”

“Okay, drive safely.”

Lu Qin reminded them, packing the lunchboxes he had already prepared into a canvas bag. He also packed a box of lotus blossom pastries and layered oil cakes, to serve as an afternoon snack for the little one.

Without a doubt, with a hearty lunch and delicious afternoon tea, the little one was sure to be the most stylish kid in kindergarten.

chapters.title: Chapter 197
chapters.content:
Chapter 197 In gambling terms, this is a total win!

At the entrance of Sunshine Primary School, the line of customers waiting early in the morning was even longer than the day before. Yesterday, Lu Qin’s snack stall had gained a large following in the Lotus District. Many food lovers from Lotus District had even traveled to Annan District just to eat Lu Qin’s breakfast.

Some customers arrived an hour early, thinking they would beat the crowd, only to find that even more people had already gathered ahead of them.

The most extreme customer arrived at 5 AM, bringing along a small stool and a thermos, showing clear experience in the art of waiting. The local city inspectors were originally tasked with monitoring stalls, making sure they closed by 8:30 AM to avoid disrupting traffic and public order.

But now, no other stalls were operating in the area, and only Lu Qin remained. The inspectors only had to keep an eye on Lu Qin’s stall, which left them feeling somewhat idle. Sometimes, when the stall went past closing time, they didn’t force him to close down. They didn’t dare.

It wasn’t that Lu Qin wouldn’t close his stall, but the customers wouldn’t let him. They were all extremely passionate.

If anyone dared to ask Lu Qin to pack up, customers would take their small stools and swing them at your head.

They really would!

Even if blood were to spill, no one would stop eating Lu Qin’s food. In fact, some of the inspectors’ family members were even in line for the food.

Who would dare stop their own parents from having breakfast, risking the wrath of being labeled unfilial?

Of course, no one dared to. So, the inspectors’ work gradually shifted to maintaining order in Lu Qin’s line.

As long as the customers didn’t cause any major disturbances, everything would go smoothly. Sometimes, the inspectors even joined the line to buy food.

They were shocked to discover that a breakfast costing only a few dozen yuan tasted like the luxury food from a five-star hotel. It even surpassed the breakfast served at a five-star hotel in terms of taste. It was absolutely delicious!

Lu Qin’s food was nothing like the other street breakfast stalls. The taste completely overshadowed everything else. No wonder so many people were willing to queue up. Even if they had to stand in line for hours, they would still come!

“Wow! So many people are already in line so early? Lu Boss said 8 AM in the group chat!”

“The line’s like a twisted knot! But for that delicious taste, waking up early is totally worth it!”

“I’ve given up on breakfast for five years, but today I’m definitely breaking my fast!”

_____

The new lotus blossom pastries and layered oil cakes were only made in small batches of 100 each for the tasting. The lotus blossom pastries, two in a box, were priced at 50 yuan.

The layered oil cakes, four pieces in a box, were priced at 35 yuan. The ingredients for these two pastries weren’t expensive, but the skill, labor, and time cost were significant. However, for the customers, the price was still very reasonable.

The taste of Lu Qin’s pastries was even better than those offered by upscale hotels, where they cost hundreds of yuan per serving. So, customers didn’t hesitate to spend. If they were slow to act, they’d miss out, and it was a bargain for them.

The early birds who managed to buy the new lotus blossom pastries and layered oil cakes couldn’t hide their proud smiles. A sense of superiority was written all over their faces.

“I got it, and you didn’t! You’re a little loser!”

“The lotus blossom pastries are really beautiful! How do they taste?”

“The layered oil cakes look so good!”

“Damn! I missed the alarm and came late! My lotus blossom pastries and layered oil cakes! I want to taste them so badly!”

“Lu Boss’ snack stall is getting better and better, I’m giving it a thumbs up! I can eat something different every day from now on!”

_____

Before 9 AM, Lu Qin closed up the stall. Over thirty customers didn’t manage to get food, and they angrily grumbled. After waiting in line for so long and now being hungry, they could only go buy from the nearby Sunshine Bun Shop.

The bun shop owner, who had been struggling to get customers, greeted the customers with a smile, surprised that he was picking up leftovers from Lu Qin’s queue.

“Boss, I’ll have two pork buns and a cup of soy milk.”

“Hey, you also have green bean paste, then give me a cup of that, and three flower rolls!”

“One corn and a shao mai, hurry up, I’m starving!”

_____

The bun shop owner rushed around, but his face was full of smiles. However, after customers ate a couple of buns, they couldn’t help but frown.

“What kind of buns are these? The dough is not soft enough, and the filling is average. It’s bad!”

“Boss, your green bean paste is sour and gooey!”

“Are these shao mais made by feet? The taste is terrible!”

“Bad! Not good!”

“Not good at all!”

____

The customers’ unanimous negative reviews left the bun shop owner stunned, and the smile on his face faded.

“How could this happen?”

The bun shop owner checked.

“What’s wrong with the buns and shao mai? The green bean paste is supposed to taste like this, there’s no sourness at all.”

He was puzzled—he’d been making the buns the same way for years, and his family always ate them without complaint.

“Forget it, forget it. This is just a low-level bun shop. Eat with your eyes closed and forget the taste!”

“I can’t eat this. I want Lu Boss’ fried rice noodles!”

“Compared to Lu Boss’ crystal shrimp dumplings, it’s like heaven and earth.”

The owner was frustrated to hear his buns being torn apart, his nose practically twitching with anger.

____

He’d at least paid for some training at New Oriental for his steaming skills. While it wasn’t anything special, they were decently tasty. But these customers were so picky!

Do they think they’re eating with the Emperor’s taste buds?!

Though he thought that to himself, the customers were still his bread and butter. They paid real money for those buns, so the owner could only swallow his frustration.

The bun shop owner wasn’t even trying to compete with Lu Qin’s snack stall. He was already picking up the customers that Lu Qin didn’t want—how could he compare?

In the afternoon, Lu Qin went back to the Lotus District Food Festival to set up his stall again. Yesterday, he was outside; today, he was right inside the venue, in a prime location, with the stall fee waived.

Very nice.

Lu Qin knew there would be a large crowd, so he prepared plenty of food for sale. As soon as the Lu Qin snack cart arrived, the other stall owners took notice. This was the big shot who, on his own, had managed to steal the whole food festival’s crowd the day before.

A food god bullying the newbie village!

But today was different. Lu Qin was now part of the food festival, and he had set up in the most prominent position. Soon enough, a huge wave of customers rushed in from the entrance.

“Has Lu Boss arrived?”

“Over there! Go get in line!”

“I want fried rice noodles!”

“Twice-cooked pork and mapo tofu, forever the best!”

_____

Not far away, Qin Hui looked at this scene, a satisfied smile on his face. Before, he didn’t realize Lu Qin’s snack stall was so popular. Now that it had joined the food festival, it was bound to bring in a massive amount of traffic.

As expected, customers flocked to Lu Qin’s stall, and a long line quickly formed. The official live stream for the food festival was restarted, and the host, Wu Yiyi, highlighted Lu Qin’s stall right at the start.

“Everyone, as you can see, Lu Qin’s stall is incredibly popular! It’s only been set up for a short time, and the line is already dozens of meters long!”

“I heard Lu Qin’s stall has been a hit at the University Town Night Market in Annan District, but I didn’t know! I’m sure the foodies in the live stream know about it, though!”

The comments section exploded. Stars, glow sticks, and other gifts flooded in!

“It’s our most handsome snack stall owner from Annan District—Lu Boss!”

“His food is so great! I want to be his wife, so I can eat his food every day! Just thinking about it makes me happy!”

“Just dreaming!”

“We’ve got new products today—lotus blossom pastries and layered oil cakes. I’m already on my way!”

_____

Wu Yiyi watched the live stream’s popularity rise and felt her confidence soar. She held her live streaming equipment, broadcasting while in line, eager to get her hands on all the snacks to do an eating broadcast.

Other stall owners, seeing the growing crowd, started preparing for a big sales push.

“Grilled skewers! Fresh lamb skewers! Buy one and get a free drink!”

“Small lobsters—75 yuan for three pounds! Get two bottles of beer for free!”

“Squid! Stinky tofu! Big sausages! Freshly fried!”

“Cold noodles! Ice jelly!”

_____

The shouting never stopped!

Ten minutes later, the stall owners were dumbfounded. They’d been yelling for so long, but not a single customer came their way—they all rushed toward Lu Qin’s stall!

It was as if there was only one stall in the whole venue—Lu Qin’s snack stall, and the rest were just decorations!

Lu Qin’s stall was really unbeatable!

In casino terms, this was a total win!

chapters.title: Chapter 198
chapters.content:
Chapter 198 There were no customers, could it be that we’re setting up a shady stall?

Food Festival Exhibition Area. It had just opened, but a sea of people had already poured in. At first glance, it seemed like a hot scene. There were more people than yesterday.

This was mainly because Lu Qin Snacks had set up a stall, attracting a large crowd of customers. But after about ten minutes, the other stall owners started to feel something was off.

Damn it!

Why were all the customers flocking to Lu Qin Snacks?

Not even a single one came to us!

Lu Qin’s stall had swept up everyone!

“Hey handsome, come have some skewers, the line at Lu Qin is so long, you’ll be waiting for ages!”

“What did you call me?”

“Handsome…?”

“Say it again.”

“Handsome…”

“Ah, that feels good~! I won’t have the skewers, I’ll save room for Lu Qin’s fried rice noodles and thousand-layer oil cake, I’m craving it!”

The skewers stall owner couldn’t help but swear, their business was down, and they even had to hear “handsome” a few more times, it was just too shameless!

_____

“Young man, our spicy hotpot is really good, come try it!”

“No, I want to eat Lu Qin’s crystal shrimp dumplings.”

____

“Pretty lady, our crayfish is very cheap, buy three pounds and get one free!”

“I won’t eat it! It definitely can’t compare to Lu Qin’s!”

____

“Boss, come try…”

“No thanks!”

“I haven’t even finished talking!!”

____

It was really strange!

Had these people been charmed by Lu Qin’s boss?

Why were they all lining up there?

The other stall owners were glaring, turning red with envy and frustration.

“Grandma! There are customers, but none come over to us! This is just weird!”

The crayfish stall owner slammed down their spoon and grabbed a spicy crayfish, biting into it with a loud crunch, not even spitting out the shell, clearly furious. The roasted pig’s trotters stall owner waved their fan, hoping the fragrant oil could attract some customers, just one would do.

But Lu Qin’s stall was busy frying twice-cooked pork, with thin slices of ham being cooked into lamp-shade shapes, glistening with oil and an incredibly rich aroma, practically tempting the customers’ noses.

Even though the roasted pig’s trotters owner was waving a banana leaf fan, it was useless. The title of the king of Sichuan cuisine wasn’t for nothing, and the twice-cooked pork under Lu Qin’s boss was just beautiful! It instantly heightened everyone’s appetite!

The spicy hotpot stall owner had yelled themselves hoarse, but not a single customer came. Out of the over a hundred food stalls, the best business was from the fruit and drink stalls. For snacks, everyone went straight to Lu Qin.

They monopolized it all!

This was pure devilry!

Not to mention not covering the stall fees, if they didn’t sell the ingredients today, they would have to throw them away. They were losing money like crazy!

If this was the case, it would have been better if Lu Qin’s stall hadn’t even set up here! Let them eat meat on the next street, and we could at least have some soup here.

What kind of food festival was this? It was just unlucky!

One by one, the stalls started to get quieter, with stall owners grumbling, hoping to chase the flies over to Lu Qin’s side. It was strange again, someone observed carefully, and there wasn’t even a single fly at Lu Qin’s stall, it was spotless. This made customers even more eager to go to Lu Qin!

Suddenly, a few delivery guys in yellow outfits came in, drawing quite a bit of attention.

“Lu Qin’s snack stall? Where is it?”

“The place with the longest line?”

In the whole food festival, you didn’t need to look anywhere else because there was only one stall with customers, and the line was terrifyingly long!

The three delivery guys hurriedly grabbed their boxes and ran over. Everyone was curious, who still orders delivery?

“Excuse me, is 【Handsome to the point of being chopped】 the boss here? Your delivery has arrived!”

“【Lu Qin’s leg accessory】, is that you? Your delivery is here!”

“I’m here!”

“Over here!”

____

Everyone saw, the one with the rather vulgar name of 【Handsome to the point of being chopped】 took the items.

Some were curious about what this person who chose such a name looked like, was he really that handsome?

After seeing him, all the onlookers fell silent. They believed this person used to be handsome, but now he seemed like he’d been chopped up beyond recognition.

To sum it up in two words—abstract!

【Handsome to the point of being chopped】 and a few others ordered deliveries, but it wasn’t food. Instead, they had small folding chairs and power banks from the supermarket. Everyone quickly understood—they were planning for a long haul!

Even if they had to wait one or two hours, they were determined to buy Lu Qin’s snacks.

As for the other stalls, they weren’t even a consideration. Wu Yiyi and her two staff members also felt that something was off. With so many stalls at the food festival, it seemed as though they were just a backdrop, and there hadn’t been a single sale. It was too strange!

Even the official live-stream crew came to queue for Lu Qin’s snacks!

Why was this happening?

Could it really be that Lu Qin’s snacks were that delicious?

The answer was clear.

Unprecedentedly delicious!

The other stall owners gritted their teeth, some even flipping their chairs in frustration, ready to close up!

In the office, the assistant rushed back in a panic.

“President!”

Qin Hui, who was sipping tea and reading the newspaper, frowned when he saw the assistant rushing in empty-handed.

“I sent you to buy Lu Qin’s snacks, why are you empty-handed?”

The assistant didn’t care about Qin Hui’s reprimand, blurting out the important matter.

“What? Not a single customer at the other stalls? How is that possible?”

Qin Hui jumped to his feet!

Earlier, someone reported that within the first two hours of the festival, the total number of visitors had already exceeded three thousand, so it should have been bustling by now.

“President, you’ll see for yourself if you go!”

The assistant couldn’t believe it either, but that was the reality. Qin Hui left the office and went downstairs. Three minutes later, when he arrived on-site, he was dumbfounded.

Not a single customer at the other stalls, and some had already packed up. But in the hot zone, there was a long line, stretching in an exaggerated S-shape, slowly narrowing.

A queue of three to four hundred people!

If they stood in a straight line instead of this crooked formation, it would probably extend beyond the festival’s area!

It was outrageous!

A monopoly!

Qin Hui frowned and asked, “Didn’t we have two to three thousand people? Why are there only a few hundred now?”

The assistant replied, “There were that many people before, but after they bought from Lu Qin, they all left.”

Qin Hui asked, “With so many snack stalls, didn’t they buy from anyone else?”

The assistant explained, “Lu Qin has main dishes, snacks, desserts, and drinks. Once customers buy from Lu Qin, they lose interest in the other stalls because their stomachs can only hold so much!”

“Damn! That’s insane!”

Qin Hui cursed under his breath, not expecting this situation to unfold. Some snack stall owners who recognized Qin Hui ran over to complain.

“President Qin, what’s going on here? I’ve been to many food festivals, and it’s usually all about variety, but why is your Lotus Zone like this, with one stall dominating everything?”

“Leader, we’ve been operating for over two hours, and not a single sale. Why did we even come here?”

“This is crazy! Not a single customer! Are we running a shady stall?”

Qin Hui was also troubled, not knowing how to respond. He couldn’t just say their food wasn’t good, that the stall owners’ skills weren’t up to par, and their food couldn’t compare.

If it was just one or two stalls, it wouldn’t be a big deal, but over a hundred stalls with no business—could all of them be bad at cooking?

Qin Hui dared not say anything rash. If he did, he’d either be beaten or drowned in spit. There were plenty of well-known snack stalls in this festival!

It could only mean that Lu Qin was too formidable!

This was a dimension reduction strike!

“Why don’t you just wait until Lu Qin’s stuff runs out?”

“When they run out of food, won’t customers start coming to your stalls?”

chapters.title: Chapter 199
chapters.content:
Chapter 199 Rescue! Dare you sell raw twice-cooked pork to me and try it!

Wait until everything from Lu Qin is sold out?

Then it’s our turn?

Is that even something a person would say?

Several stall owners were immediately angry, with the urge to rebel. They had secretly checked the Lu Qin food truck earlier. The ingredients were stacked in boxes, one on top of the other.

Sell out?

By the time Lu Qin sells out, it’ll almost be closing time.

So, what’s the point of even trying?

Looking at the dissatisfied looks of the other stall owners, Qin Hui thought for a moment and said, “If it’s really not working, you could try offering some promotions—like buy three, get one free, second item half price, or giving away drinks with the purchase. There are always ways to survive!”

What Qin Hui suggested was indeed a solution. But the long-time vendors had already thought of it.

Why wouldn’t they have tried promotions? The small lobsters, which normally sold for 40 yuan per jin, were now 75 yuan for three jin!

Grilled skewers were discounted in set meals, with Happy Fatty Water thrown in for free!

The lamb spine, normally 79 yuan a serving, was now only 19.9 yuan, at a huge discount!

The lemon tea—buy one, get one free!

These promotions had already been rolled out, but the profit margins were slim, hoping for increased sales with low margins. Yet, it was still ineffective!

The dominance of Lu Qin’s food stall was too strong. The business just wouldn’t pick up. Additionally, the herd effect—no one would visit the stalls with no customers, and the crowded stalls became even more popular!

Those in drought perished, and those in flood perished as well. At this entire food festival, only Lu Qin had any business, the other stalls had turned into mere background scenery.

It was really frustrating!

“Leader, you’ve got to help us! We can’t even make back the cost of the stall rental at this rate!”

“We are all from Lianhua District, but Lu Qin is from An’nan District. How can we let them dominate like this?”

“Exactly! We have so many famous eateries in Lianhua District. How can we let one outsider trample all over us? It’s humiliating for Lianhua District! As president of the Lianhua Food Association, you’re going to bear the brunt of this!”

Hearing these words, Qin Hui’s face changed slightly.

They weren’t wrong.

Lu Qin’s food stall came from An’nan District, yet it was dominating in Lianhua District, especially during this crucial food festival. It was being broadcast live online.

If the public picked up on this, it could have a major impact on the food culture of Lianhua District. If that happened, wouldn’t Qin Hui, as the president of the food association, be in big trouble?

A cold sweat trickled down his forehead. He looked up, narrowing his eyes at the distant Lu Qin food stall. The popularity was so high, it could easily become a trending topic!

Damn it!

It was like inviting trouble into the house!

Although this food festival had recruited food vendors from all over Jiangcheng, most of the stalls were chosen from Lianhua District. After all, Lianhua District was the host, and its local stalls had priority.

Jiangcheng frequently held food festivals.

One reason was to promote the food culture of Jiangcheng, facilitating communication and development in the food industry. The second reason was to bring a memorable food feast to citizens and tourists.

In previous food festivals, there were always crowded scenes, with customers lingering in front of their favorite stalls, eating, checking in, and taking photos.

During this period, there would always be a few popular stalls that attracted more customers and had long lines. But never before had there been such a situation—except for the prominent Lu Qin stall, all other stalls were deserted.

It was an exaggeration!
