This phenomenon must be stopped, or it would become a serious issue. Qin Hui furrowed his brows, his mind racing.

“Leader, how about we kick out Lu Qin’s food stall?”

One stall owner suggested.

“That won’t work!”

Qin Hui waved his hand. Lu Qin had been invited by him personally. There was no reason to kick them out for no reason. Moreover, even if they were driven out, they could just move to the next street and attract all the customers there, leaving this side with no business.

After thinking for a while, Qin Hui suddenly had a flash of inspiration. He motioned for the stall owners to gather around and whispered a few words to them.

The stall owners hesitated for a moment, then nodded. They had no other choice, they would have to make some sacrifices. Qin Hui’s strategy was shared among the stall owners, and most of them thought it was a good idea.

The solution was—find “plants”!

The vendors contacted their friends, relatives, and acquaintances to come and queue in front of their stalls, creating the illusion that their stall also had good business.

In this way, new customers would not mindlessly queue at Lu Qin’s stall. Otherwise, the desolate scene at the other stalls would continue, and no one could save them. The method worked!

Half an hour later, other stalls, except for the “plants,” had gained some new customers. The food festival finally started to look a bit more normal. However, Lu Qin’s food stall was still unusually crowded, continually attracting many new customers.

Although Qin Hui’s strategy was effective in bringing customers to the other stalls, the business wasn’t booming. At best, they might cover the stall rental fees.

Damn it!

The bulk of the profit was still going to Lu Qin’s food stall, the outsider. Qin Hui sat in his office, his fingers tapping on the desk. Lu Qin’s food stall was proving to be a tricky problem.

They were hosting a food festival in Lianhua District, but the An’nan District stall was making a killing here. The stalls from their own district could only scrape by with a little profit to cover the rent. This couldn’t continue.

The first two days of publicity for this food festival were already done, and in the coming days, even more customers would flock in. If they allowed Lu Qin’s stall to continue raking in profits, everyone would know it came from An’nan District, and Lianhua District would end up a laughing stock!

“It looks like I need to call for reinforcements.”

Qin Hui thought for a long time before muttering to himself.

As president of the Lianhua District Food Association, he had some influence in Jiangcheng’s food scene.

He pulled out his phone and called Old Zhan from Niuwai Hutong, the head chef of Siwei Ju, and Chef Qian from Chuanman Lou. After the calls, seeing that they all agreed to help, Qin Hui let out a sigh of relief.

Lianhua District wasn’t short of capable people; now, he would see if Lu Qin could still act so arrogantly tomorrow.

Meanwhile, Boss Lu had no time to care about the operations of other stalls. He was too busy with his own stall. A customer had been waiting for a long time, losing patience, and kept urging Lu Qin to cook faster.

This middle-aged man had been in the queue, smelling the strong aroma, nearly drooling. He watched other customers get their orders and immediately sit down to start eating, which made him even hungrier.

Finally, when it was his turn, he was an impatient person and urged Lu Qin several times to hurry. Normally a patient person, Lu Qin was starting to get annoyed and said, “Sir, if I cook it half-done, the taste won’t be as good. Would you accept that?”

“I accept nothing! Try selling me half-cooked twice-cooked pork, and see what happens!”

This customer was clearly the unreasonable type, and Lu Qin furrowed his brow slightly.

“Sir, you haven’t paid yet. I’m not serving you. Next!”

chapters.title: Chapter 200
chapters.content:
Chapter 200 Off-duty is no longer bribery! The pajamas are a bit too rude!

The middle-aged man was stunned, not expecting that this young person wouldn’t do business with him.

Damn!

Don’t you understand that the customer is king?

A few words of urging, and you dare to snap back at the customer?

“You dare not do business with me!”

The middle-aged man, used to being arrogant, directly blocked the stall. His tall frame stood there, refusing to move. The customers behind him wouldn’t be able to buy anything.

“You’re being unreasonable! Can’t you see that Boss Lu hardly drinks water, and he’s working all the time? Why do you keep pushing him?”

“Move aside! I won’t do business with you! Just causing trouble!”

“Everyone is lining up this way! If you can’t wait, go buy from someone else!”

Ignoring the series of complaints from the other customers, the middle-aged man stood firm, blocking the stall. He glared at Lu Qin, silently telling him, “You have to do business with me today! Otherwise, you won’t make any sales after me!”

Lu Qin’s eyes turned cold, he wasn’t afraid at all. Though fighting directly was not an option in today’s law-abiding society, he had ways to make the other party scared.

For example, dislocating his arm and then putting it back. The hospital wouldn’t find any injuries, but it would make the other party experience intense pain. His high-level combat skills were just waiting to be put to use.

When dealing with such people, you can’t be weak or polite, because the more you are, the more arrogant they become, and they’ll think they can take advantage of you. Good people are always bullied. Such people only fear toughness.

Just as Lu Qin was about to leave the food cart, a young man walked out from the queue. The young man gently patted the middle-aged man on the shoulder, and Lu Qin decided not to leave anymore.

It was Officer Zhang from the South City Police Station.

“This sir, please don’t make trouble in a public place.”

“The boss has the right to refuse to sell to you. You can go buy from someone else.”

Officer Zhang’s tone was calm.

“Who are you, to interfere in my business? Get out of the way!”

“Watch your tone.”

“Respect? What respect! I…”

Before the middle-aged man could finish his words, he was instantly drenched in cold sweat and his legs started trembling.

Officer Zhang made just one motion, pulling out his police ID from his pocket. The black vertical leather wallet was flipped open, showing a shiny police badge and the words “Public Security.” The ID card had a photo, name, police station, and badge number. It was obviously real.

Gulps

The middle-aged man swallowed hard, too scared to say anything.

“Do you know the consequences of insulting a police officer? Do you want me to educate you on it?”

“Do you want me to make an extra shift?”

“No, no, sorry, officer, I was wrong, I apologize, I’m leaving right now!”

The middle-aged man muttered, lowering his head and quickly running away. The crowd watched, feeling great satisfaction. It felt good to see someone like that getting a lesson!

Officer Zhang went back to the queue, but the customers pushed him to the front.

“Officer, you go first!”

Officer Zhang couldn’t refuse and had no choice but to accept the offer.

“Boss Lu, same as usual, one fried rice, two boxes of ice crystal cakes, and one order of crystal shrimp dumplings. My daughter loves your ice crystal cakes, I have to come and line up every day after work.”

“Thanks for the support.”

Lu Qin added a lotus seed pastry into the bag, as a small token of gratitude for Officer Zhang’s help earlier.

Officer Zhang smiled and said, “I’m a police officer, openly giving bribes isn’t good.”

Lu Qin also smiled and said, “Off-duty, it’s not bribery anymore.”

Officer Zhang didn’t refuse, and the lotus seed pastry looked really good. He figured his wife would like it when he took it home.

_____

This small incident didn’t affect the business of the food stall. In fact, it even attracted more customers. The queue grew longer again.

In the evening, dusk descended. After work, people began to search for food. The number of customers at the food festival surged. Lu Qin picked up the little one.

“Yao Yao, don’t get too tired, there are a lot of customers today.”

Lu Qin reminded the little one. He had prepared a lot of food, and it would take a while to sell it all. The little one probably wouldn’t be able to handle it for long.bSure enough, after one hour, the young boss lady got tired.

At that moment, the “boss lady” arrived. Shen Muyan was wearing a light blue top, with the hem tucked into a white, tight-fitting, pencil skirt. Her fair, smooth legs were long and straight. Because she was driving, she wasn’t wearing high heels, but a pair of white casual shoes.

Shen Muyan’s shoulder-length hair fell behind her, her stunning beauty and cold, graceful aura attracted everyone’s attention as she walked over. Her looks, temperament, and figure were absolutely captivating to both men and women!

“Mom~!”

The little one called out in a soft, sweet voice, causing the hearts of many men to instantly drop into an icy pit. Such a goddess, a once-in-a-lifetime beauty, not only married but also had a child.

Shen Muyan was here to grab a meal and also see if there was a chance to charm Boss Lu.

“There’s no such thing as a free lunch, get to work.”

Just as the young boss lady got tired, the big boss lady stepped in. So, the business at Lu Ji’s food stall became even more bustling!

The influence of these two beauties was extraordinary. The third window had customers screaming in line!

What kind of magical food stall is this?

The food is delicious, and the people are gorgeous!

It’s worth the long wait!

By 8:30, all the food had been sold out.

A large number of customers who hadn’t bought anything sighed. Luckily, there were plenty of other stalls nearby, so they could only make do with something else.

Though the queue was long, they felt dissatisfied not getting what they wanted. They decided to come back earlier tomorrow. The other stall owners breathed a sigh of relief as they saw Lu Ji closing so early. Finally, Lu Ji had finished selling, and now it was their turn to eat.

It had been so frustrating! Once Lu Ji closed, only then could their stalls actually open for business!

Damn!

Should they just wait until this time tomorrow to set up their stall?

Shen Muyan had personally handled the business at one of the food stall windows.

____

In just two hours, there had already been more than 20,000 in earnings.

That’s crazy!

Boss Lu wasn’t just making money, he was printing it!

“Today’s income broke 150,000, time to call it a day.”

Lu Qin checked his phone wallet, and his daily income hit a new high. Shen Muyan was deeply shocked. The income from this small food cart in a day was equivalent to the monthly gross profit of a small factory.

It was terrifying.

To bring a food stall to this level, no one else could do it. It just shows how amazing Boss Lu is!

Shen Muyan had only eaten one order of crystal shrimp dumplings, and she was very hungry. The little one’s mouth didn’t stop, eating chicken wings or sweet oil cake. But when she got home, she still wanted to eat, craving a tasty frog dish!

The group went back home. Lu Qin went to the kitchen to cook, and Xiao Mi helped. The wealthy lady and the little one watched TV in the living room. Lu Qin cooked garlic pork and stir-fried kidney, both of which the mother and daughter eagerly fought over, finishing them all.

“Dad~! Your food is always so delicious~! Can I eat it for the rest of my life~?”

Lu Qin drank some water poured by the little one, smiling and saying, “What if you grow up and get married?”

“Then I don’t want to grow up or get married, I want to stay with Dad forever~!”

“Hehe.”

Lu Qin rubbed the little one’s head, feeling warm inside.bAt night, Shen Muyan didn’t feel like going back. As usual, she slept with the little one.

After the little one fell asleep, Shen Muyan came to the living room in her pajamas. She had half-dried her hair. She couldn’t use the blow dryer for long as it would damage her hair, so she sat in the living room to let it dry naturally.

The wealthy lady’s pale pink silk pajamas were a bit revealing, and her delicate, fair collarbone could even nurture little goldfish. Boss Lu was still in the living room watching TV, and the wealthy lady walked straight over to him.

chapters.title: Chapter 201
chapters.content:
Chapter 201

Shen Muyan wore Boss Lu’s deep green flip-flops. Her pair of jade-like feet had a bewitching allure. Her ten toes were fair like pearls, sculpted like artwork—delicate and lively.

Tap, tap, tap. She moved gracefully, each step seemingly treading on Boss Lu’s heartstrings.

Just as the rich lady was about to reach Boss Lu—

Suddenly—

Splat!

Was the floor too slippery?

Shen Muyan lost her balance and fell backward. Boss Lu reacted quickly, reaching out to catch the rich lady around the waist. The air froze. Their eyes met. Time seemed to freeze at that moment.

Shen Muyan’s cool and elegant face was framed by a few loose strands of hair, her long lashes fluttering slightly. What Lu Qin felt beneath his palm was soft and springy. Through the thin silk, he could clearly sense the delicate, smooth sensation beneath his fingertips. The atmosphere was suggestive.

Their faces seemed like magnets, drawn irresistibly towards each other. Their breathing got closer. A faint blush spread across the rich lady’s fair cheeks. Looking into his deep, closing eyes, she shut her own. It was coming. The plan had worked.

Even though she was the mastermind and everything was within her expectations and control—Her heart was still racing uncontrollably.

When it came to kissing, she had zero experience. She and Lu Qin had probably never kissed. The time they drunkenly slept together, even though they did the deed, neither of them remembered anything. Even if there was a kiss, there was no feeling. So it could be counted as if they’d never kissed.

In other words, this was Shen Muyan’s first kiss. She didn’t know what it would feel like.

Wonderful? Gentle? Fiery? Wild? Sticky with passion?

Nervousness was definite—with a hint of expectation.

His lips weren’t too thin or thick, with a smooth shape—like sunlight at dawn, warm and radiant, full of confidence and charm. Hmm, probably comfortable.

Thoughts swirled chaotically in the rich lady’s mind. Suddenly, Shen Muyan felt her body lighten, then land on the ground as the arm around her waist loosened. She stood upright.

Boss Lu looked at Shen Muyan with a strange expression. He hadn’t fallen for it. The floor hadn’t been mopped—no way she slipped. That nearly-fall was stiff and obviously fake.

Where did she even get such a cliché, soap-opera trick?

Boss Lu hadn’t expected the rich lady to be so good at playing games—but it didn’t match her style. He guessed someone was giving her advice. Lu Qin thought of Fang Ya.

A single, nosy love guru giving dumb advice to a romance novice, cold CEO.

Interesting.

Boss Lu was curious to see what tricks they’d try next. The plan failed. The rich lady stood frozen for three seconds.

“Going to sleep.”

She didn’t dare meet Boss Lu’s eyes. Throwing out two words, she turned and went into the bedroom.

In the room—The little one had already powered off, sprawled out in all directions, taking up the center of the bed.

“Who did she inherit this sleeping posture from?”

Shen Muyan kicked off the flip-flops and got on the bed. She stretched out her jade-like foot and nudged the little one to the side, then grabbed the blanket and tossed it on her butt.

The little one slept like a rock, smacked her lips, instinctively grabbed the blanket and pulled it over herself, continuing her sleep mode. Shen Muyan turned off the ceiling light and switched on the bedside lamp, then muted her phone and messaged Fang Ya.

A few seconds later, Fang Ya replied.

Shen Muyan described it in full.

After repeated failures, the rich lady started to seriously doubt her love guru’s abilities.

The rich lady couldn’t be bothered to argue. She sent a selfie straight away. Fang Ya opened the picture. Silk nightgown, thin and form-fitting. This was the ultimate seduction outfit!

The rich lady already had a flawless figure, and in that sleepwear, her curves were exquisite, her tight, snow-white skin tempting the wildest fantasies. Even as a woman, Fang Ya almost got a nosebleed.

Charm? More than enough.

Then what was the problem?

Could it be that Boss Lu… wasn’t up to it?

Hmm. Very possible.

If the rich lady went full seductive mode, even women couldn’t resist—Let alone a man. He’d be emptied out several times over!

Fang Ya reached a conclusion.

Fang Ya spit out her White Rabbit candy. She had found something even sweeter.

Fang Ya popped the candy back in.

President Shen, the top student in applied science and finance with an IQ of 180, a woman with an exceptional business mind and management skills—In matters of romance, had a negative IQ. And just like that, she was tricked into the test by her dog-head strategist Fang Ya.

The rich lady pretended to go to the bathroom. She flushed the toilet to make noise, then walked out, groping around in the dark, and slipped into Boss Lu’s room. Boss Lu was already asleep. The space beside him dipped as a soft, fragrant body slipped under the covers.

Skin touched skin. One was warm. The other was cool and silky. One was truly asleep. The other was pretending to sleep. But the closeness, the skin contact—The one faking it was nervously stiff as a board.

In the dark, her pretty face was already flushed red. Boss Lu wasn’t a saint. Over the past few days, he’d seen the rich lady quite a bit—especially with her teasing behavior now and then. Naturally, thoughts had crossed his mind. He turned over in his sleep.

Drowsily, as he shifted, he seemed to touch something. It felt like someone was beside him. But he couldn’t tell if it was a dream or real—it was all muddled, and he was too sleepy to open his eyes.

The rich lady didn’t dare move, frozen like a corpse. Boss Lu reached out and hugged her waist, resting his head near her shoulder and continued sleeping. His steady breathing blew warm air against her neck, giving her a strange tingling warmth.

At the same time, waves of electricity shot through her body. Still, she didn’t dare budge. Boss Lu thought it was a dream. The rich lady treated it like a dream, too. And so, one dreaming and one awake, they lay that way for a long, long time…

Just as the rich lady was about to fall asleep—Boss Lu shifted again and draped his heavy thigh across her belly.

Ugh…

After a short while, an uncomfortable feeling spread across her stomach. Her internal organs were being pressed—of course it felt awful.

chapters.title: Chapter 202
chapters.content:
Chapter 202

The rich lady tried to push that thigh away, but after much struggling and finally pushing it aside, it draped back over her again shortly after. It was as if she was locked in place.

What should she do?

Asking online—urgent.

If she stayed locked like this all night, what would happen when Boss Lu woke up in the morning?

There would be no way to explain it.

Could she say she went into the wrong room?

Would Boss Lu believe that? She didn’t even believe it herself!

If it wasn’t a mistake, then it must’ve been intentional. Good grief, that would mean her motives were highly questionable!

Soon, Boss Lu’s arm around her waist shifted to her lower abdomen, then her shoulder, her face… and kept wandering…

It made the rich lady feel ticklish, yet she didn’t dare laugh or move. She held herself still, and it was pure torture. No one knew how long it lasted, but finally, Boss Lu turned it over.

His heavy thigh moved away, and the rich lady was finally free. She took the chance to quietly escape from the room.

At the same time, she completed the verification. Back in the little one’s room, the rich lady took out her phone.

Her eyes suddenly lit up, full of curiosity. Shen Muyan blushed. Of course, she didn’t go into detail—she simply described what had happened.

Earlier, she and Boss Lu had been lying close together. He had moved around quite a bit, and she could feel something. If she could feel it, that meant there was no problem.

If she couldn’t, then there would be a problem. Even if Shen Muyan was inexperienced in romantic matters, she still understood this kind of thing.

Fang Ya, however, wasn’t ready to let it go.

Shen Muyan didn’t want to continue with this kind of topic—Fang Ya was absolutely shameless.

Now the question remained: since Boss Lu was clearly fine, why wasn’t he interested in the rich lady?

Gay or brain-damaged—that was the question.

After thinking it over, the strategist had to admit her plan had flaws. Fang Ya told Shen Muyan to sleep first, while she planned to stay up all night studying “The Little Seductress’s Tricks to Tease the Male God.”

But the rich lady couldn’t fall asleep. Her face was still flushed, and she could still feel the warmth of Boss Lu’s breath.

A sleepless night.

…

The next morning, Boss Lu got up to wash up. He felt like he’d had a half-erotic dream last night. He dreamed that the rich lady had entered his room and gotten into his bed…

He remembered holding her waist, touching her face…

After that, there was no “after”—Boss Lu couldn’t remember. But when he woke up, he noticed that one side of the bed looked like it had been slept in. More importantly, there was a faint, pleasant scent…

It seemed like the scent on the rich lady.

Could it be…

The rich lady also woke up. The two of them met at the bathroom door. Boss Lu looked slightly puzzled and conflicted—had she actually come into his room last night?

He didn’t ask. The rich lady wore a calm, indifferent expression. Without much emotion, she greeted him with a soft “morning” and walked into the bathroom.

Afterwards, the rich lady naturally ate breakfast and went to work as usual. Boss Lu couldn’t make heads or tails of it, and in the end, decided not to worry about whether it was real or not. Whatever.

The little one was still sent off by Shen Muyan. Today, the rice noodle stall at the Jingyuan market was closed. The couple who ran it had taken a break because their daughter-in-law had given birth.

Lu Qin had just about a hundred jin of rice noodles in stock, and he didn’t want to bother sourcing from another shop. He would wait for that one to reopen. So, no fried noodles this morning. He saved the noodles for the food festival.

This morning, he sold mung bean slush, crystal shrimp dumplings, ice crystal cakes, layered oil cakes, and lotus blossom pastries. Over 200 servings of each were prepared. The morning rush lasted just one hour, and the sales were brisk.

At 3 p.m., Lu Qin restocked and continued his stall at the food festival. Yesterday, on either side of his stall were a marinated skewers stand and a roasted sweet potato skin stall. But today, those were gone.

In their place were a fried noodle stand and a crystal shrimp dumpling stall. Further along, there was a stir-fried dish stand—apparently Sichuan cuisine. The items they sold were strikingly similar to Lu Ji Snacks.

It turned out that Lao Zhan from Niuwei Alley, pastry chef Zeng Quanyou from Siweiju, and Chef Qian from Chuanmanlou had all shown up.

They weren’t there to make money—profit was secondary. The honor of the Lianhua District’s culinary scene was at stake.

The three didn’t know each other; each was simply carrying out their mission at the food festival. But when they saw the owner of Lu Ji Snacks, they all froze.

Huh!

Old Qin really overreacted. It was just a young guy!

Culinary skills come from years of practice.

Have you ever seen a twenty-something who’s already a top chef?

What is this, an anime like Cooking Master Boy?

They were older and more experienced—picking on a kid was hardly fair!

But since they were already there, they might as well have some fun. There were so many food lovers—might as well show them what real flavor is!

Ha!

These folks were in for a treat!

“Lu Ji is here! Lu Ji is here!”

“Boss Lu is two minutes late! Do you know how many crystal dumplings and cakes I could’ve inhaled in those two minutes?”

“Go line up! Go line up!”

“I’m here! I’m here!”

“My beloved twice-cooked pork!”

Before Lu Ji’s food truck had even come to a full stop, a swarm of customers rushed in. Old Zhan, Zeng Quanyou, and Chef Jiang from Chuanmanlou were almost knocked over by the wind of the stampede. Their eyes widened.

W-What?

So many people!

They had never seen such a scene—this level of frenzy!

But still!

Was it really that popular, or just a show?

They couldn’t believe a young guy could cook something that spectacular. They were more inclined to believe these were paid actors—shills hired by the boss.

Young people these days—rather than honing their craft, they just rely on flashy gimmicks and flow.

Well!

Time to teach him a lesson!

The stall was finally set up!

Now, customers noticed that next to Lu Qini’s stall were other stands also selling fried noodles, crystal dumplings, and Sichuan food! And these chefs looked legit—clearly top-tier professionals!

“Hey? That master over there just started frying noodles!”

“He looks really professional!”

“But… why is his wok folded like that?”

“You don’t know? Real pros use that kind of wok—it’s easier to toss and stir, and the food doesn’t spill during high-speed flipping!”

“Oh~! I see! But…”

They looked over at Lu Qin’s side. People started ordering fried rice noodles. Lu Qin fired up the stove…

“But Boss Lu’s wok doesn’t seem folded?”

“I’ve seen Boss Lu cook noodles—his moves are like a painting, pure artistry!”

“Boss Lu is the real deal—his skill level is maxed out!”

“But there’s always someone better. That master looks like a serious pro!”

“It feels like a cooking showdown—they’re both frying at the same time!”

“Let’s see which one tastes better—whichever’s better, I’ll buy!”

“Wasn’t Lu Ji’s food good enough for you?”

“It is good! Almost the pinnacle! But if there’s something even better, of course I’ll go for it!”

“What kind of picky taste buds have we all developed…”

…

As the crowd chatted, they watched. A cooking showdown—now that was entertainment. Some even brought out little stools, sunflower seeds, and soda to enjoy the spectacle.

Master Lao Zhan saw Lu Qin already cooking, so he also fired up his stove. But in his mind, he hesitated—should he go easy on this young guy?

chapters.title: Chapter 203
chapters.content:
Chapter 203

Old Zhan knew that the nearby Lu Qin snack stall came from the Annan District. Qin Hui brought himself over for that very reason. At the food festival, many stalls from the Lianhua District were overshadowed by a snack stall from Annan District. It was truly unacceptable.

Looking at it now, their stall is really booming!

Why are these customers rushing like addicts to line up?

Is their food really that delicious?

Or did they hire a bunch of paid actors to create a false impression?

After all, the stall owner is so young, and it’s hard to imagine someone that young having exceptional culinary skills.

Cooking isn’t something you can fake; it takes years of hard work and dedication.

In the Chinese culinary world, which famous chef hasn’t been over forty years old?

I’ve never heard of a young person in their twenties having extraordinary cooking skills—most are just apprentices.

When Old Zhan was in his twenties, he wouldn’t even eat the fried noodles he cooked himself.

So, even though Lu Qin’s business is booming, Old Zhan is sure that it’s not because the owner’s fried noodles are particularly good.

After thinking for a moment, Old Zhan cleared his mind and stopped thinking about all the irrelevant details.

To truly excel at something, you need unwavering focus—that’s the key to success. Old Zhan has been frying noodles for 28 years, in a small, dirty shop that hasn’t moved in all these years.

There aren’t any tables for customers to sit down and eat, it’s only available for takeaway. Old Zhan sells his fried noodles cheaply and never raises prices randomly.

He also wouldn’t raise prices just because his business is doing well. The only reason he might raise prices is if the cost of ingredients goes up. Old Zhan is indifferent to everything else, but he is passionate about fried noodles. Perhaps that’s why his fried noodles have reached a level of mastery.

When locals mentioned the Guozi Street in Lianhua District, everyone knew about Old Zhan’s fried noodles. Soon, someone recognized him.

“Hey, it’s Old Zhan, from Jin Feng community under my building!”

“Old Zhan’s fried noodles? Are they famous?”

“Old Zhan’s fried noodles might not be famous all over Jiangcheng, but in Guozi Street and Huaimin Road, they’re household names!”

“Old Zhan’s fried noodles may not be the hottest, but they’re definitely the tastiest!”

“Old Zhan’s noodles might not be well-known, but they’re really good. When I was in high school, my classmates asked me to bring them so often. My backpack was so heavy, filled with fried noodles!”

“Old Zhan has been frying noodles for half his life. He must be at the peak of his craft now. He’s really impressive. I ate his fried noodles for breakfast for months straight!”

“I haven’t had Old Zhan’s fried noodles. I wonder how they compare to Lu Qin’s.”

“Lu Qin’s fried noodles are great too! I’d rate them as top-tier!”

“I just got here, but I don’t think they can beat Old Zhan’s. As the saying goes, ‘The old ginger is spicier!’”

The discussion between Old Zhan and Lu Qin quickly spread among the foodies. Most of them were not only food lovers but also enjoyed gossip.

While eating delicious food, they also enjoyed the drama, adding fuel to the fire at the food festival. Before long, the crowds at both Old Zhan’s fried noodles stall and Lu Qin’s snack stall began to swell.

The vendors next to Lu Qin, Zeng Quanyu making crystal shrimp dumplings and Qian Shifu specializing in Sichuan cuisine, stopped their work to watch. They thought their only competitor was Lu Qin, but they didn’t expect there was another “rival.”

They couldn’t help but wonder, was the owner of Lu Qin not a specialist, but a jack-of-all-trades?

So young?

Could they be good at everything, but a master at nothing?

Though it wasn’t an official competition, the two stalls were side by side, both cooking fried noodles, almost like a competition for supremacy.

Lu Qin also noticed something odd. Yesterday, the stall on his left was selling skewers, but today it suddenly changed. Stalls at the food festival required an upfront payment, so changing stalls mid-way wasn’t possible.

Now, the stall next to his was also selling fried noodles. Could this be targeted at him?

And the stalls on his right, one selling crystal shrimp dumplings and the other Sichuan cuisine—are they also targeting him?

Interesting.

Lu Qin, however, was quite pleased with this “targeting.”

Every time the festival ended, there were always a lot of customers who couldn’t get food and complained. If another stall could draw some of those customers away, it would help quiet their complaints.

Old Zhan started frying noodles. Lu Qin had already taken a few orders. Lu Qin then entered his zone and began focusing entirely on frying noodles. Some customers excitedly took out their phones to start filming, already thinking about what to title the videos.

Gossip is always better when it’s juicy.

Excitement was written all over the faces of the crowd, and more people kept gathering. Qin Hui, observing from afar, anticipated this kind of effect, but he still underestimated the public’s love for gossip. The food festival became even livelier as a result.

However, this was good—Qin Hui had brought these three people over to help secure Lianhua District’s position at the Jiangcheng Food Festival. Lianhua District couldn’t let Annan District bully them like this.

The process of frying noodles was actually very short. It was fast food, relying on high heat and quick stir-frying. But the flames rising from the wok and the sight of the chef quickly flipping the ladle were quite visually striking. Some of the spectators even clapped and cheered!

“Impressive!”

Old Zhan and Lu Qin remained completely unfazed by the discussions or filming around them, staying focused. The fried noodles were ready. The aroma wafted in the air. The customers took deep breaths, their mouths watering.

Old Zhan made three portions of fried noodles, while Lu Qin made five. Lu Qin used his ladle to pack five portions into boxes, while Old Zhan placed three portions into round plastic containers.

At this moment, Old Zhan slightly turned his head and glanced at Lu Qin’s five portions. His eyes flickered.bA true expert can tell at a glance. Old Zhan had been frying noodles for almost thirty years, and just one look at a portion of fried noodles, judging the color, texture, and the wok aroma, and he knew that Lu Qin’s fried noodles must taste pretty good.

When it comes to fried noodles, the taste accounts for 30% of the quality, while texture accounts for 70%.

For experts, the main competition lies in texture. Old Zhan’s fried noodles, when they pick them up with chopsticks, they come up in a single, easy-to-handle bite, not cLumping together. But with ordinary fried noodles, a single bite can turn into a Lump the size of a fist, and you have to shake it loose—this shows the level of skill.

He didn’t expect Lu Qin’s fried noodles to reach this level. Old Zhan finally took another look at Lu Qin. He didn’t expect this young man to have such solid skills.

chapters.title: Chapter 204
chapters.content:
Chapter 204

Old Zhan and Lu Qin both finished cooking their fried noodles. The phone cameras zoomed in, sliding back and forth to focus on both stalls as everyone began to carefully compare the two. These two stalls were virtually the pinnacle of the fried noodle world!

Everyone could see that when it came to frying noodles, both Old Zhan and Lu Qin’s owner displayed peak proficiency. Their fried noodles were on an entirely different level compared to regular street vendors. Top-tier means top-tier—their skills were undeniable!

In terms of quantity, Lu Qin’s owner cooked five portions in one go, while Old Zhan only made three. Those who paid close attention knew that most vendors typically fry just one portion of noodles at a time. If they try to cook more, the taste and texture suffer. But if a vendor can fry multiple portions at once, and each one tastes the same, it means they’re an expert. So, does Lu Qin’s owner fry more portions because he’s better than Old Zhan? Not necessarily, because the taste hasn’t been judged yet.

Now that the fried noodles were ready, it was time for the most exciting moment!

Which fried noodles taste better?

The answer was about to be revealed.

To compare, the customers who ordered both types of noodles shared them with each other to taste. A group of foodies each took their chopsticks, tried Old Zhan’s fried noodles, and then moved on to Lu Qin’s fried noodles.

To be honest, both were delicious.

They were miles ahead of ordinary street fried noodles!

That was the unanimous verdict.

But if they had to pay the same price for a portion of fried noodles, they would obviously choose the better one!

And so…

The customers who had tasted both began to pick sides.

Before long, the result was clear.

2 votes for Old Zhan’s noodles, and 8 votes for Lu Qin’s noodles.

“I pick Lu Qin’s fried noodles, the texture is smoother and more bouncy!”

“I also pick Lu Qin’s, it’s more fragrant and tasty!”

“I’m sorry, Old Zhan, I’ve been eating your fried noodles for over twenty years, but Lu Qin’s is even better. So impressive!”

“Lu Qin’s fried noodles are unbeatable! I thought Old Zhan’s could hold its ground, but it turns out age doesn’t always mean the best!”

“Only one thing to say: even top-tier fried noodles have differences. I pick Lu Qin!”

“I pick Old Zhan’s, I’ve been eating his for so many years, I’m used to the flavor!”

…

Old Zhan’s and Lu Qin’s fried noodles weren’t in an official competition, but the outcome was clear—the customers knew which fried noodles tasted better.

Most customers who wanted fried noodles lined up at Lu Qin’s stall. There were still a few at Old Zhan’s, but not many. The gap between them was quite noticeable. However, Lu Qin didn’t care about winning or losing. He had only two goals for his stall—to make money and earn system points. Everything else was secondary. So he continued taking orders and frying noodles.

On the other hand, Old Zhan couldn’t continue frying noodles. He had been called over by Qin Hui for a purpose—to prevent the Annan District’s fried noodle stall from being too arrogant. Regardless of how good other fried noodles were, Lianhua District still had Old Zhan’s fried noodles.

Old Zhan had been frying noodles for 28 years, and despite his skill, he never raised prices arbitrarily. Even so, just from frying noodles, he had bought two apartments for his children in the center of Jiangcheng. This proved that his fried noodles still had many loyal customers.

Old Zhan’s fried noodles were a gold-standard brand. His usual hunchback straightened only when talking about his fried noodles. That was the pride of his life!

Frying noodles was the thing he did the most, from his youth to old age, always pushing himself to improve. Through this constant self-improvement, he had reached the top. But today, he met a young man.

Customers preferred the young man’s fried noodles. It meant his fried noodles were better.

“Could I have a taste?”

Old Zhan asked a customer who had just bought two portions of Lu Qin’s fried noodles.

“Sure.”

The customer opened the box and handed it over. Lu Qin slowed down his cooking and glanced over. With everyone watching, Old Zhan took a pair of disposable chopsticks, broke them apart, and picked up a bite.

He ate it!

After swallowing, Old Zhan’s lips moved slightly.

He didn’t speak, but in his heart, he already knew the result.

Where had he lost?

The difference wasn’t just one factor—it was several. The first was the texture—Lu Qin’s fried noodles were more bouncy and chewy.

Why was there such a difference?

One factor was proficiency—Lu Qin could fry five portions at once, while Old Zhan could only manage three. That was raw skill. Another factor was the choice of ingredients.

Old Zhan could tell the noodles used were Anyang rice noodles. He had used them himself before but found that Heyuan rice noodles tasted better, so he switched.

Maybe it was just a matter of habit, using the one he was most comfortable with. But Lu Qin used Anyang noodles and still made them taste better. Second, Lu Qin’s fried noodles had a better balance of oil—rich but not greasy.

This required control over the heat, the speed, and the force of stir-frying. Simply put, Old Zhan’s basic skills weren’t as good as Lu Qin’s. Third, the seasoning.

Everyone’s tastes are different—some like it spicy, some prefer salty, others like it sweet.

But Old Zhan knew that, based on what most people liked, Lu Qin’s seasoning was better. He fully acknowledged this. Old Zhan glanced at Lu Qin. There was no sign of frustration or anger in his eyes—only a slight sense of satisfaction.

The next generation has its champions.

Good.

Old Zhan continued to fry his noodles. He still had improvements to make. Although Old Zhan didn’t say a word, the customers noticed. By not saying anything, he was implicitly admitting that Lu Qin’s fried noodles were superior! The chef himself had acknowledged it!

Wow!

Old customers of Old Zhan’s fried noodles were shocked!

Could Jiangcheng really be hiding some amazing talents?

Lu Qin’s fried noodles from Annan District were even better than Old Zhan’s!

The official live stream of the Lianhua District food festival was broadcasting this moment. Wu Yiyi’s mouth was slightly open, her eyes wide. This scene was truly spectacular!

The live stream suddenly gained traction!

The barrage of comments in the stream was rolling fast!

Most comments were praising “Lu Qin” and “Lu Boss is awesome.”

Originally, the Lianhua District audience had dominated, but now more and more viewers from Annan District were flooding in. Little gifts filled the screen!

Lu Boss was making Annan District proud!

Exciting!

Qin Hui watched the barrage of “Annan District is awesome” comments, his brow furrowed.

How could this happen?

Old Zhan’s fried noodles couldn’t even compare?

This was supposed to be the gold-standard fried noodles of Lianhua District. Qin Hui stamped his foot, frustrated. He had no choice but to watch Master Zeng Quanyu’s crystal shrimp dumplings.

The earlier fried noodle showdown had been highly entertaining for the vendors next door, Zeng Quanyu and Qian Shifu. They knew Old Zhan’s fried noodles—an old man who was single-minded in perfecting his craft, elevating a simple dish to the highest level.

But they hadn’t expected that today, a young man had outdone him.

Was this Lu Qin snack stall from Annan District really this good?

Zeng Quanyu suddenly felt inspired and started preparing the stuffing for his crystal shrimp dumplings. Once placed in the steamer, a line began to form in front of his stall.

Freshly made, crystal shrimp dumplings were about to come out of the steamer, and the crowd eagerly awaited. Lu Qin also had crystal shrimp dumplings, and everyone who’d tasted them was too stunned to speak. They could only mumble, “So delicious, so delicious!”

Lu Qin’s crystal shrimp dumplings could easily be called the pinnacle of the dish!

But the vendor next to him making crystal shrimp dumplings—what was his story?

He seemed quite professional, and his skills shouldn’t be underestimated.

Could this be another showdown?

Here they come!

Once again, the foodies around the stall excitedly cheered!

chapters.title: Chapter 205
chapters.content:
Chapter 205

“Who is the chef making these crystal shrimp dumplings? Does anyone know?”

“I don’t know, but from the way he handles the dumplings, he seems pretty skilled. Maybe he’s someone noteworthy.”

“I don’t think his dumplings can beat Lù Boss’s. Lù Jì’s crystal shrimp dumplings are the best I’ve ever had, no doubt about it.”

Lù Qin had prepared the dumplings at home and brought them to sell at the stall, keeping them fresh and warm in a temperature-controlled box.

Xiao Mi handed out the crystal shrimp dumplings to the customers, and these top-tier dumplings were effortlessly devoured. One bite, and the smooth, fresh taste filled the entire mouth.

The customers’ eyes sparkled, and after eating just one, they seemed to float, completely relaxed and at ease. Ordinary foodies, who grind away at their jobs every day, work hard just to make money.

What’s it all for?

It’s to enjoy these delicious bites after work, isn’t it?

Food is their only source of happiness.

Customers who had already bought Lù Jì’s dumplings were eating the delicate, translucent, and tender shrimp dumplings while lining up at the stall next door.

Anyone brave enough to compete next to Lù Qin must either be crazy or have real skills. Everyone was curious about how the shrimp dumplings made by the other chef next door tasted.

Of course, the official live stream of the food festival jumped on this hot topic and immediately focused the camera on the two stalls. Both selling crystal shrimp dumplings—who would make the better one?

Some were curious about the middle-aged man in white.

Chefs are usually behind the scenes, and even if the food is delicious, customers rarely see who makes it, as they’re all busy in the back. Not all customers are interested in seeing what the chef looks like. So, Zeng Quanyu, this chef from the famous restaurant Sì Wèi Jù, wasn’t widely known.

But Lù Qin was different—he’s out there every day, showing his face.

His face, even more handsome than Wu Yanzu or Peng Yuyan, was already etched in the minds of many customers. The live stream’s viewership had reached 10,000.

Some viewers recognized the chef from Sì Wèi Jù.

“That looks like Chef Zeng Quanyu! I’ve been to Sì Wèi Jù and had his spring rolls—they were amazing!”

“Sì Wèi Jù? That place is super expensive, the average cost is over 200!”

“So it’s a big chef! He’s from Sì Wèi Jù!”

“Now it’s getting interesting! Street food stall owner vs. Chef Zeng from Sì Wèi Jù!”

“Chef Zeng: Young man, making crystal shrimp dumplings? Let’s see who’s better!”

Zeng Quanyu’s crystal shrimp dumplings were ready, and the sweet fragrance of the dumpling skins filled the air.

“Mmm, it smells so good!”

“Just as expected from a chef from Sì Wèi Jù—these dumplings look really good!”

Wang, the owner of Sì Wèi Jù, was also watching the live stream of the food festival. When he saw his restaurant’s name pop up several times in the comments, his eyelids twitched.

Zeng Quanyu had agreed to help out due to his relationship with Qin Hui, and Wang had no objections. But now, the situation had turned into a public showdown between the street stall and Sì Wèi Jù, with the whole thing being broadcast live.

If they lost this, it could affect Sì Wèi Jù’s reputation. However, a street food stall owner shouldn’t be able to beat Zeng, right?

But if Zeng won, it wouldn’t bring much glory to Sì Wèi Jù either. After all, he was an experienced chef.

It wouldn’t be impressive for an experienced chef to beat a small stall owner. But if he lost, that would be a huge embarrassment!

Wang decided to call Zeng.

“Chef Zeng, are you confident? If a street food stall beats you, it could hurt Sì Wèi Jù’s reputation. It might even affect our business!”

“Boss, this is a personal matter. I’m not representing Sì Wèi Jù here,” Zeng replied.

Zeng Quanyu had been confident at first, but after seeing the crystal shrimp dumplings from Lù Jì, his confidence had taken a hit. From the appearance and fragrance of Lù Jì’s dumplings, Zeng could already tell that they tasted just as good.

Wang snapped, “But people recognize you as the chef from Sì Wèi Jù!”

Zeng gritted his teeth.

“Don’t worry, boss. I won’t lose.”

Just as he finished speaking, a customer had already scanned the QR code and paid for one of his freshly made shrimp dumplings. One portion had eight dumplings, enough to satisfy a regular person, and just right for foodies who needed a little something to nibble on.

The customer bought only one portion—after all, they were trying it for the first time, so buying too many would be risky.

At Sì Wèi Jù, Zeng’s crystal shrimp dumplings were priced at 88 yuan per portion, a nice, lucky number. Customers who dined at Sì Wèi Jù felt that the 88 yuan for the dumplings was well worth it. The taste was excellent, and they were truly worth the price.

But now, Zeng was selling them at the same price as Lù Jì—28 yuan per portion. To be honest, this was underpricing. But what could he do? This wasn’t Sì Wèi Jù—it was a food festival, and street food stalls were packed with customers.

If he sold them for 88 yuan, customers would glance at his stall and walk away. It wasn’t because they couldn’t afford it, but because the price didn’t match the atmosphere.

Who was he fooling with that price?

A single shrimp dumpling for 88 yuan?

Meanwhile, Lù Jì’s dumplings—better looking and much cheaper at 28 yuan!

Zeng had never intended to make money here, so he set the price at 28 yuan per portion. Customers scanned the code, paid, and began to taste the dumplings.

They ate them! They ate them!

Zeng eagerly watched for their reactions.

Were they impressed?

Was it delicious?

Had they never tasted anything like it?

But…

There was no reaction.

The customers seemed calm, as though they were eating an ordinary snack.

Some even nodded slightly.

What… that’s it?

Where were their amazed expressions?

Zeng furrowed his brow. His shrimp dumplings had thin skins, juicy fillings, and a fresh, delicious flavor. They were the epitome of crystal shrimp dumplings.

How could they react so nonchalantly?

Were they pretending?

A few customers whispered.

“They’re not as good as Lù Boss’s, but they’re okay. If you can’t get Lù Boss’s, you could consider this.”

“Lù Boss’s dumplings are more bouncy and flavorful. These aren’t quite as good.”

“Turns out Lù Boss is still the master.”

“Sì Wèi Jù, huh? Compared to Lù Jì, they’re nothing.”

Zeng heard all of this and almost coughed up blood. His dumplings were praised at Sì Wèi Jù, especially his signature spring rolls, almond tofu, and crystal shrimp dumplings, which earned unanimous praise from customers.

How could they be so lackluster here?

Slander!

This was absolute slander!

Zeng refused to believe it.

He went out, spent 20 minutes, and bought a portion of Lù Jì’s crystal shrimp dumplings. The appearance of Lù Jì’s dumplings was nearly identical to his own. Zeng took a bite. One bite, and Zeng fell silent.

His expression shifted dramatically.

The onlookers watched curiously.

Seeing Zeng standing there without saying anything, someone couldn’t help but ask.

“Chef, how does Lù Boss’s taste? Come on, tell us!”

“Good, right? Never tasted anything like it?”

“Chef Zeng, losing to Lù Boss isn’t shameful. Even Old Zhan’s fried noodles lost.”

“Lù Boss’s crystal shrimp dumplings are worth studying! You might be able to take it to the next level.”

Every word hit Zeng hard.

This was unbelievable!

How could his dumplings taste like this?

Confucius once said, “Be quick to learn, and don’t be ashamed to ask for help.”

Zeng walked over and finally spoke up.

“Lù… Lù Boss, can you share your dumpling skin ratio… Could you tell me how you make it?”

chapters.title: Chapter 206
chapters.content:
Chapter 206

Once, when Zeng Quanyou had an apprentice, his teacher said that a perfect crystal shrimp dumpling has four characteristics—shrimp head and face, spider belly, crystal-clear transparency, and twelve folds.

After steaming it on high heat for five minutes, a magical moment occurs when the lid is lifted. The crystal shrimp dumplings lie in the steamer, as thin as a cicada’s wing, with skin as white as snow. When eaten, they are smooth and fresh, with a lasting aftertaste. This is what a truly delicious crystal shrimp dumpling should taste like.

It took Zeng Quanyou eight whole years to perfect this crystal shrimp dumpling. That year, his reputation in the pastry world began to unfold. But he never relaxed and continued to pursue perfection, constantly improving the taste and texture of his crystal shrimp dumplings.

He had tasted other well-known pastry chefs’ crystal shrimp dumplings, but he could proudly say that his dumplings had always remained unrivaled. Even if there was competition, it was more of a tie, with no clear winner.

But now… this young man’s crystal shrimp dumplings made Zeng Quanyou’s soul tremble. Both the taste and texture were better than his own! Before tasting them, he didn’t believe it, but now he believed it!

He couldn’t help but feel immense curiosity. What kind of hands and techniques could create such a taste and texture in crystal shrimp dumplings?

Zeng Quanyou, eager to learn, asked Lu Qin. But after asking, he regretted it. Because he knew asking was futile.

Could someone easily reveal their techniques? Those techniques are their livelihood. Even a chef would keep a secret when teaching their apprentice. No one would lay their soul bare.

But to Zeng Quanyou’s surprise, Lu Qin casually revealed his methods. This young man, who was so handsome, smiled slightly and said in a calm tone, “Take 100 grams of clear flour, 50 grams of cornstarch, mix evenly, pour in 200 grams of boiling water to cook, then add 50 grams of cornstarch and knead.”

A qualified clear flour skin must have a thickness of just 0.2mm. This miraculous moment comes from using a white starchy water that discards the gluten in wheat flour. It separates the wheat into its fine elements, leaving behind powder that is then settled and dried—this is the completely gluten-free wheat starch, called clear flour.

Zeng Quanyou furrowed his brows. The key to the texture of the crystal shrimp dumpling skin lies in the proportions. But when he heard Lu Qin’s proportions, he was stunned. This ratio was identical to his own!

So why was there such a difference in texture?

And how did Lu Qin make the skin so thin?

Thin, yet not breakable?

The key reason why his shrimp dumplings were not as good as Lu Qin’s lay in the texture of the clear flour skin. Seeing Zeng Quanyou’s confusion, Lu Qin said, “According to the characteristics of the clear flour, the key is to heat it in steam. You must use boiling water and stir quickly, allowing the starch to gelatinize as it heats, which will make the clear flour dough smoother, whiter, and stickier.”

With this answer, Zeng Quanyou had an epiphany. It was like the mystery that had haunted him for years was suddenly revealed.

He understood now. Truly, he had made a huge leap forward.

“Thank you for the guidance.”

Zeng Quanyou looked at Lu Qin with gratitude. The sunlight illuminated the young man, casting a golden halo around him. To easily reveal such a key technique made Lu Qin’s image in Zeng Quanyou’s eyes instantly grow taller.

Master Lu!

Zeng Quanyou felt no disappointment in losing; instead, he was filled with joy for what he had gained. Zeng Quanyou’s willingness to ask questions only proved his point.

Lu Qin had won again!

This time, he had won over two experienced masters from Lianhua District. It was truly impressive!

Qin Hui saw that the two masters he had invited were defeated by this young man, so he hurried over to ask, “Old Zeng, what’s going on? You lost?”

The pastry shop in Lianhua District was as renowned as South Bowen!

How could it lose to a street food stall?

This couldn’t be happening!

“I lost, but it’s not unjust. Master Lu is indeed exceptional!”

Zeng Quanyou was in a good mood. Although his skills were inferior, he had learned a lot. He even added Lu Qin as a friend, intending to exchange pastry techniques with him in the future. Master Lu didn’t reject him and agreed.

Zeng Quanyou was overjoyed. This time, he had truly gained.

“Master Lu?”

Qin Hui’s mouth twitched, and he felt something was wrong. It seemed like the people he invited had become stepping stones for Lu Qin.

Master Lu had become “Master Lu”?

This wasn’t how the script was supposed to go!

Qin Hui looked towards his last hope.

Master Qian, it was your turn!

Master Qian, who had been observing, finally understood what had happened. This young man had managed to defeat three masters!

In addition to pastry, could he also make Sichuan dishes?

A jack-of-all-trades?

Master Qian couldn’t believe it.

But then, he saw the young woman in the food truck handing out dishes of twice-cooked pork, chicken tofu flowers, and imperial chicken wings to customers. He had no choice but to believe it.

Could he really make a dish as sophisticated as the chicken tofu flower?

Not possible, right?

Master Qian didn’t want to start cooking and went directly to the counter to order a serving. He thought it was absurd to sell a national banquet dish at a street stall. But when he saw the chicken tofu flower, he was stunned.

The dish demanded not only a superior taste but also a reflection of profound technique and cultural heritage. The broth alone requires over ten ingredients, including chicken, duck, and pork bones, simmered for over six hours to create a clear, flavorful stock. However, what he received was just a simple plastic takeout box.

Master Qian frowned, thinking this wasn’t possible. But when he tasted the broth, his taste buds exploded. Master Qian froze for a moment. This was not just water—it was soup!

Clear as water, yet rich with the flavor of meat.

This wasn’t just plain broth, it was genuine mao tang, a national banquet-level stock. Mao tang is made by washing the chicken and duck bones and simmering them slowly over low heat, cleaning away any impurities. If the heat is mishandled, it becomes simple white soup. The difference in taste and texture is substantial. Master Qian sipped the tofu flower soup.

In the clear broth, tender chicken meat blended seamlessly. For someone unfamiliar with it, they would mistake it for tofu flower. But it was more than that!

It wasn’t just chicken, it was “chicken-like,” “meat-like,” and better than meat. This was the magic of chicken tofu flower!

Master Qian knew this wasn’t something he could make himself. Staring at the chicken tofu flower in his plastic container, Master Qian was humbled.

chapters.title: Chapter 207
chapters.content:
Chapter 207

Seeing Master Qian’s expression, the onlookers immediately understood. Boss Lu has won again, again, and again!

The crowd couldn’t help but want to shout “Who else?” for Boss Lu. Qin Hui’s heart, which had been hanging, finally dropped. He lost again. His entire plan had collapsed.

It wasn’t that the teammates he invited were weak, it was that this young man was just too freakishly talented!

Could this kid be the reincarnation of a food god?

His culinary skills had reached such an incredible level. Qin Hui finally understood why Lu Qin Street Food Stall had dominated yesterday, while other stalls hadn’t made a single sale.

Food is heaven for people.

Lu Qin is their heaven!

Qin Hui couldn’t stop thinking about how delicious the stir-fried noodles from Lu Qin were. They were so tasty that no other stall could compare at the food festival. No wonder customers were desperately lining up to get a taste!

Qin Hui had no way to respond to that.

No solution.

After this incident, Lu Qin’s food stall gained even more fame.

“Hey, which stall has the best food at this food festival?”

“Well, that’s easy to answer—Lu Qin!”

Word spread at the food festival, reaching the entire Lianhua District and even neighboring areas. More and more customers were heading to the food festival.

The flow of people had already exceeded 20,000. Qin Hui was both excited and helpless. This food festival had broken all records for traffic, so he could proudly take credit to the tourism bureau leadership. But the stall driving all that traffic was a small street food stall from Annan District. This was just frustrating.

If you can’t beat them, recruit them.

Suddenly, Qin Hui had an idea.

If he could persuade Lu Qin Street Food Stall to set up every day in Lianhua District, it would be a win-win situation. Qin Hui immediately pulled Boss Lu aside, away from the crowd, and proposed this idea.

“No, I need to take my daughter to school every day, so setting up in Lianhua District is inconvenient for me,” Lu Qin refused.

So it was because of his daughter. That’s easy to solve.

Qin Hui said, “You mean Xingguang Kindergarten? Our Lianhua District’s Leguo Kindergarten is much better. I know the principal, Boss Lu. If you’re willing, I can help you contact them and complete the procedures tomorrow.”

Qin Hui spoke with sincerity.

Lu Qin still shook his head. His daughter had already made friends at her current kindergarten, and switching to a new place might not be ideal.

Seeing that Lu Qin was unwilling to agree, Qin Hui began to panic. This was a huge opportunity, and he had to find a way to bring Lu Qin to Lianhua District. Qin Hui learned that Lu Qin rented a house in Annan District and immediately offered a tempting deal—discounted housing prices.

Qin Hui was glad that he had good connections in real estate. With his wide network, offering the lowest internal price for buying a house was no problem.

“Boss Lu, I can help you get the price down to the minimum for a school district house!”

Qin Hui focused on his daughter as a breakthrough, emphasizing that it was for her future education. Little did he know that Lu Qin already had a stack of business cards, including some from real estate moguls. Additionally, his wife also had extensive connections. Thus, Qin Hui’s offer held no appeal for Lu Qin.

Lu Qin had already built a large customer base in Annan District and wasn’t interested in moving. Qin Hui scratched his head, his mouth dry from talking, but Lu Qin remained unmoved.

“I need to go back to work, the customers are waiting. Sorry,” Lu Qin said, returning to his stall, which immediately attracted the customers’ chatter.

“Boss Lu, if you want to starve me, just say it. Don’t use the restroom as an excuse!”

“Ten minutes in the bathroom, Boss Lu, you’re not really… I know an old Chinese doctor…”

“Boss Lu, if you don’t come back soon, I’m going to report you! Your food is so good, it’s highly likely you’ve been kidnapped!”

Qin Hui had no other way for now and decided to wait for later. Back in his office, Qin Hui received a call from his leader.

“Right, right, boss. The foot traffic during the day has already surpassed 20,000, and it’s likely to reach 35,000 to 40,000 at night, a record high… Yes, yes, it’s all thanks to your brilliant leadership…”

After hanging up the phone, Qin Hui felt good after receiving praise from his superior. Half an hour later, the leader would personally come to inspect the event. Qin Hui immediately instructed staff to prepare and went to meet them at the entrance.

Half an hour later, a black official car pulled up at the food festival entrance, followed by two Mercedes-Benz cars. The leaders of the tourism bureau got out of the car, and several middle-aged men, who looked like business owners, got out of the other cars. Qin Hui quickly went up to greet them warmly.

After some brief introductions and pleasantries, Qin Hui learned that the middle-aged men were big investors from Kyoto. They were interested in investing in a commercial food street project in Jiangcheng, a multi-billion-dollar project.

Qin Hui’s heart skipped a beat. He immediately became more cautious and serious, not daring to slack off. These investors had come to check out the food festival in Lianhua District as part of their investigation, hoping to sample some of the standout local snacks.

Such a large project, however, was still just an intention—they would need a serious investigation to proceed. Jiangcheng had been mocked as a ‘food desert’ by outsiders, and if its food culture wasn’t developed, no matter how good the investment conditions were, these investors wouldn’t invest.

“It’s really lively here,” the leader said and nodded, signaling Qin Hui to lead the way and show the investors around the food festival.

Qin Hui, confident in his role as president of the food association, began enthusiastically presenting the specialty foods of Lianhua District.

“This is [Zhang Ji Beef Pancake], with crispy skin, tender meat, and a unique fragrance that customers love. We have three branches in Lianhua District, and it’s very well-known.”

“This one here is [Fatty’s Water Boiled], another Lianhua District specialty. The difference between water-boiled and spicy hotpot is the richer and more fragrant broth. It’s also packed with a variety of marinated ingredients. No dipping sauce is needed, but the flavor will leave you craving more.”

“Our [Jiang Ji Crawfish] is our most iconic dish, with the most branches. We use fresh crawfish and the flavor is rich and delicious…”

As expected, Qin Hui was great at presenting food. The leaders nodded in approval, clearly impressed with his descriptions. The leader walked a few steps forward and casually asked a few customers for feedback on the water-boiled dish and the beef pancake.

Receiving positive responses, the leader turned back to the investors, smiling. However, the investors had expressionless faces, as if they had seen this sort of thing a thousand times before.

They had visited other cities and tasted their so-called ‘specialty food,’ but nothing had stood out. Now, all the food being presented here felt very similar. The flavors were inconsistent, and it wasn’t enough to build a true food street.

Jiangcheng already had decent commercial food streets like Tianhong Square and Changlin Road University City Night Market. To stand out and be competitive, it needed something unique and eye-catching.

The leader could tell the investors weren’t interested in the so-called ‘Lianhua District specialties.’ His plans to impress them with food were stalling.

He quickly signaled Qin Hui to introduce something more impressive. Qin Hui understood the look in the leader’s eyes, but he was sweating—he had already presented the best and most famous food in Lianhua District.

If only there was something even more substantial to show…

But as they walked ahead, an incredibly popular food stall caught everyone’s attention!

chapters.title: Chapter 208
chapters.content:
Chapter 208

A long queue stretched in front of Lu Qin’s snack stall, its intimidating presence overpowering the other nearby stalls. Before Qin Hui could speak, several investors became intensely interested.

“What’s going on with this snack stall?”

“The number of customers here is really something, isn’t it?”

They had seen many food stalls with long lines before, but they had never encountered such an overwhelming queue! To call it terrifying wouldn’t even be an exaggeration!

The leader from the Cultural Tourism Bureau immediately brightened up and urged Qin Hui to quickly introduce the stall. The leader had never expected such a popular food stall in the Lianhua District. Since there were so many people waiting in line, the taste must surely be undeniable.

Qin Hui, feeling a bit anxious, had originally intended to introduce Lu Qin’s stall from the day the food festival began, as it had been extremely popular from the start.

However, given that it came from the Annan District, how could he introduce it?

“Leaders and distinguished investors, this is the popular food stall, Lu Qin, from the Annan District…”

But this was the food festival of Lianhua District!

Now that the leader had already spoken, and the investors were watching him closely, Qin Hui had no choice but to omit the Annan District origin, giving a simple introduction. The investors were intrigued.

According to Qin Hui’s introduction, this stall offered many types of food.

The leader of the Cultural Tourism Bureau said, “Gentlemen, you can try some of their snacks.”

The investors nodded. With so many customers in line, they were curious to see how good the food at this stall really was.

Qin Hui replied, “Please wait a moment, I’ll go tell them.”

One investor stopped him, saying, “No need for special treatment, we’ll queue up just like everyone else.”

Qin Hui was taken aback and reminded, “There are a lot of people in line; it might take a long time…”

“It’s fine, we’re customers too, we’ll wait our turn,” one investor said.

The other investors had no objection either. In fact, they wanted to see if this exaggerated number of people was genuine or a staged trick.

Qin Hui had no choice but to join them in line, though he couldn’t help but worry. After waiting for about ten minutes, the investors couldn’t take it anymore.

The mouth-watering smell of the food was wafting through the air, teasing their taste buds. As more customers passed by with food, the investors looked at the delicious snacks in their hands, and it became harder to resist. It smelled incredibly good, and their cravings were awakened.

“Leaders, gentlemen…” Qin Hui began.

At first, he thought the investors were frustrated with the long line, but then he noticed their intense stares – they were hungry. To be honest, the leader was also starving.

At first, he didn’t want to make exceptions, but seeing the investors’ expressions, he couldn’t hold back anymore. Qin Hui quickly made arrangements.

He was informed that the leader and the investors had arrived for an inspection, and it was a significant matter, so they gave them VIP treatment. Of course, they still paid for the food.

Once the food was in hand, the investors were eager to try it. They were excited, almost desperate.

Although they didn’t show it outwardly, inside, they were starving. After tasting the crystal shrimp dumplings, the Princess Chicken Wings, and the chicken tofu flower. Their faces immediately lit up with pleasure, and their eyebrows shot up in delight.

“This is so good! These shrimp dumplings are so tender!”

“The tofu flower! I never thought a snack stall could make such a delicious tofu flower! Amazing!”

“These Imperial Chicken Wings are authentic! Stunning!”

Their praise gave the leader of the Cultural Tourism Bureau a sense of confidence and pride. The big shots from Kyoto must have seen a lot in their time, but right now, their faces looked like a poor kid trying gourmet food for the first time. The leader also tried some fried noodles.

!!!

He was stunned. It was like an electric shock ran through him—surprised and delighted at the same time. Qin Hui found an empty table, cleaned it, and helped the leaders and investors sit down, then quickly went to buy more food from Lu Qin’s stall.

The earlier food was just for tasting, now they were going to indulge. The leader feigned apology and said, “Gentlemen, I truly apologize for the poor arrangement. To have dinner here in such an open-air space… I am so sorry.”

Actually, seeing how the investors were devouring the food, it was clear they were thoroughly enjoying it. How could it be a bad reception?

It was actually impeccable!

Even a grand banquet at the Jiangcheng Grand Hotel couldn’t compare to this level of satisfaction. The investors quickly waved it off, saying that although they had initially planned for a banquet, they had eaten enough luxurious food already. Now, encountering such delicious street food, it was even better than anything they had experienced. Qin Hui quickly brought back several more portions of food.

“Here’s some mapo tofu, and Chinese-style dim sum like ice crystal cakes, lotus pastry, and layered oil cakes…”

Each dish was laid out on the table. Qin Hui had barely set the food down when the investors, unable to hold themselves back, started grabbing pieces of food.

Chomp, chomp, chomp!

They ate while feverishly praising the food. Seeing how hungry the investors were, the leader figured they wouldn’t have enough to eat, so he pulled back a bit and let the guests have more. Finally, after eating their fill, the investors let out a few loud burps.

“This is the best meal we’ve had on this trip!”

“It looks like we’ll be staying in Jiangcheng a bit longer.”

The leader and Qin Hui exchanged glances, both hearts pounding. They knew the investment deal was now a real possibility. Their biggest fear was that the investors would hurry off. If that happened, the chances of a deal would be slim. The leader was almost grinning ear to ear. The investors pulled out their phones and began snapping photos of Lu Qin’s snack stall.

“This Lu Qin snack stall is definitely a signature food stop in Jiangcheng!”

“Yes, yes, absolutely!” the leader nodded, agreeing with a big smile.

But Qin Hui’s mind was racing.

Damn it, I still haven’t managed to win over Lu Qin. The stall is from Annan District.

What am I going to do?

If these investors find out the stall is from Annan District, it could ruin everything!

He could feel sweat forming on his forehead.

…

At Annan District, Investment Promotion Bureau

Office of the Director…

The director was furious, swearing loudly without any filter. It was clear that the director was absolutely livid. The investors from Kyoto were initially supposed to come to Annan District. The director had worked hard to make connections to get them to consider Annan District first.

However, the investors had suddenly decided to visit Lianhua District instead. This was largely due to Lianhua District organizing a food festival.

On top of that, people from Lianhua District had secretly played a part in stealing the investors away. The Investment Promotion Bureau in Annan District had prepared everything, and now, they were left empty-handed.

“How could your confidentiality work be so poor?”

“Did the information leak out?”

“Why did Lianhua District suddenly host a food festival? It must have been targeted at these investors!”

The director had no one else to blame, so he lashed out at his secretary. The secretary was nervous and didn’t dare say a word. The director had failed to cover all his bases. Initially, if Lianhua District had held a food festival, Annan District could have held one too. It would have been a fair competition. But now, it was too late.

Lianhua District had stolen the investment opportunity right from under their noses.

How shameless!

chapters.title: Chapter 209
chapters.content:
Chapter 209

Shen Muyan was waiting in the Investment Promotion Bureau for Director Nie Guoqiang’s call. She had a project to discuss with her partners from other provinces, and as she had a good relationship with Director Nie, she decided to come to discuss it.

Hotels like Euray, the five-star chain, have surprisingly low profits. After accounting for the high operational costs, the annual profits are minimal. However, the real money comes from developing shopping centers and commercial streets around the hotels, which were acquired when the hotel was built.

Although wealthy women have money, they need partners to share the risks. Shen Muyan was working even harder now because Lu Boss’s earning ability had surpassed hers. The president of a five-star hotel chain was making less money than a street vendor—this was indeed shocking.

Shen Muyan sat elegantly on the sofa in the lobby, sipping her tea. The secretary, looking disheveled, came out, having just endured the wrath of Director Nie.

In truth, this matter had nothing to do with him; it was because Lianhua District had stolen the investors, making their boss angry with nowhere to vent, so the secretary had to bear the brunt of it.

The secretary walked out, looked up, and saw Shen Muyan sitting on the sofa. He adjusted his posture but couldn’t bring himself to smile.

“President Shen, you can go in now, but Director Nie is not in a good mood,” the secretary said.

He didn’t elaborate, as mentioning this would be embarrassing for Annan District. Shen Muyan stood up and walked into the director’s office. Director Nie had just finished venting his anger and was calming down with a cup of tea. He cleared his throat and started discussing the matters at hand. However, the issue with the investors kept bothering him.

Halfway through, Director Nie received a call from the red hotline. This hotline connected directly to senior leaders. Shen Muyan stopped speaking, stood up, and moved to the window, wanting to avoid the conversation.

Director Nie spoke on the phone for about five minutes, and his expression began to change dramatically. He heard several key words.

“Lu Qin Snack Stall.”

The latest news was that a group of investors from Kyoto had decided to invest in Jiangcheng, and the key reason was the “Lu Qin Snack Stall.” It seemed that these investors were food lovers. A few snacks had won them over.

Who would dare say Jiangcheng was a food desert now?

What excited Director Nie even more was that Lu Qin Snack Stall was originally from their Annan District. However, Lianhua District was doing everything possible to try to keep Lu Qin’s owner in their district. Even if they couldn’t win his heart, they had to keep him there.

Director Nie hung up the phone, murmuring to himself, “Lu Qin Snack Stall?”

In the quiet office, this sentence reached Shen Muyan’s ears. She turned her head and looked at Director Nie.

The Lu Qin Snack Stall? It couldn’t be another stall with the same name, right?

Why would Director Nie mention Lu Qin?

Shen Muyan was intrigued. Director Nie quickly called his secretary in. He realized that Shen Muyan was looking at him and smiled awkwardly.

“President Shen, I apologize for the interruption. Please wait a moment. I have something urgent to handle.”

As he spoke, the secretary entered the office.

Director Nie quickly said, “Go check if there’s a snack stall called Lu Qin in Annan District. Has it been to the Lianhua District food festival recently?”

“This is important. Go now, it’s related to a large investment.”

As soon as the secretary heard the word “investment,” he immediately thought of the Kyoto investors. He didn’t dare delay and quickly went to investigate.

Meanwhile, Director Nie’s tension remained high. Though the Lu Qin Snack Stall came from Annan District, there was still a risk of it being swayed to Lianhua District. After all, moving from one district to another wasn’t that hard.

As long as the temptation was strong enough, it could happen easily. Shen Muyan, now certain that the “Lu Qin Snack Stall” mentioned by Director Nie referred to Lu Boss’s stall, recalled that she had witnessed his ability to make money at the Lianhua District food festival just the day before. She was deeply impressed.

“Director Nie, the Lu Qin Snack Stall you mentioned, I might know it,” Shen Muyan said calmly.

“President Shen, you know it?”

Director Nie was taken aback but quickly realized and stepped out from behind his desk.

“President Shen, please, please sit.”

He personally poured Shen Muyan a cup of tea and looked at her expectantly, eager for more details.

“The owner of the Lu Qin Snack Stall is the father of my child,” Shen Muyan said nonchalantly.

Her statement was accurate, and she had no intention of explaining the details of their divorce. Director Nie’s mind immediately buzzed. This was a surefire win!

The owner of Lu Qin Snack Stall was Shen Muyan’s husband. There was no way Lu Boss would be swayed to Lianhua District now. As long as his wife and child were in Annan District, Lu Boss would never leave. Finally, the weight on Director Nie’s chest lifted.

From then on, he stopped mentioning the snack stall and focused on finalizing Shen Muyan’s project. The discussion proceeded smoothly, with all the favorable policies granted. Thanks to Lu Qin’s connection, Shen Muyan managed to profit easily from the negotiations.

Once the deal was done, Shen Muyan took a sip of her tea, reflecting on how it would have been better if she hadn’t divorced Lu Boss. Impulsiveness was indeed the devil.

She had once thought her decision was well-thought-out, but now it seemed childish. Lu Boss, he was such a great guy. As she thought about Lu Boss, Shen Muyan felt content. Director Nie, noticing the smile at the corner of her lips, assumed it was because the discussion had gone well and quickly said,

“President Shen, when you have time, please bring your husband along. I’ll treat you both to a meal.”

The invitation from the director of the Investment Bureau was a great honor. Shen Muyan naturally agreed. Although she didn’t consult with Lu Boss, she had already taken the lead in this matter. Now, she just needed to convince him. If he didn’t agree, she would have to use a strategy.

Her “strategist” thought of a trick.

“A spoiled woman is always the most effective.”

Shen Muyan disliked this idea and couldn’t bring herself to do it. So, this idea was promptly discarded. But if it came down to it, she might just use it.

…

Lianhua District

Food Festival

The food ingredients inside the food truck were visibly diminishing. Lu Qin estimated the time. At this rate, the little one wouldn’t be able to play “little boss lady” today.

By 5 p.m., everything should be sold out. Qin Hui was still waiting for Lu Qin to finish her shift. The leader had already spoken: they must do everything possible to recruit Lu Boss.

Once Lu Boss moved to Lianhua District, the investment deal would follow. Qin Hui was still amazed that a small snack stall could influence such a big investment project.

Incredible!
