Zhai Lin had acted for more than 20 years and always treated his work seriously. He never brought a script to the set, all his lines and his co-actors’ lines were memorized by heart, ready to be delivered at any time.

“Young people have no responsibility. When something goes wrong, they shirk responsibility and blame a serving of fried noodles. It’s ridiculous!”

“Director Wen, let me demonstrate for him!”

Director Wen glanced at Zhai Lin and nodded, stepping aside. He admired Zhai Lin and decided to let him take over.

Zhai Lin took the fried noodles with confidence. “Watch closely, this is how it’s done!”

Wu Chen: “Th-that fried noodles, I already ate it.”

Zhai Lin chuckled, “What’s the big deal? Back in the day when conditions were tough on set, we ate stale food and still performed perfectly.”

This is what being an actor is all about!

Wu Chen: “…”

Actually, he still wanted to finish the remaining fried noodles.

Director Wen casually called “Action” and let Zhai Lin start his performance. Zhai Lin’s expression became serious as he got into character. He took a bite of the fried noodles, and the wonderful taste exploded on his taste buds. His expression changed instantly.

These fried noodles!

How… how are they so good?

Zhai Lin’s expression changed, and then, just like Wu Chen before, he couldn’t stop himself and ate several more bites!

The moment he did this, a cold sweat broke out on his back!

Damn!

He messed up!

He also forgot to say his lines!

In just a few seconds, his entire attention had shifted to the deliciousness of the fried noodles!

This!

These fried noodles must be cursed!

chapters.title: Chapter 221
chapters.content:
Chapter 221

This stir-fried noodles really is something!!

Zhai Lin broke out in cold sweat. The moment he took the first bite of the stir-fried noodles, he was completely stunned!

His body uncontrollably wanted a second bite!

A third bite!

As he chewed, the crispy rice noodles bounced around in his mouth, the savory and rich ham cubes, and the tender, almost fish-like eggs created a delightful taste that kept teasing his taste buds. It was impossible to stop!

When you encounter this kind of stir-fried noodles, who can even remember their lines?

Zhai Lin stopped talking.

Wen Zhiyong was puzzled.

Why was Zhai Lin also experiencing the same issue as Wu Chen?

Assistant Director Chen Dong looked on, confused. Shu Man’er and the other actors also had stunned expressions.

What’s going on here?

The onlookers outside murmured.

“What’s happening? Is this scene that difficult?”

“Why is everyone forgetting their lines after eating the noodles?”

“Have these actors never eaten street-side stir-fried noodles before? No way!”

“Haha! So it’s Mr. Lu’s stir-fried noodles! Okay, that explains it! We understand now!”

“What’s so special about it? Is the stir-fry really that good?”

“Can’t explain it, you’ve got to try it yourself. You’ll be spending your meal allowance at Lu Ji every month!”

“No way! Really?”

“Mr. Lu is amazing! I love seeing these celebrities acting like they’ve never had stir-fried noodles!”

“This is hilarious! One plate of stir-fried noodles has made two seasoned actors forget to act! What if we brought out crystal shrimp dumplings, Bosi oil cake, twice-cooked pork, or mapo tofu… what would happen then?”

“Stop naming dishes, buddy! You’re making us hungry!”

“When are we finishing this? I’m waiting to buy some Lu Ji snacks!”

…

On the set, Director Wen walked up to Zhai Lin. He looked at the veteran actor, then at Wu Chen, still confused. At this point, Wu Chen felt no pressure anymore.

See? It’s all the noodles’ fault.

Zhai Lin, the veteran actor, was just as affected! No one was immune!

Wu Chen secretly enjoyed seeing Zhai Lin’s embarrassment. Wasn’t he the one trying to lecture me before?

Now, look at you, all red in the face. Feels good, doesn’t it?

Zhai Lin said nothing. He silently handed the remaining third of his stir-fried noodles to Wen Zhiyong.

He gave him a look, signaling, “You’ll understand once you try this stir-fried noodles.”

Wen Zhiyong frowned, didn’t bother changing his chopsticks, and grabbed a bite of the noodles.

Immediately, the same expression appeared on Wen Zhiyong’s face. His eyes slowly rolled, then stopped, displaying the words “Delicious”!

The crew members all stared at Wen Zhiyong. From his expression, they could tell the key information. The stir-fried noodles must be exceptionally good!

So good that even the set tyrant, Director Wen, didn’t say a word!

Wen Zhiyong took one more bite, then silently finished the remaining third of the noodles and tossed the plastic food box into the trash.

“Xiao He!”

He called his assistant over.

“We can’t use Mr. Lu’s noodles. Go outside and buy some regular stir-fried noodles or something. Hurry up!”

“Yes, yes, okay.”

The assistant was confused but quickly obeyed and ran out of the set.

Wen Zhiyong told the crew, “Take a short break. We’ll shoot once the props arrive.”

Then he walked towards Lu Qin.

At this point, everyone figured it out.

Shu Man’er was the first to walk over to Wu Chen and nudged his arm, asking, “Wu Chen, is Mr. Lu’s stir-fried noodles really that good?”

“Absolutely! Didn’t you see I messed up twice already?”

Hearing Wu Chen’s answer, Shu Man’er felt a bit hungry. She wasn’t the only one feeling that way. Several other actors, like Peng Kai, were also tempted. Director Wen walked up to Lu Qin, his serious face replaced with a smile.

“Mr. Lu, I’m hungry. What do you have on your food cart?”

Seeing business coming, Lu Qin stood up, pointing to the electronic menu on the cart.

“Here, everything we’re selling today is listed up there. You can take a look.”

Lu Qin walked over, and a minor role actor quickly stepped aside.

“I’ll take an order of stir-fried noodles with two portions of ham. Mr. Lu, start cooking, I’ll decide on the rest in a bit!” Wen Zhiyong said as he studied the menu.

He glanced at the menu and was surprised by how many food options Mr. Lu’s street food cart had.

“Mr. Lu, I also want an order of Yu Xiang shredded pork, Princess Chicken wings, and a piece of this Ice Crystal Cake dessert, plus a cup of green bean soup.”

Wen Zhiyong ordered a “Food and Drink Combo”, which was sure to fill him up.

Lu Qin began preparing the stir-fried noodles, and other dishes were taken out of the heat-preserving container. Wen Zhiyong couldn’t wait any longer.

“Mr. Lu, don’t pack it in bags. Just give it to me directly! How much is it?”

“One hundred and six dollars and fifty cents.”

Wen Zhiyong scanned the QR code to pay and walked over to sit down in front of the monitor, holding the food. Then… he opened the stir-fried noodles, his face filled with excitement.

Bam! Bam! Bam!

Started showing off!

While eating the noodles, he opened the containers of Princess Chicken wings and Yu Xiang shredded pork. When he tasted those two perfect Sichuan dishes, his face was filled with joy.

This was the ultimate!

The ultimate delicacy!

Wen Zhiyong, who had been through countless dinner parties and tasted countless delicacies, now realized that the taste he was experiencing was “something that should only exist in heaven, not something you’d taste on earth!”

The other people on set stared at Wen Zhiyong’s actions and expressions, stunned for a while. Then they started to feel an urge to try the food. The first one to act was Wu Chen.

Since Wen Zhiyong said it was a break, what was there to wait for? He quickly ran over to get stir-fried noodles!

The second one was Zhai Lin!

The third was Chen Dong!

Shu Man’er tried to hold her dignity and asked her assistant to go buy the food. But when she saw the entire crew running over to the food cart, she knew there was no time to wait for her assistant anymore. She quickly stood up.

In an instant, a long line formed in front of Lu Qin’s food cart.

“Mr. Lu, two portions of stir-fried noodles! What’s this Bosi oil cake? Never mind! I’ll take one! Any drinks? Two iced green bean soups!”

“I want a portion of stir-fried noodles! You’ve got dry fried fish? That’s my favorite! One portion!”

“Is there milk tea? What is it? Osmanthus oolong? One cup!”

“Mr. Lu, stir-fry some noodles! Ice Crystal Cake! One box! Pickled chili chicken feet! One portion!”

…

When the crew started eating Lu Ji’s food, whether they were hungry or not, they all began indulging in a meal. It was like watching a large-scale eating show.

“Man’er, are you crazy? It’s stir-fried noodles, so oily, how high in calories is that?”

Shu Man’er, who usually only ate vegetable salad and focused on her figure, didn’t listen to her assistant’s warning. She pushed them away and started devouring the stir-fried noodles with big mouthfuls.

Once done, she slurped down a cup of Osmanthus oolong milk tea. It was so comfortable, like floating in the clouds!

The young male lead Peng Kai was also eating with his mouth full of oil, and his assistant couldn’t stop him. They were about to cry!

The crew members, while eating stir-fried noodles, realized that what they’d eaten before, those boxed meals, were garbage. If they could eat such delicious food every day, they’d live in the crew forever.

The extras, who made 100 to 150 a day, hadn’t even received their pay yet, but they’d already spent all of it on this meal. The entire set was filled with the sound of people eating.

The producer returned from outside after ordering lunch boxes, only to see everyone sitting or standing with chopsticks in hand, already eating. He was utterly confused.

What’s going on here?

How did the restaurant work so efficiently? They just ordered lunch boxes, and they were delivered right away?

Also, it wasn’t even time for the crew to eat yet!

chapters.title: Chapter 222
chapters.content:
Chapter 222

The producer wasn’t cheap, he was just good at budgeting. At a small restaurant called “Yu Ji Stir-Fry,” he argued for over ten minutes to lower the price of a 15-yuan boxed meal to 13 yuan.

Don’t underestimate those two yuan, with over 300 people in the crew, including extras, that little bargain saved 600 yuan for the budget.

Money should be spent wisely, and as for the taste… well, as long as it’s filling, who cares? Not everyone likes exotic dishes, right?

If you’re not happy, you can pay for takeout yourself. The crew offers free boxed meals, so what’s the fuss about choosing?

Even Director Wen eats the same boxed meal.

Since joining the “Happy Sunshine” crew, whether you’re an actor or staff member, everyone grumbles about the food every day, but no one dares to voice their complaints.

Who would dare argue with the producer?

Before, Director Wen merely suggested a change, but the producer went on and on for half an hour, saying that the crew’s budget was tight.

Fine, boxed meals it is. If there’s something good nearby, just pay for it yourself.

Even Director Wen had to compromise, so the rest of the crew had no choice. But today, by chance, the props supplier brought in a food cart, and it turned out the food was delicious. It felt like a sudden stroke of happiness!

The food cart was arranged by Assistant Director Chen Dong, who received a lot of praise and was very pleased with himself. Right now, there was still a line in front of the Lu Qin food cart. Some of the people had already eaten and were full, but the food was so delicious that they couldn’t resist going back for more. They could lean against the wall and eat more—who cares!

Compared to the crew’s free boxed meals, the stir-fried noodles from the food cart were heaven!

When the producer arrived at the set, he was confused to see so many people already eating. Chen Dong was pulled aside by the producer and asked what was going on.

“Director Wen sent someone to buy props. It’s the break now, and everyone’s probably hungry, so they went to grab some street food,” Chen Dong explained, not directly mentioning that the producer had ordered the boxed meals.

“Street food?”

The producer frowned and looked at the Lu Qin food cart.

Was it really that good?

People were stretching their necks in line, it looked like a charity event!

The producer saw many people leaning against the railing, their bellies bulging from overeating.

“In an hour and a half, the boxed meals will be here. How are they going to eat them when they’re so full?”

Boxed meals?

No one would eat that garbage!

Chen Dong rolled his eyes secretly, agreeing, “Seems like they won’t be able to eat the boxed meals.”

“How about we order fewer meals?”

The producer had an idea. If they ordered fewer meals, they could save money again. He immediately took out his phone and called the restaurant.

The restaurant was barely making a profit on the 13-yuan meals, so they were trying to sell in large quantities. When the owner heard the order was halved, he wasn’t happy. The negotiation lasted half an hour. The producer was stubborn, refusing to back down.

After hanging up, the owner told the kitchen to reduce the meat in the chili stir-fried pork and add more soup to the scrambled eggs with tomatoes to make it thinner.

Was 13 yuan supposed to buy a feast?

…

The props arrived. Authentic stir-fried rice noodles from a street-side stall. Wu Chen grabbed the “prop” and immediately started shooting.

One take, and it was perfect!

Wu Chen straightened his back proudly!

See? It really was the stir-fried noodles!

My acting is indisputable!

At mealtime, the boxed meals arrived. The producer stood by, watching the distribution of the meals, worried that an extra might take two meals under the guise of getting one for someone else. He watched closely.

But to his surprise…

Not only did the staff not come for their meals, even the extras didn’t come!

The free boxed meals had always been the favorite of the extras!

What’s going on?

The producer turned around and saw everyone rushing over to the Lu Qin food cart!

What was going on?

The producer was speechless. Over a hundred boxed meals, and not one was claimed!

Not even a glance at the meals!

They had no interest in the boxed meals at all.

Unbelievable!

Was Lu Qin’s food cart really that delicious?

Driven by curiosity, the producer walked over.

He saw extras buying several portions, spending over a hundred yuan for a meal. They didn’t blink while scanning to pay!

Wow!

The producer couldn’t understand!

People with money spend it this way, but those without money do the same?

They made one or two hundred yuan a day as extras, and they were spending it all on food? But after the producer personally stood in line and bought a portion of stir-fried noodles, he realized—those boxed meals? Not even dogs would eat them!

After finishing the stir-fried noodles, the producer went to buy other things.

“What? Princess chicken wings for 28 yuan a portion?! Are they robbing us?”

He could have bought two boxed meals for that!

Used to being frugal, the producer hesitated for a few seconds but still paid.

After eating, his face showed the “this is delicious!” expression.

Looking at the untouched boxed meals, the producer took out his phone and called the restaurant owner.

“Can we return the boxed meals?”

The phone was silent for ten seconds. Then, the restaurant owner’s response could be summed up with a GIF—Liu Haizhu, the straw hat mage.

After a scolding, the owner hung up the phone and blocked the producer’s number. The producer was speechless.

At 6:30 PM, as the evening darkened, the shooting for the day came to an end. Since the Lu Qin food cart only sold within the crew, not all the food had been sold yet. Once the shooting was over, the crew didn’t clean up their things but instead rushed to Lu Qin’s food cart.

“Hurry up, hurry up! There’s not much left!”

“Dammit! The dry-fried fish is gone! Two portions of stir-fried noodles and an Ice Crystal Cake! What? The Ice Crystal Cake’s gone too?”

“Quick! Go line up! There’s not much stir-fried noodles left!”

“Damn it! Who stepped on my shoes?”

In half an hour, everything was sold out. Shu Man’er stamped her feet angrily. She was a big star, still in her filming clothes, makeup on, and couldn’t run as fast as those ravenous extras.

By the time she lined up, everything was sold out. She was very upset. But no one sympathized with her.

Sympathize with others?

Then you won’t get anything to eat yourself.

“Now, we only have ice cream left. If you want some, line up.”

In her free time, Lu Qin had already prepared the ice cream mixture.

“Wow! Mr. Lu has ice cream too!”

“Perfect dessert after a meal, gotta get one!”

“Red bean mugwort ice cream? I’ll try one!”

“I want one with sweet wine and fermented rice!”

“This ice cream is amazing! It beats Haagen-Dazs in an instant!”

Shu Man’er, forgetting her image, squeezed her way to the front of the line and bought a mint watermelon ice cream.

Her assistant, almost in tears, tried to stop her.

“Man’er, it’s ice cream, you’ve been on a diet for two years, are you really going to eat it?”

Since Shu Man’er had tasted Lu Qin’s food, the word “diet” no longer existed in her vocabulary.

Eat!

She had to eat!

So, Shu Man’er enjoyed the best ice cream in the world!

The ice cream was sold out. The crew’s taste buds had been satisfied like never before. Everyone was looking forward to Mr. Lu’s food cart was coming again tomorrow.

“Of course, Mr. Lu’s food cart still has two more days of shooting!”

Chen Dong patted his chest in assurance.

chapters.title: Chapter 223
chapters.content:
Chapter 223

There was a cheer in the crew. The producer was even happier because they could save money on the boxed meals!

Chen Dong walked over to discuss with Lu Qin.

“Mr. Lu, come again tomorrow. We still need two more days of shooting at the Flower and Fruit Mountain Park, and your food cart will continue as a prop.”

Not only could it be used as a prop, but it also solved the crew’s food needs.

Lu Qin nodded.

“Sure, but let’s settle today’s payment first.”

Since they hadn’t signed a contract with the crew, the payment needed to be settled on the spot.

“No problem!”

Chen Dong turned to the producer.

“Producer, we rented Mr. Lu’s food cart as a background prop for the shoot. This was a last-minute decision, so the payment should go directly to Mr. Lu, right?”

“Sure, how much is it?”

Chen Dong asked Lu Qin, “Mr. Lu, we agreed that the payment would be for a day’s business, right?”

Lu Qin: “Yes.”

Producer: “Mr. Lu, give us an estimate, just round it off.”

This kind of temporary renting of things from outsiders often happens, and the food cart probably made about 1,000 to 1,800 yuan in a day. The producer took out his phone and transferred the money while adding Lu Qin’s WeChat.

Lu Qin spoke, “Let’s make it 130,000.”

“130,000?!!!”

“Mr. Lu, let’s add each other on WeChat, and I’ll transfer the money to you.”

The producer took out his phone and scanned the QR code.

To be honest, Mr. Lu’s cooking skills were superb. Every dish he sold was delicious, surpassing many five-star restaurants, so it was a must to add him on WeChat.

The only downside? It was too expensive.

That was what the frugal producer was thinking.

After adding each other on WeChat, Lu Qin quoted a price.

“130,000 yuan.”

After deducting the costs, the revenue for the day was about 134,000 yuan.

Lu Qin gave a discount, so it was set at 130,000 yuan. Both Chen Dong and the producer were taken aback by this price.

After a few seconds, their eyes widened in shock.

“130,000!”

Chen Dong’s smile froze.

“Mr. Lu, you’re not joking, are you?”

Lu Qin shook his head.

“I’m not joking.”

The producer almost shouted.

“Why don’t you just rob a bank instead?”

He stared at Lu Qin.

“Mr. Lu, this price is ridiculous. We’ve rented food carts like yours before, and the most they ever made was about 1,800 yuan a day.”

Lu Qin frowned slightly.

“1,800? That doesn’t even cover my costs. You guys aren’t planning to cheat me, are you?”

He turned to look at Chen Dong.

“Vice Director Chen, we agreed on the daily earnings from the cart, right?”

Chen Dong nodded.

“Yeah, you’re saying your food cart earns 130,000 yuan a day? That’s more than a year’s worth!”

The producer said, “How about this, Mr. Lu? If you come again tomorrow, I’ll give you 2,000 yuan for the day. Is that good?”

Lu Qin sighed.

“You might not understand the market. Let me show you yesterday’s sales.”

Lu Qin opened his Alipay income statement. Scrolling down a long list of numbers, the total income at the bottom was…

68,732.50 yuan!

Similarly, the WeChat income was 70,534 yuan!

Total combined income: 139,266.50 yuan!

Shocking!

Unbelievable!

A street food cart’s daily revenue hit 130,000 yuan!

The producer and Chen Dong were stunned!

The entire budget for a day of shooting for “Happy Sunshine,” excluding the actors’ pay, didn’t even reach 130,000 yuan!

Was this food cart made of gold?

Chen Dong broke into a cold sweat. He was the one who brought this food cart into the crew.

What he thought was a win-win situation now felt like a disaster. The producer was so shocked that his phone almost fell to the ground.

If they paid this 130,000 yuan, it wasn’t just burning through the budget—it was an explosion!

The crew members also overheard the conversation, and immediately started gossiping!

“What? Mr. Lu’s food cart earns over 100,000 yuan a day?!”

“No way! That’s impossible!”

“100,000 yuan? Is he selling golden fried rice? No matter how good the business is, it should only make 2,000-3,000 yuan a day!”

“My friend runs a street stall selling braised food, and even though business is good, they only make around 1,500 yuan a day.”

“130,000? Are you kidding? Even top celebrities in China only make about 100,000 yuan a day for filming!”

“Maybe! I bought from Mr. Lu three times today, and each time I spent over 100 yuan! I contributed 300 yuan on my own!”

“Some extras spent over 150 yuan each! They’re really splurging! With so many people in the crew, it’s scary to think about!”

“Damn! Mr. Lu is amazing! Earning this much from a street food cart!”

“Mr. Lu’s food is so delicious, he definitely deserves to earn this money!”

“We’re done for! Our stingy producer is going to have a stroke! 130,000 yuan! It’s like cutting him open!”

…

Chen Dong’s voice was trembling.

“Mr. Lu, how could you earn so much in a day? Why didn’t you tell us earlier?!”

Lu Qin: “Vice Director Chen, you always talk about your big production, I thought you guys weren’t short on money!”

The producer laughed nervously.

“Mr. Lu, this price is just too outrageous! Our crew’s budget is tight. How about 5,000 yuan? What do you think?”

Lu Qin raised an eyebrow.

“We agreed that this was for the food cart’s daily earnings, right? The people outside are all my customers. They were originally going to buy from me. But with the crew interfering, I’m missing out on their business, and that’s a big loss for me.”

Lu Qin pointed to the crowd outside, many of whom were waiting for his food. At that moment, the customers also heard the conversation.

“This big crew rented Mr. Lu’s food cart and only offers this little compensation? Who are they looking down on?”

“If you don’t pay, we’ll make a video and post it on Douyin, and you’ll make headlines!”

“Damn it! We’ve been waiting to eat Mr. Lu’s food, and you delayed us for a whole day! All the food was bought by the crew! This is too much!”

…

The crowd kept voicing their discontent, and the actors and staff from the crew also started discussing the matter…

Since the price had already been agreed upon, it had to be paid. Given that the crew had millions in production costs, this 130,000 yuan was nothing—they could afford it. The producer was just too stingy.

Under the pressure of everyone, the producer finally gritted his teeth and paid the money, his heart bleeding!

Once Lu Qin received the payment, he finally smiled.

“Pleasure doing business with you. Are you renting again tomorrow?”

Chen Dong and the producer immediately shook their heads like rattlesnakes.

What a joke! 130,000 yuan?

Even if they customized a similar food cart, it wouldn’t cost that much!

There was no way they were going to rent such an expensive cart as a prop!

The only regret was that the crew was going back to eating boxed meals!

Not edible ones either!

Wu Chen walked over, hopeful.

“Mr. Lu, can you set up your cart here tomorrow?”

Lu Qin: “Not sure, it depends on the situation.”

Wu Chen: “Don’t worry about the situation! Whether you set up here or not, this place not only has all of us from the crew, but we also attract a lot of bystanders watching the filming. Your business is definitely going to boom!”

Lu Qin: “My business has never been hard to do.”

Wu Chen was stunned by how tasty the food was. Wu Chen didn’t know what to say.

Shu Man’er also walked over. She admired Lu Qin and pleaded, “Mr. Lu, please come tomorrow. Your ice cream is amazing. If you don’t come, I won’t be able to have it!”

Several other actors also approached.

“Mr. Lu, come set up your food cart tomorrow!”

“Yeah, yeah! We all love your food. We’re celebrities with a lot of fans, and we can help you promote your business!”

“Here’s the deal! Just come tomorrow, and I’ll give you my filming pay for the day!”

Peng Kai was craving Mr. Lu’s food. He lined up three times today and only ate two portions of stir-fried noodles and one serving of Princess Chicken Wings. He wasn’t satisfied at all. His daily pay was around 50,000 yuan!

But he didn’t care about the pay—he made his money from endorsements. So, spending the pay didn’t bother him. Lu Qin’s eyes lit up. He pulled Peng Kai aside and began to discuss.

chapters.title: Chapter 224
chapters.content:
Chapter 224

Peng Kai took out his 50,000 yuan pay for the day and asked Lu Qin to specifically come to Flower and Fruit Mountain Park to set up his food cart?

Although it didn’t matter where the cart was set up, the priority would definitely be for the crew members. The outside customers would likely have to make do with just the “wind” from the cart.

The customers at Lu Ji’s food cart were all well-off, unafraid to spend their wife’s allowance. So why should he only sell to the crew?

Just because they were famous actors?

As a principled businessman, Lu Qin knew that fairness was key. While Lu Qin was pondering, Shu Man’er also jumped in.

“Mr. Lu, I’ll give you my pay for tomorrow if you come and set up your food cart here!”

Shu Man’er said this with a sincere look, her voice carrying a hint of playfulness. She even blinked her big eyes.

Lu Qin was speechless.

Who is she fooling?

Lu Qin remained unfazed. The difference between this female star and a rich woman was clear.

Peng Kai tugged at Lu Qin’s arm and whispered, “Mr. Lu, Shu Man’er is the lead actress. Her pay is very high, probably around 100,000 yuan a day.”

100,000 yuan? Add Peng Kai’s 50,000 yuan, and that’s 150,000 yuan. Hmm, that could be worth considering.

Principles could be bent when the offer was good enough.

Lu Qin nodded and agreed. He needed to prepare more food for tomorrow and also plan for a portion to be set up outside for Xiaomi to run.

The producer watched Peng Kai and Shu Man’er, stunned, thinking to himself that these two had lost their minds. They were giving away their pay just to have Lu Qin set up a food cart?

But then he thought, they weren’t exactly short on money. The younger generation often lacked control, and after tasting such incredible food, they were completely hooked, unable to resist.

Well, it wasn’t his money being spent, so it was fine. Tomorrow, the crew’s boxed meal budget would definitely be saved.

Other actors in the crew were eyeing the food cart too. Honestly, even though the actors earned a lot, filming conditions could be harsh. Take the crew’s food, for example. It was really unappetizing.

Anyone who’s been in a film crew knows that even extras, when they see free boxed meals, can’t muster the energy to eat. It’s just that bad.

But they had no choice, they needed the energy for filming, so they had to eat the crew’s meals. Main actors, though, weren’t short on cash. They could choose not to eat the boxed meals and could order from a restaurant on their own. But sometimes, it wasn’t so easy.

For example, around Flower and Fruit Mountain, the only option was a fast-food place like Wallace. The producer had to order those awful boxed meals that even dogs wouldn’t eat, running five or six kilometers just to get them.

Wallace? Would you eat there?

For those suffering from constipation, it was a blessing, the little happiness for the common folk, spreading Western-style hamburgers and fries across China’s small towns. A burger costs ten yuan, and for just an extra two yuan, you can get a drink.

The key is: would you buy it?

Would you dare eat it?

Ever heard of “Jet Fighter” diarrhea?

Some Wallace stores even have signs saying, “No restrooms available.”

How considerate! They’re afraid you’ll sit down and eat inside.

If you’ve been constipated for two and a half years, then sure, Wallace is the place for you.

Netizens even created a “Wallace Jetting Chart.” It’s simply legendary.

Earlier today, Peng Kai had his assistant grab something to eat, and they chose Wallace, close by. A combo of roast chicken and iced cola. He felt fine right after eating, but then suddenly, his stomach felt off. He apologized to the director and rushed to the bathroom, suffering for an hour, only feeling better after taking some medicine. The whole crew knew about it.

After that explosive experience, Peng Kai’s handsome image in the crew was completely shattered. Afterward, Peng Kai scolded his assistant harshly.

So, no one dared to order food from random places anymore. It was safer to stick to the boxed meals. But with Lu Qin’s food cart accidentally entering the crew, everyone was suddenly happy.

After tasting Lu Qin’s food, the crew didn’t feel like they were filming, they felt like they were on a vacation eating gourmet food.

What’s the best part of filming?

When the director calls for a break!

As soon as the break began, everyone would rush to queue up for Lu Ji’s food!

They’d traveled all over filming, but they’d never had food this good!

Is Mr. Lu some kind of food god?

No pretending—he definitely is!

…

The next day, Lu Qin didn’t set up his cart early. Instead, he spent more time preparing and brought plenty of food. The food cart was packed with large and small boxes, and Xiaomi’s three-wheeled cart was also full of large plastic containers.

Inside were ice crystal cakes, soft fresh rice noodles soaking in liquid, pickled vegetables, and pickled chicken feet.

At 1 PM, Lu Qin and Xiaomi arrived at Flower and Fruit Mountain Park. The entire crew was eagerly waiting. Outside the park, the crowd watching was even larger than yesterday.

It seemed the word had gotten out that the crew of “Happy Sunshine” was filming here, so more people had come to watch. Assistant Director Chen Dong saw Lu Qin’s food cart slowly pulling up and immediately ran over excitedly, calling several staff members to help set up. Today, the crew didn’t rent Lu Qin’s cart. It was just too expensive to rent.

But thanks to Peng Kai and Shu Man’er’s generous offer, Lu Qin agreed to set up his food cart in the crew. This was basically turning the crew into a “food truck” for the day!

At this point, more than ten scenes had been filmed. Lu Qin’s food cart set up, drawing everyone’s attention. Lead actor Wu Chen had another NG. His eyes were fixed only on Lu Qin’s food. Oh, no, it was really just his food.

Stir-fried rice noodles, iced green bean soup, ice crystal cakes, Yu Xiang shredded pork…

His mouth watered just thinking about it!

“Click!”

Director Wen shouted through the walkie-talkie and scolded Wu Chen. The last shot was just about to be done, but Wu Chen had lost his focus!

Director Wen glanced at Lu Qin’s food cart, thinking, This really is the worst timing.

Suddenly, the crew members with nothing else to do immediately rushed over to queue up. Shu Man’er, who was touching up her makeup, Zhai Lin, who was rehearsing lines, and Peng Kai, who was memorizing his lines… all rushed over.

In just a few seconds, a long line had formed in front of the cart. It was faster than a military assembly!

chapters.title: Chapter 225
chapters.content:
Chapter 225

At Lu Qin’s food cart, facial recognition wasn’t going to help. No matter how famous they were, they had to wait in line.

Shu Man’er tried to get ahead by exchanging spots with an extra, but nothing worked. Not even asking for a signature or WeChat!

It was a joke!

At that moment, Shu Man’er was second to last in line, and the extra was third in line. Just as Shu Man’er was about to switch spots, the extra firmly said, “No way!”

Shu Man’er struggled all the way to the end of the line and only managed to get half a serving of stir-fried noodles.

…

Wu Chen saw the line growing long, and in his hurry and distraction, he NG’d again. An entertainment journalist snapped a photo and quietly wrote in their notebook.

“Click!”

Director Wen couldn’t take it anymore and shouted “Cut” again. Not only had Wu Chen messed up his lines, but the cameraman’s shot was also off. Everyone was completely distracted by the aroma wafting from Lu Qin’s food cart, pulling their souls away!

Director Wen swallowed hard and reluctantly announced a 30-minute break. The word “break” acted like a stimulant, and Wu Chen and the rest of the crew immediately ran over.

Today’s menu featured more dishes–layered oil cake, spicy boiled fish fillets, twice-cooked pork, and mapo tofu!

Just looking at the names was enough to make your mouth water. The producer shook his head and sighed. A once serious film crew had turned into an open-air cafeteria!

“Li Tian! You, kid, trying to cut in line!”

The producer put his hands on his hips and grabbed a stagehand, pulling them out of line.

“Someone, get Li Tian into the pig cage!”

“…”

The producer had been in line early. He was in the first group.

“Layered oil cake! This is so delicious! Soft, sweet, and sticky! Even better than what I had in Fuchun!”

“Mapo tofu is invincible! Ah, so good, it’s almost making my crown fly off!”

“I still love stir-fried noodles! Never get tired of them!”

“Mr. Lu’s iced green bean soup is amazing! This is my third cup! I’m not worried about diarrhea!”

…

If Lu Qin’s food cart had a review feature, it would be flooded with five-star reviews!

The only negative reviews would be about things like “too long to wait in line,” “too many people in line,” and “it’s addictive!”

After 30 minutes, filming resumed. Actors and staff members took their positions. Director Wen and the producer called the people waiting in line back.

“You! Yes, you! Why are you still in line?”

“And you, pedestrian A, get to your position!”

“Extra B, what are you doing? Change into your costume!”

…

Several extras were pulled away, feeling frustrated. They only made about 100 yuan a day, and now they couldn’t even eat Lu Qin’s food. Honestly, it wasn’t worth it!

But there was no choice, the filming had to go on to earn a living. For now, Lu Qin’s food cart was temporarily closed. A portion of the outside customers who were waiting could hardly wait.

Lu Qin told Xiaomi to take some of the food outside and sell it to non-crew customers.

“Hey, hey, hey!”

“You can’t! There’s not enough for the crew!”

Vice Director Chen Dong saw what was happening and quickly ran over to stop it.

“Mr. Lu, you can’t sell the food outside! The crew’s counting on your food!”

“Everyone who’s had your stir-fried noodles is more energized for filming!”

“Some people didn’t get enough to eat yesterday!”

“Can you just sell inside the crew? Trust me, it’ll all sell out!”

Chen Dong passionately tried to persuade Lu Qin.

“I’ve prepared a lot, Vice Director Chen. There’s enough to sell to the crew.”

“If you don’t let the outside customers buy, I think today your crew will be torn apart,” Lu Qin pointed to the outside crowd.

In the crowd, many eyes were glaring at Chen Dong, seemingly ready to eat him alive. Chen Dong swallowed and pulled his neck back.

“Mr. Lu, are you sure there’s enough for everyone?”

“There’s enough.”

Lu Qin had decided on a purchasing limit for the day, not allowing people to gorge themselves. It was fine as long as they ate enough—eating until you couldn’t move wasn’t healthy anyway.

After eating Lu Qin’s mapo tofu, Wu Chen’s taste buds were satisfied, and his acting was on point. Several scenes were completed in one take.

At last, he was living up to his title as a promising young actor.

“Alright, cut!”

Director Wen took off his headset and instructed the assistant director to prepare for the next scene. Suddenly, there was an unexpected round of applause in the set.

Everyone turned to see what was going on. A tall man had appeared from nowhere. He was wearing a mask and sunglasses, hiding his face.

Wu Chen looked puzzled, then suddenly his eyes widened in shock as he shouted, “Senior Brother!!”

“What are you doing here?”

Wu Chen was extremely surprised and ran over immediately. The young man took off his sunglasses and mask, revealing a familiar handsome face.

It was He Zhuo!

“He Zhuo!!”

“He Zhuo!!”

“It’s He Zhuo!”

“Didn’t expect there to be such a top-tier star in the ‘Happy Sunshine’ crew!”

“Oh my god! What luck! I actually saw He Zhuo in person!”

He Zhuo was a top-tier actor in the film and television industry, having starred in countless quality films and series, and endorsed numerous big brands. He had both looks and acting skills, winning multiple awards.

He was from the same entertainment agency as Wu Chen. Wu Chen’s role in Happy Sunshine was recommended by He Zhuo. The original plan was to cast He Zhuo as the lead actor, but due to scheduling conflicts and a high pay rate, He Zhuo turned it down.

Not wanting the role to go to someone outside, He Zhuo recommended Wu Chen. Director Wen and the producer greeted He Zhuo politely when they saw him.

He Zhuo made a surprise visit, just in time to see that Wu Chen had nailed several scenes in one take. He gave a satisfied nod.

“Director Wen, the person I recommended isn’t bad, right? This kid’s acting is trustworthy.”

Director Wen nodded and smiled.

“His acting is indeed good, but he still needs some improvement in his willpower.”

He Zhuo heard this and immediately understood. Although Director Wen was generally satisfied with Wu Chen, there were still some issues.

Suppressing his curiosity, He Zhuo turned to Director Wen and said, “If Wu Chen’s not doing well in any aspect, feel free to scold him! Don’t hold back!”

After a few exchanges, He Zhuo pulled Wu Chen aside.

“You, kid, what does Director Wen mean by your willpower needing improvement?” He Zhuo frowned and asked seriously.

He Zhuo really liked Wu Chen, but he was also strict with him. Getting scolded by him was better than being scolded by a director on set.

“Senior Brother, it’s because the food cart came to the set, and I was distracted by the smell during filming,” Wu Chen scratched his head. “It’s not really my fault—Zhai Lin NG’d several times too.”

chapters.title: Chapter 226
chapters.content:
Chapter 226

A snack stand arrived at the film set. He Zhuo looked up and saw the Lu Ji Snack Food Truck not far away.

He frowned, “You really can’t focus. Stop finding excuses. When filming, you must be single-minded. Do you understand?”

Such street snacks are everywhere. He Zhuo had eaten them before, back when he hadn’t debuted, but he hasn’t had them since. One reason is that they are unhealthy, and the second is that fried food is a nightmare for maintaining a good figure. He has always been very disciplined.

“I understand, Senior Brother.” Wu Chen always trusted He Zhuo’s words without doubt.

“Senior Brother, I’m really happy that you came to visit my set!”

Wu Chen was very excited.

“I didn’t have lunch and came here directly. You figure it out,” He Zhuo said with a smile.

“Senior Brother, let me take you to a great place!”

Wu Chen was about to grab He Zhuo’s arm.

“Hey, you little brat, go shoot the scene first. Afterward, we can go. Work always comes first.”

“Senior Brother, we’re not going anywhere else. The good food is right here on the set!”

He Zhuo was stunned.

“There’s good food on set? You’re not planning to treat me to a boxed meal, are you?”

Wu Chen gave a mysterious smile.

“Of course not, here, it’s that snack stand!”

“Street food? I don’t eat that,” He Zhuo frowned, instinctively resisting.

He was now very disciplined and health-conscious, and he would never touch unhealthy food. Wu Chen, knowing He Zhuo’s habits, immediately explained when he saw him frown.

“Senior Brother, don’t worry. It’s absolutely delicious and healthy, and it’s clean and hygienic. Trust me!”

Wu Chen pulled He Zhuo and walked toward Lu Qin’s food truck. He Zhuo reluctantly followed, but he had already decided in his heart that he would never touch such junk food.

Moreover, He Zhuo remembered that Wu Chen didn’t like that kind of street food either. Why had he suddenly developed a liking for it?

For an actor, dietary discipline is very important. He still needed to remind Wu Chen later about the importance of managing one’s diet. If he wanted to go far in this profession, he must control his eating habits and be as self-disciplined as He Zhuo.

Earlier, the film crew had been busy shooting the scene. At this point, Lu Qin’s stand was temporarily taking a break and had no business. Now, Wu Chen dragged He Zhuo over.

“Boss Lu, two portions of fried noodles, add all the toppings!”

“One portion of mapo tofu, spicy fish fillets, an ice crystal cake, one portion of layered oil cake, and two cups of green bean soup.”

“Wu Chen, that’s enough!”

He Zhuo immediately stopped him. He wasn’t even sure if he would eat street food, and Wu Chen ordered so much at once. How could they possibly finish it?

“Senior Brother, this might not be enough. Buy a little more. Once other people come over, it’ll be hard to get anything!”

While He Zhuo was distracted, Wu Chen ordered three or four more dishes. In short, he ordered nearly everything available. Lu Qinn, while waiting for the frying pan to heat up, took out other food from the preservation box.

“Boss Lu, I’ll pack it myself. You fry the noodles and mapo tofu.”

Wu Chen expertly packed the food and then paid by scanning the code.

“Senior Brother, try this ice crystal cake, it’s amazing!”

“I don’t eat sweets.”

He Zhuo waved his hand.

He Zhuo had always controlled his sugar and oil intake to maintain his figure. Unless it was exceptionally delicious, he would taste it, but otherwise, he would never break his discipline.

“Wu Chen, you ordered too much. I really don’t like eating this stuff.”

He Zhuo looked at Lu Qin’s food truck and noticed that the hygiene was acceptable. The stall owner was a young, handsome guy, not a greasy, middle-aged man, so it wasn’t unpleasant to look at.

He Zhuo glanced at Lu Qin a few more times and noticed that the owner’s looks were almost on par with his own, which was rare.

He Zhuo thought to himself, in terms of temperament, he was still slightly superior.

But when he saw Lu Qin concentrating on frying the noodles, He Zhuo momentarily lost his thoughts. Alright, this stall owner’s charm when focusing on work was no less than his own when seriously filming.

“Senior Brother, the cake made by Boss Lu uses natural cane sugar. It’s very healthy. You can trust it.”

Wu Chen had chatted with Lu Qinn yesterday and learned that the food Lu Qinn made was much healthier than what they would find in a star-rated restaurant.

In those fancy restaurants, in order to enhance the flavor of the food, they use seasonings to enhance the freshness and aroma. So, the seasonings likely contain various additives.

Additives won’t kill you, but if consumed over the long term, it can cause many sub-health issues, like high blood pressure, high cholesterol, and gout. You can’t see the seasonings in the restaurant kitchen, so you only know it tastes good but don’t know if it’s healthy.

Wu Chen explained all this to He Zhuo to ease his concerns. He Zhuo was slightly surprised after hearing this. It was the first time he heard that sugar could be good.

“Of course, sugar is a necessary nutrient for our bodies. How could it be bad?”

“It’s just that industrial sugar produced by unscrupulous businesses using machines is unhealthy. If you eat too much, it will harm your body!”

Wu Chen spoke with confidence.

“Heh, didn’t know you were a nutrition expert,” He Zhuo raised an eyebrow.

“Hehe! It’s all thanks to Boss Lu.”

Wu Chen and He Zhuo simultaneously looked at Lu Qin. Lu Qin smiled lightly and nodded, continuing to fry the noodles.

“Boss Lu, do you know my Senior Brother?”

“It should look familiar.”

Lu Qin glanced at He Zhuo, probably not recognizing him since he didn’t watch dramas. But out of politeness, he pretended to recognize him, not wanting to make the big star feel embarrassed.

He Zhuo could tell from Lu Qin’s tone that the man didn’t recognize him. He silently smiled wryly. It seemed his popularity wasn’t as high as he had imagined.

“Senior Brother, are you really not going to try it?”

Wu Chen couldn’t help it and popped a piece of ice crystal cake into his mouth. The soft, glutinous, sweet, and wonderful taste spread across his tongue. This ice crystal cake was unbelievably delicious. It was far tastier than those Western desserts, a hundred times better.

He Zhuo looked at the plastic box in Wu Chen’s hand. Inside, the ice crystal cake was delicate and transparent, looking like a work of art. He hadn’t had lunch and was starving.

Coupled with Wu Chen’s earlier explanation that the dessert made by Boss Lu was very healthy, he decided it was probably safe to try.

“I’ll give it a try.”

He Zhuo swallowed and picked up a piece of the ice crystal cake.

“Senior Brother, the outer layer of this ice crystal cake is made from an ice skin, and inside is a sweet sticky rice dessert. It’s aromatic and pure. I promise you’ll want more once you start eating!”

He Zhuo was tempted, and immediately took a bite. After chewing, his taste buds exploded with delight. This ice crystal cake was not only sweet and soft but also had a cool taste. When eaten, it was refreshing and smooth. This feeling was incredible!

chapters.title: Chapter 227
chapters.content:
Chapter 227

He Zhuo had never experienced such a taste before. He had tried those delicate Western desserts, which looked very delicious and were indeed tasty, but no matter how good they were, after a few bites, they would start to feel too rich and overwhelming.

However, if something wasn’t sweet, it wouldn’t taste good. So, some Western pastry chefs would add fruits like melons to help balance out the heaviness. Most Western desserts used cream, which He Zhuo couldn’t accept because it became too greasy.

But this ice crystal cake he was eating was not greasy at all. Its refreshing, soft, and glutinous texture constantly stimulated his salivary glands, and it was impossible to stop eating. Moreover, He Zhuo was already starving and ate several pieces.

Wu Chen panicked.

“Senior Brother, leave two pieces for me!”

The ice crystal cake was quickly devoured by the two of them. They then drank some iced green bean soup, each with a cup. He Zhuo slurped a sip, and his eyes lit up. It was delicious!

At this moment, the aroma of stir-fried rice noodles filled the air. Both Wu Chen and He Zhuo simultaneously sniffed and couldn’t help but salivate.

“Your fried noodles are ready.”

“Thank you.”

Lu Qin then started cooking mapo tofu.

Wu Chen opened the spicy boiled fish, and He Zhuo couldn’t help but pick up a piece. The tender, fresh, and spicy fish was both delicious and appetizing!

The two continued eating the fried rice noodles while enjoying the boiled fish. Wu Chen saw that He Zhuo was eating with great enjoyment and proudly said, “Senior Brother, it’s delicious, right? You’ve never had this before, have you?”

He Zhuo ate with a clear gaze and a satisfied expression.

He nodded.

“It’s really good! Visiting your set today was the right choice!”

“Hehe!” Wu Chen beamed proudly, as if Lu Qin stayed at the set to run the stall just for him.

Halfway through their meal, the crew finished a round of shooting, and it was time for a break. People began to gather.

“Senior Brother, quickly, let’s buy more. When the others come, it’ll be hard to get anything!”

“Hurry up! They’re coming!”

…

He Zhuo had broken his discipline today and ate until his stomach was about to burst!

He originally came to visit the set briefly and was planning to fly to the Shanghai market, but now he changed his mind and decided to stay in Jiangcheng for a couple of days.

He asked Wu Chen, “Will Boss Lu come again tomorrow?”

“Of course! If he doesn’t come, we’ll drag him to the set!” Wu Chen said confidently.

It seemed like now the whole crew couldn’t do without his little snack stand. Shu Man’er posted a set of food photos on her Moments, accompanied by a very cheerful mood.

Soon, a large number of friends in the industry liked and commented on it.

“Man’er, aren’t you filming in Jiangcheng? Which restaurant is this? It looks so delicious!”

“Sister Man’er, stop tempting me! I’m on a diet!”

“Shu Man’er, you’re not dieting anymore? Why this?”

…

“It’s the divine snack stand in the crew. The food is so delicious! If you have time, come visit me, I’ll treat you to Lu Qin Snacks! He Zhuo has already visited, and once he came, he didn’t want to leave!” Shu Man’er replied.

“Really? I’m in Haishi! I’m coming over now!”

“I’ve just finished a busy few days. Time to indulge a little. Man’er, I’m on my way!”

Others like Peng Kai, Zhai Lin, etc., also posted on their Moments.So, on the third day, several stars came to visit the set. Some of them were quite well-known.

The entire crew was dumbfounded. Why were so many people visiting the set all of a sudden?

As soon as these stars arrived on set, the first thing they asked wasn’t to visit the set but.

“Where’s Lu Qin Snack?”

Everyone suddenly realized.

Oh, they all came for Lu Qin’s snack stand!

It’s really something!

Shu Man’er led several stars to line up.

“So many people, how long will we have to wait?”

“Can I just skip the line if I show my face?”

“I’m Li Haigang, the lead actor of ‘Top Secret Mission.’ You must know me, right? Can I go first? I’ve come from so far!”

“Nope, I won’t let anyone cut in line, not even my mom!”

Li Haigang was stunned.

“What? Can’t I skip the line?”

Shu Man’er covered her mouth and laughed.

“Don’t say that. Even He Zhuo can’t skip the line. Everyone waits their turn!”

Peng Kai nodded.

“Lu Qin’s snack stand treats everyone equally!”

So, everyone obediently lined up.

After they got their food, the delicious taste immediately opened up a new world of flavor for them. All of them had the same thought.

This trip is worth it!

Shu Man’er, Peng Kai, and the others wore proud expressions.

“Visiting the set was the right choice, wasn’t it? So delicious, right? Never had this before, right?”

Several stars nodded while finishing their boxes of food.

“Ah~! Satisfied! The taste is amazing!”

“It’s been so long since I’ve eaten this much! But my diet plan is ruined!”

“I canceled tomorrow’s schedule. I’m continuing to eat!”

After they ate their fill and drank enough, they finally took a good look at Lu Qin and Xiao Mi, who were packing up.

“Wow~! The young stall owner is so handsome! Great looks, great cooking skills. He’s my dream man!” Actress Zhang Manlin said, her eyes filled with hearts.

Another actress nodded in agreement. Setting aside the looks, just the ability to make such delicious food was already a killer!

“What a perfect man!”

Shu Man’er laughed and said, “You girls can forget it. Boss Lu is already married!”

“Married? So what? They can get divorced!”

“I can be the other woman.”

Shu Man’er was speechless.

“Where are your morals?”

As they chatted, they walked away. Wu Chen, Peng Kai, and other stars who were usually high up, actively helped pack up.

“Boss Lu, want a signature and a photo?”

Li Haigang spoke up. He had starred in two successful dramas this year and gained a lot of popularity. His signature and photos were quite valuable. He spoke up to build a good relationship with Lu Qin.

“Then… can you sign my name? Thanks.”

Although Lu Qin wasn’t very familiar with him, since Li Haigang offered, he decided to give him some face. After all, he was a customer. Lu Qin handed him a notebook.

Li Haigang grabbed a pen and quickly scribbled his artistic signature. Stars are like that, always signing in a messy, unrecognizable way.

Lu Qin took the book and stared at it for a while before asking, “Sorry, what’s your name?”

Li Haigang froze and asked in surprise, “Boss Lu, don’t you know me?”

“I don’t really watch TV.”

Pffft!

Shu Man’er couldn’t help but laugh!

chapters.title: Chapter 228
chapters.content:
Chapter 228

Li Haigang was speechless.

So, it turns out that Boss Lu doesn’t even watch popular TV dramas?

He’s not acting like a young person at all!

Everyone else treated their signatures as treasures. But to Boss Lu, it was like a useless piece of paper. Not only did he not follow celebrities, but he also didn’t recognize them.

Li Haigang felt a bit awkward. He had wanted to make a good impression on Boss Lu, but now he couldn’t. Other actors, who would normally try to get autographs or pictures, would consider it an honor to be friends with someone like Boss Lu, a master chef.

But Boss Lu wasn’t into celebrities, and for him, these usually privileged stars were no different from regular customers. It turned out Boss Lu was such a rare species, and several actresses were even more fond of him now.

He was handsome, with delicate features like a male god from Hong Kong films in the 70s and 80s, and he was an amazing cook, perfect in every way.

Shu Man’er pulled a few of the actresses aside.

“Wipe your drool, ladies. I heard Boss Lu already has a kid. If not, I’d have gone after him already! What business do you have now?”

Shu Man’er blushed after saying that.

The actresses didn’t listen. So what if he had a child? He could still get a divorce.

In this day and age, it’s hard to find such a high-quality male god, and to have him with a child—what a jackpot!

Shu Man’er was speechless.

Just as the actresses were about to launch their attack on the “lamb” that was Boss Lu, Shen Muyan appeared, holding Lu Xiyao’s hand. Shen Muyan had a stunning face, pure yet regal in her aura, with a tall and perfect figure, and holding an incredibly cute “super pet.”

Instantly, the attention of the entire crew was drawn to them. Especially the male colleagues, who all stared at Shen Muyan and Lu Xiyao. This was truly the ideal wife and daughter of the world!

No one knew who would be so lucky to have such a perfect wife and daughter. It wasn’t until the wealthy woman and her little one walked directly to Lu Qin that everyone finally realized.

Oh, they’re from Boss Lu’s family.

All the men were deeply envious!

Meanwhile, Shu Man’er and the actresses felt a pang of bitterness in their hearts. It was true that couples destined to be together do end up together, and they were witnessing it firsthand!

The moment Shu Man’er saw Shen Muyan, she felt somewhat embarrassed about herself. Shu Man’er’s looks were well-known in the entertainment industry, and she was considered a “new generation flower,” which meant her looks were among the top tier of actresses.

But in front of Shen Muyan, Shu Man’er felt the other woman was so beautiful—her features were exquisite, naturally beautiful, not plastic at all.

Her aura was even more impressive—she had that cold, elegant older sister vibe, and could easily be a cover model for a fashion magazine. This kind of aura could make men fall at her feet easily. Shu Man’er felt that compared to Shen Muyan, she was completely outclassed. It was truly heartbreaking.

The actresses’ enthusiasm had cooled completely. They had once said they could still get divorced if the man was married, but now it seemed like they had no right to compare to the first wife. They might as well not embarrass themselves.

“Daddy~!”

Lu Xiyao’s soft, adorable voice rang out. She waddled with her little legs and flew into Lu Qin’s arms. Lu Qin picked up the little one and pressed her soft, fair cheek against his own.

“Have you had dinner yet?”

“Not yet. Mommy and I both want to eat Daddy’s cooking~!”

Shen Muyan walked over, her cold eyes looking at Boss Lu, a trace of tenderness flickering in them. In front of the food truck, the heavenly family of three was the envy of everyone.

“Alright, let’s go home and cook dinner.” Lu Qin said with a smile.

“Daddy~! Is there ice cream? Mommy wants some!”

The little one looked up at Lu Qin eagerly, swallowing her saliva, clearly craving it. Lu Qin’s ice cream was incredibly delicious. Now, the little one refused to eat the supermarket ice creams.

Earlier at Shen Hong Da’s villa, the little one didn’t even ask for ice cream, which had made Shen Hong Da and his wife wonder.

Shen Muyan pinched the little one’s cheek and said faintly, “Let’s make it clear, do you want it, or does Mommy want it?”

Lu Xiyao, feeling the imposing aura of the wealthy woman, quickly changed her words: “Yao Yao wants it~!”

Seeing how quickly the little one gave in, Shen Muyan decided to let it go. Lu Qin had purposely left a little extra for the little one to satisfy her cravings.

Now that he had mastered the ultimate ice cream-making skills, he didn’t have to worry about additives harmful to the body. His homemade ice cream was cleaner, more hygienic, and healthier.

“Wait a minute.”

He walked into the food truck, pressed the ice cream machine’s output button, and placed a crispy cone underneath. He was able to make two ice creams at once.

One was red bean and mugwort flavor, the other was fragrant rice wine flavor.

“Here, big beauty, little beauty, one for each!”

Lu Qin smiled faintly, handing over the ice creams.

“Oh yay~! Thanks, Daddy~! Daddy is the best~!”

Lu Xiyao eagerly grabbed the red bean and mugwort-flavored ice cream with her little hand, then leaned her little head towards the other ice cream, trying to take a bite. Typical of wanting what others have.

Shen Muyan reacted quickly, her slender, white fingers pinched the little one’s mouth shut.

“So greedy at such a young age, eat your own!”

“Oh, okay.”

The little one, caught in the act, looked adorably innocent as she began eating her own ice cream.7

“Shen, did you drive here?” Lu Qin looked at Shen Muyan and asked.

“No.”

Shen Muyan shook her head. She had specifically asked the driver not to wait and to go off work directly. The driver, of course, was happy; the money was easy to earn.

Lu Qin understood. The rich woman not only wanted to eat for free but also wanted to stay for free. Lu Qin had Xiao Mi drive the snack truck back, while he drove with Shen Muyan and the little one.

Today’s street vending income was a little over 140,000. Although they didn’t set up the early stall, they sold a lot in the afternoon. It could be said that they had fed an entire film crew.

The crew of “Happy Sunshine” had actually filmed most of the scenes at the Flower and Fruit Mountain Park location, and they were ready to move to the next spot. But, in order to enjoy another day of Lu Qin’s cooking, the screenwriter stayed up all night rewriting the script to add another day’s worth of scenes. Some of the scenes featured a guest appearance by He Zhuo.

On the fourth day, the main actors, Wu Chen and veteran actor Zhai Lin, were willing to offer a day’s salary to invite Lu Qin to set up his stall at the filming location.

Lu Qin reluctantly agreed and explained that this would be the last time. If he didn’t go set up the stall elsewhere, the customers would revolt. The car played soft, relaxing music, and Lu Qin used the autopilot feature to let the car drive itself.

In the back seat, the two beauties ate ice cream at the same time. The beautiful woman sat in the queen’s seat, casually resting her long legs on the footrest, in a relaxed posture after work. The younger beauty, on the other hand, lay across the back seat with her head resting on the big beauty’s lap, licking her red bean and mugwort ice cream all the way.

chapters.title: Chapter 229
chapters.content:
Chapter 229

When they returned to the rented house, Lu Qin parked the car in the public roadside parking space in front of the yard. The parking space was public, so anyone could park there, but the neighbors in the community were very friendly and had agreed not to occupy that spot so that Lu Qin could park there whenever he returned at night.

The three of them entered the yard, and the lights in the house were on. It seemed that Xiao Mi had been working inside on her own. When they entered the house, they found someone sitting on the sofa in the living room. It was Shen Muyan’s strategist—Fang Ya.

Xiao Mi could tell who was who and knew about the relationship between the boss lady and Fang Ya, so she invited Fang Ya in.

Fang Ya was nibbling on an apple. When she saw the three of them come back, she immediately rubbed her stomach and said pitifully, “ Ugh , you’re finally back, I’m about to starve to death!”

“Fang Ya Auntie, you’re here?”

The little one was full of joy and ran over.

“Hey! Yao Yao baby, come let Fang Ya Auntie have a bite!”

“Then I’ll go cook, you guys sit for a while and watch TV.”

Lu Qin tied an apron, hanging on the side of the fridge, and walked into the kitchen.

“ Tsk tsk tsk , what a good and considerate man!”

Fang Ya looked at Lu Qin’s back and praised him. Shen Muyan glanced at Fang Ya.

“You came to freeload without giving a heads-up?”

Fang Ya leaned over, looking sly.

“What? You’re afraid that if I suddenly came, I’d ruin your sweet little moments?”

Fang Ya made a mischievous expression.

“ Pooh! You’re full of yellow thoughts!” Shen Muyan spat.

“Oh? Did you forget all the advice I gave you? And now you’re saying you don’t care?”

“I don’t care~!” Shen Muyan pouted.

It had been so many days, and Shen Muyan hadn’t even made it to first base. Fang Ya truly was a strategist!

Shen Muyan increasingly realized that Fang Ya’s ideas were all terrible. If she really had any ability, she wouldn’t be single herself. Shen Muyan felt it was better to rely on herself.

“I do care, fine, I’ll give you another plan later!”

Fang Ya had been quite addicted to being a strategist these days, realizing it was even more fun than dating herself. One dish after another was ready.

Xiao Mi brought them out and placed them on the dining table. Four dishes and one soup, with plenty of food. Kung Pao chicken, red oil mouth-watering chicken, twice-cooked pork, Mapo tofu, and a winter melon rib soup.

“Wow! There’s mouth-watering chicken! I haven’t eaten this in so long! It looks so tempting!”

“Yao Yao’s favorite twice-cooked pork!”

Shen Muyan, Fang Ya, and Lu Xiyao all stared at the dishes, their eyes nearly popping out. It was mouth-watering. When the rice was brought to the table, they started eating, all of them devouring the food.

The usually high-class rich woman temporarily gave up her image and ate like a pig. After all, it was just in front of Lu Qin, and it didn’t matter. The meal was eaten so quickly, with the sounds of plates clinking.

All the dishes and rice were finished, and the soup was almost gone. The three of them were all full.

“Ai, I can’t move, I can’t stand up!”

Fang Ya ate the most. She hadn’t had Lu Qin’s cooking in a few days, so she was the hungriest. She ate four and a half bowls of rice and a lot of dishes. Now she was sitting in the chair, her stomach bulging like she was four or five months pregnant.

Shen Muyan couldn’t help but roll her eyes.

“Were you a starving ghost in your past life?”

Lu Qin brought out the digestion tablets he won in the system lottery.

“Take one of these digestion pills, it’ll help.”

“Thanks, Lu Boss.”

Fang Ya was very grateful. After taking a digestion pill, about ten minutes later, the effect was surprisingly good. The food in her stomach digested quickly, and she didn’t feel uncomfortable at all.

“I’m good now, Lu Boss, your digestion pills really work!”

Fang Ya stood up, feeling completely relaxed. She had finished eating, so she decided to leave.

“Not going to sit a while longer?”

Shen Muyan saw Fang Ya off at the door.

“Stop being so polite, I know my limits. I only came to freeload on the food, I won’t disturb your warm family time.”

Before leaving, Fang Ya secretly shoved something into Shen Muyan’s hand.

“What’s this?”

Shen Muyan looked down and found it was a small umbrella. She was immediately startled, quickly shoving it into her pocket. She glanced around the room, making sure no one saw, then let out a sigh of relief.

That Fang Ya!

Shen Muyan’s face burned.

Okamoto 001 ultra-thin model, she was really helpless!

Shen Muyan pressed the pocket, knowing she couldn’t let the little one or Lu Qin find it. But since it was in their house, she couldn’t hide it in any drawer either. But putting it in her pocket…

Shen Muyan was wearing tight jeans today, and the box of small umbrellas looked particularly awkward in her pocket.

Throw it away!

Shen Muyan had a sudden thought and hurried outside.

“I’ll go send Fang Ya off.”

Fang Ya had already walked a minute ago, but Lu Qin was in the kitchen cutting fruit and didn’t pay much attention. Shen Muyan walked outside, intending to throw the item away, but she found a group of neighbors taking a post-dinner stroll.

When they saw Shen Muyan, they immediately smiled and greeted her.

“Hey? Yao Yao’s mom, are you out for a walk?”

“Are you alone? Where’s Lu Boss and Yao Yao?”

Shen Muyan froze for a moment. With so many people around, she definitely couldn’t throw away the small umbrella. She coldly responded a few words and had no choice but to turn around and go back.

“Yao Yao’s mom is so cold!”

“She’s the president of a big hotel, her status is there, she must always be high up, otherwise, how could she intimidate her employees?”

“That’s true, she’s really beautiful, but her personality is too cold!”

“Although, just now when she saw us, she seemed to panic a little. Not sure why?”

…

Back in the house, Shen Muyan saw that Lu Qin was still in the kitchen, and the little one was watching cartoons. She started looking for a place to hide the small umbrella. It was definitely not safe in the room, so after looking around, Shen Muyan found a corner in the shoe cabinet. This should be safe.

After hiding it, Shen Muyan casually walked over to the living room to watch TV with the little one. Xiao Mi finished her work and left on her tricycle.

Lu Qin locked the yard gate and came inside, then opened the shoe cabinet. It had been a while since he cleaned his shoes, so he decided to take them out and clean them. He would air them out tonight to get rid of any odor.

After all, Lu Qin had a fatigue-eliminating aura, so doing these things didn’t tire him at all. Therefore, he had become more diligent, thinking of something and doing it immediately with strong execution and never procrastinating.

“Yao Yao, there are a pair of sneakers under your bed, go get them. Daddy’s going to clean all the shoes.”

“Okay~!”

The little one jumped off the sofa and ran to the room. Shen Muyan didn’t pay much attention at first and continued watching the cartoons. A few seconds later, her heart started to race!

Clean shoes!

chapters.title: Chapter 230
chapters.content:
Chapter 230

Lu Qin had once won a powerful cleaning and care kit in a high-level system lottery, which contained around ten bottles, including shampoo, shower gel, conditioner, dish soap, laundry detergent, shoe cleaner, toilet cleaner, and kitchen grease remover…

Essentially, it covered all cleaning products for daily living, and they were all in 1-liter bottles. It had to be said that a high-level lottery draw was quite generous. If it were a regular lottery, he would have gotten at most one bottle, and it would be only 500 milliliters.

Lu Qin had already stocked up on seven or eight bottles of powerful dish soap, so he wasn’t worried about running out anytime soon. The shampoo and shower gel came with random scents—rosemary and lavender, both with a light fragrance.

Lu Qin wasn’t sure whether the little one and the rich woman would like those scents, but he didn’t mind, as long as it wasn’t unpleasant. He had specifically checked the instructions, and the cleaning products he got from the system lottery were non-toxic, harmless, and gentle on the skin. He could use them without worry. Plus, their cleaning power was extremely strong, far better than any products available on the market.

For instance, the shampoo could effectively reduce dandruff, stop itching, control oil, and leave hair soft and smooth for several days. It also protected hair follicles, scalp, and hair quality. Once used, it was hard to go back to anything else.

After unpacking the care kit, Lu Qin found the bottles beautifully packaged, though none of them had brand labels. He placed them in their respective spots for easy access.

Once everything was in place, Lu Qin grabbed the shoe cleaner and took a few pairs of shoes from the shoe cabinet to clean them in the small yard.

Sitting on a small stool, Lu Qin squeezed some of the cleaner. The cleaner was foamy and stuck to the shoes’ surfaces. After waiting a few seconds, it could be brushed away with a soft bristle brush.

It didn’t take much effort to clean the shoes, just a little pressure. After brushing them, Lu Qin wiped them down with a dry towel, revealing a shiny, brand-new look. Even the dirt in the seams of the shoelaces was cleaned off. They looked like new shoes.

Using this cleaner was quite therapeutic. However, there weren’t many really dirty shoes at home—his and the little one’s shoes only had a bit of dirt on them.

After cleaning four or five pairs, the shoes held a faint, pleasant scent. He left them on the windowsill for a short while to air out before putting them back in the shoe cabinet.

Lu Qin asked the little one to bring a pair of sports shoes from under the bed to clean, and then he went back to the shoe cabinet for more shoes.

Clunk!

A red box wrapped in plastic fell to the ground. Lu Qin set the shoes down and bent down to pick it up. Just then, Shen Muyan hurried over. When she saw Lu Qin holding the Okamoto 001, her usually composed face immediately showed signs of panic.

Lu Qin glanced at the unopened package in his hand and then at the rich woman. Looking at the expression on her face, he instantly understood that this was the item she had placed in the shoe cabinet. Lu Qin’s expression became awkward.

What did this mean?

Had she been planning this for a while? Was it now obvious? Or was she just trying to cover up a mistake?

Men need to protect themselves well not only outside but also stay alert at home.

At that moment, the rich woman’s face turned bright red, so embarrassed that she could practically dig out a beach house with her toes.

“It’s Fang Ya’s,” she quickly came up with an excuse.

“Fang Ya left it behind” and “Fang Ya dropped it” were only one word apart, and she thought they meant the same thing. She figured it didn’t matter as long as it wasn’t something she had bought. She definitely wouldn’t buy something like that.

As for what Lu Qin thought, that didn’t matter to her. How could Fang Ya have left such an item at someone else’s house? And does one need to bring such things when freeloading for a meal?

This was a philosophical question!

“Oh,” Lu Qin said, handing the box over. “Make sure to return it to her.”

Shen Muyan was about to take it, but just then, she heard the sound of footsteps. The little one came running, holding a pair of sports shoes.

“Daddy~! Here are your shoes~!”

Lu Qin, with lightning speed, shoved the small umbrella into his pocket. Shen Muyan pulled her hand back and pretended to fix her hair. The little one didn’t notice anything unusual. She handed the shoes to Lu Qin and then ran back to the living room to continue watching cartoons.

“I’m going to brush my teeth.”

“I’m going to make tea.”

Once the little one left, both Lu Qin and the rich woman instinctively found something to do, casually passing by each other. That box of small umbrellas remained in Lu Qin’s pocket.

At 9:15 p.m., after taking their showers, they all went to bed. The little one fell asleep in no time as usual, carefree and innocent. Meanwhile, Lu Qin and the rich woman lay in bed, their minds entangled with complex feelings toward each other. Neither of them could fall asleep for a long time.

Thinking back to their first encounter, an unexpected roll in the sheets led to a child before they had even tasted the fruits of their connection. Then came marriage, having a daughter, working, and raising the child.

But after all that, they had never really been a married couple.

There had never been love between them; that word was taboo. They had both consciously avoided it. Others were living together as couples, but they were merely “living together” in a platonic sense, with no intimacy.

They were both normal adults. Even though they were attracted to each other, they hadn’t even shared a single intimate moment. Neither love nor passion had ever developed between them. Their relationship was just so strange.

And then, they had somehow, out of nowhere, chosen to divorce. People really do act against their own interests sometimes. When they got married, they ignored each other. Now that they were divorced, they were starting to “flirt.” What started as mutual indifference slowly turned into mutual liking.

Though it wasn’t illegal or immoral, it was infuriating to watch.

Why go through with a divorce in the first place?

It was just because they were bored. Unable to sleep, both of them got up, one for the bathroom, the other to get some water.

Under the dim light of the entrance, their shadows met on the wall of the living room, reflected on the TV background. They stared at each other for several seconds, almost as if drawn together by magnetic force. Slowly, they moved closer.

Lu Qin’s deep eyes moved between the rich woman’s clear, bright eyes and her delicate cheeks. In the soft light, her slender, graceful body seemed even more ethereal, and the sheer black camisole she wore highlighted her smooth, pale skin, which was enough to make Lu Qin’s throat tighten.

Lu Qin could smell a faint fragrance, familiar but not quite identifiable—like jasmine, but also resembling lily. It was the unique scent of the rich woman. The air seemed to freeze for a moment.

Slowly, they closed the distance, and finally, their lips met. It wasn’t clear how long it lasted. The rich woman’s face had turned as red as a cherry, and her body felt soft, almost limp.

Lu Qin’s arms wrapped around her delicate waist. Thanks to his improved physical condition, his breathing remained steady, though his heart was racing. He acted like a novice, but some things were instinctual, or perhaps just self-taught. Just when things were heating up…

Click!

A crisp sound of a switch being flipped came from the little one’s bedroom.

Had the little one gotten up to go to the bathroom?

Lu Qin dashed into his room at lightning speed. Shen Muyan hurriedly fixed her messy hair and adjusted her camisole as she headed back to her room.

“Hmm? Yao Yao, are you going to the bathroom too?”

“Mm-hmm, Mama, can you come with me?”

“Sure.”

Shen Muyan accompanied Lu Xiyao to the bathroom, and then they both returned to their room to continue sleeping.

chapters.title: Chapter 231
chapters.content:
Chapter 231

The rich woman was lying on the bed, covered with a blanket, savoring the wonderful sensation from earlier. Her bright eyes were hazy with mist. Her heart was racing, almost leaping to her throat.

Although she was incredibly nervous, the experience was very satisfying. She planned to continue next time. She smiled faintly, her red lips curled up, and drifted off to a sweet dream.

In another room, Boss Lu was in a great mood, opening the system to check his points.

8746.

He went for eight consecutive draws.

…

As the system’s voice echoed in his mind, Lu Qin kept receiving rewards. The high-level draw was a game of luck, and this time his luck was poor. The better rewards included [Full-Level Lamb Soup Noodles Cooking Skill].

Other rewards, like mint candies, weather cards, and various daily supplies, weren’t very valuable. After finishing the draw, Lu Qin turned off the lights and went to bed.

The next morning, the sun rose in the east. The red egg yolk hung in the sky, and the rays of dawn were incredibly beautiful. Lu Qin got up, washed up, and Xiao Mi had already bought all the ingredients from the market and was busy in the kitchen.

He decided not to go to the morning stall; today was the last day the film crew had a stall. When selling food outside, customers came from all over.

In the film crew’s stall, the customers could only be from the crew. It was clear this wasn’t sustainable, no matter how much they paid. Originally, he wasn’t supposed to sell in the crew today, but the stars, directors, and producers begged him. Some even offered their acting fees in exchange. So, Lu Qin agreed to make it the last day.

At 7 AM, Shen Muyan woke up quietly, not waking up the little one who was dreaming of eating ice cream. She walked outside the room, her hair in a mess, but still looking beautiful. Lu Qin was busy in the kitchen, his back looking quite handsome.

Shen Muyan took a cup of warm water, leaned against the kitchen door, and drank it while admiring the man seriously cooking. It was only when Xiao Mi went to the fridge for ingredients that she noticed her.

“Good morning, Mrs. Boss.”

“Morning.”

Lu Qin turned around and saw Shen Muyan wearing a camisole, revealing two smooth, white legs. One of the straps had slipped off her shoulder, and her chest was barely covered. However, Shen Muyan didn’t notice, sipping her warm water while gazing at Lu Qin with clear, focused eyes.

“Can you wear your clothes properly?”

Lu Qin put down the knife, wiped his hands, and pushed Shen Muyan into the bathroom, sending her off with a red card.

Inside the room, it was just Lu Qin, and the burst of spring was his alone. But Lu Qin was too afraid to handle it. At breakfast, Shen Muyan casually pushed the half-eaten porridge toward Lu Qin, while she ate shrimp dumplings.

Lu Qin slowly sipped his half-bowl of porridge. Lu Xiyao, biting a fried egg, looked at Lu Qin, then at Shen Muyan, and stared blankly for a few seconds.

She felt that something was different between Lu Qin and Shen Muyan today, but she couldn’t put her finger on it. After three seconds of suspicion, she continued eating the delicious fried egg.

At 1:30 PM, Flower and Fruit Mountain Park. The crowds at Lu’s food stall were overflowing. It was common to see ordinary people rushing to buy food, but seeing celebrities scrambling was rare.

When attending events or interviews, these stars were polite and humble, almost writing “quality” on their faces. But in front of Lu’s food stall, they stomped on “quality,” acting as if they knew each other well.

However, when it came to eating Lu’s fried rice, no one got in. Today was probably the last meal, and everyone was eating slowly, savoring the wonderful taste.
