Wu Chen: “I love this Bosnian oil cake, crispy on the outside and tender on the inside, sweet and soft. I’ve never had such delicious oil cake!”

He Zhuo: “The twice-cooked pork is amazing! It’s better than what the chefs at a seven-star hotel in Shanghai made!”

Shu Man’er: “Can the Ice Crystal Cake be delivered? I can’t live without it every day!”

Zhai Lin: “This is great! Gained two pounds in three days! I can’t control myself!”

…

Director Wen was eating fried rice while asking the screenwriters to add more scenes.

“There are so many actors from outside the crew now. Get them to do some cameo roles and delay for another day!”

“Please, Director Wen! I’ve really tried my best! The scenes are already completely off-track, like an old lady’s foot binding, stinky and long. The audience is going to tear me apart!”

The screenwriter looked miserable. This forced scene addition was difficult to write. The content was crap, like forcibly adding drama. If the cameo actors weren’t He Zhuo with a high status, the audience wouldn’t accept it!

“Really no way to do it?”

“Really no way.”

“Well, fine.”

Director Wen sighed helplessly. Tomorrow would be the last day for Lu’s food. The rest of the scenes would be shot in Haishi.

“Boss Lu, we’re done with the scenes in Jiangcheng, and we’ll be in Haishi tomorrow. I really don’t want to leave!”

Wu Chen almost hugged Lu Qin’s legs, crying as if it were the end of the world.

Zhai Lin wasn’t in such a bad mood.

“I’ve got four or five days of scenes left before wrapping up. Hey, it’s just over two hours from Haishi, so I’ll take a break and enjoy the food at Lu’s stall in Jiangcheng for a few more days.”

He was “playing” by eating Lu’s food every day.

He had gained two pounds in three days, but he didn’t mind gaining another five in the next seven days.

A group of celebrities actively tried to add Lu Qin’s contact, but he rejected them.

“Boss Lu, you’re the first one who’s rejected me trying to add you on WeChat!”

The more unattainable something was, the more they loved it. The actresses admired Lu Qin even more. Unable to add Boss Lu’s personal contact, they could only join his foodie group.

Every celebrity, actor, and director applied to join. Lu Qin accepted the requests. The group was very active, and when a new member joined, many began to chat.

“Oh, there’s a new foodie!”

“We’ve got over 1,800 members now. Boss Lu is amazing!”

Peng Kai: “Hello everyone, I’m Peng Kai!”

“Heh, you’re Peng Kai? What a coincidence, I am too!”

Peng Kai: “???”

He Zhuo: “I’m He Zhuo.”

“If you’re He Zhuo, then I’m Peng Yuyan!”

He Zhuo: “……”

Shu Man’er: “I’m Shu Man’er, absolutely genuine.”

“You’re Shu Man’er? Then who am I?”

A group member whose avatar was Shu Man’er’s quietly said.

The celebrities were stunned.

What’s going on in the world?

When you speak the truth, people don’t believe you, but when you lie, everyone believes it.

Finally, Wu Chen cleverly uploaded a video to the group. Everyone opened the video and was stunned.

“Holy cow! It really is Wu Chen!!”

“Celebrities are joining Lu’s foodie group?!”

“It’s true! Over the last few days, Lu Qin’s been selling food at the crew, and He Zhuo came to visit. He loved the food so much that he couldn’t stop!”

“So the He Zhuo above, is he the real one?”

He Zhuo: “It’s me.”

The group exploded!

The messages flew at an unbelievable speed!

chapters.title: Chapter 232
chapters.content:
Chapter 232

“Unbelievable!”

“Unbelievable!”

The foodies in Boss Lu’s group were on fire!

Even a superstar like He Zhuo joined the group, along with Shu Man’er, Wu Chen, Peng Kai…

Previously, some big bosses and entrepreneurs from the Jiangcheng business world also joined, but those impressive figures were not well-known, and they were very low-key, basically not talking much, only watching the group channel posted by Lu Qin about where he set up his food stall that day.

But stars were different.

Take He Zhuo, for example. With over a hundred million fans nationwide, beloved by both young and old.

The foodies in the group couldn’t even dream of being in the same group as a big celebrity like He Zhuo. Instantly, everyone started adding He Zhuo and other celebrities as friends.

However, He Zhuo had set his account to prevent adding friends.

How could a star like him easily let fans add him as a friend?

Otherwise, he’d be swamped with friend requests all day.

Not able to add them as friends? No problem! You could always @ them in the group.

@He Zhuo: “I’ve liked you for eight years! Do you know 27? Wow ah ah ah ah~!”

@He Zhuo: “The TV drama Sea of Flowers starring you is so good, I’ve watched it three times!”

@Shu Man’er: “Shu Man’er! I love you!”

@Wu Chen: “I’m your fan! Your acting is amazing!”

…

Thankfully, the foodies in the group were pretty well-behaved. No one @ed the celebrities with selfies or private pictures. Everyone was just showering them with love and excitement.

And the stars, with their high status, didn’t engage with the foodies in the group. Otherwise, the messages would never end. The excitement lasted for a while before everyone calmed down.

Then they began to wonder—Why did these stars join the group?

In the end, they were all fans of Boss Lu!

Wow!

Boss Lu was the real deal!

The foodies almost started worshiping him!

…

Last night, Lu Qin had unlocked the Full-Level Lamb Soup Noodles Recipe, which required simmering lamb soup, and you’d need a large pot for it, which would take up quite a bit of space. So, if he were to sell lamb soup noodles at his stall, he would have to give up one-third of the menu items.

Lu Qin left the choice to the customers, letting them decide whether to choose the lamb noodle soup or the old menu. He posted a poll in the penguin group. Being an “old man” who didn’t know how to play with group functions, he had to find a tutorial online.

In the group app, there was an option to create a poll. There was a single-choice poll and a multiple-choice poll. The foodies in the group realized that Lu the Chef was about to launch a new product, and they were super excited.

However, the new item was lamb soup, and since a whole pot of soup took up a lot of space, they’d have to sacrifice some other dishes on the menu. This made the foodies hesitate.

Why not have both?

But it was not possible.

The group members voted enthusiastically, and the results were in quickly. Most people preferred to try the new product.

“Been so long since I’ve had the original lamb soup, I miss that taste!”

“I remember the first time I had lamb soup in a small county in your province. The lamb soup was made early in the morning with all fresh ingredients. The taste was so fresh! I haven’t had real lamb soup since!”

“Now I see lamb noodle soup signs everywhere, but I can’t find a truly authentic one!”

“I heard that the lamb noodle soup shops nowadays use specially made powder for the soup base. They don’t even simmer lamb bones!”

“That’s scary! No wonder lamb soup doesn’t taste authentic anymore!”

“Looking forward to Lu Boss’s lamb noodles!”

…

After seeing the poll results, Lu Qin went out to buy the ingredients for the lamb soup. Just as he got in the car, he received a call from the wealthy woman, Shen Muyan.

Shen Muyan asked Lu Qin where he was and if he could make lamb noodle soup.

Lu Qin was surprised and asked, “Yes, why?”

The rich woman said she urgently needed help.

Lu Qin thought it was a bit of a coincidence, as he was also preparing to make lamb noodles, and here she was calling him. Lu Qin agreed to help, but made it clear he’d charge for it.

The wealthy woman readily agreed, and said “Sure.”

Lu Qin turned his car around and drove towards the Ourei Hotel. Not the one in the Annan District, but the one in Hongchang District. It was a bit far, taking nearly forty minutes.

At the Ourei Hotel in Hongchang District,

A middle-aged customer, after tasting the third bowl of lamb noodle soup, spat it out.

“The taste is still off!”

His act of spitting out the food into the trash can was undoubtedly a huge insult to the chef. Chef Bai had already made two large pots of lamb soup, adjusting the spice ratio several times, but it still didn’t meet the customer’s standards.

Shen Muyan furrowed her brow, unsure what kind of flavor the customer was actually looking for in the lamb noodle soup. The Ourei Hotel’s menu didn’t have lamb noodles listed as an item.

The first bowl of lamb noodles had been bought from a shop outside. The most authentic lamb noodle soup shop in Hongchang District, where customers lined up every day, and everyone who had eaten there praised the delicious and rich soup base.

But after it was brought to the customer, the man took a small bite and pushed the bowl away, saying, “The taste is off.”

He didn’t say what exactly was wrong, though. He just sat there, not speaking, making it clear that he wanted a new bowl of lamb noodle soup.

Because of this middle-aged man’s special status, the restaurant manager didn’t dare to neglect him, so he went straight to Chef Bai in the kitchen.

The manager hurriedly said, “Chef Bai, can you make authentic lamb noodle soup?”

Chef Bai had once wandered through Yunnan, Guizhou, and Sichuan, and had learned how to make lamb soup there. Though he hadn’t made it in a long time, he still remembered the recipe.

He nodded and said he could give it a try.

So the kitchen prepared the ingredients, and Chef Bai simmered the lamb soup. Once it was done, the entire kitchen was filled with the rich fragrance of lamb soup.

The restaurant manager tasted a bowl and his eyes lit up, saying, “Chef Bai, you weren’t kidding. It’s been so long, but the taste is still amazing!”

The restaurant manager was very pleased. This flavor should definitely satisfy the customer.

Chef Bai couldn’t hide a little smugness, saying, “You just need to remember the spice ratio. It’s actually not that difficult.”

This comment seemed a bit boastful. Other kitchen staff also tasted the soup, and they all praised Chef Bai for his great skills. The lamb soup was then served to the customer.

After tasting it, the customer still said the same thing–”The taste is off.”

But he didn’t explain what exactly was wrong.

The restaurant manager was puzzled.

How could a soup this good still be unsatisfactory?

Was the customer just picky, or was he deliberately being difficult?

Some of the kitchen staff were ready to storm into the dining room and teach the “snob” a lesson!

The restaurant manager was at a loss and had no choice but to call Shen Muyan. Later, Chef Bai made another batch. This time, he carefully adjusted the spice ratios, but again, the taste was still not right.

chapters.title: Chapter 233
chapters.content:
Chapter 233

In the service industry, dealing with picky and unreasonable customers is nothing new. When you open for business, you’ll inevitably encounter all kinds of customers.

But what can you do? The customer is always right, you have to serve them well.

This was also a crisis management situation for the hotel. Moreover, this particular customer was no ordinary guest; it seemed they came from a military district.

Shen Muyan didn’t dare to offend him, so she did everything she could to meet his requirements. While on her way, she called Lu Qin.

Lu Qin was well-known for his skills in Sichuan cuisine and noodles, but Shen Muyan didn’t know if he could make lamb soup.

After all, no one is perfect. How could anyone do everything?

But to her surprise, Lu Qin said he could make it. When Lu Qin arrived, Shen Muyan and the others were in the kitchen.

The strange, picky customer was still sitting in the dining area, probably not leaving until he got the lamb soup he wanted.

Chef Bai had given up. He told Shen Muyan and the restaurant manager,

“Ms. Shen, as you can see, I’ve done my best. Everyone has tasted the lamb soup I made, and it’s fine. The problem lies with the customer. It’s impossible to satisfy everyone’s taste; who knows what he’s expecting?”

Chef Bai was frustrated because the customer had spit out the soup he painstakingly made. That was the greatest insult to a chef.

Spitting out the food meant the customer didn’t appreciate the chef’s work.

No matter how good the dish was, if the customer didn’t like it, there was nothing you could do.

At that moment, Shen Muyan’s phone rang—it was Lu Qin, saying he had arrived.

When Lu Qin entered the kitchen, he immediately smelled the rich aroma of lamb soup.

“It smells great.”

“You are…”

Everyone turned to look at Lu Qin. The restaurant manager didn’t know who this handsome young man was, and was about to ask when Shen Muyan spoke up, “This is Chef Lu, whom I invited to make lamb soup noodles.”

Shen Muyan and Lu Qin exchanged a glance.

Chef Bai sized up Lu Qin and thought, Shen, having invited this young guy, might be a little desperate. Usually, just like in medicine, older chefs tend to have more experience and higher skill. Younger chefs are generally still maturing.

Shen Muyan led Lu Qin outside, explained the situation, and emphasized the picky customer.

Lu Qin thought for a moment, then looked at Shen Muyan and first brought up the money.

“If this works, how much will I get?”

Lu Qin stretched out two fingers and rubbed them together.

Shen Muyan rolled her eyes and said, “Are you a chicken feather on a shuttlecock, falling into a money trap?”

“One hundred thousand?” Lu Qin said with a smile.

“I’m providing the materials, and you’re just selling your skills. How can you ask for one hundred thousand? At most, one thousand.”

Shen Muyan gave him a look, annoyed that despite their relationship, he was still trying to bargain.

“Eight thousand!”

“One thousand five hundred.”

…

After haggling, they finally agreed on a price of thirty thousand.

Shen Muyan was eager to see what made a thirty-thousand-yuan lamb soup so special. She asked Lu Qin how he planned to make it and what ingredients he would need.

Lu Qin shook his head and said, “I’m not sure yet. I’ll need to assess the situation first.”

Shen Muyan was speechless, but she felt more at ease with Lu Qin there. Lu Qin entered the kitchen, took a look around, and then smiled.

“Let me first taste this lamb soup noodle. Please serve me a bowl.”

The restaurant manager personally served Lu Qin a bowl of lamb soup. After tasting it, Lu Qin nodded.

“Mmm, not bad. The flavor is pure, the broth is rich and delicious.”

Chef Bai’s face lit up. He knew exactly what level his lamb soup was at, and the flavor was undoubtedly top-notch.

Shen Muyan asked, “Is this lamb soup okay?”

Lu Qin replied, “Compared to what I make, of course, it’s not the same. I would say this is above average.”

Arrogant!

Chef Bai was not happy to hear this.

This young man is being too boastful. His tone is bigger than his skills! He thought.

“Above average?”

“Oh? You think you’re that good? Can you show us your skills?”

It sounded like a challenge.

The restaurant manager didn’t expect this young man invited by Shen Muyan to be so arrogant. He sided with Chef Bai.

There are many people like this nowadays—loud but lacking skill. The kitchen staff had already categorized Lu Qin as one of those types.

Chef Bai was responsible for the entire kitchen, and his culinary skills were beyond question. But now, this unknown young man was saying that Chef Bai’s lamb soup, compared to his own, was far inferior. They absolutely didn’t believe that.

The lamb soup in the pot had already been tasted, and it was delicious!

Was this Lu Qin really going to make something much better?

They definitely didn’t think so.

“No rush, I need to meet the customer first.”

Lu Qin analyzed the situation based on what Shen Muyan had told him earlier. He thought for a while and concluded that the problem likely wasn’t with the dish itself but with the customer. So, it was necessary for Lu Qin to meet the customer.

Shen Muyan took him to meet the customer. After the brief exchange, Lu Qin didn’t say anything special, just a simple greeting, and then returned to the kitchen.

“That’s it? What was the purpose of meeting the customer?” Shen Muyan asked in confusion.

Chef Bai and the others also wondered why Lu Qin had returned so quickly.

Lu Qin replied, “The customer is probably from East Province. He has a slight accent.”

Chef Bai asked, “What does that have to do with it?”

“Lamb soup has four major regional styles: Lingbao lamb soup from Hunan Province, Cangshu lamb soup from Su Province, Shanxian lamb soup from East Province, and Pingquan lamb soup from Beiping. Each style has a long history and its own unique legend. Naturally, the taste of the lamb soup differs from place to place.”

“For example, Lingbao lamb soup has the oldest history. During the Qin Dynasty, when the Qin army defeated the Six Kingdoms Alliance at the Hangu Pass, the king sent eight hundred fat lambs from Qin Chuan to the front lines to reward the soldiers. The soup was rich and savory, and the soldiers couldn’t stop eating it. The cooking pot used at that time was called Xun, a type of copper pot, which gave the lamb soup its earliest name, Xun Soup…”

“As for the Shanxian lamb soup from East Province, it has a stronger, more distinct gamey taste. But this is a pleasant kind of gamey flavor. The customer has been saying the lamb soup doesn’t taste right, which indicates that the cooking method is wrong.”

After Lu Qin finished explaining, everyone was stunned. No one had expected this young man, who had been so boastful earlier, to actually know what he was talking about.

Chef Bai’s heart skipped a beat. Could it really be that the cooking method was wrong?

He was starting to think that maybe Lu Qin was onto something!

He knew that his lamb soup was good, but could it be that he had missed something crucial?

Regarding the four styles Lu Qin mentioned, Chef Bai had heard of them but didn’t know how to cook them. His own skills in making lamb soup came from Hunan Province, which only used a simple, plain recipe, and as long as the taste was fresh and rich, it was considered good enough.

Shen Muyan’s eyes sparkled.

It was clear that Boss Lu was truly talented—there was no doubt about his skills in cooking.

“Can you make Shanxian lamb soup?” she asked.

“Yes,” Lu Qin said and nodded.
